PN CN215462117-U
TI Water vapour separating device for detecting total            organic carbon, has condensing glass tube inserted with            spiral rod, where spiral part of spiral rod and            condensing tube form gas channel for gas flow.
AU SUN J
   XING C
   XIANG K
AE ANHUI WAYEE SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202214928S
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a water vapour                separating device, comprising a refrigerating unit                and a condensing glass tube set in the                refrigerating unit, the condensing glass tube is                cooled by the refrigerating unit, the condensing                glass tube is inserted with a spiral rod, the                spiral part of the spiral rod and the condensing                glass tube form a gas channel for gas flow; so that                the gas enters the condensing glass tube from the                gas inlet of the condensing glass tube, and flows                in the gas channel. The utility model is inserted                into the spiral rod in the condensing glass tube,                the mixed gas of water vapour and carbon dioxide                and other gas enters the condensing glass tube from                the gas inlet of the condensing glass tube, the                tube wall of the condensing glass tube flows, and                the temperature of the inner wall of the condensing                glass tube in the whole condensing glass tube is                the lowest, Therefore, when the steam flows in the                gas channel, it is easier to generate condensation                effect, the high efficiency of the gaseous water                vapor is converted into liquid water, realizing                complete separation of water vapor and other                gas.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Method for waste water treatment by carbon emission reduction used in sewage treatment comprises e.g. mediating chlorine-containing waste water and removal of chloride ions, removing salt by centrifugation, and introducing upper layer liquid to resurrection tank for re-regenerative treatment.
AU WANG L
   SHI J
   LIU T
   WANG Z
AE SHIJIAZHUANG LVJIE ENERGY SAVING TECHNOL (SHIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202207629J
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises (S1) mediating the chlorine-containing waste water and the removal of chloride ions into the treatment tank, fully stirring, allowing to stand for stratification, introducing the lower layer water to the recovery water tank, and then introducing the upper layer liquid to the resurrection tank, (S2) adding the resurrection liquid to the resurrection tank and feed carbon dioxide simultaneously, removing the salt by centrifugation, and introducing the recovery water tank for secondary utilization to obtain the recovered scavenging chloride ion mediation, and (S3) fully stirring in the recovery water tank, allowing to stand for stratification, leading the water from the lower part to obtain the treated waste water, and introducing the upper layer liquid to the resurrection tank for re-regenerative treatment.
   USE - Method for waste water treatment by carbon emission reduction, which is used in sewage treatment field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problems of poor treatment effect on chlorine-containing wastewater and inability to reduce carbon emissions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the method for waste water treatment by carbon emission reduction (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The resurrection liquid is liquid ammonia. The carbon dioxide is carbon dioxide obtained after the flue gas is processed.

AU LI F
   LIU K
   ZHOU L
   ZHANG P
   WANG R
AE HEBEI LUOJI SUPPLY CHAIN MANAGEMENT CO L (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 202201872A
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a dustproof assembly of vehicle monitoring device, relating to the technical field of vehicle monitoring device. comprising a base and a camera set at the bottom of the base, the inner part of the base is provided with a motor, the output shaft of the motor is connected with the camera, two sides of the base are provided with a radiating hole, the base is provided with a cleaning assembly for cleaning the dust-proof net and a collecting component for collecting the dust, through the cleaning assembly, when the camera is used for a period of time, the dust-proof net is blocked by dust, causing the problem of heat dissipation in the base, the temperature in the base rises, the carbon dioxide gas in the piston bottle is heated and expanded to push the piston and the push rod to move upwards, so that the rotating plate is moved upwards and separated from the gear, the brush moves downwards quickly under the action of the spring A, scraping the dust attached on the dust-proof net, recovering the normal radiation in the base; avoiding the problem that the temperature in the base is too high, causing the damage of the camera.

PT P
PN CN113786727-A
TI Treating gas-fired water heater heat exchanger coated with composite catalyst comprises using high temperature resistant binder to coat layer of composite catalyst on surface of heat exchanger in low temperature section of gas water heater, realizing absorption of thermal energy and zero emission.
AU ZHOU Z
AE ZHOU Z (ZHOU-Individual)
GA 202200069M
AB    NOVELTY - Treating gas-fired water heater heat exchanger coated with a composite catalyst comprises using a high temperature resistant binder to coat a layer of composite catalyst on the surface of the heat exchanger in the low temperature section of the gas water heater, where the composite catalyst is a water-based coating prepared by using iron-copper transition metal double oxides as active components and rice husk ash containing amorphous silica as supporting materials, when the flue gas generated by the combustion flows through the low temperature heat exchanger, under the action of the composite catalyst, the unburned carbon monoxide is efficiently oxidized to carbon dioxide in a wide temperature range above 100degrees Celsius, and realizing the absorption of thermal energy and zero emission of carbon monoxide.
   USE - The methodism useful for treating gas-fired water heater heat exchanger coated with a composite catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical; has stable catalytic activity under the action of the catalyst, achieve the effect of energy saving and emission reduction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The process of the composite catalyst oxidizing carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide comprises the silica in the rice husk ash obtained by combustion below 700degrees Celsius mainly exists in an amorphous form. The composite catalyst can react carbon monoxide with oxygen at temperatures above 100degrees Celsius. The reaction equation is as given in the specification, (1) when the carbon monoxide concentration is lower than 5000 ppm, under the action of the composite catalyst, when the reaction temperature is higher than 100degrees Celsius, the conversion rate of carbon monoxide in the oxidation reaction can reach 100%. The composite catalyst is coated with a thickness of 0.1-1 mm on the surface of the heat exchanger, and is dried and formed at room temperature. The specific implementation method comprises (a) preparation of the active components of the composite catalyst adopts the excessive impregnation method, dissolving iron(III) nitrate nonahydrate and copper(II) nitrate trihydrate in deionized water with a stoichiometric ratio of iron and copper of 2:1 to obtain a uniform mixed solution, magnetically stirring the precursor solution at room temperature until the nitrate in the solution is completely dissolved to obtain the immersion solution of the iron-copper active component, (b) preparing of rice husk ash-based composite catalyst by burning at a temperature of 500-700 degrees C is immersed in the iron-copper active component dipping solution prepared in step (a), immersion for 0.5-1 hours, using compressed air to blow out the residual liquid in the pores, then drying, roasting at 450-600degrees Celsius for 5-8 hours, and then cool to room temperature, repeating the dipping for many times to obtain rice husk ash-based iron-copper double oxide composite catalyst; (c) the composite catalyst 10-20g obtained by the surface coating of the gas-fired water heater heat exchanger, depending on the length of the heat exchanger tubes, adding deionized water, adding high temperature resistant binder, stir evenly to form slurry, where the mass ratio of composite catalyst, deionized water and high temperature resistant binder is 10:150:1.6, immersing the processed heat exchanger tube section in the prepared slurry, taking it out after 0.5-2 hours, blowing off excess residual liquid in the channel with compressed air, and drying, repeating immersion, purging, and drying for many times, and roasting in a muffle furnace at 350-450degrees Celsius for 5-8 hours, and (d) detection of catalytic combustion and heat exchange performance Install the low temperature section heat exchanger with the composite catalyst on the test platform, the activity of the catalyst and the heat exchange performance of the heat exchanger were tested, and the carbon monoxide conversion rate reached 100%, and the heat exchange performance of the heat exchanger did not change.
DC G02 (Inks, paints, polishes â€“ polymer-based paints and inks are also classified in Section A (C09D, F, G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); Q74 (Heating, ranges, ventilating (F24))
MC G02-A05; J04-E11; J04-E09; J04-E04A; Q74-A02A
IP B01D-053/62; B01D-053/86; B01J-023/745; B01J-037/02; C09D-001/00; F24H-001/10

TI Tinted mortar composition used in concrete and            brick as building material to form slabs, comprises            pigment, treated palm oil fuel ash as binder, fine            aggregate, sodium hydroxide and silicate alkali            activator, and aluminum hydroxide.
AU AHMAD Z A
   MASLEHUDDIN M
   JOHARI M A M
   SALAMI B A
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 2021E1368T
AB    NOVELTY - Tinted mortar composition comprises a pigment                consisting of iron oxide, carbon black, chromium                oxide, and titanium dioxide, a treated palm oil                fuel ash as a binder, a fine aggregate, an alkali                activator comprises an aqueous solution of sodium                hydroxide and sodium silicate, and aluminum                hydroxide as a strength enhancer. The treated palm                oil fuel ash is present in an amount of 20-30 wt.%,                based on a total weight of composition.
   USE - Tinted mortar composition used in concrete,                brick, or other structural element as building                material, and used to form slabs, panels, precast                panels, wall boards, floor and roof tiles, catch                basins, manholes, beams, columns, posts, conduits                and pipes, insulators, external cladding, slate,                concrete decking, e.g. swimming pools, surfaces and                surrounds, ceramic style products, marble like                products, sink tops, bar tops, bathroom tops, table                tops, fireplace tiles, fire proof walls, building                blocks (e.g. masonry blocks).
   ADVANTAGE - The tinted mortar composition has compressive                strength and durability properties, such as reduces                POFA waste volumes in landfills, conservs natural                materials, and also reduces carbon dioxide                emissions and energy consumption required for                cement manufacture in cost-effective manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Conditions: The treated palm oil                  fuel ash has a median particle size (d50) of                  0.5-2.0 mum. The treated palm oil fuel ash has a                  loss on ignition (LOI) of less 3 wt.%, and a                  specific surface area of 1.4-1.6 m2/g. The weight                  ratio of the fine aggregate and treated palm oil                  fuel ash is 1:1-2:1. The weight ratio of the                  aluminum hydroxide and treated palm oil fuel ash                  is 0.01:1-0.05:1. Preferred Components: The                  mortar composition is preferably consists of the                  pigment, the treated palm oil fuel ash, the fine                  aggregate, sodium hydroxide, sodium silicate,                  aluminum hydroxide, and water.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN113522343-A
TI Catalyst useful for oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane comprises carrier and active component, where the carrier is manganese oxide molecular sieve, and the active component comprises metal oxide of Group V, VI, VII, VIII, IB, or IIB.
AU LIU F
   ZHANG R
   WU Y
   LI M
   JIN C
AE SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM PROCESSING (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2021C4228Q
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises carrier and active component, where the carrier is manganese oxide molecular sieve. The active component comprises oxide of metal M, where the metal M is metal oxide of group V, VI, VII, VIII, IB, or IIB.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst uses the special crystal structure of manganese oxide molecular sieve, has high speed conversion and ethylene selectivity at high altitude in ethane oxidation dehydrogenation reaction which is high level, has excellent stability, is suitable for continuous reaction, reduces the regeneration frequency. The method is simple, has good repeatability, which is good for industrial popularization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of the ethane oxydehydrogenation catalyst comprising: dissolving the reducing state manganese compound and metal M salt in water to obtain mixed solution; mixing the oxidation state manganese compound with the mixed solution, performing hydrothermal reaction, collecting precipitate; and drying and calcining the precipitate to obtain the ethane oxidative dehydrogenation catalyst; and
   (2) oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane, comprising contacting ethane and carbon dioxide mixed gas and manganese oxide molecular sieve or the ethane oxidation dehydrogenation catalyst at 600-850degrees Celsius to react to obtain ethylene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Based on the dry basis weight of the ethane oxydehydrogenation catalyst, the content of the carrier is 80-99.5 wt.%, the content of the active component is 0.5-20 wt.%, based on the metal oxide. The manganese oxide molecular sieve is birnessite, buserite, vernadite, hollandite, cryptomelane, or inesite. The metal M is chromium, cobalt, molybdenum, iron, nickel, copper or zinc. The oxidation state manganese compound is potassium permanganate, potassium manganate and sodium permanganate. The reducing manganese compound is manganese sulfate, manganese nitrate, manganese acetate or manganese chloride.The molar ratio of the oxidation state manganese compound to the salt of the metal M is (0.2-3):1:(0.01-1). The salt of metal M is nitrate, sulfate, hydrochloride and carbonate. The volume ratio of ethane to carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is 1-10:1. The reaction is carried out in a fixed bed reactor. Preferred Method: The method further comprises adding acid to the mixed solution before the hydrothermal reaction, and adjusting the pH value of the mixed solution to 0.2-3. The method comprises: carrying out hydro-thermal reaction to the aqueous solution containing the oxidation state manganese compound and the reducing state manganese compound; collecting the solid product and performing the first drying and the first calcining to obtain the manganese oxide molecular sieve; loading the salt of the metal M to the manganese oxide molecular sieve, and obtaining the ethane oxidation dehydrogenation catalyst after the second drying and the second calcining. The first drying temperature is 80-350degrees Celsius, the time is 1-24 hours, the calcining temperature is 200-900degrees Celsius, and the time is 0.5-12 hours. The second drying is 80-350degrees Celsius, the time is 1-24 hours, the temperature of the second calcining is 200-900degrees Celsius, and the time is 0.5-12 hours.

PN CN113401886-A
TI Producing dicalcium phosphate by digesting waste residues of nano light calcium carbonate, comprises e.g. calcining limestone at high temperature, sieving calcium hydroxide slurry, and adding stabilizing agent, filtering and crushing.
AU LI J
   LU M
   LI P
   FAN L
   JIN H
   SUN H
   WANG Z
AE XIANGYANG XINGFA CHEM CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021B0915G
AB    NOVELTY - Producing dicalcium phosphate by digesting waste residues of nano light calcium carbonate, comprises (i) calcining limestone at high temperature to generate calcium oxide (CaO) and carbon dioxide (CO2) gas, taking CaO and water to digest and reacting to obtain calcium hydroxide (Ca(OH)2) slurry, (ii) sieving Ca(OH)2 slurry, preparing pure Ca(OH)2 slurry to produce nano calcium carbonate, (iii) transferring digested waste residue into a collecting tank, adding water to mix for second time for 0.5-3 hours, and filtering by a screen to obtain Ca(OH)2 emulsion, (iv) adding process water into the reaction kettle, adding the hot phosphoric acid into the reaction kettle, preparing diluted phosphoric acid, adding prepared Ca(OH)2 slurry into the reaction kettle, controlling reaction temperature, and controlling reaction end point pH value, (v) reaching reaction end point, and adding stabilizing agent for curing, and (vi) pressing, filtering cooked material liquid, drying and crushing.
   USE - The method is useful for producing dicalcium phosphate by digesting waste residues of nano light calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces use of the traditional calcium oxide, and reduces the carbon emission.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the calcium carbonate content in the limestone is more than 97.5%, the particle size is 10-30 nm, and calcined at 1200-1300 degrees C, and the calcined lime activity is greater than or equal to 350 ml/4mol hydrochloric acid (HCl). In the step (iv), the reaction temperature is controlled within 45 degrees C, the reaction rate is controlled to be less than 0.4 Kg Ca(OH)2/hour, and the pH of the reaction end point is controlled to be 7.2-7.5. In the step (vi), the curing time is controlled within 1-2 hours. In the step (vii), the drying temperature is controlled between 110-120 degrees C. Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the main components of the digestion waste residue comprises 35-40 wt.% calcium hydroxide, water 35-40 wt.%, 10-15 wt.% calcium oxide, 8-12 wt.% silicon dioxide, and 2-5 wt.% magnesium oxide. In the step (ii), the screen is 80-100 mesh, and the digested waste residue is filtered by the screen and configured into a calcium hydroxide solution with a mass concentration of 5-10%. In the step (iv), the mass concentration of dilute phosphoric acid is configured to be controlled at 35-37%. In the step (vi), the stabilizing agent is sodium hypophosphite.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

   BALASUBRAMANIAN R
   POWER C
   SCHACHT P F
   STAUB R
   BUNDERS C
   LI J
AE ECOLAB USA INC (ECON-C)
GA 2021A66807
AB    NOVELTY - Treating a membrane system involves contacting a membrane with a peroxyformic acid composition comprising peroxyformic acid, formic acid, hydrogen peroxide, an acidulant and an anionic surfactant. The composition is membrane compatible and does not damage the membrane as measured by a decrease in flux of the membrane, and removed biofilm, microbial growth and/or mineral deposits from the membrane surface.
   USE - Method for treating a membrane system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses membrane which is compatible peroxycarboxylic acid composition comprising peroxyformic acid composition generated in situ or on site for use to remove and/or reduce biofilm growth and mineral deposits on membranes, and the cleaning compositions are biodegradable, decompose into non-hazardous products, which therefore leave no toxic traces on the treated membranes (due to rapid degradation into water, carbon dioxide and formic acid which are recognized as GRAS) and do not negatively interfere with the membranes, and the peroxyformic acid composition is suitable for generation in situ or on site of a point of use, allowing a user to promptly apply the composition to a membrane in need of treatment to contact the membrane surface and control biofilm growth at the place where the biofilm bacteria adhere and initiate biofilm formation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The membrane is a reverse osmosis membrane, nanofiltration membrane, ultrafiltration membrane, or a microfiltration membrane. The membrane comprises cellulose, cellulose acetate, nitrocellulose, polysulfone, polyethersulfone, fully aromatic polyamide, polyvinylidene fluoride, polytetrafluoroethylene, polyacrylnitrile, polypropylene, carbon, alpha-aluminum oxide, zirconium oxide, ceramic and/or stainless steel. The peroxyformic acid composition is free of 2,2-dibromo-3-nitrilopropionamide. The acidulant is nitric acid, sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid, and/or methyl sulfonic acid. The anionic surfactant is an alkyl aryl sulfonate, alkyl sulfonate, alkyl ether sulfate, alkyl sulfate, and/or alcohol sulfate. The alkyl aryl sulfonate is a linear alkyl benzene sulfonate and/or xylene sulfonate. The membrane is fouled with a hydrocarbon, microorganisms, biofilm, mineral scale, and/or iron sulfide. The peroxyformic acid composition further comprises a wetting agent, a chelant, solvent and/or an additional surfactant. The contacting is intermittent. The peroxyformic acid composition comprises an additional peroxycarboxylic acid. Preferred Conditions: The peroxyformic acid composition comprises 0.001-5% (w/w) hydrogen peroxide, and 0.0001-20% (w/w) peroxyformic acid. The intermittent contacting occurs daily, bi-weekly, or weekly.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN113354228-A; CN113354228-B
TI Treating oily sludge by supercritical water            oxidation comprises crushing oil-containing sludge,            homogenizing, pre-heating, boosting, mixing            oil-containing sludge with heat transfer auxiliary            agent, quickly heating, oxidizing, and separating.
AU DENG M
   CHAO J
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC GUANGZHOU ENG CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC ENG GROUP CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 2021A7646U
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises (i) orderly crushing                oil-containing sludge, homogenizing, pre-heating                and performing boosting treatment, ensuring the                oil-containing sludge temperature reaches 150-200                degrees C, reaching the subsequent processing                requirement, (ii) mixing the oil-containing sludge                from step (i) with heat transfer auxiliary agent,                quickly heating to 350-400 degrees C in presence of                no oxidant under high flow rate state, (iii)                separating the oil-containing sludge, entering heat                transfer auxiliary agent and separated                oil-containing sludge to a subsequent processing                step, (iv) adding oil-containing sludge in to                supercritical water environment, performing                oxidation treatment in the presence of an oxidizing                agent, completely oxidizing organic matter in                oil-containing sludge to carbon dioxide, water,                nitrogen and non-toxic small molecule compound e.g.                salt, initially separating material after reaction                into two parts including gas phase material as one                part, and reaction residue as other part.
   USE - The method is useful for treating oily sludge                by supercritical water oxidation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has difficult coking; easy system;                consumed less energy in oil-containing sludge                oxidation reaction; realizes efficient harmless                treatment of oil-containing sludge in supercritical                water environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (ii) comprises                  quickly heating oil-containing sludge to 351-380                  degrees C. The method comprises directly                  returning separated heat transfer auxiliary agent                  to the fast heating step to be used circularly.                  The method comprises firstly sending separated                  heat transfer auxiliary agent to the reaction                  step, taking heat from the reaction step and then                  returning to the fast heating step for recycling.                  Preferred Components: The heat transfer auxiliary                  agent is carbon dioxide. The oxidizing agent                  comprises oxygen, liquid oxygen, air or hydrogen                  peroxide.

PN CN113145065-A
TI Preparation method of fly ash-based adsorption material for biogas purification involves taking incineration fly, pulverizing and sieving, placing in vertical tube furnace, activating by nitrogen-rich aqueous solution, cooling, soaking in alkali/alkaline earth metal chloride solution, and drying.
AU LI K
   XIE J
   MA S
   CHEN Z
   CUI Y
   HU B
   LU Q
AE UNIV NORTH CHINA ELECTRIC POWER (UYHD-C)
   STATE GRID EASTERN INNER MONGOLIA ELECTR (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID CORP CHINA (SGCC-C)
GA 202189515J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of fly ash-based adsorption material for biogas purification involves taking incineration fly ash as a raw material, using a ball mill to pulverize and sieve, selecting fly ash whose particle size is less than or equal to a preset value, placing in a vertical tube furnace, activating by a nitrogen-rich aqueous solution at 600-900 degrees C for 1-3 hours, cooling to room temperature under an inert atmosphere, adopting excessive dipping method to ultrasonically soaking in alkali/alkaline earth metal chloride solution for 1-3 hours, and performing rotary evaporation drying to obtain the product loaded with alkali/alkaline earth metal chloride.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of fly ash-based adsorption material for biogas purification.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorption material has excellent synergistic removal effect on carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and other impurity gases in the biogas, and low raw material price, is prepared by simple preparation method, and realizes the resource utilization of fly ash and the efficient purification of biogas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The fly ash is formed by burning coal, biomass or domestic waste. Preferred Conditions: The preset value is 0.20 mm. The rotary evaporation drying is carried out at a drying temperature of 70-80 degrees C, a drying vacuum degree of 0.07-0.1 MPa, and a drying speed of 60-90 rpm. Preferred Components: The nitrogen-rich aqueous solution comprises aqueous ammonia, urea aqueous solution or hydrazine hydrate solution. The inert atmosphere comprises nitrogen, argon or helium atmosphere. The alkali/alkaline earth metal chloride salt solution comprises aqueous solution of sodium chloride, magnesium chloride, calcium chloride or barium chloride, where the concentration of the solution is 1-5 wt.%. Preferred Composition: The concentration of the solution is 10-20 wt.%, where the volumetric space velocity is 10-30 ml/(hour g). The loading amount of the alkali/alkaline earth metal chloride salt is 0.5-2 wt.%.

PN US2021220770-A1
TI Desulfurizing hydrocarbon feedstocks by introducing feedstock comprising hydrogen sulfide to absorber having metal chelate and introducing reduced metal chelate to photobioreactor comprising phototrophic bacterium, e.g. Chlorobium tepidum.
AU WEISSMAN J C
   LIU Y
   NIELSEN R D
   LEVINE S W
   HEINNICKEL M L
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 2021823252
AB    NOVELTY - Desulfurizing hydrocarbon feedstocks involves introducing a feedstock comprising hydrogen sulfide to an absorber comprising a metal chelate to form a reduced metal chelate; and introducing the reduced metal chelate to a photobioreactor comprising a phototrophic bacterium.
   USE - The process is useful for desulfurizing hydrocarbon feedstocks (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The bacterium has increased photosynthetic efficiency as compared to corresponding wild-type bacterium. The genetically modified bacteria, such as green-sulfur bacteria, may be coupled with an anaerobic metal redox reaction to remove or reduce hydrogen sulfide from hydrocarbon and natural gas streams. Additionally, processes including genetically modified bacteria may include carbon or nitrogen fixation reducing the amount of carbon dioxide (CO2) within the hydrocarbon stream and the amount produced in the purification of hydrocarbons. The combination has the potential to lower the cost of hydrogen sulfide treatment while also lowering the carbon emissions associated with a refinery and/or gas treatment processes. Additionally, these approaches may be used when a Claus plant is not feasible due to costs or location.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus comprising an absorber; and a photobioreactor fluidly connected to the absorber, where the photobioreactor comprises an anaerobic vessel with a light source.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: The process further involves introducing a fixation gas to one or more of the absorber or the photobioreactor.Preferred Components: The feedstock further comprises a fixation gas, and the process further involves obtaining a fixation product. The fixation gas comprises carbon dioxide, nitrogen, or combination(s). The fixation gas comprises carbon dioxide and the fixation product comprises a polysaccharide, a lipid, a biosurfactant, or combination(s). Alternatively, the fixation gas comprises nitrogen and the fixation product comprises ammonia, an amino acid, or combination(s). The phototrophic bacterium is a green sulfur bacterium, where the green sulfur bacterium is of the genus Chlorobium. The phototrophic bacterium is a green sulfur bacterium from the genus Chlorobium that is Chlorobium ferrooxidans and/or Chlorobium tepidum. The bacterium comprises a 16S ribosomal RNA sequence of 80%, 90%, or 95% or greater sequence identity with the nucleotide sequence of fully defined 1449 bp (SEQ ID NO. 5) given in the specification. The bacterium is from the genus Chlorobium and is free of a bacteriochlorophyll c synthase gene, where the bacteriochlorophyll c synthase gene has a 50% or greater sequence identity with the nucleotide sequence of fully defined 1077 bp (SEQ ID NO: 1) given in the specification. The bacteriochlorophyll c synthase gene has a 95% or greater sequence identity with the nucleotide sequence of SEQ ID NO: 1. Preferably, the bacterium is from the genus Chlorobium and is free of a phytoene synthase gene, where the phytoene synthase gene has 50% or 95% or greater sequence identity with the nucleotide sequence of fully defined 933 bp (SEQ ID NO. 3) given in the specification.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Device: The light source is a filtered or unfiltered artificial light source selected from a light emitting diode, a fluorescent light, an incandescent light, or a laser. The apparatus further comprises a separation unit fluidly connected to the absorber. The separation unit is configured to separate sulfur from metal chelate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal chelate comprises a metal ion selected from iron, copper, cobalt, nickel, manganese, vanadium, and combination(s).

The production                  of aromatic hydrocarbons from the biomass                  involves extracting methane from the biomass and                  performing methane to benzene (MTB) in which                  benzene is produces as aromatic hydrocarbon from                  methane by converting 6 molecules of methane to                  form benzene (C6H6) and hydrogen gas (9H2), or                  using carbon dioxide, methylating obtained                  benzene, in which toluene is produced as aromatic                  hydrocarbon by reacting benzene (C6H6) and                  methane to form toluene (C7H8) and hydrogen,                  using hydrogen produced from the MTB process and                  methylation process, in which methane and carbon                  dioxide are extracted from biomass, and                  electrolyzing water obtained from the MTB process                  in which hydrogen gas is generated. PN CN113003959-A
TI Producing compound mineral admixture using concrete residue comprises collecting concrete residue slurry, drying in oven, grinding, and determining optimal matching ratio range of raw material doping amount in compound mineral admixture.
AU CAO J
   SUN Y
   MENG Y
   ZHANG Y
   WANG Z
AE SHANDONG CHINA WEST CONSTR CO LTD (CSCE-C)
GA 202176122G
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises selecting and collecting concrete residue slurry with a liquid level below 10 cm. The obtained residue slurry is immediately dried in an oven. The dried residue is grinded by a powder mill. The particle size of the residue fine powder sample is detected after grinding. The mixing amount of the compound mineral admixture is determined according to the mixing amount of the grinded residue fine powder, clinker, silicon ash and mineral powder. An orthogonal test is designed. The optimal matching ratio range of the raw material mixing amount in the compound mineral admixture is determined.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing compound mineral admixture using residual concrete residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the waste slurry recycling in the concrete production process, reduces the environment pollution and resource waste, and obtains certain degree of social and economic benefit. The admixture replaces cement with large doping amount, and greatly reduces the dosage of cement. The regenerated concrete has good strength, rubber sand 28d intensity reaches more than 95%, saves limestone resource and energy, and reduces the discharge of carbon dioxide.

The                polyamide is polyolefin-modified polyamide or                elastomer-modified polyamide.

   ADVANTAGE - The sufficient stability is provided under                impulse loads to maintain the function of the                entire system in terms of tightness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for producing hose.

PT P
PN CN112619674-A
TI Preparing terbium-doped flower spherical bismuth oxychloride supported zinc oxide photocatalytic degradation material comprises e.g. adding bismuth nitrate, potassium chloride, sodium oxalate and cetyltrimethylammonium bromide.
AU TANG X
AE TONGXIANG QICHENG TECHNOLOGY PARTNERSHIP LP (TONG-Non-standard)
GA 202141462V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing terbium-doped flower spherical bismuth oxychloride supported zinc oxide photocatalytic degradation material comprises (i) adding bismuth nitrate, potassium chloride, sodium oxalate and cetyltrimethylammonium bromide to the mixed solution of deionized water and glacial acetic acid, and stirring and reacting for 40-60 minutes to obtain bismuth oxychloride precursor, (ii) adding terbium(III) nitrate to the above-mentioned precursor solution, uniformly mixing by ultrasonic, transferring it to the reactor, placing it in an oven for reaction, centrifuging the product, washing and drying to obtain terbium-doped curd-shaped bismuth oxychloride, and (iii) adding terbium-doped curd-shaped bismuth oxychloride, sodium hydroxide and zinc chloride to the deionized water solvent, uniformly dispersing in ultrasonic instrument, placing in a water bath and reacting at 30-50 degrees C for 1-4 hours, centrifuging the product, washing and drying to obtain zinc oxide nano-flowers.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing terbium-doped flower spherical bismuth oxychloride supported zinc oxide photocatalytic degradation material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can also directly react with organic pollutants; and oxidizes the organic pollutants into carbon dioxide and water to achieve the purpose of degrading organic pollutants.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of bismuth nitrate, potassium chloride, sodium oxalate, cetyltrimethylammonium bromide and terbium(III) nitrate in the steps (i) and (ii) is 100:50-60:100-110:30-40:0.5-2. In the step (3), the mass ratio of terbium-doped curd-shaped bismuth oxychloride, sodium hydroxide and zinc chloride is 100:1.5-4:5-14. The ultrasonic instrument device in the step (iii) includes a motor, the motor is movably connected with the rotating shaft, the rotating shaft is fixedly connected with a guide wheel, and the guide wheel is movably connected with the rotating wheel. The rotating wheel is fixedly connected with a stirring rod, an ultrasonic transmitter is arranged inside the ultrasonic instrument device, and a display is arranged on the surface of the ultrasonic instrument device. Preferred Method: The reaction temperature of the reaction in the oven in the step (ii) is 150-200 degrees C for 5-15 hours.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AB    NOVELTY - A mixture for coating metal substrate to                prevent or limit scale formation comprises 20-90                wt.% oxide ceramic material, aluminosilicate                mineral, and/or glass frit; 1-20 wt.% clay; and                3-70 wt.% alkali metal silicate.
   USE - A mixture for coating metal substrate to                prevent or limit scale formation for use in a                slurry (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The barrier formed limits or prevents the                formation of scale during heating or reheating of                metal substrates. The barrier is a physical barrier                which is at least impermeable to water and carbon                dioxide. The mixtures can be applied to a metal                substrate irrespective of whether the metal                substrate is cold prior to heating, or already hot                from a prior process. Accordingly, the metal                substrate does not need to be cooled prior to                applying the mixture, which reduces energy                consumption and costs associated with the                process.

   lurry for coating a metal substrate to                prevent or limit scale formation, comprising the                mixture suspended in water; and
   preventing or limiting scale formation on a                metal substrate which involves coating the metal                substrate with the mixture.

Aluminosilicate mineral comprises mica. Alkali                  metal silicate comprises any of sodium silicate,                  potassium silicate, or lithium silicate. Clay                  comprises china clay or ball clay. Mixture                  comprises 30-85 wt.%, preferably 35-80 wt.% oxide                  ceramic material, aluminosilicate mineral, and/or                  glass frit. Mixture comprises 3-18 wt.%,                  preferably 5-17 wt.% clay. Mixture comprises 5-60                  wt.%, preferably 6-55 wt.% alkali metal silicate.                  Mixture comprises a rheology modifier. Glass frit                  comprises a set of different glass frits, where                  each respective glass frit has a different                  chemical composition. Glass frit comprises 2-5                  different glass frits. Mixture comprises water.                  Preferred Properties: Each respective glass frit                  has a different softening point. Slurry has a                  density relative to water of 1.2-2.2. Preferred                  Processes: The method comprises coating the metal                  substrate with the mixture to provide a layer                  with an average thickness of 100-400 &#956;                  m. The method comprises coating the metal                  substrate with the mixture when the temperature                  of the metal substrate is anywhere between                  ambient temperature and about 1300&#176;                  C.

TI Vaginal retractor, has cylindrical guiding part whose end face is provided with sealing cover and reflecting element, where side wall of cylindrical guiding part is provided with square opening, and reflecting mirror surface located in square opening.
AU ZHENG X
AE ZHENG X (ZHEN-Individual)
GA 2021008370
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a vaginal retractor, belonging to the technical field of medical appliance. It solves the problem that the existing vaginal retractor is easy to act on the non-working surface when the laser of the carbon dioxide laser treating machine is emitted by mistake, causing the problem of medical accident. The vaginal retractor comprises a cylindrical main body; one end of the cylindrical main body is provided with a cylindrical connecting part and the outer cylindrical surface of the cylindrical connecting part is provided with an outer screw thread; the other end of the cylindrical main body is provided with a cylindrical guide part; the cylindrical guide part is detachably and fixedly provided with a reflecting element obliquely provided with a reflector surface; the end face of the cylindrical guiding part is provided with a sealing cover and the reflecting element is fixed in the cylindrical guiding part through the sealing cover; the side wall of the cylindrical guiding part is provided with a square opening; the reflecting mirror surface is located in the square opening. The utility model has convenient operation, high accuracy and good protection effect.
DC P31 (Diagnosis, surgery (A61B).); S05 (Electrical Medical Equipment)

TI Microencapsulating natural ingredient in            encapsulation material chosen from plant-based and            inorganic encapsulation material, by mixing natural            ingredient or encapsulation material, preparing and            spraying microemulsion through nozzle.
AU TRAMBITAS D O
   SOTOODEH F
   WOERLEE G F
AE FOLIUM BIOSCI EURO BV (FOLI-Non-standard)
   FOLIUM BIOSCI EURO BV (FOLI-Non-standard)
   FOLIUM BIOSCI EURO BV (FOLI-Non-standard)
GA 2020C7165P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for microencapsulating natural                ingredient in an encapsulation material chosen from                plant-based and inorganic encapsulation material                involves mixing the natural ingredient for                microencapsulation in a first liquid for providing                a first mixture, mixing the encapsulation material                in a second liquid for providing a second mixture,                where first liquid and second liquid being                substantially immiscible, preferably displaying                phase separation after being mixed, preparing a                microemulsion from the first mixture and second                mixture using mechanical shear forces, and spraying                the microemulsion through a nozzle into a                high-pressure vessel while drying it under                continuously circulating supercritical carbon                dioxide to a powder having a particle size of                0.1-100 mu m, preferably 0.1-10 mu m.
   USE - The method is useful for microencapsulating                natural ingredient in an encapsulation material                chosen from plant-based and inorganic encapsulation                material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a powder                having a particle size of 0.1-100 mu m of                microencapsulated natural ingredient, encapsulated                in an encapsulation material derived from plant                material, which is obtainable by the method, and                resulting in microencapsulated natural ingredient,                preferably with a standard deviation in size of 10%                or less of the average particle size.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The first liquid is                  polar and second liquid is nonpolar. The first                  liquid is nonpolar and second liquid is polar.                  The first liquid is hydrophilic, preferably first                  liquid is chosen from water, methanol, ethanol,                  and their mixtures. The first liquid is                  hydrophobic. The encapsulation material is chosen                  from encapsulation material which crystallizes                  when sprayed in high-pressure vessel with                  supercritical carbon dioxide, preferably at a                  pressure of 80-350 bar, more preferably at a                  temperature of 55-80 degrees C. The natural                  ingredient is extracted from plant-base material,                  preferably natural ingredients chosen from                  pigment, colorant, antioxidant, essential oil,                  oil, wax, natural aroma, protein, enzyme, and                  their combinations, preferably chosen from                  flowers, fruits and vegetables, more preferably                  anti-oxidants are chosen from phenols and                  polyphenols which have been extracted from plant                  materials. The natural ingredients are retrieved                  from plant material and are chosen from pigment,                  essential oil, antioxidant, enzyme and their                  combinations. The encapsulation material is                  substantially derived from plant-based material,                  preferably encapsulation material is chosen from                  starch, starch-based material, sugar,                  sugar-derived material, wax, oil, fat, and their                  mixtures. The encapsulation material is                  substantially derived from plant-based material,                  preferably phyllosilicates, phosphates like                  phytoxyapathites, and their mixtures, or                  encapsulation material is chosen from natural or                  mineral clay, salt, mixtures of salts in the form                  of oxides and hydroxides, and their mixtures, and                  mixtures. The encapsulation material is                  substantially derived from plant-based material,                  preferably encapsulation material is chosen from                  natural long and short chain poly-carbohydrates,                  preferably derived from wheat, maize (corn),                  peas, cassava, potatoes, beans, rice, tapioca,                  and their mixtures, long and short chain mono or                  poly saccharides type, preferably chosen from                  glucose, galactose, fructose, dextrose, ribose,                  trehalose, maltodextrin, maltol, and their                  mixtures, cellulose derivatives, preferably                  chosen from methyl, ethyl cellulose type                  cellulose and their combinations, gums,                  preferably chosen from rosin, xanthan, guar, and                  their combinations, from alginate, proteins and                  their mixtures, preferably dissolved in water.                  The encapsulation material is chosen from oxides,                  preferably chosen from rutile, zincite,                  periclase, hematite, maghemite, magnetite, and                  their combinations, hydroxides, like goethite,                  carbonates, like calcite, magnesite, sulfates                  like for instance gypsum, anhydrite, chlorides,                  for instance halite, like sylvite, phosphates,                  like hydroxyapatite, phyllosilicates like                  palygorskite, sepiolite, kaolinite, like                  montmorillonite, saponite and hectorite,                  tectosilicates like zeolite, talc, and their                  mixtures. The material is substantially derived                  from plant-based material, preferably                  encapsulation material is chosen from                  polysaccharides including lipophilic starch                  derived from corn, from cationic potato starch,                  vegetable-based protein obtained from pea and soy                  beans from rice, from corn and from potato, from                  heat-resistant rice starch from corn-derived                  polysaccharide, and their mixtures. The source                  material, preferably plant material, for at least                  one natural ingredient is contacted with at least                  one solvent for extracting at least one natural                  ingredient from source material, and solvent with                  at least one natural ingredient is contacted with                  a supercritical fluid, preferably supercritical                  carbon dioxide, for producing at least one                  natural ingredient.

PN EP3747925-A1; WO2020244992-A1; TW202112883-A; CN114008106-A; KR2022019099-A; US2022089804-A1; JP2022535451-W
TI Radiation-curable composition used to form            adhesive, sealant or coating comprises photo-base            generator to generate super base, (macro)monomers to            undergo anionic polymerization via Michael addition,            optionally polyethylenimine and additive.
AU ITO K
AE HENKEL AG & CO KGAA (HENK-C)
   HENKEL AG & CO KGAA (HENK-C)
   HENKEL AG & CO KGAA (HENK-C)
GA 2020C2860W
AB    NOVELTY - Radiation-curable composition comprises or                consist of: (A) at least one photo-base generator,                which can generate a super base; (B)                (macro)monomers which are able to undergo an                anionic polymerization via Michael addition                comprising or consisting of (B1) at least one                (macro)monomer having at least one Michael donor                group and at least one Michael acceptor group                and/or (B2) at least one (macro)monomer having at                least two Michael donor groups and at least one                (macro)monomer having at least two Michael acceptor                groups; (C) optionally at least one                polyethylenimine; and (D) optionally at least one                additive.
   USE - The composition is useful for obtaining an                adhesive, sealant or coating (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: exhibits reasonable reaction                times for industrial applications; and provides                continuous cured polymer film without carbon                dioxide bubbles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) an adhesive, sealant or coating obtainable                by radiation-curing the composition; and
   (2) radiation-curing the composition                comprising providing the composition and exposing                the composition to radiation i.e. UV, preferably                UVA-C with an intensity of 0.35-0.5 mW/cm2,                preferably 0.4 mW/cm2, for 10 seconds to 5 minutes,                preferably 30 seconds to 2 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The photo-base                  generator comprises                  1,2-diisopropyl-3(bi(dimethylamin)methylene)guanidium-2-(3-benzoylphenyl)propionate,                  1,2-dicyclohexyl-4,4,5,5-tetramethylbiguanidiumn-butyltriphenylborate                  and                  (Z)-((bis(dimethylamino)methylidene)amino)-N-cyclohexyl(cyclohexylamino)methaniminium                  tetrakis(3-fluorophenyl)borate, nitrobenzyl                  capped 1,8-diazabicyclo-(5.4.0)undec-7-ene,                  benzyl capped                  1,8-diazabicyclo-(5.4.0)undec-7-ene,                  2-(9-oxoxanthen-2-yl)propionic acid                  1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene salt and/or                  1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene, preferably                  the photo-base generator comprises                  1,2-diisopropyl-3(bi(dimethylamin)methylene)guanidium-2-(3-benzoylphenyl)propionate,                  1,2-dicyclohexyl-4,4,5,5-tetramethylbiguanidium                  n-butyltriphenylborate and/or                  (Z)-((bis(dimethylamino)methylidene)amino)-N-cyclohexyl(cyclohexylamino)methaniminium                  tetrakis(3-fluorophenyl)borate, and the                  photo-base generator is                  1,2-diisopropyl-3(bi(dimethylamin)methylene)guanidium-2-(3-benzoylphenyl)propionate.                  The (macro)monomer has at least one Michael donor                  group and at least one Michael acceptor group B1                  (i) has a molecular weight of 130-12000 g/mol,                  preferably 200-1000 g/mol and/or (ii) comprises a                  Michael donor group comprising aldehyde, ketone,                  nitrile and beta -dicarbonyl and/or comprises as                  a Michael acceptor group an alpha , beta                  -unsaturated carbonyl group and/or (iii)                  comprises (meth)acrylates or enones, preferably                  acetoacetoxyalkyl (meth)acrylates. The                  (macro)monomer has at least two Michael donor                  groups of B2 (i) has a molecular weight of                  130-12000 g/mol, preferably 200-1000 g/mol and/or                  (ii) comprises Michael donor groups comprising                  aldehyde, ketone, nitrile and beta -dicarbonyl                  and/or (iii) is                  2,2-bis((((3-oxobutanoyl)oxy)methyl))butyl                  3-oxobutanoate and/or the (macro)monomer having                  at least two Michael acceptor groups of B2 (i)                  has a molecular weight of 130-12000 g/mol,                  preferably 200-1000 g/mol and/or (ii) comprises                  as Michael acceptor groups an alpha , beta                  -unsaturated carbonyl group and/or (iii)                  comprising alkyldi(meth)acrylates, alkyl triol                  tri(meth)acrylates, alkyl tetraol                  tetra(meth)acrylates and/or bisphenol A                  di(meth)acrylates, preferably 1,6-hexanediaol                  diacrylate, trimethylolpropane triacrylate,                  pentaerythritol tetraacrylate, bisphenol A                  glycerolate diacrylate and/or bisphenol A                  glycerolate dimethacrylate. The polyethylenimine                  has a number average molecular weight of                  200-70000 g/mol, preferably 300-10000 g/mol. The                  additive comprises tougheners, rheology                  modifiers, thickeners, leveling agents and/or                  radiation-sensitizers. In (B2) the (macro)monomer                  has a Michael donor group and at least one                  (macro)monomer having a Michael acceptor group                  are present in a mole ratio of 2:1-1:2,                  preferably 1:1, where (A) is present in 0.1-10                  wt.%, preferably 0.5-5 wt.% and/or (B) is present                  in 70-99.8 wt.%, preferably 85-90 wt.% or 80-88                  wt.% and/or (C) is present in 0.1-10 wt.%,                  preferably 0.5-5 wt.% and/or (D) is present in                  0-15 wt.%, preferably 0.01-10 wt.%. The reaction                  temperature is 0-70 degrees C, preferably 10-35                  degrees C. The reaction is performed in an inert                  atmosphere, preferably under nitrogen.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN112028506-A; CN112028506-B
TI Co-producing sulfoaluminate-potassium magnesium phosphate composite gel material and sulfur, comprises mixing calcium carbide slag and desulfurized magnesium slag, drying, adding calcined aluminum ash, homogenizing and heating.
AU REN C
   WANG W
   WU S
   YAO Y
   SONG Y
AE UNIV QILU TECHNOLOGY (UYQQ-C)
GA 2020C4328V
AB    NOVELTY - Co-producing sulfoaluminate-potassium magnesium phosphate composite gel material and sulfur, comprises mixing calcium carbide slag and desulfurized magnesium slag and drying to obtain dried material, calcining aluminum ash, converting aluminum nitride in aluminum ash into alumina to obtain calcined aluminum ash, grinding the dried material and calcining aluminum ash, homogenizing, heating the raw meal mixture at 1250-1300 degrees C for calcination to obtain sulfoaluminate-potassium magnesium phosphate composite cementing material clinker and sulfur dioxide-containing flue gas, and carrying out catalytic reduction of flue gas containing sulfur dioxide to obtain sulfur, where the mass ratio between aluminum ash, desulfurized magnesium slag and calcium carbide slag is 10-15:60-70:20-25.
   USE - The method is useful for co-producing sulfoaluminate-potassium magnesium phosphate composite gel material and sulfur.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves the disadvantage of poor water resistance of magnesium phosphate cementing materials; utilizes industrial solid waste; reduces excessive carbon dioxide emissions; intensifies the greenhouse effect of the environment; and can produce large amount of sulfur; and reduces the cost of sulfur production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for system for co-production of sulfoaluminate-potassium magnesium phosphate composite gel material and sulfur.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The moisture content of the drying material is 1-2%. The raw meal mixture comprises 60-70 pts. wt. magnesium oxide, 10-15 pts. wt. calcium oxide, 10-15 pts. wt. aluminum oxide, 35-10 pts. wt. sulfur oxide, and 3-5 pts. wt. silicon dioxide. The particle size after grinding is less than 8 mu m. The sulfoaluminate-potassium magnesium phosphate composite cementing material clinker comprises 60-80 wt.% magnesium oxide, 20-25 wt.% calcium sulfoaluminate and 0-5 wt.% dicalcium silicate. Preferred Method: The method comprises mixing and grinding the sulfoaluminate-potassium magnesium phosphate composite cementing material clinker and potassium dihydrogen phosphate to obtain sulfoaluminate-potassium magnesium phosphate composite cementing material, preferably the molar ratio between magnesium oxide and potassium dihydrogen phosphate in sulfoaluminate-potassium magnesium phosphate composite cementing material clinker is 5-7:1; and the fuel used for the sulfoaluminate-potassium magnesium phosphate composite cementing material clinker obtained by calcination is pulverized coal or coal gas. The flue gas produced by the calcination of aluminum ash provides heat for drying after mixing the calcium carbide slag and the desulfurized magnesium slag. The method comprises carrying out catalytic reduction of the sulfur dioxide-containing flue gas after dust removal to obtain sulfur; or heating the low-temperature working fluid water in the waste heat exchange equipment containing sulfur dioxide-containing flue gas to obtain high-temperature working fluid water, and cooling the sulfur dioxide-containing flue gas, and subjecting to catalytic reduction to obtain sulfur, preferably the sulfur dioxide-containing flue gas after cooling is subjected to catalytic reduction after dust removal to obtain sulfur.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

PT P
PN WO2020235622-A1; CN113840653-A; JP2021520837-X; EP3974057-A1
TI Amine-containing polymer material used for gas-absorption material, comprises polymer of monomer mixture comprising monofunctional monomer and polyfunctional monomer.
AU YU H
   CHIE Y
   YUKI T
   TOMOMI F
   TAKESHI W
   WATANABE T
   FUJIWARA T
   TERAYAMA Y
   YAMASHITA C
   HOSHINO Y
AE UNIV KYUSHU NAT CORP (UKUY-C)
   UNIV KYUSHU NAT CORP (UKUY-C)
GA 2020B6506U
AB    NOVELTY - An amine-containing polymer material comprises a polymer of a monomer mixture comprising a monofunctional monomer and more than 10 mol% and 30 mol% or less polyfunctional monomer.
   USE - Amine-containing polymer material is used for gas-absorption material used in gas-absorption cartridge, gas-supply device, and gas-recovery device (all claimed) for recovering acidic gas such as carbon dioxide, and water vapor generated from thermal power station, iron mill, and cement plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The amine-containing polymer material has low water content and excellent gas-reversible-absorption capability, and produces gas-absorption material having excellent handleability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of the polymer material, which involves polymerizing a monomer in a reaction mixture comprising the monofunctional monomer, polyfunctional monomer, a solvent and an initiator, and impregnating the polymer with a treatment liquid comprising an amine. The total monomer concentration of the reaction mixture is 0.7 mol/L or more. When the monofunctional monomer comprises an amino group, amine impregnation is optionally performed; and
   (2) gas-absorption material, which comprises the polymer material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the gas-recovery apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Heat exchanger (21)
   Desulfurizer (22)
   Gas-absorption component (23)
   Pipe (24)
   Circulation path (26a)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas-absorption material further comprises a powder filler comprising microparticles having a particle size of 1000 nm or less and a water contact angle of 70 degrees or more. The powder filler is water-repellent fumed silica.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The monofunctional monomer comprises an amino group. The polyfunctional monomer is N,N'-alkylenebis(meth)acrylamide. The solvent is water and an alcohol. The total monomer concentration of the reaction mixture is preferably 3 mol/L or less.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer material further comprises a surfactant. Preferred Property: The polymer has a water content of 3 g or less measured after immersing 1 g dry polymer in water at 30 degrees C overnight. Preferred Process: Manufacture of the polymer material further involves heating the mixture comprising amino-group-containing monomer, polyfunctional monomer, and water, and adding a monomer comprising a hydrophobic group as an alcohol solution to the mixture before producing the polymer, and crushing the amine-impregnated polymer, filtering, and drying.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Emergency breathing device for internal medicine clinical use, has breathing connecting pipe whose outer side is movably connected with roller valve, where end of connecting pipe is provided with breathing cover, check valve and filter.
AU YAN F
   QIAN B
   WU J
AE YAN F (YANF-Individual)
GA 2020B9942R
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims an emergency breathing device for internal medicine clinical use, comprising an oxygen bag, a breathing connecting pipe and a breathing cover; one side of the oxygen bag is fixedly connected with a breathing connecting pipe; the outer side of the breathing connecting pipe is movably connected with a roller valve; one end of the breathing connecting pipe is provided with a breathing cover; a check valve and a filter. The emergency breathing device for medical clinic, through the check valve and filter, the check valve is set, when the patient exhaled air, can prevent the exhaled carbon dioxide into the connecting pipe, avoids the harmful bacteria pollution in the connecting pipe; the filter is set, capable of filtering the absorbed oxygen, the bacteria doped in the gas, virus or other contaminants, which is good for patient health, by setting the oxygen bag; when the patient is subjected to oxygen inhalation treatment, often needs to be connected with large oxygen bottle, heavy, inconvenient to carry and carry, the device comprises an oxygen bag, capable of timely carrying out oxygen treatment; it is convenient to carry, and the operation is simple.

PN CN111922029-A; CN213162405-U
TI Screening method for waste stored in landfills, involves using first-level drum screen and disc sorter to screen fine soil garbage, and using wind sorter to screen garbage, and secondary drum screen to screen rejects and pack combustibles.
AU ZHANG L
AE GUANGDONG HENGYI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B5485V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves stabilizing the garbage dump, digging and transporting the garbage, shredding large portions of garbage, vibrating to disperse the garbage, and using a first-level drum screen (11) and a disc sorter (12) to screen the fine soil garbage. A wind sorter (13) is used to screen aggregate garbage, and a secondary drum screen (14) is used to screen the rejects and pack combustibles. The gas injection pipe and the gas extraction pipe are driven into the garbage pile, the gas injection pipe is pressurized to inject fresh air into the garbage pile, and the suction pipe extracts carbon dioxide gas from the garbage pile. The gas injection and suction directions are changed every 1 to 2 hours. The stabilization treatment time is 7-10 days.
   USE - Screening method for waste stored in landfills.
   ADVANTAGE - The existing waste is separated into four types of materials such as fine soil, aggregate, rejects, and combustibles, and are recycled separately to avoid the accumulation of waste in landfills, and achieve the goal of harmlessness, resource utilization and reduction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the screening method. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. TI Supercritical extraction technology device of astaxanthin comprises bottom plate connected with temperature control box through bolt, and provided with heating pipe connected with constant temperature box through bolt.
AU ZHAO J
   CHU X
AE YUNNAN YUTENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B0009N
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a supercritical extraction technology device of astaxanthin, comprising a bottom plate, the bottom plate is connected with a temperature control box through a bolt, and the temperature control box is provided with a heating pipe, the heating pipe is connected with a constant temperature box through a bolt, and one side of the constant temperature box is welded with a first gas pipe, one end of the first gas pipe is in threaded connection with a connector, and the connector is connected with a first air pump through a bolt; one end of the first air pump is connected with a pneumatic press through a bolt. The utility model can keep the storage of astaxanthin at a certain temperature, which can stabilize the air pressure in the reservoir, so as to better store the astaxanthin; it can prevent the astaxanthin extraction liquid from blocking caused by the change of water content; the blocking pipeline influences the extraction effect; it can adjust the air pressure in the extraction tank through the air pump, The invention can purify the extracted carbon dioxide and circularly use, and increase the utilization rate of the gas.

PT P
PN CN111838141-A; CN111838141-B
TI Processing vacuum antibacterial storage method for mulberry leaves and mulberry twig for detecting carbon nitrogen isotope comprises taking calcium carbonate block and nano titanium dioxide powder, adding deionized water and dispersant.
AU ZHOU Y
   YANG H
   ZHENG H
AE CHINA NAT SILK MUSEUM (CHNA-Non-standard)
GA 2020A9440G
AB    NOVELTY - Processing vacuum antibacterial storage method for mulberry leaves and mulberry twig for detecting carbon nitrogen isotope comprises (i) taking calcium carbonate block and nano titanium dioxide powder, adding deionized water and dispersant into zirconium oxide ball milling tank, ball milling for 30-45 minutes at 200-400 revolutions/minute, obtaining calcium carbonate block coated by nano titanium dioxide, (ii) placing calcium carbonate block coated by nanometre titanium dioxide in precise vacuum atmosphere furnace to calcine, calcining at 825-910 degrees C in heating stage under 20-30 degrees C/minutes, heat preservation for 30-60 minutes, and obtaining nano titanium dioxide coated calcium oxide block, (iii) adding dried and crushed poplar leaves in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, extracting for 2-4 hours, collecting extract, distilling extract, extracting by organic extracting solvent, and blowing solvent by nitrogen to obtain volatile oil of mulberry leaves.
   USE - The method is useful for for detecting carbon nitrogen isotope.
   ADVANTAGE - The method eliminates the influence of microorganisms on the sample, reduces the natural fractionation of carbon and nitrogen elements, reduces the loss of isotope information in the production area, and ensures the accuracy of carbon and nitrogen isotope detection.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (iv) soaking calcium oxide block coated by nano titanium dioxide obtained in step (ii) in volatile oil of yellow leaves obtained in step (iii), drying block of calcium oxide to be oxidized, obtaining calcium oxide block coated by nano titanium dioxide of volatile oil of surface loaded large leaves yellow poplar leaves, (v) adding obtained product in polypropylene nonwoven fabric bag, heat sealing and sealing, (vi) selecting fresh collected fresh, full color, no worm, no disease spot of mulberry leaves and mulberry branch, respectively cleaning surface, and (vii) adding mulberry leaves and mulberry branch after cleaning into storage bag, adding polypropylene non-woven bag obtained in step (v), and using vacuum sealing machine for vacuum sealing storage bag. In step (i), mass ratio of calcium carbonate block and nano titanium dioxide is (6-6.5): (3.5-4), dispersant is 0.2-0.3% of total mass of raw material, de-ionized water is 20-25% of total mass of raw material. In the step (iii), the extraction pressure is 35-45 MPa, the extraction temperature is 28-32 degrees C, and the extraction flow rate is 15-25 l/hours. In the step (iv), the soaking is carried out for 2-3 hours, and it becomes a dry body at room temperature. In the step (vi), the material used in the cleaning process is dust-free paper, and the solvent used in the cleaning process is 75% ethanol. In the step (vii), the vacuum degree of the storage bag after being evacuated is -0.15-0 MPa. Preferred Components: In the step (i), the particle size of the calcium carbonate block is 2-3 cm. In the step (iii), the organic extraction solvent is cyclohexane.
DC D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); F04 (Braiding, knitting - including trimmings and non-woven fabrics (D04).); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)
MC D09-A01C; D09-A03; E05-R; E11-Q03A; E31-H03; E31-N04D; E31-U01; E34-D01; E35-K02; A04-G03E; A11-C01A; A11-C05A; A12-S05G; A12-W04; F03-C02B; P13-A99

PN BR102019006913-A2
TI Process for removing contaminants from gas streams using contactors with membranes and salt water as extraction liquid, involves providing contactors with isotropic or anisotropic hollow fiber membranes and using water as absorbent liquid.
AU BRANDAO DE SOUZA MENDES F
   PIACSEK BORGES C
   HABERT A C
AE DIRETORIA-GERAL DESENVOLVIMENTO NUCLEAR (DIRE-Non-standard)
   INST PESQUISAS DA MARINHA (PESQ-Non-standard)
   UNIV RIO DE JANEIRO (UYRI-Non-standard)
GA 2020B3097H
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves providing contactors (20) with isotropic or anisotropic hollow fiber membranes. The salt water is used as an absorbent liquid. The salt water used as an absorbent liquid, may come from a purification, filtration, microfiltration, nanofiltration or reverse osmosis system. The salt water used as an absorbing liquid, may come directly from the main circulation system of an aquatic or underwater vessel. The energy integration is used through the use of pressure reducing valves and pressure lifting pumps to regulate the liquid pressure, in addition to air blowers.
   USE - Process for removing contaminants from gas streams, specifically the removal of carbon-dioxide from atmospheric air in confined environments, using contactors with membranes and salt water as the extraction liquid, mainly sea water.
   ADVANTAGE - The process eliminates the stages of regeneration of an absorbent liquid commonly used in conventional processes, which take up more space and can be a source of harmful leaks.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of a process for removing contaminants from gas streams using contactors with membranes and salt water as extraction liquid. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Pipeline (2)
   Stream (4,8)
   Current (6)
   Contactors (20)

TI Photocatalytic device for deodorization and sterilization of air, has light configured for applying light to photocatalyst sheet, and fan is configured for sending drawn air from first inlet to outlet through photocatalyst sheet.
AU HARAZONO T
   SOMEI J
   TOYOHIRO H
AE KALTECH CORP (KALT-Non-standard)
   KALTECH CO LTD (KALT-Non-standard)
   KALTECH CORP (KALT-Non-standard)
GA 2020A0535L
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a cabinet (110) having a first inlet. An outlet (114L) is formed at a right angle to the first inlet. A photocatalyst sheet (120) is arranged inside the cabinet. A light (130) is configured for applying light to the photocatalyst sheet. A fan (150) is configured for sending drawn air from the first inlet to the outlet through the photocatalyst sheet. The photocatalyst sheet is arranged at a right angle to the first inlet and to the outlet. The cabinet further includes a second inlet formed parallel to the first inlet.
   USE - Photocatalytic device for use in deodorization and sterilization of air with the use of a photocatalyst in vegetables storage compartment of refrigerator.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide generated by the catalytic action of the photocatalyst reduces the oxygen concentration in the storage compartment, keeping vegetables fresh for a longer time period by inhibiting the breathing of vegetables. Enables effective removal of odor components and germs in a large-volume storage compartment, by the photocatalytic device having a more compact configuration.
