PT P
PN CN111747820-A
TI Producing hydrocarbon involves mixing methane-rich            gas and oxygen-rich gas to form raw material gas, where            raw material gas undergoes oxidative coupling reaction            under action of catalyst in fixed bed reactor to obtain            reaction product gas.
AU LIU G
   SUN L
   WANG Z
   SHENG Z
   NIE Y
   LI S
   DING L
   ZHAO B
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC ENG INC (SNPC-C)
GA 2020A12561
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrocarbon involves mixing                methane-rich gas and oxygen-rich gas to form a raw                material gas. The raw material gas undergoes an                oxidative coupling reaction under action of a                catalyst in a fixed bed reactor to obtain reaction                product gas containing at least carbon monoxide,                carbon dioxide, ethylene, ethane, and carbon                reaction product gas of three or more components.                The fixed-bed reactor adopts multi-stage catalyst                beds in series, and 1-3 stages of heat extraction                measures are arranged between two adjacent stages                of catalyst beds, and at least one stage of heat                extraction measures adopts serpentine heat                extraction. The one stage of catalyst bed is                provided with an injection port, and injected                material includes oxygen-rich gas, methane-rich                gas, carbon monoxide, inert gas, and water. The                residence time of oxygen-enriched gas injected at                injection port from injection to reaching the                catalyst bed is not more than 100ms.
   USE - Method for producing hydrocarbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce hydrocarbon,                which ensures that reaction conditions of each                stage of bed is in appropriate range, improves                overall yield of target product, and is good for                operation and control.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The residence time of                  oxygen-enriched gas injected at the injection                  port from injection to reaching the catalyst bed                  is not more than 50ms. The mixing temperature of                  methane-rich gas and oxygen-rich gas is 20-900                  degrees C. The temperature of oxidative coupling                  reaction is 600-1100 degrees C, and pressure is                  0.01-1.5MPaG. The raw material gas flows from top                  to bottom, and volumetric space velocity is                  5000-49900 per hour. The number of stages of                  multi-stage catalyst bed is 2-6 stages. The                  implementation position of the heat extraction                  measure is located outside catalyst bed and/or                  inside catalyst bed. The heat extraction measure                  is heat extraction by a heat exchanger, and heat                  extraction medium used is selected from water,                  steam, raw material gas, reaction product gas,                  inert substance, and molten salt. The heat                  extraction measure is performed to directly                  inject low-temperature materials to extract heat,                  and low-temperature materials used are selected                  from water, steam, raw material gas, reaction                  product gas, and inert materials. The volume                  ratio of methane to oxygen at entrance of each                  stage of catalyst bed is 0.1:1-80:1. The                  unreacted methane, generated carbon monoxide, and                  ethane in reaction product gas is returned to                  reaction system to continue to participate in the                  reaction. The volume content of methane in                  methane-rich gas is not greater than 50%,                  preferably not greater than 90%, and is further                  preferred that methane-rich gas is natural gas                  and/or shale gas. The volume content of oxygen in                  oxygen-enriched gas is 12-100%. The fixed bed                  reactor is provided with a heat insulation                  structure for separating high temperature area                  from low temperature area.
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC H04-E; H04-F02E
IP C07C-011/04; C07C-002/84
PD CN111747820-A   09 Oct 2020   C07C-011/04   202092   Pages: 9   Chinese

PN CN111751300-A
TI Optical combination action measuring system comprises isotope photosynthetic instrument comprising lower cavity provided with groove and air mixing chamber connected with water vapor control device.
AU ZHANG G
AE BEIJING PURUI YIKE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020A1208U
AB    NOVELTY - Optical combination action measuring system comprises an isotope photosynthetic instrument comprising a handle, a lower cavity, an upper cavity and an light emitting diode (LED) light source. The upper surface of the lower cavity is provided with a groove, forming a lower chamber. The lower cavity is provided with a lower square sealing gasket. The inner part of the upper cavity is provided with an LED light source. The upper cavity is fixed on the handle. A gas pre-mixing module comprises a zero gas bottle, a first gas bottle, a second gas bottle and a third gas bottle. The first gas bottle, the second gas bottle and the third gas bottle are connected to a gas selection module. An air mixing chamber is connected with a water vapor control device. The water vapor control device is connected with the upper cavity through the air inlet pipe. The lower cavity is connected with a carbon dioxide isotope analysis module through an exhaust pipe.
   USE - Used as optical combination action measuring system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system realizes the photosynthesis of the plant to measure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the optical combination action measuring system.
DC S03 (Scientific Instrumentation); U12 (Discrete Devices)
MC S03-E04A1; U12-A01A

PN JP2020164409-A; JP6795115-B2
TI Cement composition comprises mixture of cement clinker containing sulfur trioxide and titanium dioxide, gypsum in terms of sulfur trioxide, and mixed material comprising limestone and blast furnace slag.
AU KADOTA H
   HARIKAI T
   TAKAHASHI T
AE MITSUBISHI MATERIALS CORP (MITV-C)
GA 202097952Q
AB    NOVELTY - A cement composition comprises a mixture of cement clinker, 1.8-2.4 %mass gypsum in terms of sulfur trioxide, and 6.5-10.0 %mass mixed material. The mixed material comprises limestone and 1.0-6.0 %mass blast furnace slag. The cement clinker comprises 0.94-1.58 %mass sulfur trioxide, titanium dioxide, manganese oxide, phosphorus pentoxide, sodium oxide, and potassium oxide.
   USE - Cement composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The cement composition has increased strength development property without increasing the amount of carbon dioxide generated.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A cement composition comprises a mixture of cement clinker, 1.8-2.4 %mass gypsum in terms of sulfur trioxide, and 6.5-10.0 %mass mixed material. The mixed material comprises limestone and 1.0-6.0 %mass blast furnace slag. The cement clinker comprises 0.94-1.58 %mass sulfur trioxide, titanium dioxide, manganese oxide, phosphorus pentoxide, sodium oxide, and potassium oxide. The cement clinker has physical property value T, M, P', N', and K' calculated by using relation: (T+M+P')/(N'+0.658x K') = 0.853-1.141 (I).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of cement finishing apparatus.
   Cement finishing apparatus (1)
   Clinker silo (2)
   Finish mill (3)
   Classifier (4)
   Mixing apparatus (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The cement clinker further comprises mineral composition comprising 48.0-70.0 %mass tricalcium silicate, 9.0-22.0 %mass 1-thiacycloprop-2-yne, 8.0-14.0 %mass tricalcium aluminate, and 7.0-12.0 %mass calcium aluminoferrite.

TI New 2,3,5,6,7,8-hexahydro-1H-pyrimido4,5-dpyrimidin-4-one derivative includes metallic substrate comprises steel, carbon steel, the corrosive medium comprises inorganic acid, inorganic salt, corrosive medium comprises carbon dioxide.
AU OBOT I B
   ONYEACHU B I
   QURAISHI M A
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 2020981783
AB    NOVELTY - 2,3,5,6,7,8-hexahydro-1H-pyrimido(4,5-d)pyrimidin-4-one derivative (I) is new.
   USE - 2,3,5,6,7,8-hexahydro-1H-pyrimido(4,5-d)pyrimidin-4-one derivative.
   ADVANTAGE - The 2,3,5,6,7,8-hexahydro-1H-pyrimido(4,5-d)pyrimidin-4-one derivative is cost effective and has high miscibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - 2,3,5,6,7,8-hexahydro-1H-pyrimido(4,5-d)pyrimidin-4-one derivative of formula (I) is new..
   R1=optionally substituted alkoxy, optionally substituted aryloxy, optionally substituted amino, and optionally substituted amide;
   R2=hydrogen, optionally substituted alkyl, optionally substituted cycloalkyl, optionally substituted arylalkyl, and optionally substituted aryl;
   R3=hydrogen, optionally substituted alkoxy, optionally substituted aryloxy, optionally substituted amino, optionally substituted amide, optionally substituted alkyl, optionally substituted cycloalkyl, optionally substituted arylalkyl, and optionally substituted aryl;
   X=O or S; and
   Y=O or S.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for inhibiting corrosion of metallic substrate in contact with corrosive medium, which involves introducing 2,3,5,6,7,8-hexahydro-1H-pyrimido(4,5-d)pyrimidin-4-one derivative of formula (I) into corrosive medium in contact with metallic substrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): No preparation method is given. Preferred Components: The metallic substrate comprises steel, carbon steel, the corrosive medium comprises inorganic acid, inorganic salt, or both. The corrosive medium comprises CO2, brine, or both. The 2,3,5,6,7,8-hexahydro-1H-pyrimido(4,5-d)pyrimidin-4-one derivative of formula (I) is also represented by formula (II). Preferred Conditions: The 2,3,5,6,7,8-hexahydro-1H-pyrimido(4,5-d)pyrimidin-4-one derivative (I) is introduced into the corrosive medium at concentration of 0.1-200 ppm. The 2,3,5,6,7,8-hexahydro-1H-pyrimido(4,5-d)pyrimidin-4-one derivative (I) has corrosion inhibition efficiency in range of 85-98%. The 2,3,5,6,7,8-hexahydro-1H-pyrimido(4,5-d)pyrimidin-4-one derivative (I) imparts corrosion rate in a range of 0.8-2.5 mils penetration per year (mpy) to the metallic substrate. The metallic substrate is portion of a system for oil or gas production, transportation, or refining..
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1=NH2, NHCH3, NHC2H5, N(CH3)2, N(CH3)C2H5, N(C2H5)2, pyrrolidinyl, piperidinyl, 4-methylpiperidinyl, morpholinyl, piperazinyl, and (N-methyl)piperazinyl.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); M14 (Other chemical surface treatments - including etching, brightening, forming non-metallic layers, passivation, cathodic protection and corrosion inhibitors, but not processes specifically for semiconductor production (C23F, C25). This Section also covers multistage processes.)

PN KR2020113453-A; KR2342009-B1
TI Manufacturing geopolymer concrete involves forming a dry mixing mixture by mixing the geopolymer binder and aggregate including fly ash and blast furnace slag, adding an aqueous alkali stimulant solution and mixing to the dry bibim mixture.
AU LEECHADON
AE UNIV CHUNG ANG IND ACAD COOP FOUND (UYCU-C)
GA 202098923S
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing geopolymer concrete involves forming a dry mixing mixture by mixing the geopolymer binder and the aggregate including fly ash and blast furnace slag. An aqueous alkali stimulant solution is added and mixed to the dry bibim mixture to form a blended mixture, added mixed water to the blended mixture, and mixed to form a pouring mixture.
   USE - Method for manufacturing geopolymer concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to manufacture geopolymer concrete is eco-friendly, enhances fluidity, and has less carbon dioxide emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a geopolymer concrete composition with enhanced fluidity, which comprises 15-20 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide (NaOH) aqueous solution of 10-12 molar concentration (M) based on 100 pts. wt., geopolymer binder includes 55-80 wt.% fly ash and 20-45 wt.% blast furnace slag, 40-45 pts. wt. sodium silicate (Na2SiO3) aqueous solution produced by mixing 14-15 wt.% sodium oxide (Na2O), 34-35 wt.% silicon dioxide (SiO2), and 50-52 wt.% distilled water (H20) as a mixture, and 5-10 pts. wt. mixed water to be additionally added after mixing the blended mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Compositions: The geopolymer binder includes 55-80 wt.% fly ash and 20-45 wt.% blast furnace slag. The includes 227-230 pts. wt. coarse aggregate based on 100 pts. wt. the binder, and 120-123 pts. wt. fine aggregate based on 100 pts. wt. the binder. The alkaline stimulant aqueous solution includes 15-20 pts. wt. an aqueous sodium hydroxide (NaOH) solution having a molar concentration (M) of 10-12 based on 100 pts. wt. the binder. The sodium silicate (Na2SiO3) aqueous solution produced by mixing 14-15 wt.% sodium oxide (Na2O), 34-35 wt.% silicon dioxide (SiO2), and 50-52 wt.% distilled water (H20), and 40-45 wt.% the binder based on 100 pts. wt.. The weight ratio of the sodium silicate (Na2SiO3) to the sodium hydroxide (NaOH) is 2-3. Preferred Condition: The mixing time of the dry bibim mixture and the alkaline stimulating agent aqueous solution is 120-180 seconds in the step of forming the blending mixture.

PN RU2732625-C1
TI METHOD FOR PRODUCTION OF MARMALADE FOR PREVENTIVE PURPOSE.
AU FROLOVA N A
AE UNIV AMUR (UYAM-Non-standard)
GA 2020A02430
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to food industry, to confectionary industry, and may be used for production of jelly marmalade of preventive purpose. Method for production of preventive marmalade includes mixing dry pectin with beet juice obtained by preliminary scalding with water t = 7580C milled into cubes with size of 1 cmx1 cm of beet, followed by aqueous extraction of beetroot to produce juice, its filtration, saturation, which consists in treatment of produced beet juice with saturation gas containing 3034% of carbon dioxide. Then one performs thickening of the juice by evaporation of up to 6570% of dry substances and cooling for 30 minutes to temperature of 30C. Then one performs swelling of a mixture of beet juice and pectin for 2 hours, filtration, boiling of the obtained mixture till moisture content is 3032% for 57 minutes. Then one adds apple-lemon juice produced by squeezing peeled apples and lemon, boiling to moisture content of 2526% for 20 minutes, cooling mass to 7880C, with further introduction of VitaLag biologically active substance. Then the produced marmalade is cooled, matured, dried and sprinkled with fine-crystal sugar at the following ratio of initial components, wt.%: pectin - 12, beetroot juice - 8083, VitaLag biologically active substance - 510, apple-lemon syrup - 14, citric acid - 0.020.09, water is balance.
   USE - Food industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention allows to produce marmalade of preventive purpose with increased biological value.
1 cl, 2 tbl, 4 ex

TI Separation of syngas in generation of turbine powdered electricity, involves separating feed stream comprising syngas in separator unit to form carbon monoxide-rich stream and hydrogen-rich stream, and reacting hydrogen-rich stream with air.
AU CALLAHAN R A
AE CALLAHAN R A (CALL-Individual)
GA 2020865994
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of syngas involves (a) feeding a separator feed stream comprising syngas, to separator unit, (b) separating feed stream in the separator unit, to form a carbon monoxide-rich stream (s1) and hydrogen-rich stream (s2), (c) feeding the stream (s1) as an oxyfuel combustor feed stream (4) to an oxyfuel combustor unit (5) in which the oxyfuel combustor feed stream is reacted with a high purity oxygen feed stream of at least 95% purity, and (d) feeding the stream (s2) as an air-fuel combustor feed stream to air-fuel combustor unit in which the air-fuel combustor feed stream is reacted with air.
   USE - Separation of syngas in generation of turbine powdered electricity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables separation of syngas with zero emission and suppressed consumption of water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the supercritical carbon dioxide turbine plant.

   Oxyfuel combustor unit (5)

   Turbine exhaust stream (12)
   Heat exchanger (13)
   Water separator unit (16)
   Removal of condensed water (17)
   Compressor (18)
   Cooler (19)
   Pump (21)

PN CN211394765-U
TI Degradable biomass fiber, has wire core whose outer surface is provided with antibacterial fiber, polylactic acid fiber and tencel fiber, and high temperature resistant layer whose outer surface is connected with elastic layer by adhesive.
AU TU W
AE SHANGRAO GUANGFENG FENGRUI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202087618R
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a degradable biomass fibre, comprising a wire core, the outer surface of the wire core is provided with antibacterial fibre, polylactic acid fibre and tencel fibre; the antibacterial fibre, one side of the polylactic acid fibre and tencel fibre far away from the wire core is provided with a high temperature resistant layer; the high temperature resistant layer is composed of polytetrafluoroethylene fibre and polyimide fibre; the outer surface of the high temperature resistant layer is connected with an elastic layer by adhesive; the elastic layer is composed of polyurethane fibre and polyurethane elastic fibre. The utility model is provided with antibacterial fibre; the bamboo fibre is easy to be decomposed by brown rot fungi and white rot fungi in the soil; by setting the polylactic acid fibre, the polylactic acid fibre is a synthetic fibre capable of completely biodegradable; it can be obtained from the grain, After the product is waste, it can be decomposed into carbon dioxide and water by microbial action in the soil or sea water; when burning, it will not emit poison gas, will not cause pollution, which is a sustainable development of ecological fibre.
DC A81 (Adhesives and binders - including chipboard.); F02 (Yarns - mechanical finishing of yarns or ropes; warping or beaming (D02, D07).); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).)

Therapeutic use of natural sludge mud of Lake Tambukan.
   USE - Medicine.

PN CN111561963-A; CN111561963-B
TI Method for evaluating coking properties and coking            thermal strength of coking coal, involves establishing            relationship model between coking properties of coking            coal and thermal strength of coke to determine weight            coefficients.
AU WANG X
   LIANG Y
   CHENG H
   HUANG S
   LI J
   GUI R
   SUN Z
   LI L
AE HEBEI IRON & STEEL CO LTD (HBIS-C)
GA 202082985W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves coking coal coking                properties to detect coking properties of a coking                coal by using a comprehensive detection device.                Thickness of a colloidal layer, expansion pressure                and volume expansion and contraction change curve                are detected by using the comprehensive detection                device. Representative characteristic parameters                are extracted from a characteristic curve. Thermal                strength of a coke is detected using a miniaturized                coke thermal strength detection device. The cooled                coke is processed into a sample with a particle                size. Carbon dioxide gas is switched to nitrogen                protection at same time. A coking coal relationship                model is constructed between coke properties and                coke thermal strength. A relationship model between                coking properties of coking coal and the thermal                strength of the coke are established to determine                weight coefficients.
   USE - Method for evaluating coking properties and                coking thermal strength of a coking coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method involves avoiding large number of                coking coal property testing tests, large-scale                coking tests and coke thermal strength testing                tests, and reducing manpower, material resources                for coking production, and production costs.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a method for evaluating coking properties and                coking thermal strength of coking coal. (Drawing                includes non-English language text).

PT P
PN CN111534837-A; CN111534837-B
TI Inert anode useful for preparing high temperature molten salt system, comprises titanium diboride substrate and oxide layer formed by e.g. in-situ reacting titanium diboride substrate surface and oxygen in electrolyte during electrolysis.
AU JIAO S
   DU Y
   WANG M
   TU J
   KOU M
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 202080690A
AB    NOVELTY - Inert anode is claimed. The anode comprises substrate and oxide layer, where the substrate material is titanium diboride, the oxide layer is formed by the in-situ reaction of the titanium diboride substrate surface and the oxygen in the electrolyte during high-temperature electrolysis, or is formed by calcination and pre-oxidation. The oxide layer prevents the chemical-electrochemical corrosion of the titanium diboride matrix material while maintaining excellent conductivity, the inert anode is suitable for the alkali/alkaline earth metal chloride molten salt system at 400-1000 degrees C and the calcium oxide-aluminum oxide-silicon dioxide-based molten oxide system at 1200-1600 degrees C, implementing at 2-3 V constant voltage electrolysis or 0.1-0.8 A cm-2 constant current electrolysis, and the corrosion rate is less than 1.5x 10-3g cm-2h-1.
   USE - The anode is useful for preparing high temperature molten salt system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The anode: has low chemical and electrochemical dissolution, strong oxidation resistance, excellent corrosion resistance and conductivity; avoid the consumption of carbon anodes and the emission of carbon dioxide gas; realizes continuous production; and saves energy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the high-temperature electrolysis process, the in-situ reaction process between the surface of the titanium diboride substrate and the oxygen in the electrolyte is from the inside to the outside: the reaction is as follows: reacting titanium diboride with oxygen to obtain Ti(BO3) and oxygen and then to obtain titanium dioxide, calcium oxide and calcium titanate. The oxide layer generated by the reaction has thickness of 1-250 um The step of calcination and pre-oxidation generation is by calcining at 800-1200 degrees C under air atmosphere for 0.5-2 hours, where the pre-oxidized oxide layer is titanium dioxide having thickness of 1-200 um. Preferred Components: The titanium diboride matrix material has purity of more than 99.5%. The alkali/alkaline earth metal chloride molten salt component includes lithium chloride, sodium chloride, potassium chloride, calcium chloride, magnesium chloride and 1-5 wt.% calcium oxide. The calcium oxide-aluminum oxide-silicon dioxide-based molten oxide system includes 5-15 wt.% iron(III) oxide, 5-15 wt.% iron(II) oxide, 5-15 wt.% titanium dioxide and/or 5-15 wt.% magnesium oxide. The mass percentage of calcium oxide-aluminum oxide-silicon dioxide in the calcium oxide-aluminum oxide-silicon dioxide-based-based molten oxide system is 85-95%.
DC M28 (Electrolytic and electrothermic production and refining of non-ferrous metals - excluding heat treatment (C25).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)
MC M28-B; M28-C01; X25-R02

PT P
PN CN111528229-A
TI Long-acting antibacterial agent comprises e.g. blending nano-silica dispersion and polyurethane aerogel dispersion, and reacting with nano-titanium dioxide dispersion, adding silane coupling agent, and reacting.
AU JIN C
   ZHANG Q
AE JIANGSU SIDIKE NEW MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020800915
AB    NOVELTY - Long-acting antibacterial agent comprises (i) preparing polyurethane aerogel, (ii) (a) preparing nano-silica dispersion, (b) preparing polyurethane aerogel dispersion, and (c) blending the nano-silica dispersion with the polyurethane aerogel dispersion for 6-24 hours, and drying under supercritical carbon dioxide conditions to obtain nano-silica polyurethane aerogel composite carrier, (iii) (1) adding nano-titanium dioxide into mixed solution of sodium hydroxide and 3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane to obtain nano-titanium dioxide dispersion, (2) dispersing the nano-silica polyurethane aerogel composite carrier into water containing dispersant to obtain nano-silica polyurethane aerogel composite carrier dispersion, and (3) adding the nano-titanium dioxide dispersion into the nano silica polyurethane aerogel composite carrier dispersion, and adding silane coupling agent, and reacting for 2-12 hours to obtain nano-titanium dioxide-aerogel carrier composite as final product.
   USE - The antibacterial agent is useful in preparing materials including coatings or films with antibacterial properties.
   ADVANTAGE - The antibacterial agent: has excellent antibacterial performance; utilizes polyurethane aerogel, which has high specific surface area and high porosity to stably adsorb nano-titania-nano-silica composite, which can prevent the agglomeration of nano-titania-nano-silica composite, and thus fully exert the antibacterial effect of nano titanium dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The polyurethane aerogel is prepared by (i) adding diphenylmethane diisocyanate and propylene glycol polyether into acetone, stirring and dissolving to obtain mixed liquid, (ii) adding dibutyltin dilaurate into the mixed solution, and (iii) allowing to stand to obtain gel, soaking in acetone, washing and then drying under supercritical carbon dioxide conditions. The nano-silica dispersion is prepared by adding ethylene bis stearamide into water, adding nano-silica powder, and stirring. The polyurethane aerogel dispersion is prepared by adding ethylene bis stearamide into water, adding the polyurethane aerogel powder, and stirring. In step (c), the blending reaction time of the nano-silica dispersion and the polyurethane aerogel dispersion is 12 hours; and the temperature of supercritical carbon dioxide drying is 55-110 degrees C, the drying time is 6-24 hours, and the drying pressure is 5-27 MPa. In step (3), the reaction time after adding the silane coupling agent is 6 hours. Preferred Components: The specific surface area of the polyurethane aerogel is 300-800 m2/g. In step (3), the addition amount of nano-titanium dioxide is 0.2-2.5 wt.%, preferably 0.8 wt.% based on nano-silica polyurethane aerogel composite carrier amount. In step (3), the silane coupling agent is vinyltriethoxysilane, vinyltrimethoxysilane and/or isobutyltriethoxysilane.

PN CN111215128-A
TI Catalyst comprises metal oxides, co-catalyst comprising manganese, sodium, potassium, silicon, zirconium, copper, aluminum and zinc, and active metal-supported molecular sieves.
AU ZHANG Y
   ZHANG S
   LIU Y
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 202050523B
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises metal oxides, co-catalyst and active metal-supported molecular sieves. The metal oxide includes oxides including iron, manganese, iron-nickel, iron-copper, iron-molybdenum, and cobalt-copper. The co-catalyst includes manganese, sodium, potassium, silicon, zirconium, copper, aluminum and/or zinc. The active metal includes copper, molybdenum, zirconium, zinc and/or gallium.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for regulating target product of carbon dioxide hydrogenation to gasoline (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has selectivity of gasoline is less than or equal to 80%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the catalyst, comprising (i) dissolving the surfactant and the precursor of the metal oxide in the solvent to obtain solution A, (ii) dissolving the sodium salt in the solvent to obtain solution B, (iii) mixing solution A and solution B to obtain solution C, crystallizing solution C under autogenous pressure, then washing with ionized water and absolute ethanol, and drying to obtain substance D, (iv) soaking substance D in the solution containing cocatalyst, evacuating and drying to obtain substance E, (v) loading the active metal with the molecular sieve by excessive impregnation, drying and calcining to obtain substance F, (vi) separately squeezing the substance E and substance F, selecting the particles, and mixing to obtain product catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass fraction of the metal oxide in the total amount of the catalyst is 10-80 wt.%. The mass fraction of the co-catalyst in the total amount of the catalyst is 0.1-20 wt.%, preferably 0.1-10 wt.%. The mass fraction of the active metal supported on the molecular sieve is 0.5-20 wt.%. The molecular sieve includes HZSM-5, ZSM-12, H- beta , SAPO-34 and/or MCM-41. The molecular sieve has silicon dioxide/aluminum oxide ratio of 15-300. In the step (i), the surfactant includes sodium lauryl sulfate, quaternary ammonium compound, PVP-K30, trisodium citrate and/or triton X-100. In the step (i), and (ii), the solvent includes deionized water, ethanol, ethylene glycol, acetone, isobutanol, ethylenediamine and/or 1-pyridine-2-ethylene glycol. The sodium salt includes sodium acetate, sodium carbonate, sodium carbonate and/or sodium nitrate. In the step (iv), the co-catalyst solution includes nitrate solution, acetate solution, and chloride solution. In the step (v), the molecular sieve has silicon dioxide/aluminum oxide ratio of 15-300, preferably 40-200. In the step (vi), the particle size is 10-100 meshes, preferably 10-50 meshes. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the temperature of the crystallization is 50-300 degrees C, more preferably 100-220 degrees C. The drying temperature is 50-200 degrees C, preferably 60-150 degrees C. In the step (v), the temperature of the calcination is 300-600 degrees C, 350-550 degrees C.

AU 
AE KLEES T (KLEE-Individual)
GA 202026602G
AB    NOVELTY - The electronic module has a module main portion that is installed in a housing, preferably made of polyvinylchloride. A cable is provided with four wires for connection to either a gas or a heating oil heater leads to the housing. A switch is provided to bypass the module in the event of a malfunction or control of the heating system on the housing. A colored LED is provided on the housing to indicate module operation. An external heat sensor is connected to a cable, for transmitting the outside temperature to the housing.
   USE - Outdoor temperature controlled electronic module for older heating systems to save energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The renewed pulse of the thermostat stops the power supply to the module, drops and the process starts again, as soon as the desired flow temperature is reached, which not only saves 25% and more of the fuel costs, but also brings about considerable advantages for the environment and for the reduced carbon-dioxide emissions. The electricity is saved for the burner and the pump, which can also be switched. The longer burner runtime is achieved, which is very important because the burner is not switched on as often, which also significantly reduces pollutant emissions.

PN CN110511460-A; CN110511460-B
TI High-temperature resistant silica aerogel thermal            insulation material comprises silica aerogel,            alkylphenol polyoxyethylene ether, organic solvent,            rubber composition, modified foaming agent, organic            peroxide and additives.
AU TANG X
AE TANG X (TANG-Individual)
   JIANGSU DAMAONIU NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A2757S
AB    NOVELTY - A high-temperature resistant silica aerogel                thermal insulation material comprises 20-60 pts.                wt. silica aerogel, 6-8 pts. wt. alkylphenol                polyoxyethylene ether, 8-24 pts. wt. organic                solvent, 150-200 pts. wt. rubber composition, 5-8                pts. wt. modified foaming agent, 4-8 pts. wt.                organic peroxide and 60-80 pts. wt.                additives.
   USE - High-temperature resistant silica aerogel                thermal insulation material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of high-temperature-resistant silica                aerogel thermal insulation material, which involves                mixing silica aerogel and alkylphenol                polyoxyethylene ether, stirring, adjusting the pH                to 5-6, increasing the temperature to 40-60 degrees                C, standing still for 25-30 hours, adding an                organic solvent, obtaining a composite gel,                uniformly mixing a rubber composition in a torque                rheometer, adding modified foaming agent, organic                peroxide and additives, uniformly kneading using a                kneader, obtaining kneaded rubber (A), further                kneading obtained component (A), extrusion-molding,                obtaining foamed rubber granules, adding obtained                foamed rubber particles to the composite gel,                stirring at room temperature for 5-6 hours, further                stirring at 70-85 degrees C for 7-9 hours and                drying in a fluidized bed for 2-4 hours using                supercritical carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The raw material of                  modified foaming agent is organic foaming agent                  and/or zinc-containing compound. The organic                  foaming agent is azodicarbonamide,                  4,4'-bis(hydrazinosulfonyl)diphenyl ether and/or                  N,N'-dinitrosopentamethylenetetramine. The                  zinc-containing compound is zinc glycine, zinc                  carbonate and/or zinc stearate. The organic                  peroxide is dicumyl peroxide and/or benzoyl                  peroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The rubber                  composition includes modified nitrile rubber and                  vinyl polymer. The rubber composition further                  comprises                  1,3-bis(3-hydroxypropyl)-1,1,3,3-tetramethyldisiloxane                  and/or                  1,3-bis(4-hydroxybutyl)-1,1,3,3-tetramethyldisiloxane.                  The modified nitrile rubber is carboxyl nitrile                  rubber, carboxy-terminated nitrile rubber and/or                  hydrogenated nitrile rubber. The mass ratio of                  carboxyl nitrile rubber with respect to                  1,3-bis(3-hydroxypropyl)-1,1,3,3-tetramethyldisiloxane                  is 1:2-4. The mass ratio of foamed rubber                  particles with respect to composite gel is                  4-10:1.

PN CN110510884-A
TI Preparing erbium-ion-doped fluorine tellurite microsphere laser e.g. used in integrated photonic application, involves mixing tellurium dioxide, barium fluoride and yttrium oxide, pouring on preheated copper plate, and annealing.
AU WANG P
   ZHAO H
   YI Y
AE UNIV HARBIN ENGINEERING (UHEG-C)
GA 2019A1477W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing erbium-ion-doped fluorine tellurite microsphere laser involves (i) taking 70 mol.% tellurium dioxide, 20 mol.% barium fluoride, 9.9 mol.% yttrium oxide and 0.1 mol.% erbium (III) oxide as glass raw material, putting the glass raw material in an agate mortar and completely stirring for 10 minutes, (ii) putting the mixture in a corundum crucible, without covering the crucible placing the mixture in a high-temperature furnace at 950 degrees C and heat-preserving in high-temperature furnace of glove box for 2 hours, (iii) pouring the glass solution on a preheated copper plate, carrying out annealing treatment and cooling to room temperature after 3 hours, (iv) heating glass fiber using a carbon dioxide laser to prepare a microspheres, where the diameter of the microspheres is 80 mu m, and (v) pumping laser at a 980 and outputting 1.56-micron laser in microspheres by adopting a tapering fiber coupling method.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing erbium-ion-doped fluorine tellurite microsphere laser used in integrated photonic, low-threshold laser, high-sensitivity biosensing, and cavity photodynamic applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The laser has low threshold, high Q value and simple structure.

PN CN110416555-A
TI Method for preparing platinum-nickel alloy catalyst for use in cathode catalyst that is utilized in membrane electrode of proton exchange membrane fuel cell, involves dissolving platinum salt and nickel salt in first solvent and stirring.
AU ZHANG N
   WANG G
   MA X
   ZHANG D
   SHEN S
   YU J
   SUN F
   CHEN H
AE BEIJING AEROSPACE PETROCHEMICAL TECH EQU (BJAP-C)
   BEIJING AEROSPACE INNOVATION PATENT INVE (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201994896T
AB    NOVELTY - A platinum-nickel alloy catalyst preparing method involves dissolving a platinum salt and a nickel salt in a first solvent, and magnetically stirring to obtain a mixed solution of a platinum salt and a nickel salt, where the first solvent is an amide solvent. The mixed solution is transferred into a reaction axe and carbon dioxide gas is bubbled into the reaction vessel, heated to 160-210 degrees C for 10-60 minutes, and cooled the reaction solution to obtain platinum-nickel alloy nano-crystalline solution. The carbon carrier is dispersed in the second solvent to obtain a carbon carrier suspension. The platinum-nickel alloy nano-crystalline solution is dispersed in the carbon carrier suspension and stirred for 3 hours, filtered, washed, and dried to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing platinum-nickel alloy catalyst for use in cathode catalyst that is utilized in membrane electrode of proton exchange membrane fuel cell (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing platinum-nickel alloy catalyst with enhanced catalytic activity, in cost effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a platinum-nickel alloy catalyst prepared by the method.

GA 201985274G

GA 202059357S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves injecting mobilizing fluid comprising vapor solvent into a voidage formed in a reservoir (44) during the thermal recovery process through a first wellbore. The fluid comprising mobilized bitumen is produced from the reservoir through a second wellbore, the second wellbore is different than the first wellbore. The non-condensable gas is injected into the reservoir while producing fluid. The vapor solvent is provided in the mobilizing fluid comprises a C2-C7 hydrocarbon, a C2-C4 natural gas liquid, a natural gas condensate, a naphtha product or an ether. The non-condensable gas comprises at least one of methane, carbon dioxide, flue gas and gas produced during the thermal recovery process.
   USE - Method for performing solvent-dominated recovery process in underground reservoir.
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes improved efficiency during solvent-dominated recovery processes in multiple underground reservoirs that have previously undergone a thermal recovery process. The hot water is co-injected or alternately injected with vapor solvent to help redistribute the energy within the reservoir and assist solvent delivery and condensation.

PN US2019263989-A1; WO2019168940-A2; WO2019168940-A3; EP3759160-A2; EP3759160-A4; US11306192-B2
TI Foam used as floral foams comprises a polylactide polymer in the form of star polymer in which the foam is an open cell foam having a predetermined density.
AU DIRLAM P T
   GOLDFELD D J
   HILLMYER M A
AE UNIV MINNESOTA (MINU-C)
   UNIV MINNESOTA (MINU-C)
   UNIV MINNESOTA (MINU-C)
GA 201974350H
AB    NOVELTY - A foam comprises a polylactide polymer in the form of star polymer having greater than or equal to 3 arms, where the foam is an open cell foam having a density of not greater than 0.1 g/cm3.
   USE - A foam used as floral foams.
   ADVANTAGE - Inclusion of polysorbate surfactant leads to low density foams that are coherent and homogeneous, and has consistent mechanical properties, including compressive strength. The foam has increased water absorbing capability. Foams are open-cell foams having low density and persistent wettability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) foam comprising (a) polylactide polymer in the form of star polymer having greater than or equal to 3 arms, (b) not greater than 5 wt.% polysorbate surfactant, and (c) not greater than 7.5 wt.% polyalkylene oxide block copolymer, where the foam is an open cell foam has a density of not greater than 0.06 g/cm3; and
   (2) preparing a foam which involves (a) providing a foamable composition comprising a polylactide polymer in the form of star polymer having greater than or equal to 3 arms; and (b) introducing carbon dioxide into the foamable composition to create an open cell foam having a density of not greater than 0.1 g/cm3.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Parameters: The foam has a density of not greater than 0.06 g/cm3, preferably not greater than 0.04 g/cm3. The foam has a water uptake relative to void volume of greater than or equal to 25%, preferably greater than or equal to 50%. The flowers do not wilt for greater than or equal to 48 hours after insertion when the stems of cut flowers are inserted into the foam. Preferred Components: Foam further comprises a polysorbate surfactant selected from polysorbate 20, polysorbate 40, polysorbate 60, and/or polysorbate 80. Amount of polysorbate surfactant is not greater than 5 wt.%. The foam further comprises a polyalkylene oxide block copolymer surfactant. The polyalkylene oxide block copolymer surfactant comprises a polyethylene oxide-polypropylene oxide-polyethylene oxide triblock copolymer surfactant. The polyalkylene oxide block copolymer surfactant is selected from Poloxamer 188, Poloxamer 338, and/or Poloxamer 403. The amount of polyalkylene oxide block copolymer surfactant is greater than or equal to 7.5 wt.%. The star polymer has greater than or equal to 4 arms. The foamable composition further comprises polysorbate surfactant, polyalkylene oxide block copolymer surfactant, agent selected from ethyl acetate, isopropanol, ethyl lactate, and/or diethyl ether, plasticizer, and secondary foaming agent. Preferred Process: The foam can be rehydrated with addition of 10 ml of water to increase flower longevity. The flowers do not wilt for greater than or equal to 132 hours with daily addition of 10 ml water when stems of cut flowers are inserted into the foam.

PT P
PN CN110137470-A
TI Preparing fluorine-based ionic liquid surface-modified lithium ion battery ternary cathode material comprises adding ternary anode electrode material into fluorine-based ionic liquid, stirring, filtering, washing, drying, and calcining.
AU XIONG X
   WANG G
   LUO Y
   FAN M
   LIN Z
   YANG C
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 201972541P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fluorine-based ionic liquid surface-modified lithium ion battery ternary cathode material comprises (i) adding ternary anode electrode material into the fluorine-based ionic liquid, where the mass ratio of the ternary anode material to fluorine-based ionic liquid is 1:2-1:10, the fluorine-based ionic liquid comprises alkyl imidazole tetrafluoroborate, 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate, N-alkylpyridine tetrafluoroborate, ammonium tetrafluoroborate, 1-ethylethyl ether-3-alkylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate, tributylphosphine tetrafluoroborate, N-alkyl-N-methylpiperidine tetrafluoroborate and/or 1-benzyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate, and (ii) sequentially adding ternary anode material into fluorine-based ionic liquid, stirring, filtering, washing, drying, and calcining to obtain fluorine-based ionic liquid surface modified lithium ion battery ternary anode material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing fluorine-based ionic liquid surface-modified lithium ion battery ternary cathode material.
   ADVANTAGE - The coating layer can effectively reduce side reactions between interfaces, can solve the problem that the ternary anode material is sensitive to carbon dioxide/water in the air, and improves cycle life and rate performance of the material. The method is simple, green and environmentally friendly, can significantly improve the electrochemical performance of the material, and has broad application prospects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the stirring temperature is 20-80 degrees C. In the step (ii), the stirring speed is 100-1000 revolutions/minute. In the step (ii), the stirring time is 0.5-24 hours. In the step (ii), the drying temperature is 60-90 degrees C. In the step (ii), the drying time is 8-14 hours. In the step (ii), the calcination temperature is 120-300 degrees C. In the step (ii), the calcination time is 2-20 hours. Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the calcination atmosphere is air and/or oxygen. The ternary anode material comprises LiNi1-x-yCoxMyO2 (I).M = manganese, aluminum and iron;x = 0-0.5; andy = 0-0.5.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PN CN110117005-A
TI Method for preparing expanded filler from pressurized expansion technique, involves controlling temperature and pressure to diffuse gas between layers of filler, and releasing pressure and temperature rise to effect gas expansion of filler.
AU LI L
   ZHAO S
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 201971837H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves inserting a layered filler with a non-oxidizing protonic acid as an intercalating agent Layer treatment to obtain intercalation filler. The protonic acid between intercalated filler layers are reacted to produce carbon dioxide using inorganic carbonate as a gas source. The temperature and pressure of the system are controlled to diffuse the gas between the layers of the filler, and the pressure and/or temperature rise is released to effect gas expansion of the filler to obtain expanded filler. One of concentrated sulfuric acid, fuming sulfuric acid, concentrated phosphoric acid, methanesulfonic acid, ethylsulfonic acid, propylsulfonic acid, benzenesulfonic acid, dichloroacetic acid, alkylsulfonic acid, and derivatives are provided. The layered filler is one of clay, graphite, boron nitride, black phosphorus, metal compound, and derivatives.
   USE - Method for preparing expanded filler from pressurized expansion technique.
   ADVANTAGE - The inorganic carbonate is reacted with the protonic acid to generate a gas, and the effective expansion of the intercalation filler by the gas is realized, by the regulation of temperature and pressure to obtain an expanded filler. The yield of the two-dimensional nanofiller prepared by the liquid phase stripping method is improved, using the expanded filler as a raw material.

TI Production of fuel and other valuable low-molecular weight hydrocarbon material used for e.g. aircraft, involves pre-treating plastic waste product, contacting with bed of particles of porous inorganic material, decomposing, and separating.
AU CHATTERJEE A
   MANE S J
   MORE A B
   BIRADAR K K
   CHOUGALE M P
   TATIKONDA M
   CHATTERJEE L
AE CHATTERJEE A (CHAT-Individual)
   MANE S J (MANE-Individual)
   MORE A B (MORE-Individual)
   BIRADAR K K (BIRA-Individual)
   CHOUGALE M P (CHOU-Individual)
   TATIKONDA M (TATI-Individual)
   CHATTERJEE L (CHAT-Individual)
GA 2019512210
AB    NOVELTY - Production of fuel and other valuable low-molecular weight hydrocarbon material involves subjecting plastic waste product to thermal pre-treatment to produce liquid plastic mass in inert gas atmosphere at 110-310 degrees C, simultaneously feeding the liquid plastic mass to a reaction apparatus, contacting the plastic mass with a bed of particles of porous inorganic material at 300-600 degrees C, subjecting to thermal-catalytic decomposition at 300-600 degrees C to obtain mixture containing hydrocarbons in vapor phase, and separating hydrocarbons from the vapor phase in reaction medium to produce liquid mixture of hydrocarbons.
   USE - Production of fuel and other valuable low-molecular weight hydrocarbon material used for automotive, aircraft, construction, medical and engineering applications. Can also be used for manufacture of container, packaging material, electronic component and textiles.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient, environmentally-friendly and economical production of fuel and other valuable low-molecular weight hydrocarbon material with high yield and selectivity by eliminating plastic waste via decomposition or breaking down, and reducing carbon footprint of plastic product by minimizing emission of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide.

PT P
PN US2019148770-A1; US11309581-B2
TI Solid garnet electrolyte membrane for e.g., composite electrolyte structure, comprises bulk composition comprising lithium garnet and surface composition comprising protonated garnet on a portion of exterior surface of lithium garnet.
AU BADDING M E
   LIU X
   LU Y A
AE CORNING INC (CORG-C)
   CORNING INC (CORG-C)
GA 201942148B
AB    NOVELTY - A solid garnet electrolyte membrane comprises bulk composition comprising lithium garnet selected from Li7La3Zr2O12 (LLZO) and doped LLZO compositions; and surface composition comprising protonated garnet on a portion of exterior surface of lithium garnet, where surface protonated portion of garnet is compositionally stable in air and insensitive to carbon dioxide.
   USE - A solid garnet electrolyte membrane for composite electrolyte structure and energy storage device (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The super-protonated surface is less prone to further reaction with ambient moisture, which renders the surface stable against formation of a surface contamination layer for a period of time exceeding for hours, permitting a substantial working time under ambient conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) composite electrolyte structure comprising protected anode structure which has 1st layer comprising ceramic electrolyte comprising the solid garnet electrolyte membrane and 2nd layer comprising a source of lithium metal, where 1st layer and 2nd layer are in physical contact, and a cathode;
   (2) energy storage device comprising cathode, anode, and the solid garnet electrolyte membrane;
   (3) sintered garnet electrolyte membrane comprising protonated LLZO garnet composition or protonated doped LLZO garnet composition; and
   (4) energy storage device comprising cathode, anode, and the sintered garnet electrolyte membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Lithium garnet is Li7La3Zr2O12, and protonated garnet is Li(7-x)HxLa3Zr2O12. Doped LLZO composition comprises Li7La3Zr2O12 doped with gallium (Ga) and/or niobium (Nb). Lithium garnet is Li(7-z)La(3-y)GayZr(2-z)NbzO12, and protonated garnet is Li(7-z-x)HxLa(3-y)GayZr(2-z)NbzO12. Lithium garnet is Li6.75La2.9Ga0.1Nb0.25Zr1.75O12, and protonated garnet is Li(6.75-s)HsLa2.9Ga0.1Nb0.25Zr1.75O12. Protonated garnet on a portion of exterior surface of lithium garnet comprises a layer having a thickness of 0.1-100 nm. Protonated garnet on a portion of exterior surface of lithium garnet comprises the entire exterior surface of the lithium garnet. Surface composition has a lithium concentration of 0.1-46 mol.%. Surface composition is substantially free of an electrically insulating Li surface species. Surface composition comprises a surface free of lithium hydroxide (LiOH) and/or lithium carbonate (Li2CO3). Composite electrolyte structure further comprises aqueous catholyte positioned between and in contact with protected anode structure and cathode. Cathode is a lithium cobalt oxide compound. Protonated doped LLZO garnet composition comprises Ga and/or Nb.x=0.1-7;y, z=0.1-0.3; and=0.1-6.75.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

   ADVANTAGE - The product has long service life and greatly                reduces the corrosion level of Chemical Industry                equipment for coal Chemical Industry production                field.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of catalytic deacidifying                agent comprising dissolving divalent metal salt and                trivalent metal salt in water, obtaining solution                A, dissolving alkali source in water, obtaining                solution B, mixing with solution A, adjusting pH                value to 6.5-11, stirring, carrying out                hydrothermal reaction at 60-150 degrees C for 1-36                hours, filtering, washing, drying, mixing with                binder, kneading, extruding, drying, and calcining;                and
   (2) application of catalytic deacidifying                agent comprising taking aqueous solution containing                organic acid, and converting into carbon dioxide                and ketones in fixed bed reactor equipped with                catalytic deacidifying agent under hydrogen or                nitrogen atmosphere at 300-600 degrees C under                0.5-10 MPa at space velocity of 0.1-10/hour.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The divalent and                  trivalent metal salt is nitrate or chloride. The                  alkali source is sodium hydroxide, sodium                  carbonate, potassium hydroxide, potassium                  carbonate or urea. Preferred Method: The                  preparation of catalytic deacidifying agent                  comprises dissolving divalent metal salt and                  trivalent metal salt in water, obtaining solution                  A, dissolving alkali source in water, obtaining                  solution B, mixing with solution A, adjusting pH                  value to 6.5-9, stirring, carrying out                  hydrothermal reaction at 80-120 degrees C for                  3-12 hours, filtering, washing, drying, mixing                  with binder, kneading, adding 1-5 wt.% extrusion                  aid comprising Sesbania powder or tung oil,                  extruding, drying, and calcining. The application                  of catalytic deacidifying agent comprises taking                  aqueous solution containing organic acid, and                  converting into carbon dioxide and ketones in                  fixed bed reactor equipped with catalytic                  deacidifying agent under hydrogen or nitrogen                  atmosphere at 350-500 degrees C under 0.5-5 MPa                  at space velocity of 0.1-5/hour.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic acid                  contained in aqueous solution is 1-4C organic                  carboxylic acid with total content of not greater                  than 15000 ppmw.

PN CN109679616-A; CN109679616-B
TI New foam drainage agent i.e. ethanamine-propan-1-ol compound useful in acidic high temperature and high salt super deep gas well drainage gas production.
AU SHEN Z
   HE X
   QIU Y
   WU C
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
GA 201941107D
AB    NOVELTY - Foam drainage agent i.e. ethanamine-propan-1-ol compound (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful in acidic high temperature and high salt super deep gas well drainage gas production (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) solves the problem that the existing foam drainage agent has poor high temperature and high salt performance in an acidic environment containing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Foam drainage agent i.e. ethanamine-propan-1-ol compound of formula (I) is new.
   R1 = 4-32C optionally substituted hydrocarbon;
   R2-R4 = H, 1-5C optionally substituted hydrocarbyl carboxylate, 1-5C hydrocarbyl sulfonate, 1-5C hydrocarbyl phosphate or 1-5C hydrocarbyl sulfate, preferably CH2C(=O)OM, (CH2)3SO3M or CH2(CHOH)CH2SO3M;
   m = number of -N(A)CH2CH2- fragments (preferably 1-10);
   A = propan-1-ol moiety of formula (II);
   p, r and t = 0-30;
   q, s, and u = 0-30;
   M = H, alkali metal or NR5(R6(R7)(R8);
   R5-R8 = H, (CH2)OH or (CH2)bCH3;
   a = 2-4; and
   b = 0-5.
   Provided that: R2-R4 is not simultaneously H, and p+r+t and q+s+u are not zero at the same time. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) reacting substituted carbonyl compounds of formula (R0(C=O)ORt) (V) and substituted amine compounds of formula (H(NHCH2CH2)mNH2) (VI) and catalyst in a molar ratio of 1:1-2:0-0.5 at 50-200 degrees C for 3-15 hours with stirring, evaporating alcohol or water formed by the reaction under normal pressure or reduced pressure to obtain amide compounds of formula (R0 CO(NHCH2CH2)mNH2) (VII), where catalyst is alkali metal hydroxides, alkali metal alkoxide or alkali metal carbonates, (ii) either reducing amide group of (VII) by catalytic hydrogenation method and carrying out heterogeneous catalytic reaction at high temperature under high pressure to form corresponding amine, or reducing (VII) with metal hydride (H-Y1+) in aprotic solvent to obtain corresponding amine of formula (R0CH2(NHCH2CH2)mNH2) (VIII), where Y1+ is metal compound, metal alkyl compound or metal amino compound, (iii) reacting (VIII) with propylene oxide and ethylene oxide in presence of a basic catalyst to obtain long chain polyamine polyether intermediate of formula (R0CH2(N((CHCH3CH2O)t(CH2CH2O)uH)(CH2CH2))mN((CHCH3CH2O)p(CH2CH2O)qH)(CHCH3CH2O)r(CH2CH2O)s)H) (IX), and (iv) mixing long-chain polyamine polyether intermediate (IX) with ionizing agent and alkali in a molar ratio of 1:1-5:1-10 in a solvent, reacting at a reaction temperature of 50-120 degrees C for 3-20 hours to form polyamine polyether carboxylate or polyamine polyether sulfonate (I), where the ionizing agent is XR15Y1 or XR15aY1a, the alkali is alkali metal hydroxides or alkali metal alkoxide. Preferred Components: In step (i), the molar ratio between (V), (VI) and catalyst is 1:1-1.3:0-0.1, the catalyst is sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium carbonate and/or potassium carbonate. In step (ii), the metal hydride is lithium aluminum hydride, lithium tri-ethoxyaluminum hydride and/or sodium borohydride, and the aprotic solvent is diethyl ether, tetrahydrofuran and/or dioxane. In step (iv), the molar ratio between (IX), ionization reagent and alkali is 1:1-2:1-4, the solvent is 3-8C ketone and/or 6-9C aromatic hydrocarbon.R0 = 3-31C optionally substituted hydrocarbon group;Rt = H or 1-8C alkyl;Y1 and Y1a = SO3M1 or (C=O)OM2;M1 and M2 = alkali metals; andX = chloro, bromo or iodo.

General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Modular respirator for e.g. recreational            activities, has mask assembly provided with mask for            covering oral and nasal passage of user, where filter            unit, mask and exhaust unit jointly form tightly sealed            passage for passing fluid.
AU FU X
AE AIMWELL HOLDING PTY LTD (AIMW-Non-standard)
   AIMWELL HOLDING PTY LTD (AIMW-Non-standard)
   AIMWELL HOLDING PTY LTD (AIMW-Non-standard)
   AIMWELL HOLDING PTY LTD (AIMW-Non-standard)
GA 2019364717
AB    NOVELTY - The respirator (10) has a mask assembly                provided with a mask (40) for covering an oral and                nasal passage of a user. A mask inlet (50) is                formed at end of the mask. A mask outlet (52) is                formed at an opposite end of the mask. The mask                inlet is releasably fastened to a filter outlet.                The mask outlet is releasably fastened to an                exhaust inlet such that the filter unit is located                around an anterior triangle on a side of a neck and                the exhaust unit is located around an anterior                triangle on an opposite side of the neck. A filter                unit, the mask and the exhaust unit jointly form a                tightly sealed passage for passing fluid.
   USE - Modular respirator for recreational activities                or as a sports training activities.
   ADVANTAGE - The respirator maintains high protection                efficiency and reduces carbon-dioxide and moisture                in the mask. The respirator helps asthma patients                to correct breathing habit and breathe less air,                and eventually reduce the attacks and improve                wellbeing.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a                modular respirator.

   Blower unit (22)

PT P
PN WO2019044438-A1; JP2019044075-A; JP6589951-B2
TI Liquid perbasic metal complex composition used in e.g. electric wire, contains liquid perbasic metal complex, hydrocarbon-based solvent at specified boiling point, monohydric alcohol and surfactant with preset hydrophile-lipophile balance.
AU OKITA H
   KONDO M
AE SAKAI CHEM IND CO LTD (SAKI-C)
   SAKAI KAGAKU KOGYO KK (SAKI-C)
GA 201922978H
AB    NOVELTY - A liquid perbasic metal complex composition comprises a carboxylate/carbonate complex structure and/or phenate/carbonate complex structure of a group 2 metal atom of the periodic table and the composition comprises liquid perbasic metal complex, hydrogen-based solvent at a boiling point of 150 degrees C or more, monohydric alcohol with 8-20 carbon atoms and surfactant with hydrophile-lipophile balance (HLB) of 10 or more.
   USE - Metal complex composition is used in PVC resin. Uses include but are not limited to motor vehicle component, building materials, household appliance components, electric wire, pipe and resin window frame.
   ADVANTAGE - The metal complex composition suppresses the generation of the deposit of carbonate, suitable as a heat resistant imparting agent for PVC and has high compatibility with resin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the manufacture of liquid perbasic metal complex which involves mixing of hydrocarbon-based solvent, monohydric alcohol, a carboxy group-containing compound and/or a phenol type compound and surfactant to produce carboxylate and/or phenate and adding carbon dioxide to the produced carboxylate and/or phenate to produce carboxylate/carbonate complex structure and/or phenate/carbonate complex structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The group 2 metal atom is selected from at least one of calcium, barium, magnesium and strontium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The carboxylate is formed from 8-30C monohydric carboxylic acid. The phenate is formed from 9-30C alkylphenol and decaglycerol fatty acid ester and/or sorbitan fatty acid ester.
DC A95 (Transport - including vehicle parts, tyres and armaments.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A84 (Household and office fittings - including carpets and carbon paper.); A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.);

   USE - The gas conditioning sterilization method is useful for pre-conditioning squid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids the use of chemical additive, is environmentally-friendly and healthy, adopts multi-stage heating, accurately calculates the F value of the sterilizing area in each stage, kills the microbial batch sub-area of the food, shortens the temperature difference between the surface of the food and center, improves the heat damage and odor caused by high temperature, high pressure and long time processing, greatly shortens the natural cooling or water cooling time by adopting two-stage rapid cooling after the high-temperature sterilization, and reduces the damage to the texture and nutritional properties of the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The nitrogen gas filling amount is 200-300 cm3/kg squid. The carbon dioxide gas inflating amount is 500-600 cm3/kg squid. Preferred Method: The multi-stage temperature sterilization is carried out at 5-60 degrees C for 5 minutes, where it takes 3 minutes to reach 60-80 degrees C, then it takes 12 minutes to reach 80-105 degrees C, then it takes 5 minutes to increase to 105-120 degrees C, then it takes 3 minutes to reach 120-130 degrees C, then finally it takes 2 minutes to drop the temperature to 130-50 degrees C and cooled to 50-25 degrees C in 3 minutes.

PN CN108948794-A
TI Preparing nano calcium carbonate useful for polyvinyl chloride plastisols, comprises aging limestone, calcining calcium oxide, heating water, sieving, maintaining, filling, carbonizing, controlling, drying, dehydrating and crushing.
AU ZHANG Y
   AO Z
   ZHAO X
   WANG S
AE JIANGXI HUAMING NANO CALCIUM CARBONATE (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A0507B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano calcium carbonate comprises (1) aging limestone with a content of less than or equal to 98%, calcining to calcium oxide at 850-1100 degrees C, heating water, sieving and removing impurity, maintaining for 1-6 hours, cooling to obtain lime emulsion with concentration of 1.20-1.85 mol/l, (2) adding nucleus forming agent into the lime emulsion, adding it into carbonization tower or a carbonization kettle, filling the carbon dioxide with a certain concentration to the bubbling carbonization, carbonizing until the pH value is greater than or equal to 7 as a carbonization end point to obtain calcium carbonate pulp, (3) adding the calcium carbonate pulp into the activating reaction tank, adding compound surface treatment agent to make it fully contact, controlling activation reaction temperature is 55-90 degrees C which is activated calcium carbonate slurry and (4) drying the nano calcium carbonate adn activated calcium carbonate slurry, dehydrating, drying and crushing to obtain the nano calcium carbonate product.
   USE - The carbonate is useful for polyvinyl chloride plastisols.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves polyvinyl chloride plastisols of thixotropy, yield value and storage stability. The method is simple, easy operate and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nucleating agent comprises 5-30 wt.% white sugar, 5-30 wt.% 5-30 wt.% soluble carbonate and 5-80 wt.% low-molecule organic compound. The soluble carbonate is sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate and ammonium bicarbonate. The low-molecule organic compound is glycol, propylene glycol, glycerol, amino ethanol and sorbitol. The nucleating agent is added an amount of calcium hydroxide of 5.0-10.0%. The compound surface treatment agent is stearate: castor oil phosphate: emulsifier and water in mass ratio of 40-55:40-60:6-10:100-200. The stearate is calcium stearate, magnesium stearate, zinc stearate, magnesium stearate, aluminum stearate, barium stearate and barium stearate. The compound surface treatment agent is prepared from the following mass ratio component methyl methacrylate: ethyl acrylate: emulsifier: benzoyl peroxide and water 35-40:45-50:4-5:1.5-1.8:150-250. The emulsifier is OP-10, TX-10, sorbitan fatty acid ester, sodium lauryl sulfate, and sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate. The nano-calcium carbonate is controlled to have a BET specific surface area of from 28-40 m2/g.

A power switch of an ice-releasing                device is opened to release ice such that the dry                ice covers the battery when the battery reaches a                bottom part of an ice release port. PN CN108524400-A
TI Mild and soothing makeup remover involves preparing concentrated liquid from seaweed, witch hazel and Aloe, and uniformly mixing concentrated liquid, moisturizer, lecithin, citric acid, sodium citrate, and polyaminopropyl biguanide.
AU WAN J
AE HEFEI FENGRUILONG BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 201876557B
AB    NOVELTY - Mild and soothing makeup remover comprises taking seaweed, witch hazel and willow herbs at weight ratio of 5:(2-4):1; adding ethanol to perform ultrasonic vibration; adding complex enzyme; incubating for 8-24 hours under normal pressure at 35-55 degrees C under pH of 3-7; extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide extractor to obtain extract; placing extract and Aloe in crusher; crushing to obtain crushed material; centrifuging crushed material; collecting supernatant; concentrating supernatant to 50-55 vol.% to obtain concentrated liquid; taking 5-15 pts. wt. nonionic surfactant; adding 40-70 pts. wt. deionized water; heating at 70-80 degrees C while stirring for 30 minutes; cooling at 40-50 degrees C; adding 20-30 pts. wt. concentrated liquid, 8-18 pts. wt. moisturizer, 0.5-1 pts. wt. lecithin, 0.01-0.05 pts. wt. citric acid, 0.02-0.10 pts. wt. sodium citrate, and 0.15-0.25 pts. wt. polyaminopropyl biguanide; and uniformly stirring to become uniform and transparent.
   USE - Mild and soothing makeup remover.
   ADVANTAGE - The mild and soothing makeup remover has anti-inflammatory and calming effect, regulates metabolism, and skin soothing property.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Component: The complex enzyme comprises 6-10 pts. wt. cellulase, 3-4 pts. wt. pectinase, 0.2-0.5 pts. wt. protease, and 0.2-0.5 pts. wt. amylase. It is added at 10-15 wt.% of seaweed.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The ethanol is added at 2-4 times total amount of seaweed, witch hazel and willow herbs. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is performed under 10-15 MPa at 35-45 degrees C. Preferred Component: The weight ratio of extract to Aloe is 5:(2-4). The nonionic surfactant is polyethylene glycol 6 (PEG-6) caprylic/capric glyceride, poloxamer 184 (nonionic triblock copolymer), polyethylene glycol/polypropylene glycol-17/6 copolymer (PEG/PPG-17/6 copolymer), or xylitol glucoside. The moisturizer comprises serine, dipropylene glycol and Ophiopogon japonicus extract at weight ratio of 4:(1-3):(1-3).
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory; Tranquilizer.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); B04 (Natural products and polymers.    USE - Fermentation agent for preparing feed from food waste used in preparing aquatic animal feed or aquatic animal feed additive (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fermentation agent enables to realize harmlessness of production of feed product, ensure safety of food waste used as feed product and meet market feed product quality requirements.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing preparing feed from food waste, which involves:
   (A) mixing food waste with calcium oxide and alga according to weight ratio of (100-200):1:(1-5);
   (B) mixing uniformly;
   (C) introducing carbon dioxide;
   (D) processing crushed product by first sterilizing process;
   (E) adding fermenting agent to ferment, where, amount of Trichoderma koningii is 1-2 g, adding amount of Bacillus subtilis is 5-8 g, adding amount of Candida utilis is 0.5-1 g, Spirulina is 5-10 g;
   (F) mixing product obtained by fermentation with nutrient substance uniformly;
   (G) performing second sterilization treatment;
   (H) granulating; and
   (I) drying to obtain desired feed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Compositions: Fermentation agent further comprises nitrogen source, where nitrogen source is ammonium nitrate, and weight ratio of nitrogen source and food waste is 1:(100-500). The nutrient substance raw material comprises 15-30 pts. wt. wheat bran, 5-10 pts. wt. Chlorella powder, 1-2 pts. wt. methionine, 0.2-0.8 pts. wt. folic acid, 1-3 pts. wt. sodium alginate, 0.5-1 pts. wt. biotin and 3-6 pts. wt. lily powder. The nutrient substance raw material further comprises 1-2 pts. wt. alga, 2-4 pts. wt. green algae powder and 5-8 pts. wt. Tricholoma matsutake mushroom. Preferred Process: The nutrient substance is prepared by mixing raw materials after grinding uniformly and adding nutrient food waste to material components in weight ratio of (3-10):1. Preferred Conditions: The first sterilization process includes first microwave sterilizing process, second microwave sterilizing and third microwave sterilizing, specifically condition for first microwave sterilizing process includes crushing power of 750-800 megahertz, temperature is 90-105 degrees C, time is 90-120 s, when power is 450-530 megahertz, temperature is 83-92 degrees C, time is 60-130 s, after power is 550-650 megahertz, temperature is 70-85 degrees C and time is 70-100 s under condition of performing third microwave sterilizing treatment. The second sterilizing process includes first high pressure sterilization treatment and second high pressure sterilizing, specifically condition includes pressure of 180-230 megaPascals with nutrient substance after uniformly mixing product at pressure of 250-300 megaPascals, where temperature is 93-98 degrees C, time is 25-35 minutes, carrying out first processing after high pressure sterilizing, where temperature is 103-110 degrees C and time is 15-28 minutes. The fermentation temperature is 25-37 degrees C, pH value is 5.5-6.8 and time is 50-55 hours. The conditions of carbon dioxide specifically includes quantity of carbon dioxide of 50-200 ml/hour and time of 15-30 minutes.

   USE - High-performance fiber composite structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-performance fiber composite structure has excellent creep resistance and maintains original mechanical property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing high-performance fiber composite structure, which involves:
   (A) mixing, blending and extruding raw materials of component I in a screw extruder, cooling it in warm water, placing in a reactor, heating, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide gas into the reactor and placing for 1 hour to obtain a preliminary processed shaped component I;
   (B) melting and mixing preliminary processed shaped component I through a screw extruder, metering it by a metering pump, and distributed to the discharge assembly;
   (C) mixing, blending, melting and mixing raw materials of component II through a screw extruder, metering it by a metering pump, and distributing it to a discharge assembly; and
   (D) extruding obtained mixtures through a discharge assembly and cooling it to obtain a frozen yarn, which is then dried, extracted and subjected to hot drawing to obtain final product.

Preferred Components: The carbide/inorganic mixture is a mixture of two or more of graphene, silicon carbide, boron carbide, zirconium carbide, aluminum oxide, zirconium oxide, wollastonite, silicon dioxide, and glassy carbon. Preferred Method: In the step (c) sugar temperature is 60-70 degrees C. In the step (e) dissolving citric acid and slowly adding to the syrup to prevent local high acidity.
