PT P
PN CN108070726-A
TI Extracting lithium salt from lithium mica has excellent performance, involves pre-treating, roasting, mechanically activating, leaching, separating and extracting lithium, where calcium oxide and calcium acetate are calcinated.
AU TAN Y
   TAN Z
AE DAYU XURI MINING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DAYU-Non-standard)
GA 2018423088
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting lithium salt from lithium mica involves pre-treating, roasting, mechanically activating, leaching, separating and extracting lithium. Calcium oxide and calcium acetate are calcinated at 1500-2000 degrees C by plasma generator and defluorinated. Calcium oxide and calcium acetate are mixed in ratio 1:0.5. The pretreated mixture is ball milled for 3 hours. The roasting additive comprises sodium hydroxide, sodium sulfate and calcium compounds. The calcinated baking powder, dilute sulphuric acid and ammonium sulfate solution are mixed in mass ratio of solid to liquid. Water is added to the mixture, heated at 120 degrees C and pressurized for 60-90 minutes. The residual fluorine in powder is further removed during reaction to obtain solid-liquid mixed solution containing sulfate. The solution is filtered to remove slag and obtain filtrate. Carbon dioxide gas is added to solution to add carbonate or sulfate to obtain lithium carbonate salt or lithium sulfate salt.
   USE - Extracting lithium salt from lithium mica.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to extract lithium salt from lithium mica, has excellent performance, novel design, and is very good innovative project. The crystal grain size is reduced, which improves extraction and utilization of raw materials. The addition of buffer reduces energy consumption, shorten reaction time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The filter residue uses concentrated sulphuric acid solution or diluted sulphuric acid and ammonium sulfate solution with concentration of 80-95 wt.% and liquid to solid ratio of 2:1. The solution is heated at 150 degrees C, stirred for 2-4 hours, filtered, washed and residue is removed. The lithium mica reacts with sulfuric acid solution under pressure after calcining and defluorination. The control acid leaching temperature is 90 degrees C to obtain solid-liquid mixture. The mixture is cooled and freeze, and potassium, rubidium, cesium and alum are removed. The mixture is then filtered, alkali is added, and impurities are removed to obtain lithium salt. The plasma generator is used to calcine the calcined material, and then crushing calcinated material to obtain calcinated powder. The calcination is performed at 1500 degrees C for 20 minutes. The mechanical activation treatment involves activating lithium mica roast by mechanical milling to 200-300 meshes. The lithium mica calcined sand is obtained which is baked at low temperature to remove fluorine. The plasma generator is then subjected to high-temperature calcination to remove fluorine and activate calcinated powder. Preferred Conditions: The control pressure of pressurizing state is 3 atmospheric pressure.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)

PN DE102016219857-A1
TI Device for cooling piston of reciprocating internal combustion engine, has opening which is provided in housing of piston and is releasable at lubricant pressure greater than back pressure of spring element.
AU GRUBER M
   KAMMERSTAETTER S
   LANGTHALER J
   MALISCHEW F
AE BAYERISCHE MOTOREN WERKE AG (BAYM-C)
GA 2018275953
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a first nozzle (2) which is arranged on the housing, through which lubricant is conveyed in the direction of the piston. A sliding movable piston (3) is arranged in the housing, and is displaceable by a lubricant pressure against the pressure of a spring element (4). An opening (5) provided in the housing (1) of piston is releasable at a lubricant pressure greater than the back pressure of the spring element, so that lubricant is conveyed through the opening into a second nozzle (6) through which lubricant is conveyed in the direction of piston.
   USE - Device for cooling piston of reciprocating internal combustion engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The device for cooling piston of reciprocating internal combustion engine enables carbon-dioxide for cost-optimal design of lubricant pump, and at the same time, the concentric arrangement makes possible to retain the previous cooling concepts for pistons of reciprocating internal combustion engines.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The device has housing (1) in which the lubricant is conductively connectable to a lubricant pressure supply.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the structure of device for cooling piston of reciprocating internal combustion engine.
   Housing (1)
   Nozzles (2,6)
   Sliding movable piston (3)
   Spring element (4)
   Opening (5)
DC Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P))
MC Q51-A03B; Q51-F01; Q51-G02

PN CN107858203-A
TI High alpha-linolenic acid and peanut blend oil comprises peanut, hemp seed, peony seed and linseed.
AU LIN S
   XIE J
   LIN X
   YU J
AE GUANGDONG JINNIBAO TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201826409A
AB    NOVELTY - A high alpha -linolenic acid and peanut blend oil comprises 50-60 wt.% peanut, 10-20 wt.% hemp seed, 10-20 wt.% peony seed and 15-25 wt.% linseed.
   USE - High alpha -linolenic acid and peanut blend oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The oil has alpha -linolenic acid value of less than 0.5 KOH/(mg/g), unsaturated fatty acid content of more than 92% and alpha -linolenic acid content of 30-40%, quickly refines oil, and reduces acid value. The method keeps relatively high extraction rate, and separates water, free fatty acids, odor component and pigment component.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the high alpha -linolenic acid and peanut blend oil comprising crushing raw materials, uniformly mixing, adding into multistage supercritical carbon dioxide equipment extracting kettle, extracting at 40-60 degrees C for 2.0-4.0 hours at 30-40 MPa, vacuum separating into separation kettle under supercritical state, adjusting separating kettle I temperature to 40-60 degrees C at pressure of 10-20 MPa to obtain peanut blend oil, adding into molecular distilling sample tank, molecular distilling at 100-150 degrees C at pressure of 2-9 Pa, rotating speed of 100-300 revolutions/minute (rpm), feeding speed of 10-50 mL/minute and condensing temperature of 30-50 degrees C, and collecting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The blend oil comprises 50-55 wt.% peanut, 15-20 wt.% hemp seed, 10-15 wt.% peony seed and 15-20 wt.% linseed. Preferred Method: The method further involves using separation kettle comprising separating kettle II, and separating entrainer at 30-50 degrees C at 4-8 MPa.

GA 201813686J
AB    NOVELTY - The method (501) involves forming a first                layer of the electrochromic device on a substrate.                A first particle removal operation is performed to                remove particles from a surface of the substrate. A                second layer of the electrochromic device is                formed. A second particle removal operation is                performed on the surface of the substrate. The                fabrication of the electrochromic device is                completed. The substrate is contacted with one                roller, strip, or brush. The fluid is provided with                carbon dioxide, and sprayed onto the                substrate.
   USE - Method of fabricating electrochromic                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The operation of forming the electrochromic                stack is performed without depositing an ion                conducting layer. The electronically conducting                layers and electrochromically active layers are                prevented from contacting layers of the opposite                polarity and creating a short circuit in regions.                The encapsulating layer is prevented from ejecting                from the device stack and risking a short circuit                when subsequent layers are deposited.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the method                for forming an electrochromic device modified by                introduction of defect-mitigating insulating                layers.

TI New mesoporous titanium apatite used for preparing mesoporous titanium apatite-loaded rare earth oxide composite material for interior decoration harmful gas removal.
AU XIAO F
   LI X
   WANG J
   GUO M
   MA Z
   REN Q
   WANG X
   ZHANG P
   FAN C
   WANG Y
   MA J
AE UNIV SHIJIAZHUANG TIEDAO (USTD-C)
   UNIV SHIJIAZHUANG TIEDAO (USTD-C)
GA 2018146014
AB    NOVELTY - A mesoporous titanium apatite (I) is new. The mesoporous titanium apatite has cellular porous structure, average pore size of 10-25 nm, and specific surface area of 50-200 m2/g.
   USE - Mesoporous titanium apatite used for preparing mesoporous titanium apatite-loaded rare earth oxide composite material for interior decoration harmful gas removal (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The compound (I) oxidizes formaldehyde to carbon dioxide and water under illumination, and has forbidden band width of 2.7 eV, strong adsorption capacity of indoor decoration harmful gas, and no secondary pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A mesoporous titanium apatite of formula TixCa(5-x)(F, Cl, OH) (I), is new. The mesoporous titanium apatite has cellular porous structure, average pore size of 10-25 nm, and specific surface area of 50-200 m2/g.
   x=1-5.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the compound (I); and
   (2) preparation of the mesoporous titanium apatite-loaded rare earth oxide composite material comprising mixing cerium oxide and compound (I) at ratio of 1:10-1, dissolving in 8-10 wt.% water, adjusting pH to 9-11 using ammonia water, stirring at speed of 200-400 revolutions/minute for 1-3 hours, filtering, washing with deionized water, and calcining to 400-600 degrees C for 2 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): The compound (I) is prepared by dispersing calcium carbonate mesoporous template powder into water to obtain 0.1-0.5 mol/L suspension, adding titanium source, dispersing, adding 0.01-0.15 mol/L phosphoric acid hydrogen salt solution at rate of 1-3 mL/minute, water bath reacting at 60-90 degrees C while stirring at speed of 200-400 revolutions/minute for 1-3 hours, adjusting pH to 9-11 using ammonia water, and purifying. Preferred Components: The mesoporous titanium apatite is metal element-doped mesoporous titanium apatite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The metal element is copper, aluminum or bismuth.

PN CN113637151-A; CN113637151-B
TI Preparing a polybenzoxazine carbonate useful in e.g. high molecular polyurethane material comprises e.g. reacting peroxyacetic acid with 4-vinylphenol to prepare 4-epoxy phenol and reacting with isopropylamine and paraformaldehyde using organic solvent to prepare 4-epoxy benzoxazine.
AU DU L
AE UNIV ANHUI (UANH-C)
GA 2021D3506B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a polybenzoxazine carbonate comprises (i) using 4-vinylphenol as basic raw material and organic solvent as reaction medium, reacting peroxyacetic acid with 4-vinylphenol to prepare 4-epoxy phenol, (ii) reacting 4-epoxy phenol with isopropylamine and paraformaldehyde and using organic solvent as reaction medium to prepare 4-epoxy benzoxazine, adding resultant 4-epoxy benzoxazine, ethyl zinc and water into the reaction kettle, and then introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reaction kettle and reacting, releasing the unreacted carbon dioxide in the reaction kettle, adding the reacted carbon dioxide to acetone to dissolving the polymer at the bottom of the reactor, and then washing with 5% dilute hydrochloric acid and then dropping 10 times of ethanol under fast stirring to obtained final product
   USE - The polybenzoxazine carbonate is useful in high molecular polyurethane material and other fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The polybenzoxazine carbonate: has excellent thermal stability, mechanical, physical and chemical properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is chloroform, acetone or dioxane. In the step (i) the molar ratio of 4-vinylphenol, peroxyacetic acid and chloroform is 1:1:100. In the step (ii) the molar ratio of 4-vinylphenol, isopropylamine and paraformaldehyde is 1:2:5. In the step (iii) the molar ratio of ethyl zinc, water and 4-epoxybenzoxazine is 10:1:10000.Preferred Method: In the step (i) the reaction temperature is 30degrees Celsius and the stirring time is 8 hours. In the step (ii) the reaction temperature is 50degrees Celsius and the reaction time is 8 hours. In the step (iii) the reaction temperature is 180-200degrees Celsius and the reaction time is 3-6 hours and the pressure of carbon dioxide is 0.1-1microPa.

(including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).)

AE CHINA NAT PETROLEUM CORP (CNPC-C)

GA 202154993U
AB    NOVELTY - A hollow fiber composite membrane enriched with covalent organic framework materials on active separation layer surface comprises supporting layer, and active separation layer formed on one side surface of supporting layer. The active separation layer comprises high molecular film matrix and covalent organic framework material loaded on high molecular film matrix. The supporting layer comprises polysulfone hollow fiber ultra-filtration membrane.
   USE - The hollow fiber composite membrane enriched with covalent organic framework materials on active separation layer surface is used in separation of carbon dioxide and methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product can fully exert advantages of carbon dioxide preferential adsorption and strengthening diffusion and has high filling density.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDNET CLAIM is included for a method for preparing hollow fiber composite membrane enriched with covalent organic framework materials on active separation layer surface comprising dispersing covalent organic framework material into solvent to obtain dispersion liquid, mixing and stirring dispersion liquid and polymer film substrate, standing to obtain homogeneous casting solution, soaking polysulfone hollow fiber ultra-filtration membrane, taking out, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The high molecular film matrix is block polyether amide resin. The mass ratio of block polyether amide resin and covalent organic framework material is 100:1-10. The covalent organic framework material has two-dimensional sheet layer structure, transverse size of 100-500 nm, thickness of sheet layer of 1-6 nm and aperture of 0.6-2 nm. The covalent organic framework material is formed by polycondensation of 1,3,5-trihydroxybenzene and amino small molecule organic substance. The amino small molecule organic substance is hydrazine hydrate, p-phenylenediamine, biphenyl diamine or triaminoguanidine hydrochloride. The solvent is ethanol aqueous solution, butanol or isopropanol. The mass ratio of covalent organic framework material to solvent is 0.001-0.005:1. The high molecular film matrix is block polyether amide resin. The mass ratio of block polyether amide resin to dispersion liquid is 0.01-0.1:1.

AU WANG S

   WANG J
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 2020B2819V
AB    NOVELTY - Metal oxide-molecular sieve catalyst comprises                composite metal oxides and acid molecular                sieves.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in carbon dioxide                hydrogenation to prepare low-carbon olefin                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: improved carbon dioxide                conversion rate, high selectivity and yield, and                reduced selectivity of the by-product carbon                monixide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Metal oxide-molecular sieve catalyst comprises                composite metal oxides and acid molecular sieves.                The composite metal oxide is M1aM2bOc (I).
   M1 = IIIA group metal;
   M2 = VIB group metal; and
   a, b, and c = total valence of the composite                metal oxide as zero.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing metal oxide-molecular sieve catalyst                comprising mixing aqueous IIIA metal salt, aqueous                VIB metal salt and water to obtain mixed metal ion                solution, mixing the mixed metal ion solution with                auxiliary agent and reacting to obtain precursor,                the auxiliary agent includes complexing agent or                precipitation agent and calcining the precursor to                obtain composite metal oxide, and further mixing                composite metal oxide and the acid molecular sieve                to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The IIIA metal                  includes aluminum, gallaium or indium. The VIB                  group metal includes chromium, molybdenum or                  tungsten; the molar ratio between a and b is                  0.1-5:0.01-1. The acidic molecular sieve includes                  H-SAPO-34, H-RUB-13 or H-SSZ-13. The complexing                  agent includes glucose, citric acid, tartaric                  acid, salicylic acid and/or adipic acid; the                  precipitation agent includes aqueous carbonate                  and/or aqueous bicarbonate; the molar ratio                  between the total metal ions and the auxiliary                  agent in the mixed metal ion solution is 1:0.5-6;                  the mass ratio betwene the composite metal oxide                  and the acid molecular sieve is 1:4-4:1; and the                  metal oxide-molecular sieve has particle size of                  10-60. Preferred Method: The reaction temperature                  is 60-95 degrees C, and time is 2-10 hours. The                  calcination temperature is 350-750 degrees C, and                  the calcination time is 3-12 hours.

AE SOUTHWEST CHEM IND RES & DESIGN INST (CNCC-C)
GA 202007844K
AB    NOVELTY - Noble metal catalyst comprises 60-95 wt.% carrier, 0.0-1 wt.% active component, 2-35 wt.% active additive and 2-15 wt.% molding additive.
   USE - The catalyst is used in carbon dioxide gas purification (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst uses noble metal as the main active component, strengthens the synergistic effect between the noble metal and the additive by adding active additive, improves the activity and stability of the catalyst, has high purification precision, and long service life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing the catalyst involving (i) uniformly mixing carrier, molding additive and water-insoluble active additive precursor, adding water-soluble active additive precursor to the solution for kneading, extruding and molding, (ii) drying, calcining and cooling to room temperature to obtain a catalyst precursor, (iii) preparing the active component precursor into a solution and adding the catalyst precursor for soaking, and (iv) drying the product, calcining and cooling to room temperature to obtain the finished catalyst product; and
   (2) the use of the catalyst in carbon dioxide gas purification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carrier is aluminum oxide, silicon dioxide and/or molecular sieve. The active component is palladium and/or platinum oxide. The active additive is iron, manganese, copper, nickel, cobalt, cerium, zirconium and/or lanthanum oxide. The molding additive is sesbania powder, kaolin clay, attapulgite clay, glass fiber powder and/or high alumina cement. The active component precursor is nitrate, hydrochloride, and acetate of palladium and/or platinum. The active additive precursor is nitrate, hydrochloride, acetate and carbonate of iron, manganese, copper, nickel, cobalt, cerium, zirconium and/or lanthanum. Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the drying process is carried out at 90-120 degrees C for 3-12 hours, and the calcination process is carried out at 300-800 degrees C for 3-6 hours. In step (iii), the soaking process is carried out for 2-12 hours, the drying process is carried out at 90-120 degrees C for 3-12 hours, and the calcination process is carried out at 300-500 degrees C for 2-5 hours. The reaction volume space velocity of the noble metal catalyst in carbon dioxide gas purification is 1000 to 20000 hour-1, the reaction temperature is controlled at 200-450 degrees C, and the reaction pressure is controlled at 0.1 to 10 MPa.

AU SHI Q

   XI H

   FAN W
   LIU J
   SONG Y
   ZHANG W

   YANG W

   HUO X
AE ZHENGZHOU TOBACCO RES INST CNDC (CNTA-C)
   ZHENGZHOU TOBACCO RES INST CNDC (CNTA-C)
GA 201990253G
AB    NOVELTY - An additive comprises a fatty alcohol polyoxypropylene ether type compound (I).
   USE - The additive is useful for extracting plant essential oils.
   ADVANTAGE - The additive: realizes the more efficient extraction of the aroma substance of the natural plant material; effectively improves the extraction efficiency of the plant essential oil, where the addition amount of the additive is relatively low, and the high-quality plant essential oil can be directly obtained without removing the additive after supercritical carbon dioxide extraction; and avoids the loss of aroma substances caused by the removal of the additive.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An additive comprises a fatty alcohol polyoxypropylene ether type compound of formula: R(CH2CH2CH2O)nH (I).
   R = 6-12C fatty alcohol;and
   n = polymerization degree, preferably 5-10.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for extracting plant essential oils, which involves adding the additive and supercritical carbon dioxide solvent to a plant raw material to perform a plant essential oil extraction treatment, where the additive is added in an amount of 0.02-0.8 %mass based on the supercritical carbon dioxide solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound (I) is a branched alkane alcohol polyoxypropylene ether compound of formula: (CH3(CH2)m1)(CH3(CH2)m2)CHCH2O(CH2CH2CH2O)5-10H (II). The mass ratio of the plant raw material to the supercritical carbon dioxide solvent is 1:8-1:12 g/ml. The plant raw material is derived from roots, stems, leaves, flowers, fruits and/or peels of fresh plants. Preferred Method: The method of extracting the plant essential oil involves placing the plant raw material and the additive in an extraction kettle, then injecting the supercritical carbon dioxide solvent to carry out extraction at 45-65 degrees C and 15-35 MPa for 1-5 hours to obtain an extract, separating and purifying the extract. The step of separating and purifying the extract involves initially introducing the extract into a first-stage separator for cooling treatment, and then introducing into a second-stage separator to remove carbon dioxide to finally produce the plant essential oil.m1, m2 = 0 or positive integer, where the sum of m1 and m2 is 2-8.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R = 6-12C linear aliphatic alcohol or 6-12C branched aliphatic alcohol, preferably 6-12C alkane alcohol, 6-12C olefin alcohol or 6-12C alkyne alcohol.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified biochar comprises taking biomass and iron salts as raw materials, cracking the biomass raw material to obtain the biological carbon, fully immersing the obtained bio-carbon in the iron salt solution to obtain the raw material after dipping, drying the raw material after dipping and roasting at 450-600 degrees C under an inert gas atmosphere to obtain the modified bio-char.
   USE - The modified biochar is useful for reducing carbon dioxide emission in soil (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The modified biochar can reduce the release of carbon dioxide in soil; helps to reduce the loss of soil organic carbon, also can improve the accumulation of soil organic carbon, and improves the soil quality. The method is simple and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use method of modified biochar in reducing carbon dioxide emission in soil comprising placing modified biochar in the soil at a mass ratio of 0.5-1%, mixing modified biochar with the soil evenly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The biomass raw material is straw, leaves or herbs, the iron salt is ferric nitrate, ferrous sulfate and ferric chloride. The ratio of biomass raw material and iron salt is 1:1-2. The biomass raw material is rice straw, Populus alba leaves, Miscanthus. The thermal cracking biomass raw material temperature is 280-320 degrees C. The dipping time is 20-24 hours. In the step (3) drying at 100-110 degrees C for 36-48 hours. In the step (4), roasting at rate of 5-10 degrees C/minutes, raising the temperature to 450-600 degrees C, roasting for 1-2 hours. The biomass granularity of raw material after crushing is 80-100 meshes. The temperature of thermal cracking in the step (1) is 300 degrees C. In the step (2), the mass fraction of solute in the iron salt solution is 33-50%.

TI Preparation of one-dimensional cuprous            oxide/silver/zinc oxide photocatalyst used for            photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction under            ultraviolet visible light irradiation, involves adding            zinc nitrate hexahydrate to water and ethanol.
AU TANG Z
   ZHANG F
   XU Y
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 2019707492
AB    NOVELTY - A one-dimensional cuprous oxide/silver/zinc                oxide photocatalyst is prepared by adding zinc                nitrate hexahydrate to water and ethanol mixed                solution, adding 28 wt.% ammonia water to adjust pH                value, stirring for 180 minutes, autoclaving at 160                degrees C for 10 hours, cooling, collecting white                precipitate, washing 3 times with ethanol and                water, drying, crushing, calcining to obtain                one-dimensional zinc oxide photocatalyst,                ultrasonically dispersing into deionized water,                adding copper sulfate pentahydrate solution and                sodium hydroxide solution, ultrasonicating for 20                minutes, adding silver nitrate solution,                ultrasonicating for 20 minutes, adding ascorbic                acid, stirring for 20 minutes, centrifuging,                washing with deionized water, and drying.
   USE - A method for preparing one-dimensional cuprous                oxide/silver/zinc oxide photocatalyst used for                photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction under                ultraviolet visible light irradiation                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has higher photocatalytic carbon                dioxide reduction performance and stability under                ultraviolet visible light irradiation. The method                is simple, mild reaction condition, and suitable                for sustainable development of environment and                energy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The one-dimensional                  cuprous oxide/silver/zinc oxide photocatalyst is                  prepared by adding 5.95 g adding zinc nitrate                  hexahydrate to 4:5 volume ratio of water and                  ethanol mixed solution, adding zinc nitrate                  hexahydrate, water and ethanol with 28 wt.%                  ammonia water to adjust pH value to 8, stirring                  for 180 minutes, autoclaving at 160 degrees C for                  10 hours, cooling, collecting white precipitate,                  washing 3 times with ethanol and water, drying at                  60 degrees C, crushing, calcining at 350 degrees                  C for 3 hours under air atmosphere to obtain                  one-dimensional zinc oxide photocatalyst,                  ultrasonically dispersing into deionized water,                  adding 1:1 volume ratio of 0.05 mol/L copper                  sulfate pentahydrate solution and 0.1 mol/L                  sodium hydroxide solution, ultrasonicating for 20                  minutes, adding 0.1 mol/L silver nitrate solution                  according to molar ratio of 1:4 or 2:3 or 4:1,                  ultrasonicating for 20 minutes, adding 30 mg                  ascorbic acid, stirring for 20 minutes,                  centrifuging, washing with deionized water, and                  drying.

PN KR2019088219-A; KR2096862-B1
TI Regenerating an absorbent involves absorbing the acidic gas using an absorbent for removing the acidic gas to obtain an absorbent absorbing the acidic gas, and regenerating the absorbent by removing the acidic gas.
AU BAEK I H
   NAMSUNGCHAN
   PARKSUNGYEOL
   UMAIR H B
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 2019677100
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerating an absorbent involves absorbing the acidic gas using an absorbent for removing the acidic gas to obtain an absorbent absorbing the acidic gas, and regenerating the absorbent by removing the acidic gas by adding a transition metal oxide catalyst to the absorbent absorbing the acidic gas, where the transition metal oxide is one selected from the group consisting of niobium pentoxide (Nb2O5), copper oxide (CuO), manganese(IV) oxide (MnO2), silver(I) oxide (Ag2O), and nickel oxide (NiO), and promotes decomposition of carbamate in an acid gas removal absorbent solution.
   USE - Method for regenerating an absorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to regenerate an absorbent, where the Bronsted acid position and the Lewis acid position present on the surface of the metal oxide absorb acidic gas, carbon dioxide is removed by promoting the decomposition of carbamate combined with absorbent and carbon dioxide, and this makes it possible to regenerate the absorbent with regenerating heat lower than the conventional regenerant heat of the absorbent, hence regenerating the absorbent with high efficiency and low cost, and capturing the carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The absorbent is monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, triethanolamine (TEA), tetraethylenepentamine (TEPA), triethylenetetramine (TETA), N-methyl diethanolamine, 3-isobutoxypropylamine, dimethylaminoethylamine, and diglycolamine. The acid gas is carbon dioxide. Preferred Conditions: The transition metal oxide catalyst is added in an amount of 0.1-20 wt.% based on the weight of the absorbent. The regenerating step is carried out at a temperature range of 40-85 degrees C. The method for regenerating an absorbent further comprises the step of reusing the regenerated acid gas removal absorbent, method of regenerating the absorbent for acid gas removal using a transition metal oxide catalyst.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN109395749-A; CN109395749-B
TI Bismuth oxyhalide nanomaterial used as catalyst            for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide,            comprises single-layer sheet structure, and bismuth            oxyhalide nanomaterial is bismuth oxybromide            nanomaterial or bismuth oxyiodide nanomaterial.
AU SUN Y
   WU J
   XIE Y
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 2019231528
AB    NOVELTY - Bismuth oxyhalide nanomaterial comprises                single-layer sheet structure and the thickness of                single layer sheet is 0.80-1.0 nm. The bismuth                oxyhalide nano-material is tetragonal phase                crystal. The lattice parameter of nanomaterial is                200-500 nm.
   USE - Bismuth oxyhalide nanomaterial used as                catalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon                dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The bismuth oxyhalide nanomaterial has strong                catalytic ability and can photocatalytically reduce                carbon dioxide under normal temperature and normal                pressure conditions, has high efficiency, high                stability, is environmentally friendly and                sustainable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing a bismuth oxyhalide nanomaterial,                which involves subjecting hydrothermal reaction of                water-soluble cerium salt and                1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium halide salt in water in                the presence of polyvinylpyrrolidone, performing                solid-liquid separation of the obtained reactant to                obtain a bismuth oxyhalide organic-inorganic                layered structure precursor, dispersing the bismuth                oxyhalide organic-inorganic layered structure                precursor in an organic solvent and ultrasonically                stripping to obtain bismuth oxyhalide nanomaterial                with a single layer of thin film structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bismuth oxyhalide                  nanomaterial is nano material of bismuth                  oxychloride, bismuth oxybromide nanomaterial or                  bismuth(III) oxyiodide nanomaterial. Defect state                  oxyhalide bismuth nano material comprises                  single-layer sheet structure including oxygen                  vacancies. The water-soluble cerium salt is one                  or more selected from the group consisting of                  cerium nitrate and cerium chloride. The                  imidazolium halide salt is selected from the                  group consisting of 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium                  chloride, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide or                  1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium iodide. Preferred                  Conditions: The hydrothermal reaction is                  performed at 140-180 degrees C for 24-48 hours.                  The bismuth oxyhalide nano material is irradiated                  with an ultraviolet lamp of 200-600 watts for                  2-12 hours to obtain an oxygen-containing                  vacancy. Preferred Process: The method for                  preparing a defect-type bismuth oxyhalide                  nanomaterial, involves performing hydrothermal                  reaction of water-soluble cerium salt and                  1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium halide in water in                  the presence of polyvinylpyrrolidone. The                  obtained reactant is subjected to solid-liquid                  separation to obtain a bismuth oxyhalide                  organic-inorganic layered structure precursorhe                  which is dispersed in an organic solvent. The                  bismuth oxyhalide nanomaterial having a                  single-layer lamella structure is obtained by                  ultrasonic stripping method. The bismuth                  oxyhalide nanomaterial is irradiated with                  ultraviolet light to obtain a defective bismuth                  oxyhalide nanomaterial containing oxygen                  vacancies.

PN CN109112502-A
TI Preparing titanium dioxide film comprises e.g. purging the cleaning pipeline and the deposition chamber, passing titanium tetrachloride into the deposition chamber and introducing a reaction gas hydrogen and carbon dioxide.
AU GAO S
AE HEFEI GUOXUAN HIGH TECH POWER SOURCE CO (HEFG-C)
GA 201903929L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing titanium dioxide film comprises (i) purging the cleaning pipeline and the deposition chamber before depositing; (ii) passing titanium tetrachloride into the deposition chamber through a carrier gas by bubbling method is useful for and introducing a reaction gas hydrogen and carbon dioxide into the deposition chamber; and (iii) turning off the carbon dioxide of the carrier gas and the reaction gas, maintaining hydrogen gas flow rate of the reaction gas, waiting until there is no reaction in the deposition chamber, turning off the hydrogen gas of the reaction gas when the temperature of the chamber is deposited cools to below 300 degrees C under the protection of an inert atmosphere taking prepared titanium dioxide film.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing titanium dioxide film.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces titanium dioxide film with excellent properties, uniform and dense and has excellent strength; and directly forms uniform coating on the high temperature resistant work piece of the complicated structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises purging the cleaning pipeline and the deposition chamber with nitrogen or hydrogen for 1-2 hours. The pressure in the deposition chamber is normal pressure at 700-900 degrees C. The step (ii) comprises passing reaction gas hydrogen and the reaction gas carbon dioxide through the first gas mixing tank, mixing with the carrier gas carrying titanium tetrachloride in the second gas mixing tank and passing into deposition chamber. The temperature of the first gas mixing tank and the second gas mixing tank are both 100-120 degrees C. The heating of the titanium tetrachloride is carried out at 25-35 degrees C. Preferred Components: The carrier gas is an inert gas, nitrogen or hydrogen, where flow rate of the carrier gas is 0.8-1.4 slm, the flow rate of the reaction gas hydrogen is 1.2-2 slm, and the flow rate of the reaction gas carbon dioxide is 30-80 sccm. The purity of the titanium tetrachloride is 99%, and hydrogen gas of the reaction gas and the carbon dioxide of the reaction gas are both 99.999%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The deposition chamber includes a mixing chamber is connected to the air inlet and a substrate placement area connected to the air outlet, the cross-sectional area of the mixing chamber perpendicular to the gas flow direction is twice the cross-sectional area of the substrate placement area perpendicular to the gas flow direction.

TI Composition for mineralizing gases of carbon            dioxide and nitrogen oxide and for use as additive in            e.g., paints, lacquers, and varnishes, comprises a            mixture of magnesium, iron, calcium monoxide, titanium            dioxide, and silicon dioxide.
AU LLOBREGAT AGUSTI J C
   SIVERA MARZA J A
   MARZA J A
   AGUSTI J C
   LLOBREGAT A J C
   SIVERA M J A
AE PRIMLAB GLOBAL SL (PRIM-Non-standard)
   PRIMLAB GLOBAL SL (PRIM-Non-standard)
   PRIMLAB GLOBAL SL (PRIM-Non-standard)
   PRIMLAB GLOBAL SL (PRIM-Non-standard)
   PRIMLAB GLOBAL SL (PRIM-Non-standard)
GA 201863837V
AB    NOVELTY - A composition for mineralizing gases of carbon                dioxide and nitrogen oxide comprises a mixture of                1-25 wt.% magnesium, 1-23 wt.% iron, 1-25 wt.%                calcium monoxide, 0.1-11 wt.% titanium dioxide, and                16-75 wt.% silicon dioxide, with a particle                diameter of 100 nm to 4000 mu m.
   USE - A composition for mineralizing gases of carbon                dioxide and nitrogen oxide and for use as additive                in paints, lacquers, and varnishes and as additive                in the manufacture of rubber and rubber elements                which are worn (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition allows to capture toxic gases                and reduce the increase in greenhouse effect and                its effect on health.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) use of the composition as additive in                paints, lacquers, and varnishes which involves                using 0.5-15 wt.% composition; and
   (2) use of the composition as additive in the                manufacture of rubber and rubber elements which                involves using 0.5-15 wt.% composition.
TF EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Carbon dioxide sensor for use in spacesuit, has conduit for connecting to source of breathing air, sensor for detecting modification in radiation, and rigid cover for surrounding radiation source, test chamber and sensor.
AU MURRAY S K
   AVALONE R G
   QUINN G J
AE HAMILTON SUNDSTRAND CORP (SUNH-C)
GA 201858004G
AB    NOVELTY - The sensor has a conduit for connecting to a source of breathing air and delivering a sample of breathing air into a test chamber. A radiation source applies radiation across the chamber. A sensor detects modification in the radiation as the radiation passes through the air sample in the test chamber, and is communicated with electronics to identify percentage of carbon dioxide in the sample. A rigid cover surrounds the radiation source, the test chamber, and the sensor. An inner surface of the cover is made of nickel, aluminum or gold.
   USE - Carbon dioxide sensor for use in a spacesuit (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The sensor allows an astronaut to leave a space vehicle for relatively prolonged amounts of time. The sensor monitors a breathing air supply to provide adequate oxygen flow to the astronaut.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a spacesuit.
   Spacesuit (50)
   Air supply (51)
   Area receiving sensor (52)
   Air supply line (54)
   Port (56)

TI Preparing pig micro-ecological composition            comprises mixing probiotic bacteria mud and mixed            protective solution, dropping into calcium chloride            solution to obtain probiotic micro-capsule, soaking in            sodium carboxymethyl cellulose and inulin mixed            solution, drying and spraying with coating            material.
AU ZHANG H
   CHEN L
   LIU Z
   YANG F
   QI R
   YANG Y
   ZHANG Y
   YAN H
   LIU J
AE INST ANIMAL SCI CAAS (CAGS-C)
   UNIV SOUTHWEST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USWS-C)
GA 2022A71545
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pig micro-ecological composition                comprises (i) preparing probiotic bacteria mud;                (ii) adding carrageenan into the supercritical                carbon dioxide reaction device, introducing carbon                dioxide, stirring and soaking for 120 minutes at                16-18 MPa and 45-65&#8451;, releasing pressure,                adding ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid solution,                introducing carbon dioxide again at 16-18 MPa at                45-65&#8451;, stirring and reacting for 120                minutes, releasing pressure at a certain speed,                precipitating and drying to obtain carrageenan wall                material; and (iii) adding 10-20 pts. wt.                carrageenan wall material and 25-35 pts. wt. stable                protection agent into 150-250 pts. wt. water,                stirring for 30-45 minutes and introducing ozone                for 60-90 minutes and nitrogen for 30-45 minutes in                sequence to obtain mixed protective solution.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing pig                micro-ecological composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares pig micro-ecological                composition with good stability and gastric acid                resistance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method of preparing                  pig micro-ecological composition further                  comprises (iv) adding 10-15 pts. wt. probiotic                  bacteria mud into 40-150 pts. wt. mixed                  protective solution, mixing uniformly, dropping                  into the calcium chloride solution, centrifuging                  and separating to obtain the probiotic                  micro-capsule; (v) soaking the probiotics                  microcapsule in the mixed solution of sodium                  carboxymethyl cellulose and inulin, stirring and                  soaking for 5-10 minutes, taking out and washing                  for 3-5 minutes with sterile deionized water to                  obtain probiotic micro-capsule coated with sodium                  carboxymethyl cellulose and inulin; and (vi)                  transferring the probiotic microcapsules coated                  with sodium carboxymethyl cellulose and inulin on                  the outer layer into a fluidized granulation                  coating dryer for drying and spraying the dried                  microcapsules with a coating material to form a                  coating.Preferred Components: The probiotics                  comprises Lactobacillus reuteri, Bifidobacterium                  longum, Bacillus subtilis or Enterococcus                  faecalis.

PN TW766640-B1
TI Method for assessing blood glucose concentration            for user to detect concentration in non-invasive            manner, involves performing blood glucose assessment by            using carbon dioxide concentration in final exhalation            extracted from expiratory gas.
AU LIN C
   LU C
   TOSHIO K
AE UNIV NAT TAIWAN NORMAL (UTWN-C)
GA 202298867N
AB    NOVELTY - An evaluation method for blood glucose                concentration for users to measure in a                non-invasive manner, including the steps of                exhalation capture and measurement, and the steps                of blood glucose evaluation. The expiratory capture                and measurement step is to capture the final                expiratory volume of an expiratory gas exhaled by                the user in a continuous time interval, and the                final expiratory volume is not greater than the                total expiratory volume of the expiratory gas 10%,                and measure the carbon dioxide concentration of the                end expiratory flow to estimate the user's blood                sugar concentration. Next, in the blood glucose                assessment step, blood glucose assessment is                performed by using the carbon dioxide concentration                in the final exhalation extracted from the                expiratory gas, so that the interference of dead                space gas in the expiratory gas can be excluded,                thereby improving the accuracy of the assessment.                In addition, the present invention also provides a                detection device for carrying out the                aforementioned evaluation method.

   ZHOU C
   BAI J
   SUN S
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 2021E9597F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing epsilon-, z-, and eta-cyanocarboxylic acid comprises taking olefin and cyclic ketoxime as raw materials, iridium, 2,4,5,6-tetra(carbazol-9-yl)benzene-1,3-dicarbonitrile (4CZIPN) or ruthenium as photocatalyst, and then reacting under the action of reducing agent, dimethyl sulfoxide as solvent, introducing carbon dioxide gas, and reacting to obtain series of cyanocarboxylic acid compounds.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing epsilon-, z-, and eta-cyanocarboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high yields and simple post-treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing epsilon-, z-, and eta-cyanocarboxylic acid comprises taking olefin and cyclic ketoxime as raw materials, iridium, 2,4,5,6-tetra(carbazol-9-yl)benzene-1,3-dicarbonitrile (4CZIPN) or ruthenium as photocatalyst, and then reacting under the action of reducing agent, dimethyl sulfoxide as solvent, introducing carbon dioxide gas, and reacting to obtain series of cyanocarboxylic acid compounds.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reaction conditions are: reacting at room temperature for 12-24 hours under 2x3 W blue light emitting diodes, then adding iodomethane into 50degrees Celsius oil bath and reacting for another hour. The molar ratio between olefin and cyclic ketoxime is 1:1.5-2, and the pressure of the carbon dioxide is 0.1 MPa.Preferred Components: The photocatalyst is (2,2'-bipyridine)bis(3,5-difluoro-2-(5-(trifluoromethyl)-2-pyridinyl)phenyl)iridium(III) hexafluorophosphate, tris(2,2'-bipyridine)ruthenium bis(hexafluorophosphate) or 2,4,5,6-tetra(9-carbazolyl)Isophthalonitrile, and the addition amount of catalyst is 2 mol.% of the moles of olefin. The reducing agent is N,N-diisopropylethylamine or triethylamine, and the molar ratio between reducing agent and olefin is 3.0:1. The olefin is compound of formula (I), and the cyclic ketoxime is compound of formula (II).R' and R = H, phenyl, methyl, naphthyl or thienyl (which are substituted with fluoro, chloro, bromo, methoxy, ester and p-trifluoromethyl);R1 = methyl or H;R3, R4 and R5 = aryl, methyl, cyano, benzyl or ester;X = C, O, or N; andR2 = p-CF3C6H4CO.n = 1, 2 or 3.

TI Low carbon dioxide emission humus conversion based            on biodegradable polymer materials comprises forming a            composite material system from biodegradable polymer            material and material which slowly releases nutrient            nitrogen or nitrogen and phosphorus.
AU XIANG Y
   ZHANG X
   WANG L
   CHEN T
   FAN H
   LIU Y
AE SHANXI ZHONGBEI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV NORTH CHINA (UYZB-C)
GA 2021B7469C
AB    NOVELTY - Low carbon dioxide emission humus conversion                based on biodegradable polymer materials comprises                forming a composite material system from                biodegradable polymer material and material which                slowly releases nutrient nitrogen or nitrogen and                phosphorus, where t he mass ratio of carbon content                to nitrogen content in the composite material                system is 1-35:1.
   USE - The method is useful to promote the conversion                of biodegradable polymer materials into soil humus                or compost humus (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can promote the conversion of                biodegradable polymer materials into soil humus or                compost humus, instead of being converted into                greenhouse gas carbon dioxide and discharging into                the atmosphere, thus, it is of great significance                for energy saving and emission reduction,                environmentally friendly, involves efficient                utilization of biodegradable polymer materials, and                efficient and environmentally friendly utilization                of biodegradable polymer material waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                of above method to promote the conversion of                biodegradable polymer materials into soil humus or                compost humus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the composite material                  system, the biodegradable polymer material and                  the material that slowly releases nutrient                  nitrogen or nitrogen and phosphorus are                  compounded through physical blending or through                  the hydrogen bond interaction between the                  functional groups of each component. The                  physically blending the biodegradable polymer                  material and the material slowly releasing                  nutrient nitrogen or nitrogen and phosphorus                  includes uniformly mixing the biodegradable                  polymer material with the material that slowly                  releases nutrient nitrogen or nitrogen and                  phosphorus, and then extruding the uniformly                  mixed material system through an extruder and                  pelletizing to obtain a composite material                  system. The method of compounding the                  biodegradable polymer material and urea aldehyde                  through the hydrogen bond interaction between the                  functional groups of each component comprises (i)                  adding formaldehyde and urea into the reactor,                  adjusting the pH of the system, and reacting at                  the set temperature, pouring the reaction                  solution into a beaker and sealing it, freezing                  it in the refrigerator, and then extracting and                  filtering out the remaining liquid, finally,                  placing the sample in a vacuum oven to dry and                  pulverizing to obtain hydroxymethyl urea powder,                  (ii) uniformly mixing the biodegradable polymer                  material with the urea aldehyde precursor                  hydroxymethyl urea powder prepared in step (i),                  then extruding and pelletizing the uniformly                  mixed material system through an extruder,                  carrying out melt polycondensation reaction in                  the extruder barrel, then, the biodegradable                  polymer material and the urea aldehyde forming a                  composite material system through the hydrogen                  bond interaction between the functional groups of                  each component, carrying out recombination of the                  biodegradable polymer material and the derivative                  of urea aldehyde through the hydrogen bond                  interaction between the functional groups of each                  component comprising (a) adding formaldehyde and                  urea into the reactor to adjust the pH of the                  system, reacting at a set temperature, pouring                  the reaction solution into a beaker and sealing                  it, freezing it in the refrigerator, and                  extracting and filtering out the remaining                  liquid, finally, placing the sample in a vacuum                  oven to dry and pulverizing to obtain                  hydroxymethyl urea powder and (b) mixing the                  biodegradable polymer material uniformly with the                  urea aldehyde precursor hydroxymethyl urea powder                  and phosphate prepared in step (a), subsequently,                  extruding and pelletizing the uniformly mixed                  material system through an extruder, the added                  urea aldehyde precursor hydroxymethyl urea                  undergoes a melt polycondensation reaction in the                  extruder barrel, then the biodegradable polymer                  material and the derivative of urea aldehyde form                  a composite material system through the hydrogen                  bond interaction between the functional groups of                  each component. The biodegradable polymer                  material and the material slowly releasing                  nutrient nitrogen or nitrogen and phosphorus form                  a composite material system which is added to the                  soil or compost raw material in a ratio of the                  total carbon content to the mass ratio of the                  soil or compost raw material of 1:60-10900.Preferred Components: The biodegradable                  polymer material is a natural biodegradable                  polymer material and/or a synthetic biodegradable                  polymer material. The natural biodegradable                  polymer material is starch, cellulose, sodium                  alginate and/or chitosan; the synthetic                  biodegradable polymer material is polyvinyl                  alcohol, polylactic acid, polybutylene succinate                  and/or polyadipate/butylene terephthalate. The                  material that slowly releases nutrient nitrogen                  or nitrogen and phosphorus is slow-release                  fertilizer, controlled-release fertilizer,                  polyurea, polyacrylamide, polyaspartic acid,                  protein, melamine copolymer resin and/or                  phosphate polymer. The slow-release fertilizer                  includes urea aldehyde or its derivatives.

GA 202165903T

   ADVANTAGE - The chitosan-zeolite complex: has excellent thermal stability and ion exchange performance; is non-toxic and capable of selectively adsorbing large amounts of carbon dioxide. The method is environmentally friendly and environmentally friendly; exhibits excellent physicochemical properties; and has excellent storage performance and excellent reproducibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) chitosan-zeolite complex prepared by above mentioned method; and
   (2) adsorbent comprising the chitosan-zeolite complex.

The concentration of zeolite dispersion is 0.1-20% (g/ml). The zeolite dispersion subjected to ultrasonic treatment to prepare it in a uniformly dispersed state. The organic solvent is acetone, and/or ethanol. The chitosan-zeolite complex is crystalline. The chitosan-zeolite complex has BET surface area of 740-840 m2/g, preferably 444 m2/g. The chitosan-zeolite complex has carbon dioxide adsorption capacity of 30-60/ccg. The chitosan-zeolite complex has a hydrogen adsorption capacity of 80-140/ccg. Preferred Method: In the step (c), the drying is carried out at 50-100 degrees C. In the step (d), the calcination is carried out at 300-600 degrees C for 2-20 hours.

PT P
PN CN112520746-A
TI Preparing food-grade white carbon black comprises dissolving sodium hydroxide in water, adding sodium alginate, adding carbon dioxide under stirring, alkalizing production of white carbon black, drying and crushing.
AU GAO H
   XIN D
   WU B
   DONG C
   PENG K
AE JIANGXI SHUANGLONG SILICON MATERIAL TECH (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021315747
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing food-grade white carbon black, comprises (i) dissolving sodium hydroxide in water and controlling the pH to 10-11, adding sodium alginate quality 5-10% soybean lecithin, stirring and dissolving to obtain an alkaline solution of soybean lecithin, taking sodium alginate and dissolving in the above-mentioned alkaline solution of soybean lecithin which is 20-30 times the mass of sodium alginate, adding polyvinyl alcohol 0.5-0.8 times the mass of sodium alginate and 0.2-0.8 mass of sodium alginate, dissolving 0.3 times of PEG2000, dissolving the sodium alginate in sodium silicate with 2-3 times the mass of sodium alginate, adding a silane coupling agent with a mass of 5-10% of the sodium alginate, stirring, adding carbon dioxide under stirring until the solution becomes turbid, and stopping the introduction of carbon dioxide to obtain sodium alginate ion exchange layer solution.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing food-grade white carbon black.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (ii) taking ceramic Raschig ring and dipping in the sodium alginate ion exchange layer solution prepared in step (i) by dip coating, heating to 75-85 degrees C, fixing the ion exchange layer, stacking the ceramic Raschig ring with the ion exchange layer in the manner of an absorption tower to form an ion exchange column, spraying with 0.3-0.5% dilute hydrochloric acid as a spray liquid, and repeating 5-8 times, (iii) alkalizing the production of white carbon black with high-quality quartz sand or bentonite to produce sodium silicate solution, and passing carbon dioxide through the carbonization method until the pH of the sodium silicate solution reaches 8-9, and passing the sodium silicate solution into the ion exchange column prepared in the first step, the processing capacity of the ion exchange column is a sodium silicate solution 50-70 times the mass of the adsorbed sodium alginate, and the ion exchanged sodium silicate solution is collected, and (iv) the ion-exchanged sodium silicate solution collected in step is aged for 30-40 minutes, and then carbon dioxide is continuously introduced to control the pH to 5-6, aging again for 40-60 minutes, filtering, drying and crushing the completely settled white carbon black to obtain food-grade white carbon black that meets the quality standard. In the step (i), the modulus of the sodium silicate is 2-3. The method comprises after the ion exchange column is used, 0.5-1% dilute hydrochloric acid can be used as a spray liquid for spraying, and the spray liquid is circulated 8-10 times, regenerated and reused. The sodium alginate ion exchange layer solution prepared in the step (i) can be heated and fixed in the coil pipe; the coil pipe also can perform ion exchange to remove heavy metal ion. In the step (iv), the white carbon black drying technique is spray drying. The white carbon black drying process is spray drying. The temperature of spray drying is controlled at 250-280 degrees C. Preferred Components: The silane coupling agent comprises KH550, KH560 and/or KH570. In the step (iii), the sodium oxide content of the sodium silicate solution is controlled to be 2.5-2.6%. The content of silicon dioxide is controlled to be 7.4-7.6%. The food-grade white carbon black is white in color, and the lead content is less than 2ppm. The content of heavy metals (calculated as lead) is less than 5ppm, the content of arsenic is less than 1ppm, and the mass fraction of soluble dissociation salt (calculated as sodium sulfate) is less than 2%.

PN CN112342151-A; CN112342151-B
TI Anaerobic culture method of Akkermansia            muciniphila comprises carrying out anaerobic culture            with mixed gas of nitrogen, hydrogen, and carbon            dioxide, and modified brain heart infusion medium.
AU WANG M
   SHI C
AE JUNWEIAN WUHAN LIFE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JUNW-Non-standard)
GA 202117541H
AB    NOVELTY - Anaerobic culture method of Akkermansia                muciniphila comprises carrying out anaerobic                culture with mixed gas of 85-94% nitrogen, 5%                hydrogen, and 1-10% carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for anaerobic culture of                Akkermansia muciniphila.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures growth and reproduction of                Akkermansia muciniphila is not restricted by the                height of the liquid level, and can grow well in                the shake flask or on the surface of the plate,                reduces the difficulty of culturing Akkermansia                muciniphila and the growth rate from 3-4 days to                24-36 hours, greatly shortens the culture time,                improves the activity of Akkermansia muciniphila                and promotes the growth Akkermansia muciniphila and                is of great significance for the expansion of                Akkermansia muciniphila and in-depth                research.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - AN INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for: an                anaerobic culture method of Akkermansia                muciniphila, comprising carrying out anaerobic                culture by mixed gas nitrogen, hydrogen, and carbon                dioxide and medium of modified brain heart infusion                medium supplemented with mucin, yeast extract, and                0.7-7 mM sodium bicarbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The mixed gas                  comprises 90% nitrogen, 5% hydrogen, and 5%                  carbon dioxide. The medium used for the anaerobic                  culture is a modified brain heart infusion                  medium, which is supplemented with mucin and                  yeast extract, and comprises 37 g/l brain-heart                  infusion extract, 5 g/l yeast extract, 2.5 g/l                  mucin, 1 mg/l resazurin, and 100 mg/l L-cysteine                  hydrochloride and 0.7-7 mM, preferably 3.5 mM                  sodium bicarbonate. Preferred Method: The method                  for preparing the modified brain heart infusion                  medium comprises: (i) dissolving mucin in 1%                  (wt./vol.) phosphate buffered saline buffer,                  sterilizing, allowing to stand overnight,                  centrifuging at 12000 rotations/minute for 5                  minutes, and taking the supernatant; (ii)                  dissolving L-cysteine hydrochloride in ultrapure                  water to obtain 10% (wt./vol.) L-cysteine                  hydrochloride solution, filtering and                  sterilizing; and (iii) adding 37 g brain-heart                  infusionExtract, 5 g yeast extract, 1ml 0.1%                  (wt./vol.) resazurin into 1 l water, mixing, and                  autoclaving, taking 375 ml sterile solution and                  adding 125 ml mucin solution, mixing well, adding                  10 ml L-cysteine hydrochloride solution. The                  culture temperature used in the anaerobic culture                  is 37 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN111841595-A; CN111841595-B
TI Preparing carbon material catalyst co-doped with nitrogen and phosphorus used in catalyzing reduction reaction of carbon dioxide, involves mixing carbon source precursor, nitrogen source precursor and phosphorus source precursor uniformly.
AU WANG Y
AE FUJIAN MATERIAL STRUCTURE INST (CAFU-C)
GA 2020A9660Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon material catalyst co-doped with nitrogen and phosphorus involves mixing carbon source precursor, nitrogen source precursor and phosphorus source precursor uniformly. The mixture is roasted at a high temperature 800-1000 degrees C and carbon material catalyst co-doped with nitrogen and phosphorus is obtained. The nitrogen source precursor is one or more of urea, hexamethylenetetramine, dicyandiamine, and melamine, preferably dicyandiamine. The phosphorus source precursor is one or more of phosphoric acid, triphenylphosphorus, tetrabutylphosphonium bromide, tetrabutylphosphonium chloride, and phytic acid, preferably phytic acid.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon material catalyst co-doped with nitrogen and phosphorus used in catalyzing the reduction reaction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has synergistic effect of nitrogen and phosphorus and the high specific surface area, the carbon material co-doped with nitrogen and phosphorus exhibits significant catalytic carbon dioxide reduction performance, does not require any metals, are cheap and easily available, have short reaction time, simple and feasible process, convenient operation and strong universality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The carbon source precursor is one or more of sucrose, glucose, and fructose. The molar ratio of the carbon source precursor and the nitrogen source precursor is 1:1-15, and the molar ratio of the carbon source precursor and the phosphorus source precursor is 1:0.1-0.5. Preferred Process: The carbon source precursor and nitrogen source precursor are added in an agate mortar and grind uniformly, phosphorus source precursor is added to grind uniformly, moved mixture into a tube furnace and roasted at a high temperature in an argon atmosphere at 1-10 degrees C/minutes, the roasting temperature is 800-1000 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, naturally cooled to room temperature, taken out the sample, and ground uniformly again to obtain a carbon material catalyst co-doped with nitrogen and phosphorus. The specific surface area of the carbon material catalyst is 1000-1500 m2/g. The carbon material catalyst is a mesoporous material, and the pore diameter is 2-10 nanometer. The carbon material catalyst has a sheet structure. The carbon material catalyst realizes an effective reduction reaction of carbon dioxide under neutral conditions and a low overpotential.

PT P
PN CN111826230-A
TI Extracting essential oils comprises freezing raw materials inside the refrigeration equipment into solid state, grinding, heating, extracting wet residue in high-pressure boiler with carbon dioxide, distilling and post treating.
AU WANG Y
AE GOLDEN TECHNOLOGY GROUP LTD (GOLD-Non-standard)
GA 2020A7440Y
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting essential oils comprises freezing the raw materials inside the refrigeration equipment into a solid state by controlling the refrigeration time of the refrigeration equipment, grinding the solid raw materials to obtain solid slag, heating the solid slag to obtain a mixed puree, separating material of the mixed raw slurry to obtain solid residue wet material and a mixed liquid, controlling the pressure and temperature of the high-pressure boiler, then controlling the state of carbon dioxide in the high-pressure boiler, extracting the wet residue in the high-pressure boiler with carbon dioxide in different states to obtain the first essential oil, distilling the mixed liquid through a distillation boiler to obtain the second essential oil and mixing first essential oil and the second essential oil to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting essential oils.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the extraction efficiency and yield of essential oils and has controlled refrigeration time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The method comprises detecting the first ambient temperature in real time and the preset first temperature threshold, controlling the working time of the compressor of the refrigeration equipment, so that the real-time detected first ambient temperature is within the first temperature threshold range and freezing the raw materials in the refrigeration equipment into a solid state, receiving the first ambient temperature in the refrigeration equipment collected by the first temperature probe, controlling the compressor to start when the first ambient temperature is within the first temperature threshold range until the first ambient temperature is lower than the first temperature threshold range, then controlling the compressor to shut down, heating the solid crushed slag, controlling the electric control valve of the refrigeration equipment when the first ambient temperature is higher than a preset second temperature threshold range, discharging the mixed puree into the material separation equipment, separating to obtain a solid residue wet material and a mixed liquid includes, controlling the speed of the material separation equipment based on the preset speed value, and centrifuging the mixed puree in the material separation equipment to obtain wet residue and the mixed liquid, controlling the state of carbon dioxide in the high-pressure boiler by controlling the pressure and temperature of the high-pressure boiler includes: detecting the pressure of the high-pressure boiler in real time, controlling the electric control valve of the high-pressure boiler to open so that liquid carbon dioxide can be injected into the high-pressure boiler, when the pressure of the high-pressure boiler is within the preset first pressure threshold range, heating the high-pressure boiler by electric heating device, so that the carbon dioxide in the high-pressure boiler is in a supercritical state, extracting the wet residue from the carbon dioxide in a supercritical state to obtain extract, controlling the electric control valve of the high-pressure boiler to close, controlling the electric heating device to stop heating, opening pressure relief valve of the high-pressure boiler, so that the carbon dioxide in the extraction liquid is vaporized to obtain the first essential oil. The heating is high-pressure boiler by electric heating device, and the steps of making the carbon dioxide in the high-pressure boiler in supercritical state comprises receiving the second ambient temperature in the high-pressure boiler collected by the second temperature probe in real time, controlling the electric heating device to turn on so that the carbon dioxide in the high-pressure boiler is in a supercritical state when the second ambient temperature is within the preset third temperature threshold range, controlling the activation of the ultrasonic generator in the high-pressure boiler when the carbon dioxide in the high-pressure boiler is in a supercritical state, performing ultrasonic treatment on the wet residue, opening when the pressure relief valve of the high-pressure boiler, and controlling the ultrasonic generator in the high-pressure boiler to turn off. The step of obtaining the second essential oil includes: controlling the start of the steam generator in the distillation boiler, generating steam in the distillation boiler, heating the distillation boiler, the mixed liquid is steam distilled by the steam to obtain hot steam of the distillate, condensing the hot steam to obtain the distillate, allowing to stand and stratifying the distillate to obtain the second essential oil, controlling the activation of the ultrasonic atomizer in the distillation boiler, and spraying the mixed liquid in the form of dispersed droplets into the distillation boiler through the ultrasonic atomizer. The method further comprises performing real-time detection of the pressure in the distillation boiler, controlling the start of the vacuum pump of the distillation boiler to suck hot steam in the distillation boiler when the pressure in the distillation boiler is within the preset second pressure threshold range, and condensing the sucked hot steam to obtain the distillate.

   LIN J

AU KUANG Y
   HAN L
   SUN X
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 202080690D
AB    NOVELTY - Copper nanoelectrode having high-index crystal                surface comprises conductive substrate and copper                nanowire aggregate loaded on the surface of                conductive substrate, where the copper nanowire has                a face-centered cubic crystal structure and                high-index crystal face, and the high-index crystal                face includes (200), (220), (222), (311), (310),                (320), (331), (400), (510), (511), (533), (610) and                (755).
   USE - The copper nanoelectrode is useful for                electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide                (clsimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The copper nanoelectrode: improves selectivity                of high carbon products including ethylene, ethanol                and isopropanol; and inhibits side reactions when                used for electrochemical reduction of carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing copper nanoelectrode, comprising (i)                adding copper salt solution into alkali solution,                uniformly mixing, adding ethylenediamine, then                adding hydrazine solution into mixed solution and                reacting to obtain copper nanowire dispersion                liquid, and (ii) coating copper nanowire dispersion                liquid obtained in step (i) on conductive                substrate, drying to obtain copper nanowire-coated                conductive substrate and processing using square                wave potential method for a certain period of time                to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The conductive                  substrate is carbon paper, carbon cloth, copper                  foil, copper foam and/or titanium foam. The                  copper nanowire has a length of 10-200 mu m and                  length-to-diameter ratio of more than 1000. The                  surface of copper nanowire has a stepped                  morphology. The electrolyte of the square wave                  potential processing process is potassium                  bicarbonate solution, sodium bicarbonate                  solution, potassium carbonate solution, sodium                  carbonate solution, potassium sulfate solution,                  sodium sulfate solution, potassium chloride                  solution, potassium bromide solution, sodium                  bromide solution or sodium chloride solution.                  Preferred Method: The square wave potential                  method adopts three-electrode system. In step                  (ii), the working electrode is copper                  nanowire-coated conductive substrate, the                  reference electrode is silver/silver chloride                  electrode, the counter electrode is carbon rod,                  carbon paper or carbon cloth, the square wave                  potential processing time is more than 5 minutes,                  the square wave potential switching frequency is                  more than 2 Hz, the absolute value of the current                  density at high potential of working electrode is                  lower than the absolute value of the current                  density at low potential, the high potential of                  the working electrode is more than 0.15 V, and                  the low potential is lower than 0 V when compared                  with the standard hydrogen electrode.
