AB    NOVELTY - Hydrophobic modified fracturing fluid proppant                is claimed. The hydrophobic modified fracturing                fluid proppant is obtained by mixing and reacting                proppant and modifier, where the modifier is erucyl                chloride (I).
   USE - The hydrophobic modified fracturing fluid                proppant is useful in fracturing and reforming                shale oil and gas reservoirs in liquid or                supercritical carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrophobic modified fracturing fluid                proppant has simple operation steps, excellent                hydrophobic effect and overcomes disadvantages of                common proppant in non-polar carbon dioxide                fracturing fluid system i.e. slow dispersion, easy                agglomeration and fast settling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Hydrophobic modified fracturing fluid proppant                is claimed. The hydrophobic modified fracturing                fluid proppant is obtained by mixing and reacting                proppant and modifier, where the modifier is erucyl                chloride of formula (I). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                also included for preparing hydrophobic modified                fracturing fluid proppant comprising (i)                pretreating proppant, (ii) preparing erucyl                chloride, and (iii) mixing pre-treated proppant                with modifier, adding into anhydrous                tetrahydrofuran, reacting at 35-50 degrees C for                3-8 hours, filtering, washing with absolute                ethanol, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pretreatment method of                  proppant comprises placing proppant in mixed                  solution of hydrogen peroxide and concentrated                  sulfuric acid, and reacting at 60-80 degrees C                  for 0.5-3 hours, washing to neutrality and drying                  for use. The preparation method of erucyl                  chloride comprises mixing sulfoxide chloride and                  erucic acid, adding catalyst, and stirring the                  reaction at 70-80 degrees C for 5-10 hours,                  distilling off light components under reduced                  pressure. Preferred Components: The mass                  concentration of concentrated sulfuric acid is                  98-98.3%, and the mass concentration of hydrogen                  peroxide is 30-32%. The volume ratio between                  concentrated sulfuric acid and hydrogen peroxide                  is 1-5:50; and total volume of concentrated                  sulfuric acid and hydrogen peroxide is greater                  than the volume of proppant. The catalyst is N,                  N-dimethylformamide. The mass ratio between                  proppant and modifier after pretreatment is                  1:0.04-0.10. The particle size of proppant is                  20-40 mesh.
DC E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).)

TI Power transformer comprises main body provided with radiating device, group of terminal pillar, bracket, oil chamber, oil pipe, and interlayer filled with compressed carbon dioxide.
AU TAO S
AE TAO S (TAOS-Individual)
GA 202024378X
AB    NOVELTY - A power transformer comprises main body (1) provided with a radiating device (11). The top portion of the main body is provided with a group of terminal pillar (12). The top of the main body one side bracket is fixedly connected with an oil chamber (13). The oil chamber is connected with the inner portion of the main body by an oil pipe. The oil chamber is arranged with an interlayer. The interlayer is filled with compressed carbon dioxide. The oil chamber is protected by compressed carbon dioxide, so that the oil chamber is isolated from oxygen. The possibility of deflagration after the oil chamber is heated is increased and guarantees safe and stable operation of the transformer.
   USE - Power transformer.
   ADVANTAGE - The transformer reduces combustion of the transformer oil, ensures safety, and avoids greater safety accidents.

TI Manufacturing methionine by reacting            3-methylmercaptopropionaldehyde and hydrocyanic acid            with carbon dioxide and ammonia, hydrolyzing,            crystallizing, supplying liquid containing methionine            salt and stripping gas and removing ammonia.
AU MIZUNO T
   YAMASHITA D
   KOIZUMI Y
   IZUNAGA Y
AE SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
   SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
   SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
   SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
   SUMITOMO CHEM CO LTD (SUMO-C)
GA 201954024P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for manufacturing methionine involves                (i) carrying out a removal step for reacting                3-methylmercaptopropionaldehyde and hydrocyanic                acid or a compound obtained by reacting                3-methylmercaptopropionaldehyde and hydrocyanic                acid with carbon dioxide and ammonia and obtaining                a liquid comprising                5-(2-methylmercaptoethyl)hydantoin, (ii)                hydrolyzing 5-(2-methylmercaptoethyl)hydantoin to                obtain a liquid containing a methionine salt, (iii)                crystallizing by introducing carbon dioxide into                the liquid containing a methionine salt to                precipitate methionine, (iv) separating                solid-liquid of a slurry of methionine obtained by                crystallizing into a cake and a mother liquid of                the methionine, (v) supplying the liquid containing                the methionine salt to a stripping tower from its                top portion (vi) supplying a stripping gas to the                tower from its bottom portion and (vii) removing                ammonia by stripping, where the stripping gas                comprising a process gas generated by the                methionine manufacturing process.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing                methionine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enhances efficiency of ammonia                removal and reduces usage amount of heating                steam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves                  carrying out bubbling process on the liquid                  containing 5-(2-methylmercaptoethyl)hydantoin by                  blowing an inert gas and carrying out                  concentration process on the mother liquid, where                  the stripping gas comprises at least one process                  gas chosen from a process gas generated in the                  bubbling process, a process gas generated in the                  concentration process (preferred) and a process                  gas generated in hydrolysis process.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN PL422202-A1
TI Preparing activated carbon for adsorption of carbon dioxide involves mixing dried activated carbon source with aqueous activator solution or wet activated carbon source with solid activator, and carbonizing under chemically inert atmosphere.
AU SERAFIN J
   LEWICKA K
   MICHALKIEWICZ B
AE UNIV ZACHODNIOPOMORSKI TECHNOLOGICZNY (UZAC-C)
GA 201913888M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing activated carbon for adsorption of carbon dioxide comprises: mixing in a weight ratio of 1:0.1-5 of dried activated carbon source with an aqueous activator solution or a wet activated carbon source with a solid activator, where the activator is potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, sodium carbonate and/or potassium carbonate; and leaving the mixture to stand for 0-24 hours, drying, carbonizing under a chemically inert atmosphere, washing and drying, cooling, washing the obtained product with distilled water until neutral, treating with hydrochloric acid and again washing with distilled water until neutral and drying the obtained active carbon. The active carbon source is nettle (Urtica dioica). The carbonization is carried out at a temperature of 500-1000 degrees C. The inert gas is chemically fed at a rate of 0.5-50 l/hour. The chemically inert gas is nitrogen or noble gas.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing activated carbon for adsorption of carbon dioxide.

PN CN109054043-A; CN109054043-B
TI Yttrium-(III)-based six-nuclear rare-earth cluster metal-organic framework material useful as catalyst for catalyzing carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction.
AU HAN Z
   CHEN Z
   LIU L
AE UNIV LIAONING (UNLN-C)
GA 201900897U
AB    NOVELTY - Yttrium-(III)-based six-nuclear rare-earth cluster metal-organic framework material is claimed. The metal-organic framework material is prepared by (i) adding yttrium nitrate hexahydrate, 2',5'-dimethylterphenyl-4,4''-dicarboxylic acid (L), O-fluorobenzoic acid, N,N-dimethylformamide and absolute ethanol in container, and stirring at room temperature for 20-30 minutes, (ii) sealing container, placing in oven at 100-120 degrees C for 2-4 days, and (iii) slowly cooling mixture to room temperature, allowing to stand, washing obtained crystal with N,N-dimethylformamide, filtering, and drying to obtain product.
   USE - The yttrium-(III)-based six-nuclear rare-earth cluster metal-organic framework material is useful as catalyst for catalyzing carbon dioxide cycloaddition reaction (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use method of yttrium-(III)-based six-nuclear rare-earth cluster metal-organic framework material as catalyst comprising adding metal-organic framework material in reactor containing the epoxide and the cocatalyst, introducing carbon dioxide, and heating at 25 degrees C at 0.1 MPa, and stirring.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The yttrium (III)-based six-nuclear rare earth cluster metal organic framework material is cubic crystal system having space group of Fm3m. The epoxide is propylene oxide. The cocatalyst is tetrabutyl ammonium bromide. The amount of catalyst is 40-70 mg/1.5 ml epoxide and cocatalyst is 0.2-0.4 g/1.5 ml epoxide. Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (1) adding 0.040 mmol yttrium nitrate hexahydrate, 0.020 mmol 2',5'-dimethylterphenyl-4,4''-dicarboxylic acid (L), 0.0675 mmol O-fluorobenzoic acid, 2 ml N,N-dimethylformamide and 0.5 ml absolute ethanol in container, stirring at room temperature for 20-30 minutes, sealing, placing in an oven at 100-120 degrees C for 2-4 days, (2) slowly cooling mixture to room temperature, allowing to stand, washing obtained crystal with N,N-dimethylformamide, filtering, and drying to obtain product. In the step (ii), raising oven temperature at 4-8 degrees C/minute speed. In the step (iii), slowly cooling to room temperature at 20 degrees C/hour speed, and allowing to stand at room temperature for one day.

PN CN108753389-A
TI Purification of biogas, involves water-cooling to remove water vapor, foam and other dirt from biogas fermentation process of anaerobic microorganisms, washing, desulfurizing, performing carbon dioxide aeration and applying water adsorption.
AU ZHANG Q
   HAO B
   MENG X
   SUN W
AE ANHUI GOLDEN SEED WINERY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201888542H
AB    NOVELTY - The purification method of biogas involves (1) water-cooling to remove water vapor, foam and other dirt from biogas fermentation process of anaerobic microorganisms, (2) washing and desulfurizing, performing carbon dioxide aeration, applying water adsorption and absorption of hydrogen sulfide to achieve oxygenation effect, (3) performing gas-water separation so as to reduce the water content in the biogas, (4) performing mist separation to reduce the water content in the biogas, (5) performing dry desulfurization and biochemical reaction by desulfurizing agent so as to realize desulfurization, (6) storing pressure to overcome the resistance and loss of methane in conveying process, and (7) performing mist separation, reducing the methane content of the biogas to reduce corrosion during end use.
   USE - Purification method of biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables purification of biogas with stable operation, no side effects, high desulfurization and acid gas carbon dioxide aeration efficiency, and reduced biogas purification treatment operation cost and investment cost, by simple process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The water cooling temperature is controlled at 15-25 degrees C in the step (1). In the step (6), the pressure is 4000-7000 Pa. Preferred Composition: After the gas-water separation in the step (3) is completed, the water content in the biogas is about 20-30%. After the mist separation is completed in the step (4), the methane content is about 10-15%. Preferred Process: The biogas in the step (5) is dehydrated again before passing through desulfurizing agent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The desulfurizing agent is ferric oxide. Preferred Property: The ferric oxide is in the form of particles.

PN WO2018195182-A2; US2020048086-A1; WO2018195182-A3
TI Operating a system involves utilizing an            electrical power plant, an electrolyser connected to a            regional electrical power grid, and a hydrocarbon            oxidation device, and then providing water and/or            carbon dioxide to the electrolyser.
AU MOORE J
AE MOORE J (MOOR-Individual)
   MOORE J (MOOR-Individual)
GA 201883980P
AB    NOVELTY - Operating a system involves utilizing an                electrical power plant, an electrolyser connected                to a regional electrical power grid, and a                hydrocarbon oxidation device, providing water                and/or carbon dioxide to the electrolyser,                providing electricity from the regional electrical                power grid to the electrolyser for electrolysis of                the water and/or carbon dioxide to produce oxygen                and providing the oxygen from the electrolyser to                the hydrocarbon oxidation device for the oxidation                of a hydrocarbon.
   USE - Method for operating a system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a system, which comprises an air                separation unit, a hydrocarbon reformer, and a                Haber-Bosch process unit, where the air separation                unit provides nitrogen to the Haber-Bosch process                unit and hydrocarbon reformer unit provides                hydrogen to the Haber-Bosch process unit; and
   (2) a vehicle, which comprises a fuel tank                containing methanol, a heat exchanger arranged to                convert the methanol into syngas and a solid oxide                fuel cell arranged to receive the syngas and                generate electricity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The power plant has a                  utilization rate of less than 50% of its                  availability when the marginal price of                  electricity on the regional electrical power grid                  is less than two times the regional wholesale                  cost of natural gas. The electrical power plant                  has a utilization rate of less than 50% of its                  availability when the marginal price of                  electricity on the regional electrical power grid                  is less than three times the regional wholesale                  cost of natural gas. The electrolyser has a                  utilization rate of less than 50% of its                  availability when the marginal price of                  electricity on the regional electrical power grid                  is more than one and a half times the regional                  wholesale cost of natural gas. The electrolyser                  has a utilization rate of less than 50% of its                  availability when the marginal price of                  electricity on the regional electrical power grid                  is more than two times the regional wholesale                  cost of natural gas. The heat from the electrical                  power plant is provided to the electrolyser for                  heat-assisted electrolysis. The electricity from                  the electrical power plant is provided to the                  electrolyser when supply of electricity from the                  electrical power plant exceeds other electricity                  demand. The hydrocarbon oxidation device at least                  partially oxidizes the hydrocarbon to produce                  hydrogen or syngas. The hydrogen or syngas is                  provided to a solid oxide fuel cell. The                  hydrocarbon oxidation device is an auto-thermal                  reformer or hydrocarbon gasifier. The hydrocarbon                  is methane. The hydrocarbon is coal or biomass.                  The oxy-fueled power plant is an Allam cycle                  power plant. The electrical power plant is a                  nuclear power plant, a solar thermal or CPV power                  plant, or geothermal power plant. The electrical                  power plant has an electricity generating                  capacity of at least 50 megawatts. PN CN108265297-A; CN108265297-B
TI Removing supercritical fluid of artificial polycrystalline diamond composite sheet involves placing inorganic acid solution in lining of reaction kettle, and then putting magnetic stirrer into the lining of reaction kettle.
AU XU S
   WU R
   BI Y
   CUI W
   HAN Z
   WANG H
AE UNIV HENAN TECHNOLOGY (UYHY-C)
GA 201857519G
AB    NOVELTY - Removing supercritical fluid of artificial polycrystalline diamond composite sheet involves placing 5 to 15 ml of the inorganic acid solution in a 150 ml lining (polytetrafluoroethylene) of the reaction kettle. The magnetic stirrer is put into the lining (polytetrafluoroethylene) of the reaction kettle. The polycrystalline diamond composite sheet is placed in a basket lining the high pressure reactor to seal the reaction vessel. The temperature of the reaction kettle is adjusted to 80-150 degrees C. The carbon dioxide is injected carbon dioxide to the preset pressure through a high pressure DB-80 single cylinder pump. The magnetic stirrer is started and dipping reaction time is controlled for 8-24 hours. The reaction kettle is cooled to 50 degrees C. After completion, the carbon dioxide is slowly discharged into a container filled with water through an exhaust valve.
   USE - Method for removing supercritical fluid of artificial polycrystalline diamond composite sheet.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to remove supercritical fluid of artificial polycrystalline diamond composite sheet in a cost-effective manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic acid is hydrochloric acid, nitric acid or a hydrofluoric acid. The supercritical fluid is supercritical carbon dioxide. Preferred Conditions: The mass fraction of the inorganic acid is 20 to 35 wt.%. The pressure of the supercritical fluid is 7.5-15.0mpa.

AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of precipitated calcium carbonate                involves (a) pulverizing the waste concrete fine                powder to prepare a waste concrete powder, (b)                dissolving the waste concrete powder in water and                separating the solution (S1) and the residue, (c)                dissolving the residue in an acid solution, and                separating the solution (S2) and the residue, (d)                adding an oxidizing agent and a coagulant chosen                from polyacrylamide, iron sulfate and iron chloride                to the solutions (S1) and (S2) to precipitate a                metal salt and obtain a leaching mother liquor, (e)                adding a heavy metal remover and a pH adjuster to                the leaching mother liquor to precipitate and                separate the residual metal salt to obtain a                calcium mother liquor, (f) performing a multi-stage                carbonation reaction of the calcium mother liquid                and carbon dioxide at least twice or more and (g)                filtering and drying the calcium carbonate slurry                produced by the carbonation reaction to obtain                precipitated calcium carbonate.
   USE - Manufacture of precipitated calcium carbonate                using waste concrete fine powder generated during                urban redevelopment and dismantling and new                construction of buildings.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical manufacture of                precipitated calcium carbonate. The method                contributes to environmental protection by                recycling the waste concrete fine powder, which is                building waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of precipitated calcium carbonate                involves (a) pulverizing the waste concrete fine                powder to prepare a waste concrete powder, (b)                dissolving the waste concrete powder in water and                separating the solution (S1) and the residue, (c)                dissolving the residue in an acid solution, and                separating the solution (S2) and the residue, (d)                adding an oxidizing agent and a coagulant to the                solutions (S1) and (S2) to precipitate a metal salt                and obtain a leaching mother liquor, (e) adding a                heavy metal remover and a pH adjuster to the                leaching mother liquor to precipitate and separate                the residual metal salt to obtain a calcium mother                liquor, (f) performing a multi-stage carbonation                reaction of the calcium mother liquid and carbon                dioxide at least twice or more and (g) filtering                and drying the calcium carbonate slurry produced by                the carbonation reaction to obtain precipitated                calcium carbonate. In step (d), the coagulant is at                least one chosen from polyacrylamide, iron sulfate                and iron chloride. In step (e), the heavy metal                removing agent is at least one chosen from sodium                hydrogen sulfide and sodium sulfide, and the pH                adjusting agent is aqueous ammonia.

TI Preparing calcium carbonate oligomer reinforced            siloxane modified polyvinyl alcohol solution useful for            protection of sandstone cultural relics, comprises e.g.            dissolving polyvinyl alcohol in water, stirring,            centrifuging, pouring off supernatant, adding ethanol,            sonicating, and allowing to stand.
AU DONG S
   YAN S
   HE L
   WEI Z
   LIANG J
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2022B6533G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium carbonate oligomer                reinforced siloxane modified polyvinyl alcohol                solution comprises dissolving polyvinyl alcohol in                water, and stirring to obtain an aqueous solution                of polyvinyl alcohol, dissolving tetramethyl                orthosilicate and dimethyldimethoxysiloxane in                absolute ethanol, and stirring to obtain silane                oligomer, dissolving anhydrous calcium chloride in                anhydrous ethanol, stirring until the solution is                clear and transparent, adding triethylamine                solution, continuously stirring and introducing                carbon dioxide gas until the solution changes from                turbid state to translucent state, and continuously                stirring to obtain amorphous calcium carbonate                solution, centrifuging the amorphous calcium                carbonate solution, pouring off the supernatant,                adding ethanol, and sonicating to obtain an                amorphous calcium carbonate oligomer, mixing the                silane-modified polyvinyl alcohol solution with the                amorphous calcium carbonate oligomer, stirring and                allowing to stand to obtain product.
   USE - The calcium carbonate oligomer reinforced                siloxane modified polyvinyl alcohol solution is                useful for the protection of sandstone cultural                relics, and also used as an adhesive or                reinforcement agent for bonding, reinforcement and                protection of sandstone cultural relics                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium carbonate oligomer reinforced                siloxane modified polyvinyl alcohol solution has                high-strength water/salt environment resistance and                long protection period, protects sandstone cultural                relics to maintain the original appearance for a                long time under high temperature and high humidity                environment, and reduces ability of water/salt                infiltration, erosion and damage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing calcium carbonate oligomer                reinforced siloxane modified polyvinyl alcohol                solution comprises dissolving polyvinyl alcohol in                water, and stirring to obtain an aqueous solution                of polyvinyl alcohol, dissolving tetramethyl                orthosilicate and dimethyldimethoxysiloxane in                absolute ethanol, and stirring to obtain silane                oligomer, dissolving anhydrous calcium chloride in                anhydrous ethanol, stirring until the solution is                clear and transparent, adding triethylamine                solution, continuously stirring and introducing                carbon dioxide gas until the solution changes from                turbid state to translucent state, and continuously                stirring to obtain amorphous calcium carbonate                solution, centrifuging the amorphous calcium                carbonate solution, pouring off the supernatant,                adding ethanol, and sonicating to obtain an                amorphous calcium carbonate oligomer, mixing the                polyvinyl alcohol aqueous solution with the silane                oligomer, stirring until the solution is in a clear                and transparent state to obtain a silane-modified                polyvinyl alcohol solution, mixing the                silane-modified polyvinyl alcohol solution with the                amorphous calcium carbonate oligomer, stirring and                allowing to stand until the solution is clear and                transparent to obtain calcium carbonate                oligomer-enhanced siloxane-modified polyvinyl                alcohol solution.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                calcium carbonate oligomer reinforced siloxane                modified polyvinyl alcohol solution useful for                protection of sandstone cultural relics.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the method, the mass                  fraction of the polyvinyl alcohol aqueous                  solution is 5-10 wt.%. In the method, the mass                  fraction of the silane oligomer is 7.5-12.5 wt.%,                  the mass fraction of the methyl orthosilicate is                  4.4-8.8 wt.%, and the mass fraction of the                  dimethyldimethoxysiloxane is 3.1-5.6 wt.%. In the                  method, the mass fraction of the amorphous                  calcium carbonate oligomer is 0.11-0.34 wt.%. In                  the method, the mass fraction of the                  silane-modified polyvinyl alcohol solution is                  6.25-11.25 wt.%. In the method, the mass fraction                  of the calcium carbonate oligomer-enhanced                  siloxane-modified polyvinyl alcohol solution is                  3.26-5.73 wt.%.Preferred Components: The calcium carbonate                  oligomer-enhanced siloxane-modified polyvinyl                  alcohol solution is infiltrated from the                  weathered layer of sandstone cultural relics by a                  drop coating method for penetration reinforcement                  and protection. The calcium carbonate                  oligomer-enhanced siloxane-modified polyvinyl                  alcohol solution is mixed with sandstone cultural                  relic powder by grouting reinforcement method,                  where the mixture in a mud state is formed, and                  the mixture in a mud state is poured into the                  cracks and/or hollows between layers of sandstone                  cultural relics for bonding, reinforcement and                  protection.

TI Agrochemical adjuvant composition used for controlling harmful organisms, comprises drift reducing ingredients, rain-fastness additives, spreading agents, uptake enhancing agents, formulants and carriers.
AU FAERS M
   SATO Y
AE BAYER AG (FARB-C)
   BAYER CROPSCI KK (FARB-C)
   BAYER AG (FARB-C)
   BAYER CROPSCIENCE KK (FARB-C)
GA 2022650799
AB    NOVELTY - Agricultural adjuvant composition for tank-mixture with crop protection products, comprises (a) drift reducing ingredients, (b) optionally rain-fastness additives, (c) spreading agents, (d) uptake enhancing agents, (e) other formulants and (f) optionally carriers to 100%.
   USE - The agrochemical adjuvant composition is used in application of the agrochemical compounds for controlling harmful organisms. The agrochemical adjuvant composition is used in tank mixes and for application on plants or crops with textured leaf surfaces (all claimed) such as wheat, barley, rice, rapeseed, soybean and cabbage. The composition is used for foliar application at low spray volumes and controlling agricultural pests, weeds or diseases caused by fungi. The weeds include e.g. Abutilon theophrasti, Datura stramonium, Galium aparine, Ipomoea purpurea, Polygonum lapathifolium, Portulaca oleracea, Senecio vulgaris, Sida spinosa, Sinapis arvensis, Solanum nigrum, Stellaria media, Xanthium orientale, Cyperus rotundus and Amaranthus retroflexus.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition increases the spray droplet size. The composition requires less energy to transport and apply the spray liquid, is faster both from quicker filling of the spray tank and faster application, reduces carbon dioxide generation from both the reduced volume of spray liquid to transport and from the use of smaller and lighter vehicles. The composition reduces soil compaction damage and enables cheaper application systems. The composition improves the efficiency of spray application of agricultural products onto target plants. The composition reduces off-target drift, increases the retention on leaves, increases the wetting, coverage and uptake, and reduces the wash-off by rainfall. The composition provides lower carbon dioxide emissions. The composition produces sufficient food in a safe and sustainable way. The composition enhances the safety, quality and yield of crop product. The composition significantly reduces the drift of the active ingredients, both when sprayed, while at the same time preferably reducing high volumes of spray liquid and reducing the weight of the equipment required to apply the product. The composition improves formulation stability and ensures simplified manufacture, less cost of goods as well as less impact on the environment. The composition provides good leaf coverage, uptake and biological efficacy against fungicidal pathogens, weeds and pests, and provides good rain-fastness. The composition reduces the gravitational separation of the dispersed active ingredient. The penetration rate of tebuconazole was tested in apple cuticle. The result showed that the penetration rate of tebuconazole was 54% at 10 l/ha spray volume when mixed with the adjuvant composition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of the formulation in application of agrochemical compounds for controlling harmful organisms, where the composition is applied by unmanned aerial vehicle, unmanned guided vehicles or pulse-width-module.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Composition: The agricultural adjuvant compositions comprises 5-25%, preferably 12-18% component (a), 0.1-5%, preferably 0.5-1.5% component (b), 25-55%, preferably 33-52% component (c), 0.5-20%, preferably 1.5-18% component (d), 0.01-2%, preferably 0.5-1.5% component (e3) and balance of component (e4) to make up to 100%. Preferred Components: The composition does not contain filler (f) and the composition is free of water. The active ingredient comprises trifloxystrobin, bixafen, prothioconazole, inpyrfluxam, isoflucypram, fluopicolide, fluopyram, fluoxapiprolin, isotianil, spirotetramat, tetraniliprole, ethiprole, imidacloprid, deltamethrin, flupyradifuron, spidoxamat, triafamone, tembotrione, thiencarbazone-methyl, isoxadifen-ethyl and cyprosulfamat. Preferred Method: The component (b) is applied at a rate of 0.01-50 g/ha, preferably 1-30 g/ha. The component (c) is applied at a rate of 5-150 g/ha, preferably 10-60 g/ha.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The component (c) comprises polyalkylene oxide modified heptamethyltrisiloxanes, dioctylsulfosuccinate, alcohol ethoxylates and ethoxylated diacetylene-diols with 1-6 ethylene oxide and ethoxylated, propoxylated 3,5,5-trimethyl-1-hexanol, preferably polyalkylene oxide modified heptamethyltrisiloxanes, dioctylsulfosuccinate and ethoxylated diacetylene-diols with 1-6 ethylene oxide. The component (d) comprises ethoxylated coconut alcohols, ethoxylated branched alcohols, propoxy-ethoxylated alcohols, ethoxylated mono or diesters of glycerin comprising fatty acids with 8-18C and an average of 10-40 ethylene oxide units, or ethoxylated oleic acid. The component (e) comprises antifoam substance (e3) and antifreeze agent (e4).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The component (b) is mandatory. The component (a) comprises poly(ethylene oxides) having an average molecular weight of 0.5-12 million g/mol, hydroxypropyl guar, vegetable oils and vegetable oil esters and diesters. The component (b) is an emulsion polymer or polymer dispersion with glass transition temperature of -100 to 30 degrees C, where the polymer is a copolymer of an acrylate and a styrene. The acrylate comprises 2-ethyl-hexyl acrylate, butyl acrylate, sec-butyl acrylate, ethyl acrylate, methyl acrylate, acrylic acid, acrylamide, iso-butyl acrylate, methyl methacrylate or their combinations and the styrene comprises styrene, tert-butyl styrene, para-methyl styrene or their combinations.
   ACTIVITY - Pesticide; Herbicide; Fungicide.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - Application of the agrochemical formulation is at a spray volume of 1-25 1/ha, preferably 5-15 1/ha by spraying (claimed), dipping, vaporizing, atomizing, broadcasting, painting, pouring or injecting.
   EXAMPLE - Typical composition comprised: 50 wt.% Silwet 806 (RTM: 3-(Methyl-bis(trimethylsilyloxy)silyl)propan-1-ol); 16.30 wt.% AGNIQUE ME 18 RDF (RTM: Rape seed oil methyl ester); 1 wt.% SAG 1572 (RTM: Dimethyl siloxanes and silicones); 10 wt.% Synperonic PE/L62(RTM: Block copolymer of polyethylene oxide and polypropylene oxide); 2.20 wt.% Synperonic 13/7-85 (RTM: Alcohols, 11-14C-iso-, 13C-rich, ethoxylated); 1.50 wt.% Crovol CR70G (RTM: fats and glyceridic oils, vegetable, ethoxylated); 19 wt.% propylene glycol.

PN US2022016544-A1
TI Dewaxing apparatus for e.g., phytochemical            extraction system comprises column that includes inner            vessel containing liquid solution of phytochemicals and            waxes and/or lipids, and jacket, and sight glass            connected to top or bottom ends of the column.
AU KOGON B D
AE KOGON B D (KOGO-Individual)
GA 2022145534
AB    NOVELTY - A dewaxing apparatus for phytochemical                extraction system (100), comprises a first column                that includes an inner vessel that contains a                liquid solution of (1) phytochemicals and (2) waxes                and/or lipids; and a jacket that surrounds the                inner vessel and includes an inlet port that is                operable to introduce liquid carbon dioxide (CO2)                into the jacket, and the jacket further includes an                openable exhaust port that is operable, when                opened, to cause a first portion of liquid CO2to                boil and a second portion of the liquid CO2to                freeze into dry ice snow which accumulates inside                the jacket, cooling the surface of inner vessel and                causing the waxes and/or lipids to precipitate out                of the solution; a first sight glass connected to                top end of the first column, and has an input port                for introduction of liquid solution into the inner                vessel, and provides a view into the inner vessel;                and a second sight glass connected to the bottom                end of the first column.
   USE - A dewaxing apparatus for phytochemical                extraction system and for dewaxing a liquid                solution of phytochemicals in a dewaxing column                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - By combining the carbon dioxide jacket                refrigeration method and by using cooled, jacketed                column to hold solvent, it is possible to                efficiently and quickly cool solvent down to                operating temperatures, without the difficulty of                working with dry ice. The phytochemical compounds                remain soluble at lower temperatures than the waxes                to be separated. The 'middleman' of cooling a                circulating heat-exchange fluid is removed, thus,                reducing the cost and complexity of the cooling                operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for dewaxing a liquid solution of phytochemicals in                a dewaxing column (2) which involves collecting the                liquid solution in inner vessel of the dewaxing                column, where the liquid solution includes (1)                hydrocarbon solvent, (2) phytochemicals, and (3)                waxes and/or lipids; cooling the liquid solution to                a target temperature of &#8722;40&#8451;;                precipitating the waxes and/or lipids from the                solution by holding the liquid solution at target                temperature for 30-60 minutes; and draining the                solution through the filter stack and into a                collection vessel, where the filter stack separates                the precipitated waxes and/or lipids from the                solution.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the                phytochemical extraction system.
   2Dewaxing column
   3, 4Solvent columns
   5Solvent storage tank
   6Collection pot
   100Phytochemical extraction system

PN CN113753924-A; CN113753924-B
TI Extraction of lithium carbonate and co-production of sodium aluminum silicate involves performing activated water-soluble method by e.g. roasting mixed raw material under high temperature condition to obtain roasted clinker.
AU MA R
   SUN Y
   MA Y
   PAN A
AE PAN A (PANA-Individual)
   BEIJING RUNJIE HAODA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   PAN A (PANA-Individual)
GA 202204934W
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of lithium carbonate and co-production of sodium aluminum silicate involves performing activated water-soluble method by evenly mixing a lithium-rich clay with an alkali and a melting agent, grinding to a size of 100-300 mesh to obtain a mixed raw meal, calcining the mixed raw meal under high temperature conditions to obtain a calcined clinker, placing the calcined clinker in water, filtering and separating the reaction product to obtain a water leaching solution (S1) and a water leaching residue, adding sodium aluminate or sodium silicate to solution (S1), aging, obtaining a precipitate and a carbon separation mother liquor, heating the carbon separation mother liquor, using the obtained crystallization as an alkali to return to the next batch of raw materials in the above process to reduce the consumption of raw materials after being completely evaporated to dryness, washing the precipitate with water, and drying to obtain lithium carbonate product.
   USE - Extraction of lithium carbonate and co-production of sodium aluminum silicate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple and easy operation method, no special requirement for the device, good technical parameter controllability, green and environmentally-friendly, no wastewater generation, low cost, high quality of the obtained product, good economic benefit and develops a new way for development and utilization of lithium-rich clay.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extraction of lithium carbonate and co-production of sodium aluminum silicate involves performing activated water-soluble method by evenly mixing a lithium-rich clay with an alkali and a melting agent, grinding to a size of 100-300 mesh to obtain a mixed raw meal, calcining the mixed raw meal under high temperature conditions to obtain a calcined clinker, placing the calcined clinker in water, filtering and separating the reaction product to obtain a water leaching solution (S1) and a water leaching residue, adding sodium aluminate or sodium silicate to solution (S1) to adjust the concentration of sodium aluminate and sodium silicate, aging, filtering to obtain a water leaching solution (S2) and sodium aluminosilicate precipitate, introducing carbon dioxide gas into solution (S2), adjusting pH of the solution to 8.5-10.5, stopping feeding of carbon dioxide, separating the solution by filtration to obtain a precipitate and a carbon separation mother liquor, heating the carbon separation mother liquor, using the obtained crystallization as an alkali to return to the next batch of raw materials in the above process to reduce the consumption of raw materials after being completely evaporated to dryness, washing the precipitate with water, and drying to obtain lithium carbonate product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of lithium-rich clay and alkali is 1:0.5-1.5. The melting agent is sodium sulfate. The alkali is sodium carbonate. The solid-liquid ratio of calcined clinker and water is 1:3-10. The concentration of carbon dioxide gas is maintained at 30-40%.

PN CN113731460-A
TI Preparing N-P modified acetylene hydrochlorination non-metal catalyst used in acetylene hydrochlorination reaction, involves crushing and screening sawdust or shells serving as raw material, and then soaking sawdust or shells in solution.
AU NIU Q
   XIE R
   ZHAO C
   ZHU R
AE ERDOS HANBO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ERDO-Non-standard)
   INNER MONGOLIA ERDOS POWER METALLURGY (INNE-Non-standard)
GA 2021E7034K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing N-P modified acetylene hydrochlorination non-metal catalyst involves crushing and screening sawdust or shells serving as a raw material, then soaking the sawdust or shells in a sulfuric acid or hydrogen peroxide solution with the mass concentration of 5-10 wt.% for 16 hours, filtering, washing and drying to obtain activated carbon 1. The activated carbon 1 is soaked in a solution containing P for 5-10 hours, and dried at 120degrees Celsius to obtain activated carbon 2. The activated carbon 2 in a solution containing N for 5-10 hours, and dried at 120degrees Celsius to obtain activated carbon 3. The activated carbon 3 is placed under N atmosphere at 500-600degrees Celsius for carbonization for 2-6 hours, then placed under a mixed gas atmosphere of water vapor and carbon dioxide, activated at 700-800degrees Celsius for 1-3 hours to obtain activated carbon 4.
   USE - Method for preparing N-P modified acetylene hydrochlorination non-metal catalyst used in acetylene hydrochlorination reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple preparation, high catalytic efficiency and solves the problem of mercury pollution of industrial low-mercury catalysts.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing N-P modified acetylene hydrochlorination non-metal catalyst involves crushing and screening sawdust or shells serving as a raw material, then soaking the sawdust or shells in a sulfuric acid or hydrogen peroxide solution with the mass concentration of 5-10 wt.% for 16 hours, filtering, washing and drying to obtain activated carbon 1. The activated carbon 1 is soaked in a solution containing P for 5-10 hours, and dried at 120degrees Celsius to obtain activated carbon 2. The activated carbon 2 in a solution containing N for 5-10 hours, and dried at 120degrees Celsius to obtain activated carbon 3. The activated carbon 3 is placed under N atmosphere at 500-600degrees Celsius for carbonization for 2-6 hours, then placed under a mixed gas atmosphere of water vapor and carbon dioxide, activated at 700-800degrees Celsius for 1-3 hours to obtain activated carbon 4. The activated carbon 4 is purified by acid washing with 5 wt.% hydrochloric acid or sulfamic acid or benzenesulfonic acid, and dried to obtain activated carbon 5. The activated carbon 5 is soaked in a mixed solution of urea and PVP or a mixed solution of melamine and polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP) for 6 hours, dried at 120degrees Celsius, and roasted at 400degrees Celsius for 3-10 hours to obtain activated carbon 6. The activated carbon 6 is impregnated with cupric chloride (CuCl2) solution and dried.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The active agent 6 is active carbon for acetylene hydrochlorination non-noble metal catalyst. The specific surface area of the active agent 6 is 900-1500 meter square per gram, the average pore diameter is 1.3-15 nm, the nitrogen content is 2-5 wt.%, and the phosphorus content is 2-5 wt.%. Preferred Components: The P-containing solution is phosphoric acid, ammonium polyphosphate solution and ammonium phosphate. The N-containing solution is ethylenediamine, melamine and urea. Preferred Compositions: The water vapor and the carbon dioxide are mixed in volume ratio of 1:1. The concentration ratio of the urea and PVP is 0.5 mol/L:1 mol/L. The concentration ratio of the melamine and PVP is 0.5 mol/L:1 mol/L. The mass of CuCl2 is accounts for 8-15 wt.% acetylene hydrochlorination catalyst of the N-P modified activated carbon.

PN CN113604531-A
TI Preparing mussel functional lipids and active polypeptides comprises e.g. homogenizing mussel meat, freeze-drying, extracting, enzymatically hydrolysing on defatted mussel residue, inactivating enzyme, separating using membrane separation technology, and vacuum freeze-drying polypeptide solution.
AU ZHOU D
   LIU X
   CHEN K
   JIANG P
   ZHANG X
   WANG X
   YIN F
AE UNIV DALIAN POLYTECHNIC (UYDP-C)
GA 2021D2109R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mussel functional lipids and active polypeptides comprises (i) homogenizing mussel meat at a rotation speed of 6000-8000 revolutions/minute, and freeze-drying in a vacuum to a moisture content of 0-10% to prepare a freeze-dried powder; (ii) extracting for 2-3 hours using supercritical carbon dioxide combined with cosolvent to obtain mussel functional lipids and defatted mussel powder; (iii) selecting a protease to perform enzymatic hydrolysis on the defatted mussel residue, adjusting the pH value to be constant, inactivating the enzyme for 24-36 hours to obtain a mussel mixed peptide; (iv) adding absolute ethanol into the mussel mixed peptide according to the ratio of 1:3-10, allowing to stand for 18-24 hours, centrifuging and adding water; (v) separating using membrane separation technology with a molecular weight cut-off of 3-5 KDa to obtain a polypeptide solution; and (vi) vacuum freeze-drying the polypeptide solution and crushing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing mussel functional lipids and active polypeptides.
   ADVANTAGE - The polypeptide is clear and bright, good for high added value and has less impurity, and avoids the quality deterioration and heat sensitive active component loss in subsequent processing and storing process, and realizes high value and high quality utilization of mussel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing mussel functional lipids and active polypeptides comprises (i) homogenizing mussel meat at a rotation speed of 6000-8000 revolutions/minute, and freeze-drying in a vacuum to a moisture content of 0-10% to prepare a freeze-dried powder; (ii) extracting for 2-3 hours using supercritical carbon dioxide combined with cosolvent to obtain mussel functional lipids and defatted mussel powder; (iii) selecting a protease to perform enzymatic hydrolysis on the defatted mussel residue, adjusting the pH value to be constant, inactivating the enzyme for 24-36 hours to obtain a mussel mixed peptide; (iv) adding absolute ethanol into the mussel mixed peptide according to the ratio of 1:3-10, allowing to stand for 18-24 hours, centrifuging at 10000-15000 revolutions/minute, taking the supernatant, placing at 60-70degrees Celsius to evaporate to 1/5-1/10 of the original volume, adding 3-10 times water; (v) separating using membrane separation technology with a molecular weight cut-off of 3-5 KDa to obtain a polypeptide solution; and (vi) vacuum freeze-drying the polypeptide solution and crushing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the cosolvent is 90-100% ethanol, and the amount is 4-40 wt.% and the mussel functional lipid contains Omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids. In the step (iii), the proteases include pepsin, trypsin, and neutral protease. In the step (iv), the addition amount of the absolute ethanol is 5-10 times.Preferred Method: The vacuum freeze-drying comprises heat preserving at -50degrees Celsius for 2-4 hours, heat preserving at -40degrees Celsius for 3-5 hours, heat preserving at -30degrees Celsius for 3-6 hours, heat preserving at -20degrees Celsius for 5-10 hours, heat preserving at -10degrees Celsius for 5-15 hours, heat preserving at -0degrees Celsius for 10-15 hours, heat preserving at 5degrees Celsius for 10-15 hours, and heat preserving at 10degrees Celsius for 10-15 hours. In the step (i), the freeze-dried powder is made by crushing mussel meat, which is passed through a 40-80 mesh sieve. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction conditions is carried out at 35-55degrees Celsius with fluid pressure of 25-35 Mpa. The use method of cosolvent comprises injecting 90-100% ethanol and continuously extracting for 1-2 hours. The enzymatic hydrolysis conditions of the pepsin include pH 2-4 and temperature 25-40degrees Celsius, the enzymolysis conditions of the trypsin include pH 7.5-9 and temperature 37-45degrees Celsius and the enzymolysis conditions of the neutral protease include pH 7-8 and temperature 37-50degrees Celsius.

AU WANG G
   ZHANG C
AE JIANGSU VADER BOILER CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a control box that is provided with two groups of control clamps distributed on two sides of bearing roller (4). The inner side of bearing plate is provided with a gas storage cavity filled with carbon dioxide, and the bottom end face of bearing plate is provided with two sealing films. The inner side of air storage cavity is suspended with a puncture portion corresponding to two sealing films. One end of puncture portion is passed through the upper portion of the bearing cavity.
   USE - Module type gas central hot water furnace maintenance system for hot water furnace maintenance field.

The puncture portion at the bearing cavity is pressed, the sealing film is pierced, after pressing the puncture portion, and the carbon dioxide overflow in the module is detected. The possibility of the fire is reduced, the hot water furnace is maintained, and the personal safety the staff is improved. The personal safety of the hot water furnace and the person is maintained. The controller operates the micro electric rod to shrink from the locating ear plate. The bearing roller is falling under the action of gravity, so as to trigger the flame-retardant effect of the elastic gas-spilling membrane. The air storage bag is expanded and cracked under the action of stretching and extruding, the inert gas in the inner portion is sprayed out, and the diffusion degree of the fire extinguishing powder is improved. The bearing cavity and end wall of bearing roller are coated with magnetic absorbing layer, which is good for bearing roller smoothly falling to the bearing cavity, and the stability of the connection is improved.

PN CN113521795-A
TI Extracting active substances from rhizoma dioscoreae useful for e.g. enhancing immunity, by peeling and cutting rhizoma dioscoreae, extracting, depressurizing, separating into carbohydrates with lighter and heavier specific gravity and separating active substance with lighter specific gravity.
AU ZHENG C
AE ZHENG C (ZHEN-Individual)
GA 2021C2793D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting active substances from rhizoma dioscoreae by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction involves peeling the rhizoma dioscoreae, cutting into pieces, adding into a raw material barrel, allowing to stand to stabilize the active substance contained in the rhizoma dioscoreae, adding the rhizoma dioscoreae into an extraction pot, extracting, adding into extraction barrel, allowing the rhizoma dioscoreae, then depressurizing, adding into a first separation pot for separation into carbohydrates with a lighter specific gravity in the upper layer and a heavier specific gravity in the lower layer, adding the active substance with a lighter specific gravity of the upper layer into second separation pot, allowing to stand, depressurizing, performing separation, decomposing and filtering circulating solvent for recycling.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting active substances from rhizoma dioscoreae, which is used in combination of glyconutrients for activating cells in body, providing cell nutrients in body, increasing number of leukocytes and lymphocytes in blood and fibroblasts in inner layer of skin, enhancing immunity, promoting synthesis of collagen and skin maintenance and delaying deterioration of cognitive ability of the brain due to age (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and cost-effective, saves time, recycles solvent and has high extraction rate and application value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting active substances from rhizoma dioscoreae by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction involves (a) peeling the rhizoma dioscoreae, cutting into pieces, adding into a raw material barrel, and allowing to stand for at least 10 minutes to stabilize the active substance contained in the rhizoma dioscoreae, (b) adding the rhizoma dioscoreae obtained in step (a) into an extraction pot, where the extraction pot is connected with a solvent circulation pump that provides the solvent to pass through, extracting and adding into extraction barrel, (c) allowing the rhizoma dioscoreae obtained in step (b) in extraction barrel to stand for 30 minutes, then depressurizing, adding into a first separation pot for separation into carbohydrates with a lighter specific gravity in the upper layer and a heavier specific gravity in the lower layer, (d) adding the active substance with a lighter specific gravity of the upper layer into second separation pot, (e) allowing the active substance in second separation pot to stand for 30 minutes, then depressurizing and performing separation and (f) decomposing into active substance components with a specific gravity of less than 1, filtering the circulating solvent through a low-pressure filter and a circulating solvent cooler to condense the solvent into the solvent storage tank for recycling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (a), the rhizoma dioscoreae is purple rhizoma dioscoreae. In step (b), a solvent preheater is arranged between the extraction pot and the solvent circulation pump. In step (c), the active substance component is a cofactor of glutathamine peroxidase with a light specific gravity, where the main components of the glutathamine peroxidase cofactor are quercetin-3-o-rutinose and longevity protein. In step (c), a high pressure filter and a first separation preheater are sequentially arranged in series between the extraction barrel and the first separatiF12on pot. In step (f), a circulating solvent dryer is provided between the circulating solvent cooler and the circulating solvent condenser, where the solvent storage barrel is connected with a vacuum pump and the circulating solvent dryer provides moisture in the circulating solvent to reduce the generation of non-condensable solvent in the system and affect its extraction rate. In step (d), a second preheating separator is provided between the first separating pot and the second separating pot. In step (d), the first separation pot and the second separation pot are respectively connected to the first product barrel (low pressure), the first product pump, the second product barrel, and the second product pump, respectively, where first product pump and second product pump are simultaneously connected to a finished product storage tank. In step (b), the solvent is carbon dioxide solvent.
   ACTIVITY - Immunostimulant; Dermatological; Nootropic.

TI Oxygen-enriched flue gas carbon hydrogen fuel desulfurization of the photocatalytic hydrogen fuel in the reactor comprises first reactor, second reactor and third reactor, where the one side of first reactor is provided with air inlet.
AU ZHAO Y
   WANG J
   XIONG Z
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2021192866
AB    NOVELTY - Oxygen-enriched flue gas carbon hydrogen fuel desulfurization of the photocatalytic hydrogen fuel in the reactor comprises first reactor (1), second reactor (2) and third reactor (3), where the one side of the first reactor is provided with an air inlet (4). The gas to be processed enters the first reactor from the air inlet. The sulfur dioxide gas in the gas to be processed is converted into sulfite ion in the first reactor between the first reactor and the third reactor is provided with a gas channel. The carbon dioxide gas in the gas to be processed enters the third reactor through the gas channel(9). The upper portion of the second reactor and the third reactor are provided with a light source (15), which are used for performing the photocatalytic reaction between the first reactor and the second reactor.
   USE - Oxygen-enriched flue gas carbon hydrogen fuel desulfurization of the photocatalytic hydrogen fuel in the reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxygen-enriched flue gas carbon hydrogen fuel desulfurization of the photocatalytic hydrogen fuel in the reactor has high processing efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using the photocatalytic reactor, which involves adding bicarbonate solution in the first reactor, add bicarbonate, photocatalyst and redox medium in the second reactor, adding photocatalyst and redox medium in the third reactor, passing the gas to be treated into the first reactor, and turning on the light sources above the second reactor and the third reactor, absorbing sulfur dioxide in the gas to be treated by the bicarbonate in the first reactor and converting into sulfite ions and entering the second reactor, using sulfite ions entering the second reactor in the photocatalyst and the redox medium, oxidizing to sulfate ions under the action of, while the redox medium is reduced and enters the third reactor, entering carbon dioxide in the gas to be treated the third reactor from the gas channel, where the photocatalyst and the redox medium in the third reactor undergo a photocatalytic reaction to generate carbon monoxide and hydrocarbons, the redox medium is oxidized, and the oxidized redox medium enters the second reactor, maintaining the charge and mass balance in the second reactor and the third reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a reactor.
   First reactor (1)
   Second reactor (2)
   Third reactor (3)

   Light source (15)

The redox medium added to the second reactor and the third reactor is I-, and the redox medium in the second reactor and the third reactor redox media in the same.

PN CN112280049-A; CN112280049-B
TI Preparing high-strength polycarbosilane for            preparing silicon carbide fiber, by uniformly            dispersing nano gamma-aluminum oxyhydroxide in aqueous            silane solution, distilling, dripping organic solvent,            heating, vacuuming and distilling.
AU WU B
   HOU Z
   WU D
AE JIANGXI XINDA AVIATION TECHNOLOGY NEW (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGXI XINDAHANGKE NEW MATERIAL TECHNOL (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202114651N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing high-strength                polycarbosilane, involves (i) uniformly dispersing                nano gamma -aluminum oxyhydroxide in aqueous                solution of KH550 (RTM:                3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane), and ultrasonically                shaking to obtain a mixture (I), (ii) performing                polydimethylsilane (PDMS) distillation, collecting                fraction at 103 degrees C, drying, dripping organic                solvent and continuously stirring to obtain a                solution (II), (iii) pouring mixture (I) to                solution (II), heating, and stirring to obtain a                mixture (III), (iv) placing mixture (III) in a                reaction kettle, introducing a mixed gas of carbon                dioxide and inert gas, pressurizing to 5-10 MPa,                increasing temperature to 500-520 degrees C                according to a certain temperature program,                maintaining for 12-24 hours, and cooling with                furnace to room temperature to obtain a crude                product (IV), and (v) dissolving crude product (IV)                in an organic solvent, filtering, vacuuming and                distilling under reduced pressure.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                high-strength polycarbosilane for preparing silicon                carbide fiber (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method introduces aluminum oxide whiskers                into polycarbosilane, because aluminum oxide has                excellent heat resistance, when the temperature                reaches 1200 degrees C or higher, it generate a                substance with good fluidity to heal the internal                cracks of the system, where the normal temperature                and high temperature mechanical properties of                silicon carbide fiber prepared with the                polycarbosilane as the precursor improves the                thermal shock performance of the product silicon                carbide fiber.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing high-strength                polycarbosilane, involves (i) uniformly dispersing                nano gamma -aluminum oxyhydroxide in aqueous                solution of KH550 (RTM:                3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane), and ultrasonically                shaking for 0.5-2 hours to obtain a mixture (I),                (ii) performing polydimethylsilane (PDMS)                distillation, collecting the fraction at 103                degrees C, drying, dripping organic solvent and                continuously stirring until the mass ratio of                organic solvent and PDMS is (5-15):100 to obtain a                solution (II), (iii) pouring the mixture (I) to the                solution (II), heating in a water bath at 60-80                degrees C, and stirring for 2-6 hours to obtain a                mixture (III), (iv) placing the mixture (III) in a                reaction kettle, introducing a mixed gas of carbon                dioxide and inert gas, pressurizing to 5-10 MPa,                increasing the temperature to 500-520 degrees C                according to a certain temperature program,                maintaining for 12-24 hours, and cooling with the                furnace to room temperature to obtain a crude                product (IV), and (v) dissolving the crude product                (IV) in an organic solvent, filtering, vacuuming                and distilling under reduced pressure to obtain the                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inert gas used in                  step (iv) comprises nitrogen and argon. Preferred                  Composition: The volume ratio of carbon dioxide                  and the inert gas in step (iv) is 1:(1-3), where                  the total gas flow is 200-300 ml/minute.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent                  used in step (ii) is acetone or xylene. The                  organic solvent used in step (v) is acetone or                  xylene.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves                  dispersing 2-8 pts. wt. KH550 (RTM:                  3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane) in 100 pts. wt.                  distilled water to obtain the aqueous solution of                  KH550 (RTM: 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane), and                  adjusting the pH value to 3-5 with sulfuric acid                  or nitric acid, where the mass ratio of nano                  gamma -aluminum oxyhydroxide and KH550 (RTM:                  3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane) aqueous solution is                  (10-40):100. The heating program in step (iv) is                  maintained by heating at 60-80 degrees C at 1-2                  degrees C/minute, heat-preserving for 1-2 hours,                  heating at 250-300 degrees C at 2-4 degrees                  C/minute, heat-preserving for 24-96 hours,                  heating at 420-460 degrees C at 2-4 degrees                  C/minute, heat-preserving for 12-24 hours,                  heating at 500-520 degrees C at 1-2 degrees                  C/minute, heat-preserving for 12-24 hours, and                  cooling to room temperature with furnace.                  Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of nano and                  PDMS when the mixture (I) and the solution (II)                  are mixed in step (iii) is (2 -5):100.

AE UNIV YIBIN XIHUA RES INST (UYYI-Non-standard)
   UNIV XIHUA (UYXH-C)
GA 202101322W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for continuously extracting bamboo shoot shell pigment and dietary fiber involves (a) mixing bamboo shoot shell powder which is crushed and passed through 60-100 mesh screen with sodium hydroxide solution, centrifuging filtrate, evaporating and crystallizing the supernatant A, (b) drying precipitate B, mixing with a phosphate buffer solution, performing enzymatic hydrolysis, inactivating enzyme, and centrifuging enzymatic hydrolysis slurry, (c) filtering the supernatant C with an ultrafiltration membrane, spray drying, subjecting to the extraction kettle, and with the carrier agent and supercritical carbon dioxide gas, and (d) spray-drying precipitate.
   USE - The method is useful for continuously extracting bamboo shoot shell pigment and dietary fiber for producing bamboo shoot shell pigment, water-soluble dietary fiber and water-insoluble dietary fiber.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for continuously extracting bamboo shoot shell pigment and dietary fiber involves (a) mixing bamboo shoot shell powder which is crushed and passed through 60-100 mesh screen with sodium hydroxide solution in a vacuum packaging bag based on a mass ratio of 1:10-1:30, vacuum-packing, filtering under a pressure of 100-400 MPa, centrifuging filtrate to obtain supernatant A and precipitation B, evaporating and crystallizing the supernatant A to obtain bamboo shoot shell pigment, (b) drying precipitate B, mixing with a phosphate buffer solution in a weight ratio of 15-10:1 containing 1-4 %mass complex enzyme at a pH of 3.5-4.5, performing enzymatic hydrolysis at 35-45 degrees C, inactivating enzyme, and centrifuging enzymatic hydrolysis slurry to obtain supernatant C and precipitate D, (c) filtering the supernatant C with an ultrafiltration membrane having a molecular weight cut-off of 200-500 kDa to obtain an ultrafiltration permeate at an inlet air temperature of 120-180 degrees C, outlet air temperature of 60-80 degrees C, and compressed air flow rate of 500-800 ml, spray drying, subjecting to the extraction kettle, and mixing fluid with the carrier agent and supercritical carbon dioxide gas at a pressure of 25-35 Mpa to obtain water-soluble dietary fiber, and (d) spray-drying precipitate D under the conditions of an inlet air temperature of 120-180 degrees C, an outlet air temperature of 60-80 degrees C, and a compressed air flow rate of 500-800 ml/hour to obtain water-insoluble dietary fiber. The complex enzymes comprises papain, alpha -amylase and amyloglucosidase in a mass ratio of 1-2:2-4:1-3.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The bamboo shoot shell powder and the sodium hydroxide solution are placed under pressure of 100-400 Mpa for 1-4 minutes. The enzymolysis is performed for 1-2 hours. The centrifugation is performed at a rotation speed of 3000-4000 revolution per minute (rpm) for 10-20 minutes. The method involves using 15 wt.% carrier i.e. methanol with respect to powder. The mixed fluid has flow rate of 20-35 l/hour. Preferred Components: The sodium hydroxide solution has concentration of 0.05 mol/l.

PN US2020308334-A1
TI Crosslinked bio-based hydrophilic foam comprises reaction product of cleaned bio-based polyoxyalkylene glycol polyol, isocyanate which is premixed with polyoxyalkylene glycol polyol to create prepolymer mixture, and water.
AU NANOS J I
AE NANOS J I (NANO-Individual)
GA 2020970643
AB    NOVELTY - A crosslinked bio-based hydrophilic foam comprises a reaction product of cleaned bio-based polyoxyalkylene glycol polyol (A) comprising at least 40 mol.% ethylene oxide, 31.80-68.59 wt.% biogenic carbon and less than 15 ppm combined sodium and potassium metals, (B) an isocyanate, and (C) water. The polyol is prepared by (101) adding a metal silicate adsorbent to a bio-based polyoxyalkylene glycol polyol to create a mixture comprising adsorbent, (102) stirring the mixture in a gaseous nitrogen environment, (103) replacing the nitrogen environment with a gaseous carbon dioxide environment, (104) filtering the mixture to separate impurities from the mixture and forming the cleaned bio-based polyoxyalkylene glycol polyol. The isocyanate and cleaned bio-based polyoxyalkylene glycol polyol are premixed to create a prepolymer mixture comprising cleaned bio-based polyoxyalkylene glycol polyol, and water is admixed with mixture to yield the crosslinked bio-based hydrophilic foam.
   USE - Crosslinked bio-based hydrophilic foam.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A crosslinked bio-based hydrophilic foam comprises a reaction product of cleaned bio-based polyoxyalkylene glycol polyol (A) comprising at least 40 wt.% ethylene oxide, 31.80-68.59 wt.% biogenic carbon and less than 15 ppm combined sodium and potassium metals, (B) an isocyanate, and (C) water. The polyol is prepared by adding a metal silicate adsorbent to a bio-based polyoxyalkylene glycol polyol comprising residual sodium metals, residual potassium metals and water to create a mixture comprising 0.5-5.0 wt.% adsorbent, stirring the mixture in a gaseous nitrogen environment, replacing the gaseous nitrogen environment with a gaseous carbon dioxide environment, filtering the mixture to separate impurities from the mixture and forming the cleaned bio-based polyoxyalkylene glycol polyol. The filtering process involves utilizing metal silicate adsorbent to sequester residual sodium metals and residual potassium metals from the mixture comprising less than 15 ppm combined sodium and potassium metals and sequester water from the mixture comprising less than 0.1 wt.% water. The isocyanate and cleaned bio-based polyoxyalkylene glycol polyol are premixed to create a prepolymer mixture comprising 31.80-68.59 wt.% cleaned bio-based polyoxyalkylene glycol polyol, and water is admixed with the prepolymer mixture to yield the crosslinked bio-based hydrophilic foam.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the manufacturing method of bio-based polyurethane foam.
   Addition of adsorbent process (101)
   Stirring process (102)
   Replacing of gaseous environment (103)
   Filtration process (104)
   Mixing process (105)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bio-based polyoxyalkylene glycol polyol is derived from starting material which is bio-based raw material. The bio-based polyoxyalkylene glycol polyol is polymerized from 100% bio-based ethylene oxide. Preferred Process: The bio-based polyoxyalkylene glycol polyol is capped with isocyanate to react all hydroxyl functionality which creates the polyurethane prepolymer, and admixed with water to create the hydrophilic foam.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The bio-based polyoxyalkylene glycol polyol comprises a backbone of prepolymer in a three-dimensional network.

TI Preparation of electrode used as cathode for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide, involves preparing gas diffusion electrode using e.g. copper oxide nanowire/substrate, immersing in concentrated hydrochloric acid solution and drying.
AU ZHONG H
   DIAO G
   PAN L
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV DALIAN (UYDV-C)
GA 202089549Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of electrode involves washing copper mesh and copper foil with one or more of water, ethanol and acetone, washing with 0.5-0.8 M sulfuric acid, leveling, ultrasonically cleaning, drying the substrate under the protection of an inert atmosphere, oxidizing, obtaining copper oxide nanowire/substrate, adding alkaline precipitation agent, reducing agent and additives to copper salt, mixing, magnetically stirring, obtaining mixed solution, immersing the copper oxide nanowire/substrate in the mixed solution in reactor, electroless plating, coating a layer of nanoparticles on surface of nanowire, cooling, drying to get electrode, placing in 0.5 M sulfuric acid electrolyte and using cyclic voltammetry to scan, washing after taking it out, blow drying with inert gas, obtaining gas diffusion electrode, immersing the gas diffusion electrode in a 36-38% concentrated hydrochloric acid solution, cleaning, and blow drying with inert gas.
   USE - Preparation of electrode used as cathode for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses nanowires which are partially reduced to form hole copper, and nano-copper particles with a high specific surface are deposited on the surface to increase active specific surface area of the copper electrode, and increase the ratio of active sites on the edges and corners.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of electrode involves (1) washing copper mesh and copper foil with one or more of water, ethanol and acetone, washing with 0.5-0.8 M sulfuric acid, leveling at preset current density, ultrasonically cleaning, drying the substrate under the protection of an inert atmosphere, (2) placing the treated substrate in a tube furnace and oxidizing at preset temperature, obtaining copper oxide nanowire/substrate, (3) adding alkaline precipitation agent, reducing agent and additives to copper salt, mixing, magnetically stirring for 30 minutes to 12 hours, obtaining a mixed solution, (4) immersing the copper oxide nanowire/substrate in the mixed solution in the reactor, electroless plating, coating a layer of nanoparticles on the surface of the nanowire, cooling, taking out, washing, drying to get electrode, (5) placing the dried electrode in 0.5 M sulfuric acid electrolyte and using cyclic voltammetry to scan, realizing the structural rearrangement of the electrocatalyst, washing after taking it out, blow drying with inert gas, obtaining gas diffusion electrode, (6) immersing the gas diffusion electrode in a 36-38% concentrated hydrochloric acid solution, cleaning, and blow drying with inert gas. In step (2), the copper oxide is of formula: CuxO, where x is 1 or 2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: In step (3), the mixed solution is a precursor copper salt solution. The precursor copper salt solution is copper(II) nitrate trihydrate, copper(II) chloride dihydrate, or copper(II) sulfate pentahydrate. The alkaline precipitation agent is chosen from one or more of urea, ammonia, sodium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate and ammonium bicarbonate, Preferred Property: The concentration of copper ions in precursor copper salt solution is 0.1 mM to 1.0 M, preferably 0.5-300 mM. The concentration of the alkaline precipitation agent is 1-1.0 mol/L, preferably 5-200 mmol/L. The molar concentration ratio of the alkaline precipitation agent to the copper salt solution of the precursor is 0.5:1-50:1, preferably 1-30. The molar concentration of copper in the reducing agent and the precursor copper salt solution is 1:3-3:1, preferably 1:1-2:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The reducing agent is chosen from one of glucose, sodium citrate and vitamin C. The additive is chosen from one of sodium saccharinate, sodium tartrate and thiourea. Preferred Property: The concentration of the reducing agent in the solution is 0.01 mM to 1.5 M, preferably 0.1 mM to 0.5 M. The concentration of the additive is 0.5-500 mM, preferably 0.5-100 mM.
