PN CN110938594-A
TI Culturing functionally enhanced tumor infiltrating            lymphocytes by isolating lymphocytes from tumor tissue,            adding starter medium, culturing, suspending in            induction medium, performing half-volume exchange,            resuspending and testing.
AU XIA J
   PAN Q
   HE J
   SONG M
AE XIA J (XIAJ-Individual)
GA 2020275339
AB    NOVELTY - Method for culturing functionally enhanced                tumor infiltrating lymphocytes (TIL cells) involve                (i) isolating lymphocytes from tumor tissue, adding                starter medium, planting in a 12-well culture                plate, 4 ml/well, carrying out starter lymphocyte                culture to obtain starter TIL cells after culturing                for 10 days or 14 days, harvesting starter TIL                cells and freezing, (ii) using induction medium to                suspend the obtained lymphocytes, and placing in a                37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator for                TIL cell induction culture for 1 day, (iii)                performing half-volume exchange with expansion                medium, expanding bottle culture and expanding bag                culture of the obtained TIL cells for a total of 13                days or 14 days, (iv) collecting TIL cells on the                14th or 15th day of culture, washing with normal                saline, resuspending TIL cells with function                enhancement medium and incubating for 30 minutes,                and (v) collecting TIL cells and performing                functional tests to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing                functionally enhanced tumor infiltrating                lymphocytes.
   ADVANTAGE - The culture method obtains functionally                enhanced TIL cells with stronger tumor cell killing                activity and higher levels of anti-tumor cytokine                secretion ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for culturing functionally enhanced                tumor infiltrating lymphocytes (TIL cells) involve                (i) isolating lymphocytes from tumor tissue, adding                starter medium, planting in a 12-well culture                plate, 4 ml/well, carrying out starter lymphocyte                culture to obtain starter TIL cells after culturing                for 10 days or 14 days, harvesting starter TIL                cells and freezing, where the starting medium is                autologous serum and interleukin-2 (IL-2) on the                basis of T cell serum-free medium, (ii) using                induction medium to suspend the obtained                lymphocytes, and placing in a 37 degrees C and 5%                carbon dioxide incubator for TIL cell induction                culture for 1 day, where the induction medium is                autologous serum, CD3 monoclonal antibody and CD28                monoclonal antibody on the basis of T cell                serum-free medium, (iii) performing half-volume                exchange with expansion medium, expanding bottle                culture and expanding bag culture of the obtained                TIL cells for a total of 13 days or 14 days, where                the expansion medium is added IL-2 on the basis of                T cell serum-free medium, (iv) collecting TIL cells                on the 14th or 15th day of culture, washing with                normal saline, resuspending TIL cells with function                enhancement medium and incubating for 30 minutes,                where the function enhancing medium comprises                normal saline, human serum albumin and PD-1                antibody, and (v) collecting TIL cells and                performing functional tests to obtain the                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the isolation                  of lymphocytes from tumor tissues involves                  crushing tumor tissues in petri dish,                  centrifuging at a speed of 1800 rpm at room                  temperature for 8 minutes, discarding the                  supernatant, adding 10 ml collagenase IV solution                  to blow up, incubating in a 37 degrees C water                  bath for 2 hours, collecting the supernatant                  after digestion, passing through a 100 mu m                  sieve, washing with physiological saline 2 times,                  centrifuging at speed of 1800 rpm at room                  temperature for 8 minutes, discarding the                  supernatant, taking the pellet, suspending with                  X-VIVO, subjecting lymphocyte separation solution                  to Ficoll gradient centrifugation in a ratio of                  2:3, taking the intermediate layer of                  lymphocytes, washing with physiological saline 2                  times, and setting aside. In step (ii), the TIL                  cell induction culture involves taking 5x 106                  lymphocytes and 1x 108 feeder cells (T cells                  inactivated by gamma -rays), using the induction                  medium to suspend cells in a 25 cm2 culture                  flask, 20 ml/vial, and placing in 37 degrees C                  and 5% carbon dioxide incubator for cultivation.                  The step (iii) involves using the amplification                  medium for half-volume exchange, placing in 25                  cm2 culture bottle, 20 ml/bottle, placed in 37                  degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator,                  culturing until 4th to 5th day, expanding cells                  from 25cm2 flask to 175 cm2 flask with 100                  ml/flask using expansion culture medium, placing                  in 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator,                  culturing until the 7th to 8th day, expanding the                  cells from the 175 cm2 flask to the cell culture                  bag with the expansion medium, 1000-1500 ml/bag,                  and placing in 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide                  incubator until the 13th or 14th day to obtain                  the expanded culture TIL cell. The step (iii)                  involves simultaneously sampling the cells are                  for inspection during the TIL cell expansion                  culture process, taking 4 ml/bag, and testing                  bacteria and fungi to determine whether the                  bacteria and fungi test results are negative. In                  step (v), the function-enhanced TIL cells                  obtained by the collection involves collecting                  the cultured cells in a disposable sterile                  centrifuge cup, centrifugally collecting at 2300                  rpm for 8 minutes, discarding the supernatant,                  collecting the precipitated cells in 6-8 sterile                  50 ml centrifuge tubes, washing 2 times with 0.9%                  saline for injection, centrifuging at 800 g for 8                  minutes, discarding the supernatant, collecting                  the cells in 0.9% saline for injection, uniformly                  mixing and setting aside. Preferred Composition:                  In step (i), the starting medium comprises 10%                  autologous serum and 500-1000 IU/ml IL-2. In step                  (ii), the induction medium comprises 10%                  autologous serum, 10-30 ng/ml cluster of                  differentiation 3 monoclonal antibody, and 10-30                  ng/ml cluster of differentiation 28 monoclonal                  antibody. In step (iii), the expansion medium                  comprises 2000-4000 IU/ml IL-2. In step (iv), the                  function enhancing medium comprises 100 ml                  physiological saline, 5 ml 20% human serum                  albumin, and 10-20 mg programmed cell death                  protein 1 (PD-1) antibody.

PN CN110921894-A
TI Treating fluorescent wastewater involves adding AP demulsifier to fluorescent wastewater to flocculate the emulsified oil and colloid in fluorescent wastewater to separate and precipitate to form flocs, using air floatation equipment.
AU CHEN Q
   AN R
   YUAN X
AE GUIZHOU CHUANGWEIDAO ENVIRONMENTAL TECHN (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 202025914Q
AB    NOVELTY - Treating fluorescent wastewater involves adding AP demulsifier to the fluorescent wastewater to flocculate the emulsified oil and colloid in the fluorescent wastewater to separate and precipitate to form flocs, using air floatation equipment to float the flocs to separate them from the water out. The water is heated and pressurized to become supercritical water. The temperature of supercritical water is 400-600 degrees C. The oxygen and alkali are added to supercritical water. The organic compounds and oxygen produces heteroatoms in organic compounds of carbon dioxide and water, and the Acids, inorganic and /oxic acid + alkali inorganic + water. The organic compounds and oxygen in supercritical water includes RH + O2-R + HO2 RH + HO2 R + H2O2H2O2 + M 2HO RH + HO R + H2OR + O2 ROO ROO + RH ROOH + R ROOH + O2 C02 H20, where RH is organic compounds, M is a collision group, mainly supercritical water after the reaction.
   USE - Method for treating fluorescent wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat fluorescent wastewater reaches the recycling effect and has effectively zero discharge.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating fluorescent wastewater involves adding AP demulsifier to the fluorescent wastewater to flocculate the emulsified oil and colloid in the fluorescent wastewater to separate and precipitate to form flocs, using air floatation equipment to float the flocs to separate them from the water out. The water is heated and pressurized to become supercritical water. The temperature of supercritical water is 400-600 degrees C. The oxygen and alkali are added to supercritical water. The organic compounds and oxygen produces heteroatoms in organic compounds of carbon dioxide and water, and the Acids, inorganic and /oxic acid + alkali inorganic + water. The organic compounds and oxygen in supercritical water includes RH + O2-R + HO2 RH + HO2 R + H2O2H2O2 + M 2HO RH + HO R + H2OR + O2 ROO ROO + RH ROOH + R ROOH + O2 C02 H20, where RH is organic compounds, M is a collision group, mainly supercritical water after the reaction, the inorganic substance sinks and separates from the supercritical water, after which the supercritical water is reduced in temperature and pressure to form water. The water is filtered through various filter media to remove floc and suspended matter in water. The water is filtered through the ultrafiltration membrane to finally obtain low molecular weight solute and water. The water is filtered through the primary RO membrane and the secondary RO membrane in order to remove more than 98 wt.% of the soluble inorganic species in water and more than 99 wt.% of colloids, microorganisms, particles and organic matter.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The flocs and the inorganic matter are transferred to a sludge tank and filtered by a filter press. The filtrate after pressure filtration is recovered and processed in sequence. The concentrated water filtered is processed in sequence. The water treated is used in a conventional fluorescent inspection production line to configure a fluorescent inspection solution. The water treated is used to preheat the fluorescent wastewater through a heat exchanger. The cooling water inlet is provided at the bottom of the supercritical water. The water is sequentially passed through filter media: anthracite, sand, finely crushed garnet.

PN CN110702741-A
TI Method for calculating soil salinity, involves taking multiple soil samples, mixing, drying and sieving collect soil samples, followed by adding deionized water, and measuring molar concentration of eight major ions in soil sample.
AU YUAN Y
   WANG S
   LI B
AE WEIFANG YOURONG IND CO LTD (WFYR-C)
GA 202008312N
AB    NOVELTY - A soil salinity calculating method involves taking multiple soil samples of the same depth, mixing, drying and sieving to collect soil samples, adding deionized water to the soil sample, extracting, let it stand to collect the filtrate, taking the filtrate, adding triethanolamine solution for detection, measuring the molar concentration of eight major ions in the soil sample, calculating the relative standard deviation, removing the data and utilizing an average value of eight major ions, arranging cations and anions, and pairing in order, preparing a standard mother liquor, adjusting the pH of the composite salt solution for utilizing a saturated calcium hydroxide solution, and diluting it with secondary purified water to obtain a standard working solution, stabilizing the temperature of sample solutions, measuring the total dissolved solids and recording the test data, fitting the curve relationship between the measured data and the concentration of the mixed salt solution.
   USE - Method for calculating soil salinity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables calculating soil salinity with in simple operation, accurate and rapid manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A soil salinity calculating method involves taking multiple soil samples of the same depth, mixing, drying and sieving to collect soil samples, adding deionized water does not contain carbon dioxide to the soil sample, extracting by vigorous shaking, after extraction, let it stand for more than 4 hours to collect the filtrate, taking the filtrate, adding triethanolamine solution for detection, measuring the molar concentration of eight major ions in the soil sample at the same depth, calculating the relative standard deviation of the same ion concentration, removing the data with relative standard deviation greater than 10%, and utilizing an average value of eight major ions to represent the final eight major ion concentration, arranging cations and anions in order from large to small, and pairing in order under the remaining ions continue to pair with the oppositely charged ions with the next concentration, preparing a standard mother liquor according to the salt component, adjusting the pH of the composite salt solution for utilizing a saturated calcium hydroxide solution, and diluting it with secondary purified water without carbon dioxide to obtain a standard working solution, stabilizing the temperature of all the sample solutions to be tested, measuring the total dissolved solids with a conductivity meter and recording the test data, fitting the curve relationship between the measured data and the concentration of the mixed salt solution, and obtaining the curve relationship and R2.

TI Supported catalyst comprises complex for producing            functionalized polymer or copolymer, which comprises            double metal cyanide compound, organic complexing            agent(s) and metal salt, and specific hydrophobic            support material.
AU DAMKE J
   KLEIN J
   MARQUARDT M
   MEJIA E
   KRAGL U
   MARQUART M
   DAMKE YA E
   KLEIN Y
   MARKVARDT M
   MEYIA E
AE HENKEL AG & CO KGAA (HENK-C)
   HENKEL AG & CO KGAA (HENK-C)
   HENKEL AG & CO KGAA (HENK-C)
   HENKEL AG & CO KGAA (HENK-C)
GA 2019A8121E
AB    NOVELTY - A supported catalyst comprises a double metal                cyanide complex, which comprises a double metal                cyanide (DMC) compound, at least one organic                complexing agent and a metal salt, and hydrophobic                support material chosen from hydrophobic materials                having methanol wettability value of 30 vol.% or                more, determined by methanol wettability test,                carbonaceous inorganic solid materials and                inorganic solid materials, which are isoelectronic                with carbon.
   USE - Supported catalyst comprises complex for                producing functionalized polymer or copolymer used                as reactive component for coating, sealant or                adhesive compositions based on active hydrogen                reactive compounds (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent catalytic activity,                and can be effectively applied for ring-opening                polymerization of epoxide monomers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A supported catalyst has structural formula:                (DMCC)x bSupp (I). The catalyst contains                hydrophobic support material chosen from                hydrophobic materials having methanol wettability                value of 30 vol.% or more, determined by methanol                wettability test, carbonaceous inorganic solid                materials and inorganic solid materials, which are                isoelectronic with carbon.
   (DMCC) = double metal cyanide complex, which                comprises a double metal cyanide (DMC) compound, at                least one organic complexing agent and a metal                salt;
   Supp = hydrophobic support material;and
   b = average weight ratio of support material,                which is more than 1 wt.% and less than 99 wt.%,                preferably more than 10 wt.% and less than 70                wt.%.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) manufacture of supported catalyst, which                involves mixing at least one complexing agent,                hydrophobic support, at least one salt of formula:                M1dXg and at least one complex of formula:                M3h(M2(CN)e)f in aqueous medium, washing with                aqueous solution, and drying;
   (2) use of the supported catalyst in the ring                opening polymerization of at least one epoxide                monomer or in the co-polymerization of carbon                dioxide and at least one epoxide monomer; and
   (3) manufacture of functionalized polymer or                copolymer, which involves performing ring opening                polymerization of at least one epoxide monomer or                co-polymerization of carbon dioxide and at least                one epoxide monomer in the presence of an initiator                comprising active hydrogen-containing compound                capable of alkoxylation by an epoxide compound, and                the supported catalyst.
   M1 = Zn, Fe, Co, Ni, Mn, Cu, Sn or Pb                ion;and
   M3 = Zn, Fe, Co, Ni, Mn, Cu, Sn, Pb, Cr, Ir,                Rh, Ru or V ion, preferably equal to M1;
   M2 = Fe, Co, Mn, Cr, Ir, Rh, Ru or V                ion;
   X = anion, preferably halide;and
   d-h = more than 0 and selected such that                complex is neutral.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The hydrophobic support                  material has methanol wettability value of 30-80                  vol.%, preferably 30-70 vol.%. Preferred                  Components: The hydrophobic support material is                  chosen from hydrophobically modified inorganic                  oxides or hydroxides, hydrophobically modified                  calcium carbonate, and clay, preferably                  hydrophobically fumed silica, hydrophobically                  modified calcium carbonate, activated charcoal,                  carbon black, and graphene. The hydrophobic                  support is added before or during the washing, or                  after the drying.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The double metal                  cyanide complex (DMCC) is of formula:                  M1d(M2(CN)e)f.xM3(X)g.yH2O.wL or                  M1d(M2(CN)e)f.xM3(X)g.yH2O.zL1.aL2.L,L1,L2 = organic complexing agent chosen                  from aliphatic 1-24C alcohol, monoglyme, diglyme,                  1,4-dioxane, furan, polypropyleneglycol (PPG)                  homopolymers and polypropyleneglycol (PPG)                  copolymers;x = more than 0.1 and less than 5;y = more than 0.1 and less than 1;w = more than 0.0001 and less than 6;z = more than 0.0001 and less than                  1;anda = more than 0.0001 and less than 5.

PT P
PN CN110367486-A
TI Processing instant-flavor kelp by cutting cleaned kelp, drying, disinfecting, soaking in seasoning solution, draining, rapidly drying, subjecting to solid accumulation, spraying flavoring agents, filling with mixed gas comprising nitrogen.
AU LIN Y
   CHEN X
   SUN H
AE FUJIAN GUANFENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 201990389S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for processing instant-flavor kelp involves (i) selecting fresh kelp raw materials, washing sediment and salinized soil on the surface of the kelp with clean water, putting the raw materials into a cleaning fluid with sodium bicarbonate for secondary washing, (ii) cutting cleaned kelp into pieces and carrying out UV irradiation disinfection treatment on the pieces after the pieces are rapidly dried, (iii) soaking dried and disinfected kelp pieces into a seasoning solution and leaving still, (iv) taking out fully soaked kelp pieces from the seasoning solution, draining off excessive water on the pieces, putting into a drying machine, rapidly drying for 10-15 minutes at 120-160 degrees C to enable the moisture content to be lower than 8% and the kelp pieces to be subjected to solid accumulation after dehydration and spraying flavoring agents on the surfaces of the pieces and (v) filling processed pieces with mixed gas comprising nitrogen and carbon dioxide and packaging.
   USE - The method is useful for processing instant-flavor kelp for producing an instant-flavor kelp product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the instant-flavor kelp which can be eaten conveniently and rich in taste and adopts simple preparation process and raw materials and cost-effective manufacturing process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for processing instant-flavor kelp involves (i) selecting fresh kelp raw materials, firstly washing sediment and salinized soil on the surface of the kelp with clean water, putting the raw materials into a cleaning fluid with 3.5% sodium bicarbonate for secondary washing, (ii) cutting cleaned kelp into pieces with length of 5-8 cm and carrying out UV irradiation disinfection treatment on the pieces after the pieces are rapidly dried, (iii) completely soaking dried and disinfected kelp pieces into a seasoning solution with a weight ratio of 5-7 times and fully leaving still for 2-3 hours, (iv) taking out fully soaked kelp pieces from the seasoning solution, draining off excessive water on the pieces, putting into a drying machine, rapidly drying for 10-15 minutes at 120-160 degrees C to enable the moisture content to be lower than 8% and the kelp pieces to be subjected to solid accumulation after dehydration and spraying flavoring agents on the surfaces of the pieces in a spray treatment manner and (v) filling processed pieces with mixed gas comprising nitrogen and carbon dioxide in a ratio of 3:1 and packaging the processed pieces in an inflatable manner. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an instant-flavor kelp product obtained by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (ii) involves rapidly drying the cleaned kelp using hot air at 50 degrees C to rapidly dry the moisture of the surface, where the UV irradiation disinfection treatment time is 10 minutes and front and back surfaces of the kelp are need to be irradiated and disinfected. The step (ii) involves cutting the raw material into a shape of ribbon, strip or sheet. The flavoring agent is a mixed solution prepared by sequentially mixing malic acid, sodium citrate and maltose in the ratio of 1:3:5. The step (iv) involves spraying flavoring agents, carrying out hot air drying at 65-70 degrees C for 10 minutes, rapidly drying the flavoring agents coated and carrying out air-inflation packaging. The step (v) involves using air-conditioning packaging equipment which can be a packaged air-conditioning packaging machine, a semi-automatic air-conditioning packaging machine or a full-automatic air-conditioning packaging machine. Preferred Composition: The seasoning solution comprises 45-55 pts. wt. acetic acid, 6-10 pts. wt. ethanol, 10-18 pts. wt. glycerin, 5-8 pts. wt. salt and 160-200 pts. wt. deionized water.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PT P
PN CN110270233-A; CN110270233-B
TI Metal organic framework nanosheet mixed substrate film for separating hydrogen/carbon dioxide gas, is prepared by using polymer film as base film and performing amination treatment, followed by taking out nanosheet layer and placing in air.
AU LI Y
   ZHANG X
   ZHOU Y
   MAO J
   SU P
   ZHANG G
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 2019825984
AB    NOVELTY - A metal organic framework nanosheet mixed substrate film is prepared by using a polymer film as a base film, performing amination or hydroxylation treatment to obtain a modified base film, using 4-pyridyl-4-ylbenzoic acid and a metal salt as raw materials, dissolving in water to obtain a mixture A, mixing an anionic surfactant with isooctane to obtain a mixed liquid B, and mixing the mixed liquid A and B to obtain a reaction liquid precursor. The modified base film is immersed in the precursor of the reaction liquid to obtain an in-situ grown nanosheet layer supported by the base film. The high molecular organic polymer is dissolved in an organic solvent to obtain a mixed liquid C, supported the in-situ grown nanosheet layer by the base film, which is immersed in the mixed liquid C, taken out and placed in the air for phase transformation to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Metal organic framework nanosheet mixed substrate film for separating hydrogen/carbon dioxide gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The metal organic framework nanosheet mixed substrate film has high penetrant flux and selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A metal organic framework nanosheet mixed substrate film is prepared by using a polymer film as a base film, performing amination or hydroxylation treatment to obtain a modified base film, using 4-pyridyl-4-ylbenzoic acid and a metal salt as raw materials, dissolving in water to obtain a mixture A, mixing an anionic surfactant with isooctane to obtain a mixed liquid B having a surfactant concentration of 0.3-0.8 mol/L, and mixing the mixed liquid A and B ultrasonically to obtain a reaction liquid precursor. The modified base film is immersed in the precursor of the reaction liquid for 3-12 hours to obtain an in-situ grown nanosheet layer supported by the base film, where the ratio of the 4-pyridyl-4-ylbenzoic acid, the metal salt, and the anionic surfactant is 1:0.3-2:30-80, and the amount of water added is 2-5 ml/mmol based on the amount of the 4-pyridyl-4-ylbenzoic acid substance. The high molecular organic polymer is dissolved in an organic solvent to obtain a mixed liquid C having a mass fraction of 10-25%, supported the in-situ grown nanosheet layer by the base film, which is immersed in the mixed liquid C for 2-10 minutes, taken out and placed in the air for phase transformation for 5-72 hours to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The ammoniation treatment process involves immersing the base film in the solution A, soaking at room temperature for 2 hours or 40-70 degrees C for 5-30 minutes under heating, where the solution A is an ammoniated solvent or an aqueous solution, and the ammoniated solvent is diethylenetriamine, p-phenylenediamine, aqueous ammonia, diethylamine or triethylamine, and the volume fraction of the ammoniated solvent in the solution A is 20-100%. Preferred Components: The solution B is phosphoric acid, sodium hydroxide or nitric acid dissolved in water, preparing into aqueous solution of 2M. The metal salt is acetate, nitrate or chloride salt of zinc, copper or iron. The anionic surfactant is sodium dodecyl sulfate, sodium bis(2-ethylhexyl) sulfosuccinate, sodium fatty alcohol ether sulfate, sulfonate of an ethoxylated fatty acid methyl ester or sodium alpha -alkenylsulfonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is N,N'-dimethylformamide, N,N'-dimethylacetamide, dimethylpyrrolidone, dimethyl sulfoxide, tetrahydrofuran or dichloromethane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer film is a hollow fiber membrane or a flat membrane, where the polymer film is made of polyvinylidene fluoride, polypropylene, polyacrylonitrile, polyethylene, PVC, polysulfone, polyethersulfone or polyimide. The high molecular polymer is polyethylene glycol dimethacrylate, polyimide, polyphenylene ether, polyether ether ketone, polydimethylsiloxane or self-polymerized microporous polymer.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN110181741-A; CN110181741-B
TI Method for preparing foamed polyolefin bead for            use in insulation sound insulation system of building            floor, involves adding polyolefin resin and auxiliary            agent to mixer, stirring followed by mixing and passing            through screw extruder.
AU SHI J
AE ZHEJIANG ZHONGCHUANG MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201976068J
AB    NOVELTY - A foamed polyolefin bead preparing method                involves adding a polyolefin resin and an auxiliary                agent to a high-speed mixer, stirring, mixing and                passing through the screw extruder, where the                temperature of the kneading zone is 200-240 degrees                C to obtain uniformly mixed polyolefin resin                particles. A supercritical carbon dioxide is used                as a foaming agent, the polyolefin resin particles                are added into the foaming kettle, water and a                dispersing agent are added, the reactor is heated                and pressurized. The particles are instantaneously                released from the reaction vessel to the atmosphere                to obtain primary foamed polyolefin beads. The                primary foamed polyolefin beads are injected into                the pressure tank and compressed air is introduced                into the tank. The primary foamed polyolefin beads                are injected into the foaming machine, after the                load is completed. The foaming is carried out by                steam heating and the foaming temperature is                controlled at 60-115 degrees C to obtain the foamed                polyolefin beads.
   USE - Method for preparing a foamed polyolefin bead                for use in the insulation sound insulation system                of the building floor (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing foamed polyolefin                beads having excellent shock absorption and sound                insulation properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A foamed polyolefin bead preparing method                involves adding a polyolefin resin and an auxiliary                agent to a high-speed mixer, stirring, mixing                uniformly and passing through the screw extruder,                where the temperature of the kneading zone is                200-240 degrees C to obtain uniformly mixed                polyolefin resin particles. A supercritical carbon                dioxide is used as a foaming agent, the polyolefin                resin particles are added into the foaming kettle,                water and a dispersing agent are added, the reactor                is heated and pressurized. The particles are                instantaneously released from the reaction vessel                to the atmosphere, after reaching a foaming                temperature of 135-160 degrees C and a foaming                pressure of 1.0-3.0 MPa to obtain primary foamed                polyolefin beads having a density of 35-150 g/L.                The primary foamed polyolefin beads are injected                into the pressure tank and compressed air is                introduced into the tank. The primary foamed                polyolefin beads are injected into the foaming                machine, after the load is completed. The foaming                is carried out by steam heating and the foaming                temperature is controlled at 60-115 degrees C to                obtain the foamed polyolefin beads having a density                of 10-34 g/L.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The auxiliary agent                  comprises a nucleating agent, an antioxidant, a                  flame retardant, a toughening agent, a graphite                  derivative, a porous material and a                  crystallization accelerator. The nucleating agent                  is kaolin, talc or zinc borate, the toughening                  agent is thermoplastic elastomers,                  polyoxyethylene and/or                  poly(styrene-butadiene-styrene), the graphite                  derivative is natural flake graphite, synthetic                  graphite, graphene and/or expandable graphite,                  the porous material is activated carbon, organic                  diatomaceous earth and/or porous silica, the                  crystallization accelerator is a transparent                  agent 3988 series, NX8000K series and/or A-931                  series and the other auxiliary agent includes                  glycerin and citric acid.

PN CN110182801-A
TI Preparing granular activated carbon comprises e.g.            crushing biomass into powder, heating biomass mixture,            mechanically stirring, directly compressing the            hydrothermal carbon, activating, washing activated            carbon with hydrochloric acid.
AU ZHANG S
   YU F
   ZHANG C
   SHANG H
   LUO J
   WANG Q
AE UNIV FUDAN (UYFU-C)
GA 2019760467
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing granular activated carbon comprises                (a)drying the biomass and crushing it into a                biomass powder; (b) adding biomass powder to the                hydrothermal reactor, and deionized water to obtain                a biomass mixture; (c) heating the biomass mixture                at 150-300 degrees C, maintaining the reaction for                0.5-3 hours and mechanically stirring; (d) cooling                and filtering to obtain a solid phase hot coal,;                (e) soaking obtained solid phase hot water carbon                in an activator for 24 hours, soaking; (f) directly                compressing the hydrothermal carbon of the load                activator to obtain the pellet hydrothermal                carbon,; (g) placing pelletized hydrothermal carbon                in a tube furnace for activation to obtain granular                activated carbon under activation conditions of an                activation time of 90 minutes at 700 degrees C at                heating rate of 5 degrees C/minute under gas                atmosphere of carbon dioxide; and (h) washing                granular activated carbon with 0.1 M hydrochloric                acid and deionized water to neutrality, and drying                at 80 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing granular                activated carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves compressive strength and                high adsorption performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing granular activated carbon comprises                (a) drying the biomass and crushing it into a                biomass powder; (b) adding biomass powder to the                hydrothermal reactor, and deionized water to obtain                a biomass mixture, where the mass ratio of the                biomass powder and deionized water is 15-5:1; (c)                heating the biomass mixture at 150-300 degrees C,                maintaining the reaction for 0.5-3 hours and                mechanically stirring; (d) cooling and filtering to                obtain a solid phase hot coal, and drying the solid                phase hot coal in an oven at 80 degrees C; (e)                soaking obtained solid phase hot water carbon in an                activator for 24 hours, soaking, filtering, and                drying in an oven at 80 degrees C to obtain a                hydrothermal char of a negative activator; (f)                directly compressing the hydrothermal carbon of the                load activator to obtain the pellet hydrothermal                carbon, and controlling the extrusion molding                pressure under 0-200 MPa; (g) placing pelletized                hydrothermal carbon in a tube furnace for                activation to obtain granular activated carbon                under activation conditions of an activation time                of 90 minutes at 700 degrees C at heating rate of 5                degrees C/minute under gas atmosphere of carbon                dioxide; and (h) washing granular activated carbon                with 0.1 M hydrochloric acid and deionized water to                neutrality, and drying at 80 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (a)                  biomass is corn straw, wheat straw, soybean                  straw, cotton straw, tobacco straw, straw,                  sawdust, salix, hyacinth or swigs biomass. In the                  step (e) activating agent is zinc chloride,                  sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid, potassium                  carbonate, potassium hydroxide or ferric                  chloride. Preferred Method: In the step (f) using                  binding action of the hydrothermal carbon itself                  of the load activator, and is directly molding                  without adding a binder.

PN CN109806223-A
TI Producing sodium bicarbonate injection comprises e.g. adding water for injection into a dilution tank, and cooling the water for injection in the dilution tank, adding the soaked activated carbon and sodium bicarbonate into dilution tank.
AU ZHANG X
   HOU Y
   LIU J
   WANG D
   ZHANG J
   ZHU M
   YANG D
AE SHANGHAI WORLDBEST ANHUI JINHUI PHARM CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201948892Y
AB    NOVELTY - Producing sodium bicarbonate injection comprises e.g. adding water for injection into a dilution tank, and cooling the water for injection in the dilution tank to below 30 degrees C; adding the soaked activated carbon and sodium bicarbonate into the dilution tank and introducing pure carbon dioxide gas into the dilution tank until encapsulation is ended; adding water for injection below 30 degrees C into the dilution tank, and starting a dilution tank for stirring; introducing the solution in the dilution tank into a titanium rod filter for filtration, where the filtration pressure of the titanium rod filter is 0.1-0.4 MPa; introducing the solution in the titanium rod filter into a 0.22-0 45 mu m filter for filtration; conveying the solution in 0.22-0 45 mu m filter to a dilution tank for re-stirring, conveying the solution treated to a 0.22 mu m terminal filter for filtration, and then filling; carrying out encapsulation on the solution filled; and carrying out sterilization treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for producing sodium bicarbonate injection.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can sufficiently filter impurities in the solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing sodium bicarbonate injection comprises (a) adding water for injection into a dilution tank, and cooling the water for injection in the dilution tank to below 30 degrees C; (b) adding the soaked activated carbon and sodium bicarbonate into the dilution tank in the step (a), and introducing pure carbon dioxide gas into the dilution tank until encapsulation is ended; (c) adding water for injection below 30 degrees C into the dilution tank in the step (b), and starting a dilution tank for stirring; (d) introducing the solution in the dilution tank into a titanium rod filter for filtration, where the filtration pressure of the titanium rod filter is 0.1-0.4 MPa; (e) introducing the solution in the titanium rod filter into a 0.22-0 45 mu m filter for filtration; (f) conveying the solution in 0.22-0 45 mu m filter to a dilution tank in the step (d) for re-stirring, and repeating the step (f), carrying out the steps (d) and (e) for 3-5 times; (g) conveying the solution treated in the step (f) to a 0.22 mu m terminal filter for filtration, and then filling; (h) carrying out encapsulation on the solution filled in the step (g); and (i) carrying out sterilization treatment on the bottled solution treated in the step (h).

PN KR1942714-B1
TI Heat insulating and sound absorbing composite useful for ship, preferably fireproof glass fiber panel of ship, comprises semi-fire-retardant foam impregnated with flame retardant liquid, glass fiber panel, and nonflammable finishing panel.
AU IL C Y
AE METALPLUS INC (META-Non-standard)
GA 201936493D
AB    NOVELTY - Heat insulating and sound absorbing composite comprises a semi-fire-retardant foam (100) bonded to one side of a partition wall of the marine and impregnated with a flame retardant liquid by a sponge structure, a glass fiber panel bonded to one surface of the other surface of the marine barrier-wall bonding surface of the semi-fire-retardant foam and which is a flaky non-combustible glass fiber, and a nonflammable finishing panel (300) adhered to other surface of the semi-fireproof foam adhering surface of the glass fiber panel and provided with anti-condensation coating (310) formed on other surface of the adhered surface. The semi-fire-retardant foam has a multi-layer structure in which multiple thin semi-fire-retardant foam panels are bonded to each other, and an adiabatic adhesive agent placed on the mutual adhesive surface. The semi-fire-retardant foam is formed by inserting a capsule type air tube containing an inert gas containing carbon dioxide and nitrogen.
   USE - The composite is useful for ship (claimed), preferably fireproof glass fiber panel of ship.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Heat insulating and sound absorbing composite comprises a semi-fire-retardant foam (100) bonded to one side of a partition wall of the marine and impregnated with a flame retardant liquid by a sponge structure, a glass fiber panel bonded to one surface of the other surface of the marine barrier-wall bonding surface of the semi-fire-retardant foam and which is a flaky non-combustible glass fiber, and a nonflammable finishing panel (300) adhered to other surface of the semi-fireproof foam adhering surface of the glass fiber panel and provided with anti-condensation coating (310) formed on other surface of the adhered surface. The semi-fire-retardant foam has a multi-layer structure in which multiple thin semi-fire-retardant foam panels are bonded to each other, and an adiabatic adhesive agent placed on the mutual adhesive surface. The semi-fire-retardant foam is formed by inserting a capsule type air tube containing an inert gas containing carbon dioxide and nitrogen. The heat insulating adhesive comprises 80-90 wt.% binder and 10-20 wt.% porous inorganic fine particles having average particle size of 350-450 mu m. The binder comprises aqueous polyvinyl acetate-based polymer and/or polyacrylic polymer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the composite.
   Partition wall (10)
   Semi-fire-retardant foam (100)
   Embossing panel (200)
   Finishing panel (300)
   Anti-condensation coating (310)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The semi-fire-retardant foam is impregnated with flame retardant containing 0.5-10 wt.% of inorganic flame retardant, 20-87 wt.% liquid silicate mixture, 0.5-10 wt.% phosphorus flame retardant, 10-20 wt.% zeolite as a functional filler, 0.5-10 wt.% inorganic porous heat insulating material, 0.5-10 wt.% graphite, 0.5-10 wt.% heat expandable graphite and 0.5-10 wt.% of bentonite, and expanded polystyrene having density of 0.05-0.2 g/cm3, foaming ratio of 5-20 and thermal conductivity of 0.03-0.05 W/m/K. The glass fiber panel comprises plate-shaped glass fiber panel, and glass-fiber reinforced plastic sheet made of E-glass. The E-glass comprises 40-54 wt.% silicon dioxide, 10-14 wt.% aluminum oxide, 20-23 wt.% calcium oxide and 6-9 wt.% boron oxide. The dew condensation coating of the finish panel comprises 55-70 wt.% melamine, 15-20 wt.% polyacrylic acid resin, 0.5-1 wt.% of an antifoaming agent, 1-1.5 wt.% dispersant, 1.5-2 wt.% sedimentation inhibitor, 1-1.5 wt.% thickener, 10-14 wt.% water and 1-5 wt.% mica stirred in powder form having particle size of 100-500 mu m. The mica is muscovite, soda mica and/or phlogopite.

PN CN109609358-A; CN209652316-U
TI Micromanipulator of sperm comprises dish and lid,            where upper surface of dish is concavely provided with            sample cell and preferred channel, and the preferred            channel comprises first channel, second channel and            third channel.
AU XUE Y
   LI K
   ZHANG S
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2019357921
AB    NOVELTY - Micromanipulator of preferred sperm, comprises                a dish and a lid, where the upper surface of the                dish is concavely provided with a sample cell (1)                and a preferred channel, and the preferred channel                comprises a first channel (21), a second channel                (22) and a third channel, where the sample pool is                connected to the midpoint of the second channel                through the first channel, and multiple induction                chambers are respectively arranged on both sides of                the middle point of the second channel. The upper                surface of the dish is further divided into a sperm                collecting area (4), a sperm braking area (5) and a                microinjection area. The two ends of the second                passage are respectively connected to the sperm                collection area by an upwardly inclined third                passage, which is near the sperm collection                area.
   USE - Micromanipulator of sperm.
   ADVANTAGE - The micromanipulator of sperm can simulates                the movement of sperm in the female reproductive                tract, and the adsorption of apherectin V on                apoptotic sperm and the regulation of progesterone                on sperm function, and improves the fertilization                rate of in vitro fertilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for opmimizing sperm using the micromanipulator,                which involves:
   (A) pretreating with the membrane protein V on                the bottom surface and the side surface of the                first channel;
   (B) adding different concentrations of the                inducer in the induction chamber, so that the                concentration of the inducer in the induction                chamber is sequentially increased from the midpoint                of the second channel to the third channel;
   (C) adding the sperm culture solution to the                sample pool, preferred channel and sperm collection                area, covering the mineral oil, covering the dish,                and equilibrating in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon                dioxide incubator for 30 minutes;
   (D) adding appropriate amount of sperm sample                to the sample pool, covering the dish, and putting                it in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide incubator                for 30-60 minutes, so that the sperm with normal                physiological function and fertilization ability                can swim to the sperm collection area;
   (E) adding high-viscosity sperm brake fluid in                the sperm braking zone, using the microinjection                needle to draw sperm from the collection area under                the inverted microscope, and moving to the sperm                braking zone to brake the sperm; and
   (F) moving the braked sperm to the                microinjection zone, and completing the                intracytoplasmic sperm injection in the                microinjection zone.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                micromanipulator of sperm.
   Sample cell (1)
   Sperm collecting area (4)
   Sperm braking area (5)
   First channel (21)
   Second channel (22)

PT P
PN CN109455837-A; CN109455837-B
TI Recycling method for removing waste liquid, by mixing plant fiber raw material, weak base salt and water, replacing air by oxygen and reacting to obtain cellulose pulp and waste liquid, separating solid and liquid, drying and calcining.
AU ZENG X
   SONG X
   JIANG Y
   DING N
   TANG X
   SUN Y
   LIN L
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 201925388T
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling method for removing waste liquid involves (a) mixing the plant fiber raw material, weak base salt and water, placing the mixture in a reaction kettle, replacing the air in the reaction kettle by using oxygen and reacting to obtain a comprehensive cellulose pulp and a waste liquid, (b) adding a slight excess of calcium oxide precipitate to the waste liquid and adding polyacrylamide flocculating agent and separating to obtain a supernatant, (c) introducing carbon dioxide to the supernatant to remove excess calcium hydroxide and adding weak base salt and carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain a clear liquid, (d) returning the clear liquid to step (a) to replace the weak base salt and water to react and (e) repeating the steps (a) to (d), finally drying and calcining the clear liquid obtained in step (c) to obtain an alkaline solid and returning the alkaline solid to step (a) to replace the weak base salt.
   USE - The method is useful for removing waste liquid obtained by removing lignin from weak base salt.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling method for removing waste liquid involves (a) mixing the plant fiber raw material, weak base salt and water, placing the mixture in a reaction kettle, replacing the air in the reaction kettle by using oxygen, where the initial pressure in the reaction kettle reaches 2 MPa, and reacting at 155-165 degrees C for 2-4 hours to obtain comprehensive cellulose pulp and waste liquid, where the weak base salt comprises sodium formate, sodium acetate, sodium levulinate, sodium citrate, sodium phosphate, disodium hydrogen phosphate, sodium silicate, sodium bicarbonate and/or sodium carbonate, (b) adding a slight excess of calcium oxide precipitate to the waste liquid and adding polyacrylamide flocculating agent and separating to obtain a supernatant, (c) introducing carbon dioxide to the supernatant to remove excess calcium hydroxide and adding weak base salt until the pH value of 12-13 and carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain clear liquid, (d) returning the clear liquid obtained in step (c) to step (a) to replace the weak base salt and water to react and (e) repeating the steps (a) to (d) for 15-20 times, drying and calcining the clear liquid obtained in step (c) to obtain an alkaline solid and returning the alkaline solid to step (a) to replace the weak base salt.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves adding 15-20 wt.% weak base salt of the plant fiber raw material. The step (b) involves adding 50-100 wt.% calcium oxide of the weak base salt in step (a) and adding 0.5 wt.% polyacrylamide of the waste liquid. The step (e) involves drying at 100-105 degrees C and calcining at 530-550 degrees C for 4-5 hours. Preferred Composition: In step (a), the mass ratio of the plant fiber raw material to water is 1:(4-20).

PN CN109369907-A
TI Preparing low molecular weight nylon 46-synthetic high molecular weight nylon 46 resin, comprises e.g. polymerizing low molecular weight nylon 46 powder, water, catalyst and thermal stabilizer, separating small molecule and post processing.
AU ZHANG Y
   QIU S
AE GUANGZHOU WANJIACHENG PLASTIC CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201920399D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low molecular weight nylon 46-synthetic high molecular weight nylon 46 resin, comprises (i) adding raw materials in a polymerization kettle, then adjusting pH value to 7.5-9, controlling the reaction temperature at 110-150 degrees C and reacting for 3-16 hours under vacuum pressure of -0.06 to -0.1 MPa, and then separating small molecule by-products and water to obtain material, and (ii) adding the obtained material to a double screw extruder, then introducing inert gas (carbon dioxide), reacting at 200-325 degrees C under vacuum pressure of -0.03 to -0.1 MPa, separating small molecule by-products and water, taking the material extruded from the twin-screw extruder, cooling, cutting and conveying to a drying device, drying and packaging to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing low molecular weight nylon 46-synthetic high molecular weight nylon 46 resin.
   ADVANTAGE - The resin has excellent chemical property, mechanical property and thermal property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing low molecular weight nylon 46-synthetic high molecular weight nylon 46 resin, comprises (i) adding raw materials in a polymerization kettle, then adjusting pH value to 7.5-9, controlling the reaction temperature at 110-150 degrees C and reacting for 3-16 hours under vacuum pressure of -0.06 to -0.1 MPa, and then separating small molecule by-products and water to obtain material, and (ii) adding the obtained material to a double screw extruder, then introducing inert gas (carbon dioxide), reacting at 200-325 degrees C under vacuum pressure of -0.03 to -0.1 MPa, separating small molecule by-products and water, taking the material extruded from the twin-screw extruder, cooling, cutting and conveying to a drying device, drying and packaging to obtain final product, where the high molecular weight nylon 46 resin is obtained by carrying out polycondensation of liquid phase and solid phase combination of 100 pts. wt. low molecular weight nylon 46 powder, 5-50 pts. wt. water, 0.001-10 pts. wt. catalyst and 0.01-1 pts. wt. thermal stabilizer, the low molecular weight nylon 46 powder is a by-product having an intrinsic viscosity of 0.18 dl/g, and is obtained by polycondensation of low molecular weight monomeric butanediamine and monomer adipic acid into a nylon 46 resin of formula (I), and the catalyst is sodium hydroxide or sodium metasilicate.

PT P
PN CN109052425-A
TI Preparing sericite powder comprises selecting sericite ore, crushing, washing, soaking, sieving, heating, stirring, filtering mixture, collecting, centrifuging, precipitating, calcining, grinding and sieving particles.
AU WAN B
   QI M
   YU H
AE ANHUI HENGHAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2019009379
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sericite powder comprises (i) selecting manually sericite ore, crushing, washing, soaking sericite ore in ethanol, washing, crushing, sieving crushed product, adding sericite ore into crusher and collecting filter material; (ii) adding distilled water into sericite flake, adding potassium permanganate solution and potassium nitrate solution, heating, stirring, adding aqueous hydrogen peroxide, stirring; (iii) filtering mixture, washing with dilute acid solution, washing, and collecting filter residue; (iv) adding distilled water, heating, introducing carbon dioxide and ammonia, stirring;(v) adding silane coupling agent, surfactant, cation capture agent and treatment solution, heating, stirring, centrifuging, washing , precipitating and washing with ethanol to retain precipitate; and (vi) soaking precipitate in distilled water, drying, calcining precipitate under high temperature condition to obtain sericite particles, grinding and sieving particles.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sericite powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method fully breaks and disperses filter residue, removes impurities in filter residue, avoids effects of impurities on dispersibility and gloss of sericite, improves dispersibility and dispersion uniformity of sericite powder and prepares sericite powder with fine particles and high gloss.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing sericite powder comprises (i) selecting manually sericite ore, crushing, washing with distilled water for 30-45 minutes, soaking in ethanol for 10-20 minutes, washing with distilled water for 10-15 minutes, crushing in crusher, sieving crushed product through the sieve, adding sericite ore that has not passed through sieve into crusher for crushing until it is crushed and collecting filter material to obtain sericite flake; (ii) adding distilled water into sericite flake, adding potassium permanganate solution and potassium nitrate solution, heating at 50-60 degrees C, stirring for 2-3 hours, adding aqueous hydrogen peroxide, stirring for 1-2 hours to obtain a mixture; (iii) filtering mixture to obtain filter residue, washing with dilute acid solution for 20-30 minutes, washing continuously using distilled water until pH of distilled water is 6-7, stopping washing and collecting filter residue; (iv) adding 10 volumes of distilled water into filter residue, heating at 200-300 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide and ammonia into filter residue continuously for 60 minutes, stirring mechanically continuously to obtain sericite slurry;(v) adding silane coupling agent, surfactant, cation capture agent and treatment solution into sericite slurry, heating at 80-100 degrees C for 1-2 hours, stirring, centrifuging to remove supernatant, washing , precipitating with distilled water and washing with ethanol to retain precipitate; and (vi) soaking precipitate in distilled water for 60-80 minutes, drying on belt dryer, calcining precipitate under high temperature condition to obtain sericite particles, grinding and sieving particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mesh of sieve is 40-60 meshes and ratio of diameter and thickness of sericite flakes is 50-100:1. The volume ratio of the sericite flake, distilled water, potassium permanganate solution, potassium nitrate solution and hydrogen peroxide solution is 1:8-10:0.4-0.6:0.3-0.5:1-1.5 and mass concentration of potassium permanganate solution is 20-30% and potassium nitrate solution is 92-95%. The volume ratio of carbon dioxide and ammonia is 1:2. The volume ratio of silane coupling agent, surfactant, cation capture agent and treatment solution is 100:3-4:4-5:1-2:0.6-0.8. The silane coupling agent is vinyltriethoxysilane, vinyl trimethoxysilane or vinyl tris(2-methoxyethoxy) silane. The surfactant is lauroyl lysine. The cation capture agent is dimethyltetradecyl(3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl)ammonium chloride. The treatment solution comprises 25 pts. wt. sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate, 12 pts. wt. polysorbate and 28 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol.

PT P
PN CN108901657-A
TI Method for planting winter squash, involves selecting winter open field nursery, sealing greenhouse, followed by placing seeds into warm water, soaking, sowing seeds, pouring water, adding fertilizer, and performing preservation.
AU FANG G
AE ANHUI YUANWEI MODERN AGRIC TECHNOLOGY (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018980403
AB    NOVELTY - A winter squash planting method involves selecting winter open field with low groundwater level and convenient drainage and irrigation, pH of 5.5-7, continuous deep turning land for 3 times at intervals of 15-20 days, applying 50-100 kg lime at 50-60 cm deep, sealing the greenhouse. The seeds are placed into warm water of 55 degrees C for 15-20 minutes, soaked for 8-10 hours, the mucous membrane of the seed coat is washed with warm water, followed by removing the seeds and wrapping in gauze, carrying out the germination, pouring the seedling and closing the soil, at spacing of 55-65cm, where planting seedlings are 1200-1400 plants, after 7-10 days planting, pouring water, combined with topdressing, adding carbon dioxide gas fertilizer in the late growth period, and performing preservation by shortening sunshine and temperature management in the seedling stage of pumpkin, timely removing old leaves and diseased leaves, increasing light intensity and the yield during fruit growth period.
   USE - Method for planting winter squash.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables planting winter squash with improved quality of flower bud fertilization and fruit set rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A winter squash planting method involves selecting winter open field with low groundwater level and convenient drainage and irrigation, pH of 5.5-7, continuous deep turning land for 3 times at intervals of 15-20 days, applying 50-100 kg lime at 50-60 cm deep, sealing the greenhouse, at 50-60 degrees C for 15-25 days. The seeds are placed into warm water of 55 degrees C for 15-20 minutes, soaked for 8-10 hours, the mucous membrane of the seed coat is washed with warm water, followed by removing the seeds and wrapping in gauze, carrying out the germination under the constant temperature condition of 25-30 degrees C, and sowing the seeds when exposed to white, performing colonization by , dividing the seedlings into three levels of large, medium and small, planting in the front, middle and back rows in the north-south direction, setting the planting points, pouring the seedling and closing the soil, at spacing of 55-65cm, where planting seedlings are 1200-1400 plants, after 7-10 days planting, pouring water, combined with topdressing, adding carbon dioxide gas fertilizer in the late growth period, and performing preservation by shortening sunshine and temperature management in the seedling stage of pumpkin, timely removing old leaves and diseased leaves, artificial pollination at 6-9 am, increasing light intensity and the yield during fruit growth period.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The top dressing comprises 25-30 pts. wt. urea, 15-20 pts. wt. potassium fertilizer and 10-15 pts. wt. diammonium phosphate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The nutrient soil comprises composition of 10-30 pts. wt. sandy loam, 4-10 pts. wt. decomposed organic fertilizer, and 4-10 pts. wt. ash.

PT P
PN CN108609640-A
TI Producing aluminum acid-produced special fertilizer used for fertilizer, by mixing crushed potassium feldspar with phosphogypsum, additive and modifier, water milling clinker and carrying out solid-liquid separation.
AU LI J
   GAO G
   LU F
   LUO X
   ZHOU D
   CHEN X
AE GUIZHOU GUIFU ECOLOGICAL FERTILIZER IND (GUIZ-Non-standard)
   GUIZHOU TECHNOLOGY INST (GZTI-C)
   GUIZHOU KEXIN CHEM & METALLURGY CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018804318
AB    NOVELTY - The method for producing aluminum acid-produced special fertilizer involves (a) crushing potassium feldspar, mixing potassium feldspar with phosphogypsum, additive and modifier to obtain a raw material, transferring the raw material to a kiln and calcining, (b) water milling the clinker, dissolving and carrying out solid-liquid separation, (c) adding carbon dioxide gas to the liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation until white precipitate is not generated, filtering, washing, drying to obtain aluminum hydroxide and calcining aluminum hydroxide to obtain aluminum oxide, (d) floating the filter residue to select a sulfide, calcining sulfide and transferring flue gas to a sulfuric acid preparation system to obtain sulfuric acid, and (e) adding potassium calcium fertilizer particles to a mixer, adding urea, diammonium hydrogen phosphate, zinc sulfate heptahydrate, magnesium sulfate and borax to the mixer and uniformly mixing to obtain the fertilizer.
   USE - Method is useful for producing aluminum acid-produced special fertilizer, which is useful for peanut.
   ADVANTAGE - The aluminum acid-produced special fertilizer has high aluminum recovery rate and purity, and exhibits small damage action on soil acidity and alkalinity of peanut planting land.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for producing aluminum acid-produced special fertilizer involves (a) crushing potassium feldspar through 180-220 mesh, mixing potassium feldspar with phosphogypsum, additive and modifier to obtain a raw material, transferring the raw material to a kiln and roasting to obtain clinker, where the weight ratio of phosphogypsum to potassium feldspar in the raw material is 1:0.4-1.2, the molecular ratio of the sum of sodium oxide and alumina+iron oxide contained in the raw materials is 1:1 and the mixing ratio of the modifier is 15-20 wt.%, (b) carrying out water milling on the clinker, dissolving out the clinker and carrying out solid-liquid separation, (c) adding carbon dioxide gas to the liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation until white precipitate is not generated, filtering out the white precipitate, washing, drying to obtain aluminum hydroxide and calcining aluminum hydroxide at 850-950 degrees C for 1-3 hours to obtain aluminum oxide, (d) floating the filter residue obtained by solid-liquid separation to select a sulfide, calcining sulfide and transferring flue gas to a sulfuric acid preparation system to obtain sulfuric acid, and (e) drying and crushing filter residue to obtain potassium calcium fertilizer particles, adding potassium calcium fertilizer particles to a mixer, then adding urea, diammonium hydrogen phosphate, zinc sulfate heptahydrate, magnesium sulfate and borax to the mixture and uniformly mixing to obtain the fertilizer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Composition: The potassium feldspar comprises 10-18 %mass potassium oxide, 16-20 %mass alumina and 60-70 %mass silica. The fertilizer comprises 70-90 pts. wt., preferably 80 pts. wt. potassium calcium fertilizer particles, 60-80 pts. wt., preferably 70 pts. wt. urea, 70-100 pts. wt., preferably 85 pts. wt. diammonium hydrogen phosphate, 3-7 pts. wt., preferably 5 pts. wt. zinc sulfate heptahydrate, 2-6 pts. wt., preferably 4 pts. wt. magnesium sulfate and 20-30 pts. wt., preferably 25 pts. wt. borax. Preferred Components: The additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda. The modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. Preferred Conditions: In the step (a), the calcining step is carried out at 1050-1200 degrees C for 1-3 hours. In the step (d), the calcining step is carried out at 800-1000 degrees C for 1-4 hours. In the step (e), the potassium-calcium fertilizer particles have a moisture content of less than 0.5%. Preferred Device: The kiln is an industrial rotary kiln, an industrial tunnel kiln or an industrial vertical kiln.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

PN CN108610435-A
TI Extracting polysaccharide from Ganoderma lucidum spore comprises e.g. washing and draining the Ganoderma lucidum spore, breaking, ultrasonic dispersing, adding ethanol and n-butanol, and carrying out cyclic extraction.
AU MENG F
   WU L
   ZHAO C
   YANG D
   ZHAO M
   JIN Y
   WANG L
   WANG Y
   QIN S
   DI L
   LIU Y
   ZHONG T
   HU L
   LI J
   ZHANG Y
   LIU M
   YAO Y
   FENG W
AE UNIV JILIN ZHUHAI COLLEGE (UYJI-Non-standard)
GA 2018804145
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting polysaccharide from Ganoderma lucidum spore comprises e.g. washing and draining the Ganoderma lucidum spore, slicing, freezing, breaking at -25 to 10 degrees C under the protection of carbon dioxide gas, adding 50-85% ethanol solution for 2-3 hours, treating at 30-50 degrees C for 30-60 seconds, filtering the mixture to obtain filtrate and filter residue, ultrasonic dispersing at 60 degrees C for 1-2 hours, filtrating, combining the filtrates, concentrating and recovering ethanol to obtain extract, and pumping the obtained extract into the extraction tower, uniformly introducing n-butanol, adding into the extraction tower for high-speed countercurrent extraction, carrying out cyclic extraction for 3-5 times to obtain extract, concentrating the n-butanol and recovering, and freeze-drying under vacuum to obtain polysaccharide.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting polysaccharide from Ganoderma lucidum spore (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, and easy operation; and has high product purity and stable quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting polysaccharide from Ganoderma lucidum spore comprises (i) washing and draining the Ganoderma lucidum spore, slicing, placing in a low temperature breaking machine, freezing and breaking at -25 to 10 degrees C under the protection of carbon dioxide gas to obtain broken wall of Ganoderma lucidum spore powder, (ii) placing broken Ganoderma lucidumspore powder at -18 degrees C, freezing and carrying out embrittlement for at 30-40 degrees C for 3-5 hours, and performing ultrasonic wave breaking treatment to obtain second broken Ganoderma lucidum spore powder, (iii) placing second broken Ganoderma lucidum spore powder in a flash extractor, adding 50-85% ethanol solution for 2-3 hours, where the ratio between second broken Ganoderma lucidum spore powder and ethanol solution is 1:10-30, treating at 30-50 degrees C for 30-60 seconds, filtering the mixture to obtain filtrate and filter residue, then adding ethanol solution having a volume concentration of 75-85% into the residue in a ratio of 1:10-020, ultrasonic dispersing at 60 degrees C for 1-2 hours, filtrating, combining the filtrates, concentrating and recovering ethanol to obtain extract, and (iv) pumping the obtained extract into the extraction tower, uniformly introducing n-butanol, the ratio between extract and n-butanol is 1:1-3, and adding into the extraction tower for high-speed countercurrent extraction, controlling the extraction temperature to 40-60 degrees C, carrying out cyclic extraction for 3-5 times to obtain extract, concentrating the n-butanol and recovering, and freeze-drying under vacuum to obtain polysaccharide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), Ganoderma lucidum spore cutting into sheets having a thickness of 0.5 mm. In step (ii), the conditions for ultrasonic wall breaking are: the ultrasonic frequency is 18-24 kHz, the time is 4-16 minutes, and the rotation speed is 20-50 revolutions per minute. In step (iii), the ultrasonic frequency is 120-180 Hz.

TI Method for manufacturing super large diameter thick wall X80 steel grade spiral submerged arc welded pipe, involves unwinding and leveling, where shielding gas is mixed gas of argon and welding process comprises internal welding process.
AU SUN Z

   GU H

AE CHINA NAT PETROLEUM CORP (CNPC-C)

   CHINA NAT PETROLEUM CORP (CNPC-C)
GA 2018724507
AB    NOVELTY - A spiral submerged arc welded pipe manufacturing method involves unwinding, leveling, milling, pre-bending, forming, pre-welding, precision welding, round end of pipe end, X-ray inspection of weld seams, hydrostatic pressure test, automatic detection of parent metal ultrasonic, automatic ultrasonic testing of weld seam, ultrasonic manual inspection, mechanical flat head, visual inspection and anti-corrosion coating, the milling process, the groove is set as a double V type composite groove, the angle of the outer groove surface of the upper slope is 8.75-9.25 degrees , the up-slope depth H1 is 6.70-6.80 mm and the lower slope is 8.75-9.25 degrees , the pre-welding process uses the MIG welding to weld the welding groove on the inner side of the steel pipe, where the shielding gas is a mixed gas of argon, carbon dioxide and oxygen, the welding process comprises internal welding process and external welder and the three-wire series submerged arc automatic welding process is adopted.
   USE - Method for manufacturing super large diameter thick wall X80 steel grade spiral submerged arc welded pipe.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A spiral submerged arc welded pipe manufacturing method involves unwinding, leveling, milling, pre-bending, forming, pre-welding, precision welding, round end of pipe end, X-ray inspection of weld seams, hydrostatic pressure test, automatic detection of parent metal ultrasonic, automatic ultrasonic testing of weld seam, ultrasonic manual inspection, mechanical flat head, visual inspection and anti-corrosion coating, where the milling process, the groove is set as a double V type composite groove, the angle of the outer groove surface of the upper slope is 8.75-9.25 degrees , the up-slope depth H1 is 6.70-6.80 mm, the outer slope angle alpha 2 of the lower slope is 8.75-9.25 degrees , the slope angle beta 2 of the lower groove is 39.75-40.25 degrees , the lower groove depth H2 is 5.60-5.70 mm, the blunt edge height P is 9.00-9.50 mm and the inflection point size H is 2.45-2.55 mm, the pre-welding process uses the MIG welding to weld the welding groove on the inner side of the steel pipe, the shielding gas is a mixed gas of argon, carbon dioxide and oxygen, the welding process comprises internal welding process and external welder and the three-wire series submerged arc automatic welding process is adopted.
