PT P
PN WO2018139975-A1; SG11201904761-A1; CN110446685-A; CN110446685-B
TI Producing hydrated magnesium carbonate based            cement composite includes e.g. contacting aqueous            solution with magnesium oxide based cement to form            magnesium hydroxide and exposing magnesium hydroxide to            carbon dioxide to get cement composite.
AU UNLUER C
   NGUYEN T D
   RUAN T
AE UNIV NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL (UYTU-C)
GA 201860374E
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a hydrated magnesium carbonate based                cement composite, comprises: providing an aqueous                solution comprising a hydration agent and hydrated                magnesium carbonate seed particles; contacting the                aqueous solution with a magnesium oxide based                cement to form magnesium hydroxide (i) from the                hydration agent and the magnesium oxide based                cement, and (ii) on the hydrated magnesium                carbonate seed particles; and exposing the                magnesium hydroxide to carbon dioxide to form the                hydrated magnesium carbonate based cement                composite.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a hydrated                magnesium carbonate based cement composite.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the hydration agent and                seed particles that not only increase formation of                hydrated magnesium carbonate based cement composite                but also improves the mechanical performance of                reactive magnesium oxide cement (RMC) based                concrete formulations; utilizes the hydrated                magnesium carbonate seed particles that increase                the available contact area for hydration and                subsequent carbonation and the seed particles are                disposed homogenously in an aqueous solution during                preparation, thus enabling their thorough                dispersion in the pores of the original RMC and                avoiding accumulation and/or agglomeration of seed                particles on the original RMC; utilizes the seed                particles that serve as nucleation sites for                formation of magnesium hydroxide within the pores                and away from the original RMC grains, this not                only mitigates precipitation of magnesium hydroxide                on the original RMC grains and also provides                additional magnesium hydroxide available for                carbonation to form the hydrated magnesium                carbonate cement composite and the resultant cement                composite attains a denser structure which in turn                translates to improved compressive strength;                overcomes the limitations via use of a hydration                agent to improve the dissolution of magnesium oxide                and increase the quantity of magnesium hydroxide                (aqueous, solid) available for carbonation and the                provision of additional nucleation sites dispersed                into the pore spaces of the starting cement                material to facilitate continuous hydration and                carbonation by increasing the surface area for the                nucleation and growth of carbonate phases away from                the unhydrated magnesium oxide and uncarbonated                magnesium hydroxide; and reduces the overall curing                time, provides efficient material usage, high                performance and has a positive environmental impact                from producing the resultant cement                composite.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                hydrated magnesium carbonate based cement composite                obtainable by the method, where the hydrated                magnesium carbonate based cement composite                comprises hydrated magnesium carbonate formed from                a magnesium oxide based cement and hydrated                magnesium carbonate seed particles, where the                hydrated magnesium carbonate seed particles                comprise up to 1 wt.% of the magnesium oxide based                cement and the hydrated magnesium carbonate seed                particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Providing the aqueous                  solution comprises mixing the hydration agent                  with water before adding the hydrated magnesium                  carbonate seed particles. Providing the aqueous                  solution comprises mixing the hydration agent                  with water to form a concentration of 0-0.1 M.                  Providing the aqueous solution comprises adding                  0-1 wt.% of hydrated magnesium carbonate seed                  particles, where the wt.% is based on the                  magnesium oxide based cement and the hydrated                  magnesium carbonate seed particles. Providing the                  aqueous solution comprises stirring the aqueous                  solution in presence of the hydrated magnesium                  carbonate seed particles to disperse the hydrated                  magnesium carbonate seed particles throughout the                  aqueous solution. Contacting the aqueous solution                  with the magnesium oxide based cement comprises                  contacting the hydration agent with the magnesium                  oxide based cement at a mass ratio of                  0.55:1-0.7:1. Contacting the aqueous solution                  with the magnesium oxide based cement comprises                  disposing the hydrated magnesium carbonate seed                  particles within many pores of the magnesium                  oxide based cement. The magnesium hydroxide                  formed (ii) on the hydrated magnesium carbonate                  seed particles is disposed within the pores and                  away from the magnesium oxide based cement.                  Exposing the magnesium hydroxide to carbon                  dioxide comprises subjecting the magnesium                  hydroxide to 0.04-10 vol.% carbon dioxide at                  25-35 degrees C and a relative humidity of 75-85%                  for 24 hours or more. Exposing the magnesium                  hydroxide to carbon dioxide comprises subjecting                  the magnesium hydroxide to 0.04 vol.% carbon                  dioxide at 60 degrees C and a relative humidity                  of 75-85% for 1-2 days. The method further                  comprises subjecting the magnesium hydroxide to                  10 vol.% carbon dioxide at 30 degrees C and a                  relative humidity of 75-85% for a total carbon                  dioxide exposure period not exceeding 28 days.                  Preferred Components: The hydration agent                  comprises hydrochloric acid, magnesium acetate or                  magnesium chloride. The hydrated magnesium                  carbonate seed particles and the hydrated                  magnesium carbonate comprise magnesium carbonate                  trihydrate, artinite, magnesium carbonate                  magnesium hydroxide tetrahydrate                  ((MgCO3)4.Mg(OH)2.4H2O) or magnesium carbonate                  magnesium hydroxide pentahydrate                  ((MgCO3)4.Mg(OH)2.5H2O). The hydrated magnesium                  carbonate seed particles are disposed in many                  pores within the hydrated magnesium carbonate                  based cement composite. The hydrated magnesium                  carbonate is formed as particles comprising an                  elongated shape, where the particles are disposed                  on the hydrated magnesium carbonate seed                  particles. Each of the hydrated magnesium                  carbonate seed particles comprises a diameter of                  1-7 mu m. The hydrated magnesium carbonate based                  cement composite further comprises hydrated                  magnesium carbonate formed as a layered structure                  or a plated structure.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

TI Bacterial culture experimental apparatus            comprising a tank, a pneumatic device, a plumbing            device, an illumination device, a gas supply device and            a humidity control device.
AU YUE H
AE YUE H (YUEH-Individual)
GA 2018533415
AB    NOVELTY - Bacterial culture experimental apparatus                comprising a tank (1), a pneumatic device (2), a                plumbing device (3), an illumination device (4), a                gas supply device (5), and a humidity control                device. The cabinet includes a power cord, a                controller, a display, a front side panel and a                microscope. The display is obliquely fixed to the                rear side edge of the top of the box. The                controller is disposed at the rear of the upper                bottom surface of the cabinet and electrically                connected to the display. The microscope runs                through the top center of the box. The front side                panel is connected to the front side of the cabinet                by a hinge. The power line is connected to the                controller. The plumbing device comprises a water                tank, a heating plate, a temperature sensor and the                water tank (31) is disposed at the bottom of the                tank and at the left end of the water tank                protruding out of the box. The heating plate is                fixed in the center of the water tank.
   USE - Used as bacterial culture experimental                device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                performing bacterial culture, comprising (i)                connecting the power line to a 220V AC power                source, (ii) placing the bacteria to be cultured in                a petri dish, placing in the box and maintaining on                the same axis as the microscope, (iii) controlling                the growth of bacteria according to the influence                of the variables, adjusting the brightness of the                LED strip through the display, controlling the                heating plate to heat the water in the water tank                through the display, controlling the oxygen                cylinder and the carbon dioxide bottle through the                display to inject oxygen or carbon dioxide into the                interior of the tank, lifting the air by the air                pump, monitoring the air pressure by the air                pressure sensor and reducing the air pressure by                the air blower, increasing the humidity in the box                by the spray head and giving feedback the data to                the display in real time by the humidity sensor,                (iv) observing the growth of the bacteria by a                microscope and analyzing the culture conditions of                the bacteria in combination with the variables                adjusted on the display and (v) taking out the                culture dish, disconnecting the power supply,                opening the water outlet and emptying the water in                the water tank at the end of the experiment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the bacterial culture experimental                device.
   Tank (1)
   Pneumatic device (2)
   Plumbing device (3)
   Illumination device (4)
   Gas supply device (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The inner side edge of                  the front side panel is provided with a sealing                  ring. The electromagnetic lock is disposed on a                  top of the front side panel. The electromagnetic                  lock is electrically connected to the controller.                  The left end of the water tank is provided with a                  rectangular water injection port. The display is                  a touch screen display. The water outlet is                  provided at the lower left of the water tank. The                  sealing ring is provided on the inner side of the                  front side panel.

TI Preparing perovskite type metal cerium salt            composite photo-catalytic material with transition            metal ion regulation and control performance used in            field of photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction of            carbon comprises ultrasonicating perovskite-type            transition metal cerium salt, and grinding.
AU ZOU Z
   ZHU M
   ZHANG Y
   WANG J
   DU P
   LI C
AE UNIV NANJING (UNAJ-C)
GA 202291220S
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises (i) taking perovskite-type                transition metal cerium salt, preparing compounds                containing cerium nitrate and corresponding                transition metal ions in an equimolar ratio,                dispersing in deionized water, and stirring                uniformly to obtain A solution, (ii) adding                anhydrous citric acid to ethylene glycol solution,                ultrasonicating for 1-15 minutes to obtain B                solution dropwise into A solution, then stirring to                obtain a transparent solution, (iii) adding B                solution to A solution, stirring to obtain a                transparent solution, (iv) placing transparent                solution in a blast oven, drying at                70&#177;10&#8451; for more than 16 hours,                placing the obtained product in a mortar for                grinding, pouring into a crucible after grinding,                subsequently placing in a muffle furnace for low                temperature pretreatment for 60-240 minutes and                removing impurities, and (v) grinding resulting                impurity-removed mixture powder, then placing in a                high-temperature muffle furnace for                high-temperature reaction, and cooling.

PT P
PN CN114591796-A
TI Port wine comprises main material comprising rose            fragrance, golden fingers, and giant rose, and additive            material comprising sulfur dioxide, pectinase, yeast,            lactic acid bacteria, potato protein, tannin, and            tartaric acid.
AU ZHANG J
AE GUIZHOU NIANZUNGUO WINE IND CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2022806846
AB    NOVELTY - Port wine comprises main material comprising                rose fragrance, golden fingers, and giant rose, and                additive material comprising sulfur dioxide,                pectinase, yeast, lactic acid bacteria, potato                protein, tannin, and tartaric acid.
   USE - As port wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The port wine is capable of preventing grape                juice from oxidizing, by auxiliary nutrition                auxiliary agent, accelerating fermentation speed,                and increasing wine taste and flavor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the port wine, which involves using                clear juice fermentation by (i) adding 40 g/kl                pectinase press for rose fragrance, golden finger                and giant rose, squeezing the clear juice by                airbag, after the effect of pectinase press is                completed, adding 60 mg/l metapotassium sulfur                dioxide to the clear juice to adjust the flow, and                after the flow adjustment is completed, adding 200                g/Kl tannin Galalcool (RTM: Highly pure extract of                gallic tannins) and tartaric acid, (ii)                pre-controlling the temperature of the tank,                pre-charging carbon dioxide, placing the clear                juice in step (i) to the tank, maintaining the                temperature of the clear juice at 16-18degrees                Celsius, and clarifying for 2 days, (iii)                separation of clear juice and cloudy juice by                separating the clear juice obtained in step (ii) in                a tank pre-filled with carbon dioxide, controlling                the temperature of the tank to prepare for                subsequent alcoholic fermentation inoculation with                yeast, adding 40 g/kl pectinase CL to the turbid                juice, gradually separating the lower clear juice                and the residue, recycling the top 1-2 cm into the                clear juice tank, leaving the peel and residue in                the tank, continuously fermenting, and pay                attention to exhaust air during the process; when                the fermentation is over, (iv) performing alcohol                fermentation by controlling the temperature in the                tank to 16degrees Celsius, adding 200 g/kl yeast,                adding 1 kg/kl fresh oak chips, and 200 g/kl potato                protein, reducing the specific gravity by 10                points, adding organic nitrogen source TH.PH of 200                g/kl, adjusting the specific gravity of sugar at                1050, adding inorganic nitrogen source TH of 200                g/kl, extracting the clear juice back to the peel                residue, soaking the peel, and separating after a                week, and (v) fermentation is over; filling the                tank with the same variety of wine, controlling the                temperature to 10degrees Celsius, settling                naturally for 3-5 days, adding chlorine dioxide to                adjust sulfur after pouring the tank, and adding 10                g/ton of lactic acid bacteria until there is no                sign of fermentation at all.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method for adding                  pectinase involves using demineralized water at                  room temperature about 10 times the amount of                  pectinase in a container, adding solid pectinase                  to completely dissolve, adding liquid pectin                  directly without dissolving, and uniformly adding                  before the grapes enter the press. The yeast                  adding method involves adding 10 times the amount                  of demineralized water to a clean stainless steel                  container at 35-38degrees Celsius, opening the                  package and slowly sprinkling the yeast, gently                  stirring, leaving still for activation for 20                  minutes, slowly adding a certain amount of grape                  juice to the activated yeast liquid, so that the                  temperature is slowly lowered to 26degrees                  Celsius, adding yeast nutrients dissolved in                  water, and adding grape juice, mixing and leaving                  still for 20 minutes until the two coolings are                  similar to the temperature of the grape mash in                  the tank, where the temperature difference                  between each cooling should not exceed 10degrees                  Celsius, controlling the whole process within 45                  minutes, inoculating the grape juice to be                  inoculated, and uniformly circulating. The tannin                  addition method involves dissolve with 10 times                  of grape juice, adding to the tank after uniform                  without agglomeration, and uniformly circulating.                  The tartaric acid adding method involves dissolve                  with 10 times of grape juice, adding to the                  target tank after uniform without agglomeration,                  and uniformly circulating.

An upper end of a support fixing rod passes through the through hole and is fixedly connected with a lower-end of a magnetic plate (14). The outer end of the flexible heat conducting plate is fixedly connected with connecting block. The outer end of the connecting block (7 is rotatably connected with a movable cylinder (9) through a rotating shaft and a bearing.
   USE - Anti-toppling high-strength solar photovoltaic bracket for placing, mounting and fixing a solar photo-voltaic panel.
   ADVANTAGE - The bracket realizes under the action of the wind, releasing carbon dioxide solution in the ball shaking, the carbon dioxide gas overflows into the expansion air bag, the expansion extrusion flexible heat-conducting plate, and the memory torsion spring is heated and twisted under the heat-conduction effect, driving the movable cylinder to deflect, and driving movable ball to move downwards under the effect of gravity, shrinking the insulating magnetic film, canceling the magnetic shielding of the magnet block, repelling the magnetic plate to drive the supporting fixing rod to move to the ground phase contact cooperating with the grab floor to play the role of supporting and fixing, and at the same time, part of the gas is blown into the telescopic hose, extending to drive extruding fixing sheet tightly against the photovoltaic bracket main body

TI Composition used for hydrocarbon conversion processes, and selective catalytic reduction of nitric oxide, carbon dioxide and/or nitrogen adsorption, carbonylation reactions, and to produce monoalkylamine, comprises aluminosilicate zeolite.
AU BURTON A W
   VROMAN H B
   FALKOWSKI J M
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 202221792F
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises: an aluminosilicate zeolite having a molar ratio of silicon to aluminum of 3-10, where the aluminosilicate zeolite has a monoclinic space group C2/m with unit cell dimensions of 13.6 Angstrom plus minus 5%, b of 21.7 Angstrom plus minus 5%, c of 6.7 Angstrom plus minus 5%, and beta of 93 degrees plus minus 3 degrees , 12-ring pores along a c-axis having dimensions of 7 Angstrom plus minus 5% by 6 Angstrom plus minus 5%, and 8-ring pores along an a-axis having dimensions of 3 Angstrom plus minus 5% by 3 Angstrom plus minus 5%.
   USE - The composition is useful for hydrocarbon conversion processes (claimed), and selective catalytic reduction of nitric oxide, carbon dioxide and/or nitrogen adsorption, carbonylation reactions, and producing monoalkylamine and dialkylamine.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition utilizes zeolites that can be used as a catalyst to facilitate at least one organic compound conversion processes including many of present commercial/industrial importance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) hydrocarbon conversion processes (M1) comprising performing a hydrocarbon conversion process in the presence of the composition, where the hydrocarbon conversion process comprises cracking, hydrocracking, disproportionation, alkylation, or isomerization;
   (2) an aluminosilicate zeolite having coordination sequences of T-atoms of T1 4 10 19 32 48 69 98 126 154 192, T2 4 11 17 30 48 72 100 125 150 186, T3 4 10 20 32 47 69 96 126 159 191 and T44 10 19 31 48 71 97 125 156 189; and
   (3) producing (M2) aluminosilicate zeolite, comprising maintaining an aqueous reaction mixture comprising a silicon atom source, an aluminum atom source, and a structure directing agent comprising N,N',2-trimethylbenzimidazolium and/or N,N',2-dimethylbenzimidazolium, to a temperature of 135-180 degrees C to yield an aluminosilicate zeolite having a molar ratio of silicon to aluminum of 3-10 and a structure having a monoclinic space group C2/m with unit cell dimensions of a of 13.6 Angstrom plus minus 5%, b of 21.7 Angstrom plus minus 5%, c of 6.7 Angstrom plus minus 5%, and beta of 93 degrees plus minus 3 degrees , 12-ring pores along a c-axis having dimensions of 7 Angstrom plus minus 5% by 6 Angstrom plus minus 5%, and 8-ring pores along an a-axis having dimensions of 3 Angstrom plus minus 5% by 3 Angstrom plus minus 5%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition has an X-ray diffraction pattern including d-spacings of 11.7-11.9 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 20-40%, d-spacings of 10.8-11.1 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 35-55%, d-spacings of 6.8-7 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 20-40%, d-spacings of 6.4-6.6 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 35-55%, d-spacings of 5.7-5.9 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 10-30%, d-spacings of 4.7-4.9 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 80-100%, d-spacings of 4.4-4.6 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 30-50%, d-spacings of 3.69-3.75 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 10-30%, d-spacings of 3.65-3.68 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 20-40%, d-spacings of 3.56-3.62 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 50-70%, d-spacings of 3.44-3.49 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 50-70%, d-spacings of 3.19-3.24 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 40-60%, and d-spacings of 3.04-3.09 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 20-40% in an as-synthesized form. The composition has an X-ray diffraction pattern including d-spacings of 11.5-11.7 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 80-100%, d-spacings of 10.8-11.1 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 60-80%, d-spacings of 6.7-6.9 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 40-60%, d-spacings of 6.3-6.5 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 20-40%, d-spacings of 4.71-4.75 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 60-80%, d-spacings of 4.42-4.5 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 15-35%, d-spacings of 4.2-4.28 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 15-35%, d-spacings of 3.65-3.7 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 10-25%, d-spacings of 3.6-3.64 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 10-25%, d-spacings of 3.54-3.59 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 25-45%, d-spacings of 3.4-3.44 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 35-55%, d-spacings of 3.17-3.23 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 45-65%, and d-spacings of 3.02-3.05 d( Angstrom ) and peak relative percent areas of 15-30%, in a calcined form. The composition further comprises at least one of mordenite, a DON-type zeolite, analcime, quartz, and cristobalite. Preferred Components: The aluminosilicate zeolite further comprises a hydrogenating metal. The aqueous reaction mixture has a molar ratio of silicon to aluminum of 2-50, a molar ratio of silicon to the structure directing agent of 0.1-1 and a molar ratio of water to silicon of 20-100. The silicon atom source comprises a colloidal suspensions of silica, a precipitated silica alkali metal silicate, tetraalkyl orthosilicate, and/or a different silicon-containing zeolite. The aluminum atom source comprises aluminum nitrate, aluminum sulfate, sodium aluminate, aluminum oxide, alumina sol, alumina trihydrate, a clay and/or another aluminum-containing zeolite. The X-ray diffraction pattern of the aluminosilicate zeolite in an as-synthesized form has the d-spacings and peak relative percent areas of composition. The X-ray diffraction pattern of the calcined aluminosilicate zeolite has the d-spacings and peak relative percent areas of composition. Preferred Method: The temperature is 150-170 degrees C. The method (M2) further comprises: maintaining the aqueous reaction mixture of 135-180 degrees C for 20-36 days; treating the aluminosilicate zeolite with acid or steam then acid; and calcining the aluminosilicate zeolite at 500-900 degrees C to produce a calcined aluminosilicate zeolite, where the aqueous reaction mixture further comprises seeds having a structure of the aluminosilicate zeolite. The maintaining the aqueous mixture further yields at least one comprising mordenite, a DON-type zeolite, analcime, quartz, or cristobalite.

PT P
PN CN114016990-A
TI Direct oil extraction and waste heat utilization            system for realizing underground electric heating of            oil-rich coal for low-carbon green environment            development, has dynamic blower that blows air into            shaft, where waste heat is returned to production well            through channel and outputted to next-block.
AU CHE D
   MAO Q
   LIHUI Y
   WANG C
AE HUANENG GROUP TECHNOLOGY INNOVATION CENT (CHHN-C)
   UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 202234231R
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a wind-solar complementary                power generation device (1) for generating electric                energy and conveying the electric energy to an                electric heating well (3), where purified                alkane-based combustible gas is transported to a                combustion chamber (20) through a gas channel (15),                and mixed with twice preheated air and ignited to                generate high-temperature flue gas. The channels                are densely distributed in a coal seam area and are                connected to a flue gas heating shaft. The electric                heating well heats a local block after direct                pyrolysis of underground oil-rich coal in a local                block is completed. A dynamic blower (21) blows air                into an air cooling shaft, where air absorbs waste                heat in the coal seam area, returns the waste heat                to a production well through an air cooling                transverse channel and the waste heat is outputted                to a next-block.
   USE - Direct oil extraction and waste heat                utilization system for realizing underground                electric heating of oil-rich coal for low-carbon                green environment development.
   ADVANTAGE - The system effectively reduces resource waste                and environment pollution caused by the direct                pyrolysis of the underground oil-rich coal, and has                great economic benefit and use value. The system                outputs oil and traps carbon dioxide and geological                sealing so as to reduce discharge of carbon dioxide                into atmosphere, slow down atmospheric pollution,                and ensures that rest part of substance and energy                are fully used. PN CN113717191-A
TI Preparing sesamin comprises crushing sesame meal, mixing with pure water, adding cellulase and neutral protease, collecting enzymolysis liquid, centrifuging, mixing collected oily substance and pure water, vacuum drying, adding dried material into extraction kettle, extracting and collecting.
AU SUN H
   HE S
   MA J
   LI D
   MA Y
AE ANHUI FENGYANG YUSHAN OIL CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2021E9893L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sesamin comprises (i) crushing the sesame meal; (ii) mixing the crushed sesame meal obtained in step (i) with pure water to prepare emulsion; (iii) adding cellulase and neutral protease into the emulsion and collecting enzymolysis liquid; (iv) centrifuging the enzymolysis liquid for 15-30 minutes with the rotating speed of 7500-10000 revolutions/minute and collecting the uppermost oil substance; (v) mixing the collected oily substance and pure water according to mass ratio of 1:3-5, uniformly stirring and mixing, centrifuging at a rotating speed of 5000-6000 revolutions/minute for 10-15 minutes and collecting the upper oily substance; (vi) vacuum drying the oily substance and collecting the dried material; (vii) adding the dried material into the extraction kettle of carbon dioxide supercritical extraction device, extracting at 50-53degrees Celsius for 45-60 minutes with the pressure of 28-30 MPa and collecting the extracted product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sesamin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can reach content of sesamin in the extracted product about 2537% and has extraction rate of sesamin in sesame meal is about 0.100.12%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing sesamin comprises (i) crushing the sesame meal till the fineness is more than or equal to 100 meshes; (ii) mixing the crushed sesame meal obtained in step (i) with pure water according to mass ratio of 1:4-6 to prepare emulsion; (iii) adding cellulase and neutral protease into the emulsion, where the pH value of 6.5-8, the temperature is 40-45degrees Celsius, the stirring speed is 15-30 revolutions/minute under the condition of enzymolysis for 2.5-3.5 hours and collecting enzymolysis liquid; (iv) centrifuging the enzymolysis liquid for 15-30 minutes with the rotating speed of 7500-10000 revolutions/minute and collecting the uppermost oil substance; (v) mixing the collected oily substance and pure water according to mass ratio of 1:3-5, stirring and mixing uniformly, centrifuging at a rotating speed of 5000-6000 revolutions/minute for 10-15 minutes and collecting the upper oily substance; (vi) vacuum drying the oily substance and collecting the dried material; (vii) adding the dried material into the extraction kettle of carbon dioxide supercritical extraction device, extracting at 50-53degrees Celsius fo 45-60 minutes with the pressure of 28-30 MPa and collecting the extracted product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The enzyme activity of the cellulase is 50,000-200,000 U, and the added amount is 0.15-0.25% of the mass of sesame meal. The enzyme activity of the neutral protease is 50,000-200,000 U, and the added amount is 0.50-0.75% of the mass of the sesame meal.Preferred Method: In the step (ii) mixing the crushed sesame meal obtained in the step (i) with pure water, adding into an emulsifying device, using the rotating speed of 8000-12000 revolutions/minute for 15-30 minutes, until the mixed material presents an emulsifying state and collecting to obtain the emulsion. In the step (iv), adding 2-4% ice cubes to pure water. In the step (vi) vacuum drying at 55-65degrees Celsius for 45-90 minutes with the vacuum gauge pressure is -0.09 to -0.10 MPa.

TI Preparation of battery-grade lithium dihydrogen phosphate used for forming anode material, involves mixing phosphoric acid and pure water, adding lithium carbonate, obtaining lithium dihydrogen phosphate solution, adding activated carbon, heating, concentrating and drying slurry.
AU WANG L
AE SHANGHAI YULI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021B5692H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of battery-grade lithium dihydrogen phosphate, involves mixing phosphoric acid and pure water, feeding phosphoric acid solution in reactor, adding high-purity lithium carbonate to obtain solution (A), boiling and removing carbon dioxide in solution (A), adjusting pH, obtaining lithium dihydrogen phosphate solution, adding 1-3 vol.% activated carbon to absorb organic substance, filtering and brightening to obtain solution (B), heating and concentrating, controlling temperature at 115-135degrees Celsius to obtain lithium dihydrogen phosphate concentrated solution (C), cooling solution (C) in reactor, reducing temperature to 20-40degrees Celsius for liquid-solid separation, obtaining lithium dihydrogen phosphate slurry (P1) and lithium dihydrogen phosphate mother liquor (Y1), dehydrating mother liquor (Y1), obtaining lithium dihydrogen phosphate crude product (P2), and washing with solution (C), dehydrating, washing, and drying slurry (P1) in vacuum.
   USE - Preparation of battery-grade lithium dihydrogen phosphate for synthesizing anode material for lithium-ion battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method removes soluble impurities and insoluble impurities by physical purification, which reduces amount of soluble impurity in the mother liquor, increases cycle stability, eliminates repeated crystallization of mother liquor, reduces energy consumption and cost, has short process flow, improves recovery rate, and obtain more stable, fine and uniform product particles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of battery-grade lithium dihydrogen phosphate involves stirring and mixing phosphoric acid and pure water in ratio of 1:3, feeding phosphoric acid solution in reactor, adding required amount of high-purity lithium carbonate to obtain solution (A), where ratio of phosphoric acid and lithium carbonate and (1:1)-(1:1.1), boiling at normal pressure for 30 minutes, removing carbon dioxide in solution (A), adjusting pH of neutralized solution to 1-3 using lithium hydroxide or phosphoric acid in mixed solution (A), obtaining lithium dihydrogen phosphate solution, adding 1-3 vol.% activated carbon, and reacting for 0.5-3 hours to absorb organic substance, filtering and brightening to obtain solution (B), heating by steam, evaporating and concentrating solution (B), controlling temperature at 115-135degrees Celsius to obtain lithium dihydrogen phosphate concentrated solution (C), evaporating and concentrating in reactor for cooling, reducing temperature to 20-40degrees Celsius for liquid-solid separation, obtaining lithium dihydrogen phosphate wet product (P1) and lithium dihydrogen phosphate slurry (Y1), dehydrating slurry (Y1), obtaining lithium dihydrogen phosphate crude product (P2), washing with lithium dihydrogen phosphate saturated solution, dehydrating, and repeatedly washing 2-6 times, and drying product (P1) in vacuum state at -0.03 to -0.09 MPa and at 105-130degrees Celsius for 2-4 hours.

PN CN113233491-A
TI Removing impurities from calcium fluoride comprises e.g. precipitating calcium fluoride with potassium chloride and boric acid, separating solid and liquid, drying and crushing the calcium fluoride.
AU ZHANG J
AE JIANGXI QUCHENG FLUORINE CHEM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202194926H
AB    NOVELTY - Removing impurities from calcium fluoride comprises e.g. (i) adding hydrogen chloride solution into reaction kettle, adding calcium fluoride with impurities and reacting potassium chloride and boric acid; (ii) obtaining mixed solution of gelatinous potassium borofluoride and calcium chloride aqueous solution; (iii) precipitating gel-shaped potassium fluoborate, separating solid and liquid, and adding calcium chloride aqueous solution into the reaction kettle; (iv) adding sodium carbonate into the reaction kettle and stirring; (v) obtaining calcium carbonate precipitate and sodium chloride aqueous solution; (vi) settling and precipitating calcium carbonate, subjecting to solid-liquid separation, and adding the calcium carbonate into reactor; (vii) reacting hydrofluoric acid aqueous solution with calcium carbonate; (viii) obtaining calcium fluoride precipitate, carbon dioxide and water; and (ix) allowing calcium fluoride and water to stand, separating solid-liquid, drying and crushing.
   USE - The method is useful for removing impurities from calcium fluoride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: does not produce polluting waste; and reduces the possibility of environmental pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing impurities from calcium fluoride comprises (i) adding hydrogen chloride solution into the reaction kettle, adding calcium fluoride with impurities in specific proportion to fully dissolve it with stirring, adding potassium chloride and boric acid into the reaction kettle to react fully, where the end pH of the reaction is 7; (ii) obtaining mixed solution of gelatinous potassium borofluoride and calcium chloride aqueous solution; (iii) allowing mixed solution to stand, precipitating the gel-shaped potassium fluoborate, separating solid and liquid of calcium chloride aqueous solution and potassium fluoborate, and adding calcium chloride aqueous solution into the reaction kettle; (iv) adding the sodium carbonate into the reaction kettle containing calcium chloride aqueous solution and stirring to fully react, where the reaction end point pH value is 7; (v) obtaining calcium carbonate precipitate and sodium chloride aqueous solution; (vi) allowing the mixed solution to stand, settling and precipitating calcium carbonate, subjecting the sodium chloride aqueous solution and the calcium carbonate to solid-liquid separation, and adding the calcium carbonate into the reactor again; (vii) adding the hydrofluoric acid aqueous solution into the reaction kettle in proportion to fully react with calcium carbonate by stirring, where the reaction end point pH value is 7; (viii) obtaining calcium fluoride precipitate, carbon dioxide and water; and (ix) allowing the calcium fluoride and water to stand, settling and precipitating calcium fluoride, carrying out solid-liquid separation, drying and crushing the calcium fluoride to obtain the calcium fluoride product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the molar ratio of calcium fluoride, hydrogen chloride solution, potassium chloride and boric acid is 2:3:1:1. In the step (v), the molar ratio of calcium chloride and sodium carbonate is 1:1. In the step (viii), the molar ratio of calcium carbonate and hydrofluoric acid aqueous solution is 1:2. The molar ratio of calcium fluoride, hydrogen chloride solution, potassium chloride and sodium tetraborate pentahydrate is 8:14:4:1. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the boric acid is replaced by sodium tetraborate pentahydrate.

GA 2021657139
AB    NOVELTY - Healthcare tea and its preparation method, are claimed. The healthcare tea is prepared by (a) pulverizing Agriophyllum squarrosum seeds, adding powder to a extraction kettle of supercritical extraction equipment, controlling flow, pressure, temperature and time of carbon dioxide in extraction process, and circulating extraction to obtain crude extract of A.squarrosum seed, (b) obtaining A.squarrosum seed extract, (c) obtaining pear peel concentrate, (d) obtaining elderberry fruit extract, and (e) adding A.squarrosum seed extract, pear skin concentrate, elderberry fruit extract, Chrysanthemum, licorice and mulberry leaves to deionized water, stirring and mixing evenly, heat-soaking, sieving to obtain the filtrate, concentrating to a relative density of 1.2-1.4 g/cm3, adding with deionized water and honey, stirring to dissolve, filtering, and dividing into packaging, and sterilizing tea.

   ADVANTAGE - The healthcare tea is rich in protein, flavonoid and triterpenoid content, is very suitable for middle-aged and elderly people to eat, and has excellent taste, and no toxic and side effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Healthcare tea and its preparation method, are claimed. The healthcare tea is prepared by (a) pulverizing Agriophyllum squarrosum seeds using a plant crusher to obtain A.squarrosum seed powder, adding powder to a extraction kettle of supercritical extraction equipment, controlling flow, pressure, temperature and time of carbon dioxide in extraction process, and circulating extraction to obtain crude extract of A.squarrosum seed, (b) adding the crude extract of A.squarrosum seed and deionized water to molecular distillation equipment for refining, controlling speed, pressure, heater temperature and heating time, and obtaining A.squarrosum seed extract, after the refining is finished, (c) adding deionized water solvent and pear peel powder to the distillation flask, adding edible ethanol, extracting and purifying, and obtaining pear peel concentrate, (d) adding elderberry fruit to deionized water, heating to 80-100 degrees C, performing ultrasonic-assisted treatment, performing heat preservation and extraction, obtaining elderberry fruit extract, using gauze to filter the extract, and vacuum-filtering to obtain the elderberry fruit extract, and (e) adding A.squarrosum seed extract, pear skin concentrate, elderberry fruit extract, Chrysanthemum, licorice and mulberry leaves to deionized water, stirring and mixing evenly, heat-soaking, sieving to obtain the filtrate, concentrating to a relative density of 1.2-1.4 g/cm3, adding with deionized water and honey, stirring to dissolve, filtering, and dividing into packaging, and sterilizing tea.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves (i) performing supercritical extraction at extraction pressure of 20-35 MPa, where flow rate of carbon dioxide is 15-35 kg/hour, and (ii) performing extraction at 20-60 degrees C for 1-4 hours. The step (b) involves (i) maintaining rotational speed to 240-270 revolutions per minute (rpm) and pressure to 2-4 Pa, during refining process of molecular distillation technology, and (ii) maintaining heating temperature of heater to 80-120 degrees C and heating time to 2-5 hours during the molecular distillation technology refining process. The step (c) involves using 50-90% food-grade ethanol, and performing extraction and purification at 60-90 degrees C for 40-100 minutes. The step (d) involves performing ultrasonic-assisted treatment at a frequency of 70-80 W for 60-90 minutes, and performing heat preservation and extraction at 60-80 degrees C for 45-75 minutes. The step (e) involves adding deionized water, A.squarrosum seed extract, pear skin concentrate, elderberry fruit extract, Chrysanthemum, licorice, mulberry leaves and honey in a mass ratio of 1000:2-5:1-4:0.5-3:1.5-8.5:2.5-5:3-5:5-20.
   ACTIVITY - Respiratory-Gen.; Antiinflammatory; Nootropic; Dermatological.

PN CN112573565-A
TI Preparing potassium stannate trihydrate using tin stripping waste liquid by adding sodium carbonate solution to waste tin stripping liquid to adjust pH, separating, reacting, adding composite impurity remover, separating and evaporating.
AU GAO D
AE GUANGDONG ZHENDING ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOL (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021373733
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing potassium stannate trihydrate using tin stripping waste liquid involves (i) adding waste tin stripping liquid to reaction kettle, adding sodium carbonate solution to adjust pH, adding liquid caustic soda to adjust pH, and performing first solid-liquid separation, (ii) adding alkali to reaction kettle, mixing with crude tin hydroxide solid, heating, reacting, performing second solid-liquid separation, adding composite impurity remover to remove impurities, performing third solid-liquid separation, passing carbon dioxide to adjust pH, performing fourth solid-liquid separation and washing, (iii) adding potassium hydroxide solution and high-purity tin hydroxide solid to reaction kettle, mixing, heating, reacting, adding composite impurity remover for impurity removal, and performing fifth solid-liquid separation to remove solid, and (iv) adding the obtained solution to concentration reactor for reaction, evaporating and concentrating, and feeding the material to a centrifuge.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing potassium stannate trihydrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, environmentally-friendly and economical, has strong market competitiveness. The product is white to colorless crystal with high purity, single composition and low free alkali, reaches analytical purity standard, and meets the need of various industries such as chemical analysis, chemical synthesis, and electroplating.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing potassium stannate trihydrate using tin stripping waste liquid involves (i) adding waste tin stripping liquid to reaction kettle, adding sodium carbonate solution to adjust pH to 0-0.5, adding liquid caustic soda to adjust pH to 5-8, and performing first solid-liquid separation to obtain crude tin hydroxide solid, (ii) adding alkali to the reaction kettle, mixing with the crude tin hydroxide solid, heating at 100-110 degrees C, reacting for 1-6 hours, performing second solid-liquid separation to obtain a tin-containing alkaline solution, adding composite impurity remover to remove impurities, performing third solid-liquid separation to obtain a pure tin-containing alkaline solution, passing carbon dioxide to adjust the pH to 8.5-10, performing fourth solid-liquid separation and washing to obtain high-purity tin hydroxide solid, (iii) adding potassium hydroxide solution and high-purity tin hydroxide solid to the reaction kettle, mixing, heating at 100-110 degrees C, reacting for 2-4 hours, adding composite impurity remover for impurity removal, and performing fifth solid-liquid separation to remove solid to obtain a pure potassium stannate solution, and (iv) adding the obtained solution to concentration reactor for reaction, evaporating and concentrating at a speed of 10-30 rpm until crystals precipitate in the solution, stop concentrating, and feeding the material to a centrifuge to obtain the product as crystal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (i), the tin stripping waste liquid comprises 10-25 wt% acid radical ion, 1-5 wt.% chloride ion, 0.2-5 wt.% cyclooctadiene (COD), 2-10 wt.% tin, 1-5 wt.% copper ion and 1-5 wt.% iron ion. In step (ii), the ratio of the amount of liquid caustic soda substance and the amount of crude tin hydroxide substance is (4-8):1. In step (ii), the addition amount of hydrogen peroxide is 0.1-0.5 wt.%, and the addition amount of activated carbon is 0.5-1 wt.%. In step (iii), the addition amount of hydrogen peroxide is 0.1-0.5 wt.%, and the addition amount of activated carbon is 0.5-1 wt.%. Preferred Components: The acid ion comprises nitrate ion or sulfate ion. In steps (i) and (ii), the alkali is a sodium hydroxide solution with a mass fraction of 30-32 wt.%.

PN CN112317505-A
TI Treating wet garbage by crushing, centrifuging and filtering wet garbage, adding biogas fermentation microorganisms, allowing it to stand, separating water-insoluble organic solvent, and processing lower layer liquid by sol-gel method.
AU WU D
AE HEFEI YAOCHUANG ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 202116843P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for performing wet garbage treatment involves (a) squeezing, crushing, centrifuging and filtering the wet garbage to obtain garbage filter residue and first-grade filtrate, (b) placing the filter residue in a biogas tank, adding biogas fermentation microorganisms to the biogas tank, adjusting a pH to 7-7.6, setting temperature to 35-50 degrees C, and using obtained fermentation residue as organic fertilizer, (c) allowing first-level filtrate to stand for layering, and separating water-insoluble organic solvent to obtain upper organic solvent and lower layer respectively, (d) injecting upper organic solvent into activated sludge to degrade organic pollutants and generate carbon dioxide and water, and (e) processing lower layer liquid by sol-gel method, vacuum-drying for 9 hours, and performing calcination at 300-450 degrees C for 4-7 hours to obtain the mixture powder, and obtaining leaching solution for ICP test, measuring that the mixture powder contains calcium ions, and zinc ions.
   USE - The method is useful for performing wet garbage treatment (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method saves process of purifying sewage, processing time and processing cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for performing wet garbage treatment involves (a) squeezing, crushing, centrifuging and filtering the wet garbage to obtain garbage filter residue and first-grade filtrate, (b) placing the filter residue in a biogas tank, adding biogas fermentation microorganisms to the biogas tank, adjusting a pH to 7-7.6, setting temperature to 35-50 degrees C, and using obtained fermentation residue as organic fertilizer, (c) allowing first-level filtrate to stand for layering, and separating water-insoluble organic solvent to obtain upper organic solvent and the lower layer respectively, (d) injecting upper organic solvent into activated sludge to degrade organic pollutants and generate carbon dioxide and water, and (e) processing lower layer liquid by sol-gel method, adjusting pH value of the lower layer liquid to 3-4, adding complexing agent, and stirring lower layer liquid at 50-60 degrees C for 2-4 hours, heating it at 80-90 degrees C, stirring for 3-4.5 hours, vacuum-drying for 9 hours, and performing calcination at 300-450 degrees C for 4-7 hours to obtain the mixture powder which is acid leached with a 2 mol/l hydrochloric acid solution, and obtaining leaching solution for ICP test, measuring that the mixture powder comprises calcium ions, cobalt ions, manganese ions, nickel ions, iron ions, zinc ions, and magnesium ions, which indicates that obtained mixture powder can be reused.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Microorganism: The biogas fermentation microorganisms include organic matter decomposing bacteria and methanogenic bacteria. The organic matter decomposing bacteria adopt several kinds of hemicellulolytic bacteria, starch decomposing bacteria, protein decomposing bacteria and lipolytic bacteria. The methanogenic bacteria adopt any one or two of hydrogen-eating methanogens and acetic acid-eating methanogens. Preferred Component: The activated sludge adopts any one or two of nitrifying bacteria and nitrosating bacteria. The complexing agent adopts any one or more of polyvinyl alcohol, stearic acid, citric acid and polyvinylpyrrolidone.

PN CN111676252-A
TI Reducing acetic acid production by-product of succinic acid fermentation involves inoculating single colony of Actinobacillus succinogenes GXAS137 into seed culture medium, and culturing it to obtain an expanded culture strain of bacteria.
AU SHEN N
   ZHANG H
   JIANG M
   WANG Y
AE UNIV GUANGXI NATIONALITIES (UGXN-C)
GA 2020936503
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing acetic acid production by-product of succinic acid fermentation involves inoculating single colony of Actinobacillus succinogenes GXAS137 on plate into seed culture medium, and culturing it at 36-38 degrees C for 16-20 hours until number of bacteria reaches more than 300 million to obtain an expanded culture strain of bacteria. The expanded culture strain is subjected to secondary expansion culture in an amount of 5% (V/V) inoculum for 8 hours until bacterial count reaches more than 300 million to obtain liquid seeds. The liquid seed is inoculated into fermentation medium at an inoculum of 5-15% (V/V), 0.05-2g/L sodium bisulfite is added at 0-12 hours after inoculation, and pH buffer is used to adjust pH of fermentation broth to maintain 6.5-7.0, and subjected to batch fermentation and fed-batch fermentation in an environment full of nitrogen or carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for reducing acetic acid production by-product of succinic acid fermentation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to reduce acetic acid production by-product of succinic acid fermentation, which reduces formation of by-product acetic acid, solves problem of high by-product acetic acid in process of microbial fermentation to produce succinic acid, reduces cost of separation and purification of succinic acid, and promotes development of succinic acid production industry by microbial fermentation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reducing acetic acid production by-product of succinic acid fermentation involves inoculating single colony of Actinobacillus succinogenes GXAS137 on plate into seed culture medium, and culturing it at 36-38 degrees C for 16-20 hours until number of bacteria reaches more than 300 million to obtain an expanded culture strain of bacteria. The expanded culture strain is subjected to secondary expansion culture in an amount of 5% (V/V) inoculum for 8 hours until bacterial count reaches more than 300 million to obtain liquid seeds. The liquid seed is inoculated into fermentation medium at an inoculum of 5-15% (V/V), 0.05-2g/L sodium bisulfite is added at 0-12 hours after inoculation, and pH buffer is used to adjust pH of fermentation broth to maintain 6.5-7.0, and subjected to batch fermentation and fed-batch fermentation in an environment full of nitrogen or carbon dioxide at a speed of 100-200 revolutions per minute and temperature of 36-38 degrees C for 30-72 hours to obtain fermentation broth containing succinic acid, which is separated and purified to obtain succinic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The sodium bisulfite addition time is 6-8 hours after inoculation. The added amount of sodium bisulfite is 0.1-0.5g/L. The fed-batch fermentation is performed by adding mother liquor with a total sugar concentration of 200-300 g/L and controlling total sugar of fermentation broth to 30-35g/L. Preferred Compositions: The seed culture medium comprises 8g/L glucose, 12g/L yeast powder, 4g/L corn syrup, 4g/L sodium bicarbonate, 9.6g/L monosodium phosphate and 1.55g/L dipotassium phosphate. The fermentation medium comprises 30-40g/L initial total sugar, 5-20g/L nitrogen source, 2-10g/L potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 2-10g/L sodium bicarbonate, 0.3-1.0g/L calcium chloride and 0.3-1.0g/L magnesium chloride. Preferred Components: The initial total sugar is one or more of sugar, sugar-containing raw materials, saccharification liquid of starchy raw materials, or hydrolyzed liquid of fiber raw materials. The sugar is one of glucose, sucrose and xylose. The sugar-containing raw material is one of sugarcane molasses and xylose mother liquor. The starchy raw material is one of cassava flour, corn flour and wheat flour. The fiber raw material is one of corn stover, bagasse and rice straw. The nitrogen source is one or more of corn steep liquor, yeast powder, soybean meal, and peanut meal powder. The pH buffer is one or more of basic magnesium carbonate, sodium carbonate, magnesium hydroxide, and sodium hydroxide, and total concentration is 20-80g/L.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.)

PT P
PN CN111592009-A
TI Preparing zeolite molecular sieve by mixing silicon source e.g. silica sol or tetraethyl orthosilicate, iron source and water, adjusting pH, adding aluminum source, adjusting pH, performing static crystallization, washing and drying.
AU WANG J
   ZHOU Y
   WANG Y
   LIU X
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2020857821
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a zeolite molecular sieve, involves (i) mixing a silicon source, an iron source and water, where the reaction mixture comprises iron/silicon dioxide in a molar ratio of 0.1-2% and water/silicon dioxide in the molar ratio of 30-40 and adding an acid solution to adjust pH, (ii) adding an aluminum source to the obtained mixed solution, where the aluminum source comprises silicon dioxide/aluminum oxide in the molar ratio of 10-30 and adding an alkali solution to adjust the pH and (iii) adding the obtained mixed solution to a high-pressure reaction kettle, performing static crystallization, washing and drying to prepare the zeolite molecular sieve. The silicon source is one of silica sol, tetramethyl orthosilicate, tetraethyl orthosilicate, tetramethylsilicic acid, silicic acid or silicon oxide. The iron source is one of ferric nitrate, ferric chloride and ferric sulfate. The acid solution is one of sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, nitric acid and perchloric acid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a zeolite molecular sieve used for separating carbon dioxide from nitrogen or methane gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides an efficient, low-cost, energy-saving and environmentally-friendly synthetic route to prepare heteroatom iron-substituted FM mordenite monolithic material for carbon dioxide capture, achieves significant effect and provides the adsorbent that is easy to reuse.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing a zeolite molecular sieve, involves (i) mixing a silicon source, an iron source and water, where the reaction mixture comprises iron/silicon dioxide in a molar ratio of 0.1-2% and water/silicon dioxide in the molar ratio of 30-40 and adding an acid solution to adjust pH to 0.4-3%, (ii) adding an aluminum source to the obtained mixed solution, where the aluminum source comprises silicon dioxide/aluminum oxide in the molar ratio of 10-30 and adding an alkali solution to adjust the pH to 9-13 and (iii) adding the obtained mixed solution to a high-pressure reaction kettle, performing static crystallization at 160-200 degrees C for 10-18 days, washing and drying to prepare the zeolite molecular sieve. The silicon source is one of silica sol, tetraethyl orthosilicate, tetraethyl orthosilicate, tetramethylsilicic acid, silicic acid or silicon oxide. The iron source is one of ferric nitrate, ferric chloride and ferric sulfate. The acid solution is one of sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, nitric acid and perchloric acid. The aluminum source is one of sodium metaaluminate, aluminum sulfate and aluminum chloride. The alkali solution is a sodium hydroxide solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves adjusting the pH to 0.8. The step (ii) involves adjusting the pH to 11. The step (iii) involves adopting the high-pressure reaction kettle which is a stainless steel high-pressure reaction kettle lined with polytetrafluoroethylene, where the temperature of the reaction kettle is 180 degrees C and the volume of the reaction kettle is 50 l. The step (iii) involves performing the static crystallization for 14 days. Preferred Composition: The reaction mixture in step (i) comprises iron/silicon dioxide in the molar ratio of 0.5%.

AB    NOVELTY - Genetically modified host cell comprising: a first polypeptide comprising a sequence having at least 70% amino acid sequence identity with SEQ ID NO: 1 (comprising a fully defined sequence of 839 amino acids as given in the specification) or SEQ ID NO: 2 (comprising a fully defined sequence of 839 amino acids as given in the specification), and having an enzymatic activity to decarboxylate a phenylacetic acid into a toluene and a carbon dioxide; and a second polypeptide comprising a sequence having at least 70% amino acid sequence identity with SEQ ID NO: 3 (comprising a fully defined sequence of 315 amino acids as given in the specification) or SEQ ID NO: 4 (comprising a fully defined sequence of 321 amino acids as given in the specification), and having an enzymatic activity to cleave a S-adenosylmethionine (SAM) to form a methionine and a 5'-deoxyadenosyl radical, which can activate the first polypeptide, is new.
   USE - The cell is useful for producing substituted or unsubstituted toluene or 2-methyl-1H-indole (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a genetically modified host cell comprising a first nucleic acid encoding the first polypeptide comprising a sequence having at least 70% amino acid sequence identity with SEQ ID NO: 1 or SEQ ID NO: 2, and having an enzymatic activity to decarboxylate a phenylacetic acid into a toluene and a carbon dioxide, and optionally the first nucleic acid, or a second nucleic acid, encoding the second polypeptide comprising a sequence having at least 70% amino acid sequence identity with SEQ ID NO: 3 or SEQ ID NO: 4, and having an enzymatic activity to cleave a SAM to form a methionine and a 5'-deoxyadenosyl radical, where the genetically modified host cell is capable of expressing the first and/or the second polypeptide;
   (2) producing a substituted or unsubstituted toluene or 2-methyl-1H-indole in a genetically modified host cell, comprising culturing the genetically modified host cell in a medium under a suitable condition such that the culturing results in the genetically modified host cell producing the substituted or unsubstituted toluene or 2-methyl-1H-indole; and
   (3) a composition comprising an isolated substituted or unsubstituted toluene or 2-methyl-1H-indole produced from the method, where the composition further comprises trace amounts of the genetically modified host cell, or its parts, and/or the medium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation: No preparation method is given. Preferred Components: The first polypeptide comprises a sequence having at least 75%, preferably 99% identical to the amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 1 or SEQ ID NO: 2. The first polypeptide comprises a sequence comprising a conserved glycyl radical motif. The first and/or second nucleic acids comprise a promoter operatively linked to the open reading frame(s) of the first and/or second polypeptides. The first and/or second polypeptides are heterologous to the genetically modified host cell and/or promoter. The medium comprises SAM and the genetically modified host cell can uptake or absorb SAM and/or an unsubstituted or substituted phenylacetic acid from the medium. The genetically modified host cell is capable of endogenously synthesizing SAM and/or an unsubstituted or substituted phenylacetic acid from a carbon source. PN CN111440663-A
TI Extracting essential oil components of Dregea volubilis comprises e.g. picking Dregea volubilis flowers, vacuum freeze-drying, decomposing and collecting the reaction gas on the upper side and extracting Dregea volubilis essential oil.
AU JIANG M
   LI W
   HU L
   HUANG X
   HUANG W
   LI G
   GAO X
   CHEN Y
AE UNIV YUNNAN MINZU (UNNN-C)
GA 202071713X
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting essential oil components of Dregea volubilis comprises (i) picking Dregea volubilis flowers, (ii) vacuum freeze-drying the Dregea volubilis flowers picked in step (i) and grinding, (iii) introducing the product obtained from step (ii) into the decomposition kettle, adding the decomposition enzyme solution into the decomposition kettle and collecting the upper reaction gas and (iv) (a) mixing the Dregea volubilis flower fragments containing decomposing enzymes in the decomposition kettle with water for steam low-pressure distillation, cooling the distilled gas to obtain crude essential oil, placing the crude essential oil in a centrifuge for centrifugation in middle to separating layered oil and water to obtain Dregea volubilis essential oil 1 and (b)introducing the reaction gas on the upper side of the decomposition kettle into the supercritical carbon dioxide device and allowing to stand.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting essential oil components of Dregea volubilis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: Improved the value of Dregea volubilis flowers; and does not need to use the carrying agent in the supercritical CO2 extraction process; and utilizes steam low pressure distillation and supercritical CO2 technology, which makes the essential oil extraction degree of Dregea volubilis flower reach the highest, and obtains two different qualities of Dregea volubilis essential oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting essential oil components of Dregea volubilis comprises (i) picking Dregea volubilisflowers, (ii) vacuum freeze-drying the Dregea volubilis flowers picked in step (i) and controlling the relative moisture content to 10-15% and then grinding, where the grinding temperature of the grinding device not greater than 5 degrees C, (iii) introducing product obtained in step (ii) into decomposition kettle, adding decomposition enzyme solution into the decomposition kettle, adjusting the pH of the decomposition kettle to 5-6, controlling the initial decomposition temperature of the decomposition kettle to 15-25 degrees C, decomposing for 7-8 hours and collecting the upper reaction gas and (iv) (a) mixing Dregea volubilis flower fragments containing decomposing enzymes in the decomposition kettle with water for steam low-pressure distillation, cooling the distilled gas to obtain crude essential oil, placing the crude essential oil in a centrifuge for centrifugation in the middle to separatingthe layered oil and water to obtain Dregea volubilis essential oil 1, where the steam low-pressure distillation temperature is 40-50 degrees C and (b) introducing the reaction gas on the upper side of the decomposition kettle into the supercritical carbon dioxide device, allowing to stand, taking the upper layer after 1-2 hours to obtain Dregea volubilis essential oil 2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The picking time of the Dregea volubilis flowers is from april to september and removing the stalks of the Dregea volubilis flowers after being picked. The air pressure of the steam low-pressure distillation is 0.05-0.1 MPa, the time for the low-pressure distillation of the steam is 1.5-2 hours. The rotation speed of the centrifugation at 5000-6000 revolutions/minute and the centrifugation time is 15-20 minutes. The pressure in the supercritical carbon dioxide device is 20-35 MPa and the flow rate is 10-15 kg/hour. The method comprises cooling the distilled gas by circulating water, the temperature of the circulating water is 10-15 degrees C. Preferred Components: The decomposition enzyme solution comprises cellulose hydrolase, hemicellulase, pectinase, protease and the decomposition enzyme solution contains decomposition enzyme not less than 85%.

TI Preparing wine involves picking up immediately            after evaporation of water vapor in morning light in            year of infection with noble rot fungi, picking grape            ears with Botrytised features one by one, and removing            empty shells.
AU NIU Y
   SONG M
   MA C
AE WINE PLANT GANSU MOGAO IND DEV CO LTD (WINE-Non-standard)
GA 202033927L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing wine involves picking up immediately                after evaporation of water vapor in the morning                light in the year of infection with noble rot                fungi, picking grape ears with Botrytised features                characteristics one by one, removing empty shells,                shriveled, juice-free fruit grains, and selecting                grapes infected with fungi, directly pressing the                whole ear of grapes into airbag pressing machine to                extract juice, adding 25 mg/l pectinase into grape                juice, then freezing with a freezer to lower the                temperature by 0 degrees C, and then clarifying for                alcohol fermentation, after the fermentation                started, opening cycle 1 time a day, sprinkling the                liquor from valve into an open container basin, and                continuously stirring the liquor in the basin to                overflow carbon dioxide, then using the pump to                spray liquor in the basin from the inlet of other                tank until all the liquor completely transferred,                when the fermentation stagnant or process                requirements reached.
   USE - Method of preparing wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures the smooth implementation                of fermentation and improves the quality of noble                rot wine. The produced noble rot wine has rich and                complex fruit aroma and sweet and full taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing wine involves picking up immediately                after evaporation of water vapor in the morning                light in the year of infection with noble rot                fungi, picking grape ears with Botrytised features                characteristics one by one, removing empty shells,                shriveled, juice-free fruit grains, and selecting                grapes infected with fungi, directly pressing the                whole ear of grapes into airbag pressing machine to                extract juice, adding 25 mg/l pectinase into grape                juice, then freezing with a freezer to lower the                temperature by 0 degrees C, and then clarifying for                alcohol fermentation, after the fermentation                started, opening cycle 1 time a day, sprinkling the                liquor from valve into an open container basin, and                continuously stirring the liquor in the basin to                overflow carbon dioxide, then using the pump to                spray liquor in the basin from the inlet of other                tank until all the liquor completely transferred,                when the fermentation stagnant or process                requirements reached, teminating the sugar                preservation in time, quickly freezing the liquor                to 0 degrees C and keeping it for 24 hours, and                then separating the rotating tank and adding sulfur                dioxide at 100 mg/l to terminate the fermentation.                The raw wine of noble rot grapes obtained by                fermentation is mixed in the ratio of 3:7 and then                put into new and old oak barrels and aged for 2-3                years.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: Preparing wine                  involves clarifying at 0-5 degrees C temperature                  for 48 hours. The alcohol fermentation                  temperature is controlled at 20-22 degrees                  C.

PN CN110669727-A
TI Preparing a bone marrow mesenchymal stem cell membrane comprises e.g. aspirating bone marrow fluid and cell suspension into the test tube containing a human lymphocyte separation solution, centrifuging, inoculating and incubating.
AU BAO F
AE SHENZHEN KEKANG STEM CELL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2020086675
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a bone marrow mesenchymal stem cell membrane comprises (i) aspirating 10 ml bone marrow fluid with a 20 ml syringe, and slowly injecting the cell suspension into the test tube preset with a human lymphocyte separation solution of equal volume density of 1.077 kg/l and making a clear interface between the two, (ii) centrifuging, aspirating the middle milky white cloud-type mononuclear cell layer, rinsing with phosphate-buffer saline, centrifuging, discarding the supernatant, adding fetal bovine serum Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium (FBSDMEM) LG culture solution to make a single cell suspension, (iii) inoculating in a petri dish, and incubating and 5% carbon dioxide saturation humidity, changing the medium for the first time and discard all unattached cells and changing the fluid, (iv) converging cells into a monolayer, digesting and subculturing, (v) observing cell growth by an inverted microscope, (vi) drawing the cell growth curve and (vii) measuring the adherence rate.
   USE - The stem cell membrane is useful in treating myocardial ischemia-reperfusion injury (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical and reduces chances of contamination during operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a bone marrow mesenchymal stem cell membrane comprises (i) aspirating 10 ml bone marrow fluid with a 20 ml syringe, and slowly injecting the cell suspension into the test tube preset with a human lymphocyte separation solution of equal volume density of 1.077 kg/l and making a clear interface between the two, (ii) centrifuging at 2500 revolutions/minute for 20 minutes, aspirating the middle milky white cloud-type mononuclear cell layer, rinsing with phosphate-buffer saline, centrifuging at 1500 revolutions/minute for 10 minutes, discarding the supernatant, adding 10 ml 15% fetal bovine serum Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium (FBSDMEM) LG culture solution to make a single cell suspension, (iii) inoculating in a 50 ml petri dish at a density of 3asterisk105/cm2, and incubating at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide saturation humidity, after 4 days, changing the medium for the first time and discard all unattached cells and changing the fluid every 2-3 days thereafter, (iv) converging cells into a monolayer, digesting with 0.25% trypsin containing 0.02% EDTA to obtain the primary MSCs suspension, and subculturing at a density of 1asterisk104/cm2 and 1:2 to form cell membranes, (v) observing the cell growth by an inverted microscope, (vi) drawing the cell growth curve, and (vii) measuring the adherence rate and drawing the adherence rate curve.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the syringe contains about 0.5 ml of diluted heparin sodium 2500 U/ml. In the step (ii), the centrifuge used for centrifugation is a desktop high-speed centrifuge. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the method for aspiration of the bone marrow fluid is to select the puncture site of the upper spine, selecting the bone marrow puncture needle after local anesthesia under aseptic conditions, and removing the core after successful puncture, and a 10 ml syringe containing 5 ml of normal saline and heparin (100 U / ml) is punctured to extract 10 ml of bone marrow. In the step (v), an inverted phase-contrast microscope is used to observe the cell growth and morphological change characteristics day by day, and take pictures. In the step (vi), the third to fifth generation cells were taken and inoculated into a 24-well culture plate with 5asterisk104 cells per well, under the above conditions, 3 wells are taken daily for 7 days and number of cells in the well. In the step (vii), the third generation cells are taken and inoculated in a 50 ml culture bottle with 5asterisk104 cells per bottle, and 2 bottles are taken every 2 hours, and the culture solution including non-adherent cells are discarded, and the adherent cells were digested with 0.25% trypsin and counted to calculate the adherence rate.
