PN CN110482738-A; CN210825718-U
TI Integrated wastewater removal hardening device comprises device body, and includes pH adjustment zone, precipitation zone, aeration zone, and precipitation zone, and control module, where device body is provided with water and lye inlet.
AU WU D
AE MCWONG ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (MCWO-Non-standard)
GA 2019A0423U
AB    NOVELTY - Integrated wastewater removal hardening device comprises device body (100), and includes a pH adjustment zone, a precipitation zone, an aeration zone, and a precipitation zone, and a control module. The device body is provided with a water inlet (1) and an lye inlet (2), the water inlet is used to flow into the waste water to be treated, a water meter flow pump is arranged near the water inlet, and the lye inlet is used to add an lye inlet. The lye meter flow pump is arranged near it. The stirring device is arranged in the pH adjustment area which is electrically connected to the control module and operates based on its instructions. The aeration zone is equipped with a second pH meter and its electrical connection control module which is used to transmit the pH value information sampled in the aeration zone to the control module.
   USE - Integrated wastewater removal hardening device.
   ADVANTAGE - The integrated wastewater removal hardening device has a high-efficiency, cost-effective, and it is suitable for industries such as coal gasification wastewater with high hardness and high suspended solids.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for performing integrated wastewater treatment method, which involves:
   (A) pumping wastewater to be treated and the waste lye into the pH adjustment zone respectively;
   (B) stirring the influent wastewater with the lye and maintaining the pH value of the pH adjustment zone within a preset range based on a stirrer in the pH adjustment zone, and the residence time T1 of the adjusted water in the adjustment zone;
   (C) flowing water to the sedimentation area and the water retention time i under strong alkaline conditions, the suspended matter and partial hardness in the wastewater form a large number of flocs;
   (D) discharging sediment produced by the process through the sewage pipe and sent to the sludge treatment device;
   (E) treating water in the first sedimentation zone which is sent to the aeration zone for carbon dioxide aeration and the pH of the aeration zone is maintained between 7.0-8.0, after the aerated water is sent to the second sedimentation zone and the residence time T3, the wastewater that meets the requirements is discharged to the sewage biochemical treatment system; and
   (F) discharging sediment formed by hardness in the water to the sludge treatment device through the sewage pipe.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of integrated wastewater removal hardening device.
   Water inlet (1)
   Lye inlet (2)
   Carbon dioxide inlet (3)
   Hard outlet (4)
   Device body (100)
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN CN110230193-A; CN110230193-B
TI Processing anti-ultraviolet high-flexibility wig            comprises e.g. roughening wig by ultrasonic cleaning            with mixture of acetone and absolute ethanol, rinsing            cleaned wig, adding plant extracts, and starting            supercritical high pressure reactor.
AU ZHANG J
AE ZHANG J (ZHAN-Individual)
   SHAOYANG MEISHA HAIR PROD CO LTD (SHAO-Non-standard)
GA 2019819555
AB    NOVELTY - Processing anti-ultraviolet high-flexibility                wig comprises e.g. roughening wig by ultrasonic                cleaning with mixture of acetone and absolute                ethanol having amount of 70-80 vol.% for 10-20                minutes, where the ultrasonic power is 120-240 W,                rinsing cleaned wig with deionized water, immersing                washed wig in ethanol solution, removing out,                immersing in mixed solution of                polydimethylsiloxane/polyvinyl chloride in                tetrahydrofuran, drying at 40-60 degrees C to                obtain roughened wig, placing treated wig in                supercritical fluid device, adding plant extracts,                starting supercritical high pressure reactor,                introducing carbon dioxide gas, setting pressure at                10-13 MPa, and temperature at 70-80 degrees C,                reacting for 3-4 hours, removing wig, concentrating                by vacuum evaporation to obtain an anti-ultraviolet                wig, adding small amount of polyethylene glycol to                the hydrophobic softener, stirring, dissolving,                adding UV-protected wig, and placing in                multi-function oscillator at room                temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for processing                anti-ultraviolet high-flexibility wig.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves softness of wig. The wig:                is easy to supple and easy to comb, washable, is                stable to light and heat; has good UV resistance;                and does not decolorize.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing anti-ultraviolet high-flexibility                wig comprises (i) roughening wig by ultrasonic                cleaning with mixture of acetone and absolute                ethanol having amount of 70-80 vol.% for 10-20                minutes, where the ultrasonic power is 120-240 W,                rinsing cleaned wig with deionized water, immersing                washed wig in ethanol solution, removing out,                immersing in mixed solution of                polydimethylsiloxane/polyvinyl chloride in                tetrahydrofuran, and drying at 40-60 degrees C to                obtain roughened wig, (ii) placing treated wig in                supercritical fluid device, adding plant extracts,                starting supercritical high pressure reactor,                introducing carbon dioxide gas, setting pressure at                10-13 MPa, and temperature at 70-80 degrees C,                reacting for 3-4 hours, removing wig and                concentrating by vacuum evaporation to obtain an                anti-ultraviolet wig, and (iii) adding small amount                of polyethylene glycol to the hydrophobic softener,                stirring, dissolving, adding UV-protected wig,                placing in multi-function oscillator at room                temperature, reciprocally oscillating for 20-30                minutes at an oscillation frequency of 200-300                beats/minute, reciprocally oscillating for 10-15                minutes at an oscillation frequency of 100-150                beats/minute, removing wig, removing solvent by                drying at 60-70 degrees C to obtain finished                product wig.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  volume ratio of acetone to absolute ethanol in                  the mixture is 3-5:1, the soaking time of wig in                  the ethanol solution is 2-3 hours, the                  temperature is normal temperature, the                  concentration of the ethanol solution is 60-80%,                  the immersion time of wig in mixed solution of                  polydimethylsiloxane/polyvinyl chloride in                  tetrahydrofuran is 3-5 hours, the temperature is                  60-70 degrees C, the concentration of                  polydimethylsiloxane in the mixture is 10-15                  wt.%, and polyvinyl chloride is 1-3 wt.%, the                  polydimethylsiloxane is obtained by uniformly                  mixing polydimethylsiloxane and silicone                  elastomer curing agent in the mass ratio of                  5-8:1, the mass ratio of the wig to the mixed                  liquid is 1:15-18, the mass ratio of the wig to                  the ethanol solution is 1:10-13, the mass ratio                  of the wig to the mixed solution of                  polydimethylsiloxane/polyvinyl chloride in                  tetrahydrofuran is 1:8-10. The plant extract is                  prepared by drying fennel, radix scutellariae and                  Salvia miltiorrhiza, mixing in the ratio of                  2-5:1-3:1, pulverizing, passing through 100                  meshes sieve, adding 60-70 vol.% aqueous solution                  in material to liquid ratio of 1:20-30, placing                  at 60-70 degrees C for 2-3 hours, filtering off                  solid impurities to obtain plant liquid extract.                  In the step (2), the mass ratio of the wig to the                  plant extract is 1:10-20, the vacuum evaporation                  concentration temperature is 60-70 degrees C, the                  degree of vacuum is 0.5-0.8 MPa, and the time is                  2-3 hours. The hydrophobic softener is prepared                  by (i) adding 10-20 pts. wt. diisocyanate to the                  dry reactor, adding 15-25 pts. wt.                  polytetrahydrofuran ether glycol, 0.07-0.08 pts.                  wt. dibutyltin disilicate, and 150-200 pts. wt.                  dimethylformamide, stirring at 45-55 degrees C                  for 40-50 minutes under the protection of                  nitrogen at rotation speed of 50-80                  revolutions/minute, adding 1-2 pts. wt.                  1,4-butanediol and 3-5 pts. wt.                  3-hydroxypropionic acid, reacting for 5-15                  minutes to obtain carboxy group-containing                  polyurethane, (ii) adding carboxy                  group-containing polyurethane in reactor, adding                  20-50 pts. wt. dimethylformamide, 3,5 pts. wt.                  N,N-diisopropylcarbodiimide, and 2-4 pts. wt.                  polylactic acid homopolymer, stirring-reacting                  under nitrogen protection at 25-35 degrees C for                  3-5 days at rotation speed of 30-50                  revolutions/minute, filtering reacted product and                  drying to obtain polylactic acid-polyurethane                  copolymer, and (iii) adding polylactic                  acid-polyurethane copolymer to the reactor,                  adding 3-4 pts. wt. polyvinylidene fluoride, and                  100-150 pts. wt. dimethylformamide, mixing at                  40-50 degrees C under nitrogen protection,                  stirring for 40-50 minutes at at 60-100                  revolutions/minute to obtain hydrophobic                  softener. In the step (3), the adding amount of                  polyethylene glycol is 1-3 wt.% of the                  hydrophobic softener, the mass ratio of the wig                  to the hydrophobic softener is 1:10-13.
DC A87 (Textile auxiliaries.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); ; V06 (Electromechanical Transducers and Small Machines)

PN CN110128853-A
TI Preparing modified nano calcium carbonate comprises e.g. adding 4A molecular sieve into high-speed mixer, adding organosilane surface treatment agent, and high-speed mixing to obtain surface pretreated 4A molecular sieve.
AU JIANG X
   ZHONG T
AE XUANCHENG XINWEIHUA CHEM TECH CO LTD (XUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201972736S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified nano calcium carbonate comprises adding 4A molecular sieve into high-speed mixer, adding organosilane surface treatment agent, and high-speed mixing to obtain surface pretreated 4A molecular sieve; preparing the calcium hydroxide slurry into suspension, adding crystal form controlling agent, carrying carbonization with carbon dioxide until the pH is 6.5-7, to obtain cubic nano-calcium carbonate slurry; preparing the surface treatment agent into aqueous solution, heating to obtain surface treatment agent aqueous solution; heating the nano-calcium carbonate slurry, adding surface treatment agent aqueous solution, and treating; adding the surface pretreated 4A molecular sieve to the surface treated nano-calcium carbonate aqueous phase system, stirring, filtering to a water content of less than 60%, drying until the water content is less than 0.4%, pulverizing and sieving to obtain the modified nano calcium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing modified nano calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The modified nano calcium carbonate: can improves adsorption effect of 4A molecular sieve on hydrochloric acid gas and slow down degradation catalysis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing modified nano calcium carbonate comprises adding 4A molecular sieve into high-speed mixer, adding organosilane surface treatment agent (whose weight is 0.5-0.2 wt.% of 4A molecular sieve), and high-speed mixing for at least 30 minutes to obtain surface pretreated 4A molecular sieve; preparing the calcium hydroxide slurry into suspension having mass concentration of 90-120%, adding crystal form controlling agent (0.1-3 wt.% of calcium hydroxide slurry), carrying carbonization at 15-35 degrees C with carbon dioxide until the pH is 6.5-7, to obtain cubic nano-calcium carbonate slurry; preparing the surface treatment agent into aqueous solution having a mass concentration of 5-15%, heating to 60-90 degrees C to obtain surface treatment agent aqueous solution; heating the nano-calcium carbonate slurry to 60-90 degrees C, adding surface treatment agent aqueous solution (2-6 wt.% of the nano-calcium carbonate slurry), and treating for 0.5-3.5 hours; adding the surface pretreated 4A molecular sieve to the surface treated nano-calcium carbonate aqueous phase system in the mass ratio of 5-15:100, stirring for 30 minutes, filtering to a water content of less than 60%, drying at 100-150 degrees C until the water content is less than 0.4%, pulverizing and sieving to obtain the modified nano calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crystal form controlling agent is inorganic salt, ammonia salt, citric acid, disaccharide and/or polysaccharide. The surface treatment agent is compound of fatty acid, fatty acid salt and silane surface treatment agent.

TI Preparing tea useful for nourishing blood, calming nerves and nourishing heart, comprises grinding components, supercritical extraction, concentrating, drying, packaging, and sterilizing.
AU LV X
   YANG Y
   GUO D
   DONG W
   XU B
   CHENG Z
   CUI L
   CHEN Y
   FAN J

   ZHANG F
   ZHANG X
   YANG X
AE HENAN ACAD SCI ISOTOPE RES INST CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 2019728722
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing tea comprises e.g. grinding spina date seed, Gardenia, lobster sauce, longan pulp, lily, fried hawthorn, cinnamon, ginger, and core-removed lotus seed, supercritical extraction, concentrating, drying, packaging, and sterilizing.
   USE - The tea is useful for nourishing blood, calming nerves and nourishing heart (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, environmentally friendly, non-toxic and harmless; has high utilization rate, no chemical residue in the product; adopts supercritical extraction or ultra-high pressure extraction technology, which realizes short extraction time, low extraction temperature, high extraction rate, low extract impurity content, high active ingredient physiological activity, low energy consumption, and high safety equipment; adopts low-temperature vacuum drying technology, which does not destroy the functional active ingredients of natural plants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing tea comprises (a) providing 100 pts. wt. spina date seed, 20-40 pts. wt. Gardenia, 40-60 pts. wt. lobster sauce, 70-80 pts. wt. longan pulp, 40-60 pts. wt. lily, 70-80 pts. wt. fried hawthorn, 20-30 pts. wt. cinnamon, 20-40 pts. wt. ginger, and 40-60 pts. wt. core-removed lotus seed, mixing, crushing the mixture, pulverizing and sieving by 60 mesh sieve, (b) adding 10-90 vol.% ethanol solution into sieved mixture, where the solid-to-liquid ratio is 1:10-1:100, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, where the extraction temperature is 25-55 degrees C, the extracting pressure is 15-45 MPa, the extracting time is 200-600 minutes, the carbon dioxide flow rate is 10-20 L/hour, or for super-high pressure extracting, crushing the mixture in the step (a) after sieving into the polyethylene plastic bag, adding 10-90 vol.% ethanol solution to make the solid-liquid ratio is 1:10-1:100, extracting under super-high pressure for 1-5 times at room temperature after sealing, the time is 1-20 minutes, the extracting pressure is 100-800 MPa, combining the extraction liquid, (c) concentrating to obtain supernatant, filtering, evaporating ethanol and concentrating the supernatant by vacuum rotary evaporator, packing d) drying the extraction liquid after concentrating in low temperature vacuum drying box to obtain extract, (e) filling the extract in step (d) into breathable fiber tea bag, and the outer package is made of a polyethylene zipper bag or a paper bag to obtain a product, and (f) irradiating packaged product by 60 cobalt gamma -ray or electron accelerator to obtain saleable item. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for tea, prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: In the step (b), the volume fraction of the ethanol solution is 30-70%, and the solid-to-liquid ratio is 1:25-1:75, super-high pressure extracting time is 2-3 times, each time extracting time is 1-5 minutes, and the extracting pressure is 100-500 MPa. In the step (d), the low temperature vacuum drying temperature is 40-50 degrees C, and drying time is 2-3 days. In the step (f), the irradiation dose is 4-7 kGy.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PT P
PN CN110101054-A
TI Preparing a salted cashew nut involves soaking the fresh cashew nuts in brine, then removing and peeling with high-pressure steam, and then the surface of the peeled cashew nuts is pretreating with cashew nut.
AU ZHANG Q
AE BENXI YUXIANGMING AGRIC DEV CO LTD (BENX-Non-standard)
GA 201970776W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a salted cashew nut involves soaking the fresh cashew nuts in brine at 40 degrees C, then removing and peeling with high-pressure steam, and then the surface of the peeled cashew nuts is pretreating with cashew nut. The pretreated cashew nuts are quickly placed in cold water of 10 degrees C for 10-20 minutes, then frozen for 1-2 hours, and the cashew nuts are frozen for use. The frozen cashew nuts are taken out on a microwave tray and vacuum-dried for 20-30 minutes to obtain dried cashew nuts for use. The dry cashew nuts are added to 8-12 times the amount of ethanol solution for sonication, so as to obtain ultrasonic treatment of cashew nuts. The ultrasonic treatment of cashew nuts is placed in a supercritical device, and carbon dioxide (CO2) gas is introduced into the sealed system for dynamic extraction to obtain supercritical treatment of cashew nuts. The supercritical treatment of cashew nuts is carried out by high-speed centrifugation analysis to obtain defatted cashew nut for use.
   USE - Method for preparing a salted cashew nut.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare a salted cashew nut, that does not use any chemical additives, is healthier to eat, and can be mass-produced industrially.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a salted cashew nut involves soaking the fresh cashew nuts in brine at 40 degrees C, then removing and peeling with high-pressure steam, and then the surface of the peeled cashew nuts is pretreating with cashew nut. The pretreated cashew nuts are quickly placed in cold water of 10 degrees C for 10-20 minutes, then frozen for 1-2 hours, and the cashew nuts are frozen for use. The frozen cashew nuts are taken out on a microwave tray and vacuum-dried for 20-30 minutes to obtain dried cashew nuts for use. The dry cashew nuts are added to 8-12 times the amount of ethanol solution for sonication, so as to obtain ultrasonic treatment of cashew nuts. The ultrasonic treatment of cashew nuts is placed in a supercritical device, and carbon dioxide (CO2) gas is introduced into the sealed system for dynamic extraction to obtain supercritical treatment of cashew nuts. The supercritical treatment of cashew nuts is carried out by high-speed centrifugation analysis to obtain the defatted cashew nut for use. The defatted cashew is protected from light and nitrogen, and a kind of salted cashew nut is obtained.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The salt water concentration is 12-14%, and the soaking time is 2-4 hours. The high pressure steam temperature is 550-650 degrees C, and the flow rate is 2-4 kg/minute. The freezing temperature is (-8)-(-12) degrees C. The vacuum drying temperature is 90-11 degrees C, the vacuum degree is 4.2-5.0 megapascal, and the microwave power is 800 watt. The rotation speed is 8-10 revolutions/minute. The ultrasonic processing power is 1200-1600 watt, and the volume fraction of the ethanol is 10-15%. The dynamic extraction temperature is 27-31 degrees C, the pressure is 5-7 megapascal, and the time is 55-75 minutes. The high-speed centrifugal speed is 12,000-20,000 revolutions/minute.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D03-Q
IP A23L-025/00; A23L-005/30
PD CN110101054-A   09 Aug 2019   A23L-025/00   201968   Pages: 11   Chinese

PI CN10438510    24 May 2019

      CN107156755-A   THREE SQUIRRELS CO LTD (THRE-Non-standard)   SHU T, ZHANG L, WEI B, NIU C

PN CN109735317-A
TI Preparation of oil well neutral plugging agent involves crushing sodium polysuccinate, sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate and ammonium persulfate, adding sodium hydroxysuccinate, grinding, drying, and adding deionized water and triethanolamine.
AU ZHANG X
   LIU J
   SHI X
   XING Y
   ZHAO C
   ZHANG G
   XI H
AE XIAN OIL MAJOR OIL & GAS TECHNOLOGY CO L (XIAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV ZUNYI NORMAL (UYZU-Non-standard)
GA 201945159U
AB    NOVELTY - An oil well neutral plugging agent is prepared by crushing sodium polysuccinate, sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate and ammonium persulfate, adding sodium hydroxysuccinate, grinding, drying, adding deionized water and triethanolamine, homogenizing to obtain homogenized liquid, drying ethanediene triamine pentaacetic acid amine and sodium hypophosphite, ball milling, adding sodium nitrilotriacetate solution, dispersing, cooling, adding diisopropyl peroxydicarbonate, stirring and refluxing to obtain dispersion, heating and stirring homogenized liquid, polydienyl dimethyl dimethyl ammonium chloride and tris hydroxymethylaminomethane, adjusting pH value, adding glycerol polymethacrylate and diisopropyl peroxydicarbonate, performing high-voltage pulse electric field treatment, adding dispersion and carbon dioxide gas, mixing acrylic acid-maleic anhydride copolymer, tetrasodium glutamate diacetate, sorbitan fatty acid ester, coconut oil fatty acid and sodium amino acid, heating, and insulating.
   USE - Method for preparing oil well neutral plugging agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The product dissolves quickly, has high deblocking speed, strong dispersibility, and has the functions of emulsification, lubrication and viscosity adjustment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An oil well neutral plugging agent is prepared by taking sodium polysuccinate, sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate and ammonium persulfate, respectively subjecting to ultrasonic crushing for 1-2 times, adding sodium hydroxysuccinate, grinding into 25 mesh, drying, adding deionized water, mixing and stirring at rate of 550 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 15 minutes under - 0.03 to - 0.01 MPa, adding triethanolamine, homogenizing at rate of 700 rpm to obtain homogenized liquid, drying ethanediene triamine pentaacetic acid amine and sodium hypophosphite using gradient heating method, ball milling for 1 hour at rate of 400 rpm according to ball-to-batch ratio of 3:1, adding sodium nitrilotriacetate solution, dispersing at 55-65 degrees C, cooling, adding diisopropyl peroxydicarbonate, stirring and refluxing for 30-35 minutes to obtain dispersion, taking homogenized liquid, polydienyl dimethyl dimethyl ammonium chloride and tris hydroxymethylaminomethane, microwave heating at frequency of 320-500 MHz at 110-160 degrees C, stirring for 13-20 minutes, adjusting pH value to 7.5-8.5, adding glycerol polymethacrylate and diisopropyl peroxydicarbonate, performing high-voltage pulse electric field treatment for 10-13 minutes, adding dispersion, mixing and dispersing under high-density carbon dioxide gas condition, mixing acrylic acid-maleic anhydride copolymer, tetrasodium glutamate diacetate, sorbitan fatty acid ester, coconut oil fatty acid and sodium amino acid, heating at 70-80 degrees C under 150-180 MPa for 5-8 minutes, insulating for 15-20 minutes, cooling, adding glycerol polymethacrylate, stirring for 20-30 minutes, mixing with prepared raw material, and performing pulse spray method 2-4 times. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for oil well neutral plugging agent application device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oil well neutral plugging agent comprises 20-25 pts. wt. sodium polyepoxysuccinate, 5-7 pts. wt. sodium amino acid, 6-8 pts. wt. sodium hydroxysuccinate, 3-5 pts. wt. triethanolamine, 10-13 pts. wt. sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate, 9-12 pts. wt. sodium nitrilotriacetate, 2-4 pts. wt. sodium hypophosphite, 1-3 pts. wt. coconut oil fatty acid, 6-9 pts. wt. sorbitan fatty acid, 0.5-1 pt. wt. tetrasodium glutamate diacetate, 6-8 pts. wt. polyaspartic acid, 2-6 pts. wt. diisopropyl peroxydicarbonate, 13-18 pts. wt. acetyl diamine pentaacetic acid amine, 15-20 pts. wt. ammonium persulfate, 6-8 pts. wt. polydiallyldimethylammonium chloride, 8-11 pts. wt. 8-hydroxytetracosanoic acid. 5-6 pts. wt. glycerol polymethacrylate, 20-25 pts. wt. ethylene-methacrylic acid metal salt copolymer and 150-200 pts. wt. deionized water. The oil well neutral plugging agent further comprises 12-16 pts. wt. silver-loaded zinc dioxide, 4-6 pts. wt. polyhexamethylene sulfonium hydrochloride and 5-8 pts. wt. benzalkonium chloride. Preferred Methods: The ultrasonicating is carried out at frequency of 15-18 KHz 20-25 seconds at interval time of 10-15 seconds. The gradient heating method is carried out under 10-2 to 10-4 Pa at 60-80 degrees C for 5-8 minutes, raising temperature to 120-140 degrees C at rate of 2-4 degrees C/minute for 10-13 minutes under mixed gas atmosphere of nitrogen and oxygen at flow rate is 40 mL/minutes, and cooling to 8-12 degrees C for 20-25 minutes. The high-density carbon dioxide gas reaction is carried out under 3 MPa at 30 degrees C at flow rate of 75 L/hours for 20 minutes, and adjusting pressure to 7 MPa at flow rate of 100 L/hours for 15 minutes. The high-voltage pulse electric field reaction is carried out at rate of 150 revolutions/minute (rpm) under 0.01-0.04 MPa at high-voltage pulse electric field pulse width of 4 mu /seconds at frequency of 3-15 Hz and strength of 1-5 kV/cm. The pulse spray method is carried out at magnetic field strength of 0.2-0.6 T and magnetic field gradient of 10-20 T/m for 15-20 minutes.

PN CN109692661-A
TI Preparation of multi-scale graphene-halloysite composite aerogel material involves preparing halloysite dispersion, mixing sulfuric acid and graphite powder, ice water bath stirring, polymerizing, adding ascorbic, reacting, and drying.
AU LI H
   FU Y
   LIU H
   WEI D
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 201941792V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of multi-scale graphene-halloysite composite aerogel material comprises taking halloysite nanotubes, adding deionized water and ethanol, ultrasonic dispersing, adding sodium styrene sulfonate, polydivinylsiloxane, initiator, and tetrabromobisphenol A to deionized water and ethanol, stirring, adding halloysite dispersion, dispersing, vacuumizing, washing, dispersing in water, water bath heating for polymerization, washing, dispersing in water; mixing concentrated sulfuric acid and graphite powder, adding potassium permanganate, ice water bath stirring, adding water, stirring, adding water and sulfuric acid, stirring, washing until near neutral, adding water, and ultrasonic dispersing; and#mixing products in first and second steps, adding initiator and tris(2,3-dibromo)propyl borate, mixing, water bath polymerizing at 70-80 degrees C for 60 hours, adding ascorbic, reacting for 10-40 hours, supercritical carbon dioxide high pressure extracting and drying.
   USE - Method for preparing multi-scale graphene-halloysite composite aerogel material.
   ADVANTAGE - The aerogel has synergistic flame retardant effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of multi-scale graphene-halloysite composite aerogel material comprises:
   (A) 0.6-12 pts. wt. halloysite nanotubes, adding 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, ultrasonic dispersing, adding 0.6-12 pts. wt. sodium styrene sulfonate, 0.06-1.8 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.4 pt. wt. initiator, and 0.08-9 pts. wt. tetrabromobisphenol A to 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, stirring uniformly, adding halloysite dispersion, dispersing, vacuuming for 3 times, washing, dispersing in 100 pts. wt. water, water bath heating to 70-80 degrees C for 50 hours for polymerization, washing, dispersing to 100 pts. wt. water;
   (B) mixing 0.8-8 pts. wt. concentrated sulfuric acid and 0.3-12 pts. wt. graphite powder, adding 0.6-12 pts. wt. potassium permanganate, ice water bath stirring for 12 hours, adding 75-225 pts. wt. water, stirring, adding 360-520 pts. wt. water and 9-52 pts. wt. sulfuric acid, stirring, washing until near neutral, adding 100 pts. wt. water, and ultrasonic dispersing; and
   (C) mixing products in first and second steps, adding 0.01-1.5 pts. wt. initiator and 0.03-6 pt. wt. tris(2,3-dibromo)propyl borate, mixing, water bath polymerizing at 70-80 degrees C for 60 hours, adding 0.7-12 pts. wt. ascorbic, reacting for 10-40 hours, supercritical carbon dioxide high pressure extracting at 31-56 degrees C and 6-12 MPa for 1 hour and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The initiator is dibenzoyl peroxide or azobisisobutyronitrile.

PN CN109097220-A
TI Fermenting fruity blueberries comprises e.g.            crushing blueberries, adding pectinase, potassium            metabisulfite, compressed oak sawdust and tannin,            heating, adding yeast and inorganic nitrogen nutrient,            fermenting, storing, fining and freezing.
AU LI C
AE LINGHANG WINE IND CO LTD (LING-Non-standard)
GA 2019024346
AB    NOVELTY - Fermenting fruity blueberries comprises (i)                crushing blueberries, adding 80-100 mg/l pectinase                and 80-100 mg/l potassium metabisulfite at the                crusher outlet, adding 2 g/l compressed oak sawdust                to tank mouth, crushing all the blueberries,                circulating full tank, and adding 0.1-0.3 g/l                tannin, (ii) heating, adjusting the pH of the                blueberry juice with sodium bicarbonate to 3.4-3.8,                and accessing juice with 0.19-0.21 g/l yeast and                0.19-0.21 g/l yeast nutritional agent, (iii)                falling specific gravity in 10 points, adjusting                the sugar content to 212 g/l, falling specific                gravity in 10 points, adding 0.19-0.21 g/l                inorganic nitrogen nutrients, adding 0.1-0.3 g/l                tannin, maintaining the temperature in the late                fermentation tank at 22 degrees C and recycling the                wine, capping and charging carbon dioxide, and (v)                separating the wine by scum, adding 60 mg/l partial                weight potassium sulfite cycle uniformly, storing                full tank, and fining, clarifying, freezing,                filtering and filling into bottle.
   USE - The method is useful for fermenting fruity                blueberries.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fermenting fruity blueberries comprises (i)                crushing blueberries, adding 80-100 mg/l pectinase                and 80-100 mg/l potassium metabisulfite at the                crusher outlet, adding 2 g/l compressed oak sawdust                to tank mouth, crushing all the blueberries,                circulating full tank, and adding 0.1-0.3 g/l                tannin, (ii) heating, adjusting the pH of the                blueberry juice with sodium bicarbonate to 3.4-3.8,                and accessing juice with 0.19-0.21 g/l yeast and                0.19-0.21 g/l yeast nutritional agent, (iii)                falling specific gravity in 10 points, adjusting                the sugar content to 212 g/l, where the fermented                wine is having an alcohol content of 12% vol., when                the fermentation is most vigorous, falling specific                gravity in 10 points, adding 0.19-0.21 g/l                inorganic nitrogen nutrients, adding 0.1-0.3 g/l                tannin, when the specific gravity drops by 20                points, where the main fermentation temperature                does not exceed 20 degrees C, and the fermentation                is most vigorous, maintaining the temperature in                the late fermentation tank at 22 degrees C and                recycling the wine, capping and charging carbon                dioxide, and (v) separating the wine by scum,                adding 60 mg/l partial weight potassium sulfite                cycle uniformly, storing full tank, and fining,                clarifying, freezing, filtering and filling into                bottle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: In the step (i), 2 g/l                  fresh oak is added to the compressed oak sawdust.                  In the step (iv), the potato protein is used for                  sizing. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the                  specific gravity of the product in the                  fermentation process is measured using a                  gravimeter. The sugar content of the product is                  judged by specific gravity.

PN CN109012652-A
TI Preparation of lanthanum-carbon doped modified            titania used for degrading vehicle exhaust, involves            preparing catalyst lanthanum nitrate solution, glucose,            tetrabutyl titanate and lauric acid, drying and            calcining obtained precipitate.
AU XU T
   TIAN Q
   GUO T
   WANG H
AE UNIV NANJING FORESTRY (UNJF-C)
GA 2018A53877
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of lanthanum-carbon doped modified                titania involves washing glass container and                reaction kettle liner with deionized water, drying                for 2 hours at 100 degrees C, adding methanol,                tetrabutyl titanate, lanthanum nitrate solution,                glucose solution and lauric acid in order to the                container, continuously stirring, uniformly mixing                the solution, placing the mixture in the kettle in                an oven, reacting for 24 hours at 200 degrees C,                cooling to room temperature to obtain a solid                precipitate, washing, drying the washed                precipitate, grinding the mixture, calcining at 500                degrees C for 2 hours, developing a vehicle tail                gas catalytic degradation detection system,                evaluating the catalytic degradation of                hydrocarbon, nitrogen oxides, carbon monoxide and                carbon dioxide in the tail gas by simulated                lanthanum-carbon co-doped titania under simulated                sunlight, adjusting the amount of lanthanum and                carbon in titania, and determining the optimal                doping amount of lanthanum and carbon in titania                after repeated tests.
   USE - Preparation of lanthanum-carbon doped modified                titania used for degrading vehicle exhaust.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces catalyst having excellent                sunlight absorbability, photocatalytic degradation                efficiency, small forbidden band width, and capable                of reducing tail gas pollution and improving air                quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of lanthanum-carbon doped modified                titania involves washing glass container and                reaction kettle liner with deionized water, drying                for 2 hours at 100 degrees C, preparing 0.2-15                mol/L lanthanum nitrate solution, 0.04-15 mol/L                glucose solution as carbon source, adding 15 mL                methanol, 15 mL tetrabutyl titanate, 15 mL                lanthanum nitrate solution, 15 mL glucose solution                and 2 mL lauric acid in order to the container,                continuously stirring, uniformly mixing the                solution, placing the mixture in the kettle in an                oven, reacting for 24 hours at 200 degrees C,                cooling to room temperature to obtain a solid                precipitate, washing the impurity using absolute                ethanol, drying the washed precipitate in an oven                at 100 degrees C for 12 hours, grinding the mixture                into a powder, calcining at 500 degrees C in a box                type furnace for 2 hours, developing a vehicle tail                gas catalytic degradation detection system,                evaluating the catalytic degradation of                hydrocarbon, nitrogen oxides, carbon monoxide and                carbon dioxide in the tail gas by simulated                lanthanum-carbon co-doped titania under simulated                sunlight, adjusting the amount of lanthanum and                carbon in titania, and determining the optimal                doping amount of lanthanum and carbon in titania                after repeated tests.

PN CN108863770-A
TI Preparation of levulinic acid by mixing metal ion-modified HZSM-5 catalyst and glucose, adding gamma-valerolactone and water, mixing, reacting, cooling, filtering, and decompressing and distilling liquid.
AU LI X
   SI C
   YAO L
   LIANG M
   QIU Y
   JIANG L
   TIAN Y
   HAN S
AE UNIV TIANJIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYTC-C)
GA 201895495L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of levulinic acid comprises mixing 5-10 g HZSM-5 molecular sieve and 100 mL of 1-10 mmol/L metal chloride aqueous solution, heating in an oil bath at 60-90 degrees C, stirring for 6-12 hours, cooling to room temperature, washing the obtained solid in 200-500 mL deionized water for 2-6 times, drying at 100 degrees C for 8 hours, calcining at 550 degrees C for 4 hours, and grinding to obtain a metal ion-modified HZSM-5 catalyst; mixing the metal ion-modified HZSM-5 catalyst and glucose, adding 60-100 times gamma -valerolactone and water, uniformly mixing, adding in a reaction kettle with magnetic stirring, purging 2-6 times nitrogen, adjusting the pressure in the kettle to 5 MPa, reacting at 150-190 degrees C for 12-24 hours, placing the reaction kettle in an ice water bath to cool to room temperature, filtering, and decompressing and distilling liquid; and recovering gamma -valerolactone by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and calcining at 600 degrees C for 5 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing levulinic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The metal ion-modified HZSM-5 has high catalytic activity, and can be recycled to use. The gamma -valerolactone reduces reaction temperature, and is environment-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of levulinic acid comprises:
   (A) mixing 5-10 g HZSM-5 molecular sieve and 100 mL of 1-10 mmol/L metal chloride aqueous solution, heating in an oil bath at 60-90 degrees C, stirring for 6-12 hours to fully ion exchange the HZSM-5 molecular sieve and metal ions, cooling to room temperature, washing the obtained solid in 200-500 mL deionized water for 2-6 times, drying at 100 degrees C for 8 hours, calcining at 550 degrees C for 4 hours, and grinding to obtain a metal ion-modified HZSM-5 catalyst having a particle size of lesser than 80 meshes;
   (B) mixing the metal ion-modified HZSM-5 catalyst and glucose at a mass ratio of 1:10-10:1, adding 60-100 times gamma -valerolactone and water, uniformly mixing, adding in a reaction kettle with magnetic stirring, purging 2-6 times nitrogen, adjusting the pressure in the kettle to 5 MPa, reacting at 150-190 degrees C for 12-24 hours, placing the reaction kettle in an ice water bath to cool to room temperature, filtering for solid-liquid separation, and decompressing and distilling liquid; and
   (C) recovering gamma -valerolactone by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and calcining at 600 degrees C for 5 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The metal chloride comprises aluminum chloride, ferric chloride, nickel chloride, and/or copper chloride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The volume ratio of the gamma -valerolactone and water is 19:1.

TI Care cream with an anti-cancer function, raw materials comprises olive, turmeric, dandelion, cotton rose Hibiscus leaves, Astragalus root, notoginseng, selfheal, radix angelicae, rhubarb, saffron, radices trichosanthis and spina gleditsiae.
AU ZHANG J
AE SICHUAN SHENGMINGYUAN HEALTH MANAGEMENT (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 201884763R
AB    NOVELTY - Care cream with an anti-cancer function, raw materials comprises 35-45 pts. wt. olive, 30-40 pts. wt. turmeric, 30-40 pts. wt. dandelion, 28-38 pts. wt. cotton rose Hibiscus leaves, 25-35 pts. wt. Astragalus root, 17-27 pts. wt. notoginseng, 14-24 pts. wt. selfheal, 10-20 pts. wt. radix angelicae, 6-16 pts. wt. rhubarb, 5-15 pts. wt. saffron, 5-15 pts. wt. radices trichosanthis, 4-14 pts. wt. spina gleditsiae, 3-13 pts. wt. Cimicifuga foetida, 2-12 pts. wt. ginseng and 1-6 pts. wt. frankincense.
   USE - Care cream with an anti-cancer function.
   ADVANTAGE - The care cream is a rational prescription and has good treatment effect, it can dredge the mammary gland, discharging bruise, softening knot, effectively improve and cure hyperplasia of mammary glands and breast nodule, killed breast cancer at the source, the breast cancer has certain preventing function.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing care cream, which involves:
   (A) using high-speed universal crusher to crush the Curcuma, dandelion, Astragalus root, Cimicifuga foetida and ginseng into particles with particle diameter of 40-80 meshes powder;
   (B) mixing the weighed amount of turmeric powder, dandelion powder, radix astragali powder, Cimicifuga foetida powder and ginseng powder;
   (C) putting in the ultra-critical carbon dioxide extraction kettle;
   (D) using the up-flow speed of 15-25 per hour and infusing carbon dioxide to extract at 35-50 degrees C under the pressure of 15-30 mega-Pasacl for 1-2 hours, to obtain the traditional Chinese medicine extract solution;
   (E) using the centrifuge to centrifuge the traditional Chinese medicine extract solution with the rotation speed of 10000-15000 revolutions per minute;
   (F) centrifuging and extracting the supernatant to obtain traditional Chinese medicine liquid;
   (G) taking the lotus leaves, pseudo-ginseng, selfheal, Angelica dahurica, rhubarb, saffron, radix trichosanthis, spina gleditsiae and frankincense;
   (H) cleaning;
   (I) drying;
   (J) removing the impurity;
   (K) using high-speed universal crusher for crushing until the grain diameter is 40-80 meshes of powder to obtain the traditional Chinese medicine powder;
   (L) mixing the traditional Chinese medicine liquid and the traditional Chinese medicine powder with olive oil;
   (M) heating at 40-60 degrees C with stirring for 10-20 minutes; and
   (N) cooling to the room temperature to obtain the care cream with an anti-cancer efficacy.
TF ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.

PT P
PN CN108531494-A; CN108531494-B
TI Producing bio-based nylon-54 precursor, by subjecting CDF-4A, Trc-4A and CadA-4A fragments to homologous recombination splicing, transforming into Escherichia coli AFP111 competent cells, fermenting and subjecting to alcohol precipitation.
AU CHEN K
   WANG J
   XU S
   CHENG Q
   TIAN W
   WEI G
   OUYANG P
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 201874813D
AB    NOVELTY - Producing bio-based nylon-54 precursor, by (a) subjecting lysine decarboxylase gene cadA to PCR amplification, cloning fragment into NdeI and KpnI sites of vector pETDuet-1, obtaining plasmid pETDuet-CadA, amplifying, obtaining fragment CadA-4A, amplifying plasmid pCDFDuet-1, obtaining fragment CDF-4A, amplifying plasmid pTrc99a, obtaining fragment Trc-4A, subjecting the fragments to homologous recombination splicing, and obtaining recombinant plasmid pCDF-Trc-CadA, (b) transforming into E.coli AFP111 competent cells and obtaining an engineered strain E.coli strain SC01 overexpressing lysine decarboxylase, and (c) inoculating the engineered strain E.coli strain SC01 into aerobic fermentation medium, performing aerobic fermentation, introducing carbon dioxide into reaction system, starting anaerobic fermentation, continuously introducing an aqueous solution of lysine into reaction system, and subjecting to alcohol precipitation.
   USE - The method is useful for producing bio-based nylon-54 precursor by co-fermentation with genetic engineering bacteria.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, cost-effective and environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing bio-based nylon-54 precursor by co-fermentation with genetic engineering bacteria, involves (a) using Escherichia coli genome as a template, subjecting lysine decarboxylase gene cadA to PCR amplification using primers, cloning fragment into NdeI and KpnI sites of vector pETDuet-1, obtaining plasmid pETDuet-CadA, subjecting the plasmid pETDuet-CadA as a template to PCR amplification using upstream primer CadA-4A-F and downstream primer CadA-4A-R, obtaining fragment CadA-4A, subjecting the plasmid pCDFDuet-1 as a template to PCR amplification using upstream primer Fuse-pCDF-F and downstream primer Fuse-pCDF-R, obtaining fragment CDF-4A, subjecting the plasmid pTrc99a as a template to PCR amplification using upstream primer Trc99a-4A-F and downstream primer Trc99a-4A-R, obtaining fragment Trc-4A, subjecting the fragments CDF-4A, Trc-4A and CadA-4A to homologous recombination splicing, and obtaining recombinant plasmid pCDF-Trc-CadA, (b) transforming the recombinant plasmid pCDF-Trc-CadA obtained in step (a) to E.coli AFP111 competent cells, and obtaining an engineered strain E.coli strain SC01 overexpressing lysine decarboxylase, and (c) inoculating the engineered strain E.coli strain SC01 into aerobic fermentation medium, performing aerobic fermentation, after aerobic fermentation, introducing carbon dioxide into reaction system, starting anaerobic fermentation, during anaerobic fermentation, continuously introducing an aqueous solution of lysine into reaction system, after end of anaerobic fermentation, centrifuging, removing cells, recovering supernatant and subjecting to alcohol precipitation treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The lysine decarboxylase gene cadA is replaced with lysine decarboxylase gene ldcC. The lysine decarboxylase gene cadA is amplified using upstream primer having 31 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: Not defined) fully defined in the specification and downstream primer having base pair sequence of ggggtaccttattttttgctttcttctttc (SEQ ID NO: Not defined). Inoculation amount of E.coli strain SC01 into aerobic fermentation medium is 1-5%. The aerobic fermentation medium comprises 10 g/l peptone, 5 g/l yeast powder, 5 g/l sodium chloride, 50 mg/l kanamycin, 34 mg/l chloromycetin, 80 mg/l streptomycin and 15 g/l glucose and has pH of 6.8 or 3 g/l citric acid, 4 g/l disodium hydrogen phosphate dodecahydrate, 8 g/l potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 8 g/l diammonium hydrogen phosphate, 0.2 g/l ammonium chloride, 0.75 g/l ammonium sulfate, 1 g/l magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, 0.01 g/l calcium chloride dihydrate, 0.5 mg/l zinc sulfate heptahydrate, 0.25 mg/l copper chloride dihydrate, 2.5 mg/l manganese sulfate monohydrate, 1.75 mg/l cobalt chloride hexahydrate, 0.12 mg/l boric acid, 1.77 mg/l aluminum sulfate, 0.5 mg/l sodium molybdate dihydrate, 16.1 mg/l ferric citrate, 20 mg/l thiamine, 2 mg/l biotin, 50 mg/l kanamycin, 34 mg/l chloromycetin, 80 mg/l streptomycin and 15 g/l glucose and has pH of 6.8, where feed medium is 300 g/l glucose. The aerobic fermentation is performed at 30-37 degrees C for 30-42 hours. The aerobic fermentation is performed until 24-36 hours, after that, 0.1-1 mmol/l isopropyl beta -D-1-thiogalactopyranoside is added as inducer to reaction system and induced to end of aerobic fermentation. The carbon dioxide is introduced into reaction system at a speed of 0.1-2 vvm for 10-20 minutes. The carbon dioxide is replaced by nitrogen gas. The lysine solution is an aqueous solution of lysine or its salt. The lysine has concentration of 0.68-2.8 mol/l. The reaction system pH is controlled to 6-7 by introducing an aqueous lysine solution.

PT P
PN CN108530016-A
TI Preparing composite heat insulation aerogel material, comprises e.g. preparing dopamine hydrochloride solution, and subjecting aluminum silicate fiber felt, basalt fiber felt and mullite polycrystalline fiber to shade soak oscillation.
AU WANG H
AE SUZHOU LINGKETE NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2018748452
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite heat insulation aerogel material, comprises e.g. (1) preparing dopamine hydrochloride solution, and adjusting pH value, (2) subjecting aluminum silicate fiber felt, basalt fiber felt and mullite polycrystalline fiber to shade soak oscillation, (3) taking out treated mixed fiber and washing with deionized water, and drying, (4) mixing titanium tetrachloride and deionized water, and dissolving to obtain a solution A, (5) mixing ethyl orthosilicate and ethanol, and dissolving to obtain a solution B, (6) mixing solution A and solution B, and stirring, (7) placing into an ice water bath, then adding propylene oxide, and stirring, (8) adding the mixed fiber, stirring uniformly, pouring into a mold, sealing and aging at 50-55 degrees C for 24-27 hours, (9) taking out, soaking in acetone for 24 hours, then replacing acetone once every 8 hours, and (10) taking out from acetone, and supercritical carbon dioxide drying to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing composite heat insulation aerogel material.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite heat insulation aerogel material: has excellent mechanical property, excellent bending strength, excellent flexibility and high compression strength; and avoids sudden breakage of material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing composite heat insulation aerogel material, comprises (1) preparing 50-80 pts. wt. dopamine hydrochloride solution, and adjusting pH value to 9-10, (2) subjecting 5-10 pts. wt. aluminum silicate fiber felt, 1-3 pts. wt. basalt fiber felt and 2-5 pts. wt. mullite polycrystalline fiber to shade soak oscillation for 15-20 hours, (3) taking out treated mixed fiber and washing with deionized water, and drying the treated mixed fiber at 30-40 degrees C, (4) mixing titanium tetrachloride and deionized water, and dissolving under stirring condition to obtain a solution A, where the volume ratio between titanium tetrachloride and deionized water is 1:3, (5) mixing ethyl orthosilicate and ethanol, and dissolving under stirring condition to obtain a solution B, where the volume ratio between ethyl orthosilicate and ethanol is 3:1, (6) mixing 30-50 pts. wt. solution A and 20-30 pts. wt. solution B, and stirring for 2-3 hours, (7) placing into an ice water bath, then adding 3-5 pts. wt. propylene oxide, and stirring for 30-40 minutes, (8) adding the mixed fiber, stirring uniformly, pouring into a mold, sealing and aging at 50-55 degrees C for 24-27 hours, (9) taking out, soaking in acetone for 24 hours, then replacing acetone once every 8 hours, and (10) taking out from acetone, and supercritical carbon dioxide drying to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The supercritical carbon dioxide drying process, comprises (a) placing the gel in an autoclave, then sealing the kettle, and purging air in the kettle by purging with carbon dioxide, (b) introducing carbon dioxide, then drying at 30-35 degrees C for 40-60 minutes under a pressure of 7-8 Mpa, and (c) slowly releasing carbon dioxide to atmospheric pressure, then purging the autoclave with carbon dioxide and cooling to room temperature. Preferred Components: The aluminum silicate fiber felt and basalt fiber felt are having length of 50 mm, width of 30 mm, and thickness of 50 mu m. The mullite polycrystalline fiber has a length of 30-50 mm. The dopamine hydrochloride solution has a concentration of 5-10 mg/ml.

TI Indoor fire fighting device with            fire-extinguishing function comprises fire chamber, air            purifying chamber, water tank, and reaction chamber,            where fire chamber is set at lower side of cavity which            is provided with water storage tank.
AU JIANG B
AE SHAANXI HUABANG TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2018738971
AB    (CN207838102-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a new-type indoor                fire fighting device with fire-extinguishing                function, comprising a fire chamber, an air                purifying chamber, chamber, a water storage tank                and a reaction chamber, The device uses the sodium                bicarbonate and sulphate reaction generating                principle of a large amount of carbon dioxide gas                to extinguish the fire, there is a reaction                chamber, which is used for separately storing the                two substances, wherein the sodium bicarbonate is                stored in second storage box, one end of the second                cartridge is provided with a servo motor, the                temperature sensor detects the indoor temperature                is too high. the PLC controller automatically                judging the fire occurs, the servo motor operation                such that the second storage box in the sodium                bicarbonate solution is poured into the first                cartridge and aluminium sulphate solution, which                generates a large amount of carbon dioxide gas into                the fire chamber through air outlet branch pipe at                the top end of the reaction chamber to extinguish                the fire in the fire chamber and when the fire                occurs, the first electromagnetic valve is closed,                the suction pump operates at the same time,                extracting gas in the fire chamber accelerate the                anoxic state.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                new-type indoor fire fighting device.
   Fire chamber (1)
   Air purifying chamber (2)
   Chamber (3)
   Water tank (6)
   Air branch pipe (13)
   Air pumping pipe (14)
   Air absorbing pump (15)
   Reaction chamber (26)

   USE - New-type indoor fire fighting device.
   ADVANTAGE - The new-type indoor fire fighting device has                simple structure, reasonable design, and                accelerates anoxic state.

PT P
PN CN108440697-A
TI Preparing sevelamer carbonate by reacting allylamine with concentrated hydrochloric acid, dissolving allylamine hydrochloride monomer in solvent, polymerizing and alkalizing allylamine hydrochloride polymer with organic base solution.
AU CHEN X
   SU X
   DAI J
   WANG J
AE CHANGZHOU FANGYUAN PHARM CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201868196A
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing sevelamer carbonate involves (a) reacting allylamine with concentrated hydrochloric acid to form a salt to obtain an allylamine hydrochloride, (b) taking benzoyl peroxide as an initiator to initiate, dissolving allylamine hydrochloride monomer in a solvent, carrying out polymerization, cooling, slowly dripping the solution to methanol, carrying out crystallization, continuously stirring and filtering to obtain an allylamine hydrochloride polymer, (c) alkalizing the allylamine hydrochloride polymer with an organic base solution to obtain an allylamine polymer, (d) reacting allylamine polymer with polyethylene glycol fatty acid ester, nano-alumina and epichlorohydrin to obtain sevelamer hydrochloride, (e) neutralizing the sevelamer hydrochloride with an alkali solution to obtain a free sevelamer and reacting with an aqueous carbon dioxide solution to obtain sevelamer carbonate, and (f) mixing sevelamer carbonate with hydroxypropyl methylcellulose.
   USE - Method is useful for preparing sevelamer carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures stable and rapid preparation of sevelamer carbonate with high quality and agricultural or environmental protection industry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for preparing sevelamer carbonate involves (a) reacting allylamine with concentrated hydrochloric acid to form a salt to obtain an allylamine hydrochloride at 48-55 degrees C for 1-2 hours, where the weight ratio of allylamine to concentrated hydrochloric acid is 2-5:10-15, (b) taking benzoyl peroxide as an initiator to initiate, dissolving allylamine hydrochloride monomer in a solvent, carrying out polymerization at 92-98 degrees C for 10-15 hours, cooling, slowly dripping the solution to methanol at a rate of 30-50 drops/minute, carrying out crystallization, continuously stirring for 1-3 hours and filtering to obtain an allylamine hydrochloride polymer, where the weight ratio of benzoyl peroxide to allylamine hydrochloride monomer is 0.01-0.1:50-150, (c) alkalizing the allylamine hydrochloride polymer with an organic base solution having a pH value of 8-12 to obtain an allylamine polymer, (d) reacting allylamine polymer with polyethylene glycol fatty acid ester, nano-alumina and epichlorohydrin to obtain sevelamer hydrochloride, where the weight ratio of allylamine polymer, polyethylene glycol fatty acid ester, epichlorohydrin and nano-alumina is 1:0.5-1:1-1.3:0.1-0.3, (e) neutralizing the sevelamer hydrochloride with an alkali solution to obtain a free sevelamer and reacting with an aqueous carbon dioxide solution to obtain sevelamer carbonate, and (f) mixing sevelamer carbonate with hydroxypropyl methylcellulose, adding sodium bicarbonate solution to a mixture of sevelamer carbonate and hydroxypropyl methylcellulose, granulating, sieving the drug granules, adding riboflavin and sodium alginate to granulated drug granules, uniformly mixing, tableting and coating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic base solution is sodium methoxide, potassium t-butoxide or lithium diisopropylamide. The solvent is acetonitrile, ethanol or isopropanol. Preferred Composition: In the step (a), the weight ratio of allylamine to concentrated hydrochloric acid is 3:12. The weight ratio of benzoyl peroxide to allylamine hydrochloride monomer is 0.03:120. The weight ratio of allylamine polymer, polyethylene glycol fatty acid ester, epichlorohydrin and nano-alumina is 1:0.6:1.2:0.2. Preferred Conditions: In the step (a), the reacting step is carried out at 50 degrees C for 1.6 hours. In the step (b), the reacting step is carried out at 95 degrees C for 12 hours, the dripping step is carried out at a rate of 42 drops/minute and the continuous stirring step is carried out for 2 hours.

PT P
PN CN108392677-A
TI Method for preparing hydroxyapatite-polylactic acid composite biological material, involves mixing polylactic acid and polyvinyl acetate, adding fumigated material, adding carboxymethylcellulose and hydroxyethylcellulose and sterilizing.
AU LI Z
AE SUZHOU LINGKETE NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201868757S
AB    NOVELTY - A hydroxyapatite-polylactic acid composite biological material preparing method involves mixing 17-25 parts polylactic acid, 4-8 parts polyvinyl acetate, 3-6 parts sodium xylene sulfonate, 7-12 parts citric acid ester and 1-4 parts dispersing agent, introducing carbon dioxide fumigation for 5 minutes to obtain a ultrasonic mixture. The ultrasonic mixture is sieved to remove large particle precipitates to obtain product. The product is added with 15-25 parts carboxymethylcellulose, 6-9 parts hydroxyethylcellulose, 4-8 parts hydroxyapatite, 5-10 parts sodium tripolyphosphate, 4-6 parts trehalose and 1-3 parts butyl hydroxyanisole to a sealed calciner, filling with argon gas to obtain a reaction liquid, adding 5L of 4.5% sodium acetate solution. The reaction liquid is injected into the cold pot, added 2-5 parts stabilizer, the mixture is uniformly stirred, injecting the cooling liquid into a twin-screw extruder and sterilized by ultraviolet irradiation to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing hydroxyapatite-polylactic acid composite biological material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing hydroxyapatite-polylactic acid composite biological material with high hardness and strong anti-pressure ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A hydroxyapatite-polylactic acid composite biological material preparing method involves mixing 17-25 parts polylactic acid, 4-8 parts polyvinyl acetate, 3-6 parts sodium xylene sulfonate, 7-12 parts citric acid ester and 1-4 parts dispersing agent, introducing carbon dioxide fumigation for 5 minutes, adding the fumigated material to the ultrasonic emulsifier disperser and adding 800 ml of 3% sodium hydroxide solution to obtain a ultrasonic mixture. The ultrasonic mixture is sieved to remove large particle precipitates and the sieve aperture is 2500 mesh to obtain product. The product is added with 15-25 parts carboxymethylcellulose, 6-9 parts hydroxyethylcellulose, 4-8 parts hydroxyapatite, 5-10 parts sodium tripolyphosphate, 4-6 parts trehalose and 1-3 parts butyl hydroxyanisole to a sealed calciner, filling with argon gas, the reaction temperature is 180-200 degrees C and the reaction is carried out for 75 minutes to obtain a reaction liquid and temperature is naturally lowered to 120 degrees C, adding 5L of 4.5% sodium acetate solution and keep warm at 120 degrees C for 2-5 hours. The reaction liquid is injected into the cold pot, added 2-5 parts stabilizer, the mixture is uniformly stirred and slowly cooled until the temperature drops to 70-75 degrees C, injecting the cooling liquid into a twin-screw extruder, extruding, pressing and shaping, the press molding material is rapidly cooled and demolded in liquid nitrogen, immersed in anhydrous ethanol and sterilized by ultraviolet irradiation to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dispersing agent is selected from the group consisting of vinyl wax, ethylene bis stearamide and polyamide wax or a glyceryl tristearate. The stabilizer is selected from the group consisting of diaminodiphenyl sulfone, vinyl silicone oil, paraffin wax and linoleic acid.

PN CN108239701-A; CN108239701-B
TI Comprehensive recovery of lead and zinc in high mud carbonate-containing lead oxide zinc ore comprises grinding ore, acid leaching, absorbing, filter pressing, oxidizing iron, washing, extracting, electrodepositing and reselecting.
AU LIU X
   WU B
   GAO W
   MO X
   WEN J
AE GEN RES INST NON-FERROUS METALS (BFER-C)
   GRINM ENG TECHNOLOGY RES INST CO LTD (BFER-C)
GA 201853939L
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensive recovery of lead and zinc in high mud carbonate-containing lead oxide zinc ore comprises grinding high mud carbonate-containing lead zinc ore; leaching with sulfuric acid and absorbing produced carbon dioxide gas with sodium hydroxide aqueous solution; filter pressing leaching residue and directly collecting concentrated zinc extract solution; removing iron in concentrated zinc leaching solution by adding zinc powder, adjusting pH and subjecting solution for solid manganese dioxide to oxidize iron in solution to obtain demineralized solution; washing treated residue with treated back water to obtain low concentration zinc leaching solution; removing iron by adding zinc powder, adjusting pH and oxidizing iron in solution through solid manganese dioxide by needle iron ore method; performing multistage counter-current extraction with dioctyl phosphate to obtain zinc solution; electrodepositing solution to obtain zinc oxide product; and reselecting washed leaching residue.
   USE - Method for comprehensively recovering lead and zinc in high mud carbonate-containing lead oxide zinc ore.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves problem of difficulty in recovering lead and zinc, exhibits dissolution difference between magnesite ore and white lead ore at same pH area, realizes selective dissolution of zinc and lead, reduces waste gas and dust pollution and has low energy consumption and short treatment process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Comprehensive recovery of lead and zinc in high mud carbonate-containing lead oxide zinc ore comprises grinding high mud carbonate-containing lead zinc ore to 60-200 meshes; leaching with 25-33 wt.% sulfuric acid at 30-80 degrees C pH 1.2-2.0 and absorbing produced carbon dioxide gas using 30 wt.% sodium hydroxide aqueous solution; filter pressing leaching residue and directly collecting concentrated zinc extract solution; removing iron in concentrated zinc leaching solution by adding zinc powder 1.5-2.0 times by weight of trivalent iron, adjusting pH to 3.0-3.5 and subjecting solution for solid manganese dioxide to oxidize iron in solution to obtain demineralized solution; washing treated residue with treated back water, where washing solution is low concentration zinc leaching solution; removing iron in low-concentration zinc leaching solution by adding zinc powder 1.5-2.0 times by weight of trivalent iron for removing heavy metal ion, adjusting pH to 3.0-3.5 and oxidizing iron in solution through solid manganese dioxide by needle iron ore method; carrying out multistage counter-current extraction using dioctyl phosphate as extracting agent to obtain zinc solution with concentration of more than 140 g/L; electrodepositing solution to obtain zinc oxide product; and reselecting washed leaching residue through shaker or hydrocyclone.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The needle iron ore method comprises reacting at 70-85 degrees C for 1-2 hours and aging for 2-4 hours. The counter-current extraction is carried out by mixing dioctyl phosphate with diluent to prepare oil phase containing 20-30% dioctyl phosphate, extracting at water-oil flow rate ratio of 1:1 under 300-500 revolutions/minute for 5-8 minutes and phase separating for 3-8 minutes, where zinc is extracted into oil phase and sending back aqueous phase extraction residual liquid as treated return water, stripping oil phase by adding 185-200g/L sulfuric acid, refluxing at water-oil flow rate ratio of 1:2 for 5-8 minutes and phase separating for 3-8 minutes, zinc enters aqueous phase in form of zinc sulfate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The diluent is sulfonated kerosene or DT100.
DC M28 (Electrolytic and electrothermic production and refining of non-ferrous metals - excluding heat treatment (C25).); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)

PN CN108144637-A; CN108144637-B
TI Catalyst useful in reverse water gas shift reaction, comprises carrier, active component and auxiliary agent, the carrier is KLTL molecular sieve, the active component is platinum, and the auxiliary agent is alkali metal-M.
AU HUANG Y
   YANG X
   SU X
   ZHANG T
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201847421E
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises carrier, active component and auxiliary agent, where the carrier is KLTL molecular sieve, the active component is platinum, and the auxiliary agent is alkali metal-M.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in reverse water gas shift reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high activity, high selectivity and high stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing catalyst, comprising (i) dissolving potassium hydroxide powder in de-ionized water to prepare 20-80% potassium hydroxide solution, dissolving aluminum powder in potassium hydroxide solution, and magnetically stirring for 0.5-2 hours to obtain aluminum precursor, adding the remaining potassium hydroxide solution into the sol (mass concentration is 30%), and magnetically stirring for 0.5-2 hours to obtain silicon source precursor, adding the silicon source precursor dropwisely into the aluminum precursor, and stirring for 8-24 hours at room temperature to obtain gel, carrying out hydro-thermal treatment to obtain suspension system, centrifugal separating, washing with de-ionized water until the lotion is neutral, collecting the solid precipitate, drying at 110-130 degrees C for 12 hours, calcining the dried solid at 500-600 degrees C under air atmosphere and baking for more than 4 hours to obtain vector KLTL type molecular sieve, (ii) placing the prepared catalyst platinum-KLTL molecular sieve in a platinum metal precursor solution for ion exchange, filtering the suspension, washing the filter cake and drying to obtain platinum-KLTL catalyst, (iii) mixing platinum/KLTL catalyst powder and auxiliary agent compound precursor, grinding the mixture and vacuum drying to obtain platinum-M-KLTL catalyst, and (iv) activating the catalyst by reducing the solid catalyst obtained in the step (ii) or (iii), the reducing atmosphere is a mixture of carbon dioxide and hydrogen in a volume ratio of 2:1-5:1, the space velocity of the reducing gas is 1000-5000/hour, reducing temperature is 300-600 degrees C, heating rate is 10 degrees C/minute, pressure is normal pressure, and reducing time is 0.5-5 hours, and
   (2) using catalyst in the reverse water gas shift reaction, the reaction evaluation comprising loading the catalyst on a fixed bed reactor for reaction evaluation, feeding gas is a mixture of hydrogen and carbon dioxide in a volume ratio 2:1-5:1, the volumetric airspeed is 500-10000/hour, the reaction temperature is 200-600 degrees C, and the reaction pressure is atmospheric pressure
   .
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass content of the active components platinum in the catalyst is 0.1-5%. The alkali metal auxiliary agent-M is a mixture of lithium, sodium, potassium, and cesium.The amount of auxiliary agent in the catalyst is 0-500 in terms of M/Pt atomic ratio. The platinum source precursor used in the preparation of the catalyst is a mixture of tetraammine nitrate platinum, and dinitrosodiamine platinum or ethanolamine hydroxy platinum. The introduction of alkaline auxiliary agent in the catalyst is alkali metal nitrates, carbonates, bicarbonates, phosphates, hydrogen phosphates, dihydrogen phosphates, sulfates, hydroxides, and/or chlorides. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the hydrothermal temperature is 175 degrees C, the rotating speed is 20-50 revolutions/minute, and the time is 5-20 hours. In the step (ii), the water bath temperature for ion exchange is 50-80 degrees C, and the time is 5-48 hours.

TI Composite powder having microstructured particles,            obtainable by connecting large particles to small            particles, useful as additive, preferably as polymer            additive, implants in the field of neuro, oral rib and            shoulder surgery.
AU VUCAK M
AE SCHAEFER KALK GMBH & CO KG (SCHA-Non-standard)
   SCHAEFER KALK GMBH & CO KG (SCHA-Non-standard)
   SCHAEFER KALK GMBH & CO KG (SCHA-Non-standard)
   SCHAEFER KALK GMBH & CO KG (SCHA-Non-standard)
GA 2018216476
AB    NOVELTY - Composite powder having microstructured                particles, obtainable by connecting large particles                to small particles, is claimed. The large particles                have an average particle diameter of 0.1 mu m-10                mm, the large particles comprise at least one                polymer, the small particles are arranged on the                surface of the large particles and/or                non-homogeneously distributed within the large                particles. The small particles comprise spherical                precipitated calcium carbonate particles having an                average diameter of 0.05-50 mu m. The particles of                the composite powder have an average particle size                of 10-200 mu m.
   USE - The composite powder is useful as an additive,                preferably as a polymer additive, as an additive or                starting material for compounding, for producing                components by selective laser sintering of a                composition, for applications in medical                technology, in microtechnology, preferably,                implants for applications in the field of neuro,                oral, maxillofacial, ear, nose and throat surgery                and hand, foot, thoracic, rib and shoulder surgery,                and/or for producing foamed objects. The spherical                calcium carbonate are useful as an additive for                paper, plastics, paints and/or coatings, elastomers                and adhesives and sealants, in construction                chemistry, in dry mortar and in medical technology,                preferably as an additive in resorbable polymers                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite powder produces implant with                improved laser sintering properties, improved                flowability, and produces components improved                surface quality, and improved component density,                good shrinkage, improved dimensional stability and                good thermal conductivity outside the laser treated                area.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Composite powder having microstructured                particles, obtainable by connecting large particles                to small particles, is claimed. The large particles                have an average particle diameter of 0.1 mu m-10                mm, the large particles comprise at least one                polymer, the small particles are arranged on the                surface of the large particles and/or                non-homogeneously distributed within the large                particles. The small particles comprise spherical                precipitated calcium carbonate particles having an                average diameter of 0.05-50 mu m, where the                spherical calcium carbonate particles are                obtainable (a) providing a calcium hydroxide                suspension, (b) introducing carbon dioxide or a gas                mixture containing carbon dioxide into the                suspension from step (a), and (c) separating                occurring calcium carbonate particles, where                0.3-0.7 wt.% of at least one aminotrialkylene                phosphonic acid is added. The particles of the                composite powder have an average particle size of                10-200 mu m. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included                for spherical calcium carbonate particles,                obtainable by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composite powder                  comprises calcium carbonate particles obtainable                  by a process in which aminotrimethylene                  phosphonic acid, aminotriethylene phosphonic                  acid, aminotripropylene phosphonic acid and/or                  aminotributylene phosphonic acid are added. The                  spherical calcium carbonate particles have an                  average diameter of less than 30 mu m, preferably                  less than 20 mu m. The spherical calcium                  carbonate particles have a size distribution in                  which at least 90 wt.% calcium carbonate                  particles have a particle diameter of average                  particle diameter -30% to mean particle diameter                  30%. The spherical calcium carbonate particles                  have a shape factor, defined as the quotient of                  minimum particle diameter and maximum particle                  diameter, of greater than 0.90. The proportion of                  the precipitated calcium carbonate particles is                  at least 0.1 wt.%. The composite powder comprises                  40-80 wt.% PLLA and 20-60 wt.% precipitated                  calcium carbonate particles. Preferred Method:                  The introduction of the carbon dioxide or the                  carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture is carried                  out until the reaction mixture has a pH of less                  than 9. The reaction of the calcium hydroxide                  suspension with the carbon dioxide or the carbon                  dioxide-containing gas mixture is carried out at                  a temperature of less than 25 degrees C. The                  carbon dioxide or a carbon dioxide-containing gas                  mixture having a gas flow rate of 0.02-2 l carbon                  dioxide/(hx g calcium hydroxide) passes into the                  calcium hydroxide suspension.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The large particles                  comprise at least one thermoplastic polymer,                  preferably at least one resorbable polymer. The                  resorbable polymer has an inherent viscosity,                  measured in chloroform at 25 degrees C, 0.1%                  polymer concentration, of 0.3-8 dl/g. The large                  particles comprise poly-D, poly-L and / or                  poly-D, L-lactic acid. The large particles                  comprise resorbable polyester having a number                  average molecular weight of 500-1000000 g/mole.                  The large particles comprise polyamide and                  polyurethane.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
