PT P
PN CN107638788-A
TI Preparation of formaldehyde scavenger involves crushing tea leaves and Sansevieria leaves, extracting, adding e.g. cold brine, onion slices, and aqueous solution comprising grapefruit peel and lactic acid bacteria powder, and reacting.
AU DING Y
   LIU R
   LIU Q
AE DING Y (DING-Individual)
GA 201810599S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of formaldehyde scavenger involves adding tea leaves and Sansevieria leaves into a crusher, crushing, adding water, performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction, obtaining extract, mixing extract with cold brine, onion slices, pineapple slices and Houttuynia cordata, heating at 90-100 degrees C for 25-30 minutes, cooling, filtering, obtaining filtrate, mixing 30-40 pts. wt. filtrate with 10-20 solution (A) comprising grapefruit peel, lactic acid bacteria powder and water, and sodium citrate, penetrating agent and catalyst, reacting at 60-70 degrees C, and cooling.
   USE - Preparation of formaldehyde scavenger.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient preparation of formaldehyde scavenger. The raw materials of the formaldehyde scavenger are harmless to the human body. Since the formaldehyde scavenger does not use nucleophilic reagents such as urea for removal of harmful substances, the serious impact on human health is reduced. The plant extracts are fused to improve the scavenging ability of the formaldehyde and form a shielding net on the surface of the medium to adsorb and remove the free alcohol and other volatile substances in the medium. The penetrating agent enables quickly penetration of active ingredients into the formaldehyde-containing material, and rapid catalytic reaction of formaldehyde and oxygen in air to enhance cleaning effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of formaldehyde scavenger involves adding tea leaves and Sansevieria leaves into a crusher, crushing, adding the resultant crushed material and water in mass ratio of 1:3-4, into a container, performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction 2 or 3 times, obtaining extract, mixing 10-15 pts. wt. extract with 20-30 pts. wt. cold brine, 5-6 pts. wt. onion slices, 3-4 pts. wt. pineapple slices and 1-2 pts. wt. H.cordata, leaving still for 1-2 hours, heating at 90-100 degrees C for 25-30 minutes, cooling, filtering, obtaining filtrate, mixing 30-40 pts. wt. filtrate with 10-20 solution (A) and 5-10 pts. wt. sodium citrate, 3-4 pts. wt. penetrating agent and 1-2 pts. wt. catalyst, adding the resultant mixture into a reactor, stirring, reacting at 60-70 degrees C for 1-2 hours, and cooling the resultant reaction mixture. The solution (A) is prepared by mixing grapefruit peel, lactic acid bacteria powder and water in mass ratio of 4-5:0.8-1:1-2, wrapping the resultant mixture with a sponge, placing at 35-40 degrees C, leaving still for 1-2 days, and extruding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The cold brine comprises salt and water in mass ratio of 1:5-6.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The catalyst is tartaric acid or alumina.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The penetrating agent is fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether or sodium lauryl sulfonate.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN115180581-A
TI Sterile automatic filling device of microbial            inoculum used in sewage treatment facility, has            sterilizing unit which is set on inner side of filling            chamber and distributed at position close to two sides            of opening.
AU HU B
   YANG B
   SHEN X
   WU J
AE HANGZHOU RUIYIXIN IND CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2022D19496
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a receiving cylinder (202)                which is arranged on the filling chamber (201), and                a shunt pipe is arranged inside the filling                chamber. Two sides of the filling chamber are                respectively provided with openings for feeding and                discharging materials. A telescopic cylinder is                symmetrically and fixedly installed on the top wall                of the filling chamber. The shunt pipe is                controlled to rise and fall through the telescopic                cylinder. The shunt pipe is provided with multiple                butt pipes at equal intervals. The shunt pipe is                also connected with an elastic pipe. The bottom of                the receiving cylinder is provided with a                quantitative pump (205) for conveying the bacterial                agent. The output end of the quantitative pump is                in fixed communication with the elastic pipe                through a solenoid valve. A sterilizing unit is                arranged inside the filling chamber and is                distributed near the openings on both sides.
   USE - Sterile automatic filling device of microbial                inoculum used in sewage treatment facility for                converting organic matter contained in sewage into                inorganic nitrogen and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The device increases sealing performance when                filling butt joint. The device uniformly places a                packaging bottle on a storage groove at an inlet of                the filling chamber along with conveying of a                conveying belt, so that a set of packaging bottles                are synchronously conveyed to a lower part of butt                pipes, thus realizing synchronous filling                efficiency. The telescopic cylinder drives the                shunt pipe to press down, and the bottle opening                props a pressing cover to make the conducting pipe                elastically slide upwards so as to press a                connecting gasket to press the spring to compress,                and a bowl-shaped notch begins to tighten such that                a skid-proof convex pattern can be tightly adhered                to the opening of the bottle to realize sealing and                filling.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                sterile automatic filling device.

TI Low energy consumption carbon capturing cement            clinker production system, has lowest level cyclone            pre-heater discharging pipe whose column is connected            with smoke chamber, and that and rotary kiln connected            with cooling machine.
AU LIN M
   WU X
   CHEN C
   DAI Z
   PENG X
   HE X
AE CHINA NAT BUILDING MATERIAL GROUP CO LTD (CHNB-C)
   TIANJIN CEMENT IND DESIGN & RES INST (CHNB-C)
GA 2022D2341R
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a cooling machine, a rotary                kiln, a smoke chamber (1), a decomposing furnace, a                first cyclone pre-heater, a second cyclone                pretreatment, and a third cyclone prefabrication                and a fourth cyclone propagation that are                sequentially connected in sequence. A first row                cyclone preheater (3), a second row cyclone                pre-heater (4) and a third row cyclone pre-heater                (5) discharging pipe are connected with a                decomposing furnace feeding port. The decomposing                furnace outlet is connected with the first row of                the lowest level cyclone pre-heater air inlet. The                first column of the lowest level cyclone pre-heater                discharging pipe is connected with the smoke                chamber. The smoke chamber is not directly                connected with the decomposing furnace. The smoke                chamber and the rotary kiln are connected with the                cooling machine.
   USE - Low energy consumption carbon capturing cement                clinker production system for use in power, coal                and steel industry. Can also be used in cement                industry carbon emission related technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves carbon dioxide                concentration in the flue gas, simplifies the                process of collecting and purifying system process,                system design, and reduces the energy consumption,                and has small workload. The heat recycling                efficiency of the system is effectively ensured by                designing the tertiary air. The system design or                the improvement workload is small. The decomposing                furnace outlet is connected with the A row of the                lowest level cyclone pre-heater air inlet and the                smoke chamber is not directly connected to the                decomposing furnishing furnace feeding port, so                that the carbon-di-oxide (CO2) concentration is not                less than the required CO2 concentration. The flue                gases are collected, and purification system                process flow is simplified, and the system design                is simplified, and the workload is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing low energy consumption carbon                capturing cement clinker production system.

PN CN115127328-A
TI Energy-saving calcium carbonate production device            comprises heat absorbing fence tightly attached to            outer wall of lime cream generating container, where            lime milk generating container is used for reacting            water with quicklime.
AU LIANG R
   GONG S
AE LEIYANG BAIHUI POWDER CO LTD (LEIY-Non-standard)
GA 2022C92910
AB    NOVELTY - Energy-saving calcium carbonate production                device comprises a heat absorbing fence (1) tightly                attached to the outer wall of the lime cream                generating container. The lime milk generating                container is used for reacting water with quicklime                and discharging a lot of heat. The heat absorption                fence is provided with a liquid water and a                multiple of speed reducing plates (2) for heating                the liquid water, a plurality of the slow reducing                plates are staggered up and down, and one side of                the heat-absorbing fence is equipped with a primary                drying box and a secondary drying box for                collecting calcium carbonates with different                humidity. The first-stage drying box (5) and the                second-level drying box are respectively provided                with spiral pipes (29) with different density, and                multiple spiral pipes are communicate with the                inner part of the heating fence. The top of the                first stage drying box is set with a calcium                carbonation storage hopper (8) for storing calcium                carbonated to be dried.
   USE - The energy-saving calcium carbonate production                device is useful for producing light calcium                carbonates, which is useful in domestic industrial                production mainly is carbonization method,                calcining the limestone raw material to generate                lime and carbon dioxide, and adding lime milk to                the drying rod.
   ADVANTAGE - The device solves the problems of large energy                consumption and low calcium carbonate drying                efficiency in the process of preparing in the light                calciumcarbonate art. PN CN114980562-A
TI Method of making pure tin plate for printed            circuit board (PCB) of terminal device, involves            molding circuit board into preset size using mechanical            processing mode.
AU DUAN L
   XIE F
   YANG P
   LI Y
AE ZHUHAI ZHONGJING ELECTRONIC CIRCUIT CO (ZHUH-Non-standard)
GA 2022B51975
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves cutting the copper clad                laminate to a preset fixed size. The chemical                methods are used to deposit a layer of metal copper                on the mechanically drilled holes. The                electroplating method is used to plate the metal                hole of the sinking copper hole. The                photopolymerization reaction of the photosensitive                film is used to expose the required circuit                patterns, and the sodium carbonate is used to                remove the excess parts by taking advantage of                inability to withstand weak alkalis. The copper                layer and pure tin layer are plated on the                corresponding pattern circuit. The pure tin layer                is used as the resist layer of alkaline etching and                the welding layer of subsequent surface-mount                technology (SMT) and bonding. The mixed solution of                ammonium chloride and copper ion is used to                chemically react with metallic copper. The excess                copper layer on the pattern circuit is etched and                removed, and the solution does not react with pure                tin. The circuit board is milled to a preset                size.
   USE - Method of making pure tin plate for printed                circuit board (PCB) of terminal device (all                claimed) used in PCB, semiconductor, integrated                circuit (IC) packaging technology and other                fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The manufacturing method of pure tin plate                uses the surface processing method of plating pure                tin thickness of 4-8 um, replacing the traditional                nickel palladium gold and gold plating technique,                the cost is reduced by 65 %, at the same time,                which avoids nickel corrosion caused by component                problem, to improve the quality. The surface                treatment process cost of the substrate for forming                the gold-plating layer in the existing fifth                generation(5G) optical module product is low. The                method of manufacturing the conductive hole on the                circuit board further comprises laser drilling, UV                drilling and carbon dioxide drilling. The polyimide                film adheres a layer of insulating PI film on the                large metal surface area of the non-adhesive piece                to protect the tin surface.

PN CN114656988-A
TI Iron-titanium composite coke for low-carbon            iron-making is obtained by mixing and dry distillation            of iron-titanium additive and basic coal sample, where            iron-titanium additive is titanium concentrate,            vanadium-titanium-iron concentrate, or            vanadium-titanium magnetite powder ore.
AU WEI Z
   YIN C
   WEN L
   BAI C
   CHEN J
   ZHANG S
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 2022872772
AB    NOVELTY - The iron-titanium composite coke for                low-carbon iron-making is obtained by mixing and                dry distillation of iron-titanium additive and                basic coal sample in a mass ratio range of 0-0.2:1.                The iron-titanium additive is titanium concentrate,                vanadium-titanium-iron concentrate, or                vanadium-titanium magnetite powder ore. The base                coal sample is obtained by mixing 15-75 pts. wt.                coking coal, 10-30 pts. wt. 1/3 coking coal, 0-15                pts. wt. fat coal and 0-20 pts. wt. lean                coal.
   USE - Iron-titanium composite coke for low-carbon                iron-making.
   ADVANTAGE - The iron-making process can reduce the                temperature of the thermal storage area, strengthen                the indirect reduction reaction of iron ore,                improve the utilization efficiency of blast furnace                gas, reduce the coke ratio and reduce carbon                dioxide emissions; has high post-reaction strength                and can ensure that the blast furnace has good air                permeability and liquid permeability, with high                economic value and application prospects. The                method uses the co-existing additive of iron and                titanium to replace the iron-based additive for                coking, does not need to add expensive binder (such                as pitch), can still ensure the thermal strength of                the coke, can effectively reduce the production                cost, simplify the technological process, and                reduce the pollution load discharge. The method                directly uses blast furnace production raw                materials (such as vanadium ilmenite concentrate,                titanium concentrate) as iron and titanium                additives, and can utilize the high-quality gas                generated in the coking process to achieve a small                amount of pre-reduction of iron, vanadium and                titanium elements, and improve energy utilization.                The brick seam at the bottom and the lining surface                can effectively protect the furnace lining and                prolong the life of the blast furnace.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for manufacturing the iron-titanium composite coke                for low-carbon iron-making.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of a process flow of a method for producing the                iron-titanium composite coke. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

The system                can facilitate testing integrity of the pipe                segment at least in part by securing and sealing an                open end of the tubing in the annulus cavity to                enable fluid flow between the testing port on the                shell and a fluid conduit implemented within the                tubing of a pipe segment.

PN CN114478776-A
TI New anti-chicken toll like receptor 15 (TLR15)            protein polyclonal antibody prepared by biological            immunization of recombinant protein encoded and            expressed by recombinant plasmid pET30a-chTLR15 useful            for specifically detecting chicken TLR15 protein.
AU ZHANG W
   YOU C
   SUN Y
   LI L
   PAN P
   YU X
AE TIANJIN AGRIC COLLEGE (UNTN-C)
GA 202271193W
AB    NOVELTY - Anti-chicken toll like receptor 15 (TLR15)                protein polyclonal antibody, which is prepared by                biological immunization of the recombinant protein                encoded and expressed by the recombinant plasmid                pET30a-chTLR15 is new. The TLR15 gene sequence in                the recombinant plasmid pET30achTLR15 is a fully                defined sequence of 618 nucleotides (SEQ ID NO: 3),                as given in the specification, which is obtained                from a fully defined sequence of 606 nucleotides                (SEQ ID NO: 1), as given in the specification                through codon-optimization and the encoded amino                acid sequence is a fully defined sequence of 202                amino acids (SEQ ID NO: 2), as given in the                specification.
   USE - The antibody is useful for specifically                detecting chicken TLR15 protein (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The antibody has high specificity, which                provides important support for studying the role of                chTLR15 in poultry immunity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   preparing anti-chicken TLR15 protein                polyclonal antibody; and
   a specific detection method of anti-chicken                TLR15 protein polyclonal antibody comprising                obtaining the protein sample: culturing DF-1 cell                to about 70% at 37&#8451; and 5% carbon dioxide                incubator, extracting DF-1 cell protein, taking 100                mg chicken bursa and thymus tissue, adding 1 ml                protein lysis solution, grinding and mixing with a                freezer grinder, specific detection: transferring                the extracted protein to polyvinylidene difluoride                (PVDF) membrane after SDS-PAGE gel electrophoresis,                where the transferring condition is constant                current 110 V and 85 minutes, using quick sealing                liquid to seal for 1 hour at room temperature,                washing for 2 times by Tris-buffered saline with                Tween-20 (Polysorbate 20), using &#946;-actin                (1:1000) and chTLR15 polyclonal antibody serum (1:                1000) as primary antibodies, incubating at                4&#8451; overnight, washing with TBST for 3                times, each time for 5 minutes, taking goat                anti-mouse immunoglobulin G (IgG) (1:1000) marked                by horseradish peroxidase and goat anti-rabbit IgG                (1:1000) marked by horseradish peroxidase as                secondary antibody, incubating for 90 minutes at                normal temperature, washing for 3 times with TBST,                each time for 5 minutes, exposing by luminescent                liquid and observing the specific result.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  protein polyclonal antibody comprises (a)                  performing chicken TLR15 antigenicity analysis,                  codon optimization and synthesis of gene segment,                  (b) constructing prokaryotic expression vector                  pET30a-chTLR15, (c) using recombinant plasmid to                  transform into competent Escherichia coli,                  obtaining engineered bacteria, (d) inducing                  engineered bacteria for expression of recombinant                  protein, and purifying and (e) using recombinant                  protein for immunizing rabbit and preparing the                  polyclonal antibody.Preferred Method: The step of chicken TLR15                  antigenicity analysis and gene fragment synthesis                  method comprises analyzing the chTLR15 sequence                  published on NCBI, selecting a fragment of 606                  base pairs (bp) (76-681 bp) with strong                  antigenicity, performing codon optimization,                  adding EcoRI and XhoI restriction sites at both                  ends to synthesize the gene sequence, where the                  sequence is a fully defined sequence of 618                  nucleotides (SEQ ID NO: 3), as given in the                  specification. The steps of constructing the                  recombinant vector comprises using EcoRI and XhoI                  to digest the pET30 vector and the chTLR15 gene                  synthesis fragment respectively, cutting the gel                  to recover the fragment, and ligating to obtain                  the recombinant plasmid pET30a-chTLR15. The steps                  of preparing the engineered bacteria are                  transforming the recombinant plasmid                  pET30achTLR15 into BL21 competent cells to                  construct the engineered bacteria with the                  recombinant plasmid. The steps of expressing and                  purifying the chTLR15 recombinant protein                  comprises inducing the expression by Isopropyl                  &#946;-D-1-thiogalactopyranoside (IPTG), and                  obtaining chTLR15 recombinant protein by washing                  the nickel column with deionized water and                  purifying. The method for preparing polyclonal                  antibody in step (e) comprises immunizing New                  Zealand white rabbits with chTLR15 recombinant                  protein, where the inoculation method is                  multipoint subcutaneous injection on the back,                  using Freund's complete adjuvant for the first                  vaccination, using Freund's incomplete adjuvant                  for first, second, and third immunization in the                  fifth week, collecting rabbit serum 7 days after                  booster immunization to obtain polyclonal                  antibody against chTLR15 protein.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of gas hydrate accelerator based                on cellulose filter paper, involves using cellulose                filter paper as raw material, regulating pore                structure through phase transition of water and                performing high-temperature pyrolysis in an inert                atmosphere. The method preferably involves using                phase transition of water to modulate the pore                structure, immersing the cellulose filter paper in                water, freezing and freeze-drying at -48degrees                Celsius and 9 Pa for 48 hours to obtain                freeze-dried cellulose filter paper, and performing                high-temperature pyrolysis under inert atmosphere,                heating lyophilized cellulose filter paper to                300-900degrees Celsius at heating rate of                2-8degrees Celsius/minute under inert atmosphere,                maintaining the temperature for 1-3 hours for high                temperature pyrolysis and naturally cooling to room                temperature, where the gas hydrate accelerator has                specific surface area of 20.6-254.4 m2/g, and is                rich in mesopores with pore diameter of 2-20                nm.
   USE - Preparation of gas hydrate accelerator used                for surfactant e.g. anionic surfactant, cationic                surfactant, amphoteric surfactant and nonionic                surfactant (all claimed) for natural gas storage,                carbon dioxide sealing and gas separation                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas hydrate accelerator has large specific                surface area, hierarchical porous structure and                rich surface functional group, can obviously                enhance gas-liquid two phase contact and crystal                nucleation, so as to strengthen formation of                hydrate. The method is economical, uses easily                available raw material, and has simple preparation                technique, fast hydrate nucleating and growth,                forming dynamics controllable, high gas storage                density and excellent cycle performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                application of gas hydrate accelerator for                surfactant, which involves: (a) inhaling the gas                hydrate accelerator into surfactant solution with                amount of 1-5 times its own weight and placing in                reaction container and sealing; (b) vacuumizing the                reaction container and pipeline to remove air,                placing the reaction container at constant                temperature of -2-2degrees Celsius until the                temperature in the container is stable; and (c)                injecting natural gas into the reaction container,                so that the pressure in the container is 4-8 MPa to                carry out hydration reaction, stabilizing pressure                and temperature in the reaction container and                stopping hydration reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inert atmosphere                  is nitrogen, helium gas or argon gas. Preferred                  Composition: The concentration of the surfactant                  is 50-1000 ppm, preferably 300 ppm.

PN CN113973866-A
TI Toast comprises high-gluten flour, golden ear mushroom pulp, golden ear mushroom slurry, milk powder, soft white sugar, butter, eggs, active dry yeast, salt, and gold ear mycelium polysaccharide.
AU ZHANG W
   ZHANG S
   CAO J
   LUO X
   ZHOU P
   SUN D
   HE R
AE KUNMING INST EDIBLE MUSHROOM ALL CHINA (KUNM-Non-standard)
GA 2022299664
AB    NOVELTY - Toast comprises 250 pts. wt. high-gluten flour, 160-180 pts. wt. golden ear mushroom pulp, 15-25 pts. wt. golden ear mushroom slurry, 20-30 pts. wt. milk powder, 15-25 pts. wt. soft white sugar, 10-20 pts. wt. butter, 10-20 pts. wt. eggs, 3.75 pts. wt. active dry yeast, 2.5 pts. wt. salt, 4-6 pts. wt. gold ear mycelium polysaccharide.
   USE - Used as toast.
   ADVANTAGE - The toast can reduce the amount of heat-sensitive nutrients in the drying process and damage loss.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included forpreparation of toast, comprising: (i) classifying high-gluten flour, golden ear mushroom slurry, milk powder, soft white sugar, eggs, salt and golden ear mycelium polysaccharide into dry and wet materials, mixing the dry and wet materials, adding into the dough mixer, mixing at low speed for 5 minutes, when a complete dough is formed, adding active dry yeast and mixing at low speed for 5 minutes, when gluten appears, adding softened butter and continuing mixing at low speed for 10 minutes until a dough with a smooth surface is formed and stretching into thin fascia; (ii) placing the dough in a fermentation box at 36degreesC and a relative humidity of 75%, and fermenting for 1 hour, when the dough rises to twice its original volume, the fermentation is complete; (iii) cutting into pieces, dividing the dough into three small dough equally, cutting quickly, and ensuring that the temperature and humidity of the dough change little; (iv) kneading the dough into a small circle with a complete shape and a smooth surface, and expelling the carbon dioxide gas inside the dough to ensure that the yeast is fully propagated and fermented; (v) placing the small balls at room temperature for 10-20 minutes to promote the dough to fully ferment; (vi) squeezing the air bubbles, spreading a layer of golden ear mushrooms, folding the dough in three, squeezing and foaming, then spreading a layer of golden ear mushrooms; rolling up the dough; (vii) secondary fermenting; (viii) baking until the surface is golden brown; (ix) cooling the toast under natural conditions, and packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (i) is cleaning the golden ear mushroom to remove impurities, cutting the golden ear mushroom, dicing into 25 mm small pieces. The golden ear mycelium polysaccharide is obtained by: taking golden ear mycelium powder, adding 120 times distilled water, and performing ultrasonic-assisted extraction of mycelial polysaccharide, where the ultrasonic power is 600 W, the initial temperature is 100degreesC, and the ultrasonic time is 20 minutes, and the extraction is performed twice, centrifuging at 4000 revolutions per minute for 10 minutes, collecting the supernatant, extracting the precipitate again, mixing the supernatant, concentrating the supernatant to 1/5 of the original volume at 60degreesC, adding 4 times the volume of anhydrous ethanol, refrigerating at 4degreesC for 1-2 hours, collecting the precipitate by centrifugation, reconstituting with deionized water, deproteinizing by the Sevage method, separating to obtain the supernatant, and then precipitating with absolute ethanol again, collecting the precipitate and freeze-drying. The preparation of golden ear fungus slurry is: cutting golden ear fungus into 8-12 mm, then adding 1.5 times the volume of water, using a food processor to beat to obtain golden ear mushroom slurry.

Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).); A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); ; D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PT P
PN WO2022015717-A1; US2022017374-A1
TI Making alkali metal cyanide by contacting hydrogen            cyanide with aqueous solution of alkali metal carbonate            or mixture of alkali metal carbonate and alkali metal            bicarbonate under reaction condition sufficient to            produce cyanide solution.
AU LEFENFELD M
   HOCH R
   MANGANARO J
   NORCROSS R
   TAN S K
AE CYANCO CORP (CYAN-Non-standard)
   CYANCO CORP (CYAN-Non-standard)
GA 202214732G
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (M1) alkali metal cyanide comprises:                contacting hydrogen cyanide with an aqueous                solution of an alkali metal carbonate or of a                mixture of alkali metal carbonate and alkali metal                bicarbonate under reaction conditions sufficient to                produce an aqueous alkali metal cyanide                solution.
   USE - The methods are useful for producing alkali                metal cyanide.
   ADVANTAGE - The methods: improve production of alkali                metal cyanide, sodium cyanide production; are                beneficial to remove carbon dioxide from the liquid                phase; and are efficient and cost-effective                production of sodium cyanide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) producing (M2) an alkali metal cyanide                comprising: contacting hydrogen cyanide with an                aqueous solution of an alkali metal carbonate or of                a mixture of alkali metal carbonate and alkali                metal bicarbonate in a reaction chamber under                reaction conditions sufficient to produce an                aqueous alkali metal cyanide solution, removing a                gaseous effluent from the reaction chamber,                removing the aqueous alkali metal cyanide solution                from the reaction chamber as a liquid effluent, and                separating any solid alkali metal bicarbonate                present in the liquid effluent from the aqueous                alkali metal cyanide solution; and
   (2) producing (M3) sodium cyanide comprising                reacting hydrogen cyanide with an aqueous solution                of sodium carbonate in a first reaction chamber                under reaction conditions sufficient to produce an                aqueous sodium cyanide solution, removing a gaseous                effluent from the reaction chamber, removing the                aqueous sodium cyanide solution from the reaction                chamber as a liquid effluent, and separating any                solid sodium bicarbonate present in the liquid                effluent from the aqueous sodium cyanide solution;                and
   (3) system for producing alkali metal cyanide                comprising a reaction chamber for contacting                hydrogen cyanide with an aqueous solution of an                alkali metal carbonate or of a mixture of an alkali                metal carbonate and an alkali metal bicarbonate to                produce an alkali metal cyanide solution, a gas                phase output port in communication with the                reaction chamber for discharging a gaseous effluent                created in the reaction chamber, a liquid phase                output port in communication with the reaction                chamber for discharging a liquid effluent created                in the reaction chamber, a separation device for                receiving the liquid effluent and separating a                solid bicarbonate from the liquid effluent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method (M2) further                  comprises: the step of converting the separated                  alkali metal bicarbonate to alkali metal                  carbonate in the presence of water and,                  optionally, recycling the aqueous alkali metal                  carbonate to the contacting step; and the step of                  removing hydrogen cyanide from the gaseous                  effluent. In the method (M2) contacting step                  comprises contacting a hydrogen cyanide                  containing gas with the aqueous solution of an                  alkali metal carbonate or of a mixture of alkali                  metal carbonate and alkali metal bicarbonate                  under reaction conditions sufficient to produce                  an aqueous alkali metal cyanide solution. The                  aqueous solution of an alkali metal carbonate or                  of a mixture of alkali metal carbonate and alkali                  metal bicarbonate is contacted with excess                  hydrogen cyanide. The step of removing hydrogen                  cyanide from the gaseous effluent from the                  reaction chamber comprises scrubbing the gaseous                  effluent with aqueous sodium hydroxide. In the                  method (M3) contacting step comprises contacting                  a hydrogen cyanide containing gas with the                  aqueous solution of sodium carbonate or a mixture                  of sodium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate under                  reaction conditions sufficient to produce an                  aqueous sodium cyanide solution. The method (M3)                  further: processes the liquid stream comprises                  separating solid sodium bicarbonate from the                  aqueous sodium cyanide solution; comprises prior                  to the contacting step, the step of cooling the                  hydrogen cyanide containing gas; and comprises                  prior to the contacting step, the step of cooling                  the hydrogen cyanide containing gas. Preferred                  Components: The alkali metal is lithium, sodium                  (preferred) or potassium. The system further                  comprises a secondary absorber for receiving the                  gaseous effluent from the gas phase output port                  and recovering residual hydrogen cyanide. The                  secondary absorber includes an absorption liquid                  input port for receiving an absorption liquid for                  recovering the residual hydrogen cyanide. The                  absorption liquid is aqueous sodium hydroxide,                  aqueous potassium hydroxide or aqueous sodium                  carbonate. The secondary absorber includes a                  liquid output port for routing a liquid effluent                  back to the reaction chamber. The secondary                  absorber includes a liquid output port for                  routing a liquid effluent to a decomposition                  reactor. The system further comprises a cooling                  chamber for cooling the gaseous hydrogen cyanide                  prior to entering the reaction chamber. The                  system further comprises a decomposition reactor                  that receives a bicarbonate cake from the                  separation device and converts the bicarbonate                  cake to sodium carbonate, where the decomposition                  reactor optionally includes a liquid output port                  for routing the sodium carbonate back to the                  reaction chamber and where the decomposition                  reactor optionally includes a vapor output port                  for removing carbon dioxide.
   EXAMPLE - Three one-liter flasks were assembled. Flask A                was hydrogen cyanide generator, flask B was an                absorber, and flask C was a trap. At the start of                the experiment, flask A contains aqueous                hydrochloric acid, flask B contains an aqueous                saturated sodium bicarbonate solution with initial                pH 8.6, and flask C contains an aqueous sodium                hydroxide solution. All three flasks were at                ambient temperature. Over the course of an hour,                49.8 mg of cyanide was fed to flask A as a sodium                cyanide solution. Air was also fed into flask A so                that the generated hydrogen cyanide was swept into                flask B. The quantity of sodium bicarbonate in                flask B was stoichiometrically equivalent to the                49.8 mg of cyanide fed to flask A. At the end of                the hour, flask B contained 37.5 mg of cyanide                equivalents, corresponds to a 75% yield of sodium                cyanide fed to flask A.

PT P
PN WO2021234419-A1; RU2020116987-A
TI Composition for substrate for treating fungus related diseases of soil, for e.g., improving quality of soil, compost, growing media, or organic based fertilizer, and for use in pharmaceutical wastewater treatment, comprises carbon and ash.
AU BALAKAREV V
AE SKY EAST UK LTD (SKYE-Non-standard)
   BALAKAREV V (BALA-Individual)
   BALAKAREV S (BALA-Individual)
   KARPENKO A (KARP-Individual)
GA 2021D5305S
AB    NOVELTY - A composition comprises 65-95 wt.% carbon and 2-25 wt.% ash.
   USE - A composition for substrate used for treating fungus related diseases of the soil; for use in improving the quality of soil, compost, growing media, or organic based fertilizer; for use as soil fertility enhancer for agricultural and/or horticultural purposes, for vertical farming, and for urban greening purposes, including green roofs and walls; for use as active agent to clean soils from different contaminants which may include residues from herbicides and/or pesticides; for enhancing the properties of composts and similar growing media; for enhancing the properties of an organic fertilizer; for use in pharmaceutical wastewater treatment; and for use in carbon dioxide capture (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition is easily available at a low cost and is more environment-friendly. When applied to soil, the composition improves crop productivity, improves and extends the fertility of the soil, and reduces the quantity of fertilizers that may be required. The use of the composition makes the soil more fertile and more productive over a longer period of time than soil that does not comprise the composition. The composition adsorbs contaminants from wastewater. The use of the composition improves the fertility of soils used for green roofs and walls, keeping the plants provided in the green roof or green wall alive with minimal effort. The composition also improves carbon capture and storage, thus reducing the emissions typically associated with fertilizer production. The provision of a composition comprising micro and macro elements in the form of nutrients is beneficial for soil health.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) production of composition which involves loading feedstock into a pyrolysis reactor, heating reactor to 80-750 degrees C, carrying out pyrolysis reaction within the reactor, and extracting the composition from the reactor;
   (2) substrate comprising the composition;
   (3) improving the quality of soil, compost, growing media, or organic based fertilizer using the composition; and
   (4) preparation of substrate which comprises the composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: Feedstock is selected from pressed fruits and/or fruit pulp, vegetables, fruit stones, a by-product of whiskey or beer, banana plants, coffee beans, plants, grass, leaves, herbs, olives, leaves, flowers, tea leaves and tea plants, and/or solid digestate (waste products from an anerobic digestion process). Pressed fruits and/or fruit pulp are obtained from apples, pears, grapes, and/or berries. Fruit stones are obtained from nectarines, peaches, apricots, avocados and/ or olives. Substrate further comprises greater than or equal to 1 fertilizer and/or organic fulfillment material. Fertilizer comprises worm casts and/or where the organic fulfillment material comprises coconut wool. Fertilizer comprises worm casts or manure. Substrate is a living substrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Composition comprises 66-90 wt.% carbon and 5-10 wt.% ash. Composition further comprises 2-2.5 wt.% nitrogen. Composition further comprises nutrients selected from phosphorus, potassium, magnesium, sulfur, copper, zinc, sodium, and/or calcium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: Composition comprises 80-90 wt.%, preferably 85-90 wt.% organic matter.
   EXAMPLE - The examples do not provide sufficient details of the process of preparing the composition.

PT P
PN US2021270470-A1; WO2022061045-A1
TI Enclosure e.g. greenhouse, temperature regulation system, has ground to air heat transfer system comprising enclosure with ceiling, enclosure floor, and heat exchange manifold configured below floor of enclosure.
AU HOUPE C L
   SCHILLER L
   KUMAR S
   PLINKE M
   GORDON R A
   PINKE MARC A E
AE CERES GREENHOUSE SOLUTIONS LLC (CERE-Non-standard)
   CERES GREENHOUSE SOLUTIONS LLC (CERE-Non-standard)
GA 2021A1795X
AB    NOVELTY - The system has air moving device that is turned on by the controller (74) when a enclosure temperature exceeds an upper threshold temperature to produce a hot flow of enclosure gas into the inlet opening that is cooled in the heat exchange manifold to produce a cooled flow of enclosure gas through the outlet opening into the enclosure. The air moving device is turned on by the controller when the enclosure temperature drops below a lower threshold temperature to produce a cool flow of enclosure gas into the inlet opening that is heated in the heat exchange manifold to produce a heated flow of enclosure gas through the outlet opening into the enclosure. The heat is exchanged between the flow of enclosure gas in the heat exchange manifold and the heat reservoir, where the heat exchange takes place below the floor of the enclosure.
   USE - Enclosure temperature regulation system for use in ground to air heat transfer system (GAHT) or greenhouse for growing plants in high latitude regions and in high altitude locations for cold weather climates. Can also be used as dwelling for people or animals or for production or product, and in treatment of biosolids.
   ADVANTAGE - The greenhouse enables efficient moderation of the temperature within the greenhouse. It enables efficient plant production in higher latitude regions and in high altitude locations where there may be large temperature swings throughout the day and night. The greenhouse can be set under slight positive pressure to reduce any exchange with outside air. This will prevent disease and decrease carbon dioxide usage. A pressurized ground-to-air heat transfer system (GAHT) system will also reduce radon buildup in the greenhouse, and may be used to pre-cool or pre-heat the incoming vent air to prevent or reduce mold. It may be rolled out, such as along the roof or ceiling and may reduce the heat loss from the greenhouse at night.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a greenhouse.
   Greenhouse (10)
   Roof (12)
   Greenhouse enclosure (13)
   Controller (74)
   Interior light (122)

TI Poly(aryletherketone sulfone) blended alloy foam material is foam beads or foam sheets comprising long-chain branched poly(aryletherketone sulfone) with preset melt index, and crystalline poly(aryletherketone) with preset melt index.
AU ZHOU G
   WANG H
   ZHAO J
   WANG Z
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2021A4061R
AB    NOVELTY - A poly(aryletherketone sulfone) blended alloy foam material is foam beads or foam sheets comprising long-chain branched poly(aryletherketone sulfone) (I) or (II) as component (A), and crystalline poly(aryletherketone) as component (B). The crystalline poly(aryletherketone) is chosen from compounds (IIIa)-(IIIe). The melt index of long-chain branched poly(aryletherketone sulfone) is 20-100 g/10 minutes at 380 degrees C and crystalline poly(aryletherketone) is 10-100 g/10 minutes at 380 degrees C.
   USE - Poly(aryletherketone sulfone) blended alloy foam material.
   ADVANTAGE - The poly(aryletherketone sulfone) blended alloy foam material has improved mechanical property and room temperature compressibility increased from 10% to 100% compared with that before modification.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A poly(aryletherketone sulfone) blended alloy foam material is foam beads or foam sheets comprising long-chain branched poly(aryletherketone sulfone) of formula (I) or (II) as component (A), and crystalline poly(aryletherketone) as component (B). The crystalline poly(aryletherketone) is chosen from compounds of formulae (IIIa)-(IIIe). The melt index of long-chain branched poly(aryletherketone sulfone) is 20-100 g/10 minutes at 380 degrees C and crystalline poly(aryletherketone) is 10-100 g/10 minutes at 380 degrees C.
   m+n = 1;
   n/m = 0-0.2 (excluding 0 and 0.2);
   Ar' = group chosen from groups of formulae (a)-(f);
   Ar'' = group chosen from groups of formulae (g)-(i);
   X = group of formula (j) or (k);
   A = group of formula (l');and
   p = not defined.
   N.B: p is not defined. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the poly(aryletherketone sulfone) blended alloy foam material, which involves blending component (A) and component (B) at high speed in high-speed mixer, extruding through twin-screw extruder to obtain blended polymer alloy pellets or sheets, placing blended polymer alloy pellets or sheets in autoclave, passing carbon dioxide, reacting at 200-350 degrees C under pressure of 5-30 MPa for 15-180 minutes, quickly opening the valve of the autoclave, releasing the pressure to normal pressure, taking out and cooling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: When blended gold foam material is foam beads, the mass ratio of component (A) and component (B) is 80:20-99:1. When blended gold foam material is foam sheets, the mass ratio of component (A) and component (B) is 10:90-80:20. Preferred Condition: The extrusion temperature is 320-390 degrees C, preferably 355-390 degrees C.

TI Device for realizing comprehensive purification of oil fume pollutants in catering industry, has tube gap casing that is formed between adjacent inner capillaries in heat exchanger, and inner capillary tube in exchanger is fixedly used for cold water to pass through to form water pipe path.
AU ZHU T
   ZHAO J
   SUN Y
AE UNIV BEIHANG (UNBA-C)
GA 202199195G
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a hearth or a combustion furnace (1), a centrifugal oil smoke separating device (3), a flue gas discharging pipeline, a drainage fan (8), a high temperature flue gas pipe (2), a pipeline mixer (4), a heat catalytic oxidation processor (5), and a casing-and-tube gas-water heat exchanger (6). The hearth or the combustion furnace is provided with a high temperature flue gas outlet. The high temperature flue gas outlet is connected with one end of a high temperature flue gas conduit. The other end of the high temperature flue gas conduit is connected on the switching pipeline. The hole on the lower porous plate is used for passing through the high temperature oil smoke after the thermal catalytic oxidation processor. A tube gap casing is formed between the adjacent inner capillaries in the casing-and-tube gas-water heat exchanger. The inner capillary tube in the casing-and-tube gas-water heat exchanger is used for cold water to pass through to form water pipe path.
   USE - Device for realizing comprehensive purification of oil fume pollutants in catering industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The device adds heat catalytic oxidation technology and oil smoke changing transmission mode to realize comprehensive purification of aerosol and volatile organic compounds oxidized into carbon dioxide, water and other harmless substances in oil smoke, avoids the heat released from the cooking food to the environment air increasing the kitchen temperature, reduces the discomfort of the cooking staff, and ensures good operation environment. The heat circulation is realized, and the energy is saved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for realizing comprehensive purification of oil fume pollutants in catering industry.

PT P
PN WO2021146417-A1; CA3167773-A1; KR2022119513-A; AU2021207887-A1
TI Treating lithium solution to extract lithium            involves e.g. passing feed stream having lithium ions            by cation exchange resin, exchanging ions, expelling            raffinate stream, passing eluent stream, and            eluting.
AU BISHKIN D
   DAVID B
AE BL TECHNOLOGIES INC (BMCC-C)
   BL TECHNOLOGIES INC (BMCC-C)
GA 202183111K
AB    NOVELTY - Treating a lithium solution comprises, passing                a feed stream comprising lithium ions through a                cation exchange resin loaded with monovalent                cations other than lithium, exchanging the lithium                ions of the feed stream with the monovalent cations                of the ion exchange resin to convert the ion                exchange resin from the counterion form to a                lithium ion form, expelling a raffinate stream                comprising the monovalent cations, passing an                eluent stream comprising monovalent cations of                hydroxide or bicarbonate through the ion exchange                resin having the lithium ion form, exchanging the                monovalent cations of the eluent stream with the                lithium ions of the ion exchange resin and eluting                an eluate stream comprising lithium ions.
   USE - The method or system is useful for treating                lithium solution i.e. salar brine to extract                lithium.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes ion exchange which avoids                the direct addition of sodium carbonate, sodium                hydroxide or a similar reagent to filtered                solutions, thus reducing the potential for                contamination in the final product; increases the                conversion rate; reduces energy consumption;                reduces the production of waste products; utilizes                sodium bicarbonate and sodium carbonate during                elution which avoid gas pockets formation in the                resin bed, thus reducing the likelihood of                formation of carbon dioxide gas pockets; improves                lithium recovery and reduces sodium hydroxide                usage; and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                system for lithium metathesis, comprising, an ion                exchange resin having, a loading configuration with                a feed input and a raffinate output, an elution                configuration with an eluent input and an eluate                output, a monovalent other than lithium form in the                loading configuration and, a lithium ion form in                the elution configuration, a feed stream comprising                lithium ions and anions added to the resin through                the feed input, a raffinate stream comprising                monovalent cations other than lithium from the ion                exchange resin and the anions from the feed stream,                expelled from the resin through the raffinate                output, an eluent stream comprising monovalent                cations (other than lithium) and anions, added to                the resin through the eluent input and, an eluate                stream comprising the lithium ions of the resin and                the anions of the eluent stream, eluted from the                resin through the eluate output.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method the ion                  exchange resin: is arranged as a column or at                  least two columns arranged in series; and is                  employed in a series of columns, arranged in a                  ring, with eluent, feed, and rinses injected and                  eluate and raffinate withdrawn in a simulated                  moving bed (SMB). The feed and eluent are                  alternately passed through the column from                  opposing ends with raffinate and eluent also                  removed from opposing ends, optionally with a                  rinse buffer passed back and forth through the                  column prior to introducing feed or eluent, as in                  the manner commonly referred to as reciprocating                  bed ion exchange. The method further comprises:                  adding a low conductivity rinse to the resin                  after the addition of the feed or the eluent; a                  step of evaporative crystallizing the eluate                  stream to form a lithium rich cake and a                  concentrated mother liquor, high in                  sodium/potassium compared to the eluate. The                  method comprises: using concentrated mother                  liquor, after dilution, as a primary eluent;                  producing carbon dioxide from the evaporation                  eluate stream and at least one of (i) reusing the                  carbon dioxide to convert the mother liquor from                  the carbonate form to the bicarbonate form to use                  as a primary eluent, and (ii) reusing the carbon                  dioxide to convert fresh sodium carbonate to                  sodium bicarbonate to use as a secondary eluent.                  Preferred Components: The ion exchange resin is a                  strong acid cation exchange resin. The                  counterions are sodium ions, hydrogen ions or                  potassium ions. The eluent stream comprises                  sodium hydroxide, sodium bicarbonate, and/or                  sodium carbonate. The raffinate stream further                  comprises non-ionic components of the feed. The                  ion exchange resin is a strong acid cation                  exchange resin.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the system when the                  feed stream is added to the ion exchange resin in                  the loading configuration, the raffinate stream                  is expelled and the ion exchange resin is                  converted to the elution configuration. The                  eluent stream is added to the ion exchange resin                  in the elution configuration, the eluate stream                  is eluted and the ion exchange resin is converted                  to the loading configuration. The system further                  comprises a crystallization stage. The                  crystallization stage includes a precipitation                  stage using direct steam or boiling by indirect                  heat.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. AU MAO D

   PAN X
AE WUXI NO 9 PEOPLES HOSPITAL (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 202176871P
AB    NOVELTY - Osteogenic differentiation medium comprises alpha -minimum essential medium (MEM), 5-50% volume percentage of fetal bovine serum, 0.1-5% volume percentage of insulin-transferrin-selenium (ITS-G), 1-10 ng/ml basic fibroblast growth factor (bFGF), 1-10 ng/ml epidermal growth factor (EGF), 5-50 ng/ml vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF), 5-50 ng/ml transforming growth factor (TGF- beta ), 0.1-5% volume percentage of mesenchymal stem cell growth supplement (MSCGS), 0.1-10 millimole (mM) sodium pyruvate, 0.01-1 mM mercaptoethanol, 10-200 nM dexamethasone, 10-150 mu g/ml Vc, 1-100mM beta -glycerophosphate sodium, 10-50 mM L-glutamine, 0.1-5% streptomycin.
   USE - Osteogenic differentiation medium used for endothelial cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The osteogenic differentiation medium has simple preparation method, fast inducing speed and stable process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for method for preparing an osteogenic differentiation medium for endothelial cells, which involves:
   (A) preparing mother liquor of VEGF, TGF- beta , sodium pyruvate, mercaptoethanol, dexamethasone, Vc, beta -sodium glycerophosphate, L-glutamine, and streptomycin, and sterilizing by filtration with 0.22 mu m filter membrane;
   (B) configuring the working concentration of skin microvascular endothelial cell culture medium in the ultra-clean table according to the formula specified above;
   (C) resuscitating the skin microvascular endothelial cells from the liquid nitrogen tank, adjusting the cell state to reach the logarithmic growth phase, digesting the cells with 0.25% trypsin, and resuspending the cells with endothelial cell osteogenic differentiation culture medium, making the concentration upto 1 x 106/mL;
   (D) group culturing by takin 100 mu l of the cell suspension, adding 1 x 105 cells to a 12-well plate, adding 900 mu l of endothelial cell osteogenic differentiation medium to a 12-well plate under the conditions of 5% carbon dioxide and culturing in an incubator at 37 degrees C;
   (E) removing the culture medium in the 12-well plate, adding new endothelial cell osteogenic induction culture medium, and replacing it every 3 days, forming of osteoblasts after 21 days; and
   (F) detecting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The TGF- beta is TGF- beta 2. The skin microvascular endothelial cell is selected from epidermal microvascular endothelial or dermal microvascular endothelial. The preparation method further uses 1% by volume of non-essential amino acids, or 1% by volume of antibiotics.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing a wrinkle-removing agent extracted from umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells, which involves collecting and culturing mesenchymal stem cells, preparing cell mixture, passing at a ratio of 1:3, controlling passage within 7 generations, where the number of cells is 106 and 108, preparing 2-3 ml cells, where mesenchymal stem cells are bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells, which are obtained from bone marrow fluid collected under aseptic conditions by gradient density centrifugation, controlling the number of bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells at 106-108 in 10ml bone marrow fluid, after repeated washing, adding the cell suspension to the culture system, incubating in a constant temperature box containing 4-6% carbon dioxide and 36.5-37.5 degrees C for 40-52 hours, changing cell suspension, discarding, suspending the cells, when the adherent cells reaches 80% confluence, digesting with 0.25% EDTA trypsin, repeatedly washing the cells, passing the cell suspension in the ratio of 1:(2-4), drawing the recipient venous blood using syringe, injecting the recipient venous blood into a sterile test tube with anticoagulant, filtering and centrifuging through the mixing device, using syringe to extract the plasma on the centrifuged blood, adding growth factors and insulin to the recipient's plasma, growth factors include basic fibroblast growth factor, epidermal cell growth factor, and vascular endothelial growth factor, adding 8-11 mu g/l basic fibroblast growth factor, 4-6 mu g/l epidermal growth factor, 8-12 mu g/l vascular endothelial growth factor and 8-12 mu M insulin, using hyaluronic acid as a bio-scaffold, mixing before injection, mixing 2% hyaluronic acid, the plasma after adding growth factors and insulin, and adding mesenchymal stem cell concentrate into the syringe obtained by suction and mixing process.

TI Helix compensator for use in compensator tap of dispensing system for dispensing carbon dioxide (CO2) containing beverages such as sparkling wine, has dispensing line insert that has elongated, cylindrical or conical basic shape.
AU 
AE CARBOTEK SYSTEMS GMBH (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 2021628876
AB    NOVELTY - The helical compensator comprises a dispensing line insert which has an elongated, cylindrical or conical basic shape, which is encircled by a helically coiled screw thread so that the serving line insert in a tap line. The tap line is extended in a helical manner and tapered in cross section to the cross section of the coiled screw thread. The tap line insert has a buffer section with the circumferential screw thread and a laminarization section which is located downstream of the buffer section in the inserted state and which has a number of longitudinal grooves distributed over the circumference into which the screw thread opens on the outlet side and which, when inserted, lead into a section of the tap line located downstream of the tap line insert.
   USE - Helix compensator for use in a compensator tap of a dispensing system (all claimed) for dispensing CO2 containing beverages such as sparkling wine, lemonades on premix basis, juices, cider, beer or alcoholic mixed drinks (vodka-soda or highball whiskeys).
   ADVANTAGE - The helical compensator enables the tapping of CO2-containing beverage at a constant speed, without the adjustable dispensing speed with the compensator taps. Overall, this not only results in a cost-effective construction of the helix compensator, but also an arrangement of the length of the cross-sectional constriction required for this in a small space, which is particularly beneficial for table-top dispensing devices, in which all components of the dispensing system from the pump to the tap are in a common, compact and portable housing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a compensator tap with a throttle body which is integrated in a supply line, with which the flow rate of the beverage can be regulated;
   (2) a stout tap with a tap spout that is screwed on or otherwise fastened on the outlet side; and
   (3) a dispensing system with inline gassing for dispensing foam-forming, especially CO2 containing beverages.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a stout tap with a helix compensator outlet nozzle.

   USE - The method is useful for: improving the transfer of cannabidiol across a cellular membrane (claimed); treating a patient's body and its respective tissues for variety of medical treatments using microbubbles (OMB's) enhanced with cannabidiol (CBD); improving diagnosis and/or treating tissues of the human body and/or other animals; treating internal and/or external tissue surfaces and other wounds including tissue damage resulting from and/or experiencing delayed healing due to ischemic conditions; treating wounds and tissue injuries including skin ulcers and/or other types of damaged skin or other tissue surfaces and/or subsurface structures; delivering both CBD and oxygen to tissue; treating various forms of inflammation; formulating and utilizing a dual CBD-OMB emulsion; treating external skin surfaces and/or internal organs of the body e.g. the inner ears, lungs, nasal cavities, mouth, digestive tract and/or excretory tract of a patient; and treating soft tissue structures and/or other organs including organs/structures within the body e.g. the lungs, heart, heart tissue grafts and/or heart transplants and/or structures within the body and/or digestive tracts e.g. internal stomach surfaces (i.e. stomach ulcers), intestinal lesions, hemorrhoids, and/or ulcers of the small and large intestines.

TI Inducing culture medium of stem cell            differentiation to sweat gland-like cells, comprises            inducing culture medium is Dulbecco's modified eagle            medium/nutrient mixture F-12 cell culture medium            comprising e.g. fetal calf serum and L-glutamine.
AU WU X
   MEI Q
AE FUZHOU DERMATOSIS PREVENTION HOSPITAL (FUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202148187T
AB    NOVELTY - Inducing culture medium of stem cell                differentiation to sweat gland-like cells,                comprises inducing culture medium is Dulbecco's                Modified Eagle medium/nutrient Mixture F-12                (DMEM/F-12) cell culture medium, comprising 3-8%                fetal calf serum, 40-60 ng/ml epidermal growth                factor, 40-60 ng/ml basic fibroblast growth factor,                10-30 ng/ml recombinant human embryonic layer                dysplasias A1 protein, 10-30 ng/ml recombinant                human Wnt3aprotein, 10-30 ng/ml bone morphogenetic                protein 4, 20-30 ng/ml liver cell growth factor,                1-3 ng/ml triiodothyronine, 0.1-0.2 g/ml                hydrocortisone hemisuccinate, 0.5-1.5%                insulin-transferrin-sodium selenite, 0.5-1.5 mol/ml                L-glutamine, 80-120 U/ml penicillin and                streptomycin.
   USE - Used as inducing culture medium of stem cell                differentiation to sweat gland-like cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The medium solves requirement of sweat gland                in large area burn patient treatment process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                inducing stem cells to differentiate into sweat                gland-like cells, comprising (i) obtaining primary                cells of stem cells; (ii) inoculating 5x 104 stem                cells/well in a 6-well cell culture plate, adding                0.5-1 mL DMEM/F-12 medium containing 10% fetal                bovine serum to the culture plate in advance, and                placing the culture plate on the cells Incubate at                37 degrees C, 5% volume carbon-dioxide and                saturated humidity; (iii) adding the induction                medium after the stem cells adhere to the wall, and                replacing the induction medium every day; and (iv)                growing the stem cells to be induced to 80%                confluence rate, aspirating the culture medium,                washing with phosphate buffer, digesting the cells                with 1 ml 0.25% pancreatin for 2 minutes and using                serum-containing medium to stop the trypsin,                aspirating the digested cells into a centrifuge                tube and centrifuging under 1200 revolutions/minute                for 5 minutes, discarding the supernatant, adding                the induction medium, mixing the cell pellet, and                inoculating the cells according to the ratio of 1-3                to a new culture In the dish S5, repeating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The culture medium                  comprises 5% fetal calf serum, 50 ng/ml epidermal                  growth factor, 50 ng/ml basic fibroblast growth                  factor, 20 ng/ml recombinant human embryonic                  layer dysplasias A1 protein, 20 ng/ml recombinant                  human Wnt3aprotein, 20 ng/ml bone morphogenetic                  protein 4, 25 ng/ml liver cell growth factor, 2                  ng/ml triiodothyronine, 0.15 g/ml hydrocortisone                  hemisuccinate, 1% insulin-transferrin-sodium                  selenite, 1 mol/ml L-glutamine, 100 U/ml                  penicillin and streptomycin. Preferred                  Components: The ratio of penicillin to                  streptomycin is 1:2-2:1. The stem cells are                  mesenchymal stem cells. The mesenchymal stem                  cells are mesenchymal stem cells derived from                  human umbilical cord. In step (2), the ratio of                  penicillin and streptomycin is 1:2-2:1. Preferred                  Method: The steps of obtaining the primary cells                  of the mesenchymal stem cells derived from the                  human umbilical cord comprises: (1) immersing in                  0.9% sterilized normal saline after obtaining the                  human umbilical cord; (2) washing 3 times with                  phosphate buffered saline containing 1%                  penicillin and streptomycin, removing blood                  stains and thoroughly cleaning the umbilical vein                  lumen, peeling off the arteries and veins, taking                  the connective tissue of the umbilical cord, and                  cutting into tissue blocks of 4-5 mm3 in size;                  (3) inoculating the tissue block in a 6-well cell                  culture plate, adding 0.5-1 ml DMEM/F-12 medium                  containing 10% fetal bovine serum to the culture                  plate in advance, and placing the culture plate                  in a cell culture incubator at 37 degrees C, 5%                  volume carbon-dioxide, saturating humidity                  culture; (4) changing the medium for the first                  time after 24 hours of cell culture, and changing                  the medium every 2-3 days after 12-14 days of                  culture, growing out the primary cells after the                  primary cells are 60% full, and removing the                  tissue mass to obtain the primary cells. In the                  step (i), obtaining the primary cells further                  comprises performing an immunophenotypic                  lipogenic bone formation test on the obtained                  primary cells to verify that the primary cells                  have the characteristics of mesenchymal stem                  cells.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN DE102019128868-A1; US2021122433-A1; CN112706610-A
TI Active device for changing aerodynamic properties            of vehicle, has air flap that is pivotably mounted on            device frame, and electric motor which is set to cause            pivoting movement of air flap and designed as direct            drive for air flap.
AU SOLAZZO D
   SCHNEIDER J
AE ROECHLING AUTOMOTIVE SE & CO KG (ROHA-C)
   ROECHLING AUTOMOTIVE SE & CO AG (ROHA-C)
GA 202142378J
AB    NOVELTY - The active device has an air flap (26la) that                is pivotably mounted on a device frame (22). An                electric motor (30l,30r) causes a pivoting movement                of the air flap. The electric motor is designed as                a direct drive for the air flap. The electric motor                is set up to perform mechanical work in a form of a                rotary movement about a motor axis of rotation. The                electric motor is arranged on the device frame such                that a motor axis of rotation is formed collinear                with a pivot axis (Dla,Dra) of the air flap. The                pivot axis of the air flap is defined by a shaft                section of the air flap. The electric motor is a                bearing of the shaft section. A connection between                a rotor of the electric motor and the air flap set                up to transmit the rotary movement is formed by an                injection molding process. The electric motor                comprises an internal rotation section and an                external rotation section, which are provided                rotatable relative to each other.
   USE - Active device for changing aerodynamic                properties of vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - To reduce the carbon dioxide emission from                vehicle, the efficiency of the vehicle drive is                increased and the aerodynamic drag is reduced by                adapting the aerodynamic properties of the vehicle                by pivotably mounted and actuator-driven air flaps                in certain operating states of the vehicle by                giving the closed air flaps an essentially flat                face train compared to other operating states in                which the air flaps are open. By designing the                electric motor as the direct drive, the need for                frictional gears and linkages is avoided. The                device due to the smaller number of components is                simplified. The active device is less error-prone                and more cost-effective.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show the schematic views of the                active device with two drive air flaps and                associated air flaps in the open position and                closed position.
   Air damper device (20)
   Device frame (22)
   Air flap (26la)
   Electric motors (30l,30r)
   Pivot axes (Dla,Dra)
DC Q17 (Vehicle construction, fittings, propulsion arrangements (B60J-K, B60R, B60V-W)); X22 (Automotive Electrics)
