PT P
PN US2021032399-A1; US11279793-B2
TI Material used in composite system for self-healing            roads, comprises polyurea prepolymer layer, preferably            aliphatic polyurea moieties coated on surface of            pre-curing or tacky epoxy to form interfacial            epoxy-polyurea hybridized-matrix.
AU ATTARD T
AE ATTARD T (ATTA-Individual)
GA 202116469F
AB    NOVELTY - Material comprises a layer of polyurea                prepolymer (moieties), preferably aliphatic                polyurea moieties coated on an exposed surface of                pre-curing (tc=0), curing (0.5less than or equal                totcless than or equal to1.5), or tacky (1.5less                than or equal totcless than or equal to24) epoxy,                forming an interfacial epoxy-polyurea                hybridized-matrix (IEPM) material.
   USE - The material is useful in a composite system,                which is useful for self-healing roads, oil and gas                industries for chemical tanker protection against                terrorist attacks, dry cask storage tanks for                surface ofnuclear storage, wind turbine blade                manufacturing, sports equipment such as helmets,                chest protectors and race car components (NASCAR),                and biomedical applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has improved sustainability and                energy transferability to conventional                high-strength fiber technology, has fire-resistant                properties, prevents damage, dissipates incoming                earthquake and debris-impact energy, lowers carbon                footprint by lowering carbon dioxide consumption,                has vibration suppression, high-impact resistance                and high damage-tolerance properties, and                incorporates tremendous impact resistance including                damage mitigation and structural resiliency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a composite system comprising a substrate,                a fiber (x) reinforced polymer layer (x-FRP), which                may be a fiber (x) reinforced epoxy (x-F/E) layer,                attached to at least a portion of the substrate                IEPM material produced via IDA surface modification                attached to at least a portion of the substrate or                attached to at least a portion of the x-FRP layer                or to at least a portion of the x-F/E layer to                produce an x-IEPM-tc composite; and
   (2) a method for strengthening or reinforcing                a substrate involving forming the fiber (x)                reinforced polymer (x-FRP) layer, which may be a                fiber (x) reinforced epoxy (x-F/E) layer, on at                least a portion of the substrate, applying a                pre-curing, curing (wet), or tacky (sticky) epoxy                to the x-FRP or x-F/E, or to the substrate where                the surface is not cured, and spraying polyurea                moieties over the not cured epoxy surface to obtain                the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The IEPM material is                  formed by surface modification of the reactive                  epoxy. The production of the IEPM material                  reduces carbon dioxide emissions by utilizing                  carbon feedstocks to produce the isophorone                  diisocyanate used in an isophorone diisocyanate                  amine (IDA) modification.Preferred Components: The IEPM material                  comprises a material formed by surface                  modification of the reactive epoxy, where                  reaction of isophorone diisocyanate and                  polyoxypropylene diamine produces a latent                  amine-rich urea molecule with isocyanate                  functional groups that reacts with amine                  (hardener) functional groups from pre-curing,                  curing, or tacky epoxy. The polyurea comprises an                  aliphatic prepolymer (or pre-polymerized)                  polyurea. The polyurea layer comprises a                  fire-resistant intumescent polyurea layer. The                  polyurea layer comprises at least one of a                  fast-curing or a slow-curing prepolymer                  (pre-polymerized) polyurea. The epoxy comprises a                  low-viscosity epoxy resin. The IEPM material                  comprises a molecularly resilient, or                  regenerative, material using lower values of tc,                  low-viscosity epoxy resin, and aliphatic polyurea                  moieties. The IEPM material comprises tremendous                  loss modulus, fracture toughness, and reduced                  elastic modulus properties that are especially                  large at tc=0. The IEPM comprises an x-IEPM-tc                  composite, where the composite may include a                  fiber (x) reinforced epoxy (x-F/E) on one side of                  the IEPM material, and a layer of fully cured                  polyurea on the other side of the IEPM material.                  The fiber (x) reinforced epoxy layer comprises a                  carbon-fiber reinforced epoxy layer                  (C-F/E).Preferred Properties: The molecular                  vibrational properties of the IEPM material are                  controlled by parameter tc, where tc controls the                  thermodynamic state of the pre-curing (just-mixed                  or nearly just-mixed), curing (wet), or tacky                  (sticky to the touch) epoxy before the epoxy                  reacts with the the (aliphatic) polyurea                  moieties. The IEPM material provides moisture                  resistance, which is largest if the IEPM is                  produced using tc=0, aliphatic polyurea moieties,                  and a low-viscosity epoxy resin, and thermal                  insulative properties of greatest value if the                  IEPM is produced using tc=0, aliphatic polyurea                  moieties, and a low-viscosity epoxy resin. The                  bulk material properties of the IEPM material                  introduce tremendous energy transferability. The                  topography of the IEPM material produced using                  tc=0, low-viscosity epoxy resin, and aliphatic                  polyurea moieties is likened to that of a                  beach-pebble topography that indicates the                  ability of the IEPM material to safely transfer                  large influxes of energy arising from extreme                  natural and human-caused forces. The IEPM                  material has a width of 2-50 mum.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AU OKADA H
AE KATERA CO LTD (KATE-Non-standard)
GA 2021083847

The transparent sensor polymer structure is formed with the measurement light-emitting structure (19) formed from the resin material, to emit light under the excited state, and to change emitted light intensity according to gas concentration. PN CN112011376-A; CN213202932-U
TI Household gas producing device for light hydrocarbon oil and gas, includes programmable logic controller automatic control cabinet, light hydrocarbon oil and gas household gas producing device body, bubbling gas tank, and oil storage tank.
AU WANG Y
AE WANG Y (WANG-Individual)
GA 2020C1991P
AB    NOVELTY - Household gas producing device comprises programmable logic controller (PLC) automatic control cabinet (1), light hydrocarbon oil and gas household gas producing device body (2), light hydrocarbon oil and gas household gas producing tank (3), gas appliance I (4), light hydrocarbon oil gas household gas tank barometer (5), bubbling gas tank (6), gas appliance I switch valve (7), gas supply pipeline for gas appliance I (8), gas meter for gas appliance I (9), pressure reducing valve for gas appliance I (10), connecting pipeline between light hydrocarbon oil and gas household gas tank and light hydrocarbon oil and gas tank, light hydrocarbon oil and gas tank, gas appliance II gas meter, and the control valve of pipeline between the oil storage tank (23) and the bubbling gas tank. The ratio of the area of the pores to the area of the pipeline between the oil storage tank and the bubbling gas tank is 1:1.
   USE - Household gas producing device for light hydrocarbon oil and gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The household gas producing device has 100% gasification rate without addition of additives, flame retardants and other chemical raw materials. No residual raw materials, and no harmful substances are generated by this device. After the mixed gas of light hydrocarbon gas and air is burnt, only carbon dioxide and water are produced, without any pollution to the ambient atmosphere. This device not only saves the cost of use, and improves economic efficiency, but also greatly improves the safety of the reaction process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic diagram of household gas producing device.
   Light hydrocarbon oil and gas household gas producing device body (2)
   Light hydrocarbon oil and gas household gas producing tank (3)
   Gas appliance I (4)
   Light hydrocarbon oil gas household gas tank barometer (5)
   Bubbling gas tank (6)
   Gas appliance I switch valve (7)
   Gas supply pipeline for gas appliance I (8)
   Gas meter for gas appliance I (9)
   Pressure reducing valve for gas appliance I (10)
   Oil storage tank (23)

Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); T01 (Digital Computers)

PT P
PN WO2020216946-A1; EP3730673-A1; EP3959359-A1; US2022228274-A1
TI System for electrochemically reducing carbon monoxide into liquid methanol and gaseous hydrogen, comprises electrochemical cell comprising cathode is electrochemically inert, comprising cobalt phthalocyanine compound, on current collector.
AU ROBERT M
   BOUTIN E
AE UNIV PARIS (UYPA-Non-standard)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV PARIS DIDEROT PARIS 7 (PADI-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV PARIS (UYPA-Non-standard)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV PARIS (UYPA-Non-standard)
GA 2020A59419
AB    NOVELTY - An electrochemical system comprises an electrochemical cell comprising an anodic compartment (i) on the current collector, comprising an anode with a current collector, at least a catalyst to electrochemically oxidize water to oxygen, an anodic electrolyte solution comprising a solvent and an anodic supporting electrolyte, a cathodic compartment (ii) comprising a cathodic electrolyte solution comprising the solvent and a cathodic supporting electrolyte, the reagent carbon monoxide, a cathode comprising at least a molecular catalyst on the current collector and which is electrochemically inert, and a power supply (iii) providing the energy necessary to trigger the electrochemical reactions involving the reagent. The solvent is water having a pH of 10.5-13.5. The molecular catalyst comprises cobalt phthalocyanine compound (I) and/or (II).
   USE - System used for electrochemically reducing carbon monoxide into liquid methanol and gaseous hydrogen from fossil fuels to renewable fuels.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrochemical system has excellent stability and efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An electrochemical system comprises an electrochemical cell comprising an anodic compartment (i) on the current collector, comprising an anode with a current collector, at least a catalyst to electrochemically oxidize water to oxygen, an anodic electrolyte solution comprising a solvent and an anodic supporting electrolyte, a cathodic compartment (ii) comprising a cathodic electrolyte solution comprising the solvent and a cathodic supporting electrolyte, the reagent carbon monoxide, a cathode comprising at least a molecular catalyst on the current collector and which is electrochemically inert, and a power supply (iii) providing the energy necessary to trigger the electrochemical reactions involving the reagent. The solvent is water having a pH of 10.5-13.5. The molecular catalyst comprises cobalt phthalocyanine compound of formula (I) and/or (II).
   R1-R16 = H, F, or N+(1-4C alkyl)3, in which at least one of R1, R4, R5, R8, R9, R12, R13, or R16 is phenyl bearing N+(1-4C alkyl)3 substituent in ortho 5 position, the others R1 is H.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an electrochemical device, which comprises an electrochemical system (A) to electrochemically reduce carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide with a molecular catalyst, and a electrochemical system (B) to electrochemically reduce carbon monoxide into methanol and gaseous hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrochemical system is a flow cell to electrochemically reduce a gas reactant comprising carbon monoxide passing through a gas diffusion electrode into methanol and comprises an anodic compartment comprising an anodic electrolyte solution, at a controlled flow rate (Qa) comprising a solvent and an anodic electrolyte and an anodic electrolyte solution inlet and an anodic electrolyte solution outlet connected to the anodic compartment, to circulate the anodic electrolyte solution, cathodic compartment comprising a cathodic electrolyte solution, at a controlled flow rate (Qc) comprising a solvent and a cathodic electrolyte, a cathodic electrolyte solution inlet and a cathodic electrolyte solution outlet connected to the cathodic compartment, to circulate the cathodic electrolyte solution and the remaining reagent gas and the methanol by the outlet, a gas diffusion electrode comprising on an electrochemically inert gas diffusion porous current collector of surface, at least the molecular catalyst to electrochemically reduce the gas reactant comprising carbon monoxide into methanol in the cathodic electrolyte solution, with by-production of gaseous hydrogen, an anion exchange membrane impermeable to carbon monoxide, hydrogen and oxygen, between the anodic compartment and the cathodic compartment, a channel for passing the reagent gas, at a controlled flow rate (Qg), through the porous surface of the gas diffusion electrode, while the catholyte electrolyte solution circulates in between the gas diffusion electrode and the anion exchange membrane, and pumping unit serves to circulate by pumping the anodic electrolyte solution in the anodic compartment and the cathodic electrolyte solution in the cathodic compartment between the inlet and the outlet and control flow by pumping the comprising gas carbon monoxide in the channel, passing through the gas diffusion porous cathode. The cathode comprises on the current collector, an electrode film deposit containing the molecular catalyst, at least a binder, and optionally conductive materials as additives. The cathodic current collector is carbon paper. The conductive material (additives) comprises carbon nanotubes, or carbon powder, or reduced graphene oxide or graphite powder. Preferred Property: The molecular catalyst in the electrode film deposit has concentration of 1 nmol.cm-2-100 mu mol.cm-2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound (I) is preferably of formula (IA). Preferred Property: The solvent has a pH of 12-13.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The binder is Nafion (RTM: sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene-based fluoropolymer-copolymer) resin.

PT P
PN US2020330132-A1
TI System for correction of spinal deformities, such as scoliosis, includes two or more bone anchor implants, each having groove or channel adapted to allow unrestricted blood flow through vertebral vessels.
AU GREWAL H
AE GREWAL H (GREW-Individual)
GA 2020A2874C
AB    NOVELTY - The system includes two or more bone anchor implants (10) and a tether (20) for connecting the bone anchor implants to one another. Each bone anchor implant has at least two tines (12), each tine having barbs on at least one surface to engage the bone of a vertebral body. Each bone anchor implant has at least two recesses adapted to accept screws for securing the implant to a vertebral body. Each bone anchor implant further has a groove or channel adapted to allow unrestricted blood flow through the vertebral vessels. Each implant further has a groove for accepting the tether, the groove having a locking device for securing the tether to the implant.
   USE - System for correction of spinal deformities, such as scoliosis.
   ADVANTAGE - The size and shape of the tether and bone anchoring implants as well as the inserting device allow implantation using a minimally invasive thoracoscopic and fluoroscopic technique utilizing valved trocars that allow carbon dioxide insufflation thus avoiding large incisions and improving visualization of the spine and reducing the time taken for the operative procedure. The system allows for the correction of curvature of the spine by implanting bone anchors in multiple adjacent vertebral bodies of the lateral aspect of the convex side of the deformed spine that then are attached to a tether or tethers thus tethering the convex aspect of the spine thus allowing the contralateral untethered concave aspect of the spine to grow faster and thus correct the deformity by growth modulation. The system does not require the coagulation, interruption, mobilization or damage to this segmental blood supply, thus additionally making the minimally invasive method less complex, safer and with less risk of bleeding.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a tool for the emplacement of a bone anchor implant having tines.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an exploded perspective view showing an anchor having four tines for engaging the vertebral body, as well as a locking collar and set-screw for securing a tether or cable to the anchor.
   Bone anchor implant (10)
   Tines (12)
   Tether (20)
   Locking cap (30)
   Set-screw (40)

PT P
PN CN111760221-A; CN111760221-B
TI Air isolating function-featured power distribution            cabinet inflaming retarding device, has arc heat            transfer plate for transferring heat to air storage            film such that conical air guide pipe pierces air            storage film to make air overflow.
AU WU B
AE WU B (WUBB-Individual)
   SHANDONG JINGHAN ELECTRIC POWER ENG CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020A20626
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an umbrella-shaped plate (3)                whose lower end is fixedly connected with a storage                bin that is connected with an air isolation                ring-shaped device. The umbrella-shaped plate is                formed with an injection groove. A flame retardant                device is connected with the injection groove. A                lower end of a heat transfer plate (5) is fixedly                connected with an air ejection device that is                hinged with the flame retardant device. An upper                inner wall of a power distribution cabinet (1) is                fixedly connected with a fire alarm (10), where a                corrugated flame-retardant air bag is made of                semi-transparent rubber capsule material, a                compression film is made of elastic rubber material                and a storage ball is made of hotmelt adhesive                material. The heat transfer plate transfers heat to                an air storage film such that a conical air guide                pipe pierces the air storage film to make air                overflow instantly.
   USE - Air isolating function-featured power                distribution cabinet inflaming retarding                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device utilizes a fire alarm to initiate                an alarm when the power distribution cabinet                encounters water or overheating, transfers heat                through the thermally conductive connecting block                and realizes overheating of the multi-layer heat                transfer board to perform decomposition of sodium                azide to produce nitrogen expansion, so that the                corrugated flame-retardant airbag can be stretched                downwards to isolate outer side air, thus                preventing combustion, and hence extruding a                fine-pored silica gel in the compressed film to                absorb the water around the distribution cabinet.                The device prevents a spread of water, reduces a                risk of electric shock caused by the water,                utilizes the arc-shaped heat transfer plate to                transfer heat to the air storage film and connects                a storage ball with the power distribution cabinet                for releasing a dry ice to sublimate and absorb                heat as non-flammable carbon dioxide gas for flame                retardant.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front-sectional view of                an air isolating function-featured power                distribution cabinet inflaming retarding                device.
   Power distribution cabinet (1)
   Heat-conducting connecting block (2)
   Umbrella-shaped plate (3)
   Heat transfer plate (5)
   Fire alarm (10)

PT P
PN WO2020191393-A1; CN113748166-A; EP3941976-A1; US2022112364-A1
TI Thermoplastic vulcanizate composition used to            dynamically vulcanized thermoplastic vulcanizate pipe            used in off-shore application or on-shore application,            comprises rubber, thermoplastic polyolefin, and            polyhedral oligomeric silsesquioxane.
AU ANANTHA NARAYANA IYER K
   DOUFAS A K
   DIAS A J
   ANANTHA N I K
AE EXXONMOBIL CHEM PATENTS INC (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL CHEM PATENTS INC (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL CHEM PATENTS INC (ESSO-C)
GA 202093780E
AB    NOVELTY - Thermoplastic vulcanizate (TPV) composition,                comprises a rubber, a thermoplastic polyolefin, and                a polyhedral oligomeric silsesquioxane, where a                concentration of the rubber is 10-80 wt.% based on                a combined weight of the rubber and the                thermoplastic polyolefin; a concentration of the                thermoplastic polyolefin is 20-90 wt% based on the                combined weight of the rubber and the thermoplastic                polyolefin; and a concentration of the polyhedral                oligomeric silsesquioxane is 0.1-20 wt.% based on                the total weight of the TPV composition.
   USE - The composition is useful for preparing                dynamically vulcanized thermoplastic vulcanizate                for pipe used in off-shore application or on-shore                application (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: provides dynamically                vulcanized thermoplastic vulcanizate; provides pipe                is flexible or rigid; and has carbon dioxide                permeability at 60 degrees C of more than 40                barrers as measured according to ISO 2782-1,                abrasion loss of less than or equal to 120 mg/1000                cycle as measured according to ASTM D4060, tensile                strength at yield of greater than or equal to 9                MPa, at 23 degrees C as measured according to ISO                37 on a compression molded plaque, tensile strain                at yield of greater than or equal to 7% at 23                degrees C as measured according to ISO 37 on a                compression molded plaque, thermal conductivity of                less than or equal to 0.25 W/m-K as measured                according to ASTM C518-17, and hardness of 70 Shore                A to 60 Shore D, where Shore A hardness and Shore D                harness is measured using a Zwick automated                durometer according to ASTM D2240 (all claimed),                and improves abrasion resistance, low temperature                properties, particularly flexibility and                strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing a dynamically vulcanized                thermoplastic vulcanizate composition, comprising                melt processing under shear conditions at least                one: thermoplastic resin, rubber, curing agent, and                polyhedral oligomeric silsesquioxane and forming a                dynamically vulcanized thermoplastic vulcanizate                composition;
   (2) pipe (P1) comprising an outer sheath that                includes the TPV composition;
   (3) pipe (P2) comprising an intermediate                sheath that comprises the TPV composition;                and
   (4) pipe (P3) comprising a thermal insulation                layer that comprises the TPV composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The composition                  further comprises plasticizer, filler, slip                  agent, or nucleating agent and a cure system.                  Preferred Components: The concentration of the                  rubber is 10-30 wt.%, based on the combined                  weight of the rubber and the thermoplastic                  polyolefin, and the concentration of the                  thermoplastic polyolefin is 25-75 wt.% based on                  the combined weight of the rubber and the                  thermoplastic polyolefin. The polyhedral                  oligomeric silsesquioxane moiety of formula                  ((R1SiOa)x) (I).x = 4-15;R1 = organic substituents comprising H,                  siloxy, cyclic aliphatic, linear aliphatic, or                  aromatic; anda = 1.5The polyhedral oligomeric silsesquioxane is                  octamethyl polyoligosilsesquioxanes of                  (((CH3SiOa)8)) (II). The polyhedral oligomeric                  silsesquioxane is octaisobutyl                  polyoligosilsesquioxanes (((CH3)2CHCH2SiOa)8)))                  (III). The plasticizer comprises mineral oil,                  paraffinic oil, polyisobutylene, synthetic oil,                  triisononyl trimellitate, and/or low molecular                  weight alkyl ester. The filler comprises calcium                  carbonate, clay, silica, talc, titanium dioxide,                  carbon black, mica, and/or wood flour. The cure                  system comprises phenolic resin, peroxide,                  maleimide, hexamethylene diamine carbamate,                  silicon-based curative, silane-based curative,                  metal oxide, and/or sulfur-based curative. The                  cure system comprises hydrosilylation curative or                  a phenolic resin curative. The rubber is an                  ethylene propylene rubber, nitrile rubber, butyl                  rubber, and/or halobutyl rubber. The ethylene                  propylene rubber is an ethylene propylene diene                  monomer rubber. The ethylene propylene diene                  monomer rubber comprises a diene component that                  includes ethylidene norbomene, and/or vinyl                  norbornene. The butyl rubber comprises                  isobutylene-isoprene rubber, brominated                  isobutylene-isoprene rubber, chlorinated                  isobutylene-isoprene rubber, or isobutylene                  paramethyl styrene rubber. The butyl rubber is an                  isobutylene-paramethylstyrene rubber comprising                  0.5-25 wt.% paramethylstyrene based on an entire                  weight of the rubber. The butyl rubber is an                  isobutylene-isoprene rubber comprising 0.5-30                  wt.% isoprene based on an entire weight of the                  rubber. The butyl rubber is a brominated                  isobutylene-isoprene rubber and/or chlorinated                  isobutylene-isoprene rubber, comprising 0.3-7                  wt.% halogenation, based on an entire weight of                  the rubber. The rubber is a nitrile rubber                  comprising 1,3 -butadiene or isoprene and                  acrylonitrile. The nitrile rubber comprises 20-50                  wt.% acrylonitrile-derived content. The                  thermoplastic polyolefin comprises polypropylene,                  polyethylene, and/or polybutene-1. The outer                  sheath has a thickness of 2-30 mm. The pipe (P1)                  further comprises an inner housing and at least                  one reinforcing layer at least partially disposed                  around the inner housing. The intermediate sheath                  has a thickness of 1-10 mm. The pipe (P2) further                  comprises the inner housing, the reinforcing                  layer at least partially disposed around the                  inner housing and an outer protective sheath at                  least partially disposed around the at least one                  reinforcing layer. The thermal insulation layer                  has a thickness of 2-30 mm. The thermal                  insulation layer is applied as layers wound from                  at least one tape. The pipe (P3) further                  comprises the inner housing, reinforcing layer at                  least partially disposed around the inner housing                  and the outer protective sheath.
   EXAMPLE - Typical thermoplastic vulcanizate composition                comprised of: 175 parts per hundred rubber (phr)                ethylene propylene diene monomer; 429 phr                polypropylene homopolymer; 22 phr slip agent; 42                phr clay; 2 phr zinc oxide; 49.99 phr oil; 1.67 phr                tin(II) chloride; 12.82 phr phenolic resin in oil;                36.72 phr octamethyl polyoligosilsesquioxanes; and                124.99 phr total phr oil.

PN CN111392865-A
TI Processing of organic amine wastewater involves            adding wastewater to regulating tank, removing            impurities, denitrifying, performing anaerobic ammonia            oxidation, degrading ammoniacal nitrogen in resultant,            recycling and separating.
AU XIE W
   ZHOU J
   SHAO J
   ZHANG P
   WU J
   LIN B
   HU H
AE ZHEJIANG DERWAY ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020667193
AB    NOVELTY - Processing of organic amine wastewater                involves adding organic amine wastewater to a                regulating tank, removing impurities, adding                resultant wastewater to anaerobic reactor through a                lifting pump, overflowing resultant wastewater to                short-range denitrification/anaerobic ammonia                reactor, denitrifying bacteria in the short-range                denitrification/anaerobic ammonia reactor using                nitrate ion as electron acceptor, and biochemical                oxygen demand, carrying out anaerobic ammonia                oxidation using ammoniacal nitrogen and nitrogen                dioxide, degrading ammoniacal nitrogen, adding                resultant wastewater to aerobic reactor, converting                ammoniacal nitrogen in wastewater into nitrate ion,                recycling portion of the mixed liquid in the                aerobic reactor to short-range                denitrification/anaerobic ammonia reactor through                reflux pump as electron acceptor for                denitrification reaction, adding resultant                wastewater to sedimentation tank for mud-water                separation to clarify the mixed liquid, and                discharging.
   USE - Processing of organic amine wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical processing of                organic amine wastewater by complete ammonia                oxidation and short-range denitrification process,                and reducing imbalance of carbon-nitrogen ratio in                organic amine wastewater treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing of organic amine wastewater                involves adding organic amine wastewater to a                regulating tank, removing impurities, adding                resultant wastewater to anaerobic reactor through a                lifting pump, in which anaerobic bacteria in the                anaerobic reactor decompose organic amines, and                organic amine undergoes amination reaction to                produce small molecular acid and inorganic ammonia,                overflowing resultant wastewater to short-range                denitrification/anaerobic ammonia reactor,                denitrifying bacteria in the short-range                denitrification/anaerobic ammonia reactor using                nitrate ion as electron acceptor, and biochemical                oxygen demand as electron donor to produce nitrogen                dioxide and carbon dioxide, carrying out anaerobic                ammonia oxidation using ammoniacal nitrogen                produced in anaerobic reactor as electron donor,                and nitrogen dioxide as electron acceptor,                degrading ammoniacal nitrogen, adding resultant                wastewater to aerobic reactor, in which                heterotrophic microorganism-autotrophic                microorganism synergistic symbiosis system in the                aerobic reactor deeply removes biochemical oxygen                demand in wastewater, converting ammoniacal                nitrogen in wastewater into nitrate ion, recycling                portion of the mixed liquid in the aerobic reactor                to short-range denitrification/anaerobic ammonia                reactor through reflux pump as electron acceptor                for denitrification reaction, adding resultant                wastewater to sedimentation tank for mud-water                separation to clarify the mixed liquid, and                discharging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The processing method                  involves adding alkali liquid for stabilizing the                  reaction system pH to aerobic reactor. Preferred                  Composition: In the anaerobic reactor, the sludge                  concentration is 1000-20000 mg/L, and chemical                  oxygen demand volume is 0.3-5 kg/(m3.d). In                  short-range nitrification/anaerobic ammonia                  oxidation reactor, the sludge concentration is                  1000-20000 mg/L, dissolved oxygen concentration                  is 0-2 mg/L, chemical oxygen demand is 0.1-5                  kg/(m3.d), and total nitrogen content is 0.1-5                  kg/(m3.d). In the aerobic reactor, the sludge                  concentration is 10-20000 mg/L, dissolved oxygen                  concentration is 0.2-9 mg/L, chemical oxygen                  demand is 0.1-5 kg/(m3.day/), and ammoniacal                  nitrogen content is 0.1-5 kg/(m3.d). The alkali                  liquid is aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide,                  potassium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, sodium                  carbonate, potassium carbonate, calcium                  carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium                  bicarbonate and/or calcium bicarbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The organic amine                  wastewater includes ammonia-containing organic or                  inorganic substance(s), in which organic                  substance mainly includes methylamine,                  dimethylamine, trimethylamine, ethylamine,                  diethylamine, ethylenediamine, triethylamine,                  hydrazine, aniline, N,N-dimethylformamide,                  N,N-dimethylacetamide, piperazine, pyridine,                  imidazole, pyrimidine, urea,                  N,N-dimethylnitrosamine, N,N-diethylnitrosamine,                  amino acids, and their hydrochlorides, sulfate                  and nitrate, and inorganic ammonia mainly                  includes ammonia, aqueous ammonia, ammonium                  chloride, ammonium sulfate, ammonium nitrate and                  ammonium carbonate.

An air outlet pipe is fixedly set on the air pump. One end of the air outlet pipe is fixedly connected on the purifying device. A lift door is movably connected on the front part of the car body.
   USE - Used as an anti-suffocation car.

   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the anti-suffocation car.

PT P
PN CN111232991-A; CN111232991-B
TI Preparing silica aerogel spherical powder with special structure by forming silica sol, spraying silica sol to emulsion comprising silicone oil and polysorbate 80, adding ammonia, filtering, and treating with trimethylchlorosilane.
AU LI Z
   GE G
   WANG X
   ZHANG J
   LIU J
   WU D
AE TIANJIN NAKESHIJI NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
   TIANJIN NAKESHIJI NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020526681
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing silica aerogel spherical powder with special structure involves (a) forming uniform silica sol, (b) using silicone oil and Tween 80 (RTM: Polysorbate 80) to make an emulsion system, (c) spraying the silica sol into the emulsion system through an atomizer, fully dispersing the small droplets of silica sol in the oil phase to form silica-in-oil system, (d) adding aqueous ammonia to the silica-in-oil system, sealing and aging after stirring, (e) filtering and separating material generated in step (d), and cleaning the emulsion and impurities on the surface with n-hexane, (f) replacing the silica wet gel microspheres generated in step (e) with ethanol, (g) replacing hydrophobic modification of the silica wet gel microspheres with trimethylchlorosilane and absolute ethanol solution, (h) washing the silica wet gel microspheres after hydrophobic modification with absolute ethanol, adding them into a supercritical reaction kettle and drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing silica aerogel spherical powder with special structure (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing silica aerogel spherical powder with special structure involves (a) forming uniform silica sol, (b) using silicone oil and Tween 80 (RTM: Polysorbate 80) to make an emulsion system, (c) spraying the silica sol into the emulsion system through an atomizer, so that the small droplets of silica sol are fully dispersed in the oil phase to form a silica-in-oil system, (d) adding aqueous ammonia to the silica-in-oil system, sealing and aging after stirring, (e) filtering and separating the material generated in step (d) to obtain silica wet gel microspheres, and cleaning the emulsion and impurities on the surface with n-hexane, (f) replacing the silica wet gel microspheres generated in step (e) with ethanol, (g) replacing hydrophobic modification of the silica wet gel microspheres in step (f) with trimethylchlorosilane and absolute ethanol solution, and (h) washing the silica wet gel microspheres after hydrophobic modification in step (g) with absolute ethanol, and adding them into a supercritical reaction kettle to obtain silica aerogel microspheres after drying with supercritical carbon dioxide ball powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (i) modifying the surface of the silica aerogel microsphere powder produced in step (h) with 3-aminopropyltrihydroxysilane and anhydrous ethanol solution in a volume ratio of 3:10. In step (b), 500 ml silicone oil and 20 ml Tween 80 (RTM: Polysorbate 80) are used to make an emulsion system, and in step (d), 1 ml of aqueous ammonia is added to the silica-in-oil system. In step (c), the silica sol is sprayed into the emulsion system from the bottom of the container using an atomizer. Preferred Composition: In step (a), the molar ratio of ethyl orthosilicate, distilled water, absolute ethanol and nitric acid is 1:4:8:1.4x 10-2, and the step (a) involves evenly mixing the mixture and placing in a 60 degrees C water bath for 2 hours, and forming a uniform silica sol. In step (g), the silica wet gel microspheres replaced by the step (f) are made hydrophobic with trimethylchlorosilane and absolute ethanol solution in a volume ratio of 7:10.

PN EP3640015-A1; WO2020079064-A1; EP3640015-B1
TI Process for manufacturing rotationally symmetric plastic housing for medical device, involves introducing mandrel with shape matching inner contour of bore, into bore and machining outer surface of rod using computer numerical control lathe.
AU WAGNER S
   KLINK B
   WIEST J
   ESSE S
AE GAMBRO LUNDIA AB (GAMB-C)
   GAMBRO LUNDIA AB (GAMB-C)
GA 202031450Q
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves providing a massive cylindrical rod of a medical grade polymer. A rotationally symmetric bore (11) is produced along the longitudinal axis of the cylindrical rod. The bore extends from a first end of the cylindrical rod to a second opposite end of the cylindrical rod. The diameter of the bore decreases from the first end in the direction of the second end. A mandrel (20) having a shape matching the inner contour of the bore is introduced into the bore from the first end of the rod. A fixation cover (40) is mounted on the second end of the rod. The mandrel and the fixation cover are connected by a fastener (30) to press the rod onto the mandrel. An outer surface (12) of the rod is machined using a computer numerical control (CNC) lathe to produce a desired outer contour of a housing (10). The mandrel comprises of a disc-shaped base (21), a truncated cone (22) connected to the base and one threaded bore (23) in a face of the truncated cone opposite to the base.
   USE - Process for manufacturing rotationally symmetric plastic housing for medical device e.g. capillary dialyzer, a hemofilter, a plasma filter, an ultrafilter or a device for extracorporeal membrane oxygenation or a device for extracorporeal carbon dioxide removal (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The manufacturing process for medical device housings which uses medical grade polymers, yields housings with a high surface quality as required for medical devices and allows for lowering production cost even if only a small number of housings is produced. The vibrations induced by the cutting tool are minimized by stabilization result in a smoother surface of the final housing. Allows for more flexibility in designing the housing. Can use polymer materials that normally cannot be processed by injection molding.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic cross sectional view of a semi-finished housing and some of the components involved in the manufacturing process for medical device.
   Housing (10)
   Bore (11)
   Outer surface (12)
   Mandrel (20)
   Base (21)
   Truncated cone (22)
   Threaded bore (23)
   Fastener (30)
   Fixation cover (40)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - The medical grade polymer is selected from the group consisting of polycarbonate, polyethylene terephthalate, polymethylmethacrylate, and polypropylene. The medical grade polymer is also selected from the group consisting of polytetrafluoroethylene, silicone rubber, and polyurethane.

PN US10625102-B1
TI Gauze mask for separating inhalation and exhalation applied to nose and mouth of human face, has air valve fixed in through hole at top and bottom portions of inner and outer layer elements and inner mask element.
AU CHEN C
AE CHEN C (CHEN-Individual)
GA 202030299V
AB    NOVELTY - The gauze mask (1) has an inner mask element (13) that is placed at an inner side of an inner layer element (12), and configured to cover the mouth of human face while leaving the nose uncovered comprises a third through hole aligned with a first through hole at the bottom portion of an outer layer element (11) and a second through hole at the bottom portion of the inner layer element. The third front hole and connection portion (132) are aligned with a first front hole (112) of the outer layer element and a second front hole (122) of the inner layer element. The connection portion is protruded outward from the outer layer element. An air valve (2) is fixed in each through hole at the top portions of the inner and outer layer elements. An air valve is fixed in each through hole at the bottom portions of the inner and outer layer elements and the inner mask element. The values of the gauze mask allow separation of air inhaled from the nose and air exhaled from the nose and the mouth.
   USE - Gauze mask for separating inhalation and exhalation applied to nose and mouth of human face.
   ADVANTAGE - The improved gauze structure capable of separating inhalation and exhalation is provided with an air valve placed at top portion of an outer layer element, an inner layer element and an inner mask element. The gauze mask effectively increases the oxygen contained in the inhaled air, prevents the fresh air mixed with the exhaled air containing carbon dioxide from inhaled. The connection portion protruded out from the outer layer element of the inner mask element is provided with a cover element.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the assembly of gauze mask.

PN CN110975556-A
TI Advanced oxidation treatment method for wet and            dry coupling exhaust gas of aluminum veneer coating by            introducing paint mist, reacting ozone with water and            catalyzing ozone for advanced oxidation of volatile            organic compounds.
AU CHEN Q
   LU J
   HUANG Z
   LIU X
   LI D
   YAO Y
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 2020304846
AB    NOVELTY - Advanced oxidation treatment method for wet                and dry coupling exhaust gas of aluminum veneer                coating, involves (i) introducing paint mist                generated in spraying process and flue gas                generated in drying furnace to a wet scrubber tower                for removing paint residue particles and some                volatile organic compounds, introducing ozone from                the bottom of the scrubber tower, and reacting                partially dissolved ozone with water for absorbing                volatile organic compounds and promoting the                reunion of paint residue particles, and (ii)                transferring non-dissolved ozone and exhaust gas to                catalytic reactor, and catalyzing ozone for                advanced oxidation of volatile organic compounds                using transition metal-loaded activated carbon,                where the reaction products are carbon dioxide and                water.
   USE - Advanced oxidation treatment method for wet                and dry coupling exhaust gas of aluminum veneer                coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves oxidation reaction rate,                ozone utilization efficiency, oxidation reaction                rate and ozone utilization efficiency, has higher                catalytic activity, longer service life and stable                operation, ensures more than 98% removal rate of                volatile organic compounds, and is                environmentally-friendly and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                advanced oxidation treatment device for wet and dry                coupled exhaust gas of aluminum veneer coating has                an ozone generator, where the air outlet of the                ozone generator communicates with the bottom of a                wet scrubber, the bottom of wet scrubber is also                provided with an air inlet, the outlet of the wet                scrubber communicates with a circulating water                tank, the circulating water tank communicates with                the upper portion of the filling in the wet                scrubber through a circulating pump, the gas outlet                at the top of the wet scrubber is connected to the                gas inlet of the catalytic reactor which is used                for contact with ozone to produce a reaction, the                catalytic reactor is filled with an activated                carbon catalyst loaded with manganese-iron-cobalt                composite oxide, the top of catalytic reactor is                provided with a feed port, the bottom is provided                with an outlet, and the outlet of the catalytic                reactor is connected to the exhaust cylinder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The step (i) involves                  adding wet scrubber with ozone before use, where                  the volume ratio of ozone and volatile organic                  compounds is (1-3):1, agglomerating the paint                  residue particles for efficiently removing the                  particles in the wet scrubber tower and                  performing chemical oxidation reaction for                  improving absorption effect of volatile organic                  compounds, where the ozone and volatile organic                  compounds that are not dissolved in the water                  undergo advanced oxidation reactions in the                  contact catalytic reactor to further degrade the                  mineralized volatile organic compounds. The step                  (ii) involves filling the ozone contacts the                  activated carbon catalyst loaded with                  manganese-iron-cobalt composite oxide in the                  catalytic reactor for catalyzing the complete                  mineralization of volatile organic compounds by                  ozone. The activated carbon catalyst loaded with                  manganese-iron-cobalt composite oxide involves                  mixing potassium permanganate, ferric nitrate and                  cobalt (II) nitrate aqueous solution, mixing the                  solution with granular wood activated carbon with                  a particle size of 20-40 mesh under a                  liquid-solid ratio of (5-15):1, slowly dripping                  10 %mass aqueous ammonia, stirring until the pH                  is 10.5-11, aging, performing suction filtration,                  washing, drying and calcining in a nitrogen                  atmosphere, where a manganese-iron-cobalt-oxygen                  compound of formula: MnxFeyCozOn/activated carbon                  composite catalyst with a loading capacity of 10                  %mass ferromanganese and cobalt are obtained, and                  the concentrations of the potassium permanganate,                  ferric nitrate and cobalt (II) nitrate aqueous                  solution are all 10 %mass. Preferred Device: The                  device has a flow meter which is connected                  between the ozone generator and the wet                  scrubber.

PN CN110922302-A
TI Fuel ethanol double negative pressure crude distillation tower differential pressure distillation dehydration production process by separately degassing fermented mature mash A and B, distilling, and collecting crude alcohol condensate.
AU JIANG X
   ZHOU H
   LI J
   LU S
AE GUANGDONG ZHONGKE TIANYUAN NEW ENERGY SC (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202025905B
AB    NOVELTY - A fuel ethanol double negative pressure crude distillation tower differential pressure distillation dehydration production process includes degassing fermented mature mash A, heating, and coarse distilling; preheating fermented mature mash B, degassing, adding portion of degassed product to coarse distillation tower B, distilling, preheating another portion of degassed mash product, and adding to combined tower; discharging non-alcoholic waste mash in coarse distillation towers A and B at the bottom, collecting crude alcohol condensate, adding to crude alcohol tank, preheating, pumping to refine distillation tower, concentrating, and collecting high concentration of ethanol; and preheating degassed product in combined tower, adding high concentration of ethanol to top of combined tower, absorbing in molecular sieve dehydration device, and dehydrating to obtain absolute ethanol.
   USE - The method is for fuel ethanol double negative pressure crude distillation tower differential pressure distillation dehydration production process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can process two different mashes, avoid the influence of non-condensable gas entrained in the mash on distillation system, and has ideal degassing effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A fuel ethanol double negative pressure crude distillation tower differential pressure distillation dehydration production process comprises adding fermented mature mash A to degassing tower, degassing, heating, adding to coarse distillation tower A, and distilling; preheating fermented mature mash B, adding to degassing tank, degassing to remove carbon dioxide and other gases, adding portion of degassed product to coarse distillation tower B, distilling, preheating another portion of degassed mash product, and adding to combined tower; discharging non-alcoholic waste mash in coarse distillation towers A and B at the bottom, collecting crude alcohol condensate from condensation system of coarse distillation towers A and B, adding to crude alcohol tank to temporarily store, preheating in preheater, pumping to refine distillation tower, concentrating to remove impurities, and collecting high concentration of ethanol in top area of tower; and preheating degassed product in combined tower, adding high concentration of ethanol to top of combined tower, absorbing in molecular sieve dehydration device, and dehydrating to obtain absolute ethanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The degassing is done at top pressure of - 90 kPa to - 10 kPa, top temperature of 50-90 degrees C and bottom temperature of 55-65 degrees C. The coarse distillation tower A distilling process is done at top pressure of - 80 kPa to - 10 kPa, top temperature of 45-85 degrees C and bottom temperature of 60-100 degrees C. The coarse distillation tower B distilling process is done at top pressure of - 85 kPa to - 10 kPa, top temperature of 45-85 degrees C and bottom temperature of 60-100 degrees C. The combined tower reaction is done at top pressure of 10-150 kPa, top temperature of 80-105 degrees C and bottom temperature of 110-135 degrees C. The refine distillation tower reaction is done at top pressure of 50-400 kPa, top temperature of 90-125 degrees C and bottom temperature of 125-155 degrees C. The molecular sieve dehydration is done at pressure of - 90-300 kPa and temperature of 60-130 degrees C.

TI Non-dispersive infrared (NDIR) gas sensor of            optical device, has optical filter includes wavelength            range having maximum average transmittance with width            of specific range in wavelength range of specific            range.
AU SASAYAMA K
AE ASAHI KASEI MICRODEVICES CORP (ASAH-C)
   ASAHI KASEI ELECTRONICS CO LTD (ASAH-C)
GA 202019238X
AB    NOVELTY - The NDIR gas sensor has an optical filter                includes a substrate and a multilayer film having                the layers with different refractive indexes formed                on the substrate. An infrared light emitting and                receiving device has a semiconductor layer of a                first conductive type, an active layer, and a                semiconductor layer of a second conductive type.                The second layer has a refractive index of 3.2 or                more and 4.2 or less in a wavelength range of 2400                nm to 6000 nm. The active layer contains                aluminum-indium-antimony (AlxIn1-xSb, x is                0.02-0.2, preferably 0.02-0.12) or                indium-arsenic-antimony (InAsySb1-y, y is 0.75-1,                preferably 0.75-0.9). The optical filter includes a                wavelength range having an average transmittance of                70% or more with a width of 50 nm or more in a                wavelength range of 2400 nm to 6000 nm, and has a                maximum transmittance of 5% or more in a wavelength                range of 6000 nm to 8000 nm and an average                transmittance of 2% or more and 60% or less in a                wavelength range of 6000 nm to 8000 nm.
   USE - Non-dispersive infrared (NDIR) gas sensor used                in infrared light receiving device such as                photodiode, photoconductive sensor, thermistor, and                thermopile, and in infrared light emitting device                such as LED, lamp and micro-electromechanical                system (MEMS) heater, and used in optical device                (claimed) such as infrared radiation thermometer,                infrared spectral imaging, and human body detection                sensor, for measuring concentration of gas to be                measured such as nitric oxide, carbon monoxide,                carbon dioxide, formaldehyde, propane, methane,                water or ethanolin atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The highly accurate NDIR gas sensor and a                highly accurate optical device even using a                simplified optical filter is provided. The number                of stacked layers reduces the defects caused during                film formation, thus, improving the yield. The                warpage due to a large number of stacked layers is                reduced, which suppresses the occurrence of                chipping during dicing and enhances the mass                production stability. The design of the optical                filter is simplified, the cost is reduced, and the                mass productivity is improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross sectional view of                the optical filter.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The to-be-measured gas is nitric oxide,                  carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, formaldehyde,                  propane, methane, water or ethanol. The first                  layer contains silicon oxide, titanium oxide                  and/or zinc sulfide. The second layer contains                  silicon and/or germanium.

TI Facility for membrane permeation treatment of feed            gas flow e.g. biogas, comprises compressor, membrane            separation unit to receive flow of gas, pressure            measurement device, and valve to adjust membranes in            membrane separation units.
AU CHAREYRE J
   GRABIE V
   ZICK G
   GRABIER V
AE AIR LIQUIDE ETUDE & EXPL PROCEDES CLAUDE (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202012710F
AB    NOVELTY - A membrane permeation treatment facility                comprises a compressor for compressing the feed gas                flow containing methane or carbon dioxide, a                membrane separation unit (A) to receive a flow of                gas from the compressor and supply a permeate (P1)                and a retentate (R1), a membrane separation unit                (B) to receive the retentate (R1) and supply a                permeate (P2) and a retentate (R2), a membrane                separation unit (C) to receive the permeate (P1)                and supply a permeate (P3) and a retentate (R3), a                membrane separation unit (D) to receive the                retentate (R3) and supply a permeate (P4) and a                retentate (R4), a pressure measurement device to                measure the pressure of a gas flowing into or out                of the membrane separation unit (A), and at least                one valve able to adjust membranes in at least one                of the membrane separation units that receive. The                membrane separation units (A)-(D) comprises several                membranes.
   USE - Facility for membrane permeation treatment of                feed gas flow e.g. biogas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane permeation treatment facility                provides feed gas flow having excellent purity, and                reduced manufacturing cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for membrane                permeation treatment of a feed gas flow, which                involves compressing the feed gas flow, receiving a                flow of gas from the compressor at the membrane                separation unit (A), receiving the retentate (R1)                at the membrane separation unit (B), receiving the                permeate (P1) at the membrane separation unit (C),                receiving the retentate (R3) at the membrane                separation unit (D), measuring the pressure of                either the flow of gas received from the compressor                by the membrane separation unit (A) or a pressure                of the retentate(R1), comparing the measured                pressure with a setpoint pressure and determining a                difference, and adjusting, through use of at least                one valve, membranes of the membrane separation                units that receive a flow of gas as a function of                the determined difference between the measured                pressure and the setpoint pressure so as to keep a                methane concentration of the retentate (R2)                constant.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                membrane permeation treatment facility.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Apparatus: The pressure                  measurement device measures the pressure of gas                  flow at an inlet of the separation unit (A), and                  the pressure of the retentate (R2). The retentate                  (R4) is recycled to the compressor (w). The                  membranes of the membrane separation units have a                  same selectivity or different selectivity. The                  membrane separation units comprise at least two                  membranes with the same selectivity or different                  selectivity. Preferred process: The adjustment                  step involves adding membranes of the membrane                  separation unit (A) to the membranes of the                  membrane separation unit (A) that receive the                  flow of gas from the compressor, adding membranes                  of the membrane separation unit (C) to the                  membranes of the membrane separation unit(C) that                  receive the permeate (R3), and finally adding                  membranes of the membrane separation unit (B) to                  the membranes of the membrane separation unit (B)                  that receive the retentate (R3). The measurement,                  comparison, and adjustment are carried out                  automatically by a programmable controller.

PN WO2020025998-A1; IN202147007482-A; CN112513418-A; BR112021001508-A2; EP3830388-A1; US2021312348-A1; EP3830388-A4
TI Integrated capacity modelling system for            monitoring and controlling multiple oil and gas assets,            has data distribution module which is capable of            distributing asset capacity of assets to multiple users            using multiple applications.
AU HAFEZ H H
   BAHAMAISH J N
   AL MANSOORI Y
   SAPUTELLI L
   ESCORCIA A
   KHALAF M
   MOHAN R
   AL M Y
   MANSOORI Y A
AE ABU DHABI NAT OIL CO (ABUD-Non-standard)
   ABU DHABI NAT OIL CO (ABUD-Non-standard)
GA 202011117D
AB    NOVELTY - The integrated capacity modelling system (1)                has a data acquisition module (10), which is                capable of acquiring upstream data. A subsurface                and surface processes simulation module (20) is                capable of simulating production and injection                performance of assets from the reservoir to the                sales delivery point. A data assimilation module                (30) is capable of extracting key features in the                data for the purposes of calculating production                capacity of assets from the data and from the                simulation module. The asset capacity model is                composed of individual models of assets and for                each relevant material, namely, oil, water and gas                and other associated constituents including carbon                dioxide, nitrogen (N2), and hydrogen sulfide (H2S).                A data presentation module (40) is capable of                presenting all the components of the asset capacity                model of assets. A data distribution module (50) is                capable of distributing asset capacity of assets to                multiple users using multiple applications                (72).
   USE - Integrated capacity modelling system for                monitoring and controlling multiple oil and gas                assets of various sources and different producers                or production sites.
   ADVANTAGE - The subsurface and surface processes                simulation module, automatically updates the                reservoir performance parameters using the                variation in production and injection resulting                from surface equipment downtime, such that the                reservoir performance parameters are provided to                the data assimilation module without the need to                recur to external simulator. The computational time                in the subsurface and surface processes simulation                module is reduced. The coherent asset capacity                model is maintained with large numbers of nodes and                run scenarios in the asset capacity model are                allowed with a high performance. The integrated                capacity modelling system automatically generates                tickets for changing and adjusting the targets of                an asset, which simplifies the overall interaction                between the stakeholders.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an                integrated capacity model system, multiple assets                and facilities and multiple user                applications.
   Integrated capacity modelling system                (1)
   Data acquisition module (10)
   Surface processes simulation module                (20)
   Data assimilation module (30)
   Data presentation module (40)
   Data distribution module (50)
   Application (72)

PN WO2020028462-A2; WO2020028462-A3; EP3830001-A2; US2021323750-A1
TI System for packaging fresh food product e.g. fish, comprises several packages comprising oxygen permeable film having preset oxygen transmission rate and master pouch having interior space comprising modified atmosphere containing oxygen.
AU STOCKLEY H W
   GIUSTI B
AE CRYOVAC LLC (CRYV-C)
   CRYOVAC LLC (CRYV-C)
   CRYOVAC LLC (CRYV-C)
GA 202011102B
AB    NOVELTY - A system (10) comprises several packages (16) having a fresh food product (17) which is sealed in package, and a master pouch (12) having an interior space (14) in which the several packages are enclosed. The packages comprise an oxygen permeable film having an oxygen transmission rate (OTR) of 10000 cc(STP)/m2/day/1 atm or more at 23 degrees C and at 0% relative humidity (RH) which is measured according to ASTM D3985. The master pouch comprises a film comprising oxygen barrier layer. The film of the master pouch has OTR of 20 cc(STP)/m2/day/1 atm at 23 degrees C and at 0% of RH which is measured according to ASTM D3985. A modified atmosphere in the interior space of the master pouch comprises carbon dioxide and 0.1 vol.% or less oxygen concentration.
   USE - System for packaging fresh food product e.g. fish such as salmon (all claimed), tilapia, tuna fillets, squid, walleye, pike, wahoo fish, blue fish, marlin, amberjack, cobia, sake, mackerel, mahi mahi, swordfish, shrimp, lobster, and shellfish. Can also be used for packaging other meats such as beef, veal, pork, sausage, cured meats, chicken, goat, fowl, and bison meat, fruits and vegetables.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides packaged food product having excellent total shell life of 27-34 days by using oxygen permeable film to prevent growth of anaerobic bacteria such as Clostridium botulinum during cold storage, and effectively maintains desired appearance such as color and odor even after individual packages are removed from master pouch, during retail display for consumer purchase at grocery stores. The cold storage and transport of packaged food product can be accomplished without freezing the food product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method of packaging a fresh food product; and
   (2) packaged fresh food product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system.
   System (10)
   Pouch (12)
   Interior space (14)
   Package (16)
   Food product (17)
   Back sheets (20,22)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The system further comprises oxygen scavenging composition which is in fluid communication with modified atmosphere of master pouch. The modified atmosphere comprises nitrogen gas and/or 99.5 vol.% or more, preferably 99.9 vol.% or more carbon dioxide gas, or 20-30 vol.% carbon dioxide or 40-60 vol.% carbon dioxide or 70-80 vol.% carbon dioxide, 0.4 vol.% carbon monoxide and preferably 0.01 vol.% or less oxygen. The several packages comprises bottom film web which is thermoformed web. Preferred Properties: The oxygen permeable film has OTR of preferably 20000 cc(STP)/m2/day/1 atm or more, and thickness of 4 mils or less, preferably 3 mils or less. The thermoformed tray has an OTR of 10000 cc(STP)/m2/day/1 atm or more at 23 degrees C and 0% of RH, which is measured according to ASTM D3985.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The oxygen permeable film comprises sealant layer comprising a polymer blend comprising 98 wt.% or less, preferably 95 wt.% or less linear low density polyethylene/hexene copolymer, a core layer comprising a very low density polyethylene/octene copolymer, and an outer abuse layer comprising a blend of 98 wt.% high density polyethylene and low density polyethylene with silica, and further comprises tie layer. The sealant layer of oxygen permeable film further comprises 1-10 wt.% low density polyethylene blended with silica. The outer abuse layer of oxygen permeable film further comprises 95 wt.% or less high density polyethylene. The bottom film web comprises layer (A) comprising a blend of ethylene vinyl acetate and polybutylene, layer (B) comprising a linear low density polyethylene, layer (C) comprising a linear low density polyethylene, layer (D) comprising an ethylene vinyl alcohol, layer (E) comprising a linear low density polyethylene, layer (F) comprising a blend of blend of linear low density polyethylene and low density polyethylene, and layer (G) comprising a semi-rigid polyvinyl chloride. The master pouch is multilayer film comprises a sealant layer comprising blend of 60-90 wt.%, preferably 75-85 wt.% very low density polyethylene/octane copolymer and 10-40 wt.%, preferably 15-25 wt.% linear low density polyethylene, a core layer comprising an ethylene/butyl acrylate copolymer, and an outer layer comprising a blend of 75-95 wt.% very low density polyethylene, 10-20 wt.% low density polyethylene/octene copolymer and 0.1-2 wt.% blend of a fluoropolymer blended in linear low density polyethylene. The multilayer film of the master pouch comprises layer (I) comprising a blend of medium density polyethylene, ethylene vinyl acetate, and an ethylene copolymer, which is sealant layer, layer (II) comprising a linear low density polyethylene, layer (III) comprising a blend of linear low density polyethylene and medium density polyethylene and comprising oxygen barrier layer and layer (IV) comprising a blend of medium density polyethylene, ethylene vinyl acetate, and an ethylene copolymer, and layer (V) comprising low density polyethylene. The layer (III) of multilayer film of master pouch comprises polyvinylidene chloride methyl acrylate. Preferred Properties: The layer (G) has thickness of 10-16 mils. The multilayer film of mater pouch has OTR of 17000 cc(STP)/m2/day/1 atm or more at 23 degrees C and 0% of RH as measured in accordance with ASTM D3985.

PN US2020029858-A1; WO2020024040-A1; CA3107980-A1; EP3830571-A1
TI System for detecting and identifying e.g. volatile organic compound, in exhaled breath of person, using sensor, has communication module operable to transmit collected data from sensor module to remote processor for analysis of data.
AU REDDY R
AE NGAGEIT DIGITAL HEALTH INC (NGAG-Non-standard)
   NGAGEIT DIGITAL HEALTH INC (NGAG-Non-standard)
GA 2020095057
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a mouth piece (106) connected to a housing (104), where the mouth piece is operable to receive exhaled breath of a subject. A sensor module (108) is arranged in the housing, where the sensor module is operable to detect volatile organic compounds in the exhaled breath and to collect data associated with detection of the volatile organic compounds. A communication module (110) is arranged in the housing and in communication with the sensor module, where the communication module is operable to transmit collected data from the sensor module to a remote processor for analysis of the collected data.
   USE - System for detecting and identifying a substance e.g. volatile organic compound, volatile gas and ketone, in exhaled breath of a subject i.e. person, using a sensor (claimed) for treating various diseases during medical procedures. Uses include but are not limited to cancer, lung cancer, breast cancer, digestive cancer, diabetes, ulcer, peptic ulcer, sepsis, or levels of exhaled anesthetic gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The system measures density of exhaled air using a pair of ultrasonic transducer, nano-particle flow sensor and microelectronic flow sensor to determine oxygen and carbon dioxide concentrations in the exhaled air so as to analyze metabolic rate for a subject in an easy manner. The system utilizes a pattern recognition algorithm to obtain optimal match and/or relatively high confidence score in matching a signal and/or signal pattern to previously stored signals and/or signal patterns correlated with health conditions or diseases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a method for detecting and identifying a substance in exhaled breath of a subject for treating various diseases during medical procedures
   (2) a sensor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of a system for detecting and identifying a substance in exhaled breath of a subject for treating various diseases during medical procedures.
   System for detecting and identifying a substance in exhaled breath of a subject for treating various diseases during medical procedures (100)
   Housing (104)
   Mouth piece (106)
   Sensor module (108)
   Communication module (110)

PN CN110549717-A; CN110549717-B
TI Environment-friendly packaging material for forest fertilizer is divided into two layers of inner and outer materials, where outer material is kraft paper having corn starch, tapioca starch and pea powder, and inner material is plastic film.
AU LIN H
   CAO J
   CHEN W
   ZHOU Z
   WU C
   XIE S
   LU X
   CHEN D
   TANG J
   PAN B
   QIN Q
AE GUANGXI HUAWOTE ECOLOGICAL FERTILIZER CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI ZHUANG AUTONOMOUS REGION INST (GUAG-C)
   GUANGXI HUAWOTE ECOLOGICAL FERTILIZER CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI ZHUANG AUTONOMOUS REGION INST (GUAG-C)
GA 2019A5951Q
AB    NOVELTY - Environment-friendly packaging material is divided into two layers of inner and outer materials. The outer material is kraft paper,and comprises corn starch, tapioca starch and pea powder. The inner material is a biodegradable plastic film, and comprises chitosan, chitin, and polybutylene succinate. The inner and outer materials are bonded with a biodegradable aliphatic polyester hot-melt adhesive.
   USE - Environment-friendly packaging material for forest fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The environment-friendly packaging material packages fertilizer under natural condition such as forest soil and water, is biodegraded into carbon dioxide and water, effectively controls release of active ingredient, reduces fertilizer loss, improves fertilizer utilization, and maintains efficiency of fertilizer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing an environment-friendly packaging material for forest fertilizer, which involves stirring and mixing corn starch, tapioca starch, pea powder, and polylactic acid uniformly to obtain slurry, and grinding slurry to obtain kraft paper mixed slurry; stirring, mixing and grinding chitosan, chitin, and polybutylene succinate to obtain a biodegradable plastic film mixed slurry; mixing kraft paper mixed slurry and biodegradable plastic film mixed slurry to obtain mixture, forming, dehydrating and drying obtained mixture into a single layer of kraft paper and single layer of biodegradable plastic film; and punching hole on biodegradable plastic film with a basis weight of 20-50g/m2 uniformly, coating an adhesive on one side of a kraft paper with a basis weight of 20-50g/m2, punching hole on one surface of coated kraft paper, and then extruding and degrading a plastic film and kraft paper coated with a biodegradable aliphatic polyester hot-melt adhesive on one side to obtain final product; and
   (2) a method for packaging an environment-friendly packaging material for forest fertilizer, which involves cutting and gluing packaging material into different specifications, quantifying small packaging according to application amount of 0.1-1.0kg, and then combining small packaging into a large packaging of 25kg or 50kg.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The biodegradable aliphatic polyester hot melt adhesive comprises poly-L-lactic acid, poly-D-lactic acid, and poly-LD-lactic acid. Preferred Compositions: The kraft paper comprises 60-70 pts. wt. corn starch, 40-50 pts. wt. tapioca starch, 15-27 pts. wt. pea flour and 10-23 pts. wt. polylactic acid. The biodegradable plastic film comprises 23-35 pts. wt. chitosan, 17-29 pts. wt. chitin and 8-20 pts. wt. polybutylene succinate. Preferred Conditions: The biodegradable plastic film is provided with uniformly distributed holes. The uniformly distributed holes on biodegradable plastic film include one of a circle, an oval or a square hole. The packaging material is bonded into a packaging bag, and outer side of inner layer of biodegradable plastic film in contact with fertilizer is jointed and is made by thermal bonding.
