PN CN110527498-A; WO2021026947-A1; RU2747127-C1
TI Combustible ice fast supercooled nanoliquefaction agent used in injection liquid for combustible ice mining, comprises ammonium salt, acid, penetrating agent, amide, ester, ether, alcohol, ketone, and aldehyde.
AU FENG W
   FENG Y
   FENG Z
   FENG B
AE CHENGDU NENGSHENGCAI TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (CHEN-Non-standard)
   RENSHOU BRANCH CHENGDU NENGSHENGCAI TECH (RENS-Non-standard)
   CHENGDU ENERGY LIFE MATERIALS SCI & TECHNOLOGY DEV (CHEN-Non-standard)
   RENSHOU ENERGY LIFE MATERIALS SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (RENS-Non-standard)
   CHENGDU ENERGY LIFE MATERIALS SCI & TECHNOLOGY (CHEN-Non-standard)
   RENSHOU ENERGY LIFE MATERIALS SCI & TECHNOLOGY (RENS-Non-standard)
GA 2019A5047D
AB    NOVELTY - A combustible ice fast supercooled nanoliquefaction agent comprises 0-25 %mass ammonium salt, 0-25 %mass acid, 10-80 %mass penetrating agent, 0-25 %mass amide, 5-70 %mass ester, 2-40 %mass ether, 0-35 %mass alcohol, 0-25 %mass ketone, and 0-35 %mass aldehyde.
   USE - Combustible ice fast supercooled nanoliquefaction agent is used in injection liquid for combustible ice mining (all claimed) used for producing methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The combustible ice fast supercooled nanoliquefaction agent has improved flash point, combustible ice dissolution rate, and permeability. The agent is capable of developing combustible ice by a safe, environmentally-friendly, economical and effective method, and producing methane gas for a long period of time by rapid and economical method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) injection liquid for combustible ice mining, which comprises 0.8-1.5 %mass nanoliquefaction agent, and further comprises 0-5 %mass sodium or potassium chloride, 0-5 %mass hydrochloric acid, 0-3 %mass hydrogen fluoride and remainder of water comprising seawater, river water or tap water; and
   (2) combustible ice mining method, which involves selecting the block with large combustible ice reserves as the combustible ice mining test area, starting to distribute wells in the center of the combustible ice mining test area and drilling the water around the central well clockwise or counterclockwise according to the five-point method, seven-point method, nine-point method, reverse five-point method, reverse seven-point method or reverse nine-point method, circulating from the center to inject the injection fluid into the drilled well, testing produced methane gas according to natural gas test production standard for well injected with the injection liquid, using the wells surrounding gas production wells as water injection wells, using central well drilled by reverse seven-point method or reverse nine-point method as a water injection well, and using wells surrounding the water injection well as gas production wells, injecting the injection fluid into water injection well, and extracting methane from gas production well until methane gas cannot be produced, injecting liquid or gaseous carbon dioxide to water injection wells, and forming methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ammonium salt is at least one chosen from ammonium fluoride, ammonium hydrogen fluoride and ammonium chloride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The acid is at least one chosen from hydrochloric acid, hydrofluoric acid, propionic acid, isooctanoic acid, naphthenic acid, and dodecylbenzenesulfonic acid. The penetrating agent is at least one chosen from penetrating agent and penetrating agent JF. The amide is at least one chosen from N,N-dimethylformamide and N,N-dimethylacetamide. The ester is at least one chosen from butyl acrylate, diethyl phthalate, dibutyl phthalate, dioctyl phthalate, diisooctyl phthalate, diethyl oxalate, butyl acetate, isoamyl acetate, diethyl malonate, butyl lactate, butyl methacrylate, ethylene glycol ether acetate, ethylene glycol butyl ether acetate, ethylene glycol diacetate, diethylene glycol butyl ether acetate, ethyl acetoacetate, butyl lactate, propylene glycol methyl ether propionate, propylene glycol methyl ether acetate, methyl benzoate, ethyl benzoate, propyl benzoate, butyl benzoate, propylene carbonate, isoamyl propionate, butyl butyrate, isobutyl isobutyrate, isoamyl isovalerate, 1,4-butyrolactone, diethyl oxalate, dibutyl malonate, diethyl malonate, dimethyl maleate, glycerin diacetate, 1,2-propylene glycol carbonate, tributyl citrate, ethyl octoate, propylene glycol methyl ether acetate, propylene glycol butyl ether acetate, isoamyl butyrate, isoamyl isovalerate, dioctyl succinate, dioctyl sebacate, triacetin, isooctyl acrylate, tributyl phosphate, and 2-butoxyethyl acetate. The ether is at least one chosen from ethylene glycol methyl ether, ethylene glycol ethyl ether, ethylene glycol butyl ether, ethylene glycol dibutyl ether, ethylene glycol monobenzyl ether, propylene glycol methyl ether, propylene glycol ether, propylene glycol butyl ether, diethylene glycol, diethylene glycol diethyl ether, diethylene glycol dimethyl ether, diethylene glycol butyl ether, propylene glycol methyl ether, isoamyl ether, phenyl ether, phenyl ether, phenyl ether, 1,3-butanediol-3-methyl ether, diethylene glycol ether, and 1,2-propylene glycol-butyl ether. The alcohol is at least one chosen from methanol, ethanol, n-butanol, ethylene glycol, methyl isobutyl methanol, 2-hexanol, isoamyl alcohol, isobutanol, benzyl alcohol, and 1,2-propanediol. The ketone is at least one chosen from N-methylpyrrolidone and cyclohexanone. The aldehyde is formaldehyde, citral, nonanal or benzaldehyde.

PN WO2019207444-A1; CA3097670-A1; CN112236049-A; EP3784081-A1; US2021068475-A1; EP3784081-B1; ES2924378-T3
TI Manufacturing method of padding using            three-dimensional (3D) printing, involves depositing a            filament according to microarchitecture that entails            definition of superimposed matrices adapted to define            structure composed of open cells.
AU COCCIA S
   GARGANO F
AE CYTECH SRL (CYTE-Non-standard)
   CANNON & MACINTOSH INVESTMENT LTD (CANN-Non-standard)
   CYTECH SRL (CYTE-Non-standard)
   CYTECH SRL (CYTE-Non-standard)
   CANNON & MACINTOSH INVESTMENT LTD (CANN-Non-standard)
   CYTECH SRL (CYTE-Non-standard)
GA 201991076N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves depositing a filament                according to a microarchitecture that entails the                definition of superimposed matrices that are                adapted to define a structure (5) composed of                individual open cells (6). The cells are mutually                connected and arranged mutually opposite and side                by side. Each cell has a shape in plan view with a                variable diameter which is obtained by way of                superimposing elements that are substantially                shaped like a truncated pyramid or like a truncated                cone with a polygonal base.
   USE - Manufacturing method of padding (claimed)                using three-dimensional (3D) printing, such as a                padding of a cycling pad for cycling shorts or                pants.
   ADVANTAGE - A padding which has zones of differentiated                thickness according to the specific requirements of                the user can be obtained so as to obtain an optimal                and specific protection and a comfort that are                constant over time, while at the same time                improving environmental sustainability by                eliminating the use of adhesives and reducing the                use of carbon dioxide. The use of the particular                microarchitecture makes it possible to obtain                deformable zones that at the same time are                controlled and, owing to different heights of the                cells, are also differentiated so as to increase                the overall performance (protection and comfort) of                the cycling pad, compared to conventional cycling                pads. The microarchitecture makes it possible to                obtain a product that has characteristics, such as                density, load-bearing capacity, breathability,                flexibility/elasticity, and weight, which are not                determined solely by the type of material used, but                by the intrinsic shape of the microarchitecture.                The paddings keep their elasticity constant over                time, even after multiple washes; they have rapid                drying times, high breathability, contained                encumbrances and volumes for transport, and reduced                overall weight; and they do not trap heat, they do                not overheat during use, they do not generate any                type of allergy in contact with the skin, and they                are of low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                padding.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a cross-sectional view                perspective view showing cells defining the                padding.
   Structure (5)
   Cells (6,6a,6b)
   Point or plane of maximum dimension                (7)
   Point or plane of minimum dimension                (8)

PN CN110384823-A; CN110384823-B
TI Bionic hepatic lobules based on silk fibroin scaffold comprises stents and hepatocytes, interstitial cells and extracellular matrices, where scaffold is silk fibroin scaffold having radial sheet layer and interlamellar pore structure.
AU WANG X
   WEI G
   LIU M
   DING Y
   WEI X
   ZHAO Z
AE UNIV DALIAN MEDICAL (UYDA-Non-standard)
GA 2019925192
AB    NOVELTY - Bionic hepatic lobules based on a silk fibroin scaffold comprises stents and hepatocytes, interstitial cells and extracellular matrices placed on the stent, where the scaffold is a silk fibroin scaffold having a radial sheet layer and an interlamellar pore structure formed by freeze-drying the silk fibroin solution.
   USE - As bionic hepatic lobules based on a silk fibroin scaffold.
   ADVANTAGE - The bionic hepatic lobules ideally maintain hepatocyte growth activity and significantly improve the structure of bionic hepatic lobule tissue. The porous silk fibroin provides three-dimensional growth space for hepatocytes, supports the co-growth and organization of hepatocytes and interstitial cells, promotes the formation of hepatic lobular structures in vitro, and significantly improves the activity of hepatocytes in vitro.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for constructing the bionic hepatic lobules based on a silk fibroin scaffold, involving (a) placing silk fibroin solution with a concentration of 5-6% in a centrifuge tube, quickly immersing the centrifuge tube in liquid nitrogen for 4-5 minutes, and freeze-drying for 48 hours to obtain a silk fibroin with radial sheet and interlamellar pore structure, (b) infiltrating the silk fibroin in deionized water, and perforating the silk fibroin at a center of the central layer of the radial sheet structure to obtain a silk fibroin scaffold, (c) carrying out sterile pre-coating on the silk fibroin scaffold by using 0.05 %mass collagen solution and sucking the liquid in the silk fibroin scaffold, inoculating the mesenchymal cells into the silk fibroin scaffold, culturing for 5-7 days, and inoculating the liver cell-collagen mixed suspension into the silk fibroin scaffold, and (d) placing the inoculated silk fibroin scaffold into a multi-well culture plate at a density of 1 scaffold/well, placing the multi-well cell culture plate in a cell culture incubator, coagulating at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide, gelling for 1 hour, slowly adding a medium to each well of the multi-well cell culture plate and co-culturing for 20-30 days, where the medium is a mixture of an interstitial cell culture medium and a hepatocyte culture medium in a volume ratio of 1:1.

TI New human normal renal tubular primary cell,            preferably primary cell XHRN-01 useful for in vitro            cell analysis against pathogenesis analysis, drug            sensitivity study or drug toxicity study of            kidney-related diseases.
AU LIU H
   ZHANG X
   CHEN K
   WANG Q
   WANG X
   SHI J
   LV Q
   XIONG Z
AE WUHAN SAIER LANGLING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
   WUHAN SAIER LANGLING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 201991935S
AB    NOVELTY - A human normal renal tubular primary cell,                preferably human normal renal tubular primary cell                XHRN-01 (CCTCC NO: C201970), is new.
   USE - The human normal renal tubular primary cell,                preferably human normal renal tubular primary cell                XHRN-01 is useful for in vitro cell analysis                against pathogenesis analysis, drug sensitivity                study or drug toxicity study of kidney-related                diseases in vitro and in vivo (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) method for in vitro isolation and culture                of human normal renal tubule primary cells,                involves collecting fresh paracancerous tissues                surgically resected from patients with clinical                clear cell carcinoma, removing blood vessels, fat                and necrotic components under an microscope with an                anatomical forceps, cutting the tissue into a braid                to obtain tissue sample, digesting tissue sample                with digestive juice, where the digestive juice is                Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) medium                containing collagenase and trypsin, collecting                digested tissue sample through sterile filter to                collect cell suspension, removing supernatant by                low speed centrifugation to collect cell pellet,                adding cell pellet to red blood cell lysate at room                temperature, gently upsiding down to promote red                blood cell lysis, performing low speed                centrifugation, collecting the cell pellet,                collecting cell pellet by low speed centrifugation,                resuspending cell pellet in S6.COMR complete                medium, inoculating the pellet into cell culture                flask to obtain human normal tubular primary cell,                removing old medium in the normal renal tubular                primary cells, digesting monolayer cells with                accutase digestion solution, where the digestion                time is 3-5 minutes, centrifuging at 1000                revolutions per minute (rpm) for 5 minutes after                the end of the digestion reaction, removing the                supernatant, resuspending the cell pellet with                complete medium, inoculating the cell pellet into                culture flask, culturing at 37 degrees C and 5%                carbon dioxide to obtain human normal renal tubule                primary subcultured daughter cells; and
   (2) use of human normal renal tubular primary                cell, for in vitro cell analysis against                pathogenesis analysis, drug sensitivity study or                drug toxicity study of kidney-related diseases in                vitro and in vivo.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The short tandem                  repeat (STR) locus detected by the human normal                  renal tubular primary cell comprises 21 sites,                  and the allele of the STR locus comprises:                  AMEL-X, CSF1P0-11/12, D12S391-18, D13S317-8/12,                  D16S539-11, D18S51-14/17, D19S433-13/15,                  D2S441-11/14, D21S11-28/29 , D2S1338-17/24,                  D3S1358-15/17, D5S818-11, D6S1043-13/17,                  D7S820-8/11, D8S1179-10/14, FGA-22.2/24, Penta                  D-9/11, penta E-11/14, TH01-7/9, TPOX-9/12 and                  vWA-18/20. The amount of the digestive juice is                  10 times volume of the tissue sample, and the                  digestion condition is 37 degrees C, 60 rpm and                  time is 2-3.5 hours. The COMR complete medium                  comprises 1 mM sodium pyruvate, 5% fetal bovine                  serum to DMEM/F12 medium, 1 x non-essential amino                  acids, 10 ng/ml human epidermal growth factor, 10                  mu g/ml human recombinant insulin, 1 mg/ml                  ascorbic acid, 100 U/ml penicillin, 100 mu g/ml                  streptomycin, 0.25 mu g/ml amphotericin B and 0.6                  mg/ml cow bile.

PN JP2019148413-A; WO2019168055-A1; JP6579285-B2; CN111770678-A; US2020390116-A1; EP3753395-A1; SG11202008187-A1; EP3753395-A4; EP3753395-B1; SG11202008187-B; US11419341-B2
TI Air conditioner in a chamber comprises gas supply            apparatus which supplies low oxygen concentration air            and external air into storage that accommodates plant            for respiration, and controller which controls            operation of gas supply apparatus.
AU FUJIMOTO Y
   KAMEI N
   YUUSUKE F
   NORITAKA K
AE DAIKIN KOGYO KK (DAIK-C)
   DAIKIN IND LTD (DAIK-C)
   DAIKIN IND LTD (DAIK-C)
   DAIKIN IND LTD (DAIK-C)
GA 201975639H
AB    NOVELTY - An air conditioner in a chamber comprises gas                supply apparatus which can supply low oxygen                concentration air and external air into storage for                accommodating the plant for respiration, and                controller which controls operation of gas supply                apparatus, reduces oxygen concentration of air in a                chamber of storage to predetermined oxygen                concentration setting value when gas supply                apparatus supplies low oxygen concentration air                into the chamber of storage and performs air                composition maintenance operation in the chamber                after completion of oxygen concentration reduction                operation. In gas supply apparatus, 1st supply                operation which supplies 1st low oxygen                concentration air into the chamber of storage, 2nd                supply operation which supplies 2nd low oxygen                concentration air lower than low oxygen                concentration air with 1st oxygen concentration                into the chamber of storage, and external air                supply operation which supplies external air into                chamber of storage are performed.
   USE - An air conditioner in a chamber.
   ADVANTAGE - Oxygen concentration and carbon dioxide levels                of the air in the chamber are made to reach each                target range, and the freshness of plant, such as                fruits and vegetable, in storage is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An air conditioner in a chamber comprises gas                supply apparatus which can supply low oxygen                concentration air and external air into storage for                accommodating the plant for respiration, and                controller which controls operation of gas supply                apparatus, reduces oxygen concentration of air in a                chamber of storage to predetermined oxygen                concentration setting value when gas supply                apparatus supplies low oxygen concentration air                into the chamber of storage and performs air                composition maintenance operation in the chamber                after completion of oxygen concentration reduction                operation. In gas supply apparatus, 1st supply                operation which supplies 1st low oxygen                concentration air into the chamber of storage, 2nd                supply operation which supplies 2nd low oxygen                concentration air lower than low oxygen                concentration air with 1st oxygen concentration                into the chamber of storage, and external air                supply operation which supplies external air into                chamber of storage are performed and operation is                switched at halt condition to maintain supply into                chamber of storage of 1st low oxygen concentration                air, 2nd low oxygen concentration air, and external                air.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRONICS - Preferred Process: The controller is set to                  air composition maintenance operation so that                  oxygen concentration of air in the chamber may                  fall, oxygen concentration setting value may be                  approximated, and operation of gas supply                  apparatus may selectively be switched to one of                  2nd supply operation and standby operation from                  1st supply operation. The controller is set to                  air composition maintenance operation so that                  switching to standby operation from 1st supply                  operation of gas supply apparatus is prohibited                  after predetermined setting conditions until                  predetermined release condition is satisfied, and                  switching from 1st supply operation to 2nd supply                  operation of operation of gas supply apparatus                  may be permitted. In the air composition                  maintenance operation, the controller is                  comprised so that gas supply apparatus may be                  made to perform above the 1st supply operation                  initially. Preferred Conditions: The setting                  conditions are conditions satisfied when a raise                  of carbon dioxide levels of air in the chamber                  needs to be suppressed, when reducing the oxygen                  concentration of air in the chamber. The release                  conditions are conditions satisfied when it is                  not necessary to reduce the oxygen concentration                  of air in the chamber and a raise of carbon                  dioxide levels of air in chamber does not need to                  be suppressed. The setting conditions are                  conditions that oxygen concentration of air in                  the chamber goes into a predetermined oxygen                  concentration range including the oxygen                  concentration setting value, and state in which                  carbon dioxide levels of air in the chamber and                  difference of carbon-dioxide-levels setting value                  are more than 1st predetermined value continues                  over the 1st predetermined time. The oxygen                  concentration of air in chamber of release                  condition is below the oxygen concentration                  setting value. The carbon dioxide levels of air                  in chamber and differences of                  carbon-dioxide-levels setting value are the                  conditions that the state which is below 2nd                  predetermined value smaller than 1st                  predetermined value continues over the 2nd                  predetermined time.
DC P12 (Harvesting (A01D, F).); Q74 (Heating, ranges, ventilating (F24)); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25))
MC P12-T99; Q74-A02B; Q74-A02E; Q75-T06

PT P
PN CN110124484-A
TI Method for persulfate activation combined with UV light-induced degradation of waste gas, involves entering organic waste gas into cylinder, entering into module, spraying persulfate into atomizer followed by adsorbing aerogel in gas.
AU WANG H
   WANG Z
   WU Z
   YE H
   LIU Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201972832L
AB    NOVELTY - An organic waste gas persulfate activation combined with UV light-induced degradation method involves entering organic waste gas to be treated into the reactor cylinder through the air inlet, and filtering particulate matter and moisture in the exhaust gas through the drying module and drying, entering into atomization module, spraying persulfate into atomizer into cylinder through ultrasonic atomization, and ultrasonic atomization forms sulfate radicals to degrade gas. The atomized persulfate droplets are entered into UV light activation module together with the initially degraded exhaust gas, further enhance the generation of free radicals under UV light irradiation, and the exhaust gas is in contact with the foam oxidation ceramic in the UV activation module, and the volatile organic compounds gas molecules, fully acting on the surface of the foamed alumina ceramic with activated sulfate radicals followed by adsorbing aerogel in the exhaust gas by the filter adsorption module.
   USE - Method for persulfate activation combined with UV light-induced degradation of organic waste gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficiently treating organic waste gas containing volatile organic compounds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An organic waste gas persulfate activation combined with UV light-induced degradation method involves entering organic waste gas to be treated into the reactor cylinder through the air inlet, and filtering particulate matter and moisture in the exhaust gas through the drying module and drying, entering into ultrasonic atomization module, spraying persulfate into ultrasonic atomizer into the reactor cylinder through ultrasonic atomization, and ultrasonic atomization forms sulfate radicals to initially degrade the organic waste gas. The atomized persulfate droplets are entered into UV light activation module together with the initially degraded exhaust gas, further enhance the generation of free radicals under UV light irradiation, and the exhaust gas is in contact with the foam oxidation ceramic in the UV activation module, and the volatile organic compounds gas molecules, fully acting on the surface of the foamed alumina ceramic with activated sulfate radicals and oxygen radicals to finally form carbon dioxide and water followed by adsorbing aerogel in the exhaust gas by the filter adsorption module. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The persulfate is sodium persulfate, potassium peroxodisulfate or ammonium persulfate.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E19 (Other organic compounds general - organic compounds of unknown or indefinite structure; general mixtures of many types; organic reactions (e.g. nitration, resolution) when applied generally.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E11-Q01B; E31-E03; E31-N04C; E31-P03; J01-E01; J01-E02A; J01-G03B; J01-G03C

PT P
PN GB2570705-A; WO2019149942-A1; US2020361681-A1; EP3749520-A1
TI Coating composition comprises binder, rheology modifier that acts to increase viscosity of coating composition, and particulate component able to remove volatile organic compound from environment in contact with coating composition.
AU ZEYFERT C
   HEWITT J
   MOFFAT J
   TATLOCK E
   EDKINS A
   MATTHEWS L J D
   PALMER M
AE INNOVIA FILMS LTD (INNI-C)
   FOOD FRESHNESS TECHNOLOGY HOLDINGS LTD (FOOD-Non-standard)
   INNOVIA FILMS LTD (INNI-C)
   FOOD FRESHNESS TECHNOLOGY HOLDINGS LTD (FOOD-Non-standard)
   INNOVIA FILMS LTD (INNI-C)
   FOOD FRESHNESS TECHNOLOGY HOLDINGS LTD (FOOD-Non-standard)
GA 201968376L
AB    NOVELTY - A coating composition comprises binder, rheology modifier (3) that acts to increase viscosity of coating composition, and particulate component (4) able to remove volatile organic compound from environment in contact with coating composition.
   USE - A coating composition for substrate for use in or as a packaging structure of a package (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Increased viscosity resulting from inclusion of rheology modifier stabilizes the dispersion of particulate component in the coating composition, reducing the rate of sedimentation of particulate component, either in the presence or absence of agitation. This ensures equal distribution of particulate component throughout the coating once the coating composition has been applied to a base layer. The functional barrier layer may impart the surface of the substrate with additional beneficial properties, such as receptivity to inks and adhesives, improved optics, improved coefficient of friction, or improved blocking characteristics. The presence of functional barrier between coating and base layer can prevent migration of parts of particulate component through the substrate, which can result in parts escaping the substrate and contacting the contents of the packaging, which may comprise food. The inclusion of rheology modifier in the functional barrier layer between coating and base layer helps to further reduce wicking of water or binder into base layer. Sequential application of multiple layers of coating composition can improve loading of particulate component, increasing the level of particulate component per unit area of substrate. The modified atmosphere packaging may allow creation of reduced oxygen, elevated carbon dioxide, and/or elevated humidity environment in contact with the substrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) substrate for use in or as a packaging structure comprising base layer and coating formed from the coating composition;
   (2) packaging structure comprising the substrate;
   (3) package comprising an item capable of emitting volatile organic compound at least partially enveloped or surrounded by packaging structure; and
   (4) production of substrate which involves applying the coating composition onto the base layer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a substrate comprising base layer and coating.
   Substrate (1)
   Base layer (2)
   Rheology modifier (3)
   Particulate component (4)
   Functional barrier layer (5a, 5b)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Compositions: Binder comprises acrylic and acrylic acid polymers and copolymers, acrylates, urethanes, urethane acrylates, styrene butadiene rubbers, and/or cyclized rubbers. Rheology modifier comprises water-soluble polymers, such as polyvinyl alcohol (PVOH), polyacrylic acid, polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP), and/or cellulose derivatives, such as carboxymethylcellulose (CMC), hydroxypropylcellullose (HPC), and hydroxypropylmethylcellullose (HPMC), preferably non-ionic water-soluble polymers, such as PVOH, PVP, HPC, and/or HPMC. Binder is water-based polymeric dispersion, and rheology modifier is water-soluble polymer. Composition comprises less than or equal to 5 wt.% rheology by dry weight with respect to polymers present in the composition. Base layer comprises polymeric material, plastics material, pulp-based materials, such as paper, card, cardboard, and board, non-woven materials, wood, or metal. Substrate further comprises functional barrier layer between coating and base layer. Functional barrier comprises rheology modifier. Packaging structure further comprises active packaging systems, such as moisture control systems, antimicrobial systems, and/or volatile organic compound blockers. Packaging structure is an active or passive modified atmosphere packaging. Preferred Properties: Rheology modifier has molecular weight of greater than or equal to 1 MDa. Preferred Processes: Coating composition is applied using flexographic or screen printing. A set of layers of coating composition is sequentially applied onto the base layer. The method further includes applying functional barrier layer to base layer before applying the coating composition.
DC A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.); A18 (Addition polymers in general.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); G02 (Inks, paints, polishes â€“ polymer-based paints and inks are also classified in Section A (C09D, F, G).); P73 (Layered products (B32).); Q32 (Container/closure types, special packaging features and transit packaging (B65D))

PN CN109834439-A; CN109834439-B
TI Cladding of wear-resistant materials on surface of coal mining equipment wear-resistant medium plate includes deforming wear-resistant middle plate, using YD55 flux cored electrode as material used for surfacing welding, and cladding.
AU QIN L
   WANG H
   XUE H
   WANG P
   GUO H
   LV H
   HUANG C
   ZHAO H
   LV Y
   CUI C
   GUO X
   LI D
   ZHANG Z
   MAO R
   ZHAO S
   XIN Y
   WU X
   YU Y
   ZHAO W
   ZHAO Z
   ZHANG L
   QIN S
   SUN Y
   LIU W
   YU J
AE SHANDONG MINING MACHINERY HUANENG EQUIP (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201952462S
AB    NOVELTY - Cladding of wear-resistant materials on surface of coal mining equipment wear-resistant medium plate includes preparing plate of wear-resistant medium; deforming wear-resistant middle plate with hydraulic press; cleaning surface, using YD55 flux cored electrode as material used for surfacing welding; cladding firstly one side A of wear resistant medium plate and other side B; placing in gas furnace after cladding at normal temperature, raising temperature, insulating, cooling, discharging, using air for natural cooling; and aligning.
   USE - The method is cladding of wear-resistant materials on surface of coal mining equipment wear-resistant medium plate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can prevent surface of wear-resistant intermediate plate from cracking, faulting and peeling after cladding of wear-resistant material, and enhances wear-resisting effect of wear-resistant medium plate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method includes preparing plate of wear-resistant medium; pressing wear-resistant middle plate with hydraulic press to reversely deform, bending to back surface of wear-resistant material; cleaning surface of wear-resistant medium plate, using YD55 flux cored electrode as material used for surfacing welding at 240-260 A, 26-28 V and gas flow 20-25 L; taking center line a of wear resistant medium plate, one end as melting start end and other end as cladding termination end, using carbon dioxide protection welder, cladding firstly one side A of wear resistant medium plate line a by starting from outer edge of wear-resistant medium plate, fusing from start end of cladding to termination end of cladding and cladding in same direction for times until center line a of wear resistant intermediate plate is welded, and cladding other side B in same manner and completing cladding of wear-resistant medium plate; placing in gas furnace after cladding at normal temperature, raising temperature at 100 degrees C/hour up to 550 plus minus 15 degrees C, insulating for 2-5 hours, cooling at 50 degrees C/hour to below 300 degrees C, discharging, using air for natural cooling; and aligning.
DC M23 (Soldering; welding - including brazing, flame cutting and scarfing, cutting and welding rods, soldering and unsoldering apparatus and solder compositions (B23K).); P56 (Machine tools (B23P,Q).); X24 (Electric Welding)

PN WO2019101997-A1; CA3083550-A1; CN111386060-A; KR2020091436-A; EP3716823-A1; US2021000289-A1; JP2021504252-W; KR2323952-B1; EP3716823-B1; JP7016954-B2; CA3083550-C; ES2913264-T3; CN111386060-B
TI Machine for preparing cold beverage e.g. caffeine,            used in system, has light element that is designed to            be partially enclosing cartridge receptacle            accommodated in holding unit.
AU KRUEGER M
   FISCHER D
   EMPL G
   KRUGER M
   KRUG M
AE FREEZIO AG (FREE-Non-standard)
   FREEZIO AG (FREE-Non-standard)
   FREEZIO AG (FREE-Non-standard)
   FREEZIO AG (FREE-Non-standard)
GA 2019484262
AB    NOVELTY - The machine (3) has a holding unit (90) that                is inserted with a cartridge receptacle connected                with a cartridge. A cartridge system is inserted                with the cartridge into the machine. The holding                unit is provided with a light element (92) which is                designed in such a way the light element partially                encloses the cartridge receptacle accommodated in                the holding unit. The light element completely                encloses the cartridge receptacle accommodated in                the holding unit so that the circulating light                element is extended in a horizontal plane                perpendicular to a longitudinal axis of the                cartridge.
   USE - Machine for preparing beverage like cold                beverage or refreshment beverage such as caffeine,                carbon dioxide, fruit and/or sugar-containing soda                and juice, beer or other alcoholic or non-alcoholic                beverage, used in system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Since the cartridge receptacles are provided                with reflecting markings so that correct                positioning of the cartridge receptacle is informed                to the user when inserting the cartridge receptacle                by light reflections. Since the cartridge is made                transparent, the user is allowed to see the amount                of beverage substance available. The changes in the                beverage substance are detected by illuminating the                beverage substance so that dilution or flocculation                in the beverage substance is detected easily. Thus                the conclusion about the shelf life of the beverage                substance is drawn. Hence the operation of the                beverage preparation machine is facilitated. The                holding unit, the cartridge receptacle and the                cartridge are homogeneously illuminated so that the                unwanted shadows are avoided. Since the positive                and/or non-positive connection of the holding unit                with the cartridge receptacle is made through the                mounting flange, the secure fitting of the                cartridge receptacle in the holding unit is                ensured. The damage of the piercing unit is                prevented. Since the light element illuminates the                product identification code to be read out, the                cause of error and disturbance during readout is                reduced especially in poor lighting                conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a system; and
   (2) a method for making beverage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show the schematic views of the                beverage preparation machine without inserted                cartridge receptacle.
   Beverage preparation machine (3)
   Holding unit (90)
   Light element (92)
   Hand lever (120)

PT P
PN EP3460105-A1
TI Method for recovering lead and silicates from lead-containing glass, involves filtering second solution to extract metallic lead contained in it, with proviso that glass is not derived from cathode ray tube.
AU VOLAKAKIS E
AE EVH SRL (EVHE-Non-standard)
GA 201927831U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves grinding the glass to obtain a powder of grain size between 1 mm and 200 mm. The glass is attacked with a strongly alkaline solution at a temperature of at least 180 degree C. A first solution containing soluble silicates is extracting from the cell. The first portion of the first solution is united with the insoluble silicates particulate. The obtained mixture is recirculated. The second portion of the first solution is titrated to determine the concentration of lead ion. The second portion of the first solution is treated with metallic zinc for the reduction of lead ion to metallic lead. The second solution is filtered to extract the metallic lead contained in it, with the proviso that the glass is not derived from a cathode ray tube.
   USE - Method for recovering lead and silicates from lead-containing glass.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows an essentially complete recovery of lead from glass containing it, other than lead-glass from CRTs, in addition to the recovery of soluble silicates present in the original glass.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A first suspension consisting of a waterbased liquid phase containing soluble silicates and lead, and a particulate of insoluble silicates are obtained. The first suspension is filtered to separate the liquid phase from the particulate. The water-based liquid phase is treated in an electrochemical cell with deposit of metallic lead onto the cathode. The process is carried out at a temperature between 180 and 240 degree C with an aqueous solution of a hydroxide of an alkali metal. The electrodes of the cell electrically connected in parallel between them. The deposited lead is peeled off from the cathode by periodic inversion of the polarity of the electrodes, after electrolysis periods lasting between 10 and 60 minutes. The amount of first solution united to the particulate is between 20 and 40% of first solution. The acid is added to the second solution from which lead is removed with precipitation of pure silica. The acid is carbon dioxide. The recovery of silica is performed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for recovering lead and silicates from lead-containing glass.

PN RU2017133281-A
TI Mine furnace for burning bulk material to produce saturation gas used for cleaning diffusion juice comprises a cylindrical lined mine, loading and unloading devices, furnace gas suction box, fan for supplying air to furnace and vortex tube.
AU KOBELEV N S
   EMELYANOV S G
   PAVLOV E V
   PAVLOV I V
AE UNIV SOUTHWESTERN (UYTH-C)
GA 201932223X
AB    NOVELTY - Mine furnace for burning bulk material to produce saturation gas used for cleaning diffusion juice, comprises a cylindrical lined mine, loading and unloading devices, furnace gas suction box, gas pump which is connected by suction pipe with a furnace gas box and discharge pipe with collector furnace gas saturation and is equipped with a drive with rotation speed regulator and control unit, fan for supplying air to furnace, vortex tube with inlet at its cold end connected to discharge pipe of gas pump and outlets to divert a cold and hot stream of carbon dioxide, condensate collector with a device for removing condensed moisture and connected inlet with outlet at cold end of vortex tube, and control unit that contains a temperature controller with temperature sensor and flow controller with a flow sensor, where each of regulators consists of comparison and setting units, electronic amplifier with nonlinear feedback unit and magnetic amplifier connected to a rotational speed regulator.
   USE - Mine furnace for burning bulk material to produce saturation gas used for cleaning diffusion juice.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The lined shaft in upper portion is connected to hollow shaft asymmetrically located above the loading device, wind wheel and impeller are fixed on the hollow shaft, and wind wheel blades and wings of impeller form a tapering rotation cone, and connecting elements in the amount of not less than 4 between upper portion of the cylindrical lined shaft and hollow shaft are placed along its perimeter. There are outlet windows between the wind wheel and impeller besides the outer surface of cylindrical lined shaft that is covered with fine-fibrous basalt material made in the form of twisted bundles located elongated in height from the duct operating in the mode of air supply from the fan to the boot device, which is different in that the inner surface of the exhaust ports located between the wind wheel and impeller on the hollow shaft that is covered by a glassy film of tantalum oxide formed by ion-plasma method.

PN US2019047363-A1; DE102017118425-A1; EP3444542-A1; CN109383226-A; EP3444542-B1; US11479083-B2
TI Flow circuit system for vehicle has outside heat            exchanger connected through which second fluid flows            and not connected to second flow circuit in first flow            circuit mode and second flow circuit mode,            respectively.
AU SONNEKALB M
   FINK S
   FUNK S
AE KONVEKTA AG (KONV-Non-standard)
   KONVEKTA AG (KONV-Non-standard)
   KONVEKTA AG (KONV-Non-standard)
   KONVEKTA AG (KONV-Non-standard)
GA 201914337U
AB    NOVELTY - The flow circuit system (1) has at least one                first heat exchanger (7) arranged in the provided                flow direction of first fluid, downstream of the at                least one compressor (3) and upstream of the at                least one expansion element (15) and to which first                fluid is deliverable to implement heat exchange                connection with a second flow circuit (50). A                switching device (35) is provided in second flow                circuit and has at least two flow circuit modes.                The at least one outside heat exchanger (37) is                connected through which the second fluid flows                apart from the at least one conveyor device (31)                for second fluid and the first heat exchanger in a                first flow circuit mode. The outside heat exchanger                is not connected to second flow circuit, which                contains the conveyor device and first heat                exchanger in a second flow circuit mode. The first                fluid is carbon dioxide, The second fluid is                water.
   USE - Flow circuit system for vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - Allows a greater cooling performance since                effect of the at least one first heat exchanger as                a gas cooler or condenser can be advantageously                increased if the first flow circuit is operated for                cooling the motor vehicle. The indirect increase in                the cooling effect on the first fluid is achieved                with the arrangement of the at least one outside                heat exchanger in the second flow circuit. The                combining arrangement of the flow circuit system is                feasible in which the at least one outside heat                exchanger can be switched between parallel and                series arrangement with a switching device by e.g.                three-way valve, thus permits the advantages of                both arrangements of the at least one outside heat                exchanger in the first flow circuit mode of second                flow circuit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of operating a flow circuit system for a                vehicle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a main schematic diagram of                the flow circuit system for a vehicle.
   Flow circuit system (1)
   Compressor (3)
   First heat exchanger (7)
   Expansion element (15)
   Conveyor device (31)
   Outside heat exchanger (37)
   Second flow circuit (50)

PT P
PN CN109315486-A; CN109315486-B
TI Vegetable and fruit preservative comprises activating component comprising carrier, water absorbent and acid adsorbent, and paste matrix comprising matrix, regulator, sodium chlorite, carbonate, dispersant, metal salt and water.
AU WU M
   LIU X
   GUO X
   REN T
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY PANJIN IND TECHNO (UYDA-Non-standard)
   SHENZHEN XIAOMO INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201916804V
AB    NOVELTY - Vegetable and fruit preservative comprises an activating component and a paste matrix, where the activating component comprises 1 pt. wt. carrier, 0.2-1 pt. wt. water absorbent and 0.5-1 pt. wt. acid adsorbent. The paste matrix comprises 1 pt. wt. matrix, 0.1-0.5 pt. wt. regulator, 0.1-1 pt. wt. sodium chlorite, 0.1-1 pt. wt. carbonate, 0.05-0.5 pt. wt. dispersant, 0.01-0.1 pt. wt. metal salt and 1-5 pts. wt. water.
   USE - As vegetable and fruit preservative.
   ADVANTAGE - The preservative releases chlorine dioxide and carbon dioxide uniformly over a long period of time, delays the spoilage, mildew, deterioration and over-ripening problems, controls fungal diseases of growing crops in greenhouse and fruits and vegetables in storage and transportation, reduces fruit rotting rate, sugar and water loss, maintains the quality of the product and reduces the cost of storage and transportation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing the preservative involving (a) soaking activated carbon and/or montmorillonite in 50-500 mg/l citric acid, malic acid, sodium bisulfate, sulfamic acid, benzenesulfonic acid and sodium dihydrogen phosphate, dripping for 10-30 minutes, carrying out freezing or vacuum drying and grinding to 50-200 mesh to obtain the acid adsorbent, (b) mixing carrier, water absorbent and the acid adsorbent and placing in a polyvinyl alcohol water-soluble bag and (c) dissolving regulator, sodium chlorite, carbonate, dispersant and metal salt in water, mixing with matrix, uniformly stirring and absorbing and storing the mixture in an opaque plastic box to obtain the paste matrix; and
   (2) a fruit and vegetable preservative box comprising a opaque box separated by a removable hard polyethylene film, where the upper cover and upper space side wall of the box has a small holes with the diameter of 0.5-2 mm and the vegetable and fruit preservative is encapsulated in a box, the paste matrix is encapsulated in a lower layer of the box and the activating component is encapsulated on the upper layer of the box.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The regulator comprises polyethylene glycol 200-800, borax and boric acid. The water absorbent comprises calcium oxide, anhydrous calcium chloride, magnesium chloride, zinc chloride, manganese nitrate and/or magnesium perchlorate. The metal salt comprises potassium sulfate, sodium sulfate, sodium chloride, potassium chloride, calcium chloride and calcium sulfate. The carbonate comprises sodium carbonate, calcium carbonate, carbonic acid and/or magnesium carbonate. The dispersant is quartz sand and/or granular activated carbon.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acidic adsorbent is impregnated with citric acid, malic acid, sodium bisulfate, sulfamic acid, benzenesulfonic acid, sodium dihydrogen phosphate and activated carbon and/or montmorillonite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carrier is a resin. The matrix is a resin.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC A04-G02E2; A05-H03; A10-E09B; A12-P02; A12-W09; D03-A04; D03-A05

PT P
PN CN109275759-A
TI Flavored flower tea comprises honeysuckle, Chrysanthemum flower, peach blossom, rose, jasmine flower, lotus, sweet Osmanthus, mint leaf, rock sugar and wolfberry.
AU WANG X
   MENG S
   SONG Z
AE ANHUI YUANHETANG PHARM CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201915983G
AB    NOVELTY - Flavored flower tea comprises 3-5 pts. wt. honeysuckle, 3-5 pts. wt. Chrysanthemum flower, 3-5 pts. wt. peach blossom, 8-9 pts. wt. rose, 5-6 pts. wt. jasmine flower, 2-3 pts. wt. lotus, 5-6 pts. wt. sweet Osmanthus, 2-3 pts. wt. mint leaf, 2-3 pts. wt. rock sugar and 1-2 pts. wt. wolfberry.
   USE - As flavored flower tea.
   ADVANTAGE - The tea is prepared by simple method, has long-lasting fragrance and is more healthy and natural.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the tea involving (a) sequentially cleaning honeysuckle, Chrysanthemum flower, peach blossom, rose, jasmine flower, lotus, mint leaf and sweet Osmanthus, placing on an air drying net and drying, (b) mixing the air dried rose and sweet Osmanthus, crushing and using a conventional supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction method to obtain a volatile substance of rose and sweet Osmanthus to obtain a concentrated solution of rose and sweet Osmanthus, (c) mixing honeysuckle, Chrysanthemum flower, peach blossom, jasmine flower and mint leaf and crushing by a crusher to obtain a mixture, (d) kneading the mixture with a kneading machine for 20-50 minutes, dividing the kneaded mixture into a spherical shape with the diameter of 10 cm, pressing the spherical mixture into a circular sheet with the thickness of 3-5 mm and forming a crisscrossed groove on the surface of the sheet, (e) uniformly spraying the concentrated solution on the upper and lower surface of the circular sheet, uniformly placing the circular sheet in a closed container, leaving still at 70-80 degrees C for 3-5 hours, so that the mixture fully absorbs the flower fragrance of the roses and sweet Osmanthus and maintaining the whole aromatization process at 3-4 atmospheric pressure, (f) mixing the aromatized mixture with wolfberry, drying and controlling the moisture content within 6%, (g) placing the dried mixture into UV disinfection equipment to-be-disinfected and sterilizing for 30 minutes to obtain a sterilized mixed powder and (h) sealing and packaging the sterilized mixed powder and adding 1-2 g rock sugar into each bag, where the total weight of each bag is 5-6 g to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Properties: The rock sugar is a cube-shaped block having a side length of 5 mm. Preferred Components: The lotus is a blue flower and purple with large flower shape and full ovary development, where the picking time is in the morning of a second day of flowering. Preferred Method: The step (d) involves kneading the mixture without loosing the juice, where the damage rate of the tea cell is 80-90%.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D03-D02; D03-D03B
IP A23F-003/34

PT P
PN CN109261199-A; CN109261199-B
TI Catalyst used e.g. to directly convert high            stability synthesis gas to low carbon olefins prepared            by mixing and grinding zirconium dioxide-zinc oxide            metal oxide and microporous silica-alumina molecular            sieve.
AU MENG F
   LI X
   LI Z
   ZHANG P
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201911485J
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst prepared by manually mixing and                grinding the two components A and B according to a                mass ratio of 0.4:1-2:1, where the component A is                zirconium dioxide-zinc oxide metal oxide                composition having a zirconium/zinc molar ratio of                2:1, the component B is a microporous                silica-alumina molecular sieve metal doped pure                silicon silicalite-1 molecular sieve which is                coated with microporous silica-alumina molecular                sieve metal of a CHA structure, and the mass ratio                of microporous silica-alumina molecular sieve metal                and pure silicon silicalite-1 of the CHA structure                in the component B is 5-1:1.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for directly converting                high stability synthesis gas to low carbon olefins                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: improves the selectivity of                low-carbon olefins, reduces the carbon dioxide                selectivity of by-products and extends the catalyst                life; and has adjustable acidity, rich pore                structure and large specific surface area.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing the catalyst comprising (a)                preparing a zirconium dioxide-zinc oxide metal                oxide composition having a zirconium/zinc molar                ratio of 2:1, (b) preparing a coated structure of                microporous silica-alumina molecular sieve metal                doped pure silicon silicalite-1 molecular sieve,                where metal is silicoaluminophosphate (SAPO-34)                molecular sieve or aluminosilicate zeolite (SSZ-13)                molecular sieve, and (c) manually mixing the                zirconium dioxide-zinc oxide metal oxide                composition and coated structure of microporous                silica-alumina molecular sieve metal doped pure                silicon silicalite-1 molecular sieve in a mass                ratio of 0.4:1-2:1, grinding, pressing and sieving;                and
   (2) using catalyst for directly converting the                high stability synthesis gas to low carbon olefins                comprising firstly reducing the catalyst, then                applying the reduced catalyst to a synthesis gas,                where the conditions for the catalytic reaction                comprises reacting at 380-430 degrees C under the                pressure of 5-30 bar, the molar ratio of the                reaction gas hydrogen/carbon monoxide is 1-2.5, and                the gas space velocity is 3000-5000                ml/(g.hour).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The microporous                  silica-alumina molecular sieve metal doped pure                  silicon silicalite-1 molecular sieve is prepared                  by (i) firstly synthesizing microporous                  silica-alumina molecular sieve metal, then                  neutralizing the synthesized molecular sieve by                  washing, drying, and calcining at 500 degrees C                  for 6 hours, (ii) adding the calcined metal                  molecular sieve to 1 mol/l tetrapropylammonium                  bromide and stirring at room temperature for 3                  hours for functional grouping to obtain a                  functionalized metal molecular sieve, (iii)                  preparing tetraethylammonium hydroxide,                  tetraethyl orthosilicate and deionized aqueous                  solution at room temperature, and stirring for                  0.5-6 hours to obtain transparent sol, (iv)                  adding the functionalized metal molecular sieve                  obtained in the step (ii) to the transparent sol                  obtained in the step (iii), stirring and curing                  for 1-8 hours, then transferring to a                  hydrothermal crystallization vessel and                  crystallizing at 170-210 degrees C for 24-72                  hours, and (v) neutralizing the crystallized                  product obtained in the step (iv) by washing,                  drying, and then calcining at 500-550 degrees C                  for 3-10 hours. The conditions for reducing the                  catalyst comprises reducing at 360-440 degrees C,                  in an hydrogen/nitrogen atmosphere of 0.4-2 for                  1-10 hours. Preferred Components: The microporous                  silica-alumina molecular sieve metal of the CHA                  structure has a specific surface area of more                  than 400 m2/g and the particle size of 0.5-2 mu                  m. The microporous silica-alumina molecular sieve                  metal of the CHA structure is a SAPO-34 molecular                  sieve or SSZ-13 molecular sieve. The silicon                  dioxide/aluminum oxide molar ratio in the SAPO-34                  molecular sieve is 0.05-0.5. The silicon                  dioxide/aluminum oxide molar ratio in the SSZ-13                  molecular sieve is 15-40. The mass fraction ratio                  of tetraethylammonium hydroxide, tetraethyl                  orthosilicate and deionized water in the step                  (iii) is 6-15:15.6:50-140.
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN WO2019018411-A1; US2019044304-A1; CA3069723-A1; CN110959234-A; EP3656027-A1; US10714893-B2; US2020343687-A1; EP3656027-A4; CN110959234-B; US11289876-B2
TI Optically pumped vertical cavity laser for use in            system for providing optical spectroscopy, has periodic            gain active region provided with type-1 quantum wells,            and comprising barrier region adjacent to wells and            absorbing pump wavelength.
AU JAYARAMAN V
   LASCOLA K
   SEGAL S
   TOWNER F
   CABLE A
   SIEGEL S
   KOBER A
AE THORLABS INC (THRL-C)
   PRAEVIUM RES INC (PRAE-Non-standard)
   PRAEVIUM RES INC (PRAE-Non-standard)
GA 201909599R
AB    NOVELTY - The laser has a periodic gain active region                provided with type-1 quantum wells (210). The                periodic gain active region comprises a barrier                region (220) adjacent to the type-1 quantum wells                and absorbing pump wavelength. A cladding region                (230) is arranged adjacent to the barrier region,                which is transparent at the pump wavelength. The                quantum wells are compressively strained with                strain in a range of about 1-2%. The barrier region                comprises quinary                aluminum-indium-gallium-arsenic-antimony. The                cladding region comprises                aluminum-arsenic-antimony. A wafer-bonded interface                is formed between the active region and first and                second quarter wave wafer-bonded distributed Bragg                reflector (DBR) mirrors (240, 250).
   USE - Optically pumped vertical cavity laser for use                in a system for providing optical spectroscopy to                probe optical property e.g. constituent                concentration and gas concentration, of a sample                e.g. methane, ethane, ammonia, carbon dioxide,                water vapor, HF vapor, nitrous oxide, acetylene,                carbonyl sulfide, dimethyl sulfide, hydrogen                cyanide, ozone, carbon monoxide and gas, existing                within a civil structure e.g. airport terminal,                sports stadium, park and large public space (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The laser has less absorption and allows less                heating for a given amount of absorbed power, thus                resulting in higher output power when sufficient                pump power is available. The laser uses the quantum                wells with barriers and 1.55 micrometer range                pumping in a design to provide adequate gain and                adequate absorption length, thus enabling efficient                use of available pump power. The laser preferably                adjusts an amount of quinary material to obtain                single-pass absorption efficiency of the active                region of about 40-80% of pump light, thus giving                efficient use of pump energy in combination with                relatively uniform pumping of quantum wells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                system for providing optical spectroscopy to probe                optical property of a sample.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of a                laser structure.
   Type-1 quantum wells (210)
   Barrier region (220)
   Cladding region (230)
   Quarter wave wafer-bonded DBR mirrors (240,                250)
   Substrate (260)
   Standing wave (270)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The laser has an interfacial oxide layer                  selected from a group consisting of aluminum                  oxide, silicon dioxide, gallium oxide, arsenic                  oxide, indium oxide and antimony oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - The type-1 quantum wells containing indium,                  gallium, arsenic and antimony.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation); V08 (Lasers and Masers); W04 (Audio/Video Recording and Systems)

PT P
PN CN109047999-A
TI Method for single-weld full penetration welding of half V-shaped groove, involves using molten-polar active gas to protect arc welding process, using welding material and performing cover welding on filling bead by using positive current.
AU YANG H
   XIE Y
   WANG K
   CHU Y
   FENG Z
   ZHAO X
   FANG X
   CHENG J
AE CRRC NANJING PUZHEN CO LTD (CRRC-C)
GA 201902295E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves fixing (1) the weldment 1 and the weldment 2, the weldment 1 and the weldment 2 are perpendicular to each other. The surface of the weldment 2 adjacent to the weldment is provided with a slope. A welding material is used (2) with a diameter of 2.4 mm, the root of the semi-V-groove is single-sided welded with a negative current. A welding material is used with a diameter of 1.2 mm, and a positive current is used to fill and weld the bottoming bead. A molten-polar active gas is used to protect the arc welding process. A welding material is used (3) with a diameter of 1.2 mm. Cover welding is performed on the filling bead by using a positive current. The welding current intensity is 230-270A, and the arc voltage is 30-31V. The welding speed is 4.7-5.8mm/s. The welding is carried out (4) with argon gas with a gas flow rate of 15-18L/min, purity of 80% and carbon dioxide with a purity of 20% to form the cover weld.
   USE - Method for single-weld full penetration welding of half V-shaped groove.
   ADVANTAGE - The qualification rate of one welding is over 95% which solves the problems of low qualification rate of the existing welding method, waste of manpower and material resources for rework, and is obtained in the actual production process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating the method for single-face welding full penetration welding of half V-shaped groove. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for fixing the weldment 1 and the weldment 2, the weldment 1 and the weldment 2 are perpendicular to each other (1)
   Step for using welding material with specific diameter, the root of the semi-V-groove is single-sided welded with a negative current (2)
   Step for using welding material with a diameter of 1.2 mm and performing cover welding on the filling bead by using a positive current (3)
   Step for carrying out welding with argon gas (4)
DC M23 (Soldering; welding - including brazing, flame cutting and scarfing, cutting and welding rods, soldering and unsoldering apparatus and solder compositions (B23K).); X24 (Electric Welding)
MC M23-H; X24-B06
IP B23K-009/167; B23K-009/173

PT P
PN CN109010429-A; CN109010429-B
TI Traditional Chinese medicine formulation used e.g. for dispelling wind and relieving itching, comprises Sophora flower, mint, rhizoma atractylodis, broom cypress fruit, and excrementum bombycis meal.
AU DUAN J
   QIAN D
   GUO S
   XIU S
   WANG L
   SU S
AE UNIV NANJING CHINESE MEDICINE (UNCM-C)
GA 2018A5442C
AB    NOVELTY - Traditional Chinese medicine formulation comprises 4-8 pts. wt. Sophora flower, 4-8 pts. wt. mint, 4-8 pts. wt. rhizoma atractylodis, 6-14 pts. wt. broom cypress fruit, and 6-14 pts. wt. excrementum bombycis meal.
   USE - Traditional Chinese medicine formulation used for dispelling wind and relieving itching. It is used for preparing medicine for preventing and treating eczema, urticaria and skin pruritus (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The traditional Chinese medicine formulation has compatibility, quick effect and long-lasting effect. The microemulsion and cream have good solubilizing ability for water soluble active component and fat-soluble hair care oil component.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the traditional Chinese medicine formulation comprising using supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) fluid extraction to extract the mint and rhizoma atractylodis volatile oil by crushing mint and rhizoma atractylodis, sieving, putting in supercritical extraction kettle, under constant temperature and constant pressure, conducting circulation extracting for 2-4 hours to obtain brown volatile oil, adding the mint and rhizoma atractylodis residue with Sophora flower, broom cypress fruit and excrementum bombycis meal, adding 8-16 times by volume of 40-80% alcohol solution, reflux extracting for 1-3 times, each for 1-3 hours, combining the extract liquids, decompressing and concentrating, filtering to obtain alcohol extract, taking the volatile oil, adding oil phase material to prepare oil phase, adding alcohol extract into the water phase material to prepare water phase, adding mixed surfactant, stirring uniformly, at room temperature, stirring while adding the water phase in the oil phase to obtain clear and transparent microemulsion, or weighing oil phase auxiliary material, water bath heating to dissolve, obtaining oil phase, water bath heating the alcohol extract and water phase auxiliary material to dissolve and obtain water phase, adding mixed surfactant and stirring uniformly, adding the water phase into the oil phase to trickle under stirring, constant temperature heating under water bath and stirring, continuously stirring at room temperature, cooling naturally, adding the volatile oil, reducing the stirring rate, and cooling to room temperature to obtain a cream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The oil phase material in the microemulsion comprises soybean oil, peanut oil, olive oil, wheat germ oil, castor oil, oleic acid, linoleic acid, ethyl linoleate, isopropyl myristate, isopropyl laurate, triglyceride of medium fat chain length (8-18C), and/or preferably ethyl oleate. The water phase auxiliary material comprises water, glycerin, sorbitol, propylene glycol, benzoic acid, nipagin, sulfite, pyrosulfite, and/or bisulfite. The water phase material of the cream comprises water, glycerin, sorbitol, propylene glycol, benzoic acid, nipagin, sulfite, pyrosulfite, and/or bisulfite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The mixed surfactant in the microemulsion comprises surfactant and cosurfactant at 2:1 mass ratio, where surfactant comprises polyoxyethylene fatty acid ester, polyoxyethylene fatty alcohol ether, polyoxyethylene-polyoxypropylene copolymer, sucrose fatty acid ester, polyoxyethylene castor oil, polyoxyethylene hydrogenated castor oil, Tween 80 (RTM: polyoxyethylene (20) sorbitan monooleate), Peregal O (RTM: fatty alcohol-polyoxyethylene ether), and/or preferably polysorbate 80, and the cosurfactant comprises ethanol, isopropanol, propylene glycol, n-butyl alcohol, glycol, polyethylene glycol-400/600, and/or preferably polyethylene glycol-400. The oil phase auxiliary material of the cream includes beeswax, ozocerite, spermaceti, stearyl alcohol, cetylalcohol, stearic acid, glycerin monostearate, liquid paraffin, Vaseline (RTM: petroleum jelly based product), and/or lanolin. The mixed surfactant in the cream comprises surfactant and cosurfactant at 3:2 mass ratio, where surfactant comprises sodium dodecyl sulfate, polysorbate, polyoxyethylene fatty acid ester, polyoxyethylene fatty alcohol ether, polyoxyethylene-polyoxypropylene copolymer, sucrose fatty acid ester, polyoxyethylene castor oil, polyoxyethylene hydrogenated castor oil, Tween 80 (RTM: polyoxyethylene (20) sorbitan monooleate), and/or Peregal O (RTM: fatty alcohol-polyoxyethylene ether), and the cosurfactant comprises ethylene glycol distearate, propylene monostearate, glycerin monostearate, and/or diglyceryl monooleate. The supercritical extraction conditions comprise carbon dioxide (CO2) flow rate of 30-60 ml/minute, extraction kettle pressure of 15-40 MPa, preferably 30 MPa and temperature of 40-60 degrees C, preferably 40 degrees C, first separation temperature of 30-40 degrees C, first separation pressure of 6-10 MPa, second separation temperature of 25-40 degrees C, second separation pressure of 4-8 MPa, and extraction time of 3 hours.
   ACTIVITY - Antipruritic; Dermatological; Antiinflammatory; Antiallergic.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); ; D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)
MC B04-A08; B04-A10; B04-A98; B04-B01C1; B04-B04B2; B10-C04E2; B10-C04E3; B10-G02; B12-M02B; B14-G02A; B14-N17C; B14-N17G; B14-S18; D08-B03C; D08-B09A1; A12-V01

PT P
PN CN108862865-A; CN208747869-U
TI Wastewater treatment system comprises dosing tank, air pump, aeration device, coagulation tank, sedimentation tank, hydrolysis acidification tank and biological contact oxidation tank.
AU CHEN G
   PENG J
AE GUANGDONG SHUNDE ENVIRONMENTAL SCI CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201895948S
AB    NOVELTY - Wastewater treatment system comprises a dosing tank (2), an air pump (13), an aeration device (14), a coagulation tank (17), a sedimentation tank (18), a hydrolysis acidification tank (19), and a biological contact oxidation tank, where the production wastewater is accessed through the water inlet pipe. The coagulation tank is provided for receiving coagulant through the dosing pump to carry out mixing reaction, where the waste water in the coagulation tank enters the sedimentation tank from the inlet hole of the coagulation tank through the pipeline from the inlet hole of the sedimentation tank. The suspended solids are precipitated in the pool. The bottom of the sedimentation tank is provided with a drain pipe. The upper portion of the sedimentation tank is provided with a sedimentation tank outlet. The bottom of the concrete tank is provided with a drain pipe for discharging coagulation sedimentation sludge, where the sedimentation tank is discharged.
   USE - Wastewater treatment system.
   ADVANTAGE - The wastewater treatment system improves removal efficiency of waste water organic matter.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The water outlet of the sedimentation tank passes through the pipeline from the water inlet of the hydrolysis acidification tank to the hydrolysis acidification tank. The top of the hydrolysis acidification tank is provided with a gas collection hole, where the carbon dioxide and methane generated in the hydrolysis acidification tank escape from the gas collection hole to realize gas-liquid separation. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for treating wastewater treatment system in the production of the waterborne furniture paint, which involves:
   (A) passing production wastewater into coagulation tank through inlet pipe;
   (B) pumping the coagulant into the coagulation tank through dosing pump;
   (C) entering the wastewater from the coagulation tank from the water inlet of the sedimentation tank through the pipeline;
   (D) entering the effluent from the sedimentation tank into hydrolysis acidification tank through the pipeline outlet; and
   (E) passing the effluent of the hydrolysis acidification tank through the pipeline of the hydrolysis acidification tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the wastewater treatment system in the production of a waterborne furniture paint. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Dosing tank (2)
   Air pump (13)
   Aeration device (14)
   Coagulation tank (17)
   Sedimentation tank (18)
   Hydrolysis acidification tank (19)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The agitation tank is provided with a stirrer. The agitator is a mechanical agitator.
