PN CN108722499-A
TI Preparing titanate ink comprises e.g. dissolving the salt compound of the metal A in the organic solvent B to obtain an organic solution, adding tetraisopropyl titanate and acetylacetone to the organic solvent obtained and stirring.
AU ZHOU H
   CHEN L
   TANG X
   XIE P
   FAN T
AE UNIV SHANGHAI JIAOTONG (USJT-C)
GA 201887338V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing titanate ink comprises (i) dissolving the salt compound of the metal A in the organic solvent B to obtain an organic solution, where the metal A is strontium, calcium, and lead, and the organic solvent is ethanol, isopropanol, and n-butanol, (ii) adding tetraisopropyl titanate and acetylacetone to the organic solvent obtained in the step (i), stirring to obtain a mixed solution I, (iii) uniformly mixing the surfactant C in the mixed solution I to obtain mixed solution (II), (iv) adding nano silica particles to the mixed solution II, and ultrasonically cleaning to obtain mixed solution (III), (v) adding polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP) and concentrated ammonia water to the mixed solution III to obtain a mixed solution IV, and (vi) concentrating the mixed solution IV volatile solvent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing titanate ink.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: improves the performance of the carbon dioxide photoreduction of the material to produce hydrocarbons, and controls structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the three-dimensional structure of titanate artificial photosynthetic system, comprising (a) titanate ink to a 30 mL polypropylene 3D printing cartridge, mounting to a 3D printing device and transmitting the gas pressure device connection, connecting the borosilicate needle to the 3D printing cartridge, (b) drawing the three-dimensional model by writing G-code code to set the printing path to print the titanate ink into a three-dimensional structure, and performimg printing process on the planar Vaseline substrate at 20-30 degrees C, the humidity is 30-50%, the pressure is 0.1-0.3 mPa, the moving speed of the needle is 0.5-3 mm/seconds, (c) transferring the three-dimensional structure of the printed structure to the blast drying oven at 20-40 degrees C, carrying out insulation for 12-24 hours, (d) calcining the three-dimensional structure of titanate at 500-650 degrees C for 4-6 hours, (e) immersing the three-dimensional structure after calcination into 6 mol/l potassium hydroxide solution and placing it in a hydrothermal kettle, incubating at 160-200 degrees C for 48-72 hours, washing and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surfactant C in the step (iii) is triblock copolymer Pluronic F127 (F127), triblock copolymer-Pluronic P123 (P123), sodium dodecyl sulfate (SDS), Cetyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTAB), or dodecylbenzenesulfonic acid (DBSA). The molar ratio of the tetraisopropyl titanate to the metal A is consistent with the atomic ratio of the corresponding element of the obtained titanate. The mass ratio of the salt compound of the metal A to the organic solvent is 10-80%. The titanate IV The molar ratio of isopropyl ester to acetylacetone is 50%. The mass ratio of the surfactant C to tetraisopropyl titanate is 5-25%. The mass ratio of the nano silica particles to the tetraisopropyl titanate is 1-10%. The mass ratio of the polyvinylpyrrolidone to tetraisopropyl titanate is 4-6%. The mass ratio of the concentrated ammonia water to tetraisopropyl titanate is 1-2%. The nano silica particle size in the step (iv) is 15 nm, 30 nm or 50 nm. The titanate ink in the step (a) is the above mentioned titanate inks. Preferred Method: The amount of metal A added in step (i) is 0, and the titanate ink obtained in step (vi) is titanium dioxide ink. When the volume of the concentrate in the step (vi) reaches 10-20% of the volume of the original mixed solution, it can be used as a titanate ink. The titanate ink is semi-solid, and its storage modulus and loss modulus are both on the order of 10-4 Pa, and the storage modulus is greater than the loss modulus.

TI Biodegradable plastic masterbatch comprises e.g.            starch, polypropylene carbonate, polyhydroxybutyrate            valeric acid copolyester, magnesium stearate, epoxy            soybean oil, glycerin, hexamethylene diisocyanate,            2-phenyl-2-oxazoline and lubricant.
AU LIU X
   CHEN T
   YU J
   ZHANG S
   LV G
   WANG P
   ZHENG J
AE SUZHOU HANFENG NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2018842792
AB    NOVELTY - Biodegradable plastic masterbatch comprises                55-60 pts. wt. starch, 25-30 pts. wt. polypropylene                carbonate, 5-6 pts. wt. polyhydroxybutyrate valeric                acid copolyester, 5-7 pts. wt. magnesium stearate,                3-5 pts. wt. epoxy soybean oil, 2-5 pts. wt.                glycerin, 5-8 pts. wt. hexamethylene diisocyanate,                1-2 pts. wt. 2-phenyl-2-oxazoline, 1-3 pts. wt.                lubricant, 3-5 pts. wt. styrene-butadiene-styrene                block copolymer, 2-3 pts. wt.                1,3-dimethyl-6-aminouracil, 3-5 pts. wt.                N,N'-bis(2,6-diisopropylphenyl)carbodiimide, 1-3                pts. wt. dioctadecyl pentaerythritol diphosphite,                2-5 pts. wt. barium stearate, 0.2-0.3 pts. wt.                phenylbis(2,4,6-trimethylbenzoyl)phosphine oxide,                5-7 pts. wt. potassium chloride and 5-8 pts. wt.                lauric acid diethanolamide boronate.
   USE - Used as biodegradable plastic                masterbatch.
   ADVANTAGE - The product made by using masterbatch can be                rapidly degraded by the enzyme secreted by the                microorganism in a short time after being discarded                and eliminates white pollution by water and carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the biodegradable plastic masterbatch                comprising (i) uniformly mixing starch and                magnesium stearate, drying and grinding into a                micronized starch having a starch particle size of                2-5 &#956; m; (ii) mixing polyhydroxybutyrate                valeric acid copolyester, epoxidized soybean oil,                glycerin, hexamethylene diisocyanate, and                2-phenyl-2-oxazoline to obtain auxiliary mixture A;                (iii) uniformly mixing 1,3-dimethyl-6-aminouracil,                N,N'-bis(2,6-diisopropylphenyl)carbodiimide,                dioctadecylpentaerythritol diphosphite, strontium                stearate, phenyl                bis(2,4,6-trimethylbenzoyl)phosphine oxide and                potassium chloride, stirring and mixing to obtain                auxiliary mixture B; (iv) stirring and mixing the                micronized starch obtained in the step (i) with                auxiliary mixture A obtained in the step (ii),                lubricant, styrene-butadiene-styrene block                copolymer, and the polypropylene carbonate; (v)                stirring the initial mixture obtained in the step                (iv) with the auxiliary mixture B obtained in the                step (iii), and lauric acid diethanolamide borate                to obtain a blend; and (vi) transferring the blend                obtained in the step (v) into a twin-screw                extruder, subjecting to extrusion granulation, and                cooling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The masterbatch                  comprises 58 pts. wt. starch, 27 pts. wt.                  polypropylene carbonate, 5.5 pts. wt.                  polyhydroxybutyrate valeric acid copolyester, 6                  pts. wt. magnesium stearate, 4 pts. wt.                  epoxidized soybean oil, 3 pts. wt. glycerin, 6                  pts. wt. hexamethylene diisocyanate, 1.5 pts. wt.                  2-phenyl-2-oxazoline, 2 pts. wt. lubricant, 4                  pts. wt. styrene-butadiene-styrene block                  copolymer, 2.5 pts. wt.                  1,3-dimethyl-6-aminouracil, 4 pts. wt.                  N,N'-bis(2,6-diisopropylphenyl)carbodiimide, 2                  pts. wt. dioctadecyl pentaerythritol diphosphite,                  4 pts. wt. strontium stearate, 0.25 pts. wt.                  phenylbis(2,4,6-trimethylbenzoyl)phosphine oxide,                  6 pts. wt. potassium chloride and 6 pts. wt.                  lauric acid diethanolamide borate. Preferred                  Composition: The starch is wheat starch, corn                  starch, tapioca starch, and/or potato starch.                  Preferred Method: In step (ii) the method                  comprises pre-heating the polyhydroxybutyric acid                  valeric acid copolyester, epoxidized soybean oil,                  glycerin, hexamethylene diisocyanate and                  2-phenyl-2-oxazoline to 50-80&#176; C,                  stirring at high-speed mixer with a rotation                  speed of 80-90 rotations/minute, and mixing for                  5-8 minutes, adjusting the high-speed mixer                  heating temperature to 100-120&#176; C,                  controlling the rotation speed to 160-180                  rotations/minute, mixing the mixture for 45-60                  minutes under this condition and cooling the                  auxiliary mixture A. In step (iii) the                  1,3-dimethyl-6-aminouracil,                  N,N'-bis(2,6-diisopropylphenyl)carbodiimide,                  dioctadecyl pentaerythritol diphosphite,                  strontium stearate,                  phenylbis(2,4,6-trimethylbenzoyl)phosphine oxide                  and potassium chloride are preheated to                  30-50&#176; C, mixing a high-speed mixer with                  a rotational speed of 80-90 rotations/minute with                  light for 20-25 minutes, then the heating                  temperature of the high-speed mixer is adjusted                  to 100-120&#176; C, and the rotation speed is                  controlled to 160-180 rotations/minute under                  these conditions and the mixture is kept in the                  dark for 10-15 minutes and the auxiliary mixture                  B is cooled. In step (iv) the micronized starch                  obtained in the step (i), the auxiliary mixture A                  obtained in the step (2), the                  styrene-butadiene-styrene block copolymer, and                  the polypropylene carbonate are mixed in a high                  speed mixer, then stirring at a temperature of                  150-180&#176; C and a rotation speed of                  300-500 rotations/minute for 20-30 minutes, then                  a lubricant is added and stirring is continued                  for 5-8 minutes at the same temperature and the                  initial mixture is cooled. In step (v) the                  preliminary mixture obtained in the step (iv) and                  the auxiliary mixture B obtained in the step                  (iii) are added to a high-speed mixer at a                  temperature of 80-100&#176; C, then stirring                  at a speed of 300-500 rotations/minute for 15-20                  minutes, then adding lauric acid diethanolamide                  borate, the high-speed mixer heating temperature                  was adjusted to 120-150&#176; C, and the                  mixture was stirred and mixed at this temperature                  for 20-30 minutes and the mixture was cooled to                  obtain a blend. In step (vi) adding the blend                  obtained in the step (v) into a co-rotating                  twin-screw extruder silo having an aspect ratio                  of more than 48:1 and setting the process                  temperature of the twin-screw extruder, where the                  temperature reaches the required process                  temperature and the temperature is 30-35 minutes,                  then open the twin-screw extruder and control the                  absolute vacuum of the twin-screw extruder to                  less than 10,000 Pa. The main screw speed is                  300-500 rotations/minute, then subjecting to                  extrusion granulation and cooling to obtain                  biodegradable plastic masterbatch, where the                  process temperature of the twin-screw extruder is                  as specified.

PN DE202018104823-U1
TI Network-capable terminal for connection to network switch e.g. network actuator for interconnecting network segments, has main unit that is supplied with electrical power required for operation of main unit through connecting line.
AU 
AE MICROSENS GMBH & CO KG (MICR-Non-standard)
GA 201879207A
AB    NOVELTY - The network-capable terminal (05) has a port (09) that is connected to one of the network switch (04) coming connection line (08). The terminal is provided to exchange data packets in a computer network (01) through the connection line. The terminal is supplied with the electrical power required for the operation of the terminal through the connecting line. The terminal is designed in the manner of a network sensor (06) for measuring a physical measured quantity. The network sensor is formed with a sensor unit (07) for measuring the physical measured variable including temperature, absolute humidity, relative humidity, carbon dioxide content, brightness, pressure, volumetric flow and force.
   USE - Network-capable terminal for connection to network switch such as network sensor or network actuator for interconnecting network segments (claimed). Uses include but are not limited to office environment, industrial environment, lighting application, air-conditioning application, safety application, automation application and building application.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the network-capable terminal is connected through the connecting line to the network switch with only one connecting line, the data is exchanged between the network switch and the terminal, and the electrical power required for the operation of the terminal from the network switch terminal is allowed to flow. Thus, the wiring in the construction of networks is simplified, as only one connection line between the network switch and the terminal is pulled to connect the terminals and integrated into the data network. The functionality of the terminal is guaranteed, as the data exchange and the supply of electrical energy through the second connection line is secured. Since the network actuator is simultaneously connected through the ports to two different network switches or to a network switch with two connecting lines, the construction of safety-relevant control or control tasks from the smoke extraction technology, the safety lighting technology or staircase compressed air ventilation technology is realized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a network segment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the network segment with terminal.
   Computer network (01)
   Network switch (04)
   Network-capable terminal (05)
   Network sensor (06)
   Sensor unit (07)
   Connection line (08)
   Port (09)

PN CN108488990-A
TI Indoor environment regulating system for            regulating indoor environment temperature of air            conditioner in building, has controller comparing data            detected by outdoor and indoor detecting mechanisms to            control air and heating systems.
AU XU T
   XU C
   CHENG C
AE HUIBAO INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY SHANGHAI (HUIB-Non-standard)
GA 201871202W
AB    NOVELTY - The regulating system has a controller (300)                connected to an outdoor environment detecting                mechanism (100), an indoor environment detecting                mechanism (200), an indoor air conditioning system                (400), a fresh air system (500) and a heating                system (600). The controller compares data detected                by the outdoor and indoor environment detecting                mechanisms to correspondingly control the air                conditioning system, the fresh air system and the                heating system. The outdoor detecting mechanism                includes an outdoor temperature sensor (110), an                outdoor humidity sensor (120) and an outdoor                contaminant sensor (130). The indoor detecting                mechanism includes an indoor temperature sensor                (210), an indoor humidity sensor (220) and an                indoor pollutant sensor (230). The controller is                provided with a heating system control module                (310), an air conditioning system control module                (320), a fresh air system control module (330) and                a comparing module. The heating system control                module is connected to the heating system.
   USE - Indoor environment regulating system for                regulating indoor environment temperature and                humidity of an air conditioner in a building.
   ADVANTAGE - The fresh air system, the indoor air                conditioning system and the heating system are                combined and worked together according to the                indoor and outdoor environment temperature,                humidity and air pollution index so as to achieve                indoor environment regulating effect at lower                energy consumption level. The regulating system                avoids indoor heat environment influences and human                life comfortable feel affecting phenomenon and harm                causing to health of people caused by indoor air                pollutant. The regulating system maintains indoor                temperature in relatively comfortable range.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The outdoor contaminant sensor or indoor                pollutant sensor is selected from one of a                formaldehyde sensor, an inhalable particulate                sensor, a volatile organic compound (VOC) sensor                and a carbon dioxide (CO20) sensor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                indoor environment regulating system.
   Outdoor environment detecting mechanism                (100)
   Outdoor temperature sensor (110)
   Outdoor humidity sensor (120)
   Outdoor contaminant sensor (130)
   Indoor environment detecting mechanism                (200)
   Indoor temperature sensor (210)
   Indoor humidity sensor (220)
   Indoor pollutant sensor (230)
   Controller (300)
   Heating system control module (310)
   Air conditioning system control module                (320)
   Fresh air system control module (330)
   Indoor air conditioning system (400)
   Fresh air system (500)
   Heating system (600)
DC Q74 (Heating, ranges, ventilating (F24)); T01 (Digital Computers)
MC Q74-A02A; Q74-A02B; Q74-A02C; Q74-T20; T01-E01C; T01-J05A3

PN WO2018132420-A1; CN108454555-A; CN108466596-A; EP3568325-A1; US2019359167-A1; EP3568325-A4; US11155235-B2; US2022041126-A1; CN108454555-B; CN108466596-B
TI Airbag inflator system for airbag systems used for            autonomous vehicles, has aspirator that includes            aspiration portion partly separated from inlet portion            to define opening in between which is in flow            communication with gas generator.
AU BREED D S
   VOROPAIEV G A
   YURCHENKO N F
   PARAMONOV Y A
   KOSHIL A I
   VOROPAYEV K A
   BREED D
   EUGENE N F
   PARAMONOV Y
   KOSHER A I
   PARAMONOV A
AE AUTOMOTIVE TECHNOLOGIES INT INC (ATTT-C)
   SHANGHAI EAST JOYLONG MOTOR AIRBAG CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201856559T
AB    NOVELTY - The airbag inflator system has a housing (14)                that defines a flow passage (28) with first and                second end regions and configured to lead into an                interior of an airbag (20) when coupled to the                housing. A reverse flow valve (16) is arranged at                the second end region and selectively enables                connection of the flow passage to ambient                atmosphere dependent on a pressure differential on                opposite sides of the reverse flow valve. A gas                generator is arranged in connection with the                housing and configured to generate gas. An                activation system provides a signal to the gas                generator to cause generation of gas. An aspirator                (12) includes an inlet portion (26) alongside the                reverse flow valve and an aspiration portion partly                separated from the inlet portion to define an                opening in between which is in flow communication                with the gas generator and oriented toward the                first end region such that the generated gas flows                through the opening into the flow passage and                toward the first end region.
   USE - Airbag inflator system for aspirated airbag                systems used for autonomous vehicles                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The airbag inflator system provides an airbag                system which stops inflating upon contact with the                driver, thus substantially reducing occupant                injuries due to airbag inflation. A very low cost                airbag system is provided through the elimination                of most of the propellant, which reduces the size                and cost and improves the safety of the gas                generator. The need for satellite crash sensors is                eliminated which further reduce the cost of the                airbag system. The airbag system sufficiently                dilutes the products of combustion from the gas                generators through aspiration creating a breathable                environment after deployment when the propellant                combustion products contain pollutants, such as                carbon dioxide. The airbag system eliminates the                need for occupant sensors through friendly                non-injuring aspirated inflated airbags.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) an airbag assembly that comprises an                airbag inflator system; and
   (2) an autonomous vehicle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the cross-sectional view of                the airbag module.
   Aspirator (12)
   Housing (14)
   Reverse flow valve (16)
   Airbag (20)
   Inlet portion (26)
   Flow passage (28)

TI Preparing transition-metal-doped cerium-lanthanide            oxide compound by dissolving cerium nitrate,            water-soluble salt of transition metal, and nitrate of            e.g. lanthanide in water, adding fuel, stirring at room            temperature, and heating.
AU ALVAREZ GALVAN M C
   DAPENA OSPINA M
   ALONSO ALONSO J A

AE CONSEJO SUPERIOR INVESTIGACIONES CIENTIF (CNSJ-C)
   CONSEJO NACIONAL INVESTIGACIONES CIENTIF (CNIC-C)
   CONSEJO NACIONAL INVESTIGACIONES CIENTIF (CNIC-C)
   CONSEJO SUPERIOR INVESTIGACIONES CIENTIF (CNSJ-C)
   NAT SCI & TECH RES COUNCIL (NACO-Non-standard)
   CONSEJO SUPERIOR INVESTIGACIONES CIENTIF (CNSJ-C)
   CONSEJO NAT INVESTIGACIONES CIENTICAS (CNIC-C)
   CONSEJO NACIONAL INVESTIGACIONES CIENTIF (CNIC-C)
GA 2018525389
AB    NOVELTY - A transition-metal-doped cerium-lanthanide                oxide compound is prepared by dissolving                stoichiometric quantities of cerium nitrate,                water-soluble salt of transition metal, and nitrate                of lanthanide element in minimum amount of water                and adding fuel to the solution in a molar ratio of                0.7-1 of fuel with respect to total nitrates, (b)                stirring at room temperature until complete                dissolution of the solution, and (c) heating the                solution at 200-600 degrees C.
   USE - Preparation of transition-metal-doped                cerium-lanthanide oxide compound for use as                catalyst in reverse reaction of water gas                displacement and as catalyst in partial oxidation                of methane to synthesis gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environment-friendly as it                reduces carbon dioxide emissions in the atmosphere,                and reduces the formation of hot spots, which may                affect the improvement in the stability and the                control of the temperature at the industrial level.                The catalyst formed from the compound (I) has high                catalytic activity with respect to reverse reaction                of water gas displacement and partial oxidation of                methane to synthesis gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A transition-metal-doped cerium-lanthanide                oxide compound of formula My(Ce1-xLxO2-x/2)1-y (I)                is prepared by (a) dissolving stoichiometric                quantities of cerium nitrate, water-soluble salt of                metal (M), and nitrate of metal (L) in minimum                amount of water and adding fuel to the solution in                a molar ratio of 0.7-1 of fuel with respect to                total nitrates, (b) stirring at room temperature                until complete dissolution of the solution, and (c)                heating the solution at 200-600 degrees C.
   M=Ni, Ru, Rh, Pd, Ir, Pt, Ag, Au, or Cu;
   x=0-0.4;
   y=0.001-0.6; and
   L=lanthanide, Y, or Sc.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) use of transition-metal-doped                cerium-lanthanide oxide compound (I) as catalyst in                reverse reaction of water gas displacement;                and
   (2) use of transition-metal-doped                cerium-lanthanide oxide compound (I) as catalyst in                partial oxidation of methane to synthesis                gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (a),                  water-soluble nickel salt is nickel(II) nitrate                  hexahydrate (Ni(NO3).6H2O); water-soluble copper                  salt is copper(II) nitrate (Cu(NO3)2.6H2O);                  water-soluble platinum salt is selected from                  tetraammineplatinum dihydroxide                  ((NH3)4Pt(OH)2.xH2O) and tetraammineplatinum(II)                  nitrate (Pt(NH3)4(NO3)2). Value of x is not 0 in                  compound (I). The catalyst for reverse reaction                  of water gas displacement is selected from                  Ni0.1(Ce0.96Gd0.04O1.95)0.9;                  Ni0.1(Ce0.9Gd0.1O1.95)0.9; and                  Ni0.1(Ce0.9La0.1O1.95)0.9. The catalyst for                  partial oxidation of methane to synthesis gas is                  Ni0.1(Ce0.9Gd0.1O1.95)0.9. Preferred Properties:                  The compound (I) has a percentage of porosity of                  70-95% and an average diameter of 0.5-5%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Fuel used in step (a)                  is selected from glycine, urea, and/or citric                  acid, preferably glycine. Preferred Conditions:                  Step (c) is carried out at 200-500 degrees                  C.
   Preferred Definitions:
   Lanthanide=La, Pr, Nd, Sm, Eu, Gd, Tb, Dy, Ho,                Er, Tm, Yb, or Lu, preferably Gd or Sm;
   x=0.05-0.2;
   y=0.001-0.15; and
   M=Ni, Cu, or Pt.

PT P
PN US2018153442-A1
TI Gas sampling line for sampling of respiratory gases, has channel, gas sampling tube, drying assembly comprising casing made of polyether block amide material comprising segments comprising polyethylene oxide, and hydrophilic component.
AU ECKERBOM A
   ZYZANSKI R
AE MASIMO CORP (MSIM-C)
GA 2018450148
AB    NOVELTY - A gas sampling line having a channel (5) for conducting respiratory gases consists of a gas sampling tube (1) surrounding a portion of the channel, a drying assembly extending along the outside of the gas sampling tube, and a hydrophilic component (4) arranged within the casing and being in fluid contact with the channel. The drying assembly comprises a casing made of a polyether block amide material comprising polyether segments comprising polyethylene oxide.
   USE - Gas sampling line used for gas analysis system for sampling of respiratory gases (all claimed) from incubated patient e.g. infants and/or pediatrics.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas sampling line improves sidestream measurements of the respiratory gases. Only a small fraction of the inhaled and/or exhaled respiratory gas is diverted for measurement from the main flow of respiratory gas between the patient and a breather apparatus. When passing a sample of respiratory gases through the gas sampling tube, moisture and condensed water present in the gas sample permeate through the tube material to provide a dried gas sample, while components (such as carbon dioxide or anesthetic agents) of the gas sample passes the tube portion essentially undistorted (i.e. without being absorbed in or adsorbed to the tube material) to allow for an accurate reading at the gas monitor. The hydrophilic component provides a wicking action, transporting moisture or water to the casing through which it is removed, thus not accumulating in the drying assembly. The hydrophilic component can transport moisture or water to a large area casing, thus allowing for efficient moisture and water permeation through the casing material, while maintaining a compact design of the gas sampling line. The gas sampling line provides a smooth gas path while avoiding risks for undesirable leakage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for sampling of respiratory gases;
   (2) gas drying assembly; and
   (3) gas analysis system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic sectional view of the gas sampling line.
   Gas sampling tube (1)
   Gas monitor connector (2)
   Housing (3)
   Hydrophilic component (4)
   Channel (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The gas sampling tube is made of a material that is not permeable to moisture and/or water. The polyamide segments of the polyether block amide material are selected from polyamide-12, polyamide-11 and polyamide-12.12. Preferred Composition: The polyester block amide material comprises polyether segments and polyamide segments. The ratio of polyether to polyamide is 60:40-40:60, preferably 60:40-50:50.

PT P
PN CN108046295-A; CN108046295-B
TI Sodium bicarbonate and ammonium sulfate production            involves absorbing gas in sodium sulfate solution to            obtain absorbent solution.
AU DANG Y
   ZHANG S
   FEI D
   JI X
   FANG Y
   ZHOU L
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 201840983N
AB    NOVELTY - Sodium bicarbonate and ammonium sulfate                production involves absorbing gas in a sodium                sulfate solution at -5-10&#176; C to obtain an                absorbent solution. The sodium sulfate solution                includes 25-30 wt.%, preferably 26.5-27.5 wt.%                sodium sulfate. The absorption liquid, ammonium                bicarbonate and d-stage ammonium bicarbonate or                sodium sulfate mixed salt are mixed uniformly at                -5-20&#176; C to obtain a mixed slurry. The                mixed slurry is placed into a closed reactor                reaction for isolating sodium bicarbonate and                reaction mother liquor.
   USE - Method for producing sodium bicarbonate and                ammonium sulfate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces the sodium bicarbonate and                ammonium sulfate in a simple and cost-effective                manner with reduced environmental pollution and                high raw material utilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Sodium bicarbonate and ammonium sulfate                production involves absorbing gas in a sodium                sulfate solution at -5-10&#176; C to obtain an                absorbent solution. The sodium sulfate solution                includes 25-30 wt.%, preferably 26.5-27.5 wt.%                sodium sulfate. The absorption liquid, ammonium                bicarbonate and d-stage ammonium bicarbonate or                sodium sulfate mixed salt are mixed uniformly at                -5-20&#176; C to obtain a mixed slurry. The                mixed slurry is placed into a closed reactor                reaction for isolating sodium bicarbonate and                reaction mother liquor. The mother liquor is                combined with sodium sulfate or ammonium sulfate                double salt, where the ammonium bicarbonate or                sodium sulfate mixed salt and the frozen mother                liquor are obtained after freezing. The frozen                mother liquor is distilled to obtain carbon                dioxide, ammonia gas and ammonia mother liquor. The                mother liquor is adjusted with an acid to a pH of                4-7 to obtain an acidified solution and gas. The                acidification solution is mixed with the                crystallization mother liquor and then frozen to                obtain sodium sulfate or ammonium sulfate double                salt and secondary frozen mother liquor. The                obtained secondary frozen mother liquor is                evaporated and concentrated to separate ammonium                sulfate, where the solution after separation of                ammonium sulfate is crystallization mother liquor.                The obtained sodium bicarbonate and the obtained                ammonium sulfate are washed and dried to obtain the                product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flowchart of                the method for producing sodium bicarbonate and                ammonium sulfate. (Drawing includes non-English                language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The sodium sulfate a                  waste produced solution liquid produced by flue                  gas desulfurization and a lithium carbonate                  production process, sodium sulfate by-products or                  waste liquids made from viscose, cellophane, and                  pigments. The absorption process is performed in                  an absorption tower, preferably by countercurrent                  absorption. The sodium sulfate is a sodium                  sulfate-containing by-product produced by a                  lithium carbonate production process. The acid is                  sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, and nitric                  acid, preferably sulfuric acid. The ammonium                  sulfate crystallized mother liquor is mixed and                  frozen to obtain the sodium sulfate or ammonium                  sulfate double salt. The secondary frozen mother                  liquor and the sodium sulfate or ammonium sulfate                  double salt is returned to for recycling.

PN CN107955195-A
TI Preparing modified gelatin film comprises e.g. mixing gelatin, water and potassium permanganate, heating, and mixing with glycerin and buffer solution, and mixing with Oldenlandia corymbosa L. leaf and water, and carrying out fermentation.
AU LIU R
   YIN K
AE CHANGZHOU XIONGTU TEXTILE CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018353386
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified gelatin film comprises e.g. mixing gelatin, water and potassium permanganate, heating, and mixing with glycerin and buffer solution, mixing Oldenlandia corymbosa L. leaf and water, and carrying out fermentation to obtain fermentation broth, and mixing above product and fermentation broth, stirring the reaction at 30-40 degrees C for 10-18 hours to obtain reactant, spring the reactant on a Plexiglass plate and carrying out film formation temperature at 25-30 degrees C for 20-24 hours to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing modified gelatin film.
   ADVANTAGE - The modified gelatin film has low cell toxicity gene toxicity, excellent biocompatibility and strong degradation ability, and improved cross-linking effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing modified gelatin film comprises (i) mixing gelatin, water and potassium permanganate, heating at 50-60 degrees C, and stirring for 1-2 hours, stirring the mixture, and ixing stirring mixture with glycerin and buffer solution at 20-30 degrees C and stirring for 40-65 minutes, heating at 50-60 degrees C, stirring, and allowing to stand until no bubble, (ii) mixing Oldenlandia corymbosa L. leaf and water, soaking for 1-2 hours, then performing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction, mixing obtained the extract, buckwheat and water, soaking for 20-24 hours, filtering, mixing the filter residue and buffer solution at a pH of 4-5 and grinding, placing in a homogenizer to obtain slurry, centrifuging the slurry for 2-3 minutes to obtain precipitate, centrifuging the precipitate at 3-4 degrees C for 20-30 minutes to obtain extract, adding the extract in the fermenter at a temperature of 30-40 degrees C for 2-3 days for fermentation to obtain fermentation broth, and (iii) mixing the product obtained in step (i) and fermentation broth, stirring the reaction at 30-40 degrees C for 10-18 hours to obtain reactant, spring the reactant on a Plexiglass plate and carrying out film formation temperature at 25-30 degrees C for 20-24 hours to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the mass ratio between gelatin, water and hydrogen peroxide is 1:3-4:0.1-0.2. The mass ratio between stirring mixture, glycerol and buffer solution is 30-40:12-13:7-8. The buffer solution is mixing phosphoric acid, disodium hydrogen phosphate and water in a mass ratio of 1:1:5-6. The parameters of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction are pressures of 15-20 MPa, and the temperature is 45-50 degrees C. In step (ii), the buffer solution is mixing citric acid, sodium citrate and water in a mass ratio of 1:1:5-6. In step (iii), the mass ratio between product obtained in step (i) and fermentation broth is 10-15:1-2.

PN JP2018061022-A; JP6976113-B2
TI Non-aqueous lithium type electrical storage element for e.g. electrical storage module comprises positive electrode, negative electrode, separator, and non-aqueous electrolyte solution containing lithium ion.
AU HIRAKAWA Y
   MORITA H
   KIMURA Y
AE ASAHI KASEI CORP (ASAH-C)
   ASAHI KASEI KK (ASAH-C)
GA 201828628W
AB    NOVELTY - Non-aqueous lithium type electrical storage element comprises positive electrode, negative electrode, separator, and non-aqueous electrolyte solution containing lithium ion. The negative electrode has an anode collector and a negative electrode active material layer which is provided on greater than or equal to 1 surface of anode collector and includes a negative electrode active material, in which the negative electrode active material includes carbon material which can occlude or release lithium ion. The positive electrode contains a positive current collector and a positive electrode active material layer.
   USE - A non-aqueous lithium type electrical storage element for electrical storage module, electric power regeneration assist system, electric power load-leveling system, uninterruptible power supply system, non-contact electric power feeding system, energy harvest system, electrical storage system, photovoltaic power generation electrical storage system, electric power steering system, emergency power source system, in-wheel motor system, idling stop system, vehicle, such as electric vehicle, plug-in hybrid motor vehicle, hybrid vehicle, or electric motorcycle, quick-charge system, and smart grid system (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The non-aqueous lithium type electrical storage element exhibits excellent initial high input/output characteristics, long-term high temperature storage durability, and high load charge/discharge cycle characteristics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Non-aqueous lithium type electrical storage element comprises positive electrode, negative electrode, separator, and non-aqueous electrolyte solution containing lithium ion. The negative electrode has an anode collector and a negative electrode active material layer which is provided on greater than or equal to 1 surface of anode collector and includes a negative electrode active material, in which the negative electrode active material includes carbon material which can occlude or release lithium ion. The positive electrode contains a positive current collector and a positive electrode active material layer which is provided on greater than or equal to 1 surface of positive current collector and includes positive electrode active material which contains activated carbon. The positive electrode contains 1-50 mass% lithium carbonate, lithium oxide, or lithium hydroxide as lithium compound other than the positive electrode active material based on total mass of positive electrode active material layer. Dissolved carbon dioxide level of non-aqueous electrolyte solution is 10-5000 mu g/L.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Parameters: In element mapping obtained by scanning electron microscopy with energy dispersive X-ray spectroscopy (SEM-EDX) on surface of positive electrode, area overlap rate (A1) of fluorine mapping with respect to oxygen mapping binarized based on average brightness is 40-99%. The positive electrode active material contained in the positive electrode active material layer has micropore amount (V1 (cc/g)) derived from pores having diameter of 20-500 Angstrom and calculated by Barrett-Joyner-Halenda (BJH) method and micropore amount (V2 (cc/g)) derived from pores having diameter of less than or equal to 20 Angstrom and calculated by micropore (MP) method, in which V1 has value of 0.3-0.8, preferably 0.8-2.5 and V2 has value of 0.5-1, preferably 0.8-3. Activated carbon has specific surface area of 1500-3000 m2/g, preferably 2300-4000 m2/g, measured by Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) method. Doping amount of lithium ions of negative electrode active material is 530-2500 mAh/g per unit mass, preferably 50-700 mAh/g per unit mass. BET specific surface area of negative electrode active material is 100-1500 m2/g, preferably 1-50 m2/g. Average particle diameter of negative electrode active material is 1-10 mu m. In storage element, when initial normal temperature discharge internal resistance at cell voltage of 4 V is set to Ra ( Omega ), electrostatic capacity is set to F (F), normal temperature discharge internal resistance after storage for 2 months at cell voltage of 4 V and environmental temperature of 60 degrees C is set to Rd ( Omega ), and normal temperature discharge internal resistance after 60000 cycles of charging and discharging at rate of 300 C from 2.2 V to 3.8 V at ambient temperature of 25 degrees C is set to Re ( Omega ), product (Rax F) of Ra and F is 0.3-3, Rd/Ra is 0.9-3, amount of gas generated when stored at cell voltage of 4 V and temperature of 60 degrees C for 2 months is less than or equal to 30x 10-3 cc/F at 25 degrees C, and Re/Ra is 0.9-2. Preferred Devices: Electrical storage system comprises non-aqueous lithium type electrical storage element and lead battery, nickel hydrogen battery, lithium ion secondary battery, or fuel cell which are connected in series or parallel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Lithium compound other than the positive electrode active material of positive electrode is lithium carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The positive electrode active material layer contains greater than or equal to 1 compound of formula LiX1-OR1O-X2Li (1) in amount of 1.6x 10-4-300x 10-4 mol/g unit mass of positive electrode material layer. Non-aqueous electrolyte solution contains greater than or equal to 1 sultone compound of formula (5), formula (6), or formula (7), in which total amount of sultone compounds is 0.5-15 mass%.R1=1-4C alkylene or 1-4C halogenated alkylene;X1, X2=-(COO)n or group of formula LiX1-OR1O-X2R2 (2);n=0 or 1;R2, R3=H, 1-10C alkyl, 1-10C mono or polyhydroxy alkyl, 2-10C alkenyl, 2-10C mono or polyhydroxy alkenyl, 3-6C cycloalkyl, or aryl;X1, X2=-(COO)n or group of formula R2X1-OR1O-X2R3 (3);X1, X2=-(COO)n;R1-R6=H, halo, 1-12C alkyl, or 1-12C halogenoalkyl; andn1=0-3.
DC E19 (Other organic compounds general - organic compounds of unknown or indefinite structure; general mixtures of many types; organic reactions (e.g. nitration, resolution) when applied generally.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. PT P
PN US2018072972-A1; WO2018049342-A1
TI Fermentation device used for preparing fermented            beverage, has fermentation vessel having open and            closed fermenting configuration, specific gravity            measuring system comprising weight sensor and pressure            sensor, and fermentation controller.
AU KANG B
   KIM Y
   OH S
   SHIN J
AE ALPHA REVOLUTION INC (ALPH-Non-standard)
   KANG B (KANG-Individual)
   KIM Y (KIMY-Individual)
   OH S (OHSS-Individual)
   ALPHA REVOLUTION INC (ALPH-Non-standard)
GA 201821524S
AB    NOVELTY - Fermentation device comprises a fermentation                vessel having an open and a closed fermentation                configuration, a specific gravity measurement                system comprising a weight sensor and a pressure                sensor, and a fermentation controller configured to                access a desired brix profile, receive the sensed                level of fermentation in the open or the closed                fermentation configuration from the specific                gravity measurement system, and adjust a                fermentation parameter during a primary and/or a                secondary fermentation of a mixture contained in                the fermentation vessel in real time.
   USE - The fermentation device is useful for                preparing a fermented beverage (claimed) comprising                a beer or a wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The fermentation device is a compact,                portable, automatic and self-contained fermentation                device, provides continual and reliable data that                is both accurate and precise, maintains the taste                of the beer by avoiding any reaction between air                and the beer, and produces high-quality fermented                beverage without the need for a professional                brewer, expensive equipment, extensive labor or                inordinate amounts of time. The device allows                easier maintenance since residue left within the                channels is minimized, avoids any reaction between                air and beer and avoids the need for replacing the                fluid source (such as carbon dioxide or                nitrogen).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fermentation device (400) comprises a                fermentation vessel having an open and a closed                fermentation configuration, a specific gravity                measurement system comprising a weight sensor and a                pressure sensor, where the specific gravity                measurement system is configured to sense the level                of fermentation in the open or the closed                fermentation configuration, and a fermentation                controller configured to access a desired brix                profile, receive the sensed level of fermentation                in the open or the closed fermentation                configuration from the specific gravity measurement                system, and adjust a fermentation parameter during                a primary and/or a secondary fermentation of a                mixture contained in the fermentation vessel in                real time. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a portable beer fermentation device;                and
   (2) a method for preparing a fermented                beverage using the portable fermentation                device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a                fermentation device.
   Fermentation device (400)
   Cover (402)
   Faucet (404)
   Main chamber (407)
   Controller (412)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Device: The fermentation parameter                  comprises at least one of a temperature of the                  fermentation vessel, a pressure of the                  fermentation vessel, a specific gravity of the                  mixture, a brix of the fermentation mixture, and                  the fermentation configuration. The fermentation                  controller is further configured to (a) receive a                  weight sensed by the weight sensor of the                  specific gravity measurement system before                  primary fermentation begins, (b) receive the                  specific gravity of the fermentation mixture                  calculated from a beginning volume of the mixture                  in the fermentation vessel and a starting                  specific gravity obtained from a tag, (c) convert                  the weight drop sensed from the weight sensor                  over a period of time to specific gravity during                  the primary fermentation using a first formula,                  (d) determine a first fermentation status of the                  mixture in the fermentation vessel based on the                  converted specific gravity, and (e) adjust the                  fermentation parameter during the primary                  fermentation of the mixture in the fermentation                  vessel based on the first fermentation status and                  a desired fermentation status. The fermentation                  controller is further configured to: (f) receive                  a pressure of carbon dioxide sensed by the                  pressure sensor of the specific gravity                  measurement system when the fermentation vessel                  is in the closed fermentation configuration at                  the secondary fermentation, (g) receive a volume                  of carbon dioxide from the fermentation device,                  (h) convert the pressure and the volume of carbon                  dioxide to specific gravity using a second                  formula, (i) convert the volume of carbon dioxide                  to an amount of brix using a third formula, (j)                  determine a second fermentation status of the                  mixture in the fermentation vessel based on brix                  and/or specific gravity, and (k) adjust the                  fermentation parameter during the secondary                  fermentation of the mixture in the fermentation                  vessel based on the current fermentation status                  and a desired fermentation status. The first                  formula is specific gravity=weight/volume, the                  second formula is weight change=(90-95.74 n),                  PV=nRT and n=PV/RT, and the third formula is                  specific gravity change=sugar weight                  change/initial wort volume. The fermentation                  controller is configured to determine the first                  or the second fermentation status of the mixture                  based on a mean value of or a change in the                  specific gravity during a predetermined time                  interval. The first or the second fermentation                  status of the mixture is determined in real time.                  The tag is a quick response code, a barcode, a                  radio frequency identification tag, near field                  communication tag or any other near field data                  communication mechanism affixed to an outside                  surface of the fermentation vessel. The first or                  the second fermentation status comprises at least                  one of an amount of sugar consumption in the                  fermentation vessel and a fermentation rate of                  the mixture in the fermentation vessel. Preferred                  Process: The desired fermentation status is                  determined based on the accessed desired brix                  profile, where the accessed desired brix profile                  comprises a desired brix profile for a particular                  type of beer.

PN CN107722334-A; CN107722334-B
TI Preparation of flame-retardant pentane foaming agent involves mixing pentane foaming agent and organic solution, adding cosolvent and modified powder prepared using e.g. tetramethylammonium hydroxide solution, stirring, and pressurizing.
AU CAO P
   LI S
   XI Z
   YANG Y
   XI J
AE NINGBO ZHONGJIN PETRIFACTION CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201817928S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of flame-retardant pentane foaming agent involves mixing pentane foaming agent and organic solution, adding cosolvent and modified powder prepared using flame-retardant inorganic whisker material, tetramethylammonium hydroxide solution, inorganic powder, silicone ethanol solution and anhydrous ethanol, stirring, grinding, centrifuging, filtering, obtaining modified pentane foaming agent mother liquor, pressurizing, passing into main pipeline through branch pipe, and mixing using a static mixer and ultrasonic mixer in main conveying pipeline.
   USE - Preparation of flame-retardant pentane foaming agent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of flame-retardant pentane foaming agent involves mixing 2-4 parts mass pentane foaming agent and 1-2 parts mass organic solution, adding 0.4-0.6 part mass cosolvent and 1-1.2 parts mass modified powder, stirring, grinding for 3-6 hours, ultrasonically dispersing at 10-15 degrees C for 2-3 hours, leaving still for 3-5 days to remove suspended solids, centrifuging at 30-50 rpm for 0.5-1 hour, separating precipitate, filtering, obtaining modified pentane foaming agent mother liquor, installing a booster pump on branch pipe of main pipeline of pentane blowing agent, pressurizing the mother liquor by the booster pump, passing into the main pipeline through the branch pipe, and mixing using a static mixer and ultrasonic mixer in main conveying pipeline. The modified powder is prepared by mixing 1-2 parts mass flame-retardant inorganic whisker material and 6-8 parts mass tetramethylammonium hydroxide solution with a concentration of 20% in a reactor lined with polytetrafluoroethylene at 120-150 degrees C and 0.2-0.3 MPa for 4-5 hours, adding 0.5-1 part mass inorganic powder, crushing for 1-2 hours, filtering, washing, drying using carbon dioxide as medium, crushing, subjecting to radiofrequency plasma treatment in nitrogen as carrier gas at 30-50 Pa and 50-80 W power for 6-10 minutes, adding 10-12 parts mass 10% silicone ethanol solution, mixing at 50-60 degrees C for 0.5-1 hour, heating to 70-80 degrees C for 10-12 hours, cooling, suction-filtering, washing with anhydrous ethanol, crushing, and sieving through 200-300 mesh.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The whisker material is basic magnesium sulfate crystal whisker, magnesium chloride hydroxide crystal whisker and/or magnesium hydrate crystal whisker. The inorganic powder contains melamine cyanurate, red phosphorus flame retardant and/or pure diatom.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The silicone is chlorohexyldimethylchlorosilane, chloromethyltrichlorosilane, methyltrichlorosilane and/or ethyltrichlorosilane. The organic solution is n-hexane, isohexane, cyclohexane, benzene, ethyl ether and/or ethyl propyl ether. The cosolvent is ethyl acetate, acetone, butanediol and/or propanediol. The amount of the modified pentane foaming agent mother liquor is 1-10 wt.%.
DC A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); E19 (Other organic compounds general - organic compounds of unknown or indefinite structure; general mixtures of many types; organic reactions (e.g. nitration, resolution) when applied generally.)

TI Decarburization flash steam tailing gas utilization system has carbonization tower, conveying pump, thickener, centrifuge, aqueous ammonia tank, aqueous ammonia pump and spraying pipe that are orderly connected through pipeline.
AU YU X

PN CN112570030-A
TI Preparing Bi4O5Br2/Fe-MIL composite material with photocatalytic of reducing carbon dioxide photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to methane under simulated sunlight comprises promoting separation of photo-generated electron.
AU YIN X
   LI X
   MU M
AE UNIV TIANJIN TECHNOLOGY (UYTT-C)
GA 202137360T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Bi4O5Br2/Fe-MIL composite material with the photocatalytic of reducing carbon dioxide comprises taking the heterojunction structure of the composite material for promoting the separation of photo-generated electron and hole, accelerating the photo- generated electron transmission rate, and improving the photocatalytic of the reduced carbon dioxide.
   USE - The Bi4O5Br2/Fe-MIL composite material is useful in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to methane under simulated sunlight (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple; has high catalyst stability; uses bismuth oxyhalide to replace noble metal photosensitizer to form composite material; provides a solution for environment problem and energy shortage; and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) using solvent thermal method to synthesize hollow spherical Bi4O5Br2 nano material bismuth(III) nitrate pentahydrate and cetrimonium bromide in a mol ratio of 2:1-2.55:1, dissolving in glycerol, respectively marking as solution A and B, dropping the solution B into the A solution, continuously stirring, then transferring the suspension to the stainless steel high pressure kettle of polytetrafluoroethylene lining, heating to 120-160 degrees C, reacting for 12-16 hours, cooling, washing for multiple times with ethanol, drying at 60 degrees C overnight to obtain the precursor, then adding 0.2-0.4 g precursor into 200-300 ml de-ionized water, oil bath heating for some time, washing the precipitate by water and ethanol, drying the collected solid for overnight in a 60 degrees C oven to obtain the hollow spherical Bi4O5Br2 material, (ii) using solvent thermal method to synthesize Bi4O5Br2/Fe-MIL composite material, adding different amount of Bi4O5Br2 material in 45 ml of dimethylformamide solution, ultrasonically treating, respectively adding ferric chloride hexahydrate and 2-aminoterephthalic acid in a mol ratio of 2:1, transferring the mixed solution to a high pressure reaction kettle, reacting for 20 hours at 110 degrees C, cooling to room temperature, centrifuging the suspension, using dimethylformamide, de-ionized water and ethanol to wash for multiple times.

PN CN112279752-A; CN112279752-B
TI Performing supercritical extraction of cannabinoid            carbon dioxide for industrial hemp involves selecting            industrial hemp plants, crushing, baking, mixing with            entrainer and subjecting to carbon dioxide            supercritical extraction.
AU NIE R
   WANG Y
AE YUNNAN FUYA BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 202115206B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for performing supercritical extraction                of cannabinoid carbon dioxide for industrial hemp                involves selecting industrial hemp plants,                crushing, baking, mixing with 1-5 vol.% entrainer                and subjecting to carbon dioxide supercritical                extraction at 30-55 degrees C under pressure of                20-40 MPa to obtain an extract, where the entrainer                is heptane and ethanol in a volume ratio of                1:1-1:5.
   USE - The method is useful for performing                supercritical extraction of cannabinoid carbon                dioxide for industrial hemp.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases solubility and                selectivity of the solvent to cannabinoids in the                production process, has improved production                efficiency, prepares cannabinoid extract with                improved quality and is suitable for industrial                production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves                  selecting the industrial hemp plants by removing                  the thick stalks and non-utilized parts of the                  industrial hemp plant and retaining the flowers                  and leaves, coarse crushing by a squirrel-cage                  coarse crusher, passing the materials after                  coarse crushing through a reciprocating vibrating                  screen to separate hemp seeds and hemp leaves,                  and then crushing to obtain industrial hemp                  mosaic powder, which is collected in a storage                  bin. The industrial hemp plant is crushed and                  passed through a 15-30 mesh sieve. The method                  further involves granulating before the carbon                  dioxide supercritical extraction step,                  compressing the dried powder of industrial hemp                  plants with a powder press to obtain industrial                  hemp mosaic powder particles with a bulk density                  greater than 0.3 g/ml and then subjecting the                  obtained industrial hemp mosaic particles to                  supercritical carbon dioxide extraction. The                  baking step involves spreading the industrial                  hemp powder evenly in a baking tray, baking at                  90-150 degrees C for 2-5 hours, and after baking                  the finished materials, pulling out the oven and                  cooling to room temperature. Preferred Component:                  The entrainer is heptane and ethanol in a volume                  ratio of 1:3.

The carbon dioxide jet preparation unit is utilized for manufacturing an ultra-critical liquid carbon dioxide jet.
   USE - Space environment soil drilling and water extracting method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing the ultra-critical carbon dioxide jet for performing auxiliary rock breaking and chip removing process so as to improve drilling and chip removing efficiency. The method enables obtaining carbon dioxide in an outer star-shaped atmosphere environment in easy and environment-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a space environment soil drilling and water extracting device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a space environment soil drilling and water extracting device.

AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus has a reaction unit in which a fluid containing carbon dioxide-containing gas, alkaline earth metal chloride, alkali metal hydroxide and water flows in and reacts to generate alkaline earth metal carbonate particles, a separating part in which a fluid including alkaline earth metal carbonate particles flows in from the reaction part, and alkaline earth metal carbonate particles are separated from the fluid and an electrolysis unit in which a fluid from which alkaline earth metal carbonate particles have been removed is introduced from the separation unit, and the fluid is electrolyzed to produce an alkali metal hydroxide, a fluid containing alkali metal hydroxide generated in the electrolysis unit is introduced into the reaction unit and recycled.
   USE - The apparatus is used for mineralization of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus: has high efficiency and high purity; captures carbon dioxide in exhaust gas without a separate capture process of carbon dioxide; can react by introducing exhaust gas containing carbon dioxide, but also have a high reaction efficiency and minimize the process equipment volume, thus improving space efficiency; converts carbon dioxide into a carbonate mineral form at a high conversion rate even at normal temperature and pressure; recycles by-products generated in the mineralization reaction of carbon dioxide to be reused in the mineralization reaction, thus minimizing the use content of the reaction raw material required for the mineralization reaction; is economical; forms carbonate mineral with high purity to stably and permanently store; can use waste materials e.g. cement kiln dust as a raw material, and thus have more eco-friendly advantages; and ensures that recycles water entire process continuously.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for immobilization of carbon dioxide comprising carbonation reaction step in which a fluid containing carbon dioxide-containing gas, alkaline earth metal chloride, alkali metal hydroxide and water reacts to generate alkaline earth metal carbonate particles, separating the alkaline earth metal carbonate particles from the fluid, an electrolysis step in which the fluid from which the alkaline earth metal carbonate particles have been removed is electrolyzed to produce an alkali metal hydroxide and recycling the fluid containing the alkali metal hydroxide to the fluid of step one.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the apparatus for mineralization of carbon dioxide.

The alkaline earth metal chloride includes at least one of calcium chloride and magnesium chloride, and the alkali metal hydroxide comprises potassium hydroxide.

AB    NOVELTY - An aluminum/aluminum oxide parts regenerating method involves clamping and fixing the aluminum/aluminum oxide member of the TD/DRM process of the semiconductor 8-inch wafer film process to be regenerated by rotary clamping device, spraying carbon dioxide gas in the cylinder through the venturi nozzle, regenerating liquid and solid to the surface of the aluminum/aluminum oxide part, spraying and regenerating the dry ice particles, using nitrogen as the shielding gas, dissolving the organic pollutants, and detaching organic pollutants from the surface of the aluminum/aluminum oxide part, blowing nitrogen gas to the surface of the aluminum/aluminum oxide part, placing aluminum/aluminum oxide parts in ultra/mega sonic device, setting the frequency of the super/mega sonic device, completing the regeneration of aluminum/aluminum oxide parts, discharging the supercritical carbon dioxide containing contaminants through the outlet and separating by supercritical carbon dioxide for recycling.
   USE - Method for regenerating aluminum/aluminum oxide parts.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables regenerating aluminum/aluminum oxide parts with improved cleaning effect and efficiency, better regeneration effect, and in simple, cost effective manner, and environment-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An aluminum/aluminum oxide parts regenerating method involves clamping and fixing the aluminum/aluminum oxide member of the TD/DRM process of the semiconductor 8-inch wafer film process to be regenerated by rotary clamping device, spraying carbon dioxide gas in the cylinder through the venturi nozzle to form a high-speed fluid mixed with carbon dioxide gas, regenerating liquid and solid to the surface of the aluminum/aluminum oxide part, spraying and regenerating the dry ice particles, using nitrogen as the shielding gas, dissolving the organic pollutants, and detaching organic pollutants from the surface of the aluminum/aluminum oxide part, re-solidifying the carbon dioxide to peel off the organic pollutants, spraying and cleaning the dry ice particles, blowing nitrogen gas to the surface of the aluminum/aluminum oxide part, placing aluminum/aluminum oxide parts in ultra/mega sonic device, heating the carbon dioxide gas in the cylinder by pressure, setting the frequency of the super/mega sonic device, performing the regeneration by oscillating with supercritical carbon dioxide, completing the regeneration of aluminum/aluminum oxide parts, discharging the supercritical carbon dioxide containing contaminants through the outlet and separating by supercritical carbon dioxide for recycling.

The tableware-like embryo is                soaked in carbon dioxide at constant temperature                and constant pressure, so that the crystallization                of the non-crystallized PLA in the tableware-like                embryo is completed to prepare PLA tableware                product with the temperature resistance of more                than 100&#8451;.
   USE - Method for preparing temperature-resistant PLA                plastic-sucking tableware.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared PLA blister tableware has                temperature resistance, non-deformation in case of                heat, and can also be biodegraded, and is a new                generation of eco-friendly plastic tableware                products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The PLA tableware-like                  embryo is placed in a temperature-adjustable                  closed container resistant to high temperature                  and high pressure, injected carbon dioxide into                  the closed container, and soaked at constant                  temperature and under constant pressure.                  Preferred Conditions: The pressure-maintaining                  temperature is 35-100&#8451;, the                  pressure-maintaining pressure is 4-13 MPa, the                  pressure-maintaining time is 1-40 minutes, and                  the pressure-releasing speed is 5-45 MPa/s. The                  carbon dioxide is supercritical carbon dioxide                  fluid or subcritical carbon dioxide fluid. The                  thickness of the sheet is 0.35-1 mm, and the                  thickness of the tableware-like embryo is 0.3-0.9                  mm. The plastic tableware is a cup cover, the                  dwell temperature is 35-65&#8451;, the dwell                  pressure is 4-7MPa, and the density of the cup                  cover product relative to the sample blank is                  changed by &#8804;&#177;1%. The shrinkage                  of the cup cover product is constant, when the                  caliber of a sample is less than 100 mm, the                  relative standard deviation is &#8804;0.5 %,                  and when the caliber of the sample is &#8805;                  100 mm, the relative standard deviation is                  &#8804;1 %. The plastic tableware is a dinner                  plate, the dwell temperature is 65-95&#8451;,                  the dwell pressure is 10-13 MPa, and the density                  of the dinner plate product relative to the                  sample embryo is reduced by 20-50%. Preferred                  Compositions: The PLA plastic tableware raw                  material comprises 45-80 wt.% main material,                  10-40 wt.% auxiliary materials 10-35 wt.% filler,                  0-5 wt.% modifier. Preferred Components: The main                  material is polylactic acid (PLA). The auxiliary                  material is polybutylene adipate co-terephthalate                  (PBAT), polybutylene succinate (PBS) or a blend.                  The filler is talcum powder, calcium carbonate,                  silicon dioxide, bentonite, coffee grounds and                  bamboo powder. The modifier is nucleating agent,                  antioxidant, antistatic agent, antibacterial                  agent, color master, compatilizer, toughening                  agent, lubricant, release agent, chain extender                  or cross-linking agent. The PLA plastic tableware                  raw material is granular material prepared by                  extrusion blending granulation process.

PN CN113699202-A
TI Preparing collagen with mixed cells and artificial cell culture nest, comprises preparing mixed cell culture solution and cell nutrient solution, loading mixed cell culture solution into artificial cell culture nest, and using ELISA kit to detect collected cell supernatant.
AU ZHAO K
   ZHANG J
   WANG J
   SUN N
   ZHU M
   WAN J
   HUA Z
AE HUA Z (HUAZ-Individual)
GA 2021E1954R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing collagen with mixed cells and artificial cell culture nest, comprises (i) preparing mixed cell culture solution and cell nutrient solution; (ii) setting the parameters of the artificial cell culture nest device; (iii) loading the mixed cell culture solution into the artificial cell culture nest, and starting the circulation device for cell culture; and (iv) using an ELISA kit to detect the collected cell supernatant.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing collagen with mixed cells and artificial cell culture nest.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses artificial cell culture nests to establish simulation conditions of temperature, nutrition, acid-base balance, oxygen balance, carbon dioxide balance, and metabolite emission in the human body when collagen is produced in the human body, which makes the synthesis of collagen closer to the condition of the human body than under traditional 2D cell culture conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The mixed cell culture solution comprises 60-80 vol.% human fibroblasts, 1 vol.% mast cells, macrophages, dendritic cells, Langerhans cells and pigment cell, 0.5 vol.% epidermal stem cells, 0.005 vol.% CD4+ T cells, and 0.01 vol.% CD8+ T cells, and cell nutrient solution (remaining amount) and the cell density is 1x107/ml. The cell nutrient solution includes 50 ng/l angiotensin, 98 mug/l aldosterol, 66 pg/l B-type brain natriuretic peptide, 2.26 nmol/l digoxin, 55 mug/l hyaluronic acid, 28 mug/l laminin, 56 mug/l type IV collagen, 75 mug/l type III procollagen peptide, 10.2 nmol/l of folic acid, 396 pmol/l vitamin B12, 3 mmol/l glucose, 136 mmol/l sodium ion, 5 mmol/l potassium ion, 1 mmol/l magnesium ion, and 2.2 mmol/l calcium ion. The cell nutrient solution further comprises 10 ng/l tanshinone and 5 ng/l quercetin.Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the parameters of the artificial cell culture nest device are pressure of 90-220 mmHg, pH of 7.35-7.45, temperature of 36.2-37.5degrees Celsius, oxygen content of 15-50/100 ml culture solution, and carbon dioxide content of 30-80/100 ml culture solution. In the step (iii), the operation steps of the artificial cell culture nest device comprises (a) opening the first control valve of the liver area so that the nutrient solution is actively transported from the stomach area to the spleen area and reach the lowest set value of the liquid level in the spleen area before the operation of the equipment with the spleen area having no nutrient solution, driving by the power of the heart area with the nutrient solution reaching the heart area and entering the lung area, entering the artificial cell culture nest after the nutrient solution receives the oxygen and carbon dioxide from the gas-permeable membrane in the lung area, providing nutrition and oxygen for stem cells wrapped in simulated extracellular matrix, powering gas exchange by the second peristaltic pump and the second control valve in coordination, and providing the oxygen and carbon dioxide by sterile air; (b) allowing the nutrient solution to flow out from the artificial cell culture nest through the third control valve and entering the spleen area; (c) allowing the part of the nutrient solution in the spleen area to enter the heart area together with the nutrient solution from the liver area, and entering the artificial cell culture nest after passing through the lung area, supplying nutrients and oxygen by stem cells, driving the other part by the third peristaltic pump into the kidney area and returning to the spleen area through the fourth control valve, filtering out urea and other metabolic waste products produced during the growth of stem cells by the specific dialysis membrane in the kidney area, allowing the dialysate to enter the kidney area through the fourth peristaltic pump, and discharging through the fifth control valve; and (d) obtaining relevant instructions from the cloud data center to control cell culture by control platform, and uploading the cell culture data to the cloud data center, providing the patient's life field for the future application of cells for cell growth by control platform, maintaining culture temperature of the system by a constant temperature system, and monitoring the system state by a sensor, which is connected to the control platform through a data interface. In the step (iv), the detection steps comprises taking out the microtiter plate, adding 200 mul of standard substance in sequence, and incubating the sample in the microwell at 25degrees Celsius for 1 hour, washing the plate 3 times, adding 200 mul of substrate into each well, incubating at 25degrees Celsius for 20 minutes in backlight, adding 50 mul stop solution into each well to stop the reaction, and using the automatic microplate reader to detect, and taking the average of 3 replicates of each sample.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA.
