PN CN113512231-A
TI Rubber-plastic foaming auxiliary agent useful in e.g. automobile door and window sealing strip, buffer pad and building construction gasket comprises modified carbon nano tube, modified zinc oxide and expanded microsphere.
AU 
AE JIASHAN XIANMING NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (JIAS-Non-standard)
GA 2021C59260
AB    NOVELTY - Rubber-plastic foaming auxiliary agent comprises 1-3 pts. wt. modified carbon nano tube, 1-3 pts. wt. modified zinc oxide and 1-3 pts. wt. expanded microsphere. The preparation method of the rubber-plastic foaming auxiliary agent comprises adding the modified carbon nano-tube, modified zinc oxide and expanded microsphere into the stirring tank and mixing uniformly to obtain a rubber-plastic foaming auxiliary agent.
   USE - The product is useful in automobile door and window sealing strip, buffer pad, building construction gasket, earthquake-proof material and sports protection facility.
   ADVANTAGE - The agent: has good performance, fast decomposition speed and low temperature; can decompose carbon dioxide and ammonia; has large permeability coefficient, good quality, high strength and less shrinkage, and does not have easy deformation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the rubber-plastic foaming auxiliary agent by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The modified carbon nano-tube is prepared by (a) placing the carbon nano tube, concentrated sulfuric acid and concentrated nitric acid into the conical flask, ultrasonically oscillating at room temperature and frequency 25-35 kHz for 4 hours, adding deionized water to dilute, filtering by microporous membrane with aperture of 450 nm, washing the filter cake with distilled water until the washing liquid is neutral, performing vacuum drying for 4 hours at 80degrees Celsius, obtaining acidified carbon nano-tube, (b) placing acidified the carbon nano tube, diethylenetriamine and dicyclohexylcarbodiimide into a single-mouth flask, raising the temperature to 120degrees Celsius, refluxing and reacting for 36-48 hours, adding absolute ethyl alcohol, ultrasonically processing for 20-40 minutes at frequency of 30-50 kHz, filtering by microporous membrane, washing the filter cake for 3-5 times by deionized water, placing in an oven at 80degrees Celsius for drying for 4 hours to obtain aminated carbon nanotube, (c) placing aminated carbon nanotube, deionized water and absolute ethyl alcohol into the flask, stirring for 5 minutes under the condition of 60-100 revolutions/minute, controlling the reaction temperature at 0degrees Celsius, using constant pressure dropping funnel under the protection of nitrogen to drop the benzene solution of beta-(3,5-di-tert-buty-4-hydroxyphenyl) acryloyl chloride with mass fraction of 13.66% and 20% potassium carbonate solution in the round bottom flask, controlling the pH value to be 9-10, raising the temperature to 25degrees Celsius, constant temperature reaction for 24 hours, filtering, washing the filter cake with distilled water and benzene respectively, washing for 2-3 times and drying for 6-10 hours in the vacuum box at 50degrees Celsiusto obtain the modified carbon nano tube. The modified zinc oxide is obtained by (1) adding the sodium thiocyanate and cyclohexane into a three-mouth flask, stirring for 5 minutes at the rotating speed of 30-50 revolutions/minute, heating cyclohexane to reflux, reacting for 1 hour, distilling azeotropic dehydration to obtain dry sodium thiocyanate, (2) adding the dry sodium thiocyanate into the three-mouth flask under the protection of nitrogen, adding N,N-dimethyl formamide, tetrabutylammonium bromide and (3-Chloropropyl)trimethoxysilane into the three-mouth flask, raising the temperature to 130degrees Celsius, reacting for 3 hours under the condition of 200-300 revolutions/minute, naturally cooling to room temperature, filtering, washing the filter cake with absolute ethyl alcohol for 3-5 times, combining the filtrate, performing normal pressure distillation to remove ethanol, vacuum extracting and distilling to remove N,N-dimethyl formamide under the pressure of 30 mmHg, vacuum extracting and rectifying under the pressure of 1 mmHg to obtain coupling agent and (3) placing the deionized water, absolute ethyl alcohol and zinc oxide into the beaker, ultrasonically dispersing for 20 minutes at frequency of 20-40 kHz, raising the temperature to 85degrees Celsius, dropping obtained coupling agent to the beaker, controlling the dropping speed of 1-3 drops/second, continuing to ultrasonic for 30 minutes, filtering, washing the filter cake by distilled water for 3-5 times, drying to constant weight in the 80-90 oven to obtain the modified zinc oxide. The expaned microsphere is prepared by (1) adding acrylonitrile, methyl acrylonitrile, methacrylate, ethylene glycol dimethacrylate, lauroyl peroxide and cyclopentane into the reaction kettle, stirring uniformly at 60-80degrees Celsius to obtain oil, (2) adding deionized water, sodium chloride, silica sol, polyvinyl pyrrolidone solution and sodium nitrite into the beaker, stirring uniformly, adding in the reaction kettle, mixing at 100-200 revolutions/minute for 30 minutes, transferring to the homogenizer, stirring for 10 minutes at the rotating speed of 7000 revolutions/minute, transferring to the high pressure reaction kettle, controlling the initial pressure 0.3 MPa under the protection of nitrogen, reacting for 20 hours, reducing the temperature, discharging pressure, filtering, washing and drying to constant weight in 50degrees Celsius oven to obtain the expanded microsphere.Preferred Components: The dosage ratio of concentrated sulfuric acid and concentrated nitric acid is 1 g:120 ml:30 ml. The mass ratio of acrylonitrile, methacrylonitrile, methacrylate, ethylene glycol dimethacrylate, lauroyl peroxide and cyclopentane is 28:12:10:0.3:1:30. The content of silicon dioxide in silica sol is 20%; the mass fraction of the polyvinyl pyrrolidone solution is 3.6%.
DC A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); A93 (Roads, building, construction flooring.); A95 (Transport - including vehicle parts, tyres and armaments.)

AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing phosphate glass fiber                involves (a) preparing a mixture comprising at                least one triple superphosphates (TSP) or                phosphorus-potassium (PK) type fertilizer,                phosphoric acid and doping additives containing                trace element, (b) placing the mixture in an oven                for a pretreatment at 100-200 degrees C for 1 hour                to remove water and carbon dioxide, (c) cooking the                mixture at 950-1100 degrees C for 2 hours, (d)                quenching molten mixture in a support at 300                degrees C, and (e) preparing fiber from glass block                obtained by melt drawing spinning process at                550-750 degrees C.

AU HE J
   LIOU C

AE LIANGYU INT CO LTD (LIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021A9596A
AB    NOVELTY - A biodegradable green biomass environmental protection straw includes a straw body and a perforation. The straw body is obtained by granulating, extruding and forming a mixture. The perforation extends along the center line of the straw body and penetrates the straw body. Wherein, the mixture includes a bio-based polymer, a bio-inorganic powder and a lipid, the bio-based polymer includes bio-based polybutylene succinate, and the bio-inorganic powder is selected from calcium-containing inorganic powders Or biochar powder. The biodegradable green biomass environmental protection straw of the present invention does not contain petrochemical industrial products, not only can be decomposed by organisms, but the decomposition only produces carbon dioxide and water without producing methane, and can improve the fertility of the soil, repair the contaminated soil environment and Optimize the soil quality to meet the stringent requirements of global green environmental protection.

TI Preparing calcium cemented artificial rock core            used in reservoir prediction and seismic data inversion            of sandstone reservoir, by mixing quartz sand with            different sizes, moistening, adding calcium hydroxide,            mixing, compacting and drying.
AU HAN T
   WANG P
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 202165503M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium cemented artificial rock                core involves: (1) mixing quartz sand with                different particle sizes; (2) moistening the quartz                sand with water, adding calcium hydroxide powder,                and mixing to obtain a mixed system of quartz sand,                water, and calcium hydroxide; (3) adding the mixed                system to a core mold, pressurizing, and compacting                to obtain a compacted sample; (4) adding the                compacted sample to a reaction kettle, introducing                carbon dioxide gas for reaction, drying, and                repeating the above reaction at least once to                obtain a reacted sample; and (5) drying the                sample.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium                cemented artificial rock core, which is useful in                reservoir prediction and seismic data inversion of                sandstone reservoir (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method simulates compaction and                cementation in the rock formation process. The                prepared sample can provide powerful support for                sandstone reservoir.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The different particle                  sizes of quartz sand include 40-80 mesh, 80-120                  mesh, 100-120 mesh, 200 mesh and 400 mesh in a                  mass ratio of                  (4.7-7.3):(3.7-5):(1.7-3.5):(1.2-2.1):1.                  Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of quartz                  sand, calcium hydroxide and water is                  (17-21):(2-7):1.

Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of the epoxide to carbon dioxide is 1:1-1:10..

PN CN112812883-A
TI Extraction of Akebia trifoliata seed oil involves separating pulp and seeds of Akebia trifoliata, screening out seeds, washing, drying, preheating dried seeds, crushing preheated seeds, sieving, and extracting in supercritical extractor.
AU LI W
   YU H
   WU H
AE UNIV JISHOU (UYJH-C)
GA 202158400Q
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of Akebia trifoliata seed oil involves (i) separating the pulp and seeds of A.trifoliata, screening out seeds, washing with water and drying, (ii) preheating the dried seeds, (iii) crushing the preheated seeds, and sieving the crushed seed powder, (iv) placing the sieved seed powder into the supercritical extraction device and connecting to the carbon dioxide cylinder and (v) separating the extracted product in a separation kettle.
   USE - Extraction of Akebia trifoliata seed oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple extraction of Akebia trifoliata seed oil with increased yield of 30-37% and improved quality, stability and productivity by overcoming the shortcomings of residual oil and organic solvent residue in the physical extrusion method and time-consuming in the extraction method. By using A.trifoliata seed as raw material and supercritical extraction for producing product of A.trifoliata seed oil, so that waste turns into valuable products and improves the added value of the A.trifoliata industry.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the drying temperature is 80-90 degrees C, and the drying time is 30-40 minutes. In step (ii), the preheat treatment is dry heat treatment at temperature of 110-120 degrees C in oven for 1.5-3 hours. In step (iv), the supercritical extraction is performed at pressure of the extraction kettle of 25-35 MPa, the temperature of the extraction kettle of 30-40 degrees C, and the extraction time of 90-120 minutes. In step (v), the stage (I) separation pressure of the separation kettle is 8-10 MPa, and the temperature is 25-35 degrees C and stage (II) separation pressure of the separation kettle is 6-8 MPa, and the temperature is 25-35 degrees C. Preferred Process: In step (iii), the pulverized seed powder is passed through 200-mesh sieve. The crushing and grinding treatment is performed by placing the preheated seeds into a high-speed crusher for crushing, with a rotation speed of 3000-5000 rpm, and crushing time of 1-3 minutes.

AU SU J
   WANG Y
   LIU S
   ZHOU H
   LIU C
   JIAO W
   ZHANG L
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
GA 202144717K
AB    NOVELTY - Directly preparing olefin from synthesis gas                raw materials, comprises mixing synthesis gas raw                materials with catalyst, and reacting to obtain                product stream, performing separation treatment on                the product stream to obtain carbon dioxide,                unreacted synthesis gas stream and mixed                hydrocarbon stream, recycling at least part of the                unreacted synthesis gas stream to the synthesis gas                raw materials by gas participating in the reaction                system. In the gas participating of the reaction                system, the molar ratio of methane content is less                than 20%.
   USE - The method is useful for directly preparing                olefin from synthesis gas raw materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can ensure the high selectivity of                low-carbon olefins and high single-pass conversion                rate while inhibiting the selectivity of methane to                certain extent; can fully utilize the raw                materials; improves the carbon utilization rate in                the synthesis gas raw materials; and is suitable                for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The product stream is                  separated by a decarbonization device and a                  hydrocarbon separation device, where the                  decarburization device is an adsorption                  decarburization device, a solvent absorption                  decarburization device and/or a membrane                  separation decarburization device; and the                  hydrocarbon separation device is a pressure swing                  adsorption device, a cryogenic separation device                  and/or an oil absorption separation device. The                  product stream is subjected to separation                  treatment after heat exchanging with the                  synthesis gas raw material. The separated carbon                  dioxide is at least partially recycled to the                  synthesis gas raw material to participate in the                  reaction, preferably and the carbon dioxide and                  hydrogen is subjected to reverse water-gas shift                  reaction to obtain synthesis gas raw material,                  which is recycled to the synthesis gas raw                  material to participate in the reaction. In the                  method, the synthesis gas raw material is mixed                  with catalyst in a reactor to react to obtain                  product stream; and the reactor is a tubular                  fixed-bed reactor. In the method, the reaction                  temperature is 340-460 degrees C; the reaction                  pressure is 0.5-8 MPa; and the volumetric space                  velocity is 800-8000/hour. Preferred Components:                  In the gas participating of the reaction system,                  the molar ratio of hydrogen/carbon monoxide is                  4:1-1:2. The synthesis gas raw material is                  composed of fresh synthesis gas raw material and                  recycled unreacted synthesis gas raw material.                  The catalyst includes molecular sieve and/or                  oxide, including the weight ratio between oxide                  and molecular sieve is 1:6-6:1, preferably                  1:4-4:1. The molecular sieve is aluminophosphate                  (AlPO) molecular sieve or silicoaluminophosphate                  (SAPO) molecular sieve, preferably AlPO-18,                  AlPO-17, AlPO-34, AlPO-14, AlPO-11, AlPO-5,                  SAPO-18, SAPO-17, SAPO-11 and/or SAPO-5. The                  oxide is oxide having carbon monoxide and carbon                  dioxide conversion activity, preferably oxides of                  zinc, chromium, indium, zirconium, aluminum                  and/or gallium and their composite oxides.

GA 2020B8802Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a sulfur-nitrogen                co-doped porous carbon material involves (a)                dissolving glucosamine hydrochloride in water and                adding the resulting solution to a hydrothermal                reactor for hydrothermal carbonization, (b) washing                the obtained product repeatedly with deionized                water and drying, (c) grinding chitosan or the                dried product, mixing with potassium hydroquinone                sulfate as an activator, heat treating the                resulting mixture in a tube furnace protected by                inert gas at high temperature and cooling naturally                to room temperature, and (d) adding the product to                hydrochloric acid for treatment, repeatedly washing                with deionized water and drying to obtain the                product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a                sulfur-nitrogen co-doped porous carbon                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids the use of strong corrosive                activators such as potassium hydroxide. The                material has a high specific surface area, high                pore capacity and excellent adsorption                characteristics for carbon dioxide.

Preferred Method: The                  step (c) involves high temperature treating at                  700-900 degrees C for 1-2 hours. The step (a)                  involves carrying out hydrothermal carbonization                  at 200 degrees C. The step (b) involves drying at                  100 degrees C.

PN CN111925633-A
TI Modified polybutyrate adipate terephthalate material used for degradable anti-counterfeiting plastic bags, comprises polybutyrate adipate terephthalate, polypropylene carbonate, modified nano-lignin and modified halloysite nanotubes.
AU ZHANG W
AE JINJIANG KAIJI MACROMOLECULE MATERIAL CO (JINJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020B5530Q
AB    NOVELTY - Modified polybutyrate adipate terephthalate material comprises 55-70 wt.% polybutyrate adipate terephthalate, 10-25 wt.% poly(3-hydroxybutyrate-co-3-hydroxyvalerate), 10-15 wt.% polypropylene carbonate, 5-12 wt.% modified nano-lignin, 0.05-1 wt.% diphenylmethane diisocyanate, 5-12 wt.% modified halloysite nanotubes and 0.5-1 wt.% fructus momordicae extract.
   USE - The modified polybutyrate adipate terephthalate material is useful for degradable anti-counterfeiting plastic bags (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material has high relative tearing strength of anti-counterfeiting strips, high tensile strength, excellent barrier performance, excellent flexibility and excellent antibacterial properties, and is completely degraded into carbon dioxide and water under the action of microorganisms without pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the material, involving uniformly stirring and mixing the above-mentioned components using a high-speed mixer to obtain an initial mixture, adding the initial mixture to a twin-screw extruder, extruding, pelletizing, cooling, and pelletizing to obtain the material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The modified halloysite nanotubes are prepared by adding acetic acid solution of chitosan to aqueous solution of polyvinyl alcohol, adding pentanediol, stirring in a water bath at 40-50 degrees C, adding alkali to adjust pH to 10-11, stirring, adding titanate coupling agent, heating, stirring, adding acid solution to adjust pH to 7-8, distilling under reduced pressure, drying, cooling to obtain modified polyvinyl alcohol, dissolving the modified polyvinyl alcohol in dimethylformamide, adding halloysite nanotubes, stirring and mixing to obtain a mixed solution, adding to aluminum trichloride solution, and reacting at 55-75 degrees C for 8-20 hours to obtain the modified halloysite nanotubes, where the concentration of polyvinyl alcohol in the mixed solution is 5-15 wt.%, and the weight ratio of halloysite nanotubes to polyvinyl alcohol is 1:(5-12).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The composite silane coupling agent is prepared by slowly adding vinyltrimethoxysilane and gamma -methacryloxypropyltrimethoxysilane to distilled water under the protection of protective gas, stirring for 0.3-0.6 hour, heating at 65-70 degrees C, reacting for 0.8-1.5 hours, removing low-boiling substances in vacuum, continuously heating at 115-130 degrees C, until there are no exudates, and cooling to room temperature to obtain the composite silane coupling agent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The modified nano-lignin is prepared by (a) dissolving chlorogenic acid in organic solvent, adding sodium hypophosphite under stirring, mixing, adding nano-lignin, and continuously stirring and mixing so that nano-lignin and chlorogenic acid are fully contacted to obtain a suspension, and (b) heating at 70-80 degrees C, adding composite silane coupling agent, maintaining the reaction under high shear stirring for 30-50 minutes, draining, washing, and freeze-drying to obtain the modified nano-lignin, where the molar ratio of chlorogenic acid, composite silane coupling agent and nano-lignin is (2-4):(0.05-0.1):1. Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of polybutyrate adipate terephthalate, poly(3-hydroxybutyrate-co-3-hydroxyvalerate) and polypropylene carbonate is (60-65):(15-20):(12-15). Preferred Properties: The density of polybutyrate adipate terephthalate is 1.15-1.36 g/cm3, and the weight average molecular weight is 60000-80000, and the melting point is 100-130 degrees C.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The titanium nitride is nanocrystals, nanospheres, nanoflowers, nanowires, nanorods, and nanosheets of titanium nitride. The titanium nitride is a mixture of nanocrystals and nanowires in a weight ratio of 1-3:1. The organic polymer matrix comprises polyethylene oxide, polyvinylidene fluoride, polyvinylidene fluoride-hexafluoropropylene, polymethacrylic acid, methyl ester, polyvinyl chloride, polyvinyl alcohol, polyacrylic acid, polyacrylonitrile, polyethyl methacrylate, polye-caprolactone, and chitosan. The plasticizers are azobisisobutyronitrile, azobisisoheptonitrile, dimethyl azodiisobutyrate, tert-butyl hydroperoxide/white block, tert-butyl hydroperoxide/sodium metabisulfite, peroxide benzoyl/N,N-dimethylaniline, ammonium persulfate/sodium bisulfite, potassium persulfate/sodium bisulfite. The electrolyte includes a lithium salt and an organic solvent. The organic solvent is ester solvents, ether solvents and sulfone solvents. The ester solvent is vinyl ethylene carbonate (VEC), 1,3-propane sultone (PS), vinylene carbonate (VC), propyl acetate (PA), fluorocarbonic acid Vinyl ester (FEC), methyl propyl carbonate (MPC), ethyl acetate (EA), methyl acetate (MA), ethyl methyl carbonate (EMC), propylene carbonate (PC), diethyl carbonate (DEC), dimethyl carbonate (DMC) and ethylene carbonate (EC). The ether solvent is tetraethylene glycol dimethyl ether, triethylene glycol dimethyl ether, diethylene glycol dimethyl ether, tetrahydrofuran (THF), 1,3 dioxolane (DOL), 1,4 dioxane (DX), ethylene glycol dimethyl ether (DME), glycoside glycol dimethyl ether, 2-methyltetrahydrofuran, 2,5-diethyltetrahydrofuran, dimethoxypropane. The sulfone solvent is dimethyl sulfone, dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO), sulfolane (SUL), ethyl methyl sulfone, diethyl sulfone, methyl isopropyl sulfone, ethyl methyl sulfone One or more of oxyethyl sulfone, methoxyethyl methyl 4 sulfone, ethyl isopropyl sulfone, and ethyl n-butyl sulfone. The lithium salt includes lithium bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide, lithium bisfluorosulfonimide, lithium trifluoromethanesulfonate, lithium difluorooxalate, lithium difluorobis(oxalate) phosphate, lithium bisoxalate borate, lithium hexafluorophosphate, lithium tetrafluoroborate, lithium nitrate, and lithium perchlorate. The electrolyte solution further contains auxiliary additives, and the auxiliary additives include lithium nitrate, lithium polysulfide, potassium nitrate, cesium nitrate, barium nitrate, ammonium nitrate, lithium nitrite, potassium nitrite, and cesium nitrite, ammonium nitrite, methyl nitrate, phosphorus sulfide, lithium bromide, lithium iodide, indium iodide, diphenylthiazole disulfide, iodonitrobenzene, and triphenylphosphorus. Preferred Conditions: The molar concentration of the lithium salt is 0.5-10 mole/L. The amount of auxiliary additives added in the electrolyte is 1-5%. The content of titanium nitride in the raw material solution is 0.01-10 wt.% and the content of the polymer substrate is 1-20 wt.%. The amount of plasticizer is 40-90 wt.% amount of the organic polymer matrix and the balance is electrolyte. The spinning voltage is 10-80kV, the distance between positive and negative electrodes is 8-25cm, and the propulsion speed of the syringe pump is 10-400 microlitre/minutes. The nozzle diameter is 0.1-5 mm and the humidity is 80%. The composite gel polymer electrolyte is woven from cross-linked fibers containing organic polymer matrix, titanium nitride, plasticizer, and electrolyte.

PN CN111621055-A; CN111621055-B
TI Preparing cyclodextrin-polyimide molecular recognition supramolecular aerogel involves dissolving cyclodextrin and diamine in N-methylpyrrolidone to obtain a mixed solution, and adding the aromatic dianhydride to the mixed solution.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHANG Z
   CHENG X
   GONG L
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 2020916061
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclodextrin-polyimide molecular recognition supramolecular aerogel involves dissolving cyclodextrin and diamine with a substance ratio of 1:2-20 in N-methylpyrrolidone to obtain a mixed solution. The aromatic dianhydride added to the mixed solution and stirred to obtain a cyclodextrin-polyamide acid solution. The cyclodextrin-polyamide acid solution reacted with 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenyl)benzene, added dehydrating agent, stirred uniformly, and aging for 22-26 hours after forming gel. The aging products obtained are sequentially immersed in different solvents, dried with supercritical carbon dioxide and obtained cyclodextrin-polyimide molecular recognition supramolecular aerogel.
   USE - Method for preparing cyclodextrin-polyimide molecular recognition supramolecular aerogel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare cyclodextrin-polyimide molecular recognition supramolecular aerogel is simple and convenient operation, cheap and easy to obtain, has high specific surface area and good mechanical properties, reduces the polyimide content in the aerogel, and reduces the high cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The cyclodextrin is selected from alpha -cyclodextrin, beta -cyclodextrin and gamma -cyclodextrin. The diamine is selected from 4,4-diaminodiphenyl ether and/or 4,4'-diamino-2,2'-dimethylbiphenyl. The aromatic dianhydride monomer is selected from 3,3',4,4'-biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride. The dehydrating agent is selected from a mixture of acetic anhydride and pyridine. Preferred Composition: The amount ratio of the acetic anhydride, pyridine and aromatic dianhydride is 7.5-8.5:7.5-8.5:1. The molar ratio of the 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenyl)benzene to the aromatic dianhydride is 1:20-50. Preferred Conditions: The amount ratio of the diamine and the aromatic dianhydride is n:n+1, where n is the degree of polymerization of the polyimide segment, with a value of 20, 30, 40, 50, or 60. The N-methylpyrrolidone, N-methylpyrrolidone/acetone with a volume ratio of 3:1, N-methylpyrrolidone/acetone with a volume ratio of 1:1, N-methylpyrrolidone/acetone with a volume ratio of 1:3 soaked in acetone for 22-26 hours. The drying temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide is 50-60 degrees C. The pressure is 11.5-12.5 MPa, and the time is 46-50 hours.

The grid flaps are retracted to flush fit inside the wing for cruise flight phase.
   USE - Aerofoil surface area increasing device for an aircraft, particularly twin engine commercial aircraft.
   ADVANTAGE - Vertical tail plane surface area is optimized for both take off and cruise. Fuel consumption is reduced. Carbon dioxide atmospheric emissions are reduced. Simple structure and low weight of actuation system of aircraft wing high-lift system are ensured. Optimized cruise drag and aerodynamic stability augmentation are ensured.

PN CN111349984-A; CN111349984-B
TI Performing clean wet spinning process for preparing graphene fibers, involves extruding graphene oxide solution into glacial acetic acid coagulation bath for coagulation to obtain graphene oxide fibers, and subjecting graphene oxide fiber.
AU GUAN F
   LI X
   LIU H
   WU Y
   ZHOU Y
AE BEIJING INST FASHION TECHNOLOGY (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202063010C
AB    NOVELTY - Performing clean wet spinning process involves extruding the graphene oxide solution into a glacial acetic acid coagulation bath for coagulation to obtain graphene oxide fibers. The graphene oxide fiber is subjected to carbon dioxide laser reduction to obtain graphene fiber.
   USE - Method for performing clean wet spinning process for preparing graphene fibers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform clean wet spinning process which does not require the use of chemical reducing agents, avoids environmental pollution caused by the use of hydroiodic acid in traditional processes, and avoids energy consumption caused by thermal reduction, has high crystallinity, which is close to graphite fiber, and the XRD peak of graphite crystal appears, which is beneficial to the preparation of high thermal conductivity fiber, laser reduction time is short, the reduction of graphene oxide is achieved in a some seconds, which is greatly saves energy and cost-effective.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The concentration of the graphene oxide solution is 10-20 mg/mL. The extrusion speed is 0.5-8 mL/H. The solidification time is 5-20 minutes. The power of the carbon dioxide laser reduction is 1-5 watt, and the time is 1-10 second. The graphene oxide in the graphene oxide solution is prepared by a modified hummer method using flake graphite as a raw material. The particle size of the flake graphite is 40-200 mu m.

PN JP2020083889-A
TI New fluorene derivative used in curable composition for producing cured product e.g. optical component.
AU KONISHI T
   YASUDA R
   MIYAUCHI S
AE OSAKA GAS CHEM KK (OSAG-C)
GA 202049064X
AB    NOVELTY - A fluorene derivative (I) is new.
   USE - New fluorene derivative is used in curable composition for producing cured product (all claimed). Uses include but are not limited to graphitization precursor, carbon dioxide gas separation membrane, fuel cell membrane, luminescent material for organic electroluminescent, adhesive agent for optics, coating agent for LED, microlens for liquid-crystal projector, eyeglass lens, cathode ray tube, electronic paper, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The fluorene derivative has high refractive index even when a molecular structure whose refractive index is expected to be low is introduced and excellent handleability or moldability, and produces cured product having excellent heat resistance, scratch resistance, and flexibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A fluorene derivative of formula (I) is new.
   R1,R2a,R2b,R3a,R3b = substituent;
   k = 0-8;
   Z1a,Z1b,Z2a,Z2b = arene;
   m1,m2,n1,n2,p1,p2,q1,q2 = 0 or more;
   A1a,A1b,A2a,A2b,A3a,A3b = linear or branched alkylene;and
   R4a,R4b = H or methyl.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) dihydroxy compound of formula (II); and
   (2) manufacture of compound (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): Compound (I) is produced by reacting compound (II) with acryl-based compounds of formulae (III) and (IV).R5a,R5b = hydroxy, alkoxy, or halo.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 2 Compounds (I) are disclosed, i.e. 9,9-bis(4-(3-(acryloyloxymethyl)phenyloxy)-3-methylphenyl)fluorene of formula (IA) and 9,9-bis(6-(3-(acryloyloxymethyl)phenoxy)-2-naphthyl)fluorene.
   EXAMPLE - In 500 mL three-necked flask, 4.9 g 9,9-bis(4-(3-(hydroxymethyl)phenyloxy)-3-methylphenyl)fluorene, 15.5 g acrylic acid, 81 g toluene and 0.18 g 2-methoxyphenol were charged. The system was replaced with nitrogen and heated to 60 degrees C to homogenize the components, and p-toluenesulfone was added. 1.97 g p-Toluenesulfonic acid monohydrate was added, substituted with nitrogen, refluxed and dehydrated at 110-115 degrees C for 4 hours. The reaction mixture was washed with 289 g toluene and 30 g of 20 %mass saline, and neutralized with 30 g of 10 %mass aqueous sodium hydroxide solution and 30 g of 20 %mass saline, and the pH of aqueous layer was conformed to 10 or more. 500 mass ppm 2-Methoxyphenol was added to the organic layer to homogenize the organic layer, and the solution was washed twice with 30 g of 20 %mass saline and twice with 30 g ion-exchanged water. The organic layer was treated with activated carbon, filtered through Celite (RTM: diatomaceous earth) and microfiltered, and the filtrate was concentrated to obtain 9,9-bis(4-(3-(acryloyloxymethyl)phenoxy)-3-methylphenyl)fluorene as a pale yellow viscous liquid. The cured product produced using the 9,9-bis(4-(3-(acryloyloxymethyl)phenoxy)-3-methylphenyl)fluorene had a refractive index of 1.625 and 5-%mass temperature decrease rate of 379 degrees C.

TI Cosmetic or nutraceutical composition used to improve condition of skin or hair and to provide anti-aging effects on skin or hair of human comprises at least one active agent containing at least one plant extract of the genus Primula.
AU BERNARD P
   HIMBERT F
   DO QUOC T
   PHILIPPE B
   FRANCK H
   TUAN D Q
AE GREENPHARMA (GREE-Non-standard)
GA 2020356067
AB    NOVELTY - Cosmetic or nutraceutical composition comprises at least one active agent containing at least one plant extract of the genus Primula.
   USE - The cosmetic or nutraceutical composition is useful for: improving the condition of the skin or hair; and providing anti-aging effects on the skin or hair of a human subject, preferably on young or aged skin (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: reduces cortisol level of a living animal; increases flexibility and/or elasticity of dry or aged skin; and protects or strengthens the barrier function of the skin against an external physical, chemical or biological agent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - #obtaining extracts of plant or composition, comprising carrying out a solid/liquid extraction of the plant, optionally ground, in a solvent medium comprising at least one solvent comprising water, alcohols, ketones, ethers, polyols, chlorinated solvents or supercritical carbon dioxide;
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 0.01-2.5 wt.% at least one extract of the genus Primula in powder form or 0.01-25 wt.% a plant extract of the genus Primula in encapsulated form. The composition comprises a cream, an oil or a solution if it is intended for a topical application, where the plant extract of the genus Primula is mixed with ingredients known in cosmetics to obtain the desired presentation for the intended use. Preferred Components: The plant extract comprises extracts of Primula veris, Primula vulgaris and/or Primula sikkimensis. Preferred Method: The solid/liquid extract is produced in a water/glycerin mixture in a proportion of 10-90 vol.% water, for 1-4 hours with gentle stirring, at room temperature. The solid/liquid extraction is carried out on the aerial parts of the plant of the genus Primula.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - 11 Beta-Hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 1 (11 beta -HSD1) inhibitor. The ability of extract to inhibit 11 beta -HSD1 was tested by using in vitro assay. The result showed that the extract of Primula veris exhibited an IC50 value of 40.7 mu g/ml.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of composition is by topical or oral route (claimed). No dosage details given.
   EXAMPLE - 900 g Dried and crushed Primula veris flowers were macerated in 10.8 l ethanol and 7.2 l water for 24 hours. The extract was then filtered until a clear solution was obtained. The extract was then discolored using 45 g activated charcoal, then the extract was filtered again. The ethanol was then evaporated under reduced pressure, then the extract was spray-dried to to obtain 120 g dry extract.

AE YUNNAN LVXIN BIOLOGICAL PHARM CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)

PT P
PN CN110983043-A; CN110983043-B
TI Preparing high-grade nickel-iron using low grade laterite nickel ore comprises mixing uniformly crushed laterite nickel ore and reducing agent, granulating, self-reducing, selectively oxidizing, melting and separating metal from gangue.
AU HANG G
   XUE Z
   WU Y
   LIU C
AE UNIV WUHAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UWSC-C)
GA 202031414F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-grade nickel-iron using low grade laterite nickel ore comprises mixing uniformly crushed laterite nickel ore and reducing agent to obtain a mixture, granulating the mixture, self-reducing completely in a protective atmosphere to obtain a self-reduced product, selectively oxidizing the self-reduced product in weak oxidizing atmosphere to obtain oxidized product, melting the oxidized product and separating the metal from gangue to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful in preparing high-grade nickel-iron using low grade laterite nickel ore.
   ADVANTAGE - The nickel-iron has a nickel recovery rate of higher than 93%. The method: does not require the addition of sulfur-containing or alkali-containing additives, which avoids corrosion of equipment and pollution of these additives to nickel-iron products; has low melting temperature, low energy consumption; and realizes resource utilization of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reducing agent is a solid powdery carbonaceous reducing agent, preferably coal powder and/or coke powder. The molar number of carbon atoms in the reducing agent should be higher than the sum of molar number of oxygen atoms in nickel and iron oxide in laterite nickel ore. Preferred Method: The process of self-reducing the granulated mixture is carried out at 800-1300 degrees C under a protective atmosphere, preferably inert atmosphere including nitrogen and/or argon and/or reducing atmosphere including carbon monoxide and/or hydrogen. The weak oxidizing atmosphere is carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide/carbon monoxide mixed atmosphere, where the volume percentage of carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide/carbon monoxide mixed atmosphere is higher than 30% or one of carbon dioxide/nitrogen mixed atmosphere. The oxidation temperature is higher than 570 degrees C. The process of melting the oxidized product is carried out at 1400-1650 degrees C and separating the metal from gangue to obtain the product having grade higher than 30%.

PT P
PN CN110804587-A
TI Producing mesenchymal stem cell on large scale by            improved microcarrier cell culture comprises cutting            umbilical cord, culturing, digesting with trypsin,            seeding with microcarrier, incubating, filtering,            centrifuging and collecting pellet.
AU CHEN J
   LI J
   LIU J
AE ZHEJIANG HEALTHFUTURE BIO-MEDICINE TECHN (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202017323C
AB    NOVELTY - Producing mesenchymal stem cell on large scale                by improved microcarrier cell culture comprises (1)                cutting off the umbilical cord from arteriovenous                vein and washing, (2) adding low-sugar Dulbecco's                modified eagle medium (DMEM) culture solution                containing bovine serum and penicillin streptomycin                antibodies, culturing in carbon dioxide incubator                and changing the culture medium after 24-48 hours,                (3) waiting for mesenchymal stem cells to reach                70-80% fusion and digesting with trypsin solution,                (4) seeding mesenchymal stem cells in a                microcarrier suspension at a cell density 2.6-2.9x                105 cells/ml for stirring suspension culture and                (5) removing the upper medium after the cells are                full of microcarriers, adding trypsin solution,                incubating, filtering by cell sieve, collecting the                cell filtrate and collecting the pellet after                centrifugation.
   USE - The method is useful for producing mesenchymal                stem cell on large scale by improved microcarrier                cell culture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The trypsin solution                  in step (3) comprises 0.25% trypsin and 0.02%                  EDTA. The microcarrier suspension in step (4)                  includes a serum-free medium and a microcarrier                  and the density of the microcarrier is 2-3 mg/ml.                  The microcarrier is Cytodex1 (RTM: Surface micro                  carriers for monolayer culture), Cytodex2,                  Cytodex3, Cytopore (RTM: Macroporous microcarrier                  beads) or Cytoline (RTM: Macroporous matrix). The                  trypsin solution is EDTA-trypsin solution and the                  volume ratio of EDTA-trypsin to mesenchymal stem                  cell microcarrier is 2-3:1. The cell sieve is 70                  mu m filter. Preferred Method: The step (4)                  comprises intermittent stirring for 0-12 hours,                  stirring for 5-10 minutes at 20-30                  revolutions/minute, stopping stirring for 15-25                  minutes, stirring for 12-48 hours at 30                  revolutions/minute and for 49-96 hours at 50                  revolutions/minute with 30-50% air saturation of                  dissolved oxygen. The step (5) comprises                  culturing at 150-180 revolutions/minute for 8-12                  minutes at 37 degrees C and centrifuging at                  1500-200 revolutions/minute for 8-12                  minutes.

PN CN110743343-A
TI Method for integrated desulfurization and denitration of flue gas residue gas under oxygen-enriched combustion, involves pressurizing flue gas from burner, and sending to flue gas condenser to restore its sensible and latent heat.
AU LOU X
   SHEN H
   ZHU R
AE YIWU JULONG AUTOMATION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YIWU-Non-standard)
GA 202012806T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pressurizing flue gas from burner, sending to flue gas condenser to restore its sensible and latent heat, passing the pressurized flue gas after exiting condenser from the bottom of direct contact tower, and entering water at the top of direct contact tower. The formed acid water is collected at the bottom of direct contact tower, a portion of which is recovered to the top, and the remaining liquid acid water is used as a fresh water source after deacidification. The acidic water from the bottom of contact tower is used to remove sulfur oxides and nitrogen oxides in the form of sulfuric acid and amino acid, respectively. The carbon dioxide is removed in direct contact tower, and leaves the top of tower for subsequent compression, transportation and storage.
   USE - Method for integrated desulfurization and denitration of flue gas residue gas under oxygen-enriched combustion (claimed).

In the step (ii), the temperature in aseptic box is increased to 0.5 degrees C for every two hours until the temperature rises to 36.5-37.5 degrees C.

PN WO2019234816-A1; IN202017046724-A; IN375528-B; RU2755819-C1
TI Plant for producing urea-containing fertilizer, comprises apparatus for producing urea using ammonia, and scrubber comprising internal space for contacting off-gas comprising ammonia with acidic absorption liquid comprising carbonic acid.
AU TACHIBANA S
   TANAKA Y
   HAGIMOTO A
   KOMADA S
AE MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
GA 2019A3774Q
AB    NOVELTY - A plant (100) comprises a urea production apparatus (22) for producing urea using ammonia, and a scrubber (80B) comprising an internal space for contacting off-gas comprising ammonia which is an off-gas from the fertilizer production plant with an acidic absorption liquid (AA) comprising carbonic acid.
   USE - Plant is used for producing urea-containing fertilizer (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The plant can be easily processed without producing ammonium sulfate during manufacture of fertilizer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of fertilizer comprising urea, which involves producing urea using ammonia, and contacting off-gas generated during production of fertilizer and comprising ammonia with the acidic absorption liquid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a systematic view of the fertilizer manufacturing plant.
   Reformer (1)
   Carbon dioxide recovery apparatus (3)
   Compressor (11)
   Urea production apparatus (22)
   Granulator (61)
   Solid content removal scrubber (80A)
   Scrubber (80B)
   Plant (100)
   Acidic absorption liquid (AA)
   Methane-containing gas (HH)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Arrangement: The plant comprises a reformer (1) for reforming methane-containing gas (HH), a carbon dioxide recovery apparatus (3) for recovering carbon dioxide generated in the reformer, a carbonic acid producing apparatus for producing carbonic acid using the recovered carbon dioxide, a compressor (11) for increasing the pressure of the recovered carbon dioxide supplied to the urea production apparatus, a carbon dioxide supply system (s1) for supplying the carbon dioxide boosted in the compressor to the urea production apparatus, a carbon dioxide supply system (s2) for supplying the carbon dioxide boosted in the compressor to a carbonic acid production apparatus, a solid content removal scrubber (80A) for removing the solid content in the off-gas, a urea aqueous solution supply system for supplying the urea aqueous solution manufactured by the urea manufacturing apparatus to the solid content removal scrubber, a urea aqueous solution return system for returning the urea aqueous solution after contact with the off-gas in the solid content removal scrubber to the urea production apparatus, a combustor for burning fuel, a carbon dioxide supply system (s3) for supplying carbon dioxide generated in the combustor to the internal space, and a granulator (61) for granulating urea produced in the urea production apparatus. The plant comprises an integrated scrubber comprising the solid content removal scrubber and scrubber configured integrally with the solid content removal scrubber above the solid content removal scrubber. The carbonic acid production apparatus comprises a microbubble generator for producing the carbonic acid by dissolving carbon dioxide in water. The plant further comprises an ammonium carbonate supply system for supplying acidic absorption liquid after the off-gas contact in the scrubber to the urea-producing apparatus, and an acidic absorption liquid supply system for supplying the acidic absorption liquid after the off-gas contact in the scrubber to the solid content removal scrubber. The scrubber is arranged downstream of the solid content removal scrubber in the flow direction of the off-gas. The combustor comprises a boiler. The reformer is configured to reform the methane-containing gas using heat or water vapor generated by the combustion of the fuel in the boiler. The off-gas of the plant comprises an off-gas of the granulating device.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.)
MC C10-A13C; C11-C05; C14-T04

AU ZENG L
   YANG G
AE ZENG L (ZENG-Individual)
   YANG G (YANG-Individual)
GA 2019A61232
AB    NOVELTY - A formula powder comprises 30-55 wt.% sugar, 5-24 wt.% protein, 0-0.13 wt.% vitamin, 4.717-9 wt.% mineral, 0-20 IU/100 mL vitamin D, 0-0.3 mg/100 mL iron, and 30-50 mg/100 mL calcium.
   USE - The formula powder is used for promoting intellectual development of infants and young children and reducing incidence of autism and mental retardation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The powder promotes the body to release demand signals.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the formula powder comprising weighing raw materials, mixing mineral and vitamin, adding sugar and other components, adding to three-dimensional dry mixer, mixing for 20-30 minutes, adding to pasteurizer, sterilizing at 80-100 degrees C for 5-10 minutes, rapidly cooling to 35-45 degrees C, packaging, and vacuum filling with nitrogen or carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The formula powder further comprises 0-16 wt.% maltodextrin.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mineral includes but not limited to sodium, potassium, chloride, calcium, phosphorus, magnesium, iron, zinc, manganese, copper, iodine, selenium, rhenium and/or molybdenum.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The vegetable fat powder includes but not limited to isolated milk protein, casein powder, linoleic acid and/or alpha -linolenic acid. The vitamin includes but not limited to, vitamin A, vitamin B, vitamin C, vitamin D, vitamin E, vitamin K, folic acid, biotin, pantothenic acid and/or choline. The vitamin A is vitamin A palmitate and/or beta -carotene. The vitamin B is vitamin B1, vitamin B2, vitamin B3, vitamin B6 and/or vitamin B12. The vitamin D is vitamin D3. The vitamin K is vitamin K1.
   ACTIVITY - Nootropic.

PN WO2019223032-A1; CN110526807-A; CN110526807-B
TI Continuous reaction device useful for            hydroformylation of alpha-olefins to produce aldehyde,            comprises a reaction unit, a separation unit, and a            recycling unit.
AU AN L
AE AN L (ANLL-Individual)
   AN L (ANLL-Individual)
   SHANGHAI ZAIZHENG CHEM TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019998007
AB    NOVELTY - Continuous reaction device comprises a                reaction unit, a separation unit, and a recycling                unit. The recycling unit is located between the                reaction unit and the separation unit. The reaction                unit is connected to the separation unit. A                catalyst is circulated between the reaction unit                and the separation unit through the recycling                unit.
   USE - The continuous reaction device is useful for                hydroformylation of alpha -olefins to produce                aldehyde (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device achieve continuous production of                aldehyde, can quickly remove reaction heat, and is                conducive to the progress of the hydroformylation                reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing aldehyde by a hydroformylation reaction,                comprising performing hydroformylation reaction of                olefin, carbon dioxide and hydrogen in presence of                an aqueous solution containing rhodium and its                ligand a catalyst aqueous solution using the                continuous reaction device to prepare                aldehyde.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the continuous reaction device.
   Reactor (1)
   Oil-water separator (2)
   Purge gas condenser (3)
   Recycling pump (4)
   Feed mixer (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The device includes: a                  reactor (1), an oil-water separator (2) and a                  recycling pump (4). The reaction unit includes a                  discharge port. The discharge port is connected                  to the feed port I of the separation unit. The                  discharge port is located at an upper part of the                  reaction unit. The separation unit includes a                  catalyst outlet. The catalyst outlet is connected                  to the recycling unit. The catalyst outlet is                  located at the bottom of the recycling unit. The                  separation unit includes a catalyst feed port II.                  The catalyst feed port II is located above the                  catalyst outlet. The reaction unit includes a                  feed port III. The feed port III is connected to                  the recycling unit through a feed mixer (5). The                  reaction raw materials and catalyst enter the                  feed port III through the feed mixer. The                  separation unit includes a product extraction                  outlet. The product extraction outlet is an oil                  phase extraction outlet. The separation unit                  includes a purge gas outlet. The separation unit                  includes a condensate inlet. The cooled liquid in                  the purge gas condenser (3) enters the separation                  unit through the condensate inlet. The cooled gas                  in the purge gas condenser is processed by the                  purge gas to the heat cabinet. The reaction unit                  is a reactor. The separation unit is an oil-water                  separator, and the recycling unit is a recycling                  pump. The reactor is a gas-liquid-liquid                  three-phase reactor; the reactor is a tubular                  reactor. The operating medium of the shell in the                  tubular reactor is a cooling liquid. The aqueous                  phase in the oil-water separator is an aqueous                  catalyst solution, and the oil phase includes a                  reaction product aldehyde. The recycling pump is                  a centrifugal pump, a plunger pump, a screw pump                  or a diaphragm pump. The reactor includes a feed                  port III and a discharge port. The oil-water                  separator includes an oil phase production                  outlet, a purge gas outlet, a feed inlet I, a                  catalyst feed inlet II, a catalyst outlet, and a                  condensate inlet. The position of the catalyst                  feed port II is higher than the catalyst outlet.                  The purge gas outlet is connected to the purge                  gas condenser. The feed port I is a reaction                  mixture feed port. The discharge port is a                  reaction mixture discharge port. The outlet is                  connected to the inlet I. The carbon monoxide,                  hydrogen and olefin are mixed through the feed                  mixer and enter the reactor through the feed port                  III.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method includes at                  least: (a) adding the catalyst aqueous solution                  to the separation unit, turning on the recycling                  unit, and circulating the catalyst aqueous                  solution between the reactor and the oil-water                  separator through the recycling unit; (b) passing                  the reaction raw materials to the reaction unit;                  and (c) passing the material obtained by the                  reaction in the reaction unit through a                  separation unit to perform phase separation and                  catalyst recycling. The method includes at least                  the following steps: (i) adding the catalyst                  aqueous solution as the aqueous phase to the                  oil-water separator, start the recycling pump,                  and establish the recycling between the reactor                  and the oil-water separator, (ii) stabilizing the                  cycle, continuously introducing olefin, carbon                  monoxide and hydrogen, mixing them in the feed                  mixer as the organic phase into the reaction                  mixture feed port at the bottom of the reactor,                  performing the hydroformylation reaction under                  the action of the catalyst, and discharging the                  reaction mixture from the discharge port at the                  top of the reactor and enters the oil-water                  separator, and (iii) separating the reaction                  mixture in an oil-water separator, returning the                  aqueous phase containing the aqueous catalyst                  solution to the reactor through the recycling                  pump for continued use, and continuously                  producing the oil phase containing the product                  aldehyde and unreacted raw materials. The                  reaction in the step (ii) is carried out at                  30-220 degrees C, preferably 50-180 degrees C                  under reaction pressure of 0.5-5 MPa, preferably                  0.6-4.8 MPa. Preferred Components: The reaction                  raw materials include carbon monoxide, hydrogen                  and olefins. The aqueous catalyst solution in the                  step (i) is an aqueous solution of a                  water-soluble phosphine ligand containing                  rhodium, where the content of rhodium in the                  catalyst aqueous solution is 200-300 ppm, and the                  concentration of water-soluble phosphine ligand                  is 4.8-7.2%, in the method, the volume ratio of                  aqueous phase and organic phase is 3-5:1,                  hydrogen to carbon monoxide is 1-2:1, preferably                  1.05:1.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Biogas plant used in various fields of agriculture to produce biogas, has shaft that is provided with spiral auger with antifoam connected to gear motor, and water shutter placed on side of unit in reactor tank.
AU COVALIOV V
   COVALIOVA O
   UNGUREANU D
AE UNIV MOLD STATE (MOVA-C)
   UNIV MOLDOVA STATE (MOVA-C)
GA 2019A81407
AB    NOVELTY - The biogas plant has a reactor tank with a spiral stirrer with a rod, feed pipe and outlet pipe for processed biomass, and a gas exhaust pipe connected to the gas holder. The reactor tank is provided with a conical base with an opening. Openings for loading biomass are placed inside the external conical silo reactor. The shaft is provided with a spiral auger with antifoam connected to a gear motor, and a water shutter placed on the side of the unit in the reactor tank. The liquid is discharged from the upper portion of the reactor tank, enriched with carbon-dioxide soluble in liquid, and sent to a granular floating filter, and then to the tank for microalgae cultivation.
   USE - Biogas plant used in various fields of agriculture and processing industry to produce biogas.

PN US2019323042-A1; WO2019204029-A1; CA3097019-A1; CN111918957-A; AU2019257224-A1; KR2020136487-A; BR112020018577-A2; EP3781668-A1; IN202017048857-A; US11053517-B2; JP2021520814-W; EP3781668-A4; ZA202007102-A
TI Operating fermentation process with bioreactor containing bacterial culture in liquid nutrient medium, by passing feedstock comprising e.g. carbon monoxide to bioreactor, and intermittently passing electrolyzer feedstock to bioreactor.
AU CONRADO R J
   SIMPSON S D
   MIHALCEA C D
   CONRADO R
   SIMPSON S
   MIHALCEA C
AE LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZA TECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
GA 201988092C
AB    NOVELTY - Operating a fermentation process with a bioreactor containing a bacterial culture in a liquid nutrient medium, involves: (a) passing a C1 feedstock comprising one or both of carbon monoxide (CO) and carbon dioxide (CO2) from an industrial process to the bioreactor, where the C1 feedstock has a cost per unit; (b) intermittently passing an electrolyzer feedstock comprising one or both of CO and hydrogen (H2) from an electrolyzer process to the bioreactor, where the electrolyzer feedstock has a cost per unit; and (c) fermenting the culture to produce fermentation products. Each of thefermentation products has a value per unit.
   USE - The method is useful for operating a fermentation process with a bioreactor containing a bacterial culture in a liquid nutrient medium (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrolyzer process intermittently supplies an electrolyzer feedstock, and is capable of substantially improving the performance and/or economics of the fermentation process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: Supplementing C1 feedstock comprising CO2 with an electrolyzer feedstock comprising H2 increases the amount of CO2 fixed in the fermentation products. The C1 feedstock is treated to remove constituent prior to passing the C1 feedstock to the bioreactor. The electrolyzer feedstock is treated to remove constituent prior to passing the electrolyzer feedstock to the bioreactor. The C1 feedstock is pressurized prior to passing the C1 feedstock to the bioreactor. The electrolyzer feedstock is pressurized prior to passing the electrolyzer feedstock to the bioreactor. The electrolyzer process is completed at pressure. The electrolyzer process is powered by a renewable energy source, where the renewable energy source is selected from solar, hydro, wind, geothermal, biomass, and nuclear. The culture further produces a post-fermentation gaseous substrate. The method further involves passing the post-fermentation gaseous substrate to the electrolyzer process. Post-fermentation gaseous substrate is treated to remove one or more constituent prior to being passed to the electrolyzer process.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The C1 feedstock further comprises H2. The electrolyzer feedstock displaces a portion of the C1 feedstock as a function of the cost per unit of the C1 feedstock and the cost per unit of the electrolyzer feedstock. It displaces portion of the C1 feedstock when the cost per unit of electrolyzer feedstock is less than the cost per unit of C1 feedstock. The electrolyzer feedstock supplements the C1 feedstock when the supply of the C1 feedstock is insufficient for the fermentation process. It supplements the C1 feedstock as a function of the cost per unit of the electrolyzer feedstock and the value per unit of the fermentation product. It supplements the C1 feedstock as a function of the cost per unit of the C1 feedstock, the cost per unit of the electrolyzer feedstock, and the value per unit of the fermentation product. It supplements the C1 feedstock when the cost per unit of the electrolyzer feedstock is less than the value per unit of the fermentation product. At least one of the constituents removed from the C1 feedstock (or constituent removed from the post-fermentation gaseous substrate) is selected from sulfur compounds, aromatic compounds, alkynes, alkenes, alkanes, olefins, nitrogen compounds, phosphorus-containing compounds, particulate matter, solids, oxygen, oxygenates, halogenated compounds, silicon containing compounds, carbonyls, metals, alcohols, esters, ketones, peroxides, aldehydes, ethers, and tars. At least one constituent removed from the electrolyzer feedstock comprises oxygen. At least one of the fermentation products is selected from ethanol, acetate, butyrate, 2,3-butanediol, lactate, butene, butadiene, ketones, methyl ethyl ketone, ethylene, acetone, isopropanol, lipids, 3-hydroypropionate, isoprene, fatty acids, 2-butanol, 1,2-propanediol, and 1-propanol. At least one of the fermentation products is microbial biomass. Constituent removed from the post-fermentation gaseous substrate is sulfur.

AB    NOVELTY - The methane production apparatus (100) comprises an oxygen generator (110) for generating hydrogen ions and oxygen from water, and a methane generation unit (150) in which hydrogen ions from the oxygen generator react with carbon dioxide in the presence of microorganisms to generate methane. A separator (130) selectively permeates hydrogen ions from the oxygen generator to the methane generation unit. The oxygen generator has an anode (111), and the methane generation unit has a reduction electrode (151). A microfilm containing the microorganism is attached on the cathode. The reduction electrode is a carbon electrode. The carbon electrode is selected from graphite granules, graphite felt, graphite rods, graphite cloth, graphite granules, carbon tubes, and carbon fibers. The microorganism comprises methanothermobacter thermautotrophicus.

GA 201981805U
AB    NOVELTY - Growing homogeneous near stoichiometric lithium tantalate crystal by co-solvent method comprises (a) mixing lithium carbonate, tantalum pentoxide, and potassium carbonate in a molar ratio of 48.75:51.25:13 to obtain base material, (b) grinding and mixing the base materials to obtain a mixture, (c) heating the mixture for decomposing the carbon dioxide, and calcining the mixture to obtain lithium tantalate polycrystal material, and (d) compacting the lithium tantalate polycrystal material, then placing in a crucible, heating to a molten state, insulating, and then using Czochralski method.

PT P
PN CN110128741-A; CN110128741-B
TI Preparing polyolefin foamed material involves weighing polyolefin, intumescent flame retardant, synergist, mixing and adding extruder for melt extrusion to obtain premix.
AU PANG Y
   ZHENG W
   HUANG P
   LIU W
   WU M
AE NINGBO INST MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY & ENG C (CANM-C)
GA 2019727397
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyolefin foamed material involves weighing 60-90 pts. wt. polyolefin, 10-40 pts. wt. intumescent flame retardant, 0-5 pts. wt. synergist, mixing and adding extruder for melt extrusion to obtain a premix, adding premix to a single-screw extruder and extruding foam to obtain a polyolefin foamed material. The single-screw extruder comprises an upper-stage screw and a lower-stage screw in series, the temperature of the upper-stage screw is 130-230 degrees C, and the temperature of the lower-stage screw is 80-180 degrees C, and the extrusion port temperature of the mold is 80-180 degrees C, and supercritical carbon dioxide is injected at 1/5-1/3 of the upper screw.
   USE - Preparing polyolefin foamed material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare polyolefin foamed material, which improves dispersion effect of the flame retardant, has uniform cell structure, high expansion ratio, controllable degree of orientation, and excellent flame retardancy and mechanical properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The polyolefin is one of polyethylene, polypropylene, ethylene propylene rubber, and ethylene-octene copolymer. The intumescent flame retardant is a mixture of an acid source, a carbon source and a gas source having a mass ratio of 2-4: 0-1: 0-1. The synergist is one of antimony trioxide, calcium carbonate, magnesium carbonate, silica, montmorillonite, seaweed, and kaolin. The total mass of the intumescent flame retardant and the synergist is 10-40% by mass of the premix. The amount of supercritical carbon dioxide is 0.5-15% by mass of the premix. The injection pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide is 7-25 MPa. The temperature of the lower screw is lower than the temperature of the upper screw, and the difference is T, 15 degrees C not less than T not less than 80 degrees C. The extrusion foaming is performed to obtain a polyolefin foaming material, collected the polyolefin foaming material by using a traction device. The pulling device has a draw ratio of 0-80, pulling The stretching speed is 0.1-3.0 m/seconds. The polyolefin foam material has a cell density of 104-1014 cells/cm3 and an expansion ratio of 1.25-30.

PN CN109776463-A
TI Preparation of dialkyl 2,5-furandicarboxylate, involves performing catalytic oxidation of furfural to citric acid using copper oxide as catalyst, and introducing tannic acid, carbonate, ionic liquid and alcohol into reaction vessel.
AU MA Y
   WANG L
   DI N
   LI X
   CHEN Z
   JIE F
AE CHONGQING CHEM IND VOCATIONAL COLLEGE (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201948273J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of dialkyl 2,5-furandicarboxylate, involves (A) performing catalytic oxidation of furfural to citric acid using copper oxide as a catalyst, (B) introducing tannic acid, carbonate, ionic liquid and alcohol into a high pressure reaction vessel, sealing, purging with nitrogen three times, charging with carbon dioxide to 1-4 MPa, the reaction temperature is 100-150 degrees C, and the reaction time is 6-24 hours, the ionic liquid is 1-butyl-3methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate, 1-butyl-3methylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate, 1-butyl-3methylimidazolium nitrate, 1-ethylethyl ether-3methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate, 1-ethylethyl ether-3methylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate, 1-ethyl methyl ether-3 methylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate, 1-nitrilepropyl-3methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate, 1-nitrilepropyl-3methylimidazolium hexafluorophosphoric acid or 1-nitrilepropyl-3methylimidazolium nitrate.
   USE - Preparation of dialkyl 2,5-furandicarboxylate.
   ADVANTAGE - The dialkyl 2,5-furandicarboxylate has relatively mild reaction conditions, high catalytic activity and high reaction selectivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the copper oxide to furfural is from 0.1:1 to 0.3:1. The mass ratio of the carbonate to tannic acid is from 0.1:1 to 0.3:1. The mass ratio of the ionic liquid to tannic acid is 10:1 to 20:1. The molar ratio of the alcohol to citric acid is from 2:1 to 10:1. Preferred Condition: The catalytic oxidation reaction conditions are: nitrogen purge 3 times, filling air or oxygen to 0.1-3 MPa, the reaction temperature 30-60 degrees C, and the reaction time 4-6 hours. Preferred Components: The carbonate is sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, cesium carbonate or rubidium carbonate. The alcohol is methanol, ethanol, propanol or butanol. Preferred Process: The dialkyl 2,5-furandicarboxylate is prepared by introducing furfural and copper oxide into the high pressure reaction vessel, sealing, purging with nitrogen three times, filling with air or oxygen to 0.1-3 MPa, the reaction temperature is 30-60 degrees C, the reaction time is 4-6 hours, removing the catalyst by filtration, acidifying the filtrate to pH 3-2.5, suction filtering, water washing, drying, drying temperature is 80-120 degrees C, and drying time is 2-4 hours, the mass ratio of the catalyst to furfural is 0.1:1 to 0.3:1, sequentially introducing the carbonate, ionic liquid and alcohol into the high-pressure reaction vessel, sealing, purging with nitrogen three times, charging with carbon dioxide to 1-4 MPa, the reaction temperature is 100-150 degrees C, and the reaction time is 6-12 hours, adding organic solvent, after the reaction is completed, recovering the ionic liquid and the catalyst by filtration, distilling and recovering the organic solvent.

PN CN109694115-A
TI Treating black and stink water involves preparing a nitrogen-doped titanium dioxide photocatalyst and a silicon dioxide fixing plate, and supporting nitrogen-doped titanium dioxide photocatalyst on silicon dioxide fixing plate.
AU XU L
   YUAN H
   SU G
   CHEN T
   FU M
   WANG S
   WANG K
   LI X
   SUN G
AE UNIV TSINGHUA HEFEI INST PUBLIC SAFETY (UYQI-C)
   UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 201941763K
AB    NOVELTY - Treating black and stink water by using solar light involves preparing a nitrogen-doped titanium dioxide photocatalyst and a silicon dioxide fixing plate. The nitrogen-doped titanium dioxide photocatalyst is supported on silicon dioxide fixing plate, and density of water is close to floating on water by using a silicon dioxide fixing plate. The method involves irradiating sunlight to illuminate surface of the silicon dioxide fixing plate to which titanium dioxide is attached, and then electronic transition is carried out when titanium dioxide absorbs sunlight and electron-hole pair is generated, and pollutant in black odorous water body in electron-hole pair is adsorbed, oxidized and decomposed into non-toxic products such as carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for treating black and stink water by using solar light.
   ADVANTAGE - The drawing shows a schematic view of treat black and stink water by using solar light, which improves working duration of catalyst and optimizes treatment capability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: The nitrogen-doped titanium dioxide photocatalyst is prepared by hydrolyzing and precipitating tetrabutyl titanate to prepare hydrated titanium oxide by using a saturated urea solution, and calcining hydrated titanium dioxide in an air atmosphere at 400-700 degrees C for 2 hours to obtain nitrogen-doped titanium dioxide photocatalyst. The silicon dioxide fixing plate is prepared by adhering and compressed mesoporous silicon dioxide viscously into a silicon dioxide fixing plate by an adhesive. The nitrogen-doped titanium dioxide photocatalyst is loaded onto silicon dioxide fixing plate by dipping or spraying method.

PT P
PN CN109686501-A; CN109686501-B
TI Preparation of graphene/aluminum composite            conductive material by preparing porous graphene on            organic substrate, soaking to aluminum powder solution,            vacuum drying, vacuum sintering, peeling off composite            precursor, and pressing.
AU JU B
   CHEN J
   LI N
AE SUZHOU TANSU JIDIAN NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201940942N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of graphene/aluminum composite                conductive material comprises exposing organic                polymer film polyimide to carbon dioxide infrared                laser irradiation to carbonize surface and form                porous graphene on organic substrate; dispersing 10                g pure aluminum powder particles into 100 mL                deionized water, stirring, soaking porous graphene                until aluminum powder particles are fully                penetrated into porous mesh of graphene, taking                out, vacuum drying, vacuum sintering, and peeling                off graphene/aluminum composite precursor from                organic substrate; and pressing precursor into                pre-formed block, preheating mold and preforms,                soaking metal pure aluminum liquid melted,                releasing mold, cooling to obtain graphene/aluminum                composite material, hot extrusion molding and                thermal annealing. The portion not affected by                laser acts as substrate to support the                graphene.
   USE - Method for preparation of graphene/aluminum                composite conductive material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively avoids agglomeration of                graphene and improves conductivity and mechanical                property of product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The infrared laser                  irradiation is done at laser wavelength of                  9.3-10.6 mu m, laser power of 1-5 W, pulse                  frequency of 1-50 kHz and at laser scanning speed                  of 200-2000 mm/second. The high-temperature                  sintering is done at 200-400 degrees C. The hot                  extrusion molding is done at 300-600 degrees C.                  The thermal annealing is done at 200-100 degrees                  C for 1-12 hours. Preferred Components: The                  porous graphene has hole diameter of 1-10 mu m.                  The pure aluminum powder has particle size of                  1-10 mu m.
