PN JP2019065096-A; JP7005254-B2
TI New amidate polymer used for catalyst used in manufacture of polyurethane for e.g. coating material.
AU TAKAHASHI S
   MIYAGI M
   NITTA S
   TSUBOI H
AE KOEI CHEM IND CO LTD (KOEI-C)
   KOEI CHEM CO LTD (KOEI-C)
GA 201937021J
AB    NOVELTY - An amidate polymer (I) is new.
   USE - New amidate polymer is used for catalyst used in manufacture of polyurethane (all claimed). Uses include but are not limited to motor vehicle component, construction application, household appliance, heavy anticorrosion component, plastic coating, adhesive agent, coating material and sealing agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The amidate polymer produces catalyst which dissolves in various aprotic solvents, and has low hygroscopicity, and no generation of carbon dioxide during manufacture of polyurethane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An amidate polymer of formula (I) is new.
   R1 = bond or 1-3C alkylene;
   R2 = H or methyl;
   y = 1 or more;
   D' = group of formula (a1);
   R3-R6 = hydrocarbyl optionally containing heteroatoms;
   two or more R3-R6 = ring;
   X = N, O or S;and
   a = 0 or 1.
   When X is nitrogen atom, a is 1; (ii) when X is oxygen atom or sulfur atom, a is 0. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of polymer (I); and
   (2) manufacture of polyurethane, which involves reacting a polyol and polyisocyanate in presence of a catalyst containing polymer (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The unsaturated ethylene monomer is a vinyl-type monomer of formula (V), preferably styrene.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (claimed): The polymer (I) is produced by polymerizing a urethane compound of formula (II) and optionally an unsaturated ethylene monomer to obtain a urethane-containing polymer of formula (III), and reacting polymer (III) with a carboxylate compound of formula (IV), or copolymerizing compound (II) and unsaturated ethylene monomer to obtain polymer (III), and reacting polymer (III) with compound (IV).R21 = hydrocarbyl optionally containing heteroatoms.Preferred Components: The polymer (I) is preferably of formula (I-1). The compound (IV) is preferably 1,3-dimethylimidazolium-2-carboxylate.x = 1 or more;R15 = H or methyl;andR16-R20 = H, 1-10C hydrocarbyl, 1-10C alkoxy, halo, trifluoromethyl, cyano, cyanomethyl, or alkoxysilyl.
   EXAMPLE - In a 50 mL test tube, 0.29 g p-vinyl-N-t-butoxycarbonylaniline, 0.03 g 2,2-azobis(isobutyronitrile), 3.03 g styrene and 27.8 g dimethyl sulfoxide were added, bubbled with nitrogen for 10 minutes, and reaction was performed at 70 degrees C for 22.5 hours. The reaction solution was dripped to 250 mL methanol and purified by reprecipitation to obtain 1.7 g copolymer of formula (I-a) having a polystyrene conversion number average molecular weight of 17000 measured by gel permeation chromatography analysis. The polyurethane produced using the catalyst containing copolymer (I-a) had excellent solubility with respect to the coating material.

PN US2019106347-A1; CA3020395-A1; US11440825-B2
TI Treating waste involves processing waste activated            sludge in thermophilic digester to produce thermophilic            digestate, increasing pH of thermophilic digestate and            cooling thermophilic digestate and processing cooled            thermophilic digestate.
AU WANSTROM C M
   ANDERSON T M
   MOERMAN W H M
AE SCHWING BIOSET INC (XCMG-C)
   SCHWING BIOSET INC (XCMG-C)
GA 201932178M
AB    NOVELTY - Treating waste involves processing waste                activated sludge in a thermophilic digester to                produce a thermophilic digestate, increasing pH of                the thermophilic digestate, cooling the                thermophilic digestate; processing the cooled                thermophilic digestate in a mesophilic digester to                produce a mesophilic digestate, producing, with a                struvite reactor, a treated digestate containing                struvite and separating struvite from the treated                digestate.
   USE - Method for treating waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat waste in cost                effective manner without any maintenance                expenses.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a waste                treatment system, which comprises thermophilic                digester that produces a thermophilic digestate                from waste activated sludge, an air stripper that                removes carbon dioxide from the thermophilic                digestate to increase pH of the thermophilic                digestate, a heat exchanger that cools the                thermophilic digestate, a mesophilic digester that                produces a mesophilic digestate from cooled                thermophilic digestate, a struvite reactor that                produces a treated digestate containing struvite                from the mesophilic digestate, and unit for                separating struvite from the treated                digestate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Process: The method for treating                  waste involves dewatering treated digestate from                  the struvite reactor to separate effluent and                  biosolids enriched with struvite, separating                  struvite with a cyclone separator from the                  treated digestate prior to dewatering, thickening                  the waste activated sludge by removing filtrate,                  and dewatering the mesophilic digestate prior to                  supplying the mesophilic effluent to the struvite                  reactor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PN CN109589780-A; CN109589780-B
TI Preparation of hydrogen fluoride gas adsorbent involves carrying out acid leaching treatment of red mud, collecting solid phase, drying, grinding, mixing polyvinyl alcohol and sodium alginate, cross linking drying and calcining.
AU ZHANG Y
   HUANG Q
   LIU J
   SONG H
   WANG Y
   MO W
   WANG W
   ZENG L
   RAO R
   ZHAO C
   LI Z
   WU P
   HE B
   FAN W
   CHEN Y
   ZENG H
   XU Q
AE GUANGZHOU POWER SUPPLY CO LTD (CSPG-C)
   GUANGZHOU POWER SUPPLY BUREAU (CSPG-C)
GA 201935081K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of hydrogen fluoride gas adsorbent involves carrying out acid leaching treatment of red mud obtained by Bayer process of aluminum, removing liquid portion, collecting the solid phase, drying, grinding to obtain a solid powder of red mud, mixing a mixed solution of polyvinyl alcohol and sodium alginate with solid powder of red mud mixture, adding red mud mixture to a mixture of calcium chloride and boric acid, cross linking polyvinyl alcohol and sodium alginate in the red mud mixture, solidifying with boric acid and calcium chloride to form a precursor particles, drying and calcining.
   USE - Preparation of hydrogen fluoride gas adsorbent (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogen fluoride gas adsorbent is capable of removing hydrogen fluoride gas in an environment containing carbon dioxide gas with easily available raw materials and utilizes waste resources and easy to obtain.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The acid leaching treatment is carried out by using any one of aqueous solution of hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid and nitric acid having a concentration of 0.05 mol/L-2 mol/L. The mass ratio of acid liquid to the red mud in the acid leaching treatment is 1-10:1. The mixture of calcium chloride and boric acid has mass concentration of 2-6 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The polyvinyl alcohol is at least one of polyvinyl alcohol-6000 and polyvinyl alcohol-8000. Preferred Composition: The mixture of polyvinyl alcohol and sodium alginate has a mass concentration of 5-15 wt.% and 1-4 wt.%. The mass ratio of mixed solution of polyvinyl alcohol and sodium alginate to the solid powder of red mud is 2-10:1.

PT P
PN CN109453762-A; CN109453762-B
TI Preparing modified clay mineral loaded palladium            catalyst useful in producing hydrogen by formic acid            decomposition comprises loading modified clay mineral            carrier on active precious metal palladium.
AU HUANG Y
   ZHANG Q
   LIANG Z
AE UNIV HUNAN (UYHU-C)
GA 201925441T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified clay mineral loaded                palladium catalyst comprises loading the modified                clay mineral carrier on 0.5-5 wt.% active precious                metal palladium on the clay mineral.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in producing hydrogen                by formic acid decomposition.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent catalytic effect on                the dehydrogenation reaction of formic acid, and                has good industrial application value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                of modified clay mineral loaded palladium catalyst                in producing hydrogen by decomposing formic acid                comprising dehydrogenating the liquid phase raw                material containing formic acid and modified clay                mineral loaded palladium catalyst in a reaction                vessel, and carrying out membrane separation to                obtain hydrogen product, connecting to hydrogen                fuel cell downstream, trapping the remaining carbon                dioxide gas raw material to be converted into the                reaction vessel by hydrogenation reaction to obtain                formic acid as liquid phase raw material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The modified clay                  mineral carrier is acid modified activated clay                  mineral carrier. The clay mineral comprises                  sepiolite, hydrotalcite or attapulgite, where the                  molar ratio of each part in composition is 1:1.                  The acid modified activated clay mineral carrier                  is modified by grafting with silane coupling                  agent, where the silane coupling agent comprises                  3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane (APTMS),                  3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane (APTES),                  2-aminoethyl-3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane                  (AEAPTS) and diethylene                  triaminopropyltrimethoxysilane (TRI). The liquid                  phase material containing formic acid comprises a                  mixture of formic acid and water, where the molar                  concentration of formic acid is 0.001-25 mol/l.                  The recycled gaseous reaction product is mixed                  gas of hydrogen and carbon dioxide. Preferred                  Method: The method comprises (i) adding clay                  mineral powder to the acid solution, stirring,                  separating the clay mineral powder, washing to                  neutral, drying at 80-120 degrees C for 10-14                  hours, and grinding to obtain acid-activated clay                  mineral powder, (ii) adding anhydrous ethanol                  solution and certain amount of silane coupling                  agent in drop wise manner while stirring,                  filtering, washing with anhydrous ethanol, and                  drying at 80-120 degrees C for 10-14 hours to                  obtain silane coupling agent-modified clay                  mineral carrier, (iii) immersing in aqueous                  solution of sodium chloropalladium, adjusting pH                  with sodium carbonate aqueous solution after                  stirring for certain period of time, slowly                  adding sodium borohydride solution, reducing                  palladium suspension by filtering, washing and                  drying by vacuum drying at 50-100 degrees C for                  10-14 hours to obtain palladium catalyst                  supported by silane coupling agent clay mineral,                  where the acid solution in step (i) is                  hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, or nitric acid                  solution has a concentration of 1-10 ml/l. The                  mass ratio of clay mineral powder to acid                  solution is 1:10-50, and stirring time is 10-30                  hours. The temperature of magnetic stirring                  electric heating sleeve is 30-90 degrees C,                  rotation speed is 200-800 revolutions/minutes,                  and modification time is 15-30 hours. The mass                  ratio of silane coupling agent to acid-activated                  sepiolite powder is 0.05-1:1. The concentration                  of silane coupling agent in anhydrous ethanol                  solvent is 1-10 g/l. The temperature of magnetic                  stirring electric heating sleeve in step (ii) is                  30-90 degrees C, rotation speed is 200-800                  revolutions/minutes, and stirring time is 10-40                  hours. The palladium containing compound in step                  (iii) is palladium chloride, potassium                  chloropalladium or palladium nitrate, where the                  concentration of palladium ions in                  palladium-containing aqueous solution is 0.03-1                  mol/l. The reducing agent aqueous solution has a                  mass fraction of 60-85%, and reducing agent is                  hydrazine hydrate, borohydride, ascorbic acid and                  polyol e.g. ethylene glycol. The temperature of                  magnetic stirring electric heating sleeve in step                  (iii) is 25-80 degrees C, rotation speed is                  200-800 revolutions/minutes, adsorption load time                  is 3-10 hours, and reduction reaction time is                  0.5-12 hours. The dehydrogenation reaction is                  carried out at 25-150 degrees C under a pressure                  of 0.1-1 MPa at a stirring speed of 200-800                  revolutions/minutes.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. TI Barrier-resistant recycled concrete comprises ordinary Portland cement, natural aggregate, modified recycled coarse aggregate fly ash, polycarboxylic acid water reducing agent, sodium gluconate, nano-carbon fiber, and water.
AU LIN R
   CHEN X
AE SHUGUANG PREFABRICATED BUILDING TECHNOLO (SHUG-Non-standard)
GA 201925384Y
AB    NOVELTY - A barrier-resistant recycled concrete comprises 75-85 pts. wt. ordinary Portland cement, 50-60 pts. wt. natural aggregate, 90-100 pts. wt. modified recycled coarse aggregate, 8-10 pts. wt. fly ash, 1-2 pts. wt. polycarboxylic acid water reducing agent, 8-12 pts. wt. sodium gluconate, 5-6 pts. wt. nano-carbon fiber, and 35-45 pts. wt. water. The preparation of modified recycled coarse aggregate involves (1) crushing waste concrete to 5-40 mm aggregate, washing and drying for obtaining dry aggregate, (2) uniformly mixing penetrant, nano- titanium dioxide and water for obtaining modifying liquid, adding dry aggregate to modified liquid, and uniformly stirring to obtain suspension, (3) adding suspension to a closed container and introducing carbon dioxide gas, filtering, and air-drying.
   USE - Barrier-resistant recycled concrete.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the preparation of barrier-resistant recycled concrete.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The dry aggregate has water content of less than or equal to 3%. The modified recycled coarse aggregate comprises 10-15 pts. wt. penetrant, 1-3 pts. wt. nano titanium dioxide and 80-90 pts. wt. water. The volume ratio of dry aggregate to modified liquid is 20-30:100.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The penetrant is chosen from any one of octylphenol polyoxyethylene ether, nonylphenol polyoxyethylene ether, and fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether.

PN US10184141-B1
TI New recombinant microorganism comprising recombinant nucleic acid configured to express glucose-1-phosphate adenylyltransferase, useful for producing glycogen.
AU PFLEGER B F
   MARKLEY A L
AE WISCONSIN ALUMNI RES FOUND (WISC-C)
GA 2019073694
AB    NOVELTY - A recombinant microorganism modified with respect to a native microorganism, is new. The recombinant microorganism comprises a recombinant nucleic acid configured to express a glucose-1-phosphate adenylyltransferase. The recombinant microorganism is a Cyanobacterium, exhibits enhanced glucose-1-phosphate adenylyltransferase activity compared to native microorganism, a native glycogen synthase expression level, and native glycogen synthase activity, and produces an increased amount of glycogen compared to native microorganism while having a growth rate of at least a growth rate of native microorganism when grown photoautotrophically in presence of light and 10% carbon dioxide.
   USE - The recombinant microorganism is useful for producing glycogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The recombinant microorganism produces increased amount of glycogen compared to a corresponding microorganism not comprising recombinant nucleic acid at a rate of at least 50 mg/l/day, preferably 200 mg/l/day, more preferably 300 mg/L/day or more.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for producing glycogen, which involves culturing the recombinant microorganism.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation: No preparation method is given. Preferred Biomolecules: The glucose-1-phosphate adenylyltransferase is allosterically regulated by a compound chosen from adenosine diphosphate and adenosine monophosphate. The glucose-1-phosphate adenylyltransferase comprises a sequence which has at least 90% sequence homology to a 431 amino acid sequence (SEQ ID NO: 2) fully defined in the specification. The glucose-1-phosphate adenylyltransferase comprises a glycine at a position corresponding to position 336 of SEQ ID NO: 2. The nucleic acid comprises a glucose-1-phosphate adenylyltransferase coding sequence operably connected to a promoter not operably connected to the coding sequence in nature. The promoter is an inducible promoter or a constitutive promoter. The recombinant microorganism exhibits a native 1,4- alpha -glucan-branching enzyme expression level, or enzyme activity and producing glycogen in an amount of at least 25 %mass dry cell weight (DCW).
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109182391-A; CN109182391-B
TI Preparation of biogas by cutting crop straw into small sections, irradiating with 60 cobalt-gamma-ray at natural room temperature, mixing crop straw and inoculum, adding biomass carbon, mixing, fermenting and conducting anaerobic digestion.
AU WANG F
   SU X
   LI Q
AE UNIV HUNAN AGRIC (UYAG-C)
GA 201909292X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of biogas comprises taking crop straw, cutting into small sections, irradiating with 600-1200 kGy 60 cobalt (Co)- gamma -ray at natural room temperature, mixing the crop straw and inoculum according to volatile solid concentration ratio of 1:1.5-5, adding 2-8 wt.% (based on the total weight of crop straw and inoculum) biomass carbon, uniformly mixing, fermenting, controlling fermentation total solid concentration of 20-28%, carrying out anaerobic digestion, where pH of inoculum is 7.0-8.4, and obtaining mixture of biogas residue and biogas liquid of biogas pool normally-produced gas, or normally-produced gas.
   USE - Preparation of biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation, high straw utilization rate, fast fermentation speed, stable gas production, and methane yield(excluding carbon dioxide) of 200-250 L/kg VS.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The crop straw is subjected to natural drying or artificial drying to water content of less than 10%. It is rice straw, corn straw and/or rape straw. The solid state anaerobic digestion is conducted at 35-39 degrees C for 20-24 days. The method comprises cutting into small sections having a length of 2-4 cm.

PN CN109158111-A; CN109158111-B
TI Preparation of catalyst used for catalytic degradation of formaldehyde in air by sonicating multi-walled carbon nanotubes, manganese acetate tetrahydrate and cobalt acetate tetrahydrate solids in water, evaporating, and calcining.
AU YANG X
   JIANG Q
   LI W
   DING Y
AE FOSHAN NUOLIN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION T (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201906520E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of catalyst comprises adding beaker with multi-walled carbon nanotubes, manganese acetate tetrahydrate and cobalt acetate tetrahydrate solids, adding water, placing to ultrasonic bath, sonicating for 1 hour to dissolve solids, magnetically stirring, slowly heating until water is almost evaporated, stop heating, stirring, and evaporating remaining trace moisture using waste heat; and transferring solid to magnetic boat, putting to atmosphere tube furnace, passing through air, heating to 120 degrees C for 3 hours, passing through argon gas, heating to 500 degrees C for 3 hours, turning off heating valve, cooling furnace to room temperature, turning off argon gas, and taking out material.
   USE - Method for preparation of catalyst used for catalytic degradation of formaldehyde in air.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst converts formaldehyde into carbon dioxide and water which are non-toxic and harmless to human body, solves the formaldehyde pollution problem in air, and has low cost and no secondary pollution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume of deionized water added is 1-1.5 times the volume of the solids. Preferred Method: The slow heating is done at rate of less than or equal to 2 degrees C/minute. The air flow rate is 50-100 mL/minute. The argon gas flow rate is 100-200 mL/minute.

PN CN108975356-A; CN108975356-B
TI Producing sodium bicarbonate and ammonium sulfate from sodium sulfate and mirabilite involves introducing reaction gas to sulfate suspension for carbonizing reaction to form sodium bicarbonate and ammonium sulfate.
AU TANG Y
   GONG Y
   LEI L
   KONG D
   SONG G
   FENG R
   GUO L
   LIU C
AE SICHUAN GOLDEN-ELEPHANT SINCERITY CHEM (SICH-Non-standard)
   BEIJING EDGEIN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   BEIJING EDGEIN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   SICHUAN GOLDEN-ELEPHANT SINCERITY CHEM (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2018A2980Y
AB    NOVELTY - Producing sodium bicarbonate and ammonium sulfate from sodium sulfate and mirabilite involves introducing reaction gas to sulfate suspension for carbonizing reaction to form sodium bicarbonate and ammonium sulfate. The solid and liquid is separated to obtain sodium bicarbonate and mother liquid in carbonizing reaction or into sodium sulfate or mirabilite and reaction gas into sodium sulfate aqueous solution for carbonization reaction to produce sodium bicarbonate and ammonium sulfate, solid-liquid separation, to obtain sodium bicarbonate and mother liquid. The ratio of sodium sulfate and water in carbonization reaction is 50-79:100. The reaction gas includes carbon dioxide and ammonia.
   USE - Method for producing sodium bicarbonate and ammonium sulfate from sodium sulfate and mirabilite.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce sodium bicarbonate and ammonium sulfate from sodium sulfate and mirabilite that reduces water content in system, circulate amount of raw material in system, heat needed by distilled water when processing mother liquid and energy consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The ratio of sodium sulfate and water in carbonization reaction is 65-79:100. The ratio of sodium ion and ammonium ion is 0.85-1:1. The temperature of carbonization reaction is 25-45 degrees C. Preferred Process: The sodium sulfate suspension is prepared by mixing sodium sulfate and/or mirabilite with water. The method further involves introducing sodium sulfate or mirabilite into sodium sulfate suspension. The mixture of ammonia, carbon dioxide and water obtained by absorbing exhaust gas of carbonization reaction. The mother liquid vaporized ammonia with sodium sulfate aqueous solution or sodium sulfate suspension to form aqueous solution or suspension containing ammonium bicarbonate and send to carbonization reaction. The mother liquid I is subjected to evaporation treatment at temperature of 100-130 degrees C to obtain distilled ammonia liquid and mixed gas of carbon dioxide, ammonia and water vapor. The mixture gas is recycled to pre-carbonization reaction or Carbonization reaction. The ammonia distilling liquid is dehydrated at 80-100 degrees C to obtain recovered sodium sulfate and mother liquid II. The sodium sulfate is recycled to prepare sulfate suspension. The mother liquid II is cooled and crystallized at temperature of 5-55 degrees C to obtain mixed salt of caroboante salt, ammonium sulfate and mother liquor III. The mixed salt is recycled, combined with ammonia distilling liquid treatment at 80-100 degrees C. The mother liquor (III) is evaporated and dehydrated to obtain mother liquor (IV) and ammonium sulfate. The mother liquor (IV) is circulated and combined with mother liquor II treatment.

PN CN108565519-A
TI Recycling process of waste lithium battery electrolyte comprises washing waste lithium battery, discharging, opening explosion-proof port of battery, collecting, injecting electrolyte, setting parameters, collecting and distilling.
AU ZHANG Q
AE FOSHAN OUTEKELANG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201876916A
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling process of waste lithium battery electrolyte comprises (i) washing the waste lithium battery, discharging at a rate of 0.5-1.2 to below 0.5 V, opening the explosion-proof port of the battery, and collecting the electrolyte at a temperature less than or equal to -50 degrees C, (ii) injecting the electrolyte into the supercritical fluid extraction device under the protection of inert atmosphere, adding carbon dioxide as extracting agent, acetonitrile as co-solvent into the reaction kettle, setting parameters for extraction reaction and collecting extracting liquid and (iii) distilling the extraction liquid and separating to obtain electrolyte salt lithium hexafluorophosphate.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling process of waste lithium battery electrolyte.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents the electrolyte from exposing in the air water produced by decomposing the poisonous gas and is simple and environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the discharge is carried out at a rate of 1.0 C to 0 V, and the electrolyte collection temperature is -100 to -70 degrees C. In the step (ii), the parameters comprises extraction pressure, extraction temperature and extraction time, where the extraction pressure is 20-40Mpa preferably 25-30Mpa, extraction temperature is 25-55 degrees C, preferably 35-40 degrees C and the extracting time is 20-50 minutes preferably 35-40 minutes. In the step (iii), the distillation temperature is 100-130 degrees C preferably 120 degrees C, the time is 3-24 hours preferably 15 hours, obtained lithium hexafluorophosphate is dried, sealing and storing.

PT P
PN CN108358227-A; CN108358227-B
TI Preparing calcium carbonate bone rod-shaped nano particle assembly structure involves separating oil particles, dispersing calcium oxide particles in de-ionized water, adding compound surfactant, and uniformly stirring.
AU TANG J
AE NINGBO PLASTIC METAL PROD CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201862906T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium carbonate bone rod-shaped nano particle assembly structure involves separating oil particles, where particle diameter is 1-1.5 mm, dispersing calcium oxide particles in de-ionized water, adding compound surfactant, and uniformly stirring. The material liquid obtained is subjected to microwave process for 40-50 minutes. The obtained material liquid is added into inlet end of atomizing nozzle, and carbon dioxide gas is introduced into atomizing nozzle air inlet end under ultrasonic condition. The material liquid is sprayed, material liquid is collected, and centrifuged for separation. The clear liquid is taken, centrifuged at high speed, and washed with de-ionized water for 2-3 times. The washed product is re-dispersed in de-ionized water, centrifuged, and repeated for 3 times to obtain calcium carbonate bone rod-shaped nano particle assembly structure.
   USE - Method for preparing calcium carbonate bone rod-shaped nano particle assembly structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound surfactant is benzyldimethyldodecylammonium chloride and dodecyl dimethyl aminoacetic acid. Preferred Condition: The power of the microwave is 500-600 Watts. The ratio of calcium oxide, benzyldimethyldodecylammonium chloride and dodecyl dimethyl aminoacetic acid is 1:0.3-0.5:0.1-0.3. The ratio of the calcium oxide and deionized water is 1:250-400. The ratio of material liquid with carbon dioxide is 1 g:200-400 mL. The centrifuge rotating number is 4000-6000 revolution per minute. The ultrasonic power is 150-200 Watts. The high speed centrifuge rotating number is 8000-12000 revolution per minute. The strong ultrasonic power is 450-700 Watts.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

TI Temperature control method for heating boiler,            involves providing displacement of laplace transform to            shift control action on time coordinate by pure lag            time.
AU ZHOU Z
   FU S
   XUE D
   WANG X
   YU M
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 201857461D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing proportional,                integral, differential (PID) temperature control                system with smith compensation. The compensation                circuit is provided with the controller and smith                predictor. The influence of the pure lag portion on                the control system is eliminated. The displacement                of the laplace transform is provided to shift the                control action on the time coordinate by a pure lag                time such that the transition process and other                performance indicators of the temperature control                system are exactly same.
   USE - Temperature control method for heating                boiler.
   ADVANTAGE - The loss of the overshoot to the equipment                such as the mechanical impact on the heat transfer                pipe is reduced. The method has strong robustness.                The consumption of the coal and discharge of the                carbon dioxide can be reduced. The energy-saving                and environment-friendly can be realized.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating                the PID heating boiler temperature control                adjustment program algorithm with smith predictor.                (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PN CN108216530-A
TI Method for automatically recovering unmanned ship, involves utilizing unmanned ship which transmits status signal at fixed time intervals followed by determining that unmanned ship has fault, when ship fails to transmit status signal.
AU 
AE BEIJING POWERVISION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201853421B
AB    NOVELTY - An unmanned ship automatic recovering method involves utilizing unmanned ship which transmits status signal at fixed time intervals and determining that unmanned ship has fault, when the unmanned ship fails to transmit the status signal or transmit the status signal for more than a certain period of time, where the unmanned ship releases airbag after receiving control signal and the unmanned ship floats out of water surface, under action of the airbag.
   USE - Method for automatically recovering unmanned ship.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables automatically recovering unmanned ship in a simple and reliable manner by monitoring underwater unmanned ship in real time, utilizing controller which can issue control commands, when accident occur and unmanned ship cannot achieve safe floating and bursting air bag and utilizing chemical reaction in airbag to produce gases such as nitrogen or carbon dioxide, such that lifting unmanned ship by buoyancy of airbag and smoothly recovering relevant faults.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves utilizing gas generating device. Preferred Components: The gas generating device introduces chemical substance in gas capsule to realize bursting of airbag. The gas generating device generates nitrogen or carbon dioxide. The gas generating device includes following chemical reactions: (a) reacting sodium nitride and copper oxide to produce sodium oxide, nitrogen and copper, reacting sodium nitride, molybdenum disulfide and sulfur to produce sodium sulfide, molybdenum and nitrogen and reacting sodium nitride, potassium nitrate and silicon dioxide to produce sodium oxide, potassium oxide, silicon dioxide and nitrogen and (b) reacting sodium and nitrogen to form sodium nitride, reacting sodium and potassium nitrate to form potassium oxide, sodium oxide and nitrogen and reacting potassium oxide, sodium oxide and silicon dioxide to form potassium silicate and sodium metasilicate, (c) reacting sodium nitride and nitrogen to form sodium-nitrogen and (d) reacting calcium carbonate and hydrogen chloride to form calcium chloride, water and carbon dioxide.

   (1) a method for preparing tsaoko cardamom essential oil, which involves taking dry tsaoko cardamom, slicing to small blocks, crushing to powder, extracting fructus tsaoko oil as raw material, putting in volatile oil extractor for collecting fruit oil and calculating oil yield, where extraction method of tsaoko cardamom essential oil is selected from group consisting of steam distillation, solvent extraction, carbon-dioxide supercritical extraction technology, subcritical water extraction and microwave-assisted extraction; and
   (2) a emulsion formulation, which comprises tsaoko 10 pts. wt. cardamom essential oil, 20-35 pts. wt. solvent, 5-15 pts. wt. auxiliary agent and 40-55 pts. wt. water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Tsaoko cardamom essential oil comprises 50-63 wt.% 1, 8-cineole, preferably, 58-63 wt.%. Preferred Components: The solvent used in emulsion is selected from group consisting of methanol, ethanol, normal butanol, normal propyl alcohol, isopropanol, isobutyl alcohol, n-amyl alcohol, isoamyl alcohol, hexanol, acetone, dichloromethane, ethyl acetate, dimethyl sulfoxide, tetrahydrofuran, dimethylformamide, cyclohexanone, pyrrolidone, N-methyl pyrrolidone, or mixture of two or more, preferably, solvent is preferably, selected from ethanol, methanol, propanol, isopropanol, butanol, glycerol, acetone or mixture of two or more, more preferably, solvent is selected from methanol, ethanol or mixture of both. Preferred Process: The water vapor distillation extracting method involves drying fructus tsaoko small pieces, crushing to powder, soaking in water in weight ratio of 1:3-1:20, preferably, 1:7 for 0-24 hours, , preferably, 1 hour, extracting for 0.5-10 hours, preferably, 7 hours, collecting tsaoko cardamom essential oil, calculating oil yield, where dried fruit can be dry fruit obtained by drying. The herbal essential oil emulsion is obtained by mixing and stirring 1 pts. wt. herbal essential oil, 3.5 pts. wt. ethanol, 1.5 pts. wt. Tween and 4 pts. wt. water. It is also prepared by taking 1 pts. wt. essential oil of grass fruit, 2 pts. wt. ethanol, 1.5 pts. wt. alkyl phenol polyoxyethylene ether surfactant and 5.5 pts. wt. water, mixing and stirring uniformly. It is further obtained by mixing 0.5 pts. wt. polyoxyethylene ether surfactant, 4 pts. wt. water, 1 pts. wt. herbal essential oil, 3.5 pts. wt. ethanol, 1.5 pts. wt. alkylphenol ethoxylate surfactant and 4 pts. wt. water and stirring uniformly. Preferred Conditions: The tsaoko cardamom essential oil is formulated as bacteriostatic agent of solid, liquid, emulsion, spray, and aerosol.

AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical preparation of arylalkyl carbonates and diaryl carbonates, comprises performing anodic reaction of carboxy based compound (I) with aromatic compounds (II), naphthalene or propane-2,2-diyldibenzene in presence of at least one solvents, preferably aprotic solvents.
   USE - The compounds are useful for producing polycarbonate by melt transesterification process (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy to handle carbon dioxide as non-combustible gas than carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Electrochemical preparation of arylalkyl carbonates and diaryl carbonates, comprises performing anodic reaction of carboxy based compound of formula (R1-O-C(=O)-O-) (I) with aromatic compounds of formula (II), naphthalene or propane-2,2-diyldibenzene in presence of at least one solvents, preferably aprotic solvents.
   r1 = alkyl (preferably methyl, or ethyl or tert-butyl, or cycloalkyl, preferably cyclohexyl), or aryl (preferably tert-butylphenyl , cumylphenyl-, naphthyl or phenyl, preferably phenyl); and
   R2-R4 = H or alkyl (preferably tert-butyl or cycloalkyl, preferably cyclohexyl), preferably methyl or H.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The anodic reaction is carried out in a boron-doped diamond electrode as an anode. The anodic electrochemical reaction is carried out at current density of 0.5-100 mA/cm2 and temperature of 10-80 degrees C. (I) is generated by performing electrochemically anodic reaction of alcohol compound (R21OH) (V) with carbon dioxide at a cathode.R21 = 1-6C alkyl (preferably cycloalkyl), or aryl (preferably tert-butylphenyl, cumylphenyl, naphthyl or phenyl).Preferred Components: The solvent includes acetonitrile or mixture of acetonitrile with other solvents, preferably dimethylformamide and dimethylsulfoxide. (I) is used as salts with cationic quaternary ammonium compounds containing tetraalkylammonium, preferably tetrabutylammonium as counterions. (V) includes methanol, ethanol, n-propanol, n-butanol, tert-butanol, n-pentanol, n-hexanol, cyclohexanol, tert-butylphenol, cumylphenol, naphthol or phenol.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN107935158-A
TI Treating phenol simulated coking wastewater involves weighing out phenol and water, configuring simulated coking water, placing simulated coking water into ozone reactor, adjusting pH of simulated coking water, adding semi-coke.
AU CAI H
   WU H
   DANG Z
AE UNIV XIAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYXS-C)
GA 2018327864
AB    NOVELTY - Treating phenol simulated coking wastewater involves weighing out phenol and water and configuring simulated coking water, placing simulated coking water into the ozone reactor, adjusting pH of simulated coking water, adding the semi-coke, and mixing semi-coke in simulated coking water, connecting the ozone generator to the ozone reactor and turning on ozone generator. The ozone generated by the ozone generator flows into ozone reactor to start the reaction when air bubbles appear in the ozone reactor, and simulated coking water is treated followed by processing simulated coking water.
   USE - Method for treating phenol simulated coking wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat phenol simulated coking wastewater, which solves the technical problem that the prior ozone is difficult to completely oxidize organic matters into carbon dioxide and water in coking wastewater treatment, and the treatment makes the phenol removal rate as high as 70.01%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Condition: The unreacted ozone is introduced into a container containing a sodium thiosulfate solution and a potassium iodide solution through a pipe. The simulated coking water has a pH of 3- 11.2 and the amount of semi-coke is 4-40 times that of the simulated coking water. The temperature in the control ozone reactor is 15-36 degrees C. The flow rate of ozone from the ozone generator to the ozone reactor is 0.3L/min-0.6L/min, and the reaction time is 0.5h-4.5h. The pH of simulated coking water is adjusted to 11.02. The input amount of semi-coke is 40 times the mass of phenol in simulated coking water. The temperature in the controlled ozone reactor is 26 degrees C. The flow of ozone from the ozone generator to the ozone reactor is 0.5 L/min. The reaction time is 3.5 h.

PN CN107937422-A; CN107937422-B
TI New panD mutant gene useful for catalytically producing beta-alanine, comprises metal ions is carbon dioxide, ferrous ion, manganese, calcium, ferric ion, aluminum, and copper or nickel.
AU CHEN K
   LI H
   WANG X
   LU Y
   OUYANG P
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 201832733K
AB    NOVELTY - PanD mutant gene comprises fully defined 417 nucleotide sequence (SEQ ID No.1) as given in the specification or fully defined 417 nucleotide sequence (SEQ ID No.3) as given in the specification, is new.
   USE - The genetic engineering is useful in catalytically producing beta -alanine (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM are also included for:
   (1) panD mutant gene encoding protein, where full defined 138 amino acids (SEQ ID No.2) as given in the specification or full defined 138 amino acids (SEQ ID No.4) as given in the specification; and
   (2) recombinant plasmid comprising the panD mutant gene; and
   (3) genetic engineering bacterium, comprising recombinant plasmid; and
   (4) preparing beta -alanine, comprising adding genetically engineered bacteria into an aqueous solution of L-aspartic acid and pH buffer, and adding metal ions, reacting at 20-60 degrees C for 2-15 hours
   .
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The panD mutant gene comprises fully defined 417 nucleotide sequence (SEQ ID No.1) as given in the specification is the 34th base of the panD gene changed from A to G, and fully defined 417 nucleotide sequence (SEQ ID No.3) as given in the specification is the 50th base of the panD gene changed from T to C. The concentration of L-aspartic acid in the initial reaction system is 5-200 g/l. The metal ion is carbon dioxide, ferrous ion, manganese, calcium, ferric ion, aluminum, and copper or nickel, and the concentration of metal ion in the initial reaction system is 5-100 mM. Preferred Method: The construction of genetically engineered bacterium comprises cloning panD mutant gene into the pET-28a (+) expression vector to obtain recombinant plasmid pET28a-panDC.g, and transforming recombinant plasmid into Escherichia coli BL21 (DE3) competent cells to obtain recombinant Escherichia coli BL21(DE3)-pET28a-panDC.

PT P
PN EP3299410-A1
TI Partially cross-linked polymer foam used for e.g. vacuum insulation panels, comprises opacifying agent, and has mean pore size in median of preset range.
AU OBERHOFFER R
   MUELLER A
AE SUMTEQ GMBH (SUMT-Non-standard)
GA 201824114W
AB    NOVELTY - A partially cross-linked polymer foam comprises an opacifying agent. The polymer foam has a mean pore size in the median of 10 nm to 10 mm.
   USE - Partially cross-linked polymer foam is used for vacuum insulation panels, insulating material, and lightweight construction components (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polymer foam has improved thermal insulation property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of polymer foam, which involves adding the opacifying agent and a crosslinker to a monomer to obtain a monomer mixture, polymerizing the obtained monomer mixture, drying the obtained polymer, swelling the polymer using a swelling agent, placing the swollen polymer in a carbon dioxide atmosphere at a pressure of 50-500 bar and a temperature of 0-120 degrees C, and lowering the pressure after 10-180 minutes to atmospheric pressure; and
   (2) use of opacifying agent in the polymer foam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The polymer foam preferably has a mean pore size in the median of 100 nm to 2 mm. The polymer foam has a volume expansion ratio of 4-30, preferably 7-25, a density of 30-300 kg/m3, preferably of 45-150 kg/m3, a degree of crosslinking of 0.05-80 mol%, preferably 0.1-10 mol%, and a proportion of the total heat conduction by infrared radiation under normal conditions of up to 25%, preferably up to 10%. The opacifying agent absorbs or scatters radiation having a wavelength of 1-100 mm, preferably 3-30 mm. The opacifying agent has a mean primary particle size in the median of 0.01-5 mm, or a BET surface area of more than 10 m2/g. At least 75 vol.%, preferably at least 95 vol.% of pores in the foam have a diameter of 50 nm to 10 mu m, preferably 100 nm to 5 mu m. Preferred Composition: The content of opacifying agent in the polymer foam is 1-30 wt.%, preferably 1.5-15 wt.% with respect to total mass of the polymer foam. The polymer foam is obtained from a monomer chosen from styrene, methyl methacrylate, lactic acid, vinyl chloride, thermoplastic polyurethane and polyethylene or its derivatives.

GA 2018170437

Alarm signal is generated if the carbon dioxide concentration in the sleeping cabin exceeds a set concentration value.

PN US2022175696-A1
TI Treating disease related to impaired lymphatic function involves administering to subject in need lipophilic extract of member of Iridaceae family selected from Iris germanica, Iris pallida, and mixtures.
AU SLIWA-PRIMORAC A
   POTTERAT O
   HAMBURGER M
   DETMAR M
AE ETH ZUERICH (ETHE-C)
   UNIV BASEL (UBAS-C)
GA 202279009W
AB    NOVELTY - Treating a disease related to impaired lymphatic function involves administering to a subject in need a lipophilic extract of a member of the Iridaceae family selected from Iris germanica, Iris pallida, and mixtures.
   USE - Method for treating a disease related to impaired lymphatic function such as primary lymphedemas, and secondary lymphedemas or treating under-eye puffiness, swollen legs, and cellulite (all claimed).
Test details are described but no results given.
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. a method for treating under-eye puffiness, swollen legs, and cellulite, which involves administering to a subject the cosmetic or pharmaceutical composition comprising a lipophilic extract of a member of the Iridaceae family selected from Iris germanica, Iris pallida, and mixtures;
   2. a method for preparing a lipophilic extract of one member of the Iridaceae family, which involves extracting a part of the rhizome with carbon dioxide (CO 2) comprising 5 to 10% ethanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The lipophilic extract is a lipophilic extract of the rhizome of the member of the Iridaceae family. The lipophilic extract is a lipophilic extract of Iris germanica. The iridals are iripallidal, iriflorental, gamma-irigermanal, alpha-irigermanal, the cis isomer of alpha-irigermanal, the cis isomer of alpha-dehydroirigermanal, and its mixtures. The iridal comprises iripallidal.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic; Muscular-Gen; Ophthalmological
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - cytochrome P450 inhibitor.

GA 202295366A
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a soda lime cup (10) slid with                a top (14) into a closing mouth (7) from a front of                a connection when the closing mouth is pulled                inwards using a lateral handle that is turned                towards the front of the connection. The closing                mouth includes a bypass valve to remove the soda                lime cup during ventilation to replace soda lime                without causing significant leak in a respiration                system.
   USE - System for coupling a reusable soda lime cup                to a respiration system of a medical device to                ventilate patients.

GA 202269612R

The utility model model uses the biomass through poly-generation treatment, the comprehensive treatment cost is low, the waste is changed into the treasure, not only the CO2 is reduced, the carbon fertilizer is produced in parallel, and the environmental protection and ecological benefits are improved.

TI Chromatographic fractionation purifying method of xylose mother liquor, comprises refracting xylols mother liquor, heating, and subjecting primary extract to decolorization treatment.
AU LIU Y
   YAN J
   QIAN R
   AN Y
   WANG X
   ZHONG K
   DAI Z
   ZHOU H
AE ZHEJIANG HUAKANG PHARM CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2022668631
AB    NOVELTY - Chromatographic fractionation purifying method of xylose mother liquor, comprises (i) refracting 60-70 wt.% xylose mother liquor with, diluting with water, introducing calcium hydroxide and carbon dioxide for flocculation and precipitation treatment, subjecting suspension to vacuum drum filtration treatment and plating and frame filtration treatment to obtain a pretreatment liquid, (ii) heating pretreatment liquid, introducing primary chromatographic separation system for first separation and purification treatment to obtain xylose, (iii) subjecting primary extract to decolorization treatment, performing evaporation, and drying, (iv) concentrating first-level raffinate and processing by first evaporator, introducing secondary chromatography system, (v) concentrating and processing secondary extraction solution into a secondary concentrated solution and returning to primary chromatographic separation system, and allowing to enter the next process.
   USE - Chromatographic fractionation purifying method of xylose mother liquor.
   ADVANTAGE - The xylose mother liquor for grading and purifying, to reduce the separation difficulty of the xylose mother liquor, and improves xylose recovery rate.

PN CN114381758-A
TI Preparing nickel-doped boehmite and reduced graphene oxide composite electrocatalyst, comprises dissolving formamide and water in mixed solution, dispersing graphene oxide, and adding nickel nitrate hexahydrate and aluminum nitrate nonahydrates.
AU LIU E
   LIU T
AE UNIV NORTH MINZU (UNBN-C)
GA 202262376E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nickel-doped boehmite and reduced graphene oxide composite electrocatalyst, comprises dissolving formamide and water in a mixed solution, dissolving urea, dispersing graphene oxide uniformly, adding nickel nitrate hexahydrate and aluminum nitrate nonahydrates, and stirring uniformly, adjusting the pH value to 8-10, continuously hydrothermally reacting at 160-200degrees Celsius for 6-8 hours, cooling to room temperature, washing the obtained product, and drying.
   USE - The electrocatalyst is useful for reducing carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and provides catalyst having high catalytic activity. The electrocatalyst has large specific surface area and large conjugated structure, two surfaces of the sheet can be combined together to form a stable composite material by covalent bond with the metal hydroxide, increases the pore duct is good for the transmission of the substance, and has good conductivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for nickel-doped boehmite.

PT P
PN KR2022049206-A
TI Dehydrogenation catalyst useful for dehydrogenation of ethane for refining-petrochemical industries, comprises titanium dioxide support having mesopores, and metal supported on titanium dioxide support.
AU KO C H
   PANTUANOK
   CHO-EUI-HYUN
AE UNIV CHONNAM NAT IND FOUND (UCHO-C)
GA 202255516Q
AB    NOVELTY - Dehydrogenation catalyst comprises titanium dioxide support having mesopores, and a metal supported on the titanium dioxide support.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for dehydrogenation of ethane (claimed) for refining/petrochemical industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: is highly-efficient and economical; alleviates the problem of rapidly increasing carbon deposition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing the dehydrogenation catalyst, comprising (i) mixing the titanium dioxide precursor in a solvent and stirring at 150-300 degrees C for at least 1 hour and then calcining the product at 150-300 degrees C for at least 1 hour, and (ii) impregnating the metal precursor into the titanium dioxide support having mesopores and calcining the product at 150-300 degrees C for at least 1 hour; and
   (2) ethane dehydrogenation method, comprising injecting a mixed gas containing ethane and carbon dioxide into a reactor containing the dehydrogenation catalyst, and carrying out dehydrogenation reaction at 500-600 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The dehydrogenation catalyst comprises 80-99 wt.% titanium dioxide support and 1-20 wt.% metal supported on the titanium dioxide support based on the total weight of the solid content. Preferred Components: The titanium dioxide support has an anatase phase. The metal supported on the titanium dioxide support includes tin, germanium, gallium, indium, zinc and/or manganese. The dehydrogenation catalyst has: a Brunauer, Emmett and Teller (BET) specific surface area of 150-250 m2/g; a total pore volume of 0.1-0.7 cm3/g; an average pore size of 2-10 nm; and an acid content of 1-5 mmol/g of the catalyst by ammonia-temperature programmed desorption analysis; and exhibits one reduction peak at 300-400 degrees C by hydrogen-temperature programmed reduction analysis. The dehydrogenation catalyst satisfies the relational expression: A divide by B is 0.5-1, where A is the BET specific surface area of the titanium dioxide support, and B is the BET specific surface area of the dehydrogenation catalyst.
   EXAMPLE - 10 ml Titanium tetrabutoxide (titania precursor) was poured into 100 ml distilled water, stirred for 30 minutes, and the mixture was hydrothermally synthesized at 200 degrees C for 3 hours in a polytetrafluoroethylene-lined stainless steel autoclave. The mixture was centrifuged and the precipitated contents were washed 3 times with distilled water, then dried in an oven at 100 degrees C for 5 hours, and calcined at 200 degrees C for 3 hours to obtain a titanium dioxide support. The prepared meso-titanium dioxide support and gallium nitrate were dissolved in distilled water by wet impregnation method and calcined at 200 degrees C for 3 hours to obtain the gallium/meso-titanium dioxide catalyst.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC A01-D13; E10-J02C3; E11-E02; E11-W; E35-K01; J04-E04B; J04-E11; N03-E; N03-F01; N03-G01; N03-G02; N03-G03; N03-G04; N06-E01; N06-F; N07-C02

PN WO2022054335-A1; JP2022047294-A
TI Photocatalyst complex used for decomposition of e.g. organic substance, comprises composition containing carbon quantum dot obtained by carbonizing organic compound in presence of layered clay mineral, laminar clay mineral and photocatalyst.
AU UCHIDA J
   KATSURAO T
   SAKABE H
   SASAKI K
   CHUAICHAM C
   VELLAICHAMY B
   KATSUO T
   BALAKUMAR V
AE KUREHA CORP (KURE-C)
   UNIV KYUSHU NAT CORP (UKUY-C)
   UNIV KYUSHU (KYHU-C)
GA 2022413076
AB    NOVELTY - A photocatalyst complex comprises composition containing a carbon quantum dot obtained by carbonizing an organic compound having a reactive group in the presence of layered clay mineral, laminar clay mineral and a photocatalyst for modifying the composition.
   USE - Photocatalyst complex used for decomposition and reduction of organic substance, and inorganic substance. Uses include but are not limited to hydrogen production by decomposition of water, reduction of carbon dioxide, decomposition and purification of harmful substances in air, water, or oil, decomposition of nitrogen oxides, decomposition of organic dyes such as methyl orange, methylene blue and rhodamine B, reduction of hexavalent chromium (Cr (VI)), divalent lead (Pb (II)), divalent mercury (Hg (II)), decomposition of organic compounds such as 2,4-dinitrophenol, dibenzothiophene and naproxen, degradation of antibiotics such as ciprofloxacin, tetracycline and chloramphenicol, sterilization of Escherichia coli, Staphylococcus aureus, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst complex has excellent photocatalytic activity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The photocatalyst contains at least one compound selected from oxyhalogenated bismuth, graphite-based nitrogen oxide, titanium oxide, and zinc oxide. The layered clay mineral contains at least one of smectite and layered double hydroxide. The carbon quantum dot contains at least one heteroatom selected from oxygen, nitrogen, boron, phosphorus, sulfur, silicon and fluorine.
DC D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN114163557-A
TI Preparing organic plastic scintillator comprises mixing 2,5-diphenyloxazole, divinylbenzene, azodiisobutyronitrile and wave-shifting agent, ultrasonically dispersing, gradient heating, cooling to obtain blank, mechanically cutting and polishing blank.
AU YANG X
   ZHANG Q
   LUO X
   ZHANG Y
   LI J
   ZHONG M
   WU Z
   CHEN S
   HUANG C
AE LASER FUSION RES CENT CHINA ACAD ENG PHY (CAEP-C)
GA 202242143V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing organic plastic scintillator comprises: (i) mixing 2,5-diphenyloxazole, divinylbenzene, azodiisobutyronitrile and wave-shifting agent, and performing ultrasonic dispersion of the mixed solution, (ii) gradient heating the processed mixed solution and cooling to obtain an organic plastic scintillator blank, and (iii) mechanically cutting and polishing the blank.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing organic plastic scintillator.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes vacuum low temperature ultrasonic dispersion treatment and supercritical carbon dioxide mixing treatment, hence ensures more uniform dispersion of poly(p-phenylene oxide) (PPO); uses analytically pure reagents and raw materials beyond spectrum level (except PPO), and hence they can be directly used without purification treatment; avoids the possibility of introducing impurities in complex operation and purification process; and prepares organic plastic scintillator with good stability, no bubbles or fractures and with PPO content of up to 50%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed solution comprises 30-50 wt.% 2,5-diphenyloxazole, 40-70 wt.% styrene, 4-20 wt.% divinylbenzene, 0.2-0.5 wt.% azodiisobutyronitrile and 0-0.5 wt.% wave-shifting agent. The wave-shifting agent is 9,10-diphenylanthracene, 1,4-bis(5-phenyl-2-oxazolyl)benzene, 1,4-bis(2-methylstyryl)benzene or 1,4-bis(4-methystyrene) benzene.Preferred Method: The ultrasonic dispersion treatment in step (i) comprises adding the mixture to an ultrasonic cleaning machine set at 40-60 KHz, 200-300 W, ultrasonically dispersing for 30-60 minutes, stopping for 10-20 minutes, then ultrasonically dispersing for 30-60 minutes, and ultrasonically dispersing the mixed solution until a clear transparent solution is obtained. The mixed solution is ultrasonically processed at 0-10degrees Celsius and 100-800 Pa for 3-6 minutes, where the ultrasonic frequency is 30-60 kHz and the ultrasonic dispersion treatment is paused for 3-6 minutes to complete one cycle. The ultrasonic dispersion treatment is conducted for 1-3 hours. The step (i) further comprises performing supercritical carbon dioxide mixing treatment by adding the ultrasonically processed mixed solution to a supercritical reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide, controlling the temperature of the reaction kettle at 30-40degrees Celsius, pressure at 6-24 MPa, magnetically stirring at a speed of 1000-1500 revolutions/minute for 1-3 hours, stopping heating, controlling the pressure release rate at 0.1-5 MPa/second when the temperature of the reaction kettle is 20-30degrees Celsius, discharging carbon dioxide gas to obtain processed mixed solution. The gradient heating process comprises heating to 80-90degrees Celsius in 1-2 hours, maintaining the temperature for 24-48 hours, then heating to 90-100degrees Celsius in 1-2 hours, maintaining the temperature for 24-48 hours, then heating to 100-110degrees Celsius in 1-2 hours, and maintaining the temperature for 24-48 hours, and then finally heating to 110-120degrees Celsius in 1-2 hours, and maintaining the temperature for 48-72 hours. The cooling process in the step (ii) is carried out for 12-24 hours, where the temperature is reduced from 110-120degrees Celsius to 20-25degrees Celsius. The organic plastic scintillator blank in step (iii) is cooled to -10 to -20degrees Celsius for 12-24 hours before mechanically cutting the blank.

PN JP2021188004-A
TI Separating lignin used as raw material for thermosetting resin from black liquor discharged from process of steaming hardwood with soda, involves adding acid to black liquor for precipitating lignin, suspending lignin precipitate, adjusting pH, washing and dehydrating precipitated lignin.
AU TAGAMI A
   KANEKO R
   NURUKI Y
   TSUJI S
   MIYAWAKI S
AE NIPPON PAPER IND CO LTD (NSES-C)
GA 2021E7339N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for separating lignin from black liquor discharged from process of steaming hardwood with soda, involves (a) adding acid and/or carbon dioxide to black liquor for precipitating lignin at pH of less than 10, and dehydrating to obtain lignin precipitate (A), (b) suspending obtained lignin precipitate (A) and adjusting to pH to less than 3 for precipitating the lignin, and (c) dehydrating precipitated lignin, washing and dehydrating to obtain lignin precipitate (C).
   USE - Method for separating lignin from black liquor discharged from process of steaming hardwood with soda, where lignin is used as substitute for petroleum, and as raw material for thermosetting resins, dispersants, adhesives and rigid polyurethane foams by converting to phenol resin and epoxy resin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently separates the extracted lignin, and reduces the molecular weight of lignin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The hardwood is Eucalyptus globules. Preferred Composition: The concentration of xylan in black liquor is more than 1%, and step (a) involves precipitating lignin at 60-80 degrees C. The concentration of xylan in black liquor is not more than 1% and step (a) involves precipitating lignin at 40-60 degrees C. The solid content of black liquor is 10-40 %mass, and the solid content of lignin precipitate (C) is greater than or equal to 25%. Preferred Conditions: The dehydration in step (a) and/or (c) is performed by filter press device. The step (b) involves suspending lignin precipitate (A) and adding acid to make pH less than 3. The step (b) involves suspending lignin precipitate (A) in acidic solution to have pH of less than 3. The washing and dehydration are carried out until electrical conductivity of washing filtrate obtained by washing and dehydration in step (b) is less than or equal to 0.2 S/m.
   EXAMPLE - Black liquor was put in a beaker preheated to 80 degrees C, and agitated in a pressure-resistant container pressurized with carbon dioxide of 0.3 MPa, and treated for 30 minutes, and the pH was adjusted to 7.5. A glass filter was used to suck and dehydrate, and the lignin precipitate (A) was separated. A half amount of water of black liquor was added to the lignin precipitate (A), and the slurry was again slurried, sulfuric acid was added to the slurry and the pH was adjusted to 2. Slurry was pre-heated to 80 degrees C. Suction dehydration was performed using a glass filter, solid-liquid separation was carried out. The solid content remaining on the glass filter was washed with a half amount of hot water (80 degrees C) of the black liquor, and a lignin precipitate (C) was obtained.

The generated gas obtained by gas forming process contains hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and/or methane. PT P
PN CN113351216-A
TI Preparing low-dimensional self-assembled flower-shaped cuprous oxide-zinc oxide catalyst useful in e.g. solar hydrogen production, comprises dispersing flower-shaped cuprous oxide or zinc oxide, adding ascorbic acid, centrifuging and drying.
AU WU Y
   DAI Z
   XU L
   ZHU Y
   LI J
AE UNIV XIAMEN TECHNOLOGY (UXLG-C)
GA 2021A7783H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low-dimensional self-assembled flower-shaped cuprous oxide-zinc oxide catalyst comprises ultrasonically dispersing flower-shaped cuprous oxide or flower-shaped zinc oxide in the solution, sequentially adding zinc acetate alcohol solution, lithium hydroxide alcohol solution or lithium hydroxide, copper chloride and ascorbic acid, reacting, cooling to room temperature, taking out, washing by centrifugation, and drying.
   USE - The low-dimensional self-assembled flower-shaped cuprous oxide-zinc oxide catalyst is useful for photocatalytic degradation of environmental pollutant, carbon dioxide reduction and solar hydrogen production (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst improves the shortcomings of a single catalyst, reduces the recombination of pure cuprous oxide photo-generated electrons and holes, broadens the light response range of pure zinc oxide, realizes the absorption of sunlight in the whole waveband, improves photoelectric conversion efficiency, ensures recombination of carriers can be suppressed, and improves photocatalytic efficiency in all directions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises ultrasonically dispersing flower-shaped cuprous oxide in ethanol or methanol solution, sequentially adding 12 mg/ml zinc acetate alcohol solution and 5 mg/ml lithium hydroxide alcohol solution in volume ratio of 1:1, heating in a water bath, cooling to room temperature, taking out, washing with deionized water and ethanol alternately by centrifugation, and drying at 60 degrees C in vacuum for 6 hours to obtain flower-shaped cuprous oxide-zinc oxide catalyst, where the amount of zinc oxide quantum dot is 1-15 wt.%. The preparation of the flower-shaped cuprous oxide comprises dissolving copper nitrate, glutamic acid, glycine, sodium dihydrogen phosphate dihydrate and sodium hydroxide in mass ratio of 4.2:6:6:5:19 in 100-200 ml deionized water, stirring at 100-200 revolutions/minute for 10-30 minutes to make fully dissolved, adding into the reaction kettle and hydrothermally reacting at 140-160 degrees C for 2-4 hours, cooling to room temperature, taking out, washing with deionized water and ethanol alternately by centrifugation, and drying under vacuum at 60 degrees C. The method comprises ultrasonically dispersing the flower-shaped zinc oxide in the solution, sequentially adding 0.1 mol/l lithium hydroxide, 0.05 mol/l copper chloride and 0.025 mol/l ascorbic acid, and reacting with magnetic stirring at 15-20 degrees C and 100-200 revolutions/minute for 30 minutes, cooling to room temperature, taking out and washing with deionized water and ethanol alternately by centrifugation, and vacuum drying at 60 degrees C for 6 hours to obtain flower-shaped cuprous oxide-zinc oxide catalyst, where the amount cuprous oxide quantum dot is 1-20 wt.%. The preparation of the flower-shaped zinc oxide comprises sequentially adding 0.15-0.02 g/ml zinc acetate solution and 0.014-0.05 g/ml sodium hydroxide solution in volume ratio of 1:2-3:2 into 0.05 g/ml citric acid solution, adding 20 ml ethanol, stirring and reacting, transferring mixed solution to the reactor for hydrothermal reaction at 160-180 degrees C for 6-8 hours, cooling to room temperature, taking out, centrifuging with deionized water and ethanol, and drying under vacuum at 80 degrees C..

PN CN113106264-A; CN113106264-B
TI Preparation of electroslag remelted steel ingot,            involves obtaining electrode ingot and slag material,            electrifying slag material to slag, cooling electrode            remelting pre-melted liquid slag and electrode ingot,            and adding calcium carbonate powder during remelting of            electrode.
AU ZHANG W
   ZHENG X
   YOU T
   NING Y
AE BEIJING SHOUGANG GITANE NEW MATERIALS CO (SHGG-C)
GA 2021818294
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high-yttrium-content                electroslag remelted steel ingot, involves                obtaining an electrode ingot and a slag material,                electrifying slag material to slag, cooling to                obtain a pre-melted liquid slag, electrode                remelting the pre-melted liquid slag and the                electrode ingot to obtain an electroslag remelted                steel ingot with a high yield of yttrium element,                and adding calcium carbonate powder during the                remelting of the electrode.
   USE - Preparation of high-yttrium-content                electroslag remelted steel ingot.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases calcium oxide beneficial                component in slag, increases carbon dioxide gas                layer on surface of slag layer, maintains slag                component and fluidity, isolates air, reduces                invasion of oxygen in air, reduces oxidation of                rare earth yttrium element, improves yield of                yttrium element in electroslag remelting steel                ingot, and improves service life of material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The calcium carbonate                  powder is wrapped in aluminum foil, and 2-3 g                  calcium carbonate powder wrapped in aluminum foil                  is added for every 40-80 seconds from the start                  time of electrode remelting. The total added mass                  of the calcium carbonate powder is 1.1-1.2 g/kg                  steel ingot. The slag includes 60-70 %mass                  calcium fluoride and 30-40 %mass yttrium oxide.                  The electrode remelting is performed in a                  crystallizer that includes an upper opening and a                  lower opening, and the diameter of the upper                  opening is 9-11 mm smaller than the diameter of                  the lower opening. Preferred Condition: The                  electrode remelting includes a protective                  atmosphere of 2-10 bar inert gas, the melting                  rate of the electrode ingot is controlled at                  85-90 kg/hour, the voltage of 45-50 V, the arcing                  current of 1900-2100 A, and the smelting current                  of 2900-3000 A. The electrified slag includes a                  voltage of 30-55 V and a current of 3000-6000 A.                  The temperature of the baking is 600-800 degrees                  C, and the time of the baking is 240-300 minutes.                  Preferred Process: The electrode ingot includes                  obtaining an ingot steel, and baking the ingot                  steel. The ingot steel includes smelting the raw                  materials and post-refining. The refining                  includes using soft argon blowing for 5-10                  minutes, performing vacuum degassing for 15-25                  minutes, and then die-casting.

TI Herbal insect-preventing agent useful for e.g. artistic paint, internal and external wall paint, fiber board and wood board and preventing rat and termites, comprises air-dried leaf powder, hexaflumuron and ammonium benzoate acyl saccharide.
AU WAN Y
AE WAN Y (WANY-Individual)
GA 2021784637
AB    NOVELTY - Herbal insect-preventing agent comprises 0.5-4 pts. wt. air-dried leaf powder, 0.5-5 pts. wt. dried stem, bark and branch powder, 0-2 pts. wt. semi-carbonized stem and branch powder, 1-2 pts. wt. supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extract, 0-3 pts. wt. hexaflumuron and 1-3 pts. wt. ammonium benzoate acyl saccharide.
   USE - The agent is useful for artistic paint, internal and external wall paint, aqueous wood lacquer, oil wood lacquer, wood oil, composite board, wall board, floor board, fiber board, wood board, cement mortar and gypsum powder product (claimed), and building decoration material and preventing rat and termite.
   ADVANTAGE - The agent does need to change the construction process of the original carrier after adding the anti-insect agent, increases the function of the original carrier, and harmless to human and livestock, and is environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The preparation method of the air-dried leaf powder is: mixing 50-70 pts. wt., preferably 60 pts. wt. Derris trifoliata leaves, 20-40 pts. wt., preferably 30 pts. wt. bitter leaf, and 60-80 pts. wt., preferably 70 pts. wt. cypress leaves, and performing gradient air-drying, air-drying at 50 degrees C for 60 minutes, air-drying at 60 degrees C for 40 minutes, and air-drying at 66 degrees C for 30 minutes, cooling to 30-40 degrees C and crushing into 400-5000 meshes powder. The preparation method of dried stem, bark and branch powder is: mixing 50-70 pts. wt., preferably 60 pts. wt. Derris trifoliata stem, 5-15 pts. wt., preferably 10 pts. wt. bitter bark, 50-70 pts. wt., preferably 60 pts. wt. cypress branches and 5-15 pts. wt., preferably 10 pts. wt. cypress seed, performing first stage gradient drying at 50 degrees C for 120 minutes, 60 degrees C for 60 minutes and 70 degrees C for 50 minutes, drying, directly carrying out the second stage gradient drying, drying at 100 degrees C for 40 minutes, 150 degrees C for 30minutes, and cooling to 30-40 degrees C after crushing into 400-5000 meshes powder. The preparation method of semi-carbonized stem and branch powder is: mixing 50-70 pts. wt., preferably 60 pts. wt. Derris trifoliata stem, 50-70 pts. wt., preferably 60 pts. wt. cypress branches, and 50-70 pts. wt., preferably 60 pts. wt. sawdust, drying at 60 degrees C for 120 minutes, cooling and crushing particle of 3 mm, performing dry carbonization at 100 degrees C for 90 minutes, 140 degrees C for 50 minutes, 190 degrees C for 20 minutes, 200 degrees C for 10 minutes and 220 degrees C for 4 minutes, cooling to 30-40 degrees C and crushing into 400-5000 meshes. The preparation method of the supercritical CO2 extract is: 50-70 pts. wt., preferably 60 pts. wt. Derris trifoliata leaf, 20-40 pts. wt., preferably 30 pts. wt. bitter leaf, 50-70 pts. wt., preferably 60 pts. wt. cypress leaves, 70-90 pts. wt., preferably 80 pts. wt. Derris trifoliata stem and 60-80 pts. wt., preferably 70 pts. wt. cypress branches parts, crushing the mixture at low temperature to 40-100 meshes, performing supercritical CO2 extraction to the mixture to obtain supercritical CO2 extract, where the extracting pressure is 6-40 Mpa, extraction temperature 40-100 degrees C, CO2 gas flow rate is 1-6 l/minute, and the extracting time is 1-6 hours. The extraction pressure is 6 Mpa, 8 Mpa, 10 Mpa, 12 Mpa, 16 Mpa, 20 Mpa, 25 Mpa, 30 Mpa, 35 Mpa or 40 Mpa, and the extraction temperature is 40 degrees C, 50 degrees C, 60 degrees C, 70 degrees C, 80 degrees C, 90 degrees C, 100 degrees C, the carbon dioxide (CO2) gas flow rate is 1 l/minute, 2 l/minute, 3 l/minute, 4 l/minute, 5 l/minutes or 6 l/minute, and the extraction time is 1 hour, 2 hours, 3 hours, 4 hours, 5 hours or 6 hours.
   ACTIVITY - Insecticide.
