PN CN113042039-A
TI Palladium-based catalyst comprises alumina carrier and an active component supported on the surface of the alumina carrier, the crystal form of the alumina carrier is the theta-crystal form and the active component includes palladium.
AU HE H
   CUI C
   YU Y
   ZHANG Y
   SHI X
AE RES CENT ECO ENVIRONMENTAL SCI ACAD SIN (CAEM-C)
GA 202178935R
AB    NOVELTY - Palladium-based catalyst comprises alumina carrier and an active component supported on the surface of the alumina carrier, the crystal form of the alumina carrier is the theta -crystal form and the active component includes palladium.
   USE - Used as palladium-based catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The palladium-based catalyst has low-temperature activity in the catalytic oxidation reaction of methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing palladium-based catalyst comprising mixing the palladium precursor with the alumina dispersion and then evaporating to dryness to obtain a solid, and calcining the solid to obtain palladium-based catalyst; and
   (2) catalytically oxidizing methane using the palladium-based catalyst comprising the mixed gas containing methane passes through the catalyst bed, decomposing methane to obtain carbon dioxide after catalytic oxidation and water, where the catalyst bed is filled with the palladium-based catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The loading amount of the active component is 0.1-5 wt.%. The mixed gas comprises methane, oxygen and nitrogen, preferably, the concentration of methane in the mixed gas is 500 ppm to 5 vol.%, preferably, the volume fraction of the oxygen in the mixed gas is 2000 ppm to 20 vol.%. Preferred Method: The alumina dispersion liquid is prepared by calcining the pseudo-boehmite to obtain theta crystal form of alumina and mixing the alumina and water to prepare alumina dispersion, preferably, the roasting temperature is 1000-1100 degrees C, preferably, the roasting time is 1-3 hours, preferably, the mixing temperature is 10-50 degrees C, preferably, the mixing time is 0.1-3 hours. The evaporating temperature is 60-90 degrees C, preferably, the roasting before drying the solid matter; preferably, the drying temperature is 90-110 degrees C. The roasting temperature is 500-700 degrees C, preferably, the roasting time is 1-3 hours; preferably, the heating rate of the roasting process is 5-15 degrees C/minutes. The airspeed in the fixed bed reactor is 50000-400000 h-1. The reaction temperature of the catalytic oxidation is 100-900 degrees C, preferably, the reaction temperature of the catalytic oxidation reaches 300 degrees C or more, the methane conversion rate is greater than or equal to 50%; preferably the reaction temperature of the catalytic oxidation reaches 330 degrees C or more, and the methane conversion rate is greater than or equal to 90%.

PN US2021188737-A1; WO2021127331-A1; CN114829550-A; KR2022113529-A; GB2604559-A; EP4077590-A1
TI Alkylation system used for converting isoparaffins            and low molecular weight alkenes into alkylate product,            consists of overhead iso-butane-rich stream which is            recycle stream to alkylation reaction/regeneration            section.
AU ELGEY I C
   SINGH B B
   ELGAY I C
AE ELGEY I C (ELGE-Individual)
   SINGH B B (SING-Individual)
   KELLOGG BROWN & ROOT LLC (PULL-C)
   KELLOGG BROWN & ROOT LLC (PULL-C)
   KELLOGG BROWN & ROOT INC (PULL-C)
   KELLOGG BROWN & ROOT LLC (PULL-C)
GA 202170246W
AB    NOVELTY - An alkylation system consists of an overhead                iso-butane-rich stream which is a recycle stream                (26) to the alkylation reaction/regeneration                section (10).
   USE - Alkylation system used for converting                isoparaffins (e.g. iso-butane) and low molecular                weight alkenes (e.g. propylene, butylene, and/or                amylenes) into alkylate product.
   ADVANTAGE - The alkylation system has excellent                fractionation efficiency, reduced utility                requirements, reduced overall energy requirements,                reduced carbon dioxide/nitrogen oxide emissions,                enhanced plant safety due to less hydrocarbon and                reduced iso-butane loss from the system, and 20-30%                less capital requirement, and less plot space                (equipment footprint) requirement.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the                alkylation plant.
   Alkylation reaction/regeneration section                (10)
   Olefin feed (12)
   Alkylate reactor effluent (14)
   Dividing wall column (16)
   n-Butane product stream (20)
   Alkylate product stream (22)
   Make-up iso-butane (24)
   Recycle stream (26)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkylate product                  stream (22) is routed to a battery limit after                  preheating the alkylate reactor effluent (14) in                  a heat exchanger prior to being fed to the                  dividing wall column (DWC) (16). In the system,                  the make-up iso-butane (24) is at least a portion                  of the iso-butane product and the make-up                  iso-butane is routed to the olefin feed (12)                  upstream of the alkylation reaction/regeneration                  section to form a mixture. The alkylation                  reaction/regeneration section does not contain                  fractionation section configured to fractionating                  the mixture. The alkylate reactor effluent                  containing propane is a feed to a                  pre-fractionator section of the DWC . The DWC                  comprises an overhead product containing propane                  routed to a depropanizer column . The                  iso-butane-rich stream that is the iso-butane                  product stream is a side draw product, taken from                  a main-fractionator section of the DWC. The                  iso-butane product stream (26) is recycled to the                  alkylation reaction/regeneration section . The                  DWC has a n-butane-rich stream that is the                  n-butane product stream (20), which is a side                  draw product, taken from main-fractionator                  section of the DWC or the bottoms section of the                  DWC. The side draw product is chosen from the                  vapor and liquid. The DWC has bottom product that                  is the alkylate product stream. The alkylate                  product stream is routed to a battery limit after                  preheating the alkylate reactor effluent in a                  heat exchanger prior to being fed to the                  DWC.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The system further                  consists of an intermediate reboiler in a bottom                  section of the DWC . The alkylate product is a                  heating medium in the intermediate reboiler                  before it preheats the alkylate reactor effluent                  . The DWC comprises a dividing wall panel                  starting at or near the top of the column and                  extending downward. The alkylate reactor effluent                  is fed to a top tray of the pre-fractionation                  section of the DWC. The DWC comprises an overhead                  condenser configured to condense vapors from the                  pre-fractionation section and the main                  fractionation section, and a reflux stream from                  the overhead condenser, which is fed to a top                  tray of the main fractionation section.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN112938971-A
TI Preparing coconut shell-based mesoporous activated carbon for adsorbent, involves taking the coconut shell carbonized material, grinding and sieving to obtain coconut shell carbonized material particles.
AU XU Q
   LIU X
   WANG Y
AE INST URBAN ENVIRONMENT CHINESE ACAD SCI (CUBN-C)
GA 202171033P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing coconut shell-based mesoporous activated carbon involves taking the coconut shell carbonized material, grinding, sieving to obtain 20-60 mesh coconut shell carbonized material particles and dissolving the iron salt, adding the obtained coconut shell carbonized material particles, stirring and impregnating to obtain the impregnated iron salt carbonized material. The activation treatment is performed on the impregnated iron salt carbonized material followed by acid washing to obtain the coconut shell-based mesoporous activated carbon.
   USE - Method for preparing coconut shell-based mesoporous activated carbon for adsorbent (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses coconut shell carbonized material as raw material, adopts the method of carbothermic reduction coupled catalytic activation, utilizes the nanometer zero-valent iron formed by carbothermic reduction to catalyze the gasification reaction of carbon and carbon dioxide, and realizes the purpose of one-step pore formation and pore expansion, and prepared activated carbon has large specific surface area, developed pore structure, high mesoporosity and wide pore size distribution range.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The washing detergent includes deionized water. The iron salt includes any one or a combination of ferric nitrate, ferric chloride, ferrous chloride, ferric sulfate, or ferrous sulfate. The dissolved solvent includes water and/or ethanol, preferably water and ethanol. The activator of the activation treatment includes carbon dioxide. The device for activation treatment includes an activation furnace. The inert atmosphere includes nitrogen and/or argon. The detergent for pickling includes hydrochloric acid. Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of the iron element in the iron salt to the coconut shell carbonized material particles is (0.1-1): 100. The solid-liquid ratio of the coconut shell carbonized material particles and the iron salt solution is 1:(4-10). The stirring and dipping time is 15-30 hours. The time of the activation treatment is 40-100 minutes. The temperature of the activation treatment is 800-900 degrees C. Preferred Process: The method involves taking the coconut shell carbonized material, grinding and sieving to obtain 20-60 mesh coconut shell carbonized material particles. The mass ratio of iron element to coconut shell carbonized material particles is (0.1-1):100, and weighed a certain amount of iron salt and added to the water and ethanol mixture with a volume ratio of (0.25-4):1. The coconut shell carbonized material particles are added at a solid-liquid ratio of 1:(4-10), and stirred and impregnated the carbonized material at 300-500 rpm for 15-30 hours to obtain an impregnated iron salt carbonized material. The impregnated iron salt carbonized material is placed in an activation furnace, and the temperature is raised to 800-900 degrees C under an inert atmosphere, and switched to carbon dioxide with a flow rate of 0.2-0.6 L/minute to perform activation treatment for 40-100 minutes, cooled and pickled with 3-10 wt.% acid solution, washing with water to obtain the coconut shell-based mesoporous activated carbon.

PT P
PN CN112497556-A
TI Manufacturing method of polypropylene composite material, involves weighing each component raw material according to weight ratio, and placing component raw material in high-speed mixer and mixed evenly.
AU WANG W
AE TAIZHOU NUOMENG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (TAIZ-Non-standard)
GA 202128688M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves weighing each component raw material according to the weight ratio, and placing the component raw material in a high-speed mixer and mix until that is evenly mixed. The mixed raw materials is divided into four portions. The four mixed raw materials are heated to different temperature ranges. A first mixed raw material container is mixed with nitrogen. A second mixed raw material container is mixed with carbon dioxide. A third mixed raw material container is vaccumed. The four portions of the mixed raw materials are weighted according to the weight ratio, and placed that in a high-speed mixer and mix until that is evenly mixed. The remixed mixed raw materials are melt-extrusioned and pelletized.
   USE - Manufacturing method of polypropylene composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The manufacturing method of polypropylene composite material greatly improves the manufacturing efficiency and reduces the manufacturing cost.
DC A31 (Preliminary processes.); A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.)
MC A04-G03C; A11-A03

PT P
PN CN112342183-A
TI Establishing in vitro environmental model of            piglet gastrointestinal tract comprises selecting            piglet's small intestinal epithelial cell line and            gastric mucosal epithelial cell line for cell            subculture, sub-culturing and washing.
AU TIAN Z
   MA X
   CUI Y
   LU H
   DENG D
   LIU Z
   YU M
   RONG T
AE INST ANIMAL SCI GUANGDONG PROV ACAD AGRI (ANIM-Non-standard)
GA 2021176423
AB    NOVELTY - Establishing in vitro environmental model of                piglet gastrointestinal tract comprises selecting                the piglet's small intestinal epithelial cell line                and gastric mucosal epithelial cell line for cell                subculture, sub-culturing the cells for 3 to 4                generations until the cell viability is strong,                using trypsin-EDTA for digestion, counting and                using Transwell culture system for inoculating                cells, culturing at 37 degrees C, using 5% carbon                dioxide, then discarding the complete medium,                washing with phosphate buffered saline, and adding                differentiation medium.
   USE - The method is useful for establishing in vitro                environmental model of piglet gastrointestinal                tract.
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes the development of pig                intestinal nutrition and health research.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for in                vitro environment model of the piglet                gastrointestinal tract established by the method                for simulating the environment of the piglet                gastrointestinal tract and in vitro study of the                response mechanism of the piglet to nitrogen                nutrients.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The cell lines include                  the small intestine epithelial cell IPEC-1 cell                  line and the gastric mucosal epithelial cell                  GES-1 cell line, which are respectively                  resuscitated and grown to 80%-90% for cell                  passage. The cells are subcultured in a complete                  medium. The composition includes a basic medium,                  5-10% fetal bovine serum, and 0.05-0.10 mu g/ml                  epidermal growth factor. The composition of the                  differentiation medium includes a basal medium,                  0.5-1% ITS, and 0.05-0.10 mu g/ml epidermal                  growth factor, which is differentiated for 15-20                  days. The Transwell culture system uses 6-well or                  96-well Transwell for inoculating cells. The                  lower chamber of the 6-well Transwell was seeded                  with 2-3x 105 GES-1 cells/well. The upper chamber                  is inoculated with 2-3x 105 IPEC-1 cells/well.                  The lower chamber of the 96-well Transwell is                  seeded with 5x 103-1x 104 GES-1 cells/well, and                  the upper chamber is seeded with 5x 103-1x 104                  IPEC-1 cells/well.

TI Serum-free medium useful for culturing Chinese Hamster ovary cells, comprises dulbecco's modified eagle medium/nutrient mixture F-12 basic medium, cholesterol linolenate, transferrin, ethanolamine, lecithin, and choline sulfate.
AU XIAO Z
   DENG H
AE SHANGHAI OPM BIOSCIENCES CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202115082X
AB    NOVELTY - Serum-free medium comprises dulbecco's modified eagle medium/nutrient mixture F-12 basic medium (DMEM/F-12), 100-200 mg/l AEO-9, 5-10 mg/l cholesterol linolenate, 5-10 mg/l vitamin C, 2-5 mg/l insulin, 5-7 mg/l transferrin, 2-5 mg/l ethanolamine, 3-6 mg/l lecithin, and 10-40 mg/l choline sulfate as additive.
   USE - The medium is useful for culturing Chinese Hamster ovary cells (CHO) (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The medium: supports good growth of CHO cells; has high survival rate and less clumping.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for method for culturing CHO cells, comprising inoculating CHO cells in the medium, then culturing the CHO cells for specific period of time under the conditions of 37 degrees C and 51% carbon dioxide to the required cell volume.

PT P
PN WO2021014111-A1; AU2019457857-A1; KR2022040469-A; CN114502505-A; EP4003908-A1; US2022298014-A1
TI Producing gaseous product comprising hydrogen for            fuel cell module, by exposing gaseous hydrocarbon to            microwave radiation in presence of solid catalyst            comprising iron species supported on support comprising            ceramic material or carbon.
AU EDWARDS P P
   XIAO T
   JIE X
   PHILLIP E P
   TIANCHUN X
   XIANGYU J
AE UNIV OXFORD INNOVATION LTD (UYOX-C)
   UNIV OXFORD INNOVATION LTD (UYOX-C)
   UNIV OXFORD INNOVATION LTD (UYOX-C)
   UNIV OXFORD INNOVATION LTD (UYOX-C)
GA 202110830Y
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing gaseous product                comprising hydrogen, comprises exposing a gaseous                hydrocarbon to microwave radiation in the presence                of a solid catalyst, where the catalyst comprises                iron species supported on a support comprising a                ceramic material or carbon, and/or its                mixture.
   USE - The process is used for preparing gaseous                product comprising hydrogen for a microwave                reactor, fuel cell module and vehicle or an                electronic device (claimed), and producing hydrogen                in industry based on fossil fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides high purity hydrogen gas,                suitably with minimal carbon by-products, does not                produce environmentally harmful carbon dioxide, is                economical and environmentally-friendly, improves                production of large quantities of hydrogen, is safe                in storage and rapid in release for immediate use                in applications such as fuel cells and has high                thermal conductivity, mechanical strength and                excellent dielectric properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The iron species                  comprises elemental iron, iron alloy, iron oxide,                  iron carbide and iron hydroxide, preferably                  elemental iron and/or iron oxide. The support                  comprises ceramic material and carbon material.                  The ceramic material comprises non-oxygenated                  ceramic comprising silicon carbide, boron                  carbide, tungsten carbide, zirconium carbide,                  aluminum carbide, aluminum nitride, silicon                  nitride, ceramic material, oxides of aluminum,                  silicon, titanium or zirconium, aluminum oxide                  and/or silicon oxide. The catalyst further                  comprises carbon, elemental iron supported on                  silicon carbide, elemental iron supported on                  aluminum oxide and elemental iron supported on                  silicon dioxide. The carbon material comprises                  activated carbon, graphene, graphite, carbon                  black and/or carbon nanoparticles comprising                  carbon nanotubes. Preferred Composition: The iron                  loading of catalyst is up to about 50 wt.%, where                  the iron loading is 2-10 wt.%, preferably about 5                  wt.%. Preferred Method: The method involves                  conducting the process in the absence of oxygen                  and/or water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gaseous                  hydrocarbon comprises methane, ethane, propane                  and butane, preferably methane. Preferred                  Composition: The gaseous hydrocarbon comprises at                  least 90 wt.% methane.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PN CN112185102-A
TI Method for calculating congestion loss based on traffic flow real-time detection data, involves calculating fuel waste and additional pollution cost according to total running mileage weighted average of each vehicle type.
AU CHEN R
   DONG X
AE BEIJING AEROSPACE CHANGFENG TECHNOLOGY (CAES-C)
GA 2021052672
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves calculating average time value of a bicycle according to an average time value of cycling. A survey result of urban traffic comprehensive survey is obtained to obtain an average passenger number of each vehicle. The different vehicle utilization rate is determined according to personal working and non-working travel. An average passenger number of passengers in the average passenger number is calculated. A discharge amount is calculated according to 1 liter of petrol combustion to generate 2.32 kg of carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for calculating congestion loss based on traffic flow real-time detection data.
   ADVANTAGE - The quantitative analyzing loss of traffic jam based on traffic flow real-time detection data is enabled.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for calculating congestion loss based on traffic flow real-time detection data. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PT P
PN CN112125333-A
TI Preparing titanium dioxide nanowire comprises alkali metal hydroxide solution and nano titanium particle hydrothermal reaction to obtain solid-liquid mixture, solid-liquid mixture in the solid is titanium acid alkali metal salt nanowire.
AU ZHU G
   GAO X
   BAI S
   HAN X
   WU Z
   MIAO X
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
GA 202101516K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing titanium dioxide nanowire comprises (1) alkali metal hydroxide solution and nano titanium particle hydrothermal reaction to obtain solid-liquid mixture, solid-liquid mixture in the solid is titanium acid alkali metal salt nanowire, (2) processing the titanium acid alkali metal salt nanowire with acidic compound solution to obtain the titanic acid nanowire, (3) roasting the titanic acid nanowire in vacuum or in an inert atmosphere to obtain titanium dioxide nanowire, where the solvent of the alkali solution comprises water and polyol, the polyol is 2-3C alcohol, and the functionality of the hydroxy is 2-3, e.g. but not limited to the polyol may be ethylene glycol and glycerol.
   USE - The titanium dioxide nanowire is useful in photocatalytic degradation of pollutants, photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction and conversion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for titanium dioxide nanowire obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali metal hydroxide comprises sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, lithium hydroxide and rubidium hydroxide. The alkali concentration in the alkali metal hydroxide solution is 0.5-8 mol/l, preferably 1-6 mol/l. The volume ratio of water to polyatomic alcohol in solvent is 0.3-3. The diameter of the nano-titanium particle is 10-20 nm, more preferably 20-100 nm. The mol ratio of the alkali metal hydroxide to the nano titanium particle is 1.2-9, more preferably 3-6. The acidic compound is acids and/or ammonium salts that are acidic in solution. The acid includes hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid, nitric acid and carboxylic acid. The ammonium salt is ammonium chloride, ammonium nitrate and ammonium sulfate. The inert atmosphere is nitrogen gas and/or inert gas. The inert gas is preferably helium, neon, and argon. The diameter of the titanium dioxide nanowire is 5-50nm and the length-diameter ratio is more than 20. Preferred Method: The temperature of the hydro-thermal reaction is 150-220 degrees C, more preferably 170-200 degrees C. The time of hydro-thermal reaction is 6-30 hours, more preferably 12-24 hours. The roasting temperature is 300-800 degrees C, preferably 400-600 degrees C. The baking time is 1-3 hours, preferably 3-3 hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN112110469-A
TI Preparing vaterite calcium carbonate comprises e.g. mixing limestone and ammonium chloride, orderly grinding and roasting, leaching roasted sand, separating solid and liquid, and mixing surfactant and calcium chloride lixivium.
AU ZHAO Y
   YUAN J
   WANG J
   SONG J
   JI Z
   WANG S
   LI F
   GUO X
AE UNIV HEBEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHT-C)
GA 2020D0132P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing vaterite calcium carbonate comprises (i) mixing limestone and ammonium chloride, orderly grinding and roasting to obtain roasted sand, (ii) leaching the roasted sand obtained in step (i), and separating solid and liquid to obtain calcium chloride lixivium, (iii) mixing surfactant and calcium chloride lixivium obtained in step (ii), introducing carbon dioxide and ammonia to perform carbonization treatment, completely carbonizing, and carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain vaterite calcium carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing vaterite calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method treats large amount of ammonium chloride by-products accumulated in the calcined soda industry, realizes high-value utilization of excess resources, and greatly widens application field of the calcium carbonate. The vaterite calcium carbonate has controllable shape, uniform particle size, and good hydrophobicity crystal surface.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating the method for preparing vaterite calcium carbonate (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the molar ratio of limestone to ammonium chloride is (2-3):1, preferably (2.6-3):1, and the grinding method comprises mortar grinding and/or grinder grinding. In the method, the step (i) further comprises sieving between grinding and roasting, where the mesh size of the screen for sieving is 50-80 mesh, preferably 60 mesh. In the step (i), the roasting temperature is 300-380 degrees C, preferably 320-360 degrees C, the heating rate of the roasting is 1-5 degrees C/minutes, preferably 2-4 degrees C/minutes, and the roasting time is 20-100 minutes, preferably 40-60 minutes. In the step (ii), the leaching liquid for leaching comprises water, the liquid-solid ratio of the leaching liquid to the roasted sand is (1-3):1; the leaching temperature is 20-60 degrees C, preferably 30-50 degrees C, and the leaching time is 30-180 minutes, preferably 60-120 minutes. In the step (iii), the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 8-24 ml/minutes, and the flow rate of the ammonia gas is 16-54 ml/minutes. In the step (iii), the carbonization treatment is performed under stirring conditions, the rotation speed of the stirring is 200-800 revolutions/minute, preferably 400-600 revolutions/minute, and the stirring paddle for the stirring comprises two-blade screw type stirring paddle with paddle area of 10 cm2. In the step (iii), bubbling carbon dioxide into the calcium chloride leaching solution, and introducing the ammonia gas into the calcium chloride leachate above the liquid surface of the reactor. In the step (iii), the solid-liquid separation comprises filtering, washing and drying. where the filtering comprises gravity filtering, pressurizing filtering, vacuum filtering and/or centrifugal filtering. The washing liquid of the washing comprises water and/or absolute ethyl alcohol. The drying comprises normal pressure drying, decompression drying and/or microwave drying. Preferred Components: In the step (iii), the surfactants comprises nonionic surfactants composed of coconut oil fatty acid diethanolamide, where the amount of the non-ionic surfactant is 0.1-10% of the mass fraction of the calcium chloride leaching solution obtained in step (ii).
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC E11-K03; E11-P; E11-Q01B; E34-D03A

PT P
PN CN111874935-A
TI Preparing large-particle calcium carbonate            involves forming calcium hydroxide slurry, preliminary            carbonization, secondary carbonization, repeating,            taking slurry from carbonization tank to thickening            tank, draining dilute phase portion.
AU YAN P
AE ZHEJIANG JIANDE ZHENGFA PHARM CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020B3340Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing large-particle calcium carbonate                involves forming calcium hydroxide slurry,                preliminary carbonization, secondary carbonization,                repeating until material level in carbonization                tank reaches 100%. The certain amount of slurry are                taken from carbonization tank to thickening tank,                then drained the dilute phase portion containing                crystal nuclei. The remaining dense phase portion                are sent in the thickening tank back to                carbonization in jar. The calcium hydroxide slurry                is added to the carbonization tank to restore the                material level in the carbonization tank to 100%,                and then introduced carbon dioxide gas and stirred                to reduce the pH value of the materials in the                carbonization tank to 7-8. The finished calcium                carbonate slurry is prepared, then dried,                depolymerized and crushed.
   USE - Method for preparing large-particle calcium                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare large-particle                calcium carbonate that meets the requirements of                related fields for large particles of calcium                carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The calcium hydroxide                  slurry has solid content equivalent to calcium                  carbonate of 100-250 g/L. The solid content of                  the remaining dense phase portion in the                  thickening tank reaches more than 300 g/L. The                  filter cake has moisture content of 20-22% and                  density of 2.3-2.5g/ml. Preferred Process: The                  method involves preparing calcium hydroxide                  slurry. The calcium oxide and water are mixed and                  stirred to prepare calcium hydroxide coarse                  slurry. The coarse calcium hydroxide slurry is                  removed to obtain calcium hydroxide slurry.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

AU NONG Y

PN CN111254461-A; CN111254461-B
TI Preparing tungsten oxide/bismuth iodide            heterojunction material comprises e.g. adding bismuth            nitrate to ethylene glycol solution, adding potassium            iodide and soaking tungsten oxide nanoplate in            resultant bismuth iodide precursor solution.
AU LI G
   CHEN Z
   XIANG S
   HU X
   ZHOU X
   WANG N
AE UNIV ANHUI (UANH-C)
GA 202053643L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing tungsten oxide/bismuth iodide                heterojunction material comprises (i) adding                0.1-0.4 g bismuth nitrate to 30-50 ml ethylene                glycol solution, stirring until dissolving                completely, then adding 0.05-0.15 g potassium                iodide and continuously stirring uniformly to                obtain bismuth iodide precursor solution, (ii)                soaking tungsten oxide nanoplate obliquely in                bismuth iodide precursor solution and reacting at                160&#176; C for 4-6 hours, cooling to room                temperature, taking out resulting product, then                washing and drying to obtain final product.
   USE - The tungsten oxide/bismuth iodide                heterojunction material is useful as photoanode for                producing methanol by photoreduction of carbon                dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The tungsten oxide/bismuth iodide                heterojunction material has excellent visible light                absorption performance and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                tungsten oxide/bismuth iodide heterojunction                material obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (ii), the                  inclination angle of the tungsten oxide nanoplate                  is 30-50&#176; .

TI Preparation of polyether block amide/attapulgite mixed matrix membrane involves mixing casting solutions containing attapulgite and polyether block amide, casting on Petri dish, and drying at preset conditions.
AU SONG C
   A S
   LI R
   WANG C
   CAO L
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2020162440
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of polyether block amide/attapulgite mixed matrix membrane involves using attapulgite, polyether block amide (Pebax) and N-methyl pyrrolidone in the mass ratio of 3:6:94. The process involves dissolving polyether block amide in 70% total volume of the solvent and refluxing at 120 degrees C for 3 hours to form a uniform casting film solution (A), ultrasonically dissolving attapulgite in 30% total volume solvent to obtain casting solution (B), mixing obtained solutions, ultrasonically treating under reflux at 120 degrees C for 3 hours, pouring the casting solution into a clean and flat PTFE Petri dish, and drying in a 70 degrees C oven for 10 hours, and drying in a vacuum oven at 50 degrees C for 10 hours to remove the remaining solvent molecules in the mixed matrix film.
   USE - Preparation of polyether block amide/attapulgite mixed matrix membrane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces uniform matrix membrane having excellent carbon dioxide separation performance.

PT P
PN WO2020026746-A1; JP6695012-B1; JP2020503343-X; CN112334508-A; TW202012488-A; CN112334508-B
TI Producing liquid-crystalline resin by reacting starting-material monomers comprising aromatic hydroxycarboxylic acids, and carrying out condensation-polymerization of monomers in presence of e.g. acidic compound having boron-oxygen bond.
AU KOICHI S
   TOMOYUKI T
   SAITO K
   TADA T
AE POLYPLASTICS KK (POPL-C)
   POLYPLASTICS KK (POPL-C)
GA 202011047H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a liquid-crystalline resin, involves (i) reacting one or more starting-material monomers comprising at least one compound chosen from aromatic hydroxycarboxylic acids and their polymerizable derivatives, and (ii) carrying out condensation-polymerization of the starting-material monomers in the presence of either an acidic compound having a boron-oxygen bond or a compound capable of yielding the acidic compound in the reaction system.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a liquid-crystalline resin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces liquid-crystalline resin with excellent strength and heat resistance in a shorter time. The method easily controls the acylation reaction, and reduces the generation of carbon dioxide gas while improving the condensation-polymerization reaction rate. The method produces liquid-crystalline resin that transmits polarized light even in a molten and stationary state, and exhibits superior optical anisotropy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acidic compound having the boron-oxygen bond is an arylboronic acid having at least one electron-withdrawing group in the aryl group.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method further includes acylating the starting-material monomers in the presence of an acidic compound having the boron-oxygen bond or a compound capable of generating the acidic compound in the reaction system, before step (ii). In step (ii), a compound having a tertiary amine or its oxide is further present.
   EXAMPLE - 184 g 4-Hydroxybenzoic acid, 52 g terephthalic acid, 22 g isophthalic acid, 83 g 4,4'-dihydroxybiphenyl, 54 mg phenylboronic acid as catalyst, and 233 g acetic anhydride as acylating agent were charged into a polymerization vessel, the temperature of the reaction system was increased to 140 degrees C, and the acylation reaction was carried out at 140 degrees C for 3 hours. The temperature were further increased to 360 degrees C for 4.5 hours, and then reduced to 10 torr (i.e. 1330 Pa) for 15 minutes, and condensation-polymerization was performed while distilling off acetic acid along with excess acetic anhydride, and other low-boiling components. The time from the start of pressure reduction until the stirring torque reached a predetermined value was 20 minutes. The nitrogen was introduced to change the pressure from a reduced pressure to a normal pressure to a pressurized state, and the polymer was discharged from the lower part of the polymerization vessel. Then, the strand was pelletized to obtain a liquid crystalline resin pellet.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)
MC A05-E01A1; A09-A02A; A10-D05
IP C08G-063/60; C08G-063/84; C08G-069/44; C08G-063/87

   TANG Y

   TANG Y (TANG-Individual)
GA 2021424841
AB    NOVELTY - A modular closed chicken breeding system includes a house unit, at least one carrying device, a first exhaust device, an oxygen supply device, and a second exhaust device. A poultry breeding room, an air processing room, a first air exchange room and a second air exchange room in the house unit form a closed circulation space together. Preferred Method: In the step (2), during the preparation a natural active ingredient, setting the parameters of the carbon dioxide supercritical extraction method to a temperature of 35 degrees C and a pressure of 7.4 MPa for 2 hours. Preferred Components: The cosmetic is a cleanser, skin cream, skin lotion, eye cream or toner.

PT P
PN CN110540740-A
TI Degradable zinc complex-titanium dioxide-polylactic acid antibacterial film comprises Schiff base-zinc complex, tetraisopropyl titanate, polylactic acid and polydimethylsiloxane.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHAO C
   QIAO R
AE JIAXING YINGMAN TRADE CO LTD (JIAX-Non-standard)
GA 2019A5176B
AB    NOVELTY - Degradable zinc complex-titanium dioxide-polylactic acid antibacterial film and its preparation method, are claimed. The film comprises 3-6 pts. wt. Schiff base-zinc complex, 5-10 pts. wt. tetraisopropyl titanate, 78-90 pts. wt. polylactic acid and 2-6 pts. wt. polydimethylsiloxane. The preparation method involves the use of distilled water, cellulose powder, potassium periodate, anhydrous ethanol, L-valine, zinc chloride, dilute hydrochloric acid solution, and ethyl acetate.
   USE - As degradable zinc complex-titanium dioxide-polylactic acid antibacterial film.
   ADVANTAGE - The Schiff base-zinc complex captures photo-generated electrons e- and forms a strongly reducing excited state, which undergoes a redox reaction with active protein oxygen metabolism enzymes in bacteria or microorganisms, inhibits and destroys the metabolic process of microorganisms. The excited states of photo-generated electrons e-, strongly oxidative holes h+, and strongly reducing complexes promote the reduction of ester groups and bond cleavage in polylactic acid, and accelerate the oxidative decomposition of carbon dioxide and water in polylactic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The Schiff base-zinc complex is prepared by (a) adding distilled water into a reaction bottle, sequentially adding cellulose powder and potassium periodate, placing the reaction bottle in a constant-temperature water bath kettle, heating at 60-65 degrees C, stirring at constant speed for 2-4 hours, filtering the solution at the end of the reaction, washing the solid product by using distilled water, and thoroughly drying to prepare the cellulose selective ring-opening product, (b) adding distilled water and anhydrous ethanol to the reaction bottle as a mixed solvent in a volume ratio of (2-3):1, adding L-valine, stirring until dissolved, adding the cellulose selective ring-opening product, placing the reaction bottle in a constant-temperature water bath kettle, heating at 70-80 degrees C, performing condensation reaction at constant speed for 3-6 hours, filtering the solution at the end of the reaction, washing the solid product by using distilled water and anhydrous ethanol, and thoroughly drying to obtain the Schiff base organic ligand, and (c) adding distilled water and anhydrous ethanol to a reaction bottle as a mixed solvent in a volume ratio of (2-4):1, adding zinc chloride, stirring until dissolving, adding Schiff base organic ligand, placing the reaction bottle in a constant-temperature water bath kettle, heating at 70-85 degrees C, stirring at constant speed for 2-5 hours, filtering the solution at the end of the reaction, thoroughly washing the solid product by using distilled water and anhydrous ethanol, and drying to prepare the Schiff base-zinc complex. Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the mass ratio of cellulose powder and potassium periodate is 1:(1.5-2.5). In step (b), the mass ratio of the cellulose selective ring-opening product to L-valine is 1:(3-5). In step (c), the mass ratio of the Schiff base organic ligand to zinc chloride is 1:(3.5-6).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The antibacterial film is prepared by (i) adding 200-800 ml absolute ethanol to a reaction bottle, adding dilute hydrochloric acid solution to adjust the pH to 4-5, slowly adding tetraisopropyl titanate while stirring at a constant speed, stirring the reaction at a constant speed 2-3 hours, placing the reaction bottle in an ultrasonic treatment device, and ultrasonically dispersing at an ultrasonic frequency of 20-25 KHz for 1.5-2 hours to obtain a nano titanium dioxide precursor solution, and (ii) adding 400-1200 ml ethyl acetate to a reaction bottle, sequentially adding polylactic acid and polydimethylsiloxane, thoroughly stirring, adding the precursor solution, stirring, adding Schiff base-zinc complex, transferring the solution into a hydrothermal autoreactor, heating at 80-90 degrees C, stirring the reaction at a constant speed for 3-5 hours, cooling to room temperature, pouring the solution into a film-forming mold, and performing natural volatilization and film-forming culture to prepare the antibacterial film.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).)

PT P
PN CN110407196-A; CN110407196-B
TI Preparation of low-defect graphene film by            directionally freezing aqueous graphene oxide solution            with liquid nitrogen to obtain anisotropic graphene            oxide frozen foam, drying, reducing graphene oxide,            carbonizing and graphitizing.
AU ZHU Y
   PENG Q
   HE X
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 201994310T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of low-defect graphene film                involves directionally freezing aqueous graphene                oxide solution with liquid nitrogen to obtain                anisotropic graphene oxide frozen foam with                different shapes, drying the anisotropic graphene                oxide frozen foam using carbon dioxide                supercritical drying method to obtain anisotropic                graphene oxide foam, placing the anisotropic                graphene oxide foam in a hot-pressing reaction                furnace, reducing graphene oxide using hot-pressing                oxidation pretreatment process, placing the reduced                graphene oxide film in a hot-pressing reaction                furnace, carbonizing graphene using high                temperature vacuum heat treatment process, placing                the graphene carbon film in a high temperature                graphitization furnace and graphitizing the carbon                film using gradient heating method.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of                low-defect graphene film.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and economical                preparation of low-defect graphene film with                excellent thermal conductivity, mechanical                strength, flexibility and dispersibility.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The aqueous graphene                  oxide solution is prepared by using graphene                  oxide slurry as raw material, adding deionized                  water, stirring at 100-700 rpm for 60-120 minutes                  and ultrasonically processing for 30-60 minutes                  under frequency of 10-100 kHz. The directionally                  freezing step is carried out using                  ice-separation-induced self-assembly method. The                  drying method is freeze-drying method. The step                  of reducing graphene oxide involves heating at                  250 degrees C, mechanically pressurizing,                  controlling pressure to 20-30 MPa, continuously                  heating at 350-400 degrees C, cooling to constant                  temperature for 1-1.5 hours, removing pressure                  and continuously cooling to room temperature to                  obtain reduced graphene oxide film. The                  carbonizing step involves heating at 250 degrees                  C, mechanically pressurizing, controlling the                  pressure to 20-30 MPa, continuously heating at                  800-1000 degrees C, cooling for 2-2.5 hours,                  releasing the pressure and cooling to room                  temperature to obtain graphene carbon film. The                  graphitizing step involves heating at 1000-1200                  degrees C, 2000-2200 degrees C or 2800-3000                  degrees C for 30-35 minutes under heating rate of                  5 degrees C/minute, 10 degrees C/minute or 20                  degrees C/minute and cooling to room temperature                  for 115-120 minutes. Preferred Components: The                  concentration of graphene oxide is 1-10 mg/ml,                  preferably 3-7 mg/ml, and graphene oxide slurry                  is 20 mg/ml.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN WO2019203278-A1; TW201943856-A; KR2020130858-A; CN112004947-A; IN202017040092-A; JP2020514418-X; TW698532-B1; JP6923075-B2; KR2359738-B1
TI Manufacturing molten steel by adding carbon source to molten steel, adding direct reduced iron to perform melt-reduction, performing desulfurization by adding deoxidization material, discharging slag, decarburizing, and tapping steel.
AU HIRATA H
AE NIPPON STEEL CORP (YAWA-C)
   NIPPON STEEL CORP (YAWA-C)
GA 201988284R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for manufacturing molten steel, involves (i) adding a carbon source to molten steel left in an electric furnace as molten seed when a previous channel is tapped to obtain a carbon-containing molten iron, (ii) adding a direct reduced iron (DRI) to the carbon-containing molten iron to perform melt-reduction, (iii) performing desulfurization processing by adding a deoxidization material, (iv) discharging desulfurization slag generated by the desulfurization processing, (v) performing decarburizing treatment by blowing oxygen, (vi) discharging the decarburized slag, and (vii) tapping the steel and leaving a portion as a molten seed for a subsequent channel.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing molten steel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method manufactures molten steel with high productivity, low heat loss, and low carbon dioxide generation by melting and reducing DRI having a low metallization rate in a melting furnace such as an electric furnace.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the carbon-containing molten iron having a carbon concentration of 0.5-1.5 %mass is obtained. Preferred Conditions: When the furnace diameter of the electric furnace is D (m), the amount of seed hot water W (t) left in step (vii) is 0.3x D2-1.6x D2.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

PT P
PN CN110338457-A
TI Respiration detecting and breathing cessation            case, has sensor provided with humidity sensor and            camera for sensing elements to obtain detection data of            sensor under premise condition for starting electric            control locking structure.
AU SONG Y
AE SONG Y (SONG-Individual)
GA 201989847P
AB    NOVELTY - The case has a respiratory motion detection                unit provided with a sensor. A barn and a bin cover                form a storage space locked by an electric control                locking structure. The electric control locking                structure is connected to a control board. The                control board controls motion of the electric                control locking structure under an action of the                control plate after opening the barn and the bin                cover for providing possibility of cigarette. The                sensor is provided with a gas pressure sensor, a                microphone, a temperature sensor, an oxygen or                carbon dioxide sensor, a humidity sensor, a camera                for sensing elements to obtain detection data of                the sensor under premise condition for starting the                electric control locking structure.

AU BAO L

AE FOSHAN POLYTECHNIC (FSPL-C)
GA 201977802R
AB    NOVELTY - Jackfruit seed fatty acid extract comprises linolenic acid, linoleic acid, palmitic acid and stearic acid.
   USE - The jackfruit seed fatty acid extract is useful for novel functional oil, preventing effect for high blood fat, and cardiovascular disorders.
   ADVANTAGE - The acid extract: improves commercial value of Artocarpus heterophyllus Lam industry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing jackfruit seed fatty acid extract comprising (i) Pre-treating jackfruit seeds to obtain jackfruit seed powder, (ii) placing jackfruit seed powder obtained in step (i) in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device for extraction, and obtaining primary extract of jackfruit seed powder, (iii) extracting primary extract of jackfruit seed powder obtained in the step (ii) with ethanol solution to obtain secondary extract of jackfruit seed powder, (iv) filtering secondary extract of jackfruit seed powder obtained in step (iii), and concentrating obtained filtrate to obtain fatty acid extract of jackfruit seed powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The jackfruit seed fatty acid extract specifically comprises 12-methyl myristate, dimethyl ruthenium, methyl palmitate, methyl ester of hard acid ester, trans-oleic acid methyl ester, 16-methyl oleate, methyl linoleate, methyl linolenic acid, methyl aramate, methyl undecanoate, and methyl docate. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the pre-processing process comprises placing fresh jackfruit seeds in autoclave for high-pressure cooking, removing shell fruit seed shell, and drying shelled jackfruit seeds, the dried jackfruit seeds are placing in lyophilizer for lyophilization, the lyophilized jackfruit seeds are grounding to obtain jackfruit seed powder. In the step (ii), the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process comprises subjecting jackfruit seed powder obtained in step (i) is to extraction in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, having extraction temperature of 40-50 degrees C, pressure of 20-30 Mpa, and extracting for 30-60 minutes. In the step (iii), the ethanol solution extraction process comprises adding 30-60 pts. wt. 60-80% (v/v) ethanol to primary extract of jackfruit seed powder obtained in step (ii), and stirring mixture at 30-50 degrees C for 1-3 hours to obtain jackfruit seed powder, and placing secondary extract and cooling.
   ACTIVITY - Cardiovascular-Gen.

The conductive paste comprises components as given in the specification, the mass ratio of 600-mesh and 1200-mesh silver-coated copper powder is 2:3, the polyurethane prepolymer has an NCO value of 12-14%, and the polyurethane prepolymer has a viscosity of 1000-2000 mPa-s. Preferred Method: The method further comprises (i) placing the polyurethane prepolymer at 30 degrees C, and adding silicone to obtain modified polyurethane, and (ii) mixing and stirring modified polyurethane, silver-coated copper powder and oxazolidine at 28-32 degrees C at a rotation speed of 260 rpm for 1 hour.

PT P
PN CN109678834-A
TI Extracting anthocyanin from mangosteen peel comprises e.g. processing raw material, microwave processing, adding ethanol and inorganic salt, stirring, allowing to stand, concentrating, precipitating, and removing impurity.
AU LIN Y
AE LIN Y (LINY-Individual)
GA 201941126K
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting anthocyanin from mangosteen peel comprises (1) cleaning mangosteen peel, drying in shady place, crushing to 60 mesh sieve to obtain mangosteen peel powder, (2) microwave processing the mangosteen peel powder, mixing with hot water, extracting by ball milling to obtain supernatant, adding water to mangosteen peel residue, continuing ball milling and extracting for 2-3 times, and combining the supernatant, (3) adding ethanol and inorganic salt into the supernatant, mechanically stirring for 10 minutes, allowing to stand for phase separation, taking upper phase and vacuum concentrating, precipitating the salt by adding absolute ethanol and then concentrating, and (4) placing the concentrated anthocyanin extract in the supercritical fluid device, removing the impurity by supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain pure anthocyanin.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting anthocyanin from mangosteen peel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method extracts not less than 95% pure anthocyanin pure product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (3), the type of inorganic salt is ammonium sulfate, sodium sulfate, or sodium citrate, the inorganic salt is added in an amount of 35-50 m/m in terms of water mass. Preferred Method: In the step (2), the microwave processing condition is as follows, the power is 200-300 W, and the processing time is 10-25 minutes. In the step (2), the temperature of hot water is 60-70 degrees C, and the mass volume ratio of mangosteen peel powder and hot water is 1:10-15 g/ml. In the step (2), milling process condition is as follows, the diameter of the added metal ball particle is 3-5 mm, the number of metal balls added is 20-15, extraction time is 3-5 minutes, and speed is 3000-4500 revolutions/minute. In the step (3), the amount of ethanol added is 35-50 vol./vol. of the supernatant. In the method, after the concentration of the step (3) is concentrated under reduced pressure, the volume of the concentrated extract is 15-25 vol.%/vol.% of the volume of the raw material liquid. In the step (4), the temperature in the supercritical carbon dioxide treatment is 35-45 degrees C, the pressure is 22-24 MPa, and the treatment time is 25-35 minutes.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.)
MC E06-A01; E11-Q01C

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing doped nano zinc germanate comprises                (i) mixing zinc acetate, copper acetate and                germanium oxide in certain molar ratio in a raw                material storage tank to obtain solution 1, (ii)                dissolving sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate in a                certain amount of water, stirring and mixing evenly                in a storage tank to obtain solution 2, and (iii)                adding solution 1 and solution 2 into the reaction                vessel, adding sodium hydroxide solution to adjust                the pH value, reacting under the reaction                conditions, and washing the product with water to                neutrality.

PN CN109517341-A
TI Bio-based citric acid epoxy resin composition comprises citric acid epoxy, unsaturated bio-based dicarboxylic acid, first curing accelerator and free radical initiator.
AU YAO Y
AE JINHUA YUANCHENG NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (JINH-Non-standard)
GA 201930253L
AB    NOVELTY - Bio-based citric acid epoxy resin composition comprises 100 pts. wt. citric acid epoxy, 1-100 pts. wt. unsaturated bio-based dicarboxylic acid, 1-10 pts. wt. first curing accelerator and 1-10 pts. wt. free radical initiator.
   USE - The bio-based citric acid epoxy resin composition is useful for coating (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The bio-based citric acid epoxy resin composition has improved material mechanical property, non-toxic raw material, reduces the emission of carbon dioxide, reduces the damage to human body, reduces the pollution to the environment and is suitable for industrial use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for curing the bio-based citric acid epoxy resin composition, comprising: (i) mixing the raw materials; and (ii) curing the mixed raw materials at 70-100 degrees C for 2-6 hours, at 101-160 degrees C for 2-6 hours, and at 161-200 degrees C for 3-5 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The bio-based citric acid epoxy resin composition comprises 100 pts. wt. citric acid epoxy, 10-80 pts. wt. unsaturated bio-based dicarboxylic acid, 1-10 pts. wt. first curing accelerator and 1-10 pts. wt. free radical initiator. Preferred Components: The first curing accelerator comprises tetrabutylammonium bromide, triphenylphosphine, 2-methylimidazole, 2,4,6-tris(dimethylaminomethyl)phenol and/or boron trifluoride. The free radical initiator includes peroxide and/or azo-based curing agent, preferably tert-butyl peroxybenzoate. Preferred Method: The citric acid epoxy is prepared by: (i) mixing citric acid, N,N'-dimethylformamide and acetone at 60-90 degrees C until the citric acid is dissolved, adding potassium carbonate and allyl bromide, carrying out reflux reaction for 32-48 hours, removing the produced white solid by suction filtration, distilling the unreacted allyl bromide and acetone under reduced pressure, adding chloroform, extracting, removing N,N'-dimethylformamide and drying over sodium sulfate to obtain triallyl citrate; (ii) reacting the triallyl citrate, m-chloroperoxybenzoic acid and solvent under an inert gas atmosphere at 25-30 degrees C for 32-48 hours, precipitating the white solid, freezing at -5 degrees C for 12-18 hours, filtering the white solid by suction filtration to obtain the filtrate, removing the solvent, adding solvent to dissolve, extracting the solution at least twice with sodium sulfite solution, drying the organic phase using magnesium sulfate, removing the solvent, adding solvent to dissolve, adding frozen methanol, freezing for 12-18 hours, filtering the white solid and removing the solvent to obtain citric acid epoxy; where the molar ratio of citric acid, potassium carbonate, allyl bromide and chloroform is 0.26-0.52:0.86-2:1.56-3:2:5; and the molar ratio of triallyl citrate and m-chloroperoxybenzoic acid is 0.1-0.5:0.45-0.9.
DC A21 (Epoxides; aminoplasts; phenoplasts.); A82 (Coatings, impregnations, polishes - excluding textile finishing.)
MC A05-A04; A08-D01; A08-D02; A11-A03; A11-C02; A12-B01L

PN CN109128057-A
TI Managing landscape water biomaterial comprises adding combination of polymer terpene polymer and marine bioactive substance synthetic composition into landscape water, adsorbing and degrading pathogenic microorganisms under light and heat.
AU ZHENG X
AE ZHENG X (ZHEN-Individual)
GA 201904920A
AB    NOVELTY - Managing landscape water biomaterial comprises using a combination of a polymer terpene polymer and a marine bioactive substance synthetic composition, killing pathogenic microorganisms in landscape water using the synthetic composition under the action of light and heat, adsorbing microparticles in water, and completely degrading the pathogenic microorganisms.
   USE - The method is useful for managing landscape water biomaterial.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses marine bioactive substance, which completely dissolves in water and has has other excellent characteristics, can be degraded into small molecules of carbon dioxide within a relatively short time and does not leave any harmful substances to the environment, can be sterilized in water treatment, can be completely degraded, which effectively solves eutrophication problem and is good for environment protective agent with excellent product performance index, eliminates the original disinfectant toxic side effect to human body and prevents the harm to water quality caused by cultivated species and environmental pollution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The process of treating landscape water using the marine bioactive substance synthetic composition comprises (a) adding marine bioactive substance liquid disinfectant into the landscape water to be treated, (b) ionizing marine bioactive substance in landscape water to produce polar molecules with positive electric characters, (c) allowing a polar molecule with a positive sign to adsorb negatively charged pathogenic microorganisms under the long-term effects of light and heat, entangling pathogenic microorganisms, inhibiting the propagation of pathogenic microorganisms, blocking the passage of respiratory and nutrient transport ions, where the pathogenic microorganisms die, and (d) adsorbing the micro-particles in the landscape water after the death of the pathogenic microorganisms, and degrading into small molecules of carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The disinfection rate of the marine bioactive substance liquid disinfectant on Candida albicans is more than 99% under 500 minutes disinfection time with disinfectant amount of 500 mg/l and on Bacillus (Wax sample and Bacillus subtilis black variant spore) (sic) and hepatitis B virus is 100% under 20 minutes disinfection time with disinfectant amount of 800 mg/l. The minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) and minimum bactericidal concentration (MBC) of the disinfectant against common pathogenic bacteria of aquatic animals are 150-200 mu l/l. The content of the active ingredient of the marine bioactive substance increased by 4.4% after being placed at 37 degrees C for three months. The marine bioactive substance has a pH of 8.89.

PT P
PN CN109022811-A; CN109022811-B
TI Refining of lead from pyrite cinder rotary kiln involves mixing pyrite cinder with calcium chloride and calcium hydroxide, ball milling to obtain abrasive, placing in rotary kiln, oxidizing by chlorine, heating and cooling.
AU WANG X
AE WANG X (WANG-Individual)
GA 2018A5137H
AB    NOVELTY - Refining of lead from pyrite cinder rotary kiln comprises mixing pyrite cinder with calcium chloride and calcium hydroxide, ball milling to obtain abrasive, making abrasive into rigid pellet, placing in rotary kiln, oxidizing by chlorine into iron ore for iron making, cooling flue gas by heat exchange at 400 degrees C to obtain mist and wet liquid by humidification with water to obtain lead dust and liquid, cooling, washing, replacing liquid with iron filings to obtain copper gold and silver mud, adding soda ash solution to first reactor, heating at 70-80 degrees C, gradually adding lead gold-silver mud, stirring, stopping reaction when pH value of solution is 7-8 and no carbon dioxide overflow, filtering solution to obtain filtrate and filter cake.
   USE - Method for refining lead from pyrite cinder rotary kiln.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has lead yield of 93%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises washing filter cake with water, adding 15% acetic acid solution to second reaction vessel equipped with stirring shaft, stirring at 60 degrees C for filtration, taking cake, controlling pH value of liquid to 5.5-6, cooling, performing vacuum suction filtration to obtain gold and silver mud and lead acetate solution, adding lead acetate solution to third reaction vessel provided with stirring shaft, stirring at 50 degrees C for 5-6 hours, adding soda ash until no carbon dioxide escapes and pH value of feed liquid is 6-7, performing vacuum suction filtration to obtain filtrate and filter cake, taking filter cake as lead mud, washing lead mud, drying and casting into lead ingot. Preferred Components: The particle diameter of 85% of abrasive is less than 0.043 mm. The particle size of rigid pellets is 10-15 mm. The mass fraction of soda ash solution is 15%.

PN WO2018193901-A1; JP2019513562-X; CN110506071-A; US2020040156-A1; JP6655758-B2; EP3613801-A1; EP3613801-A4; CN110506071-B
TI Manufacture of polypropylene resin foaming particles used for forming foam molded article, involves dispersing polypropylene resin particles in aqueous dispersion medium, and heating and pressurizing obtained dispersion liquid.
AU MOCHIZUKI Y
   ITOI A
AE KANEKA CORP (KANF-C)
   KANEKA CORP (KANF-C)
   KANEKA CORP (KANF-C)
   KANEKA CORP (KANF-C)
GA 201883998J
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of polypropylene resin foaming particles involves dispersing polypropylene resin particles formed by adding 1-25 pts. wt. carbon black and 0.05-1 pt. wt. magnesium hydroxide and/or magnesium carbonate with respect to 100 pts. wt. polypropylene resin together with carbon dioxide in an aqueous dispersion medium in an airtight container, obtaining a dispersion liquid, heating and pressurizing the dispersion liquid, and discharging the polypropylene resin particles contained in the dispersion liquid to a pressure area lower than the internal pressure of the airtight container.
   USE - Manufacture of polypropylene resin foaming particles used for forming foam molded article (all claimed). Uses include but are not limited to thermal insulation material, buffer packing material, motor vehicle upholstery material, and core material for motor vehicle bumper.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of polypropylene resin foaming particles with high uniformity, providing foam molded article with excellent color tone.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for polypropylene resin foaming particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The aqueous dispersion medium is water.

PN CN108675914-A
TI Preparing trifluoroiodomethane involves entering            raw material into adsorption column to process in            closed environment under gas phase conditions to remove            water and some organic impurities.
AU DONG L
   QING F
   GUO Z
   JIA X
AE YUJI LANGFANG NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (YUJI-Non-standard)
   BEIJING YUJI TECHNOLOGY DEV CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201884761L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing trifluoroiodomethane involves                entering the raw material into an adsorption column                to process in a closed environment under gas phase                conditions to remove water and some organic                impurities. The obtained material is introduced                into a rectification column for batch                rectification. The top of the column is vented to                remove the 1-5C halogenated alkane, halogenated                alkene, halogenated alkyne, nitrogen, oxygen,                carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide impurities. The                99.99% pure trifluoroiodomethane is stored in the                adsorption column. The adsorbent is silica gel,                activated alumina, activated carbon, carbon                molecular sieve, A-type molecular sieve, X-type                molecular sieve, Y-type molecular sieve or ZSM-5                type Molecular sieves.
   USE - Method for preparing trifluoroiodomethane                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare                trifluoroiodomethane with high purity in a simple                manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flowchart of                the method for preparing trifluoroiodomethane.                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).

PN JP2018162199-A; JP6926582-B2
TI Cement composition used in manufacture of e.g. mortar, comprises cement clinker, gypsum, mixed material comprising limestone and blast furnace slag, and dust e.g. mixed dust of electrostatic-precipitation dust and chlorine bypass dust.
AU TONOKOCHI H
   IGEI H
   SAWABE N
AE UBE IND LTD (UBEI-C)
GA 201880584U
AB    NOVELTY - A cement composition comprises a cement clinker, a gypsum, a mixed material comprising more than 20 %mass to 40 %mass limestone and blast furnace slag, and dust chosen from electrostatic-precipitation dust, mixed dust of electrostatic-precipitation dust and chlorine bypass dust, mixed dust of electrostatic-precipitation dust and desalination dust, mixed dust of chlorine bypass dust and desalination dust, and mixed dust of electrostatic-precipitation dust, chlorine bypass dust and desalination dust.
   USE - Cement composition is used in manufacture of mortar and concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The cured product formed using the cement composition has improved strength such as compressive strength, and Blaine specific surface area. The cement composition effectively utilizes dust as waste material, and reduces amount of carbon dioxide generated during manufacturing process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of cement composition, which involves mixing the cement clinker, gypsum, mixed material, and dust.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The composition further comprises 0.01-0.2 %mass chloride ions. The electrostatic-precipitation dust comprises 5-20 %mass silica, 2-8 %mass alumina, 0.1-5 %mass ferric oxide, 30-50 %mass calcium, and 0.05-2 %mass chloride ions. The chlorine bypass dust comprises 1-30 %mass silica, 0.1-10 %mass alumina, 0.1-5 %mass ferric oxide, 1-65 %mass calcium, and 4-35 %mass chloride ions. The demineralization dust comprises 5-30 %mass silica, 1-15 %mass alumina, 0.1-5 %mass ferric oxide, 15-65 %mass calcium, and 0.1-2 %mass chloride ions. The composition comprises 0.1-18 %mass electrostatic-precipitation dust, 0.05-5 %mass chlorine bypass dust and/or 0.1-20 %mass demineralization dust.

   LUO B

   MIDEA GROUP CO LTD (MEDE-C)

Air flow of an air processing device is adjusted according to pollution concentration of the contaminating component to improve air quality of the indoor air. Pollution detecting value of the indoor air is detected by using a gas sensor, where the contaminating component comprises volatile organic compounds, formaldehyde, methane, toluene, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and fine particles. PT P
PN CN108557787-A
TI Recovering crude argon gas involves recycling argon gas pressure, pre-mixing rough argon gas with hydrogen to remove oxygen, cooling argon gas with temperature, and processing secondary pre-cooling treatment to obtain dry purified.
AU WANG C
AE SHANGHAI LIANFENG ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201878009J
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering crude argon gas involves recycling argon gas pressure, pre-mixing rough argon gas with hydrogen to remove oxygen. The argon gas with high temperature is cooled, and secondary pre-cooling treatment is processed to obtain dry purified. The crude argon gas is deried and sent into pressure swing adsorption unit which contains first adsorbent vessel and second adsorption vessel to fill. The oxygen, carbon dioxide, nitrogen gas component are absorbed by first absorbing container with adsorbent selectively under working pressure. The argon gas after adsorption is discharged from top of first adsorption vessel. The second adsorption vessel to adsorbent in adsorption of impurity gas is desorbed through bottom of second adsorption vessel.
   USE - Method for recovering crude argon gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to recover crude argon gas that reduces manufacturing period, and cost effective manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The pre-cooling of argon gas is processed after pressure enters first cooler for first time. The oil is filtered and active carbon is absorbed after removing oil. The oxygen and hydrogen is mixed in reaction for removing oxygen. The temperature is reduced to 40 degrees C and cooled to 5-8 degrees C by second hydrogen gas cooler. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of crude argon gas is 5-8 degrees C. The temperature of argon gas is 200-300 degrees C. The purity of argon gas is 99-99.99%. Preferred Compositions: The content of oil filter and active carbon absorber oil is less than 1 parts per million. Preferred Components: The main component is argon, nitrogen gas and oxygen gas.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN108410613-A
TI Preparing rose-scented wine by fermenting rose flower, by preparing fermentation base liquid comprising rose aroma component, fermenting, extracting aroma of rose in fermentation base liquid, introducing rose aroma component into wine.
AU FENG Z
   WANG J
   ZHENG W
   HAN H
   ZHENG J
   FENG Y
AE UNIV XINJIANG AGRIC (UYXA-C)
GA 201866545D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing rose-scented wine by fermenting rose flower, involves (a) preparing a fermentation base liquid comprising rose aroma component, where the fermentation base liquid has a refractive sugar content of 10-30%, (b) inoculating yeast into the fermentation base liquid and performing alcohol fermentation for 5-7 days, and (c) while the alcohol is being fermented, extracting the aroma component of the rose in the fermentation base liquid by the stripping action of the alcohol to produce carbon dioxide, and introducing rose aroma component into the wine through a gas pipeline, where the rose aroma components are absorbed by the natural respiration of the wine.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing rose-scented wine by fermenting rose flower (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is simple and increases annual production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the rose-scented wine obtained by the method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The fermentation base liquid is obtained by mixing roses and grapes in a mass ratio of 1:(1-3). The fermentation base liquid is obtained by mixing rose sauce with water in a mass ratio 1:(1-1.5). The rose sauce is obtained by mixing roses and white granulated sugar, and sealing and storing for 1-2 months. The rose and white granulated sugar are mixed in a weight ratio of 1:(1-3), and the rose sauce has a refractive sugar content of 50-75%. The inoculation volume of yeast in step (b) is 0.2-0.5 Torr of the volume of the fermentation base liquid. The viable cell count of yeast is (4-10)x 107 cells/ml. In step (b), the temperature of alcohol fermentation is 20-35 degrees C, and the alcohol fermentation process is accompanied by stirring at 5-20 revolutions per minute (rpm). In step (c), the wine temperature is 0-20 degrees C, and the wine absorbs the rose aroma component by stirring at 5-30 rpm. The mass ratio of the fermentation base liquid to wine is 1:(3-10).
