TI Preparation method of cyanamide aqueous solution, involves mixing industrial solid waste calcium carbonate and urea, grinding, calcining, cooling, grinding, adding to water, stirring, filtering, recycling calcium carbonate, and drying.
AU YU L
   ZHANG C
   LIANG W
   LIU J
   ZHANG N
   AN J
   GANG P
AE SHANDONG EFIRM BIOCHEMISTRY & ENVIRONMEN (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201859086B
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of cyanamide aqueous solution involves (1) mixing industrial solid waste calcium carbonate and urea, grinding, calcining at 260-400 degrees C for 2-4 hours under the protection of nitrogen, heating at 650-850 degrees C, continuously calcining for 2-4 hours, cooling to room temperature under nitrogen atmosphere, and grinding the obtained crude lime nitrogen product, (2) adding the crude lime nitrogen product in batches under the condition of continuously inletting carbon dioxide into water, stirring and reacting, controlling the processing temperature and pH value, filtering, recycling industrial solid waste calcium carbonate, drying, returning to step (1), and (3) adjusting the pH value of the filtrate obtained, and concentrating to obtain cyanamide aqueous solution with different desired concentration.
   USE - Preparation method of cyanamide aqueous solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of cyanamide aqueous solution with high yield, by economical and environmentally-friendly processes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The industrial solid waste calcium carbonate is a solid waste calcium carbonate produced in the industrial production of cyanamide Preferred Composition: The ratio of the amount-of-substance of calcium carbonate and urea in the step (1) is 1:(2-4). Preferred Condition: The temperature in the step (2) is 0-20 degrees C. Preferred Properties: In the step (2), the pH value is 6-9. In step (3), the pH value is adjusted to 4-5.

PN CN108124773-A
TI Performing apple stem segments tissue culture            involves inserting plant having more than three            centimeter of cotyledons after propagation into            substrate, where substrate is Murashige and Skoog            medium, and vermiculite.
AU CAI X
   CAI J
AE BAOJI SONGLIANG AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BAOJ-Non-standard)
GA 201848362N
AB    NOVELTY - Performing apple stem segments tissue culture                involves inserting plant having more than 3 cm of                cotyledons after propagation into a substrate,                where substrate is Murashige and Skoog medium,                vermiculite, illumination time 16 hours, 1500-2000                lux, temperature 25 plus minus 2 degrees C,                humidity 80%, carbon dioxide concentration 900-1000                partsper million, culture room cleanliness level                1000 or above. The formation of root-developed                apple intact plants after 20 days of culture.
   USE - Method for performing apple stem segments                tissue culture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform apple stem                segments tissue culture where excellent traits of                the female parent are completely preserved, and the                subculture is better than the female parent by the                tip detoxification technology, has improved root                quality of woody seedlings. The roots of tissue                culture seedlings are developed, complete, and have                a high survival rate without the need for                refining.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The Murashige and                  Skoog medium and vermiculite is 800 g of                  vermiculite per liter of the Murashige and Skoog                  medium solution. Preferred Process: The apple                  stem segments tissue culture is performed by                  collecting fresh stems of apples and washing in                  clean water. The stalks are cut into stem                  segments of about 4 cm, 2 axillary buds are                  retained, sterilized with 0.1% mercury for 15                  minutes, sterilized with 75% alcohol for 5                  minutes, and rinsed 3-5 times with sterile water.                  The Murashige and Skoog medium, 30g/L sucrose,                  6g/L agar, 0.8mg/L 6- 6-Benzylaminopurine,                  0.5mg/L indole-3-butyric acid medium is taken,                  light 2000-3000 Lux for 14 hours, temperature 25                  plus minus 2 degrees C, humidity 70%.                  Adventitious buds emerged from the axillary buds                  and the first generation is obtained after about                  25 days of cultivation. The adventitious buds is                  inserted into the medium of Murashige and Skoog                  medium, 30 g/L sucrose, 6g/L agar, 1.2 mg/L                  6-Benzylaminopurine, 0.8 mg/L Indole acetic acid,                  light 2000- 3000 Lux for 14 hours, temperature 25                  plus minus 2 degrees C, humidity 70%, cultured                  for about 25 days. Rooting is induced to achieve                  a growth of more than 3 cm, plants with more than                  3 cotyledons inserted into the substrate. The                  substrate is Murashige and Skoog medium,                  vermiculite, light time 16 hours, 1500-2000 lux,                  temperature 25 plus minus 2 degrees C, humidity                  80%, carbon dioxide concentration 900-1000                  partsper million, the culture room cleanliness                  level is above 1000. The roots of the developed                  apple intact plants are formed after 20 days of                  culture, where Murashige and Skoog medium,                  vermiculite is 800 g of vermiculite per liter of                  MS solution. The whole apple plant is                  transplanted into a biodegradable bag and                  transplanted into a greenhouse. The temperature                  is 15-30 degrees C and the humidity is 85%.

PN MX2016016753-A1
TI Obtaining astaxanthin from exoskeleton involves conditioning raw material, consisting of elimination of impurities by mechanical manner, demineralizing with hydrochloric acid at room temperature, and drying in convection oven.
AU AVILA G R
   NAFARRATE AYALA G M
   MONTOYA J M
   OTERO DIAZ INFANTE J A
AE MEXIFOOD SA DE CV (MEXI-Non-standard)
GA 2018834291
AB    NOVELTY - Obtaining astaxanthin from exoskeleton involves conditioning the raw material, consisting of the elimination of impurities by mechanical manner, demineralizing with 1 normal hydrochloric acid in 1.10-1.15 (weight/volume) ratio for 1.5-6 hours, at room temperature, drying in a convection oven, at 75 degrees C for 4 hours, crushing the material in a mill up to a size equal to or less than 2 mu m, drying the ground material at 45 degrees C for 12 hours, extracting by using super critical fluids at 34 degrees C under pressure of 337 bar, 3.4-4.8 L/minute of nitrogen, using mixture of carbon dioxide and solvent in volume ratio of 90:10, cooling of extracts obtained at (-)34 degrees C for 2 hours, centrifuging at 7740 g at (-)10 degrees C for 5 minutes, decanting and evaporating.
   USE - Method for obtaining astaxanthin from exoskeleton.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is ecofriendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The exoskeleton is obtained from blue-green and brown algae (Callinectes sapidus and C. bellicosus). The solvent used is of different polarity, from non-polar to highly polar, passing through intermediate polarity, selected from acetone and HAET (hexane+acetone+ethanol+toluene), or ethanol and isopropanol.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Preparation of aerogel-foam rubber composite particles involves weighing tetraethyl orthosilicate, anhydrous ethanol, water, octylphenol polyoxyethylene ether, acetone and nitrile foam rubber particles and then agitating and drying.
AU CHEN Q
   ZENG J
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM SCI CO LTD (XKLI-C)
GA 201844332W
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of aerogel-foam rubber composite particles involves weighing tetraethyl orthosilicate, anhydrous ethanol and water in a volume ratio of 14-16:20-25:5-8 and then mixing, adjusting pH value to 4-6 and then continuously stirring for 15-18 hours, adding octylphenol polyoxyethylene ether to the mixture and then agitating for 20-24 hours, adjusting pH value to 5-6 and then heating at 40-50 degrees C, adding acetone and nitrile foam rubber particles to the mixture and then agitating for 7-8 hours and drying using supercritical carbon dioxide for 1-3 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of aerogel-foam rubber composite particles, which are useful for processing sewage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of aerogel-foam rubber composite particles with excellent absorption effect and elasticity.

   R5,R6=trifluoromethyl.

   USE - Used as multi-generation energy island of electricity, water, hydrogen and methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy island integrates green electricity to produce hydrogen by recycling flue gas condensate and further recovers carbon dioxide and green electricity produces methanol; compensates the green electricity gap by the peak-shaving balance electricity in the island and ensures that the electricity and water polygeneration unit is coupled with wind turbines and photovoltaic power generation to achieve zero water intake outside the island and carbon neutrality within the island.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for method for poly-generation of electricity, water, hydrogen and methanol comprising (a)recycling condensed water generated by an electric water cogeneration device, utilizing the factory micro-grid to absorb and balance the green electric energy generated by a wind power and a photovoltaic power generation to electrolyze the condensed water to prepare hydrogen, further recycling the carbon dioxide generated by a electric water co-production device, synthesizing with the hydrogen to prepare methanol, where the recovery amount of carbon dioxide and the carbon dioxide emission reduction amount produced by hydrogen production, methanol production and green power plants is greater than the comprehensive energy consumption equivalent carbon equivalent of the energy island and so as to achieve the island carbon neutralization, (b) allowing a flue gas extraction hydroelectricity water co-production system to produce qualified electrolyzed water, transporting the electrolyzed water to a hydrogen production and storage device through a water storage tank to produce hydrogen, allowing a carbon collection and PN KR2021065534-A
TI Apparatus for manufacturing nanocalcium carbonate for use in e.g., paint, has extraction tank for supplying acidic solution and eluting cations in waste concrete, neutralization tank for supplying basic solution, and microbubble device.
AU CHOI J Y
   HA H Y
   AHN Y T
   HANMANHO
   PARK S
   LEESUNJAE
   MAN J J
AE KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
GA 202162845D
AB    NOVELTY - An apparatus for manufacturing nanocalcium carbonate comprises extraction tank (101) for supplying acidic solution (10) and eluting divalent or trivalent cations in waste concrete to produce elution solution; neutralization tank (102) for neutralizing elution solution by supplying basic solution (20); precipitation tank (103) for recovering impurities precipitated in neutralization process; filtration tank (104) including greater than or equal to 1 filtration membranes; storage tank (105) connected to filtration tank and storing supernatant that has passed through filtration tank; greater than or equal to 1 reaction tanks (106a, 106b) connected to storage tank and reacting supernatant liquid supplied from storage tank with carbon dioxide; and microbubble device (107a, 107b) connected to reaction tanks and supplying carbon dioxide in the form of microbubbles to reaction tanks, where solution is moved without power from the extraction tank to the storage tank.
   USE - An apparatus for manufacturing nanocalcium carbonate for use in building material, paint, paper, medicine, cosmetic, plastic, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - Calcium can be extracted from waste concrete containing various ions and selectively precipitate impurities in the form of hydroxide compounds to prepare nanocalcium carbonate through carbon dioxide reaction. The use of a pump required in the extraction, neutralization, precipitation, filtration, and storage steps is replaced by an eco-friendly method without power consumption, using the principle of siphon, which reduces the power consumption by about 18% and can reduce production costs. In addition, high-quality nanocalcium carbonate is produced by reacting nano- and micro-sized carbon dioxide using a microbubble device, ensuring competitiveness in the market. High concentrations of carbon dioxide and waste resources in the atmosphere are converted into usable resources through the method to produce added value. By designing a continuous automation program and establishing a low-power process, it is possible to preempt the market with features, such as economical production environment establishment and mass production of equal quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An apparatus for manufacturing nanocalcium carbonate comprises extraction tank (101) for supplying acidic solution (10) and eluting divalent or trivalent cations in waste concrete to produce an elution solution; neutralization tank (102) connected to extraction tank at a position lower than extraction tank and neutralizing elution solution by supplying basic solution (20); precipitation tank (103) connected to neutralization tank at a lower position than neutralization tank and recovering impurities precipitated in neutralization process; filtration tank (104) connected to settling tank at a lower position than settling tank, and including greater than or equal to 1 filtration membranes; storage tank (105) connected to filtration tank and storing supernatant that has passed through filtration tank; greater than or equal to 1 reaction tanks (106a, 106b) connected to storage tank and reacting supernatant liquid supplied from storage tank with carbon dioxide; and microbubble device (107a, 107b) connected to reaction tanks and supplying carbon dioxide in the form of microbubbles to reaction tanks, where solution is moved without power from the extraction tank to the storage tank. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of calcium carbonate which involves supplying elution solution from extraction tank to a neutralization tank, supplying the solution neutralized in the neutralization tank to the precipitation tank, supplying a solution from which the solids precipitated in the precipitation tank are removed to the filtration tank, supplying a supernatant that has passed through greater than or equal to 1 filtration membranes in a filtration tank to a storage tank, supplying the reservoir solution to greater than or equal to 1 reactors, and setting greater than or equal to 1 reactors to carbonation conditions, producing nanocalcium carbonate by supplying carbon dioxide to the reactor using a microbubble device, and recovering the calcium carbonate produced in the reaction tank, where the solution is supplied without power from the extraction tank to the storage tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the inorganic carbonation production process using waste concrete.
   Acidic solution (10)
   Basic solution (20)
   Extraction tank (101)
   Neutralization tank (102)
   Precipitation tank (103)
   Filtration tank (104)
   Storage tank (105)
   Reaction tanks (106a, 106b)
   Microbubble device (107a, 107b)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Supply of basic solution is stopped when the pH of the solution in the neutralization tank reaches 11.5. Carbonation condition in the reactor is performed by supplying a basic solution, and when the pH of the solution in the reactor reaches 12.5, the supply of the basic solution is stopped, and carbon dioxide is injected. When the pH of the solution in the reaction tank is less than or equal to 7, the injection of carbon dioxide is stopped. Preferred Conditions: Flow rate of the carbon dioxide is greater than or equal to 5 L/minute. Step of generating nanocalcium carbonate in the reaction tank is performed within 30 minutes.

An exhaust pipe with one end connected to a transition space (27) is connected to the cylinder. The other end of the exhaust pipe is connected to the middle of a pipe housing (30). A carbon dioxide storage tank (40) is provided with an intake pipe connected to the dioxygen exchange storage tank at one end. The carbon dioxide content in the transition space reaches above the set value. A piston (21) moves down to squeeze and press the valve to connect the exhaust pipe with the intake pipe. The intake pipe is provided with a fourth one-way valve (41). The inlet end of fourth one-way valve faces one end of the pipe housing. The outlet end of the fourth one-way valve is connected to the carbon dioxide storage tank.
   USE - Colour selector for processing rice.

TI Method for performing separation on dilute carbon            dioxide containing feed, involves passing heat transfer            fluids through heat transfer sections along axial            direction of radial flow adsorbent bed module during            adsorbing and desorbing.
AU SUNDARAM N
   MCCALL P P
   KEINATH B L
   NAGAVARAPU A K
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL TECHNOLOGY & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 2020590298
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves passing a feed comprising                a carbon dioxide (CO2) content of 5000vppm or less                and a first water content into a radial flow                adsorbent bed module. A heat transfer sections                (120) comprises a transfer section inner surface                that faces a central axis of the radial flow                adsorbent bed module and a transfer section outer                surface at a larger radial distance from the                central axis than the interior surface. A portion                of the adsorbed carbon dioxide is desorbed in the                presence of a purge gas under desorption conditions                to form a carbon dioxide enriched purge gas                comprising a carbon dioxide content greater than                the carbon dioxide content of the feed. The heat                transfer fluids are passed through the heat                transfer sections along an axial direction of the                radial flow adsorbent bed module during the                adsorbing and the desorbing.
   USE - Method for performing separation on dilute                carbon dioxide containing feed.
   ADVANTAGE - The system allows for efficient heat transfer                from the adsorbent beds and heat transfer fluid                conduits while still providing a compact footprint                for the adsorbent bed modules. The ability to                provide improved heat management and/or temperature                control is beneficial for improving the working                capacity of the adsorbent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for separating carbon dioxide from a dilute                feed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                radial flow adsorbent module.
   Annular module (100)
   Absorbent bed section (110)
   Heat transfer section (120)
   Central volume (130)
   Central axis (135)

PN US2019376419-A1; US11174759-B2
TI Method for producing power by using nested carbon dioxide cycle in gas turbine in combination with heat recovery steam generator, involves operating power production cycle where recycled carbon dioxide from another power production cycle.
AU ALLAM R J
   FORREST B A
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 2019A3860X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves operating first power production cycle (110) where recycled carbon dioxide stream (127) is subjected to repeated compression, heating, combustion, expansion for power production, and cooling. Second power production cycle (150) is operated where the compressed recycled carbon dioxide from the first power production cycle is heated with a heat source (160) that is independent of the first power production cycle, expanded for power production, and recombined with the recycled carbon dioxide stream in the first power production cycle. Work stream comprising recycled carbon dioxide is expanded across a turbine (120) to produce quantity of power. The heat is withdrawn from the work stream in a recuperative heat exchanger (125). The work stream is compressed.
   USE - Method for producing power by using nested carbon dioxide cycle in a gas turbine (GT) in combination with a heat recovery steam generator (HRSG).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables providing additional heating to achieve required recuperator efficiency while providing significant increases in power generation without need for significant changes to a primary equipment used in the first power production cycle. The method enables utilizing shared turbo-machinery to provide increased power output while performing optimization of a recuperative heat exchanger, and improving efficiency of the combustion cycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a power production system
   (2) a method for optimizing efficiency of a power production cycle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of power production system.
   Power production cycles (110, 150)
   Turbine (120)
   Recuperative heat exchanger (125)
   Recycled carbon dioxide stream (127)
   Heat source (160)

PN CN109709265-A
TI High-density drilling fluid carbon dioxide pollution experiment evaluation device, has lining whose top part is connected to lining cover that is opened with central hole, and cross baffle plate uniformly opened with multiple through holes.
AU LI Y
   ZHANG W
   WU J
   YUE Q
   HU Y
   HU W
AE CNOOC CHINA CO LTD ZHANJIANG BRANCH (CNOO-C)
GA 201943230M
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a tank cover connected with an aging tank and with a valve rod. An inner wall and a bottom wall of the aging tank are provided with a lining that is inserted between cross baffle plates. A top part of the lining is connected with a lining cover that is opened with a central hole. The cross baffle plate is uniformly opened with multiple through holes, where inner diameter of the aging tank is a 63.5mm and height of the aging tank is 160mm. The lining cover is provided with upper and lower layers. Upper and lower layers of the lining cover are sealed by a rubber ring. The lining cover and the cross baffle plate are made of PTFE.
   USE - High-density drilling fluid carbon dioxide pollution experiment evaluation device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can comprehensively and realistically simulates change of drilling fluid performance after carbon dioxide pollution and provides high-density drilling fluids so as to prevent and treat carbon dioxide pollution, and reduces problems of the aging tank caused by carbon dioxide corrosion products on performance of drilling fluid and lack of mixed pollution of carbon dioxide and drilling fluid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a high-density drilling fluid carbon dioxide pollution experiment evaluation method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a high-density drilling fluid carbon dioxide pollution experiment evaluation device.

PN WO2019026044-A1; WO2019026044-A8; CA3071080-A1; IN202017005723-A; CN110997629-A; EP3661913-A1; JP2020529981-W; EP3661913-B1; US2021363100-A1; IN386081-B; RU2768935-C2; ES2897549-T3; RU2020109372-A; CN110997629-B
TI Production of urea involves reacting ammonia and            carbon dioxide in which at least portion of carbon            dioxide is produced in oxy-combustion process which is            flameless process.
AU ZAMBIANCO A
   MONTRONE D
   POLIZZI R
   SALA M
   USAI G
AE SAIPEM SPA (SAIM-C)
   SAIPEM SPA (SAIM-C)
   SAIPEM SPA (SAIM-C)
   SAIPEM SPA (SAIM-C)
GA 2019139343
AB    NOVELTY - Production of urea involves reacting ammonia                and carbon dioxide. At least portion of the carbon                dioxide for the is produced in an oxy-combustion                process which is a flameless process.
   USE - Production of urea.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide is produced through the                oxy-combustion process which is flameless and                pressurized process. The capture of carbon dioxide                is simplified due to its high concentration in the                exhausts and the low content of contaminants and                inert materials. The wide flexibility of the carbon                supply which can be fed enables the use of waste                from adjacent plants which is difficult to dispose                or manage. The surplus of electric energy and/or                steam can be exported and/or integrated inside the                complex, thus increasing the efficiency. The                process of flameless and pressurized oxy-combustion                reduces the exhaust treatment section and                eliminates washing with ammonia. The quantity of                passivation air required by the urea plant is                reduced. Carbon dioxide produced can be used in a                new urea plant, irrespective of the urea production                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for urea                production plant (1), which comprises urea unit (2)                for producing urea, and an oxy-combustion unit (3)                in which carbon dioxide is produced to be sent to                the urea unit for feeding the urea and the                oxy-combustion unit is a flameless which comprises                flameless combustor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of urea                production plant.
   Urea production plant (1)
   Urea unit (2)
   Oxy-Combustion unit (3)
   Fuel supply line (4)
   Oxygen supply line (5)
   Pre-treatment unit (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The oxygen generation                  unit is defined by an air separation unit in                  particular a cryogenic air separation unit of the                  ammonia unit. The plant further comprises an                  exhaust recirculation line which connects the                  energy recovery unit to the oxy-combustion unit                  and/or energy recovery unit. The carbon dioxide                  recovery section comprises an exhaust treating                  unit, condensation unit, purification and                  compression unit, connected to the energy                  recovery unit and to each other, in series, by                  respective portions of an exhaust line.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The oxy-combustion process                  is a flameless and pressurized process and the                  oxy-combustion process is fed with oxygen                  produced in oxygen generation step in an air                  separation step. At least portion of the ammonia                  for urea synthesis reaction is produced by direct                  reaction of hydrogen and nitrogen produced in air                  separation step together with oxygen. The thermal                  energy recovered from exhausts produced in the                  oxy-combustion process is used for producing                  steam and/or electric energy. The process                  involves separating carbon dioxide stream from at                  least a portion of exhausts containing carbon                  dioxide and produced in oxy-combustion process.                  The step of recovering carbon dioxide involves                  treating the exhaust s for removing pollutants                  from exhausts, condensing exhausts by removing                  condensate water, obtaining carbon dioxide                  stream, purifying and compressing the stream. The                  excess of oxygen maintained in the carbon dioxide                  stream fed to the urea synthesis reaction, acts                  as a passivation agent. The oxy-combustion                  process is carried out at preferably 800-1500                  degrees C and pressure of 0-40 bar g. The                  combustor operates at preferably 800-1500 degrees                  C and pressure of 0-40 bar g. The oxy-combustion                  process is fed with an oxygen stream containing                  preferably at least 90 vol.% oxygen.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.)

TI Integrated trapping device for carbon dioxide and            nitrogen in flue gas, has storage tank which receives            carbon dioxide separated from hydrate decomposer and            separator and dryer which is set between decomposer and            storage tank.
AU ZHOU S
   BIAN H
   YAN C
   LI C
   RAO Y
   WANG S
   ZHAO S
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
   UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 2018575882
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a first membrane separator (12)                which is arranged in a hydrate decomposer (8). The                flue gas introduced by a fan (1) is separated into                the first separator by a heat exchanger (3) and the                hydrate formation liquid in a hydrate generator                generates carbon dioxide hydrate. An outer wall of                the hydrate generator is wrapped with a layer of                heat accumulating material to provide heat. A hot                water circulation wall (11) is provided on a                peripheral wall and a bottom surface of the hydrate                decomposer. The first separator and second                separator (16) separates the nitrogen and are set                on a pipeline that extends to a nitrogen storage                tank (17) of the first separator. A carbon dioxide                storage tank (18) receives the carbon dioxide                separated from the hydrate decomposer and the                second separator and a dryer (19) is set between                the hydrate decomposer and carbon dioxide storage                tank.
   USE - Integrated trapping device for carbon dioxide                and nitrogen in flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas-liquid contact area and hydrate                formation rate are increased. The use of heat                storage materials can achieve hydrate formation                heat for hydrate decomposition, to improve the rate                of hydrate decomposition and to achieve the use of                resources.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                integrated trapping device.
   Fan (1)
   Heat exchanger (3)
   Hot water circulation wall (11)
   Membrane separators (12,16)
   Dryers (15,19)
   Nitrogen storage tank (17)
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (18)

AU LEE J Y

AE KOREA INST IND TECHNOLOGY (KTEC-C)
GA 201825030R
AB    NOVELTY - A composition comprises a pair of ions formed of cresol red anion and tetraalkylammonium cation.
   USE - Composition is used for forming carbon dioxide-sensing indicator film for food packaging material (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition enables detection of carbon dioxide even in absence of water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) carbon dioxide-sensing indicator film, which is formed using the composition; and
   (2) formation of the indicator film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The ion pair is obtained by carrying out ion-exchange process of alkali metal salt of cresol red and tetraalkylammonium halide. Preferred Composition: The alkyl of the tetraalkylammonium cation is 4C or more, preferably 6C or more linear, branched or cyclic alkyl group. The tetraalkylammonium cation is a tetraoctylammonium ion. The amine compound is polyethylenimine, tetraethylenepentamine, triethylenetetramine, pentaethylenehexamine and/or diethylenetriamine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The composition further contains an amine compound and a hydrophobic polymer resin. The hydrophobic polymer resin is chosen from polystyrene, high-density polyethylene, medium-density polyethylene, low-density polyethylene, linear low-density polyethylene, polypropylene homopolymer, ethylene-propylene block copolymer and acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene terpolymer. The composition contains 0.1-10 pts. wt. ion pair and 0.01-5 pts. wt. amine compound. Preferred Property: The indicator film has thickness of 1-100 mu m.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: A polycyclic aromatic                  hydrocarbon-containing raw material is contacted                  with the modified low-eutectic solvent as an                  extraction agent, extracted and separated, to                  obtain a polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon-removed                  product.Preferred Composition: The dissolved amount                  of carbon dioxide gas is 1-25 wt.%, preferably                  2-16 wt.% with respect to low-eutectic solvent. The                  tetraalkylammonium halide is tetramethylammonium                  halide, tetraethylammonium halide,                  tetrapropylammonium halide and/or                  tetrabutylammonium halide. The mass ratio of                  modified low-eutectic solvent and polycyclic                  aromatic hydrocarbon-containing raw material is                  1:0.1-1:50, preferably 1:0.5-1:5. The polycyclic                  aromatic hydrocarbon-containing raw material                  contains 20-80 wt.% polycyclic aromatic                  hydrocarbon.

GA 2021263539
AB    NOVELTY - Adhesive tape, preferably angle tape with a                carrier, and with one-sided polymeric adhesive                coating, where the adhesive coating is produced                using polymers derived from carbon dioxide-based                monomers.
   USE - The adhesive tape is useful for sheathing                cables in automobiles (claimed).

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                cable harness comprising electrical cables running                in the longitudinal direction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide is                  separated from combustion gases based on                  petrochemical combustion. The adhesive coating is                  made on an acrylate basis. The adhesive coating                  is made up of acrylic acid esters produced                  through the polymerization of carbon dioxide. The                  adhesive coating is applied to one or both sides                  of the carrier. The carrier is                  single-layer or multi-layer. The thickness of the                  carrier is less than 0.8 mm. The adhesive coating                  has an application weight of 20-200 g/m2.

PT P
PN WO2020051663-A1; BR102018068334-A2; CA3112239-A1; CN113316629-A; US2021340447-A1; JP2022500545-W; BR102018068334-B1; RU2776173-C1; US11479728-B2
TI Preparing liquid hydrocarbons by fischer-Tropsch            process integrated with refining unit involves reusing            of gas stream of hydrogen generation process by reform            feed load in Fisher process Tropsch, where carbon            dioxide comprises hydrogen.
AU PONTES BITTENCOURT R C
   PONTES B R C
   BITTENCOURT R C P
AE PETROBRAS PETROLEO BRASIL SA (PETB-C)
   PETROBRAS PETROLEO BRASIL SA (PETB-C)
   PETROBRAS PETROLEO BRASIL SA (PETB-C)
GA 202021880G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing liquid hydrocarbons by                fischer-Tropsch process integrated with refining                unit involves reusing of the gas stream of the                hydrogen generation process by the steam reform as                a feed load in the Fisher process Tropsch, where                the carbon dioxide comprises 20 wt.% and the                hydrogen content is 50 wt.% v/v.
   USE - Method for preparing liquid hydrocarbons by                fischer-Tropsch process integrated with refining                unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The gas stream of the                  hydrogen generation process by steam reform comes                  from the PSA section. The hydrogen and                  carbonmonoxide ratio is about 1.2-5.5 mole/mole.                  The Fisher Tropsch process includes compression                  of the feed load from the hydrogen generation                  process by steam reforming, with a pressure of                  less than about 0.5-40 kgf / cm2 (0.39-92                  megaPascal), feeding compressed charge to a                  Fischer-Tropsch reactor and contacting a                  catalyst, and separating the liquid products                  obtained in an oil stream comprises liquid                  hydrocarbons, and an aqueous stream. The                  uncompressed purge gas stream is directed to                  compose the reformer's fuel. The temperature of                  the Fischer-Tropsch reaction is 180-300 degrees C                  and pressures between about 4-30 kgf / cm2 (0.39                  and 2.94 MPa). Preferred Components: The gaseous                  stream of the hydrogen generation process by                  steam reforming is a purge gas stream comprises                  25-35 wt.% hydrogen, 35-55 wt.% dioxide carbon,                  10-30 wt.% methane and 8-15 wt.% carbon monoxide.                  The catalyst used in the Fischer-Tropsch reaction                  is selected from cobalt compounds supported on                  alumina, titania, aluminates, silica, zirconia or                  mixtures. The unconverted residual gas fraction                  containing light hydrocarbons resumes the process                  of hydrogen generation with the reformer's fuel                  or is directed to a section of recovery of light                  olefins. The catalyst additionally contains noble                  metals selected from platinum, rhenium or                  ruthenium as promoters. The catalyst used in the                  Fischer-Tropsch reaction is selected from iron                  compounds containing, optionally, promoters of                  silica, copper, noble metals and metals alkalis                  selected from potassium oxide, copper oxides,                  silica zinc oxide. The Fischer-Tropsch reactor is                  selected from reactors of the type of mud bed,                  fluidized, fixed bed or moving bed, preferably                  compact milli or micro-channel type reactors. The                  liquid hydrocarbons are gasoline, diesel,                  lubricants. The oil stream containing liquid                  hydrocarbons obtained in the Fischer-Tropsch                  process is recycled to the existing refinery                  sections. The aqueous stream generated in the                  Fischer-Tropsch process is recycled to the acid                  water unit in the refinery.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC H04-E; H04-E05

TI Preparing dialkyl carbonate involves mixing            methanol, carbon dioxide and catalyst to form mixing            solution, adding organic acid to mixing solution to            carry out synthesis reaction of dialkyl carbonate,            where organic acid comprises formic acid.
AU CHEN Y
   LO M
   HSU H
   LU M
   XU X
   CHEN Y C
   LO M Y
   HSU H Y
AE IND TECHNOLOGY RES INST (ITRI-C)
   IND TECHNOLOGY RES INST (ITRI-C)
   IND TECHNOLOGY RES INST (ITRI-C)
   IND TECHNOLOGY RES INST (ITRI-C)
GA 201960303S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dialkyl carbonate involves mixing                methanol, carbon dioxide and catalyst to form                mixing solution, adding organic acid to mixing                solution to carry out synthesis reaction of dialkyl                carbonate, where organic acid comprises formic                acid, acetic acid, propionic acid, butyric                acid.
   USE - Method for preparing dialkyl carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare dialkyl                carbonate that has high catalytic activity and                efficiency, and improves yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises                  cerium oxide (CeO2), zirconium oxide (ZrO2),                  titanium oxide (TiO2), lanthanum oxide (La2O3).                  The dehydrating agent comprises 2-cyanopyridine,                  alkali metal silicoaluminate, benzonitrile,                  2,2-dimethoxyl propane. Preferred Composition:                  The amount of catalyst is 0.5-25 pts. wt. based                  on 100 pts. wt. of the methanol. The amount of                  organic acid is 0.01-2 pts. wt. based on 100 pts.                  wt. of the methanol. The amount of dehydrating                  agent is 0.1-5 moles relative to one mole of the                  methanol. Preferred Process: The method further                  involves adding a dehydrating agent to the mixing                  solution. The continuous reactor comprises a                  fixed-bed reactor, a moving-bed reactor or a                  continuous stirred-tank reactor. Preferred                  Condition: The synthesis reaction temperature is                  80-150 degrees C. The synthesis reaction pressure                  is 0.5-10 megapascal. The reaction time is 4-72                  hours. The synthesis reaction is carried out in                  batch reactor. The synthesis reaction is carried                  out in a continuous reactor.

Preferred Method: The method comprises performing static extraction for 15 minutes, and then adjusting the carbon dioxide flow rate to 8 l/hour, dynamic extracting for 3 hours, passing the sterol sulfate based lipid extract into separation kettle, carbon dioxide fluid extracting at 50-70 degrees C. The powder sample preparation comprises grinding dried sea cucumber sample, and taking the filtered substance after screening to obtain sea cucumber powder. The dried sea cucumber samples include Icelandic sea cucumber, imitation sea cucumber, American red ginseng, American ginseng, and Haitian melon mixture.
   ACTIVITY - Metabolic.

PN CN108947675-A
TI Biogas fertilizer accelerant comprises mixture of nitric acid-oxidized lignite obtained by oxidation of lignite by nitric acid.
AU XU X
   GUO R
   LUO S
   YANG Z
   SHI X
   GUO G
AE QINGDAO INST BIOENERGY & BIOPROCESS TECH (CAQD-C)
GA 2018A0533V
AB    NOVELTY - Biogas fertilizer accelerant comprises mixture of nitric acid-oxidized lignite obtained by oxidation of lignite by nitric acid.
   USE - Used as biogas fertilizer accelerant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes movement of carbonic acid (carbon dioxide)-bicarbonate-carbonate balance and volatilization of carbon dioxide; reduces conductivity of the biogas slurry; and improves nutrient release effect of the fertilizer and environment friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for improving effect of biogas fertilizer using accelerant, comprising mixing accelerant with biogas slurry, and promoting the effect of the biogas slurry fertilizer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The lignite is oxidized by 10-15% nitric acid for 0.5-1.5 hours at 60-95 degrees C, where the ratio of coal to acid is 3:1-6:1. Preferred Method: The method comprises adding accelerant to the biogas slurry and uniformly mixing, reducing the conductivity of the biogas slurry, improving the sustained release effect of nutrients e.g. ammonium, potassium, calcium and magnesium, reducing ammonium nitrogen volatilization and the pH of the biogas slurry, and improving the effect of the biogas slurry as a fertilizer. The amount of the accelerant added is determined according to the alkalinity of the biogas slurry; the theoretical calculation formula is: Q is kasteriskVasteriskm/100 seconds, where Q-nitrogen oxide brown coal mixture mass (kg), V is the volume of the biogas slurry mixture (m3), M is the alkalinity of the biogas slurry (g/m3, alkalinity with calcium carbonate), S is the mass concentration of lignite hydrogen ion after nitric acid oxidation (g/kg), and k is pH correction factor. The K is 0.3-0.8. The process comprises thoroughly mixing and using for more than 30 minutes after the accelerant is added to the biogas slurry.

AB    NOVELTY - Synthezing (P1) tungsten trioxide-tin(IV)                oxide nanoheterostructures, comprises e.g.:                providing (102) a compound containing tungsten;                providing (104) a compound containing tin;                dissolving (106) the compound containing tungsten                and the compound containing tin in a first solvent                to obtain a first mixture of its; stirring (108)                the first mixture at a stirring speed and at a                first temperature for a first time period to obtain                a stirred mixture; adjusting (110) the pH of the                stirred mixture to obtain a solution containing a                precipitate; stirring the solution containing the                precipitate for a second time period and at a                second temperature to obtain a stirred solution                containing the precipitate; diluting and washing                the stirred solution containing precipitate by                adding a second solvent to obtain a diluted                solution; ultrasonicating the diluted solution to                obtain an ultrasonicated solution; and centrifuging                the ultrasonicated solution to obtain a solid                product and supernatant.
   USE - The process is useful for synthezing tungsten                trioxide-tin(IV) oxide nanoheterostructures which                is used: to degrade organic dye and photoreduce                carbon dioxide gas (all claimed), and generate                green fuel in the form of methane and organic                dyemineralization; and for application in malachite                green dye degradation and carbon dioxide                photoreduction to value added fuel e.g. methane in                the presence of natural sunlight.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: is convenient, rapid, highly                scalable, transferrable, cost-effective, easily                transferrable and easy to work on; and utilizes                natural sunlight available in abundance and free of                cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthezing (P1) tungsten trioxide-tin(IV)                oxide nanoheterostructures, comprises: providing                (102) a compound containing tungsten; providing                (104) a compound containing tin; dissolving (106)                the compound containing tungsten and the compound                containing tin in a first solvent to obtain a first                mixture of its; stirring (108) the first mixture at                a stirring speed and at a first temperature for a                first time period to obtain a stirred mixture;                adjusting (110) the pH of the stirred mixture to                obtain a solution containing a precipitate;                stirring the solution containing the precipitate                for a second time period and at a second                temperature to obtain a stirred solution containing                the precipitate; diluting and washing the stirred                solution containing precipitate by adding a second                solvent to obtain a diluted solution;                ultrasonicating the diluted solution to obtain an                ultrasonicated solution; centrifuging the                ultrasonicated solution to obtain a solid product                and supernatant; repeating the steps of dilution,                ultrasonication and centrifugation more than once                to obtain a refined solid product; drying the                refined solid product in a hot air environment for                a time period to obtain a dried product; and                calcining the dried product at a calcining                temperature and for a calcining time period to                obtain the tungsten trioxide-tin(IV) oxide                nanoheterostructures. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also                included for:
   (1) degrading (P2) an organic dye employing                the tungsten trioxide-tin(IV) oxide                nanoheterostructures, comprising providing 25-100                mg the tungsten trioxide-tin(IV) oxide                nanoheterostructures in form of powder, providing                25-100 mg organic dye to be degraded in a                transparent container dissolved in double distilled                water, where the organic dye is malachite green,                mixing the tungsten trioxide-tin(IV) oxide                nanoheterostructures to the organic dye to obtain a                mixture of its, ultrasonicating the mixture in                absence of light for a homogenizing time period of                15-45 minutes to obtain an ultrasonicated mixture,                exposing the ultrasonicated mixture to natural                sunlight for a degradable time period of 5-60                minutes and degrading the organic dye in the                presence of the tungsten trioxide-tin(IV) oxide                nanoheterostructures to obtain carbon dioxide and                water as by products; and
   (2) photoreducing carbon dioxide gas employing                the tungsten trioxide-tin(IV) oxide                nanoheterostructures, comprising providing a                transparent quartz vessel having double walls,                mixing 25-100 mg the tungsten trioxide-tin(IV)                oxide nanoheterostructures in 100-200 ml water in                the transparent quartz vessel to obtain a mixture                of its, ultrasonicating the mixture for a time                period of 10-30 minutes, purging the transparent                quartz vessel continuously with nitrogen gas for a                time period of 45-75 minutes, pumping carbon                dioxide gas into the transparent quartz vessel for                a time period of 45-75 minutes till saturation,                where the flow rate of the carbon dioxide gas of                175-225 sccm, reducing the flow rate of carbon                dioxide gas to a flow rate of 10-30 sccm, sealing                the transparent quartz vessel, stabilizing the flow                of the carbon dioxide gas, exposing the transparent                quartz vessel to sunlight for a time period of 1-10                hours to reduce carbon dioxide and generate methane                gas and monitoring the photoreduction of carbon                dioxide gas, and generation of methane gas using a                gas chromatography-mass spectrophotometer (GC-MS)                analyzer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates a flow chart for                synthezing tungsten trioxide-tin(IV) oxide.
   Providing compound containing tungsten                (102)
   Providing compound containing tin                (104)
   Dissolving compound containing tungsten and                compound containing tin in first solvent                (106)

   Adjusting pH of stirred mixture (110)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The stirring speed is                  480-720 rpm, the first temperature is 25-35                  degrees C and the first time period is 1-10                  minutes. The step of adjusting the pH of the                  stirred mixture includes addition of concentrated                  hydrochloric acid to the stirred mixture, the                  concentrated hydrochloric acid is added in a drop                  wise manner, where the flow rate of concentrated                  hydrochloric acid of 1-10 drops/minute, the                  concentration of hydrochloric acid is 1-3 N and                  the pH of the stirred mixture is 1-2. The step of                  stirring the solution containing precipitate is                  carried out at a stirring speed of 8000-12000 rpm                  and the second time period is 1-10 minutes. The                  steps of dilution, ultrasonication and                  centrifugation are repeated 2-10 time. The step                  of drying is carried out in hot air environment                  at a temperature of 60-85 degrees C and the step                  of drying is carried out in hot air environment                  for a time period of 10-5 hours. The calcining                  temperature is 450-550 degrees C, and the                  calcining time period is 45-75 minutes, the                  content of the compound containing tungsten,                  preferably tin is 10-75 wt.% and the amount of                  the solvent is 20-50 ml. Preferred Components:                  The compound containing tungsten is sodium                  tungstate dehydrate, the compound containing tin                  is sodium stannate trihydrate and the first                  solvent and the second solvent are independently                  double distilled water.
   EXAMPLE - 1 g (~3 mmoles) Sodium tungstate dihydrate and                0.8085 g (~3 mmoles) of sodium stannate trihydrate                were dissolved in 30 ml of double distilled water                taken in a glass beaker under magnetic stirring at                600 rpm and 32 degrees C. To the equimolar                solution, 2.6 ml of concentrated hydrochloric acid                was added drop by drop till the pH reached to 1-2                under the magnetic influence until thick white                precipitation was observed. The precipitated                solution was further stirred vigorously under the                same setting for the next 30 minutes. Then, the                resultant white residue was diluted with distilled                water and centrifuged at a speed of 10000 rpm for 5                minutes to obtain the product. A continuous cycle                consisting of centrifuging, ultrasonication, and                washing with distilled water (5-6 times) ensured                the complete removal of organic impurities. The                product was dried in a hot air oven at 75 degrees C                for 12 hours. The oven dried equimolar                tungsten-tin(II) hydroxide mixture powder. The                tungsten-tin(II) hydroxide sample was subjected to                calcination at 500 degrees C for 1 hour. Similarly,                control samples namely, tungsten hydroxide (WO-OD)                and tin(II) hydroxide (SO-OD) were synthesized by                above mentioned methodology with concentrated                hydroxic acid amounts in 1 and 1.6 ml,                respectively. Post overnight oven drying in a hot                air oven, WO-OD and SO-OD samples were calcined to                obtain furnace dried powders and will be referred                to as WO-FD and SO-FD, respectively. The                greenish-yellow color of pristine tungsten trioxide                was not observed in the case of pure tin(IV) oxide                and tungsten trioxide-tin(IV) oxide                heterostructures. Additionally, a series of                tungsten trioxide-tin(IV) oxide                nanoheterostructures were formed by adding 0.2023                (25 mol.%) sodium stannate trihydrate to the                mixture of 1 g of sodium tungstate dihydrate in                water.

GA 201816340K
AB    NOVELTY - Active regeneration and cyclic removal of carbon dioxide from calcium-based absorbent comprises taking fresh calcium-based absorbent and carbon based fuel into calcining decomposing reactor and passing oxygen gas, decomposing calcium carbonate in fresh calcium-based absorbent into calcium oxide and releases carbon dioxide, hydrating calcium oxide produced with the steam in the steam activation reactor, taking e.g. calcium oxide in flue gas carbonation reaction to generate calcium carbonate, passing calcium carbonate into the calcination decomposition reactor, starting next cycle repeatedly.
   USE - The method is useful for active regeneration and cyclic removal of carbon dioxide from calcium-based absorbent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Active regeneration and cyclic removal of carbon dioxide from calcium-based absorbent comprises (a) taking fresh calcium-based absorbent and carbon based fuel into calcining decomposing reactor and passing oxygen gas, calcining decomposition reactor at 800-1000 degrees C, carbon based fuel and oxygen gas combustion reaction is absorbent decomposition heating occurs, decomposing calcium carbonate in the fresh calcium-based absorbent into calcium oxide and releases carbon dioxide, passing calcium carbonate into the steam activation reactor, discharging carbon dioxide from the top of the calcining reactor, in order to achieve the removal and enrichment of carbon dioxide in flue gas, (b) maintaining steam activation reactor temperature at 300-400 degrees C, where the reaction atmosphere is steam and nitrogen gas or a mixed gas of steam and air, where the steam concentration is 10-100%, hydrating calcium oxide produced with the steam in the steam activation reactor, feeding calcium hydroxide into the dehydration reactor, (c) maintaining the dehydration reactor temperature at 500-650 degrees C, maintaining the reaction atmosphere at 500-650 degrees C with high temperature nitrogen gas or air, passing the calcium hydroxide into the dehydration reactor to form calcium oxide, passing calcium oxide in the carbonation reactor, and (d) setting the carbonizing reactor temperature at 650-700 degrees C, taking calcium oxide and carbon dioxide in flue gas carbonation reaction to generate Calcium carbonate, passing calcium carbonate into the calcination decomposition reactor, starting the next cycle repeatedly, removing the carbon dioxide gas discharged from the carbonation reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fresh calcium-based absorbent is limestone, dolomite or calcium-based absorbents with a mass percentage of calcium oxide above 20%. The carbon based fuel is coal, biomass, natural gas, coal gas or synthesis gas.

PN US2021188671-A1
TI Buffer-free process cycle for carbon dioxide            sequestration and carbonate production from brine waste            stream by extracting divalent ions from brine solution            and introducing carbon dioxide for precipitation of            divalent ions as carbonate salt.
AU SIMONETTI D
   LA PLANTE E C
   WANG B
   WEI Z
   SANT G
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
GA 202170143J
AB    NOVELTY - Buffer-free process cycle for carbon dioxide                sequestration and carbonate production from brine                waste stream with high salinity, involves using a                chelating agent, extracting divalent ions from a                brine solution as complexes of the chelating agent                and the divalent ions, using a weak acid,                regenerating the chelating agent and producing a                divalent ion salt solution, and introducing carbon                dioxide to the divalent ion salt solution to induce                precipitation of the divalent ions as a carbonate                salt.
   USE - Buffer-free process cycle for carbon dioxide                sequestration and carbonate production from brine                waste stream with high salinity.
   ADVANTAGE - The process reduces energy consumption,                because it does not involve energy-intensive stages                such as electrolysis or temperature swing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves                  extracting the divalent ions by introducing the                  chelating agent to the brine solution, and                  subjecting the brine solution to filtration by                  ultrafiltration, nanofiltration, or reverse                  osmosis for producing a retentate solution                  comprising the complexes of the chelating agent                  and the divalent ions. The step of regenerating                  the chelating agent involves introducing the weak                  acid to the retentate solution to induce                  precipitation of the chelating agent and to                  produce the divalent ion salt solution. The step                  of regenerating the chelating agent involves                  adjusting the pH of the retentate solution to                  less than or equal to4. The method further                  involves adjusting the pH of the divalent ion                  salt solution to more than 3.6, prior to                  introducing the carbon dioxide. The step of                  introducing the carbon dioxide involves inducing                  precipitation of calcium carbonate and/or                  magnesium carbonate.Preferred Conditions: The weak acid has a                  acid dissociation constant (pKa) of greater than                  3.6 at 298 K.

TI Preparing cyclic carbonate by conversion of carbon dioxide using vanadium-based catalyst comprises e.g. reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide, conjugated vanadyl porphyrin microporous polymer material with tetra-n-butylammonium bromide.
AU ZHAO J
   ZHANG X
   LV M
   CAO Y
   XU Z
AE HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
GA 201830087E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic carbonate by conversion of carbon dioxide using vanadium-based catalyst, comprises taking carbon dioxide and epoxide as raw materials, conjugated vanadyl porphyrin microporous polymer material as heterogeneous catalysts, tetra-n-butylammonium bromide as cocatalyst, and then reacting at room temperature and normal pressure without solvent for 12-48 hours to final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic carbonate by conversion of carbon dioxide using vanadium-based catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process, fast reaction speed, mild reaction conditions and low production cost; and is safe, and is suitable for industrial production; and the cyclic carbonate has high catalytic activity and stable structure performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The conjugated vanadyl porphyrin microporous polymer material is prepared by performing Scholl coupling reaction of vanadium tetraphenylporphyrin as a monomer in presence anhydrous aluminum trichloride catalyst, where the conjugated vanadyl porphyrin microporous polymer material is (5Z,10Z,14Z,19Z)-5,10,15,20-tetra-p-tolyl-porphyrin vanadium complex of formula (I). The epoxy compound is propylene oxide, butylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, epibromohydrin, styrene oxide, glycidyl phenyl ether or cyclohexene oxide. The addition amount of active component of heterogeneous catalyst is 0.2-0.5 mol.% mass of epoxy compound. The addition amount of co-catalyst is 3.6-7.2 mol.% mass of epoxy compound. The conjugated vanadyl porphyrin microporous polymer material can be replaced with a conjugated vanadyl porphyrin dibenzyl-amine microporous polymer material, and the conjugated vanadyl porphyrin dibenzyl-amine microporous polymer material prepared by carrying out Scholl coupling reaction of vanadium tetraphenylporphyrin and benzylamine as monomers in presence of anhydrous aluminum trichloride catalyst, where conjugated vanadyl porphyrin dibenzyl-amine microporous polymer material is (5Z,10Z,14Z,19Z)-5,10,15,20-tetra-p-tolyl-porphyrin vanadium complex of formula (II).n = not-defined.

PN CN108220213-A
TI Constructing attenuated strain of Riemerella anatipestifer CH3 lacking ompA gene by freezing strain, resuscitating trypticase soy agar, preparing genome of CH3 strain, extracting recombinant plasmids and transforming into suicide plasmid.
AU WEI Y
   LI H
   CUI J
   SU J
   WU Y
AE ANHUI ANLONG GENE MEDICAL LAB CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201853339X
AB    NOVELTY - Method for constructing an attenuated strain of Riemerella anatipestifer CH3 lacking outer membrane protein A (ompA) gene, involves freezing RACH3 strain at -80 degrees C in a refrigerator, culturing in 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 16 hours, using R.anatipestifer genome as template and ompA-L F/ompA-L R and ompA-R F/ompA-R and screening mutant strain, expressing spectinomycin resistance gene in G-bacteria and RA sensitive to spectinomycin (Spc) resistance, extracting recombinant plasmids pT-ompA-L, pT-ompA-R and pT-Spc and placing in water bath, centrifuging for 10 minutes, obtaining supernatant, air-drying and digesting using SphI and BamHI, transforming suicide plasmid pDS-ompALSR in Escherichia coli 19851, amplifying spectinomycin resistance gene Spec by PCR, 16 S rRNA gene and ompA gene open reading frame (ORF) of R.anatipestifer and using ECL to develop color after 15 minutes.
   USE - The method is useful for constructing an attenuated strain of R.anatipestifer CH3 lacking ompA gene.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for constructing an attenuated strain of Riemerella anatipestifer CH3 lacking the outer membrane protein A (ompA) gene involves (a) freezing RACH3 strain at -80 degrees C in refrigerator, resuscitating in trypticase soy agar (TSA) plate, culturing in 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 16 hours, transferring the selected strain in 4 ml tryptic soy broth (TSB) liquid medium, standing at 37 degrees C for 1-4 hours in carbon dioxide incubator, culturing cells in shaking incubator at 37 degrees C for 10 hours, collecting cells by centrifugation and preparing genome of CH3 strain in accordance with procedures of instruction provided in genomic kit, (b1) using R.anatipestifer genome as template with outer membrane protein (omp)A-L F/ompA-L R and ompA-R F/ompA-R, ligating target fragments into pGEM-Teasy vector and transforming into Escherichia coli DH5 alpha competent cells, identifying by restriction enzyme digestion is pT-ompA-L, pT-ompA-R and sequenced and (b2) screening mutant strain, expressing spectinomycin resistance gene in G-bacteria and RA sensitive to spectinomycin (Spc) resistance, using pFW5 plasmid as template, amplifying with the primer spec F /R, and the PCR product, subjecting to agarose gel electrophoresis, ligating gel-backed fragment to pGEM-T-easy vector and transforming into E.coli DH5 alpha competent cells and identifying the positive plasmid by restriction enzyme digestion and sequences pT-Spc, (c) extracting recombinant plasmids pT-ompA-L, pT-ompA-R and pT-Spc, digesting pT-ompA-L recombinant plasmid with BamHI and MscI, pT-ompA-R recombinant plasmid with SphI and Xanthomonas holcicola (XhoI) and pT-Spc recombinant plasmid with MscI and XhoI, performing in water bath at 37 degrees C for 2.5 hours, separating by agarose gel electrophoresis, excising target fragment, purifying using gel recovery kit, purifying water double-digested products of pT-ompA-L, pT-ompA-R and pT-Spc, detecting by agarose gel electrophoresis and performing bands of about 882 bp for ompA-L, 795 bp for ompA-R and 1119 bp for spc and connecting the rubber recovers product overnight at 4 degrees C, using above ligated product as template, ompA-RR and ompA-L F as primers, performing PCR amplification, identifying target fragment by agarose gel electrophoresis DNA fragment of 2800 bp in size, ligating target fragment to pGEM-Teasy vector, transforming E.coli DH5 alpha competent cells and identifying as positive by restriction enzyme digestion and sequencing pTompA-LSR, (d) preparing pT-ompA- lipolysis-stimulated lipoprotein receptor (LSR) plasmid using small kit, pDS132 plasmid by small-lifting medium kit, digesting pDS132 plasmid with SphI and BamHI enzymes at 37 degrees C for 2.5 hours, digesting at 37 degrees C for 2.5 hours using 4 ul sodium cyanide, 100ul absolute ethanol and cooling at -80 degrees C overnight in refrigerator, centrifuging next day at 12000 revolutions per minute for 10 minutes, removing supernatant, resuspending in 75% ethanol and centrifuging at 12000 revolutions per minute for 10 minutes, repeating supernatant twice, air-drying and digesting with SphI and BamHI at 37 degrees C for 2.5 hours, recovering 1 mu l BamHI, 1 mu l SphI, 5 ul cutsmart buffer, 43 mu l purified water and recovering pDS132 double-cut product 6000bp band, digesting 2800 bp band of pT-ompA-LSR, connecting two gum recovery products overnight at 4 degrees C, screening of recombinant plasmids on LB plates of ampicillin, picking single colonies to LB liquid, culturing at 37 degrees C for 6 hours while shaking, (e) transforming suicide plasmid pDS-ompALSR in E.coli19851, selecting recombinant plasmid by LB plate containing 100 mu g/ml ampicillin, culturing single colony in LB liquid medium containing same resistance, culturing at 37 degrees C for 6 hours and identifying positive recombinant plasmid by restriction enzyme digestion, (f) combining the BW19851 E.coli containing the suicide plasmid in step (e) with R.anatipestifer CH3 using parental membrane filter and transduction method, introducing homology arm into the genome R.anatipestifer CH3 and screening spectinomycin resistance gene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The TSB liquid medium in step (vi) comprises 80 mu g/ml spectinomycin 80 and 50 mu g/ml kanamycin. The phosphate buffered saline Tween20 (RTM: Polysorbate 20) with PBST contains 0.05% Tween20 (RTM: Polysorbate 20) in PBS. The R is primer PCR to amplify the left and right side fragments of ompA gene as homologous recombined homology arm. The digestive reaction system comprises (i) 1 ul BamHI, 1 ul MscI, 5 ul cutsmart buffer, 15 ul pT-ompA-L double digested plasmid and 28 ul purified water and make up to 50 ul, (ii) 1 ul XhoI, 1 ul MscI, 5 ul cutsmart buffer, 15 ul pT-Spc and 28 ul purified water and make up to 50 ul and (iii) 1 ul XhoI, 1 ul SphI, 5 ul cutsmart buffer, 15 ul pT-ompA-R and 28 ul and the 15 ul reaction system comprises 1.5 ul 10xbuffer, 4 ul ompA-L, 4.5 ul Spc, 4 ul ompA-R, 1 ul T4 DNA ligase. The reaction system in step (d) comprises 1 ul BamHI, 1 ul Sph, 5 ul cut smart buffer, 15 ul pDs132 and 28 ul purified water and make up to 50 ul for pTompA-LSR with single digestion. The partial enzyme digestion system in step (d) comprises 1 ul ScaI, 5 ul cutsmart buffer, 15 mu l pT-ompA-LSR and 29 mu l purified water. The 10 mu l reaction system in step (d) comprises 1 mu l 10xT4 DNA ligase buffer, 4 mu l ompA-LSR DNA fragment, 4 mu l pDS132 vector, 1 mu l T4 DNA ligase into 100 mu g/ml E.coli 19851 competent cells. The amount of BW19851-pDS132- TonB1-LSR in step (f) is 2x 108 colony-formed unit (CFU) and R.anatipestifer CH3 is 4x 108 CFU. The clone with the genotype 16 S rRNA+Spec+OmpA- in step (f) is deletion strain of the OmpA-dependent outer membrane protein receptor ompA gene. Preferred Biomolecule: The R.anatipestifer CH3 conserved sequence primer comprises forward primer and reverse primer, where the forward primer comprising base pair sequence: cagcttaactgtagaactgc (SEQ ID NO: Not defined) and the reverse primer comprising base pair sequence: tcgagatttgcatcacttcg (SEQ ID NO: Not defined) and the specific 16SrRNA of R.anatipestifer CH3 is amplified to determine whether the candidate mutant strain is R.anatipestifer CH3. The primers with spec resistance gene comprises spec forward primer and spec reverse primer, where the spec forward primer comprising base pair sequence: gttggccacgttcgtgaatacatgttat (SEQ ID NO: Not defined) and the spec reverse primer comprising base pair sequence: cactcgaggcgcttaccaattagaatg (SEQ ID NO: Not defined) and 1119 bp fragment of the resistance gene is amplified to determine whether the gene exchange occurred. The primers with in OmpA comprises ORF forward primer and ORF reverse primer, where the ORF forward primer has a base pair sequence: actcaaggaagagcggatca (SEQ ID NO: Not defined) and ORF reverse primer has a base pair sequence: gcttcagcagaaccaactcc (SEQ ID NO: Not defined) and the gene interest is amplified to detect whether the ompA has been knocked out. Preferred Method: The method involves primer for amplifying the spectinomycin resistance gene Spec by PCR is Spec F/R, and the primer for 16 S rRNA gene of R.anatipestifer is amplified by PCR as 16 S rRNA F/R, and the ompA gene is amplified by PCR. The primer for the ORF is in OmpA ORF F/R. The dilution ratio of horseradish peroxidase-labeled goat anti-mouse secondary antibody is 1:8000. In step (f), the spectinomycin resistance gene is screened by (i) taking fresh E.coli BW19851-pDS132-OmpA- lipolysis-stimulated lipoprotein receptor (LSR) and R.anatipestifer CH3, streaking on Luria broth (LB) solid medium containing 100 mu g/ml Amp and TSA solid medium and culturing at 37 degrees C until the middle of logarithmic growth, (ii) washing the two bacteria from the plate with 10 mmol/l magnesium sulfate (5 ml), collecting 1 ml cells into 1.5 ml centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 4 degrees C and 5000 revolutions per minute for 5 minutes, washing twice with 10 mmol/l magnesium sulfate (1 ml) and resuspending 10 mmol/l magnesium sulfate (1 ml), (iii) mixing donor bacteria BW19851-pDS132-ompA-LSR and the recipient bacteria CH3 in ratio of 1:2, filtering on sterile with 0.22 mu m microporous mixed fiber filter, (iv) using sterile forceps to clamp the filter on TSA solid medium and incubating at 30 degrees C for 8 hours in 5% carbon dioxide incubator, (v) removing filter, eluting the bacteria on filter with 10 mmol/l magnesium sulfate solution (5 ml), taking 50-100 mu l coated with spectinomycin, 80 mu g/ml solid culture of TSA and 50 mu g/ml kanamycin, culturing at 37 degrees C for 24 hours in carbon dioxide incubator and (vi) picking single colony to TSB liquid medium containing spectinomycin and kanamycin, (vii) screening and identifying gene deletion strain CH3-ompA R.anatipestifer CH3 strain by picking bacterial clones grown on the Spc and Kan resistant selection plates as candidate mutants for PCR amplification and Western blot analysis, picking possible recombinant clonal proliferation, amplifying spectinomycin resistance gene Spec by PCR, 16 S rRNA gene and ompA gene ORF of R.anatipestifer, performing Western blot analysis, CH3 wild strain, transferring to TSB medium, CH3-ompA strain, transferring to TSB medium with 50 mu g/ml kanamycin and 80 mu g/ml spectinomycin, transferring CH3-ompA strain to 50 mu g/ml kanamycin and TSB medium containing 80 mu g/ml and 1 mu g/ml cefoxitin, culturing at 37 degrees C, centrifuging at 12000 revolutions per minute for 5 minutes, discarding supernatant, washing precipitate for 3 times with phosphate-buffered saline, mixing with equal amount of 2xbuffer, boiling for 10 minutes, subjecting to SDS-PAGE electrophoresis with protein of 5 mu g, taking gel after electrophoresis, rinsing cellulose membrane for 3 times in PBST for 3-5 minutes each time, where the membrane was blocked in PBST containing 10% milk at 4 degrees C, rinsing 3 times with PBST for 15 minutes, diluting 1:200 of type 1 WJ4 OmpA positive serum for 1 hour, rinsing 3 times with PBST for 15 minutes each time, adding horseradish peroxidase labeled goat anti-mouse secondary antibody for dilution diluting for 1 hour, rinsing 3 times with PBST and using ECL to develop color after 15 minutes.
