PT P
PN DE102017000576-A1
TI Producing biomethane from carbon dioxide and water comprises feeding water to photobioreactor and carbon dioxide-containing external gas stream, discharging oxygen formed, separating biomass, crushing, fermenting and recovering methane.
AU REULE W
AE REULE W (REUL-Individual)
GA 2018581252
AB    NOVELTY - Producing biomethane from carbon dioxide and water comprises feeding water to a photobioreactor substantially carbon dioxide-containing external gas stream and a carbon dioxide in a molar ratio of 2, discharging the oxygen formed in the oxygene photosynthesis involving oxygen to carbon dioxide in a molar ratio of 2 as a product gas, separating water and nutrients from separated biomass from the culture solution in the photobioreactor, crushing, unlocking, and fermenting in an at least one-stage anaerobic fermentation into methane and carbon dioxide, separating the gas mixture produced in the anaerobic fermentation into methane and carbon dioxide-containing stream, recovering methane in a molar ratio with carbon dioxide of 1 as a product, supplying the carbon dioxide-containing stream to the photobioreactor along with the external gas stream, where the amount of methane, which can be extracted is independent of the elemental composition of the photosynthetically formed biomass.
   USE - The biomethane is useful as energy carrier or chemical raw material in natural gas network.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces biomethane using external biomass and carbon dioxide, which is obtained in many industrial processes as regenerative (or also fossile) carbon dioxide; is economical; recycles energy sources including biogas or biomethane; and reduces the particle size of biomass to a size of less than 0.1 mm for comminution tasks.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing biomethane from industrial and agricultural biomass and biogenic residues (feed biomass) and integrated produced phototrophic biomass from algae and bacteria (algal biomass) by anaerobic fermentation (fermentation), comprising taking carbon dioxide produced during fermentation as a carbon source, the nutrient-containing aqueous phase from fermentation as a source of mineral nutrients for photosynthesis of algae or cyanobacteria, subjecting the algae biomass resulting from the total amount of carbon dioxide released and absorbed to fermentation along with the external biomass, so that methane is additionally formed from the carbon assimilated from the carbon dioxide into the algal biomass and the hydrogen, which is integrated with water, completely converting carbon dioxide generated from fermentation of feed biomass and algae biomass into methane, where molecular oxygen is produced from the oxygen taken up in the form of water in the photosynthesis process;
   (2) quantitatively determining the amount of methane and oxygen recoverable in the above method, comprising determining the elemental composition of the dry biomass, the number of carbon, hydrogen, oxygen and nitrogen atoms of the feed biomass and generated algal biomass (carbon, hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen) in the chemical formula of the feed biomass (CcHhOoNn) or the algal biomass (CCHHOONN) formulated and thus formulated stoichiometric quantity and element balance, calculating the amount of feed biomass to be fermented in the fermentation to produce as much carbon dioxide capable of producing algal biomass, finally extracting from the feed biomass and algal biomass recoverable amounts of methane (YCH4) and Oxygen (YO2) using the formulae: (YCH4 is equal to (c/2+(h-3n)/8-o/4)+1/X(c/2+(h-3n)/8-o/4))x mol CH4/ mol feed-biomass) (I), (X) is equal to (4C+H-2O-3N)/4c-h+2o+3n)x (mol feed biomass-biomass)/(mol algal biomass-biomass)) (II), and (YO2 is equal to 1/X(C+(H-3N)/4-O/2)x mol oxygen/mol feed biomass-biomass) (III); and
   (3) a processing plant for carrying out the above method, comprising a comminution stage for the mechanical pretreatment of the feed biomass, a unit with one to two stages for cell disruption for feed biomass and algae biomass, a stage for carrying out a hydrolysis of the digested biomass, optionally with a separation unit for obtaining hydrolysis products as valuable substances, optionally two-stage fermentation unit of acidification reactor and biogas methane reactor with one stage for solid-liquid separation. a separation stage set for the separation of minerals. a separation stage set for separating the carbon dioxide from the biogas. A photobioreactor set for cultivating photoautotrophic microorganisms (algae) with a device for fumigation with carbon dioxide. A stage is set for separating the algal biomass and a pipeline for returning the algal suspension to the cell digestion, the connection pipelines, delivery units, the valves, measuring and control devices, storage tanks for reaction and auxiliary materials. The connections and aggregates are set for the provision of energy (electricity, steam, hot water). A safety equipment is set for explosion protection. A laboratory equipment is set for off-line analysis and measurement. A process automation equipment is set for data acquisition, processing, communication and documentation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method comprises adding carbon dioxide from external gases to photosynthesis in addition to biomass or biogenic residues (feed biomass) and biomass from algae and bacteria (algae biomass), and adding additional photosynthetic biomass after anaerobic fermentation of the additional biomass for additional methane extraction. The method further comprises extraction of biomethane as the main product in addition with other products from the external biomass and/or internally produced photosynthetic biomass, where further products including proteins, peptides, amino acids, lipids, carbohydrates, vitamins or enzymes and mineral nutrients or fertilizers are produced.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN WO2022186319-A1; JP2022135912-A; JP7168751-B2
TI Reducing agent useful for producing carbon            valuables by reducing carbon dioxide, and gas            production, comprises granular carrier and oxygen            carrier carried on granular carrier and having oxygen            ion conductivity.
AU RISA S
   KEISUKE I
   KOJI T
   SOUICHIROU S
   SAKURAI R
   IIJIMA K
   TAKIZAWA K
   SUZUKI S
AE SEKISUI CHEM CO LTD (SEKI-C)
GA 2022B59422
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing agent comprises a granular carrier                having pores and a repose angle of 45&#176; or                more and an oxygen carrier carried on the carrier                and having oxygen ion conductivity.
   USE - The reducing agent is useful for producing                carbon valuables by reducing carbon dioxide, and                gas production.
   ADVANTAGE - The reducing agent is highly efficient in                converting carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) (1) a method for producing a gas,                involving contacting a reducing agent with a raw                material gas containing carbon dioxide to reduce                the carbon dioxide and producing a gas product                containing carbon monoxide; and
   (2) (2) a method for increasing conversion                efficiency, involving using a reducing agent                comprising an oxygen carrier having oxygen ion                conductivity supported on a granular carrier having                pores and a repose angle of 45&#176; or more to                increase the conversion efficiency of carbon                dioxide to a carbon valuable material by the                reducing agent as compared with a case in which the                oxygen carrier is used alone.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carrier comprises                  at least one element belonging to groups 2-4, 12                  and 13 of the periodic table. The carrier                  comprises magnesium, cerium, titanium, zirconium,                  aluminum or silicon. The oxygen carrier comprises                  at least two elements belonging to groups 2-4,                  11-13 of the periodic table. The oxygen carrier                  comprises magnesium, cerium, zirconium, copper,                  zinc or indium. The reducing agent is a reducing                  agent, which is used for producing a product gas                  containing carbon monoxide as a carbon valuable                  material by reducing carbon dioxide by bringing                  the reducing agent into contact with a raw                  material gas containing carbon dioxide. Preferred                  Composition: The content of the carrier is 50                  mol.% or more with respect to 100 mol.% of the                  reducing agent. Preferred Method: The oxidized                  reducing agent is reduced by contact with a                  reducing gas containing hydrogen. The reducing                  agent is used in separate reaction steps for the                  reduction reaction of carbon dioxide and the                  reduction reaction of the oxidized reducing                  agent. Preferred Properties: The carrier has an                  average pore size of 0.1 nm or more, average                  particle size of 0.1-50 &#956; m, and                  specific surface area of 400 m2/g or more.
   EXAMPLE - Copper (II) nitrate trihydrate and zinc                nitrate hexahydrate were prepared as raw materials                and raw material was dissolved in water to prepare                a 0.5 mol/l raw material aqueous solution. The                molar ratio of copper and zinc in the raw material                aqueous solution was adjusted to 0.5:0.5 and the                raw material aqueous solution was dropped onto the                polygonal columnar silica particles (SBA-15). The                amount of polygonal columnar silica particles used                was set to be 83.3 mol.% with respect to 100 mol.%                of the total (reducing agent) of the generated                oxygen carrier and polygonal columnar silica                (silica(II)oxide) particles was obtained, and the                liquid component was removed from the raw material                aqueous solution in which the polygonal columnar                silica particles was immersed using a rotary                evaporator. After drying the collected silica                (silica(II)oxide) particles at 120&#176; C for                4 hours, and the mixture was calcined at                700&#176; C for 3 hours to produce a reducing                agent.

   USE - The composition or device is useful for (i)                preparing formic acid, (ii) removing molecule                chosen from carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide,                (iii) performing waste gas treatment, and (iv)                purifying air (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition is reusable without lowering                efficiency, and is capable of converting toxic                carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide from among waste                gases at room temperature and normal pressure into                formic acid without by-products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a filter comprising carbon monoxide                dehydrogenase and formate dehydrogenase;
   (2) a method (A) for producing formic acid                involves contacting a gas containing at least one                molecule chosen from carbon monoxide and carbon                dioxide with carbon monoxide dehydrogenase and                formate dehydrogenase; and
   (3) a method (B) for removing molecule i.e.                carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, performing                treatment of waste gas, or purifying air involves                contacting gas containing at least one molecule                chosen from carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide with                carbon monoxide dehydrogenase and formate                dehydrogenase.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Biomolecules: The carbon monoxide                  dehydrogenase is derived from microorganisms of                  at least one genus chosen from Carboxydothermus                  spp. and Thermococcus spp., preferably                  C.hydrogenoformans-derived carbon monoxide                  dehydrogenase II, C.hydrogenoformans-derived                  carbon monoxide dehydrogenase IV and                  T.onnurineus-derived carbon monoxide                  dehydrogenase. Preferred Method:                  The method (A) involves (i) contacting gas with                  carbon monoxide dehydrogenase, and (ii)                  contacting the gas contacted with the carbon                  monoxide dehydrogenase to formate dehydrogenase,                  where the gas is a continuously supplied gas. The                  gas is contacted with carbon monoxide                  dehydrogenase and formate dehydrogenase at the                  same time as the electron transporter.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of preparation of                carbon monoxide dehydrogenase (CODH) and formate                dehydrogenase (FDH), that do not support the                claims.

TI Removing carbon dioxide in high-purity gas            comprises passing high-purity gas raw material into            polyethyleneimine-loaded adsorbent for adsorption and            removal of carbon dioxide.
AU LIAO N
   WANG W
   PENG J
   TANG M
   LUO J
   XIAO J
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2022762274
AB    NOVELTY - Removing carbon dioxide in high-purity gas                comprises passing 0.025-1 g/h high-purity gas raw                material into polyethyleneimine-loaded adsorbent                for adsorption and removal of carbon dioxide, where                the high-purity gas raw material includes CO2 and                target gas, and the CO2 accounts for the                high-purity gas raw material mass ratio of 10-1000                ppm, the mass ratio of the target gas to the                high-purity gas raw material is 99-99.99%, and the                polyethyleneimine supported adsorbent comprises a                carrier and 10-50% polyethyleneimine supported on                the carrier.
   USE - The method is useful for removing carbon                dioxide in high-purity gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyethyleneimine-loaded adsorbent has                high selectivity and adsorption capacity for CO2,                and can remove CO2 gas to less than 0.5ppm, achieve                efficient and deep removal of CO2 impurities, and                obtain 99.9999% high-purity gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                high-purity gas obtained by removing carbon dioxide                by using the above-mentioned method, where the mass                concentration of the target gas in the high-purity                gas is 6 N.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The target gas                  comprises hydrogen, trifluoropropane or                  monofluoromethane. The dosage ratio of the                  high-purity gas raw material to the                  polyethyleneimine-loaded adsorbent is: 0.05-0.5                  g/h of the high-purity gas raw material is                  introduced per gram of the                  polyethyleneimine-loaded adsorbent. The mass                  ratio of the CO2 to the high-purity gas raw                  material is 20-200 ppm, and the target gas to the                  high-purity gas raw material mass ratio is                  99.99%.Preferred Method: The high purity gas raw                  material is introduced into the polyethyleneimine                  loaded adsorbent to absorb and remove the carbon                  dioxide, the adsorption temperature is -20 to                  75&#8451; when the adsorption is removed, and                  the adsorption pressure is 0.1-5 Mpa. The                  preparation method of the polyethyleneimine                  loaded adsorbent comprises (a) dissolving                  polyethyleneimine in an organic solvent, the                  mass-to-volume ratio of the polyethyleneimine and                  the organic solvent is 1 g/(20-50) ml, and                  stirring and mixing to obtain a polyethyleneimine                  solution, where the organic solvent is methanol,                  ethanol or acetone, (b) adding the dried carrier                  to the polyethyleneimine solution of step (a),                  and continuing to stir to form a slurry mixture,                  and stirring at room temperature under nitrogen                  atmosphere for 0.25-6 hours, where the drying                  temperature in the drying process of the dried                  carrier is 80-120&#176;C for 3-24 hours, the                  carrier is silica gel, mesoporous molecular sieve                  or resin, (c) continuously purging the material                  obtained in step (b) under a nitrogen flow                  atmosphere of 40-50&#176;C until the sample                  forms a dry powder, and the temperature of the                  nitrogen flow atmosphere is 35-50&#176;C, the                  nitrogen flow rate is 30-100 ml/minute, and the                  blowing time is 4-12 hours.

PN CN114262906-A
TI Molten salt electrolysis technology for capturing carbon dioxide to prepare carbon monoxide, comprises using molten alkali metal/alkaline earth metal carbonate/halide containing homogeneous catalyst as trapping agent and electrolyte and performing electrolysis using molten salt electrolytic cell.
AU DU K
   DENG B
   HU L
   WANG D
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 202251371K
AB    NOVELTY - Molten salt electrolysis technology for capturing carbon dioxide to prepare carbon monoxide comprises using molten alkali metal/alkaline earth metal carbonate/halide containing homogeneous catalyst or mixture as trapping agent and electrolyte, introducing the gas containing carbon dioxide into the molten electrolyte containing homogeneous catalyst, using molten salt electrolytic cell, metal or non-metal material as cathode and inert material as anode, performing electrolysis in the temperature range of 50-800degrees Celsius to obtain high content carbon monoxide.
   USE - The molten salt electrolysis technology for capturing carbon dioxide to prepare carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple technique, suitable for atmosphere containing different concentrations of carbon dioxide, low electrolytic energy consumption, green and pollution-free electrolytic system, stable catalyst physicochemical property, good selectivity for the gaseous product carbon monoxide, and the carbon dioxide high-throughput is converted into carbon monoxide under the condition of more mild.

The alkali metal or alkaline earth metal halide is lithium fluoride, sodium fluoride, potassium fluoride, calcium fluoride, barium fluoride, magnesium fluoride, lithium chloride, sodium chloride, potassium chloride, calcium chloride, barium chloride or/and magnesium chloride. PN CN111514897-A; CN111514897-B
TI Use of highly dispersed carbon-doped mesoporous silicon nanotube nickel-based catalyst in carbon dioxide methanation reaction and method comprises e.g. loading catalyst into fixed-bed reactor and feeding gas hydrogen into reactor.
AU ZHOU S
AE TAIZHOU HEYI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (TAIZ-Non-standard)
GA 202078759Y
AB    NOVELTY - Use of highly dispersed carbon-doped mesoporous silicon nanotube nickel-based catalyst in the carbon dioxide methanation reaction, where use method comprises loading catalyst into a fixed-bed reactor, feeding the raw material gas hydrogen and carbon dioxide into the reactor at a molar ratio of 1-10, maintaining the feed space velocity of 5000-50000/hour, maintaining the total pressure of the reaction system at 0.1 MPa, and carrying out the catalytic reaction of carbon dioxide methanation at 100-450 degrees C, where the catalyst uses carbon-doped mesoporous silicon nanotubes as a carrier and supports active metal nickel and the mass fraction of nickel in the catalyst is 2-15 wt.%.
   USE - The highly dispersed carbon-doped mesoporous silicon nanotube nickel-based catalyst is useful for carbon dioxide methanation reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: exhibits high catalytic activity and selectivity in the carbon dioxide methanation reaction; and has good application prospects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of the feed gas is preferably 2-6, preferably 4; the feed space velocity is preferably 5000-30000/hour, preferably 20000/hour. The mass fraction of nickel in the catalyst is 5-10 wt.%. In the step (1), the mass ratio of template agent to 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane is 1:1-5, and the mass ratio of 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane (APTES) and 1,2-bis(trimethoxysilyl)ethane is 1:3-8. In the step (1), the nickel salts are nickel nitrate, nickel chloride, and/or nickel sulfate; the concentration of the nickel salt precursor solution is preferably 0.02-0.05 M. Preferred Method: The preparation method of the catalyst comprises (1) dissolving template cetyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTAB) in a mixed solution of water and ethanol, and magnetically stirring to dissolve, adding 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane (APTES) and 1,2-bis(trimethoxysilyl)ethane (BTME) to the above solution in the mass ratio of 1:1-10, stirring continuously for 1-2 hours, pouring the mixture into a polytetrafluoroethylene liner and allowing it stand at 60-100 degrees C for 24-48 hours, centrifuging the reactants, and using a mixed solution of ethanol and hydrochloric acid to extract the template at 40-80 degrees C under reflux, centrifuging and washing to obtain organic bridged phenylene periodic mesoporous organosilica containing amino groups, (2) dissolving soluble nickel salt in ethanol solution to obtain 0.01-0.1 M nickel salt precursor solution, immersing the amine-phenylene periodic mesoporous organosilica prepared in step (1) as a carrier in the above nickel salt precursor solution, ultrasonicatng for 2-4 hours, making the amino group in the phenylene periodic mesoporous organosilica and the metal nickel ion fully carry out the coordination reaction, anchoring the nickel ion to the surface of the carrier by the coordination bond, after the reaction is over, centrifuging the product, washing it thoroughly with deionized water and ethanol, and drying at 60-100 degrees C, and (3) placing the product in a tube furnace, under the protection of nitrogen, increasing the temperature rate of 2-5 degrees C/minutes to 500-900 degrees C, calcining for 2-6 hours to obtain the carbon-doped mesoporous silicon nanotube nickel-based catalyst. In the step (1), the temperature of the allowing to stand reaction is preferably 60-90 degrees C, and the time is preferably 24-36 hours. In the step (3), the calcination temperature is preferably 600-800 degrees C, and the time is preferably 3-4 hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid electrolyte chosen from potassium hydroxide electrolyte, and potassium bicarbonate electrolyte. The flexible membrane comprises gas composition is configured for at least one of carbon dioxide reduction reaction, oxygen reduction reaction (ORR), oxygen evolution reaction (OER), hydrogen evolution reaction (HER), hydrogen oxidation reaction (HOR), and nitrogen reduction reaction (NRR). The carbon dioxide reduction reaction comprises carbon dioxide composition, water, flexible hydrophobic nano polyethylene membrane having thickness of up to 12 mu m, gold catalyst layer having thickness of 10-20 nm, comprising gold nanoparticles having particle diameter of 10-30 nm. The ORR configuration comprises oxygen composition, water, flexible hydrophobic nano polyethylene membrane having thickness of up to 12 mu m, bi-layer of silver or platinum catalyst having thickness of 50-80 nm. The OER configuration comprises oxygen composition, water, flexible hydrophobic nano polyethylene membrane having thickness of up to 12 mu m, gold or nickel or iron oxides catalyst layer having thickness of 50-100 nm. The HER configuration comprises hydrogen composition, water, flexible nano polyethylene membrane having thickness of up to 12 mu m, gold/platinum catalyst layer having thickness of 40-80 nm. The HOR configuration comprises hydrogen composition, water, flexible nano polyethylene membrane having thickness of up to 12 mu m, and gold/platinum catalyst layer having thickness of 40-80 nm. The NRR configuration comprises nitrogen composition, water, flexible nano polyethylene membrane having thickness of up to 12 mu m, and gold/platinum catalyst layer having thickness of 10-20 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The membrane comprises nanoporous polyethylene membrane and further comprises hydrophilic nanoporous film and porous membrane having pore radius of up to 500 nm.

PN CN109577922-A
TI Method producing coal gas, involves pressurizing injection device to appropriate pressure, injecting carbon dioxide and nitrogen to coal bed gas, sealing hole at input end of injection device, and carrying coal seam gas mining.
AU HE D
   ZHANG X
   CAO M
   FENG H
   LIU X
   HU S
   JI Y
   GUO H
   FENG X
   LI Q
   LI A
AE SHANXI YIGAO COALBED METHANE CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201933420Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves surveying target position for drilling exploration. A survey location is punched after survey is completed. An injection device is mounted on a hole. Nitrogen and a carbon dioxide gas injection tank are placed beside the injection device. An output end of the nitrogen is connected with an output end of the carbon dioxide tank. Flow rate of the nitrogen tank and the carbon dioxide gas tank is controlled by an input end of the injecting device. The injection device is pressurized to appropriate pressure. A carbon dioxide and nitrogen is injected to a coal bed gas. A hole at an input end of the injection device is sealed. Coal seam gas mining is carried after period of sealing.
   USE - Method producing coal gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing carbon dioxide to improve coal recovery ratio and single and methane yield, and increasing yield of the coal bed gas.

PN CN108483394-A; CN108483394-B
TI Hydrogenating carbon dioxide with hydrogen storage material involves placing magnesium powder and titanium dioxide powder in ball mill, and then adding zirconia grinding ball as grinding medium.
AU CHEN H
   LIU J
   FENG X
   MA N
   WANG Y
   LIU G
   YUAN W
AE LUOYANG NORMAL COLLEGE (LUOY-Non-standard)
GA 2018713309
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogenating carbon dioxide with a hydrogen storage material involves placing magnesium powder and titanium dioxide powder in a ball mill, and then adding a zirconia grinding ball as a grinding medium, where argon gas is activated by ball milling under argon atmosphere. The supply of argon gas is turned off, and the ball mill tank is opened, and then zirconia grinding balls are replaced by copper balls and iron balls. The hydrogen gas is introduced, and the ball is stored under a hydrogen atmosphere. The hydrogen storage material is stored in a reaction vessel, and then carbon dioxide is introduced into the reaction vessel. The reaction vessel is heated to 200-300 degrees C, and carbon dioxide is reacted with hydrogen in the hydrogen storage material to generate lower alcohol.
   USE - Method for hydrogenating carbon dioxide with a hydrogen storage material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide hydrogenating method has low raw material cost, high reactivity, mild use conditions and high selectivity of lower alcohols.

AB    NOVELTY - The device has a nuclear reaction kettle                filled with a dense oil sample. A storage tank                stores carbon dioxide. A temperature-pressure                controller regulates temperature and pressure of                the storage tank. The storage tank is connected                with a temperature control chamber to analyze a                different phase carbon dioxide discharging oil. An                extracting tank extracts petroleum components of                carbon dioxide from an output reaction and detects                gas phase chromatography sample. The nuclear                reaction kettle is provided with a temperature and                a pressure sensor.

The device diffuses and                discharges the carbon dioxide in different phase,                thus assisting to understand the mechanism of                diffusion and mass transfer of carbon dioxide in                dense oil.

TI System for synthesis of dimethyl ether from carbon            dioxide and hydrogen comprises catalytic membrane            reactor including channels and bi-functional catalyst            material, in which outer surface of reactor has            water-permeable membrane coating.
AU KLINGHOFFER N
   LI S
AE GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
GA 201805281H
AB    NOVELTY - A system, which synthesizes dimethyl ether                from carbon dioxide and hydrogen, comprises                catalytic membrane reactor including channels and                having outer surface with water-permeable membrane                coating, in which reactor further contains                bi-functional catalyst material including methanol                synthesis catalyst component to catalyze reaction                of carbon dioxide and hydrogen to form methanol and                water and dehydration catalyst component to                catalyze dehydration of methanol to form dimethyl                ether. Upon formation of product, water permeates                through the water permeable membrane coating and                exits the reactor.
   USE - A system for synthesis of dimethyl ether from                carbon dioxide and hydrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system produces dimethyl ether in high                yield at low cost and uses catalysts with long                lifetime and excellent catalytic performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for formation                of dimethyl ether from carbon dioxide and hydrogen                which involves introducing carbon dioxide and                hydrogen into multi-channel ceramic catalytic                membrane reactor at dimethyl ether synthesis                conditions to form dimethyl ether.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Water-permeable                  membrane coating comprises NaA zeolite membrane.                  Multi-channel catalytic membrane reactor                  comprises aluminum oxide (Al2O3). Methanol                  synthesis catalyst component of bi-functional                  catalyst material comprises copper, zinc, zinc                  oxide, alumina and/or silica, preferably silica                  and alumina. Dehydration catalyst component of                  bi-functional catalyst material additionally                  contains catalyst promoter e.g. greater than or                  equal to 1 phosphate catalyst promoter.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Catalytic membrane                  reactor comprises hollow fibers, each fiber                  forming greater than or equal to 1 of the                  channels.

AE LAWRENCE LIVERMORE NAT SECURITY LLC (USGO-C)
   LAWRENCE LIVERMORE NAT SECURITY LLC (USGO-C)
   LAWRENCE LIVERMORE NAT LAB LLC (REGC-C)
GA 201984792K
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus comprises: (i) an electromethanogenesis reactor; (ii) an anode electrode conductor in the reactor; (iii) a cathode electrode conductor in the reactor; (iv) submicron to micron scale pores in the cathode electrode conductor; (v) electromethanogenesis enzymes in the submicron to micron scale pores in the cathode electrode conductor; (vi) electromethanogenesis microbes in the submicron to micron scale pores in the cathode electrode conductor; (vii) a system for introducing carbon dioxide into the reactor; (viii) a voltage system for applying a voltage to the anode electrode conductor and the second cathode electrode conductor where fuel gas is produced in the reactor; and (ix) a storage system for storing the fuel gas for subsequent use.
   USE - The apparatus is useful for: electromethanogenesis of carbon dioxide to methane; and generating energy and storing the energy for subsequent use (claimed), i.e. power generation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a reactor apparatus for electromethanogenesis of carbon dioxide to methane, comprising an electromethanogenesis reactor housing, and (ii)-(viii) as above per se, where methane is produced in the electromethanogenesis reactor housing;
   (2) generating (M1) energy and storing the energy for subsequent use, comprising providing carbon dioxide, electromethanogenesis of the carbon dioxide into a fuel gas, and storing the fuel gas for subsequent use; and
   (3) electromethanogenesis (M2) of carbon dioxide to methane, comprising providing the reactor apparatus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cathode electrode conductor is a graphene aerogel cathode electrode conductor, or a resorcinol-formaldehyde aerogel cathode electrode conductor. Preferred Method: In the method (M1), the step of electromethanogenesis of the carbon dioxide into a fuel gas comprises electromethanogenesis of the carbon dioxide into methane gas, or electromethanogenesis of the carbon dioxide into syngas; and the step of storing the fuel gas for subsequent use comprises using the fuel gas for power generation. The method (M2) further comprises selecting the electromethanogenesis enzymes and the electromethanogenesis microbes to provide maximum current density in the cathode electrode conductor.

PT P
PN CN110102341-A
TI Carbon dioxide reduction photocatalyst comprises graphene oxide coated methyl ammonium lead tri-bromide for electron transport in process of photoreduction of carbon dioxide.
AU SHEN Y
   YU Z
   WANG Q
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 201971620Y
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide reduction photocatalyst comprises graphene oxide coated methyl ammonium lead tri-bromide (MAPbBr3) which is used for electron transport in the process of photoreduction of carbon dioxide.
   USE - Carbon dioxide reduction photocatalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is simple and controllable, and less in pollution. The prepared graphene oxide coated methyl ammonium lead tri-bromide has excellent photoelectric catalytic carbon dioxide performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a preparation of carbon dioxide reduction photocatalyst, which involves (S1) dissolving methyl ammonium bromide and lead(II) bromide in a solution of graphene oxide in dimethylformamide, adding oleic acid and oleylamine and stirring well to prepare a pre-formed solution, and the volume ratio of oleic acid to oleylamine is 25:2, (S2) adding the pre-formed solution in the step (S1) to the toluene solution having a temperature of 40-80 degrees C, and performing a reaction to obtain a mixed solution having the reaction product, and the volume ratio of the pre-formed solution to the toluene solution is 1:25, and (S3) adding a mixed solution of toluene and acetonitrile to the mixed solution having the reaction product obtained in the step (S2), centrifuging to obtain a precipitate, and the precipitate is methyl ammonium lead tri-bromide coated with graphene oxide, and the volume ratio of toluene to acetonitrile is 1:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The yield of the product methane can reach 1.05 mu mol/(cm2.hour). The concentration of graphene oxide in the dimethylformamide solution is not more than 1 mg/ml in step (S1). The volume ratio of the mixed solution of toluene and acetonitrile to the mixed solution having the reaction product is 1:1 to 4:1. Preferred Process: The size of the graphene oxide-coated methyl ammonium lead tri-bromide particles is adjusted by adjusting the concentration of methyl ammonium bromide and lead(II) bromide dissolved in graphene oxide in dimethylformamide solution in step (S1), and the obtained graphene oxide-coated methyl ammonium lead tri-bromide particles have a larger particle size, and conversely smaller when the concentration of methyl ammonium bromide and lead(II) bromide dissolved in graphene oxide in dimethylformamide is high. The desired oleic acid and oleylamine are used as ligands for methyl ammonium bromide and lead(II) bromide to form methyl ammonium lead tri-bromide, and the resulting methyl ammonium lead tri-bromide product is relatively more and vice versa when the ligand content is relatively high. Preferred Condition: The reaction time is not less than 10 minute, and the temperature of the mixed solution of the entire reaction product is lowered to 0 degrees C or below to stop the reaction after completion of the reaction.

PN CN109355929-A; WO2020077702-A1; US2021355632-A1
TI Treating supercritical carbon dioxide fluid as medium anhydrous fiber dyeing, involves mechanically compacting dry fiber in layered form in special porous cage on cotton or filling, placing cotton or loaded yarn cage in dyeing tank.
AU LONG J
   CAI C
   GAO C
   GAO W
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   SHANDONG GAOJIN GROUP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   SHANDONG GAOMEI GROUP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG GAOMIAN GROUP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 201919164T
AB    NOVELTY - Treating supercritical carbon dioxide fluid involves mechanically compacting the dry fiber in a layered form in a special porous cage on cotton or filling, placing cotton or the loaded yarn cage in a high-pressure dyeing tank for pretreatment, after pretreatment is completed. The supercritical carbon dioxide medium and the dissolved special dye are introduced into the high pressure dyeing tank, followed by supercharging, heating and heat preservation dyeing according to a predetermined dyeing process, after the thermal insulation dyeing is finished. The dyeing system is cooled by the clean supercritical carbon dioxide medium, until system temperature is lowered to a certain temperature. The online floating color cleaning is performed under certain conditions, and finally the fluid medium in the dyeing system is recycled, until the non-fibrous dyeing process in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid medium is completed.
   USE - Method for treating supercritical carbon dioxide fluid as medium anhydrous fiber dyeing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat supercritical carbon dioxide fluid which is simple and convenient to prepare, it is effectively realizes dry dyeing process, has mild reaction condition, avoids large amount of water in traditional dyeingprocess, and has high efficiency, and eco-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The specific dye for dissolving state is a reactive disperse dye, and the active group is one or more of a vinyl sulfone, a vinyl group, a homotriazine type, and a nicotinic acid structure, or their derivative compounds. The solvent for dissolving state is one or more of supercritical carbon dioxide, ethanol, acetone, methanol, and deionized water. Preferred Conditions: The dry fiber is a short fiber such as cotton in a natural fiber, or a processed hemp fiber, or a staple fiber processed into a synthetic fiber such as viscose, polyester, nylon or acrylic. Preferred Conditions: The mechanical pressing ptocess is to perform a uniform and uniform layer-by-layer extrusion processing on the fluffy cotton by a mechanical external force, so that the flat filling is performed according to a certain tightness. The porous yarn cage is made of an outer covering Teflon or other non-conductive hot surface material, where multiple hollow holes are arranged around the saddle and central hollow tube. The layered form refers to a dry fiber extrusion filling as a layer after mechanical tapping or filling, and then performing the next layer of extrusion filling. The dry fiber is treaded in cotton in the saddle, the certain degree of tightness is 50-300 kilo gram per cubic meter. The pretreatment medium is one or more of saturated steam, superheated steam or other polar solvent. The pre-main condition is pressure of 0-1 mega Pascal and the time of 5-180 minutes. The ratio of the solvent and two kinds of mixed solvents is 1:5-5:1. The pressure is 7-35 mega Pascal, the dynamic and static cycle time ratio of the fluid is 1:5-10:1, and the processing time is 10-180 minutes. The system temperature is lowered to a certain temperature, and the temperature is 30-100 degrees C. The on-line floating color cleaning under the certain conditions has a process condition of a temperature of 30-100 degrees C, a pressure of 8-35 mega Pascal, and a dynamic-to-static cycle time ratio of the fluid is 1:5-10:1, the processing time is 10-120 minutes. The carbon dioxide is separated and recovered by the recovery system for the next recycling, and the carbon dioxide gas in the dyeing system is recovered to atmospheric pressure to realize direct opening of the dyeing cylinder.

TI Composition useful for adsorbing and desorbing carbon dioxide, comprises hydrazine-based derivative, and metal hydroxide and phosphoric acid as corrosion inhibiting additive.
AU HUR N H
   LEE K H
   KYE S H
   HYOUNG L G
   KYE S
AE UNIV SOGANG RES FOUND (USOG-C)
   MURATA MFG CO LTD (MURA-C)
GA 2018707073
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises hydrazine-based derivative (I), and metal hydroxide (II), and phosphoric acid (III) as corrosion inhibiting additive.
   USE - The composition is useful for adsorbing and desorbing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition suppresses corrosion effect, has excellent thermal stability, and large carbon dioxide absorption power at normal pressure, desorbs carbon dioxide even with small amount of energy, and is economical, highly efficient and stable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Composition comprises hydrazine-based derivative of formula (I), and metal hydroxide of formula (II), and phosphoric acid of formula (III) as corrosion inhibiting additive.
   R1 and R2 = 1-30C aliphatic hydrocarbon group containing Si, O, S, Se, N, P, As, F, Cl, Br and I, 3-30C aliphatic cyclic group containing Si, O, S, Se, N, P, As, F, Cl, Br and I, 3-30C hetero aliphatic cyclic group containing Si, O, S, Se, N, P, As, F, Cl, Br and I, 5-30C aromatic ring group, and 5-30C heteroaromatic ring group containing Si, O, S, Se, N, P, As, F, Cl, Br and I (all optionally substituted) or H;
   M = alkali metal or alkaline earth metal; and
   y = 1-2.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) adsorbing and desorbing carbon dioxide using the composition; and
   (2) carbon dioxide absorption products of formula (IV) or (V) comprising composition in which the hydrazine-based compound is produced by absorbing carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition adsorbs carbon dioxide under non-solvent conditions, and in presence of solvent. The solvent includes water, alcohols, ethers, 1-30C aliphatic hydrocarbons, and/or 5-30C aromatic hydrocarbons. The alcohol includes methanol, ethanol, propanol, isopropanol, n-butanol, isobutanol, sec-butanol, tert-butanol, n-pentanol, isopentanol, sec-pentanol, tert-pentanol, hexanol, heptanol, octanol, nonanol, decanol, undecanol, dodecanol, pentadecanol, ethylene glycol, glycerol, erythritol, xylitol and/or mannitol. The ether includes dimethyl ether, diethyl ether, tetrahydrofuran and/or dioxins. Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 1-100 pts. wt. solvent based on 100 pts. wt. hydrazine or its hydrazine derivative. Preferred Method: The adsorption of carbon dioxide is carried out under solventless conditions, or in the presence of solvent at -20 to 100 degrees C under pressure of 2-3000 psi. The carbon dioxide desorption is performed by desorbing carbon dioxide adsorbed to the composition by the addition of water.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN114180601-A
TI Green and low-carbon method for producing soda ash and caustic soda from waste salt and waste residue involves using ammonium bicarbonate or ammonia and carbon dioxide, and waste salt or waste residue, and circulating salt mother liquor.
AU PENG Q
   PENG C
   PENG X
   PENG M
   PENG Z
   PENG J
   PENG S
AE PENG S (PENG-Individual)
GA 2022440369
AB    NOVELTY - Green and low-carbon method for producing soda ash and caustic soda from waste salt and waste residue involves using ammonium bicarbonate or ammonia and carbon dioxide, and waste salt or waste residue, circulating salt mother liquor for metathesis reaction, separating to obtain solid sodium bicarbonate and heavy alkali mother liquor, calcining solid sodium bicarbonate, recycling carbon dioxide to carry out double metathesis reaction, mixing first solid ammonia mother liquor with the circulating ammonium mother liquor and evaporating to obtain sodium chloride, sodium sulfate and salt-making mother liquor, preparing salt mother liquor to obtain ammonium chloride, ammonium sulfate and ammonium-making mother liquor and recycling ammonium-making mother liquor, calcining calcium carbonate product and coal to obtain lime and carbon dioxide, and circulating carbon dioxide to carry out metathesis reaction, reacting second solid ammonia mother liquor with ammonia and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Green and low-carbon method for producing soda ash and caustic soda from waste salt and waste residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low raw material cost, reasonable process flow, high energy consumption of electric energy freezing technique, high boiling point calcium chloride solution and high heat energy evaporation process, can relieve the long-term problem of soda industry ammonia alkali method distillation waste liquid discharge, and generates large circulation economic benefit, with raw material waste salt and waste residue, waste liquid, carbon dioxide comprehensive utilization, new green, low carbon, and good environmental benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Green and low-carbon method for producing soda ash and caustic soda from waste salt and waste residue, involves using ammonium bicarbonate or ammonia and carbon dioxide, with waste salt or waste residue, circulating salt mother liquor as raw material to carry out metathesis reaction for 0.1-12 hours, separating and obtaining solid sodium bicarbonate and heavy alkali mother liquor, calcining portion of solid sodium bicarbonate to obtain soda ash product and carbon dioxide and recyling carbon dioxide to carry out metathesis reaction, adding lime causticization to obtain caustic soda, calcium carbonate product and carbon dioxide after the other part of solid sodium bicarbonate is dissolved in water and circulating carbon dioxide to carry out metathesis reaction, diluting mother liquor and first distillation mother liquor or reacting with calcium sulfate, glauberite and calcium chloride for 0.1-12 hours, converting free ammonia into solid ammonia to obtain solid ammonia mother liquor and calcium carbonate product, dividing solid ammonia mother liquor into first solid ammonia mother liquor and second solid ammonia mother liquor, mixing the first solid ammonia mother liquor with circulating ammonium production mother liquor and evaporating at high temperature of 60-150degrees Celsius to obtain sodium chloride, sodium sulfate and salt production mother liquor, preparing salt mother liquor by evaporating at low temperature of 30-120degrees Celsius to obtain ammonium chloride, ammonium sulfate and ammonium-making mother liquor and recycling ammonium-making mother liquor for high-temperature evaporation, calcining calcium carbonate product and coal at high temperature to obtain lime and carbon dioxide and circulating carbon dioxide to carry out metathesis reaction, reacting second solid ammonia mother liquor with ammonia, carbon dioxide and distillation mother liquor for 0.1-12 hours, and dividing the distillation mother liquor into first distillation mother liquor and second distillation mother liquor and recycling ammonia and carbon dioxide to carry out metathesis reaction, mixing the second distillation mother liquor with sodium sulfate carrier for 0.1-12 hours to obtain calcium sulfate and circulating salt mother liquor, and circulating portion of calcium sulfate and reacting with heavy alkali mother liquor, and circulating the salt mother liquor to carry out metathesis reaction process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the green and low-carbon method for producing soda ash and caustic soda from waste salt and waste residue. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of the first solid ammonia mother liquor or the second solid ammonia mother liquor to the solid ammonia mother liquor is 0-100%. The content of each component in the circulating salt mother liquor is 1-360 g/l sodium chloride and 1-360 g/l sodium sulfate. The content of each component in the heavy alkali mother liquor is 1-360 g/l ammonium chloride, 1-360g/l sodium chloride, 1-360g/l ammonium bicarbonate, 1-360g/l ammonium carbonate, 1-360g/l sodium bicarbonate, 1-360 g/l sodium sulfate and 1-360 g/l ammonium sulfate. The content of each component in the salt-making mother liquor is 1-360 g/l ammonium chloride, 1-360 g/l sodium chloride, 1-360 g/l sodium sulfate, and 1-360 g/l ammonium sulfate. The content of each component in the ammonium preparation mother liquor is 1-360 g/l ammonium chloride, 1-360 g/l sodium chloride, 1-360 g/l sodium sulfate, and 1-360 g/l ammonium sulfate. The content of each component in the distillation mother liquor is 1-360 g/l sodium chloride, 1-360 g/l calcium chloride or 1-360 g/l sodium sulfate. Preferred Components: The sodium sulfate carrier is glauberite, decahydrate or waste liquid or waste residue containing sodium chloride monosalt, sodium sulfate monosalt or sodium chloride and sodium sulfate mixed salt. The ammonium bicarbonate, waste salt or waste residue solution controls the depth of the metathesis reaction and directly goes to the evaporation separation system of sodium chloride, sodium sulfate, ammonium chloride and ammonium sulfate.

PN CN114100688-A
TI Preparing immobilized bionic enzyme used for capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas, involves preparing water-phase immobilized bionic carbonic anhydrase at normal temperature and normal pressure.
AU HUANG F
   GE B
   WANG X
   LONG Y
   WANG G
   WANG Y
   XIANG Y
   SUN S
   YU D
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 202237791Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing immobilized bionic enzyme involves preparing water-phase immobilized bionic carbonic anhydrase at normal temperature and normal pressure, and using a surfactant Triton(RTM) X-100 (RTM: nonionic surfactant) as a blocking agent in preparation process to regulate and control the particle size and specific surface area of the bionic carbonic anhydrase to obtain more active sites with catalytic activity. The factory-discharged flue gas is introduced into a flue gas purification system for desulfurization, denitrification and dedusting. The purified flue gas is cooled by using a heat exchanger or a water washing method, controlled the temperature at 50-60degrees Celsius, introduced the flue gas into a primary absorption tower from bottom to top, and made the flue gas reversely contact with a sodium carbonate solution sprayed from the top, where carbon dioxide in the flue gas and the sodium carbonate solution are subjected to absorption reaction in the process.
   USE - Method for preparing immobilized bionic enzyme used for capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process, does not cause secondary pollution to the environment, and is suitable for capturing the carbon dioxide in the flue gas of the existing chemical plants and power plants. The method enables to accelerate the conversion rate of carbon dioxide absorption due to presence of bionic enzymes, which heats or decompresses sodium bicarbonate solution to collect pure carbon dioxide, and accelerate the desorption of carbon dioxide in process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing immobilized bionic enzyme involves preparing water-phase immobilized bionic carbonic anhydrase at normal temperature and normal pressure, and using a surfactant Triton(RTM) X-100 (RTM: nonionic surfactant) as a blocking agent in the preparation process to regulate and control the particle size and specific surface area of the bionic carbonic anhydrase to obtain more active sites with catalytic activity. The factory-discharged flue gas is introduced into a flue gas purification system for desulfurization, denitrification and dedusting. The purified flue gas is cooled by using a heat exchanger or a water washing method, controlled the temperature at 50-60degrees Celsius, introduced the flue gas into a primary absorption tower from bottom to top, and made the flue gas reversely contact with a sodium carbonate solution sprayed from the top, where carbon dioxide in the flue gas and the sodium carbonate solution are subjected to absorption reaction in the process, and accelerated by the existence of the bionic carbonic anhydrase. The flue gas after primary absorption is subjected to secondary absorption in order to prevent the primary absorption tower from being incapable of completely absorbing the carbon dioxide in the flue gas, where solution after secondary absorption is a sodium carbonate solution, and the carbon dioxide in the flue gas is removed in a spraying manner. The flue gas is evacuated after secondary absorption. The solution is subjected to primary absorption into a carbon dioxide desorption system, performed high-temperature desorption at 80degrees Celsius by using the waste heat of the heat exchanger, and generate carbon dioxide and a sodium carbonate solution after desorption. The bionic carbonic anhydrase is used to catalyze the reverse reaction of carbon dioxide hydration due to the reduction of the pH of the solution, so that the desorption rate is increased. The regenerated carbon dioxide is separated in a steam purging mode, then separated water vapor and carbon dioxide through condensation, and pumped out the carbon dioxide by a vacuum pump for storage. The sodium carbonate solution generated by desorption is introduced into the primary absorption tower again for recycling without a temperature reduction. The absorption condition of the secondary absorption tower is detected, and introduced the primary absorption tower for operation if the pH value of the sodium carbonate solution is reduced to 11, and replaced the sodium carbonate solution in the secondary absorption tower with a new one.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation of immobilized bionic carbonic anhydrase is carried out by respectively preparing a zinc acetate solution and a 2-methylimidazole solution, dissolving surfactant TritonTrade Mark X-100 with proper mass into the prepared zinc acetate solution, stirring for 5 minutes, adding into the prepared 2-methylimidazole, stirring for 30 minutes, and centrifuging to obtain the immobilized bionic carbonic anhydrase ZIF (zeolitic imidazolate framework)-8. The surfactant is Triton X-100 having concentration of 0.2-4 mM in the final system. The sodium bicarbonate solution is desorbed carbon dioxide by a pressure reduction system.

PN CN114854440-A
TI Producing hydrogen, activated carbon, and carbon            comprises placing flue gas generated by combustion of            heating chamber of pyrolysis gasifier into dryer, and            using drying heat source to dry and dehydrate fresh            reed bamboo, and cooling flue gas.
AU WANG B
AE HAINAN GUOKE YINNENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HAIN-Non-standard)
GA 2022A9692C
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen, activated carbon, and                carbon comprises placing fresh reed bamboo into                crusher for crushing, drying the crushed reed                bamboo, and placing the produced powder into roller                granulator for extrusion and granulation. The water                vapor produced by drying is used as pyrolysis                gasification. The steam is mixed with the flue gas                generated by the pure oxygen combustion of the                carbon monoxide-rich gas obtained by gasification                to obtain high-temperature superheated steam. The                generated reed bamboo particles are transported to                the feed screw of the pyrolysis gasifier,                transported to the feed section of the pyrolysis                gasifier, and slowly slid to the discharge section                to match the high temperature from the hot blast                stove. The superheated steam is in countercurrent                contact, where the reed bamboo is pyrolyzed and                gasified, and the solid carbon produced is                activated.
   USE - Method for producing hydrogen, activated                carbon, and carbon captured by hydroxylation and                pyrolysis of phosphorus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method makes full use of the moisture and                biomass in fresh reed bamboo to obtain hydrogen,                activated carbon, and carbon capture to obtain                carbon dioxide, and realizes hydrogen production,                carbon dioxide capture and preparation of activated                carbon materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing hydrogen, activated carbon, and                carbon comprises placing fresh reed bamboo into                crusher for crushing, drying the crushed reed                bamboo, and placing the produced powder into roller                granulator for extrusion and granulation. The water                vapor produced by drying is used as pyrolysis                gasification. The steam is mixed with the flue gas                generated by the pure oxygen combustion of the                carbon monoxide-rich gas obtained by gasification                to obtain high-temperature superheated steam. The                generated reed bamboo particles are transported to                the feed screw of the pyrolysis gasifier,                transported to the feed section of the pyrolysis                gasifier, and slowly slid to the discharge section                to match the high temperature from the hot blast                stove. The superheated steam is in countercurrent                contact, where the reed bamboo is pyrolyzed and                gasified, and the solid carbon produced is                activated. The solids generated by pyrolysis                gasification and steam activation are discharged                from the pyrolysis gasifier and then indirectly                cooled. The cooled solids generate reed bamboo                activated carbon, and the gas generated by                pyrolysis gasification enters the high temperature                metal bag filter dust removal, and then entered the                gas-gas heat exchanger for cooling, in which the                preheated air is used as the combustion-supporting                air in the heating chamber of the pyrolysis                gasifier. The cooled gas is indirectly condensed,                and the condensed gas is washed with water to                remove it completely. The impurities are entered                into the hydrogen pressure swing adsorber to obtain                hydrogen products. The adsorbed combustible gas is                rich in carbon monoxide. The flue gas generated by                the combustion of the heating chamber of the                pyrolysis gasifier enters the shell side of the                dryer, and is used as a drying heat source to dry                and dehydrate the fresh reed bamboo, and the cooled                flue gas enters the water washing tower for                washing. The dried and dehydrated gas enters a                carbon dioxide pressure swing adsorber for                adsorption to obtain carbon dioxide and realize                carbon capture.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for producing hydrogen, activated carbon and carbon                captured by bamboo reed hydroxylation pyrolysis,                which comprises outlet of the giant reed                pulverizing unit connected with inlet of the powder                conveyor, outlet of the powder roller press                connected with the inlet of a particle elevator,                and an outlet of an oxygen generator connected with                a gas inlet of a hot blast stove.

TI Device for purifying exhaust gas from ship, has a wet scrubber for converting carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide contained in exhaust gas into inorganic salts by reacting exhaust gas generated from generator with water and reaction solution.
AU KIM K B
   LEE K H
   RHOHYUNGJU
   LEE M W
   SON W
AE HI AIR KOREA CO LTD (HIAI-Non-standard)
GA 2021C0683Y
AB    NOVELTY - Device has a wet scrubber for converting carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide contained in exhaust gas into inorganic salts by reacting the exhaust gas generated from an exhaust gas generator with water. An inlet is provided through which the exhaust gas flows into the wet scrubber, and an outlet through which the exhaust gas is discharged to outside of the wet scrubber. Circulation tank is provided for preparing a reaction solution reacting with exhaust gas and supplying it to the scrubber. First solid-liquid separation device is provided for separating solid suspension from the inorganic salt and the reaction solution generated in the scrubbers. Mixing tank is provided for reacting an inorganic salt contained in the reaction solution transferred from the wet scrubber with a divalent metal oxide or a divalent metal hydroxide to generate sludge in which carbonate and sulfate and the regenerated reaction solution are mixed, and a storage tank for supplying a divalent metal oxide to tank.
   USE - Device for purifying exhaust gas (claimed) from ship.
   ADVANTAGE - The device absorbs and solidifies pollutants contained in exhaust gas discharged from a ship and transporting them to land thus do not affect the environment, reducing the required amount of the reaction liquid, and remarkably reducing the purification cost of the exhaust gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device has a wet scrubber for converting carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide contained in exhaust gas into inorganic salts by reacting the exhaust gas generated from an exhaust gas generator with water and reaction solution. An inlet is provided through which the exhaust gas flows into the wet scrubber, and an outlet through which the exhaust gas is discharged to the outside of the wet scrubber. Circulation tank is provided for preparing a reaction solution reacting with the exhaust gas and supplying it to the wet scrubber. First solid-liquid separation device is provided for separating solid suspension from the inorganic salt and the reaction solution generated in the scrubbers. Mixing tank is provided for reacting an inorganic salt contained in the reaction solution transferred from the wet scrubber with a divalent metal oxide or a divalent metal hydroxide to generate sludge in which carbonate and sulfate and the regenerated reaction solution are mixed, and a storage tank for supplying a divalent metal oxide or a divalent metal hydroxide to the mixing tank. Second solid-liquid separation device is provided for separating carbonate from the sludge transferred from the mixing tank, a heat exchanger is provided for cooling the sludge from which the carbonate is separated, and a third solid-liquid separator for separating sulfate from the sludge from which the cooled carbonate is separated. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for purifying exhaust gas, which involves reacting carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas emitted from the ship with water, reacting sulfur dioxide and water contained in the exhaust gas with sulfurous acid and sulfuric acid, reacting carbonic acid produced in the first step and sulfurous acid produced and sulfuric acid to produce an inorganic salt, separating the solid suspension from the reaction solution containing the inorganic salt through the first solid-liquid separation device, reacting the reaction solution containing the inorganic salt generated with the divalent metal oxide or divalent metal hydroxide to generate carbonate and sulfate while simultaneously regenerating the reaction solution, separating carbonate contained in the regenerated reaction solution through the second solid-liquid separation device, cooling the reaction solution containing the sulfate through the heat exchanger and separating the sulfate through the third solid-liquid separation device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reaction solution is a monovalent alkali aqueous solution. The monovalent alkali aqueous solution is lithium hydroxide aqueous solution, sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, potassium hydroxide aqueous solution, or aqueous ammonia. The divalent metal oxide is calcium oxide or magnesium oxide, and the divalent metal hydroxide is calcium hydroxide or magnesium hydroxide. Preferred Condition: The divalent metal oxide or divalent metal hydroxide is supplied in the form of powder, aqueous solution or sludge.

   W1 = halo comprising Cl, Br, F or I or linear or branched, optionally saturated 1-20C alkoxy, preferably 2-10C, optionally substituted aryloxy groups or oxylamine groups;
   a = 0-4, preferably 1 or 3;
   b = 0-4, preferably 1 or 4; and
   c = 0 or 1, preferably 0.
   Full definitions are given in the DEFINITIONS (Full Definitions) Field. Provided that: at least three of R4-R7 are different from H; and the sum a+b+c is equal to 4 and that at least one of a and b is different from 0. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a transition metal complex is of formula (Ia); and
   (2) preparation of a transition metal complex of formula (Ib), comprising reacting at least one transition metal complex of formula (IV), in the presence of at least one organic solvent comprising halogenated solvents e.g. trichloromethane, dichloromethane, 1,2-dichloroethane, 1,1,1-trichloroethane and/or chlorobenzene, preferably trichloromethane, alcohols e.g. methanol, ethanol, iso-propanol, butanol and/or 1-octanol, preferably methanol, with at least one hydrogen halide acid e.g. hydrochloric acid, hydrobromic acid, hydroiodic acid and/or hydrofluoric acid, preferably hydrochloric acid, in that to the mixture of the transition metal complex (IV) and the organic solvent, a first aliquot of the at least one hydrogen halide acid is added and the mixture obtained is left to react, at a temperature comprised of 30-70 degrees C, preferably 40-60 degrees C, for a time of comprised of 3-7 hours, preferably 4-6 hours and subsequently a second aliquot of the hydrogen halide acid is added and the mixture obtained is left to react, at a temperature comprised of 30-70 degrees C, preferably of 40-60 degrees C, for a time of comprised of 8-15 hours, preferably 10-13 hours and subsequently a third aliquot of the hydrogen halide acid is added and the mixture obtained is left to react, at a temperature comprised of 30-70 degrees C, preferably comprised of 40-60 degrees C, for a time of comprised of 18-30 hours, preferably 20-26 hours.
   Y1a = azide anion; and
   Y1b = halide anion e.g. fluoride anion, chloride anion, bromide anion or iodide anion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The process is carried out in the presence of at least one organic solvent comprising aliphatic hydrocarbons e.g. pentane, octane, decane and/or cyclohexane, aromatic hydrocarbons e.g. benzene, toluene and/or xylene, halogenated hydrocarbons e.g. dichloromethane, chloroform, carbon tetrachloride, 1,1-dichloroethane, 1,2-dichloroethane, ethylchloride, trichloroethane, 1-chloropropane, 2-chloropropane, 1-chlorobutane, 2-chlorobutane, 1-chloro-2-methylpropane, chlorobenzene and/or bromobenzene, preferably it is dichloromethane, the organic solvent being used in a ratio by volume with respect to the propylene oxide comprised of 0:100 and 99:1, preferably comprised 0:100 and 90:1. The propylene oxide acts as a solvent. The catalytic system and the propylene oxide are used in a molar ratio comprised of 1:100 and 1:100000, preferably comprised of 1:1000-1:10000 and/or in the catalytic system, the catalyst comprising complexes of a transition metal (I) and the co-catalyst are used in a molar ratio comprised of 100:1 and 1:100, preferably comprised of 2:1 and 1:2. The process is carried out, at a temperature comprised of 0 degrees C and 250 degrees C, preferably comprised of 10 degrees C and 120 degrees C and/or at a pressure comprised of 1-100 atm, preferably comprised of 2-60 atm and/or for a time comprised of 30 minutes and 48 hours, preferably comprised of 2-30 hours.

PN CN110251435-A; CN110251435-B
TI Essential oil spray with insect repellent and antipruritic effect contains lilac and jasmine distillate, ledum essential oil, lemongrass essential oil, menthol, emulsifier, glycerin, propylene glycol, ascorbic acid, and deionized water.
AU XIE Y
   FU D
   HUANG B
   YANG F
   SUI X
   LI P
   ZHANG X
AE HEILONGJIANG ACAD SCI ADVANCED TECHNOLOG (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 201984590K
AB    NOVELTY - An essential oil spray with insect repellent and antipruritic effect comprises 35-50 %mass lilac and jasmine distillate, 2-5 %mass ledum essential oil, 2-5 %mass lemongrass essential oil, 1-3 %mass Eucalyptus leaves extract, 0.3-1.5 %mass wormwood extract, 0.2-1 %mass menthol, 5-10 %mass emulsifier, 2-5 %mass hydrogenated castor oil, 0.5-3 %mass glycerin, 0.2-1 %mass propylene glycol, 1-3 %mass hyaluronic acid, 0.5-2 %mass ascorbic acid, 0.8-2 %mass lutein and balance deionized water.
   USE - Essential oil spray with insect repellent and antipruritic effect.
   ADVANTAGE - The essential oil spray solves the problem of existing essential oil type mosquito repellent products which are volatile. The spray has short effective mosquito repelling time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of essential oil spray,which involves (1) collecting fresh burlap lilac flowers and jasmine flowers, cleaning, mixing with deionized water, placing in the Soxhlet extractor at 95-110 degrees C, heating and refluxing for 2-5 hours, centrifuging, taking the supernatant, obtaining distilled lilac flowers and jasmine distillate, taking ledum flowers and leaves, washing and drying in a ventilated place, placing the dried Eucalyptus and ledum leaves in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction unit at 40-50 degrees C and an extraction pressure of 15-30 MPa, and carbon dioxide flow rate of 10-20 L/hour, extracting for 90-150 minutes, obtaining ledum essential oil, collecting fresh lemongrass stems and leaves, washing and drying in a ventilated place, placing dried lemon grass stems and leaves in the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device under at 35-45 degrees C and extraction pressure of 18-35 MPa, and carbon dioxide flow rate of 12-18 L/hour, extracting for 90-120 minutes to obtain lemongrass essential oil, (4) collecting fresh Eucalyptus leaves, washing and drying in a ventilated place, mixing 30-60 pts. wt. dried Eucalyptus leaves, 100-200 pts. wt. deionized water and 60-200 pts. wt. absolute ethanol, placing in the Soxhlet extractor at 75-95 degrees C, heating and refluxing for 2-4 hours, centrifuging, taking the supernatant and concentrating to 1/4-1/8 of the volume, obtaining Eucalyptus leaves extract, (5) collecting fresh wormwood, washing and drying in a ventilated place, mixing 15-40 pts. wt. wormwood, 60-200 pts. wt. deionized water and 100-200 pts. wt. absolute ethanol, placing in the Soxhlet extractor at 75-95 degrees C, heating and refluxing for 1.5-4.5 hours, centrifuging, taking the supernatant and concentrating to 1/4-1/8 of the volume, obtaining wormwood extract, (6) mixing ledum essential oil, lemongrass essential oil and hydrogenated castor oil to obtain a stable phase (A), (8) dividing the emulsifier into emulsifier (a) and emulsifier (b) in a mass ratio of 1: 1-3, mixing Eucalyptus leaves extract, wormwood extract, burdock and jasmine distillate, menthol, hydrogenated castor oil, glycerin, propylene glycol, hyaluronic acid, ascorbic acid, lutein and deionized water and emulsifier (a), ultrasonically dispersing to obtain light brown transparent liquid phase (B), (9) heating light brown transparent liquid phase (B) at 30-45 degrees C in a constant temperature water bath for 20- 45 minutes, mixing the stabilized phase (A) with emulsifier (b), stirring at 50-200 rpm, adding the mixture to the light brown transparent liquid phase (B) at a rate of 5-30 mL/minute, stirring for 15-30 minutes, cooling naturally, (10) leaving still for 24-72 hours, packing with light-proof glass bottles and ultra-micro atomized spray pump head, subjecting canned products to outer packaging and plastic packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The emulsifier is chosen from one or more of 5-25 pts. wt. alkyl glycoside, 2-30 pts. wt. polyoxyethylene sorbitan monooleate, 0 -15 pts. wt. sorbitan monooleate, 2-25 pts. wt. polyoxyethylene sorbitol beeswax derivative, 5-30 pts. wt. sorbitan sesquioleate, 0-20 pts. wt. sorbitan monopalmitate and 3-20 pts. wt. polyoxyethylene cetyl ether. The essential oil spray preferably comprises 46-50 %mass lilac and jasmine distillate, 4-5 %mass ledum essential oil, 4-5 %mass lemongrass essential oil, 2.5-3 %mass Eucalyptus leaves extract, 0.5-1.5 %mass wormwood extract, 0.6-1 %mass menthol, 7-10 %mass emulsifier, 4-5 %mass hydrogenated castor oil, 0.9-3 %mass glycerin, 0.5-1 %mass propylene glycol, 1.6-3 %mass hyaluronic acid, 0.7-2 %mass ascorbic acid, 0.8-1 %mass lutein and balance deionized water. Preferred Condition: In step (2), the dried ledum flowers and leaves are placed in the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device at preferably 40-46 degrees C, an extraction pressure of preferably 18-30 MPa, and carbon dioxide flow rate of preferably 16-20 L/hour, extracted for 120-150 minutes to obtain ledum essential oils. In step (3), the dried lemon grass stems and leaves are placed in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device under the conditions of an extraction temperature of preferably 38-45 degrees C, an extraction pressure of preferably 25-35 MPa, and carbon dioxide flow rate of preferably 12-16 L/hour, and extracted for preferably 90-100 minutes to obtain lemongrass essential oil. In step (4), 50-60 pts. wt. dried Eucalyptus leaves, 100-150 pts. wt. deionized water, and 160-200 pts. wt. absolute ethanol are mixed. In step (5), 5-30 pts. wt. wormwood, 150-200 pts. wt. absolute ethanol, and 150-200 pts. wt. deionized water are mixed.
