PN WO2018199738-A1; ID201904563-A; CN110545907-A; CN110545907-B
TI Apparatus for producing synthetic gas from exothermic reaction and endothermic reaction, has pipes which absorb heat released from exothermic reaction in inner chamber.
AU BIN HALIM RASIP A
AE BIN HALIM RASIP A (RASI-Individual)
GA 201885643T
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a generally cylindrical outer chamber (1) which comprises a top opening (11), a bottom discharge outlet (13), a gas outlet (15), and a throat (16) near the bottom discharge outlet. The perforations are provided in side and bottom portions of a cylindrical inner chamber (2). The inner chamber is coaxially mounted within the outer chamber. Multiple interconnected pipes (3) are provided between the inner and outer chambers. Each pipe is included with a top charging inlet (33) and a bottom discharge outlet (34), while having a gas inlet (31) and a gas outlet (32). The inner chamber is charged with carbonaceous materials for the exothermic reaction while the pipes are pre-loaded with carbon-coated pellets for the endothermic reaction. The pipes absorb the heat released from the exothermic reaction in the inner chamber.
   USE - Apparatus for simultaneously accommodating exothermic and endothermic reactions to produce syngas, charcoal, coke, and carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The exothermic and endothermic reactions are simultaneously accommodated in economical and sustainable manner. The carbon footprint is reduced in economical manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The apparatus includes a unit for insulating heat on the outer chamber. A unit for insulating heat on the outer wall of the outer chamber using a heat insulating coating is provided. A pair of electrodes (8) surrounding the inner chamber is provided. A top cover (12) is provided over the top opening of the outer chamber. A slidably-open door or folding door (14) is provided at the bottom discharge outlet of the outer chamber. A stackable container (4) is mounted within the inner chamber, and comprises a perforated base with multiple generally V-shaped cup-like structures for holding pre-carbon-coated pellets. The stackable container is supported at and near the throat of the outer chamber. An ignition ring pipe (5) is provided around the outer chamber, while having connecting pipes extending from the ring to the outer chamber for providing a source of ignition. A steam injection ring (6) is provided around the outer chamber, while having connecting pipes extending from the steam injection ring to the outer chamber for providing steam. The pellets are made from either low or high heat capacity materials. The diameter ratio of the carbon-coated pellets to the pipe is 0.55 - 0.75. The exothermic reaction is performed by combustion of carbonaceous material, and conversion of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for producing synthetic gas, which comprises an apparatus for producing synthetic gas from an exothermic reaction and an endothermic reaction. A power generation facility is provided for generating electricity from the synthetic gas. A unit for separating carbon dioxide from exhaust gas produced by the power generation facility is provided. A unit for collecting synthetic gas from the apparatus for producing synthetic gas, and distributing the synthetic gas to the power generation facility is provided. A unit for collecting the exhaust gas produced by the power generation facility is provided. A unit for collecting the carbon dioxide from the separating unit and distributing the carbon dioxide to the pipes of the apparatus for producing synthetic gas is provided.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the apparatus for producing synthetic gas from exothermic reaction and endothermic reaction.
   Generally cylindrical outer chamber (1)
   Cylindrical inner chamber (2)
   Pipe (3)
   Stackable container (4)
   Ignition ring pipe (5)
   Steam injection ring (6)
   Electrode (8)
   Top opening (11)
   Top cover (12)
   Bottom discharge outlet (13)
   Slidably-open door or folding door (14)
   Gas outlet (15)
   Throat (16)
   Gas inlet (31)
   Gas outlet (32)
   Top charging inlet (33)
   Bottom discharge outlet (34)

PT P
PN WO2018158370-A1; KR2019123759-A; CN110582352-A; EP3589402-A1; US2020062898-A1; JP2020510728-W
TI Preparing polycarbonate ether polyol used to make e.g. polyurethane, comprises reacting carbon dioxide and an epoxide in presence of double metal cyanide catalyst, catalyst of metal complexes and starter compound having substituted alkyls.
AU KEMBER M
   KABIR R
   BLACKBURN A
AE ECONIC TECHNOLOGIES LTD (ECON-Non-standard)
   ECONIC TECHNOLOGIES LTD (ECON-Non-standard)
   ECONIC TECHNOLOGIES LTD (ECON-Non-standard)
   ECONIC TECHNOLOGIES LTD (ECON-Non-standard)
GA 2018704657
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a polycarbonate ether polyol, comprises reacting carbon dioxide and an epoxide in the presence of a double metal cyanide (DMC) catalyst, a catalyst of metal complexes (I), and a starter compound having substituted alkyl compounds (II), where the DMC catalyst contains at least two metal centers, cyanide ligands, a first complexing agent and a second complexing agent, and the first complexing agent is a polymer.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a polycarbonate ether polyol, where the polycarbonate ether polyol is useful for preparing polyurethane or other higher polymer (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids hydrating the DMC by introducing water molecules to any further processing steps e.g. washing and filtration using anhydrous solvents; and prepares polyols having a high ratio of m/(n+m) under mild conditions e.g. 10 bar or below.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a polycarbonate ether polyol, comprises reacting carbon dioxide and an epoxide in the presence of a double metal cyanide (DMC) catalyst, a catalyst of metal complexes of formula (I), and a starter compound substituted alkyl compounds of formula (II), where the DMC catalyst contains at least two metal centers, cyanide ligands, a first complexing agent and a second complexing agent and the first complexing agent is a polymer.
   M1, M2 = Zn(ll), Cr(ll), Co(ll), Cu(ll), Mn(ll), Mg(ll), Ni(ll), Fe(ll), Ti(ll), V(ll), Cr(lll)-X, Co(lll)-X, Mn(lll)-X, Ni(lll)-X, Fe(lll)-X, Ca(ll), Ge(ll), Al(lll)-X, Ti(lll)-X, V(lll)-X, Ge(IV)-(X)2 or Ti(IV)-(X)2;
   R1, R2 = alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl, haloalkyl, aryl, heteroaryl, alkoxy, aryloxy, alkylthio, arylthio, alicyclic or heteroalicyclic (all optionally substituted), H, halide, NO2, nitrile, imine, NH2, ether, silyl, silyl ether, sulfoxide, sulfonyl, sulfinate or acetylide;
   R3 = alkylene, alkenylene, heteroalkylene, heteroalkenylene, heteroalkynylene (all optionally substituted by aryl, heteroaryl, alicyclic or heteroalicyclic), alkynylene, arylene, heteroarylene or cycloalkylene;
   R5 = aliphatic, heteroaliphatic, alicyclic, heteroalicyclic, aryl, heteroaryl, alkylheteroaryl, alkylaryl (all optionally substituted) or H;
   E1 = C or N;
   E2 = O, S or NH;
   E3-E6 = N, NR4, O or S, when E3-E6 are N, the broken bond is double bond and when E3-E6 are NR4, O or S, the broken bond is single bond;
   R4 = aliphatic, heteroaliphatic, alicyclic, heteroalicyclic, aryl, heteroaryl, alkylheteroaryl, -alkyl-C(O)OR19, -alkyl-CN or -alkylaryl;
   X = aliphatic, heteroaliphatic, alicyclic, heteroalicyclic, aryl, heteroaryl (all optionally substituted), OC(O)R1x, OSO2R1x, OSOR1x, OSO(R1x)2, S(O)R1x, OR1x, phosphinate, halide, nitrate, hydroxy, carbonate, amino or amido, where each X may be the same or different and X may form a bridge between M1 and M2;
   R1x = optionally substituted aliphatic, haloaliphatic, heteroaliphatic, alicyclic, heteroalicyclic, aryl, alkylaryl, heteroaryl (all optionally substituted) or H;
   G = absent or neutral or anionic donor ligand which is a Lewis base;
   Z = alkylene, alkenylene, alkynylene, heteroalkylene, heteroalkenylene, heteroalkynylene, cycloalkylene, cycloalkenylene, hererocycloalkylene, heterocycloalkenylene, arylene, heteroarylene (all optionally substituted), or alkylarylene, heteroalkylarylene, heteroalkylheteroarylene and/or alkylheteroarylene;
   a = at least 2; and
   R1z = -OH, -NHR1a, -SH, -C(O)OH, PR1a(O)(OH)2, -P(O)(OR1a)(OH) or -PR1a(O)OH, preferably R1z -OH, -C(O)OH or -NHR1a.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a polymerization system for the co-polymerization of carbon dioxide and an epoxide, comprising the catalyst, the DMC catalyst and the starter compound;
   (2) a polyol prepared by the method; and
   (3) a polyurethane or other higher polymer prepared from a polycarbonate ether polyol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first and second metal centers of the DMC catalyst are represented by M1 and M2 respectively, where M1 comprises zinc(ll), iron(ll), nickel(ll) (all preferred), cobalt(II), ruthenium(ll), ruthenium(lll), manganese(ll), molybdenum(II), tin(ll), lead(ll), iron(lll), molybdenum(IV), molybdenum(Vl), aluminum(lll), vanadium(V), vanadium(Vl), strontium(ll), tungsten(IV), tungsten(Vl), copper(ll) or chromium(lll) and M2 comprises cobalt(II), cobalt(III), iron(ll), iron(lll), chromium(ll), iridium(lll), chromium(lll), nickel(ll) (all preferred), manganese(ll), manganese(lll), rhodium(lll), ruthenium(ll), vanadium(IV) or vanadium(V).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The DMC catalyst comprises 5-80 (preferably 20-50) wt.% of the first complexing agent. The second complexing agent comprises alcohols, ethers (both preferred), ketones, esters, amides, or ureas. The alcohol comprises methanol, ethanol, isopropyl alcohol, n-butyl alcohol, isobutyl alcohol, sec-butyl alcohol, (m)ethoxy ethylene glycol, buten-1-ol, 2-methyl-3-buten-2-ol, 2-methyl-3-butyn-2-ol, 3-methyl-1-pentyn-3-ol or propylene glycol, preferably tert-butyl alcohol. The ether comprises dimethoxyethane (preferred), ethylene glycol monomethyl ether, diglyme or triglyme. The DMC catalyst contains a further complexing agent. (I) have a symmetric macrocyclic ligand or asymmetric macrocyclic ligand. (I) comprises 26 aromatic metal complexes e.g. aromatic metal complexes of formula (III)-(VII). The starter compound comprises diols e.g. 1,2-ethanediol (ethylene glycol), 1-2-propanediol, 1,3-propanediol (propylene glycol), 1,2-butanediol, 1-3-butanediol, 1,4-butanediol, 1,5-pentanediol, 1,6-hexanediol, 1,8-octanediol, 1,10-decanediol, 1,4-cyclohexanediol, 1,2-diphenol, 1,3-diphenol, 1,4-diphenol, neopentyl glycol, catechol, cyclohexenediol, 1,4-cyclohexanedimethanol, dipropylene glycol, diethylene glycol, tripropylene glycol, triethylene glycol, tetraethylene glycol, polypropylene glycols (PPGs) or polyethylene glycols (PEGs) having an number average molecular weight of up to 1500 g/mol e.g. PPG 425, PPG 725, PPG 1000, triols e.g. glycerol, benzenetriol, 1,2,4-butanetriol, 1,2,6-hexanetriol, tris(methylalcohol)propane, tris(methylalcohol)ethane, tris(methylalcohol)nitropropane, trimethylol propane, polypropylene oxide triols and polyester triols, tetraols e.g. calix(4)arene, 2,2-bis(methylalcohol)-1 ,3-propanediol, erythritol, pentaerythritol or polyalkylene glycols (PEGs or PPG5) having 4-hydroxy groups, polyols e.g. sorbitol or polyalkylene glycols (PEGs or PPG5) having at least 5-hydroxy groups, diacids e.g. oxalic acid, malonic acid, succinic acid, glutaric acid, adipic acid, pimelic acid, suberic acid, azelaic acid, sebacic acid, undecanedioic acid, dodecanedioic acid or other compounds having mixed functional groups e.g. lactic acid, glycolic acid, 3-hydroxypropanoic acid, 4-hydroxybutanoic acid or 5-hydroxypentanoic acid, preferably 1,6-hexanediol, 1,4-cyclohexanedimethanol, 1,12-dodecanediol, poly(caprolactone)diol, PPG-425, PPG-725, or PPG-1000. The first complexing agent comprises 5-80 (preferably 20-50) wt.% in the DMC catalyst.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The reaction is carried out at a pressure of 1-60 (preferably 1-10) bar carbon dioxide. The reaction is carried out at a temperature of 50-110 degrees C, preferably 55-80 degrees C. The DMC catalyst is prepared by treating a solution of a metal salt with a solution of a metal cyanide salt in the presence of the first and the second complexing agent, preferably the metal salt comprises substituted metal salts (M1(X1)pX1) (IA), the metal cyanide salt comprises substituted the metal cyanide salt ((Y1)qM2(CN)b(A)c) (IB). The DMC catalyst comprises substituted metal cyanides (M1d(M2e(CN)f)g) (IC). The DMC catalyst additionally comprises water, an acid and/or a metal salt. The DM0 catalyst comprises substituted metal cyanides (M1d(M2e(CN)f)g).hM3X2i.jR1c.kH2O. IHrX3.Pol (ID).X1 = anion comprising halide, hydroxide, sulfate, carbonate, cyanide, oxalate, thiocyanate, isocyanate, isothiocyanate, carboxylate or nitrate;p = 1 or more and the charge on the anion multiplied by p satisfies the valence of M1a;Y1 = proton, alkali metal ion or alkaline earth metal ion (e.g. K+);A = anion comprising halide, hydroxide, oxide, sulfate, cyanide oxalate, thiocyanate, isocyanate, isothiocyanate, carboxylate or nitrate;q, b = 1 or more;c = 0 or 1 or more, where the sum of the charges on the anions Y1, CN and A multiplied by q, b and c respectively (e.g. Yx q + CNx b + Ax c) satisfies the valence of M2;d-g = integers and are chosen to so that the DMC catalyst has electroneutrality, preferably, d is3, e is 1, f is 6 and g is 2;M3 = M1 and/or M2;X2 = anion comprising halide (preferred), hydroxide, oxide, sulfate, carbonate, cyanide, oxalate, thiocyanate, isocyanate, isothiocyanate, carboxylate or nitrate;i = 1 or more and the charge on the anion X2 multiplied by i satisfies the valency of M2;X3 = anion comprising halide (preferred), sulfate, phosphate, borate, chlorate, carbonate, cyanide, oxalate, thiocyanate, isocyanate, isothiocyanate, carboxylate or nitrate;r = integer that corresponds to the charge on the counterion X3;h = 0-4;j = 0.1-6;k = 0-20;I = 0-5;R1c = second complexing agent; andPol = first complexing agent which is a polymer.Preferred Components: The polymer comprises a polyether, a polycarbonate ether, or a polycarbonate. The polymer is a polyether having a molecular weight of 1000-10000 Daltons, preferably the polyether is a polypropylene glycol (PPG) e.g. a PPG polyol, where the PPG has a molecular weight of 250-8000 (preferably 400-4000) Daltons. The second complexing agent is tert-butyl alcohol and the polymer is a polyether, preferably the polyether is PPG polyol, where the PPG has a molecular weight of 250-8000 (preferably 400-4000) Daltons.
   Preferred Definitions:
   M1 and/or M2 = Mg(ll), Zn(ll), or Ni(ll);
   E3-E6 = NR4, where at least one of E3-E6 is different to the remaining occurrence(s) of E3-E6;
   R4 = H or alkyl;
   E1 = C;
   E2 = O;
   R5, R2 = H;
   R3 = optionally substituted 2-3C alkylene; and
   R1 = alkyl, alkenyl, aryl, heteroaryl, silyl, silyl ether, alkoxy, aryloxy or alkylthio (all optionally substituted), H, halide, NH2, NO2, sulfoxide, sulfonyl or sulfinate;
   R1z = -OH; and
   a = 2-6.
   EXAMPLE - 5 mg Double metal cyanide catalyst was taken into a 100 ml oven dried reactor along with 0.78 ml of propoxylate glycerol. The catalyst and starter were dried at 110-120 degrees C under vacuum for 1-2 hours. The reactor was cooled down to room temperature and 15 ml of propylene oxide solution of 45 mg of nickel aromatic catalyst of formula (Ia) was injected into the vessel via a syringe under continuous flow of carbon dioxide gas. The vessel was heated to 70 degrees C and filled to 5 bar carbon dioxide pressure. The reaction was continued for 16 hours. Once the reaction was finished, the reactor was cooled to below 10 degrees C and the pressure was released to obtain polycarbonate ether polyol.

TI Measuring natural gas calorific value based on Raman spectroscopy involves carrying out acquisition and pretreatment on natural gas-related pure-component Raman spectrum and establishing natural gas calorific value Raman analysis model.
AU DAI L
   WEN G
   CHEN Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201868097X
AB    NOVELTY - Measuring natural gas calorific value based on Raman spectroscopy involves: (1) carrying out acquisition and pretreatment on a natural gas-related pure-component Raman spectrum by (1.1) obtaining the original spectrum of pure natural gas components such as methane, ethane, propene, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, hydrogen and carbon monoxide and (1.2) performing background spectral subtraction, baseline correction and area normalization on the original spectrum of the pure component to obtain a pure component normalized spectrum; (2) establishing a natural gas calorific value Raman analysis model; and (3) obtaining the original spectrum of the natural gas sample to be measured for calorific value and the area ratio of the spectral area of each component to the methane spectrum, and taking the area ratio as input and then substituting into the established natural gas calorific value Raman analysis model to obtain the calorific value of the sample.
   USE - The method is useful for measuring natural gas calorific value based on Raman spectroscopy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of rapidly measuring the natural gas calorific value based on Raman spectroscopy with high precision, and good repeatability and sample-field-free maintenance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Measuring natural gas calorific value based on Raman spectroscopy involves: (1) carrying out acquisition and pretreatment on a natural gas-related pure-component Raman spectrum by (1.1) obtaining the original spectrum of pure natural gas components such as methane, ethane, propene, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, hydrogen and carbon monoxide and (1.2) performing background spectral subtraction, baseline correction and area normalization on the original spectrum of the pure component to obtain a pure component normalized spectrum; (2) establishing a natural gas calorific value Raman analysis model by (2.1) preparing a number of typical natural gas mixed sample gas as training sample gas to obtain a calorific value of each training sample gas and performing Raman spectroscopy measurement on the training sample gas to obtain the original spectrum of the training sample gas, (2.2) performing background spectral subtraction, baseline correction and area normalization on the original spectrum of the training sample gas to obtain a normalized spectrum of the training sample gas, (2.3) representing hydrocarbon components other than methane, ethane, propene, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, hydrogen and carbon monoxide in the training sample gas as special component tetravalent carbon and decomposing the normalized spectrum of training sample gas into a weighted sum of known pure component normalized spectrum and N peak functions according to an equation (1) as given in the specification, (2.4) calculating the spectral area of the pure components such as methane, ethane, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, hydrogen and carbon monoxide in the modeled area by using an equation (2) as given in the specification and (2.5) establishing a natural gas calorific value Raman analysis model based on the calorific value of all training samples and the relative spectral area of each component according to an equation (3) as given in the specification; and (3) obtaining the original spectrum of the natural gas sample to be measured for calorific value and the area ratio of the spectral area of each component to the methane spectrum according to steps (2.2)-(2.4), and taking the area ratio as input and then substituting into the established natural gas calorific value Raman analysis model to obtain the calorific value of the sample.

(FLR) is greater than or equal to 30% and the functional liver reserve is not reduced by the results of three tests of C-reactive protein (CRP) is greater than or equal to 1,6, R15 is lesser than 10, type (ALPPS) associating liver partition and portal vein ligation for staged hepatectomy or p-ALPPS or if the remaining volume of the liver is insufficient FLR is less than or equal to 30%, the functional liver reserves are reduced by the results of three tests of CRP lesser than 1.6, R15 greater than 10, 13-carbon dioxide integral in the exhaled air by the 120th minute is less than 20%, palliative techniques are feasible of radiofrequency thermoablation, microwave ablation, laser ablation or cryo-destruction.

TI Making formate or formic acid used to make            methanol by forming bicarbonate solution of bicarbonate            from mixture of carbon dioxide and carbonate, ammonia            and/or hydroxide, and treating bicarbonate solution by            reaction with metal catalyst.
AU MELIN K
   HURSKAINEN M
   KAISALO N
   SIMELL P
AE VALTION TEKNILLINEN TUTKIMUSKESKUS (VALW-C)
GA 2022747190
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a formate or formic acid from carbon                dioxide, comprises: forming a bicarbonate solution                (5) comprising at least bicarbonate from a mixture                (2) which comprises at least carbon dioxide and                further comprises carbonate, ammonia and/or                hydroxide (3); and treating the bicarbonate                solution by a reaction with a metal catalyst in a                reactor (8), in which hydrogen and/or carbon                monoxide (6) is supplied to the reactor and                temperature is 10-70 degrees C and pressure is                1-100 bar during the reaction, in order to produce                the formate, and optionally producing the formic                acid from the formate.
   USE - The method is useful for producing formate or                formic acid from carbon dioxide, which is used in:                a treatment of captured carbon dioxide; a                production of a chemical or liquid for using as a                carrier of carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen; and/or a                production of methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, easy, and energy- and                cost-effective; utilizes captured carbon dioxide to                produce formate or formic acid; and offers a                possibility to produce the formates and formic                acids with good properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an                apparatus for producing formate or formic acid from                carbon dioxide, comprising at least one equipment                into a mixture comprising at least carbon dioxide                and further comprising carbonate, ammonia and/or                hydroxide, which is subjected for forming a                bicarbonate solution comprising at least                bicarbonate, and at least one reactor in which the                bicarbonate solution is treated by a reaction with                a metal catalyst at temperature of 10-70 degrees C                and pressure of 1-100 bar and at least one feeding                device for supplying the hydrogen and/or carbon                monoxide into the reactor in order to produce the                formate, and optionally to produce the formic acid                from the formate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a flow chart process.
   Mixture (2)
   Carbonate, ammonia and/or hydroxide                (3)
   Bicarbonate solution (5)
   Hydrogen and/or carbon monoxide (6)
   Reactor (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The apparatus                  comprises at least one capture device or ejector                  for capturing the carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide is                  captured, and the mixture is formed. The carbon                  dioxide is captured from a gas comprising carbon                  dioxide. The gas comprising carbon dioxide is                  treated in at least one ejector stage for                  capturing the carbon dioxide, where a motive                  medium is injected to at least one ejector of the                  ejector stage, the gas is sucked into the same                  ejector in which the gas is mixed with the motive                  medium for forming a blend of the gas and motive                  medium, and the mixture is formed from the blend                  of the gas and motive medium or from a liquid                  phase of the blend. The carbonate, ammonia and/or                  hydroxide is added to the mixture. The hydrogen,                  carbon monoxide, synthesis gas, gas comprising                  carbon monoxide and/or gas comprising carbon                  monoxide and hydrogen is supplied to the reactor.                  The temperature is 15-50 degrees C during the                  reaction in the reactor. The pressure is 20-60                  bar during the reaction in the reactor. A strong                  acid is added to the formate, and the formate is                  treated in order to form the formic acid. A                  formed salt product produced as a by-product in                  the production of the formic acid is decomposed                  by a heat treatment or by heating back to a                  strong acid. The formed strong acid is recycled                  to the production of the formic acid. Preferred                  Components: The bicarbonate solution contains the                  bicarbonate and a solvent. The solvent of the                  bicarbonate solution comprises water and                  ethanol.
   EXAMPLE - Carbon dioxide was captured and mixed with                sodium carbonate to form a mixture which was an                aqueous solution. In the mixture carbon dioxide                reacts with the sodium carbonate to form sodium                bicarbonate, and bicarbonate solution comprised of                the sodium bicarbonate was formed. The bicarbonate                solution was supplied to a reactor. Further,                hydrogen was supplied to the reactor. In the                reactor, the bicarbonate solution was hydrogenated                with a metal catalyst at 20-50 degrees C and in                pressure of 30-60 bar for forming the sodium                formate. Further, the formic acid was formed from                the sodium formate. A phosphoric acid was added to                the sodium formate, and the sodium formate was                treated with the phosphoric acid by decomposing the                sodium formate to the formic acid and sodium                phosphate.

PN WO2022056589-A1
TI Aqueous solvent solution that absorbs carbon dioxide and produces a bicarbonate, comprises at least one base that is dissolved in water, and catalytic compound, which is alkali salt of an N-substituted amino acid, dissolved in water.
AU HOOPER B
   STEVENS G
   OSBORNE C
AE UNO TECHNOLOGY PTY LTD (UNOT-Non-standard)
GA 202242959V
AB    NOVELTY - An aqueous solvent solution that absorbs carbon dioxide and produces a bicarbonate, comprises at least one base that is dissolved in water; and catalytic compound dissolved in water. Catalytic compound is an alkali salt of an N-substituted amino acid that reacts with carbon dioxide to form a catalytic intermediate having a carbamate group, and catalytic intermediate reacts with a base which deprotonates the carbamate and undergoes hydrolysis which removes a carbonate group from the catalytic intermediate and yields a bicarbonate precipitant.
   USE - An aqueous solvent solution is useful for absorbing carbon dioxide and producing a bicarbonate (claimed), where the carbon dioxide is from a gas stream including a pre-combustion gas stream such as a natural gas stream, a syngas stream that is either pre-combustion or post combustion, and a flue gas stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The aqueous solvent solution is capable of absorbing carbon dioxide and producing a bicarbonate with high reaction kinetics. It can be used repeatedly in this cycle and within the absorption mixture thus maintaining high rates of absorption as the mixture becomes loaded with carbon dioxide. It requires little or no heating to regenerate the amino acid derivative in the cycle and during the regeneration of the solvent solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for absorbing carbon dioxide from a gas stream which involves contacting gas stream and aqueous solvent solution in an absorber vessel so that carbon dioxide in the gas stream reacts with the amino acid derivative of the solvent solution and adding a carbonate group to the amino group of the amino acid; derivative to form a catalytic intermediate having a carbamate, where the absorbing step is carried out under alkaline conditions so that the catalytic intermediate deprotonates and the carbamate group undergoes hydrolysis which produces a bicarbonate and regenerates the amino acid derivative; and precipitating bicarbonate as an alkali bicarbonate precipitant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The at least one base dissolved in water is an alkali metal of varying levels of carbonation. The at least one base dissolved in water is potassium alkali including potassium hydroxide having a zero level of carbonation, potassium carbonate having a 2:1 ratio of carbonation, and potassium bicarbonate having a 1:1 ratio of carbonation. Solvent solution has a pH of greater than or equal to 8, preferably 9-13.5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The base deprotonates the carbamate of the intermediate catalyst and which then undergoes hydrolysis to hydrolyze the carbonate group, yielding bicarbonate and reforming the catalytic compound. The base is at a high concentration in the aqueous solution so that the bicarbonate precipitates as a salt with the base. The base for the catalytic compound is of formula M+-OOC(CHR2)HNR1 (I). The catalytic compound is N-alkyl amino acid, where R1 is an alkyl group and R2 is any group having at least one C atom. The catalytic compound is N-methyl amino acid, where R1 is a methyl group and R2 is any group having at least one C atom. The catalytic compound is N-methyl glycine in which R1 is a methyl group and R2 is H. The catalytic compound is N-methyl alanine, in which R2 is a methyl group and R1 is a methyl group. The catalytic compound is n-methyl serine, in which R2 is a methyl group and R1 includes a methyl hydroxyl (MeOH) group.R1=group having at least one C atom;R2=H or group having at least one C atom; andM+=alkali metal.Preferred Processes: The process includes a temperature control step that includes cooling the solvent solution during the absorbing step. The temperature control step includes either one or a combination of: cooling the solvent solution in situ in an absorbing vessel during the absorbing step; and/or withdrawing a side-stream of the solvent solution from absorbing step, cooling the side stream, and returning the side-stream back to the absorbing step. The process includes providing the solvent solution with adequate base to deprotonate the amino acid derivative. The process includes monitoring the pH of the solvent solution at a level greater than 8, preferably 9.5-13.5. The process includes adjusting the pH of the solvent solution by adding an alkali metal base. The process includes a separating step, in which the alkali bicarbonate precipitant is separated from the solvent solution, and the solvent solution is recycled back to the absorbing step. The process includes a desorbing step in which bicarbonate is converted to carbonate using a heat source and, forming a gas stream rich in carbon dioxide. The gas stream is a post combustion gas stream. The process includes a temperature controlling step that controls the temperature of the absorbing step to 40-95 degrees C. The process includes a temperature control step that controls the temperature of the desorbing step to 70-170 degrees C. Absorbing step is carried out at a pressure of 100-1000 kPa absolute. Absorbing step is carried out at a pressure of 100-150 kPa absolute. The gas stream is a high pressure gas stream including, but not limited to, a pre-combustion gas stream, and the absorbing step is carried out at a pressure ranging from 1000-8000 kPa absolute, and suitably at a pressure of 2500-6500 kPa absolute. Solvent solution has potassium carbonate in the range of 10 to 80 wt.%, suitably 20 to 70 wt.%, and suitably 30 to 60 wt.%.

PN CN113980200-A
TI Preparing solid polycarboxylate water reducing agent useful in mortar and concrete, comprises e.g. converting carbon dioxide gas into supercritical carbon dioxide, heating unsaturated polyether large monomer, melting, transferring to supercritical carbon dioxide, heating, slicing or grinding.
AU GAO C
   WANG H
   GUO J
   HUANG Y
   GAO R
AE CHINA BUILDING MATERIALS ACAD (CHNB-C)
GA 2022232492
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing solid polycarboxylate water reducing agent by using supercritical carbon dioxide as solvent comprises converting carbon dioxide gas into supercritical carbon dioxide under the pressure of 10-30MPa, heating the unsaturated polyether large monomer, melting into the state of liquid, transferring to supercritical carbon dioxide, adding initiator, unsaturated acid, anhydride small monomer and molecular weight regulator, mechanically stirring, heating to 35-80degrees Celsius, controlling the rotating speed at 100-400 revolutions/minute, thermally insulating, reacting for 2-6 hours, finishing reaction, vacuum extracting, adding neutralizing agent, introducing carbon dioxide gas, controlling the pressure and temperature, stirring uniformly, adjusting the pH of the mixed solution is 5-7, vacuum extracting, cooling to room temperature, slicing or grinding to obtain solid polycarboxylate water reducing agent.
   USE - The solid polycarboxylate water reducing agent is useful in mortar and concrete (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses supercritical carbon dioxide fluid as reaction medium, has no toxic and harmful organic solvents, has simple and convenient process, reduces cost, and solves problems of high material viscosity, difficult heat dissipation and gelation in the preparation process of traditional solid polycarboxylate water reducer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   The relative molecular mass of the solid polycarboxylate water reducing agent is 20000-50000g/mol;
   m or n = 50-120.
   k = 0-12; and
   h = 0-10;
   g = 0-10;
   f = 0-15;
   e = 0-20;
   d = 0-10;
   c = 0-40;
   b = 0-40;
   a = 0-100;
   M = sodium ion or potassium ion;
   mortar, comprising solid polycarboxylate water reducing agent; and
   concrete, comprising solid polycarboxylate water reducing agent.
   (1) solid polycarboxylate water reducing agent having molecular formula represented by (C2H3COOM)a(C3H5COOM)b(C2H3CONHM)c(C7H13NO4SM)d(C5H9O(C2H4O)mH)e(C4H7O(C2H4O)mH)f(C4H8O2(C2H4O)mH)g(C5H9O(C2H4O)m(C3H6O)nH)h(C4H7O(C2H4O)m(C3H6O)nH)k (I).
   (2)
   (3)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The mass ratio of the unsaturated polyether macromonomer, unsaturated acid or acid anhydride small monomer, initiator, molecular weight regulator and neutralizing agent is 1: (0.13-0.26): (0.002-0.005) (0.005-0.01 ): (0.01-0.02). In the method, the mass ratio of the supercritical liquid carbon dioxide to the unsaturated polyether macromonomer is 0.5-2:1. The water reducing rate of the solid polycarboxylate water reducing agent is not greater than 30%.Preferred Components: The unsaturated polyether macromonomer is at least one of isopentenyl ether, isopentenol polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene ether, methyl allyl polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene ether, methyl allyl polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene ether and vinyl glycol ether-based polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene ether. The relative molecular mass of the unsaturated polyether macromonomer is 2000-5000g/mol. The unsaturated acid or acid anhydride small monomer is at least one of acrylic acid, maleic acid, fumaric acid, maleic anhydride, fumaric anhydride, methacrylic acid and 2-acrylamide-2-methylpropanesulfonic acid. The initiator is at least one of ammonium persulfate, dicyclohexyl peroxide dicarbonate, dibenzoyl peroxide and azobisisobutyronitrile. The molecular weight regulator is at least one of mercaptoethanol, mercaptoacetic acid and mercaptopropionic acid. The neutralizing agent is at least one of sodium hydroxide and potassium hydroxide. The mortar comprises cementitious material, sand, mineral admixture, solid polycarboxylate superplasticizer and water in the ratio of (200-600kg/m3): (700-1200kg/m3): (0-300kg /m3): (0.2-2.0kg/m3): (120-300kg/m3), and the cementitious material is at least one of cement, gypsum and lime. The concrete comprises cement, sand, crushed stone, mineral admixture, solid polycarboxylate water reducing agent and water in the ratio (200-600kg/m3): (700-1200kg/m3): (900- 1400kg/m3): (0-300kg/m3): (0.2-2kg/m3): (120-300kg/m3).

PT P
PN WO2021076672-A1; WO2021076672-A8
TI Synthesizing porous polymeric carbon sorbent, used            for direct air capture of carbon dioxide comprises            mixing polymer with additive to form mixture, and            heating mixture to temperature.
AU TOUR J M
   ALGOZEEB W
   SAVAS P E
   KITTRELL C
AE UNIV RICE WILLIAM MARSH (RICV-C)
GA 202141739D
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing (m1) a porous polymeric carbon                sorbent, comprises: (a) mixing a polymer with an                additive to form a mixture, where the polymer has a                polymer chain order comprising order and short                range order as detected by powder X-ray diffraction                (XRD), and the additive is an activation reagent                comprising metal hydroxide, metal oxalate, metal                acetate, and/or metal acetyl acetonate; and (b)                heating the mixture to a temperature to form a                porous polymeric carbon sorbent via chemical                activation, where the chemical activation results                in a loss of the order and the short range order of                the polymer as detected by powder XRD so that the                porous polymeric carbon sorbent material is a rigid                porous polymeric carbon sorbent, and the porous                polymeric carbon sorbent is operable for direct air                capturing of at least 4 wt.% carbon dioxide at room                temperature and 4 mbar partial pressure of carbon                dioxide in air.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing a porous                polymeric carbon sorbent (claimed) for carbon                dioxide capture for flue gas from power plants and                for gases from other post-combustion carbon dioxide                emission outlets, for gas storage, and for direct                air capture of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: eliminates the risk forming                potassium metal, (potassium is flammable and can                even be explosive upon contact with air or water),                which is one of the biggest challenges standing                before commercializing the synthesis of porous                carbon; and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   a rigid porous polymeric carbon sorbent i.e.                operable to direct air capture more than 4 wt.%                carbon dioxide at room temperature and 4 mbar                partial pressure of carbon dioxide in air;                and
   ynthesizing (m2) a porous polymeric carbon                sorbent comprising (a1) selecting a rigid porous                polymeric carbon sorbent, and (b1) utilizing the                rigid porous polymeric carbon sorbent to direct air                capture more than 4 wt.% carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The activation                  reagent: is an acetate salt of potassium or                  calcium; is potassium acetate; and is calcium                  acetate. The polymer comprises nitrogen                  atoms.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of heating is                  performed at a temperature of 550-700&#176;                  C, preferably 575-600&#176; C. Weight ratio                  of the additive and the polymer in the mixture is                  0.5:1-5:1, preferably 2:1-4:1. The porous                  polymeric carbon sorbent: is operable for                  releasing the carbon dioxide direct air captured                  by heating the porous polymeric carbon sorbent to                  at most 200&#176; C, preferably 75&#176;                  C; is operable for releasing the carbon dioxide                  direct air captured by heating the rigid porous                  polymeric carbon sorbent to at most 100&#176;                  C; is operable for releasing the carbon dioxide                  direct air captured by removing the carbon                  dioxide with vacuum; is operable for direct air                  capturing of at least 6 wt.% carbon dioxide at 4                  mbar partial pressure of carbon dioxide in air;                  is operable for releasing the carbon dioxide                  direct air captured by heating the rigid porous                  polymeric carbon sorbent to at most 110&#176;                  C; and is operable for releasing the carbon                  dioxide direct air captured by removing the                  carbon dioxide with vacuum. The method further                  comprises controlling pore size of the porous                  polymeric carbon sorbent by controlling pressure                  during the step of heating. The step of heating                  the mixture is performed at a near vacuum                  pressure of at least 0.01 bars. In the method                  (m2) the method further comprises: releasing the                  direct air captured carbon dioxide by heating the                  rigid porous polymeric carbon sorbent; releasing                  the direct air captured carbon dioxide by                  applying a vacuum to the rigid porous polymeric                  carbon sorbent; and repeating the direct air                  capture and release of the carbon dioxide by the                  porous polymeric carbon sorbent for at least 1000                  cycles. The carbon dioxide is released by heating                  the rigid porous polymeric carbon sorbent to at                  most 200&#176; C, preferably 100&#176; C.                  Preferred Components: The rigid porous polymeric                  carbon sorbent comprises a polymer that has no                  polymeric order and no polymeric short range                  order detectable by powder X-ray diffraction                  (XRD).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer comprises                  high density polyethylene (HDPE), low density                  polyethylene (LDPE), and/or polypropylene (PP);                  PVC, nylon, melamine mixed with HDPE, melamine                  mixed with LDPE, melamine mixed with PP,                  melamine-formaldehyde resins, polyethylene imine                  (PEI), PEI mixed with HDPE, polyurethanes (PU),                  polyacrylonitrile, (PAN), and/or                  polyethyleneteraphthalte (PET); and a plastic                  from a waste plastic source.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AE CHINA PETROLEUM ENG & CONSTR CORP (CNPC-C)
GA 2020B2168K
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an upstream trunk line (1) and a downstream trunk line (3) for connecting upstream and downstream pipelines to form dense phase carbon dioxide transmission channels. A trunk line cutoff valve (2) controls a communication or cutoff between the upstream trunk line and the downstream trunk line. A pipeline bypassing valve chamber is connected to the upstream trunk line of the trunk line cutoff valve. A valve chamber bypass system provides a maintenance venting bypass of the upstream trunk line and the downstream trunk line after the trunk line is cut off and provides a connecting bypass when the upstream trunk line or the downstream trunk line is ready to restart. Two-stage relief system utilizes staged pressure relief of dense phase carbon dioxide. An inter-stage heat exchange system is provided in the two-stage relief system and adjusts a temperature of a medium discharged from an upper stage.
   USE - Dense phase carbon dioxide pipeline safety discharge system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system combines conventional oil pipeline maintenance and release schemes, considers the characteristics of low temperature solidification of dense phase carbon dioxide, effectively avoids the risk of dry ice formation in the emptying system of dense-phase carbon dioxide pipelines by setting up two-stage relief system, inter-stage heat exchange system, main line temperature detection system and control system, effectively ensures the material safety of the main line discharge pipelines, and realizes safe discharge of dense-phase carbon dioxide pipelines.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a dense phase carbon dioxide pipeline safety discharging method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a dense phase carbon dioxide pipeline safety discharge system.

   Downstream trunk line (3)

GA 201833774M

The carbon dioxide tail gas is from a thermal power plant, a cement plant, or a waste incineration plant. The recycled aggregate comprises coarse aggregate and fine aggregate.
   USE - The concrete is useful for carbon dioxide to preserve recycled aggregates (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The concrete utilizes carbon dioxide curing to treat recycled aggregates and improves the mechanical properties and durability of recycled aggregates, increases structural concrete of utilization of recycled aggregates and reduces emissions of greenhouse gases.

The carbon dioxide tail gas is from a thermal power plant, a cement plant, or a waste incineration plant. The recycled aggregate comprises coarse aggregate and fine aggregate. The coarse aggregate comprises recycled coarse aggregate, natural gravel, pebbles and artificial lightweight aggregates, the fine aggregate comprises river sand, washed sea sand, quartz sand and artificially manufactured various kinds of fine sand, and the admixture comprises kaolin powder, silica fume, filled fine powder and fly ash, and where weight ratio of water and cement is 0.3-0.6: 1 and total amount of cement and additives is 300-600 kg/m3. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing concrete, comprising (i) placing recycled aggregate in a closed and maintained box with a certain pressure and maintaining carbon dioxide gas for a certain period of time to obtain treated aggregate; (ii) mixing the cement, treated recycled aggregate, water, admixtures and mineral additives in proportion and stirring to obtain the concrete mixture; and (iii) passing uniformly mixed mixture into the construction site for pouring.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The artificially manufactured various kinds of fine sand comprises glass sand and crushed stone sand, and the filled fine powder comprises limestone powder and titanium white powder. AE ANGANG STEEL CO LTD (ANSH-C)
GA 2022966233
AB    NOVELTY - Improving sintering ignition quality comprises                dividing sintering area grid corresponding to                ignition furnace corresponding to the ignition                furnace from head of sintered machine to grid area                corresponding to No. 1-3 air box, dividing grid of                the ignition device corresponding to grid of                ignition device, symmetrically dividing horizontal                distance between trolley walls at two sides and                trolley central line into 2-6 sub-areas, adjusting                corresponding area ignition system by ignition                control system, calculating ignition effect index                of each grid area corresponding to sintered                material to reach best ignition effect, where                carbon monoxide waste gas, carbon dioxide waste gas                and oxygen waste gas are the mass fractions of                carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and oxygen in the                sintering flue gas in the corresponding area,                calculating difference between ignition effect                index of grid area and standard ignition index, and                adjusting corresponding area ignition system.
   USE - The method is useful for improving sintering                ignition quality.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing sintering ignition                fuel consumption, improves the sintering index and                improves the ore quality. The method allows the                ignition control system to automatically adjust the                ignition burner air-fuel ratio corresponding to the                grid area, so that the ignition effect uniformity                detection system and analysis method can accurately                analyze the different area sintering material                ignition effect, provides key parameter for sinter                ignition system adjustment, and realizes the                sintering ignition quality in efficient                manner.

   JIN Y
   HUANG L
   TANG X

   UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 202296973K
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an oil-gas separating module                for separating oil and gas mixture based on                dissolution equilibrium, where separated and mixed                gases are entered into a photoacoustic cell of a                photoacoustic module and mixed gas in the                photoacoustic cell absorbs energy of a modulated                light source generating acoustic wave. An intensity                signal is entered to a data analysis module through                an acquisition module. The data analysis module is                used for correcting concentration of each gas in                the oil-gas mixture in a transformer. The data                analysis module utilizes a photoacoustic                spectroscopy to solve six characteristic gases in                transformer oil based on corresponding                photoacoustic intensities of the six characteristic                gases at characteristic spectral lines, where the                six characteristic gases are carbon monoxide,                carbon dioxide, methane, ethane, ethylene and                acetylene.
   USE - Detecting system for detecting gas in                transformer oil in an electric device online                detection field. Uses include but are not limited                to detect methane, ethane, ethylene, acetylene,                carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has high anti-interference ability,                and improves precision of a gas detection result in                photoacoustic spectroscopy oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for detecting gas in transformer oil.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a                detecting system. (Drawing includes non-English                language text).

PT P
PN CN114249901-A
TI Aminated zeolitic imidazolate framework-8 gyroscope structure which is prepared by e.g. stirring and mixing long chain organic amine to ethanol aqueous solution, adding potassium chloride, adding zeolitic imidazolate framework-8 powder, sealing, and stirring reaction mixture in water bath.
AU YE J
   ZHANG S
   SHEN Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 202250674W
AB    NOVELTY - Aminated zeolitic imidazolate framework-8 (ZIF-8) gyroscope structure prepared by adding long chain organic amine to volume concentration of 50-100% ethanol aqueous solution, uniformly stirring and mixing for 20-50 minutes, adding potassium chloride, uniformly stirring and mixing at room temperature for 20-50 minutes, adding ZIF-8 powder, sealing, stirring and reacting at room temperature for 2-3 hours, then stirring the reaction mixture in the water bath of 50-70degrees Celsius until the ethanol evaporates completely, weighing after each time of washing until the two adjacent weights changed less than 0.5%, and finally drying to obtain the finished product, where the long-chain organic amine is direct chain alkyl amine of 6-10C, is claimed.
   USE - Used as aminated ZIF-8 gyroscope structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The structure: is added with metal cation to make the ZIF-8 surface hexagonal hole belt positive charge; is assembled with long chain organic amine; greatly reduces the adsorption isotherm of carbon dioxide and amino-functionalized ZIF8 by introducing large number of van der Waals attraction inside the material, which is easy to generalize; and has mild reaction conditions which makes secondary structure preservation rate high and show excellent adsorption performance in carbon dioxide adsorption experiments.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The long-chain organic amine comprises but is not limited to tetraethylenepentamine and/or pentaethylenehexamine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The ZIF-8 powder is pre-dried in an oven at 80degrees Celsius for overnight to remove water vapor in the pores. The ZIF-8 powder is prepared as: dissolving zinc nitrate hexahydrate and 2-methylimidazole in ultrapure water, stirring the mixture at room temperature for 30 minutes, centrifuging, precipitating with ultra-pure water to wash for three times, and then drying at 80degrees Celsiusfor overnight to remove any remaining solvent to obtain ZIF-powder, where the mass ratio of the zinc nitrate hexahydrate and 2-methylimidazole is 1:5.5, and the volume dosage of the ultrapure water is 65 ml/g in terms of the mass of the zinc nitrate hexahydrate.Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the ZIF-8 powder and long-chain organic amine is 1:0.4-0.6. The mass ratio of the ZIF-8 powder and potassium chloride is 1:0.01-0.1. The volume dosage of the ethanol aqueous solution is 100-500 ml/g based on the mass of the ZIF-8 powder. The stirring speed is 800-1000 revolutions per minute. The vacuum of vacuum filter is 0.08-0.1 MPa.

PN CN113583887-A
TI Enrichment culture system for degrading pesticide 2,4-D, obtained by carrying out anaerobic culturing by adding selenium/tungsten mixed salt and trace elements to the base inorganic salt, adding reducing agent.
AU KIM H
   LV Y
   WANG J
   YANG Y
   YAN J
   LI X
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI APPL ECOLOGY INST (CSAE-C)
GA 2021C76497
AB    NOVELTY - Enrichment culture system, obtained by carrying out anaerobic culturing by adding selenium/tungsten mixed salt and trace elements to the base inorganic salt, adding reducing agent at 35-45degrees Celsius, adding pH buffer salt, and then using the mixing plate to pass nitrogen/carbon dioxide mixed gas into the system to make the culture medium system pH 7.2-7.5, and adding vitamins and electron donors after sterilization, is claimed. The reducing agent is L-cysteine, sodium sulfite nonahydrate and DL-dithiothreitol.
   USE - The enrichment culture system is useful for degrading pesticide 2,4-D (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The enrichment culture system does not contain sulfate components and contains a specific reducing agent. The system can completely convert 2,4-D into methane and carbon dioxide under anaerobic conditions, which not only solves the environmental problems caused by incomplete degradation. It can also provide important microbial remediation materials for 2,4-D pesticide pollution remediation in deep soil, groundwater, river and lake sediments and other anaerobic environments.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included forenriching and cultivating anaerobic bacteria that degrade pesticide 2,4-D by using the culture system, inoculating the environmental samples into the anaerobic culture system under anaerobic conditions, adding 2,4-D as an electron acceptor and culturing under dark conditions to obtain an enrichment of anaerobic bacteria that degrade pesticide 2,4-D.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the method, a carbon source can be added to the culture medium after sterilization. The enrichment culture is carried out through a mixing plate under the condition of continuous nitrogen flow in the headspace of the base system: first adding selenium/tungstate mixed salt and trace element solution into the inorganic salt of the anaerobic medium, then adding reducing agent and pH buffer salt at 35-45degreesC, and finally change the medium system The headspace is adjusted to a mixture of N2/CO2 (80/20, volume/volume) so that the pH of the culture medium system is maintained between 7.2 and 7.5, and vitamins and electron donors are added after sterilization in a sealed bottle. The temperature of the static culture in the dark is 27-33degreesC.Preferred Components: In the anaerobic enrichment medium, the final concentration of L-cysteine is 0.03-0.04 g/l, sodium sulfite nonahydrate is 0.04-0.05 g/l, DL-dithiothreitol is 0.1-0.2 g/l and carbon source is 4-6 mmol/l, where the carbon source is lactic acid and/or acetic acid; the electron donor is pure hydrogen, and the addition amount is 5-10 ml/60 ml headspace. In the selenium/tungsten mixed salt of anaerobic enrichment medium, the final concentrations of the microelements are 5-7 mg/l sodium selenite pentahydrate, 7-9 mg/l sodium tungstate dehydrate, and 0.4-0.6 g/l sodium hydraoxide; the final concentrations of each component of the trace elements in in the anaerobic enrichment medium are 1-2 g/l ferric chloride tetrahydrate, 0.1-0.2 g/l cobalt chloride hexahydrate, 0.05-0.2 g/l manganese chloride hexahydrate, 60-80 mg/l zinc chloride, 5-7 mg/l boric acid, 30-40 mg/l sodium molybdate dihydrate, 20-30 mg/l nickel chloride hexahydrate, 1-3 mg/l copper chloride dihydrate; and the final concentration of each component of the inorganic salt in the anaerobic enrichment medium is 1 g/l sodium chloride, 0.5 g/l magnesium chloride hexahydrate, 0.2 g/l potassium phosphate, 0.3 g/ ammonium chloride, 0.3 g/l potassium chloride, 0.015 g/l calcium chloride dihydrate, and the final concentration of the oxygen indicator resazurin in the culture medium is 0.001 mass%. The pH buffer salt is sodium bicarbonate, and its final concentration in the medium is 2-3 g/l. The environmental sample includes soil, bottom sludge, sludge or water; the inoculation amount is 2-5% (mass/volume) of the anaerobic culture system. The enrichment material can degrade 2,4-D or its analogues anaerobicly, and the final product of the conversion is methane and carbon dioxide; among them, 2,4-D analogs include 2,4,5-trichlorophenoxyacetic acid, 2,4,6-trichlorophenol and 2,4-dichlorophenol.

TI Composition used for heating and cooling of            article used as working fluid in heat transfer system            such as refrigeration system, comprises carbon dioxide,            difluoromethane, and component containing            1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane,            trans-1,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene and            1,1,1,2,3,3,3-heptafluoropropane.
AU LOW R E
AE MEXICHEM FLUOR SA DE CV (MEXI-Non-standard)
   MEXICHEM UK LTD (MEXI-C)
GA 202195718F
AB    NOVELTY - A composition comprises carbon dioxide                (R-744), difluoromethane (R-32), and component                (III) selected from 1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane                (R-134a), trans-1,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene                (R-1234ze(E)), 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene                (R-1234yf), 1,1,1,2,3,3,3-heptafluoropropane                (R-227ea) and its mixtures.
   USE - Composition used for heating and cooling of                article used as working fluid in heat transfer                system such as refrigeration system such as                commercial refrigeration system, supermarket                display refrigeration system, beverage cooler                refrigeration system, warehouse refrigeration                system, transportation refrigeration system and                cold-room refrigeration system, and heat pump or                air-conditioning system such as mobile or                transportation air-conditioning system to vehicle                such as bus, car, train and truck (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has low/non-flammability,                improved lubricant miscibility and performance                properties when used in refrigeration systems,                especially in air-conditioning system, excellent                compressor efficiency and durability, and reduced                load and pressure difference.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a heat transfer device, which comprises                the composition;
   (2) cooling of article, which involves                condensing a composition and evaporating the                composition in the vicinity of the article to be                cooled; and
   (3) heating of article, which involves                condensing a composition in the vicinity of the                article to be heated and evaporating the                composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The component (III) is                  one or more of preferably                  trans-1,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene (R-1234ze(E)),                  2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene (R-1234yf), and                  1,1,1,2,3,3,3-heptafluoropropane (R-227ea). The                  composition do not comprises                  1,1,2-trifluoroethylene (R-1123). The lubricant                  is selected from mineral oil, silicone oil,                  polyalkylbenzene, polyolester, polyalkylene                  glycol, polyalkylene glycol ester, polyvinyl                  ether, and/or poly( alpha -olefins). Preferred                  Composition: The composition comprises 65-98                  wt.%, preferably 84-95 wt.% carbon dioxide, 1-25                  wt.%, preferably 5-11 wt.% difluoromethane and                  1-20 wt.%, preferably 5-11 wt.% component (III),                  and further comprises 1-20 wt.%, preferably 5-10                  wt.% 1,1-difluoroethylene (R-1132a). Preferred                  Properties: The composition is non-flammable as                  determined according to ASHRAE 34:2019, and has                  global warming potential (GWP) of less than 300,                  preferably less than 145, a critical temperature                  which is more than the critical temperature of                  carbon dioxide, such as more than 40 degrees C,                  volumetric refrigeration capacity of at least 75%                  or at least 90%, coefficient of performance (COP)                  which is equal to or more than that of carbon                  dioxide, and temperature glide in an evaporator                  or condenser which is less than 12K, such as less                  than 6K.
   EXAMPLE - 65% Carbon dioxide, 10% difluoromethane                (R-32), 10% 1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane (R-134a) and                15% 1,1-difluoroethylene were added and mixed, to                obtain composition having coefficient of                performance of 2.69, volumetric refrigeration                capacity of 14497 kJ/m3, and condensed pressure of                90.2 bar.

PN CN112973480-A; CN112973480-B
TI Preparing chitosan mixed matrix film with high UiO-66 nanoparticle loading amount comprises e.g. dispersing UiO-66-amino group nanoparticle, stirring, scraping cross-linked coating liquid on polyethersulfone base film, and drying.
AU XIAO W
   HUANG Y
   JIANG X
   HE G
   WU X
   LI X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202172254Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing chitosan mixed matrix film with high UiO-66 nanoparticle loading amount comprises (i) dispersing UiO-66-amino group nanparticle in dimethylformamide, adding sodium hydride, removing hydrogen at 40-60 degrees C for 6-8 hours, adding epibromohydrin and reacting at 40-60 degrees C for 12-24 hours to obtain reaction liquid, centrifuging the reaction liquid, washing and drying to obtain EPB-UiO-66-NH2 nanoparticle; (ii) dissolving chitosan in a 1% (V/V) aqueous acetic acid solution to obtain chitosan solution; (iii) adding EPB-UiO-66-NH2 nanoparticles into 1% (V/V) aqueous acetic acid solution, ultrasonically stirring to obtain EPB-UiO-66-NH2 dispersion; (iv) adding chitosan solution into EPB-UiO-66-NH2 dispersion, ultrasonically stirring, reacting under nitrogen atmosphere to obtain cross-linked coating liquid; and (v) scraping the cross-linked coating liquid on polyethersulfone base film to obtain a film, and drying in an oven.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing chitosan mixed matrix film with high UiO-66 nanoparticle loading amount.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for the separation of carbon dioxide-containing mixed gas; has carbon dioxide/nitrogen selectivity is 40; and improves interface compatibility with loading amount of nanoparticle upto 20 wt.%.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for preparing chitosan mixed matrix film with high UiO-66 nanoparticle loading amount.

AE FOSHAN GUANGGU CONDIMENT CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 202167212H
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model relates to the technical field of food fermentation device, especially to a fermentation device for raw material of fermented soybean oil, comprising a fermentation tank, a first temperature sensor, an oxygen supply pump; an air pumping pump and a controller; the fermentation tank is detachably connected with a sealing cover; the sealing cover is provided with a humidity sensor and a carbon dioxide concentration sensor; the fermentation tank is orderly provided with a plurality of transverse air channels from bottom to top; the lower side of the plurality of transverse air channels is provided with a vent; a plurality of first temperature sensors are correspondingly installed in the transverse air passage and detecting the temperature in the fermentation tank through the vent hole; the air inlet of the plurality of transverse air passage is connected with the air outlet of the oxygen supply pump; the sealing cover is provided with an exhaust port; the air pumping port of the air pump is connected with the exhaust port. The utility model of the fermented soy sauce raw material fermenting device can prevent the temperature sensor from crushing; it also can ensure the carbon dioxide concentration and humidity in the fermentation tank in the set range; the fermentation quality of the raw material of the fermented soy sauce is ensured.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D03-K03; D03-N; D05-A04D
IP A23L-011/50; C12M-001/00; C12M-001/02; C12M-001/04; C12M-001/34; C12M-001/36; C12M-001/38
